PN US2022024767-A1
TI Carbon nanotubes formed by grinding, melting and forming raw mantle peridotite into glass cells, exposing glass cells to air wind, and processing mineralized carbon into nanotubes, for preparing nanostructured-carbon-base-material.
AU CANUTO T A
AE CANUTO T A (CANU-Individual)
GA 202217278G
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon nanotubes (6) is formed from a process involving grinding, melting and forming raw mantle peridotite (1) into glass cells (3); exposing the glass cells to air wind to capture carbon dioxide (5) in the air (4) to convert the glass cells into mineralized carbon; and processing the mineralized carbon into nanotubes.
   USE - The carbon nanotubes are useful for preparing nanostructured-carbon-base-material used in catalyst for battery and fuel-cell use; and used in biotechnology applications selected from a biosensor, a drug delivery system, and cultivation of cells (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon nanotubes provide material that has strong adsorption, high affinity binding, and high surface area properties. The peridotite carbon nanotubes are efficient and reliable to use for better and low cost electrodes for lithium-ion batteries, carbon nanotubes to epoxy composites in stronger/stiffer components of windmill blades or aircraft components, carbon nanotubes ink, carbon fibers, e.g. baseball bats, golf clubs, airplane body, car panels, any structure where metal can be replaced by carbon fiber, carbon fiber reinforce plastics or thermoplastics, carbon fibers (twill), carbon fiber tapes, tow and sleeves, carbon nanotubes for computer chips, and carbon nanotubes mirrors for lightweight telescopes in cube sats. The new material which is the carbon nanotubes made from mantle peridotite carbon mineralization based activated carbon is an economical non-precious metal catalyst capitalizes on carbon nanotubes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a catalyst for battery and fuel-cell use comprising nanostructured-carbon-base material, where the nanostructured-carbon-base material is mantle peridotite carbon mineralized based activated carbon nanotubes; and
   (2) a nanostructured-carbon-base-material comprising mantle peridotite carbon mineralization based activated carbon nanotubes, where the carbon nanotubes provide material that has strong adsorption, high affinity binding, and high surface area properties, such that the material is useful in biotechnology applications selected from a biosensor, a drug delivery system, and cultivation of cells.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of peridotite rocks, ground peridotite rocks, a peridotite glass cell, peridotite photovoltaic glass panels that capture CO2 in air, CO2, and carbon nanotubes.
   Peridotite rock (1)
   Glass cell (3)
   Air (4)
   Carbon dioxide (5)
   Carbon nanotubes (6)

PN CN113928602-A
TI Device for measuring enthalpy of special test medium of electric arc heating test, has pressure measuring device for measuring pressure in pressure stabilizing chamber, and data analyzing terminal obtaining air flow enthalpy value based on gas flow and actual measured pressures and gas flow rate.
AU ZENG H
AE CHINA ACAD AEROSPACE AERODYNAMICS (CAER-C)
GA 202217678X
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a pressure measuring device (4) for measuring pressure in a mixed pressure stabilizing chamber (2) to measure hot state pressure when an electric arc heater (1) is working. A flow rate measuring device is used to measure gas flow in a mixing chamber and the mixing chamber. A pressure measurement value and a flow measurement value are output to a data analyzing terminal (6) in real-time. The data analyzing terminal is based on a gas flow enthalpy value and an actual measured pressure and the gas flow rate to obtain an air flow enthalpy value in real time. The pressure measurement device is provided with a pressure sensor. The pressure sensor is connected with the pressure sensor. The data analyzing terminal obtains an air flow enthalpy value in real time based on gas flow, actual measured pressures and gas flow rate.
   USE - Device for measuring enthalpy of special test medium of electric arc heating test.
   ADVANTAGE - The data analyzing terminal is based on the relationship between the air flow enthalpy value and the actual measured pressure and the gas flow, and obtains the gas-flow-enthalpy values in real time, thus accurately determining the electric arc heating test special test medium gas flow and gas flow rate in an accurate manner, and hence realizing the determination of new high-speed aircraft e.g. flying Mach number and heating environment arc heating ground test gas flow. The device is suitable for air, carbon dioxide, nitrogen dioxide and other test mediums, thus realizing the measurement of the air, carbon dioxide and nitrogen dioxide in a simple and accurate manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for measuring enthalpy of special test medium of electric arc heating test.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the device for measuring enthalpy of special test medium of electric arc heating test (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Electric arc heater (1)
   Mixed pressure stabilizing chamber (2)
   Spraying pipe (3)
   Pressure measuring device (5)
   Data analyzing terminal (6)

PT P
PN WO2022008268-A1; EP3936598-A1
TI Recovery of fractional substances from vapors during alcohol reduction of beverage, by supplying alcoholic beverage to degasser of recovery apparatus, and passing alcoholic beverage through degasser to device for reducing alcohol.
AU SAUTNER K
   GUELTLINGER C
AE API SCHMIDT-BRETTEN GMBH & CO KG (MITU-C)
   API SCHMIDT-BRETTEN GMBH & CO KG (MITU-C)
GA 2022075050
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering at least one fractional substances from vapors during the alcohol reduction of a beverage, involves supplying an alcoholic beverage to a degasser (62) of a fractional substance recovery apparatus (60); removing vapors from the degasser and feeding the alcoholic beverage passed through the degasser to a device (10) for reducing alcohol; feeding the vapors to fractional condensation stages (66, 67, 68) which follow one another in series and in which at least one fractional substance is separated from the vapors under pressure and/or temperature; and collecting the fractional substances separated in the respective fractional condensation stage in a collecting container or feeding to a inoculation station (19), through which separated fractional substances are fed in dosed form to a reduced-alcohol beverage.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering at least one fractional substances from vapors during the alcohol reduction of a beverage (claimed), where the beverage is alcoholic beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The fractional substances desired for the enrichment of the reduced-alcohol beverage, in particular aroma substances or fragrances, can be specifically returned to the beverage, where a flavor enrichment and improvement of the beverage is achieved. The process can also enable the carbon dioxide contained in the alcoholic beverage to be recovered as a fractional substance prior to treatment of the alcohol reduction. This results in a reduction in the emission of carbon dioxide to the environment, as direct recirculation is possible.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a fractional substance recovery device for recovering at least one fractional substance from vapors during alcohol reduction of a beverage, with a degasser, with fractional condensation stages arranged in series one after the other, which are connected downstream of the degasser, where at least one line extending through the fractional condensation stages is provided, and each condensation stage comprises at least one fractional substance which is discharged through the at least one separator (75).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a fractional substances recovery device.
   Device for reducing alcohol (10)
   Inoculation station (19)
   Fractional substance recovery apparatus (60)
   Degasser (62)
   Fractional condensation stages (66, 67, 68)
   Separator (75)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: In the fractional condensation stage , the supplied vapors are pressurized via a pump (71), preferably vacuum pump, and subsequently cooled with at least one cooler (72, 73), in particular heat exchanger, and subsequently at least one fractional substance is separated via a separator. The vapors are pressurized with increasing pressure in the fractional condensation stages following one another in series and are preferably cooled to a constant temperature range of the fractional condensation stages before leaving the respective condensation stage. In a first fractional condensation stage, the vapors are compressed from preferably 100 mbar to in particular 1.5-2 bar and preferably cooled to 40-50 degrees C. In a second fractional condensation stage, which is connected downstream of the first condensation stage, the remaining vapors are compressed from, for example, 1.5-2 bar to an increased pressure, preferably 3-5 bar, and are preferably cooled to 5-10 degrees C. In a third fractional condensation stage, the remaining vapors of for example 3-5 bar are compressed to an increased pressure, preferably 10-12 bar, and are preferably cooled to 5-10 degrees C. Before the alcohol reduction of the alcoholic beverage, the vapors of the alcoholic beverage are removed. The fractional substances separated by the fractional condensation stages are fed through the inoculation station to an alcohol-reduced beverage which is finished by the alcohol reduction apparatus and has been discharged from the alcohol reduction process. In the inoculation station the amount of the separated fractional substances from the fractional substance recovery device, which are supplied to the finished alcohol-reduced beverage, are selected and adjusted.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN113834253-A
TI Fresh-maintaining refrigerator, has nitrogen-rich film assembly and oxygen-rich film assembly fill nitrogen into fresh-maintaining space, where gas adjusting assembly has oxygen-rich membrane assembly for separating oxygen from fresh-maintaining space.
AU CHEN J
   JIAO W
   CHENG X
   LIU C
AE GREE ELECTRIC APPLIANCES INC (GREZ-C)
GA 202207636D
AB    NOVELTY - The refrigerator has a sealed drawer (10), where a fresh-keeping space relatively isolated from air is formed in the sealed drawer. A gas conditioning module, has an oxygen-enriched membrane module (20). The oxygen-enriched membrane module is used to separate oxygen from the fresh-keeping space. A nitrogen-enriched membrane module (40) is used for filling the fresh-keeping space with nitrogen. An adjustable air-conditioning membrane assembly (30) is provided on the sealed drawer. A modified atmosphere membrane is used to adjust the oxygen concentration and carbon dioxide concentration of the fresh-keeping space relative to the external space. A vacuum pump (50) includes a suction pipe and an exhaust pipe.
   USE - Fresh-maintaining refrigerator. Uses include but are not limited to fruit and vegetable fresh-keeping field range by controlling temperature in storage environment, humidity, carbon dioxide, oxygen concentration and ethylene concentration, inhibiting respiratory effect of fruit and vegetables, delaying metabolism process, and fruit vegetable is in dormant state, preventing corrosion and deterioration, and prolonging storage period.
   ADVANTAGE - The refrigerator effectively reduces oxygen in a fresh-maintaining space, and prolongs time of fruit and vegetable. The refrigerator prevents oxygen aggregation and nitrogen aggregation. The oxygen-enriched film assembly is utilizes for separating oxygen from the fresh-maintaining space and the nitrogen-rich film assembly for filling the nitrogen into the sealed drawer, so that the oxygen concentration and nitrogen concentration in the drawer can be effectively reduced, thus prolonging life span of fruits and vegetables stored in the refrigerator, and hence reducing consumption amount of the oxygen. The nitrogen is filled into the drawer through the nitrogen separation membrane module, so as to prevent the nitrogen from being discharged into atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a control method of fresh-keeping refrigerator;
   (2) a storage medium containing computer executable instruction.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a fresh-maintaining refrigerator.
   Oxygen sealing drawer (10)
   Oxygen-enriched membrane component (20)
   Adjustable air adjusting film assembly (30)
   Nitrogen-rich membrane component (40)
   Vacuum pump (50)

PT P
PN IN202011028602-A
TI Hybrid biofilm-suspended photobioreactor dome-shaped upper surface has baffle covered with non-woven fabric material for algal-biofilm development, and passive plastic light pipes internally coated with titania- and iron-nanoparticles.
AU PRAJAPATI S K
   SINGH M R
AE INDIAN INST TECHNOLOGY ROORKEE (INTE-Non-standard)
GA 2021D4462D
AB    NOVELTY - Hybrid biofilm-suspended photobioreactor (PBR) (1) comprises regularly spaced baffles (2) inside the reactor equipped vertically availing riser and down comer zone (3) inside the PBR. The baffle is covered-up with non-woven fabric material as a support material for algal-biofilm development, and air/carbon dioxide gas mixer inlet (4) provided in the riser zone at the bottom of the reactor. A gas outlet section is intruded at the top of the reactor, and conical-shaped passive plastic light pipes (5) for maintaining a little portion above the dome surface. The dome-shaped upper surface has passive plastic light pipes which is internally coated with titania nanoparticles, and iron nanoparticle as supplementation in the algae-growth media.
   USE - The hybrid biofilm-suspended photobioreactor is useful for high-density algae cultivation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The hybrid biofilm-suspended PBR: has cylindrical PBR, which has dome-shaped lid along with conical light pipes, ensures maximum solar light trapping and utilization due to a higher level of internal illumination compared to the conventional column reactors; comprises light pipes which is internally coated with titania nanoparticle solution that can enhance its refractive index and hence improves solar light capture and transmission; contains conical shaped light pipes which are vertically intruded inside the photobioreactor alongside the upper surface of the photobioreactor and coated with titania nanoparticles thus enhancing its refractive index; comprises passive plastic light pipes of conical shape which has a dome shaped upper surface and a pointed bottom and are internally coated with titania nanoparticle solution that transmits the maximum of the solar light into the PBR; and utilizes adequate dose (20-30 mg/l) of the iron (in nano form) which stimulates microalgae photosynthetic efficiency, growth and biofuel precursors, during cultivation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a 2-dimensional schematic representation of the hybrid biofilm-suspended photobioreactor for high-density algae cultivation.
   Hybrid biofilm-suspended photobioreactor (1)
   Regularly spaced baffles (2)
   Down comer zone (3)
   Air/carbon dioxide gas mixer inlet (4)
   Conical-shaped passive plastic light pipe (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The conical shaped light pipes were vertically intruded inside the photobioreactor alongside the upper surface of the photobioreactor. The non-woven fabric materials are configured with a water contact angle of less than 90 degrees C to keep material's surface wet enough to ensure soluble nutrients availability continuously for microalgae growth that shall support development of a healthy microalgal biofilm.

PN WO2021180404-A1
TI Air conditioning arrangement for vehicle e.g. rail vehicle, has intermediate circuit with cooler through which refrigerant flows, where Luftbehandlerein direction and adjoining sections of intermediate circuit are hermetically sealed.
AU HILDEBRANDT A
   KASAP I
   SCHNELL B
AE SIEMENS MOBILITY GMBH (SIEI-C)
GA 2021A6566C
AB    NOVELTY - The arrangement has a refrigerant circuit (1) that is arranged with a compressor (3), a condenser (4) with an associated condenser fan (5), an expansion valve (6), and a plate heat exchanger (7). An air treatment device (10) is arranged with a fan (11) for conveying conditioned supply air into an interior (14,15) of the vehicle. The heat exchanger is designed to thermally interact the first refrigerant with a second refrigerant and is integrated into an intermediate circuit (2). The intermediate circuit is arranged with a cooler (9) through which the second refrigerant is flowed, to form an air treatment device. The air treatment device and sections of the intermediate circuit are arranged for adjoining the treatment device and are hermetically sealed with respect to the rest of the air-conditioning arrangement. The first refrigerant is selected from carbon dioxide, propane, a hydrofluoro-olefin or mixtures thereof, and the second refrigerant is water glycol.
   USE - Air conditioning arrangement for passenger transport vehicle (claimed) vehicle such as rail vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The arrangement reduces the risks when using refrigerants with a global warming potential of greater than or equal to 150, and specifies a vehicle whose low risk air conditioning system also uses a refrigerant with a Global warming potential can be operated. Due to the hermetic sealing of the air treatment device from the refrigeration circuit, the first refrigerant cannot even in the event of a leak in the refrigerant line V. It can happen that leaked refrigerant gets into the air handling device and thus also into the interior of the vehicle. In this respect, the air conditioning circuit of the arrangement is operated without further ado with refrigerant which can be selected from the group comprising carbon dioxide, propane, a hydrofluoro-olefin or mixtures. The second refrigerant can be, for example, water glycol.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the air conditioning arrangement.
   Refrigerant circuit (1)
   Intermediate circuit (2)
   Compressor (3)
   Condenser (4)
   Condenser fan (5)
   Expansion valve (6)
   Plate heat exchanger (7)
   Cooler (9)
   Air treatment device (10)
   Fan (11)
   Interior (14,15)

PN CN112961216-A; CN112961216-B
TI New streptococcus mutans specifically targeted antimicrobial peptides useful for inhibiting re-planting of caries bacteria.
AU TU H
   QIU L
   ZHANG X
   PAN Y
   REN P
AE UNIV WENZHOU MEDICAL STOMATOLOGY SCHOOL (WEZN-C)
GA 202168755K
AB    NOVELTY - Streptococcus mutans specifically targeted antimicrobial peptides, is new.
   USE - The Streptococcus mutans specifically targeted antimicrobial peptides is useful for inhibiting re-planting of caries bacteria.
   ADVANTAGE - The peptides prevents caries for a long time, bound to the surface of Streptococcus mutans is connected with the broad-spectrum antimicrobial peptide fragments, and the specific targeting antimicrobial peptides of Streptococcus mutans.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Streptococcus mutans specifically targeted antimicrobial peptides, comprises an amino acid sequence of TFFRLFNRGGGRRWWRF, is new. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for: (1) preparing specific targeting antimicrobial peptides of Streptococcus mutans comprising; and (2) minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) and minimum bactericidal concentration (MBC) determination of Streptococcus mutans specific targeting antimicrobial peptides comprises (i) picking single colony in 10 ml BHI medium, placing in a constant temperature anaerobic culture tank at 37 degrees C overnight anaerobic culture (80% nitrogen, 10% hydrogen, 10% carbondioxide), (ii) pipette 100 mu l of overnight bacterial solution and adding 10 ml medium, placing in a constant temperature anaerobic culture tank at 37 degrees C overnight anaerobic culture (80% nitrogen, 10% hydrogen, 10% carbon dioixde), diluting bacterial solution to 2x 106 CFU/ml for later use, (iii) using 2-fold dilution method to add 20 mu l of peptide solution to the 96-well plate, 80 mu l of BHI medium and 100 mu l spare bacterial solution, making final concentration of peptides 512-0.5 mu g/ml, placing orifice plate in a constant temperature anaerobic culture tank at 37 degrees C anaerobic culture (80% nitrogen, 10% hydrogen, 10% carbon dioxide) for 24 hours, (iv) MIC is the lowest clarified peptide concentration in a 96-well plate, (v) taking 100 mu l bacterial solution from clear well and spreading on Brain Heart Infusion agar plate, placing in a constant temperature anaerobic culture tank at 37 degrees C overnight anaerobic culture (80% nitrogen, 10% hydrogen, 10% carbon dioxide) 48 hours, (vi) MBC is the lowest polypeptide concentration formed by aseptic colonies on the plate, (vii) chlorhexidine was used as a positive control, and BHI medium was used as a negative control, (viii) repeating experiment 3 times with 3 parallel samples in each group.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The Streptococcus mutans specific targeting antimicrobial peptide is formed by combining CSP fragments with a short chain of artificial antimicrobial peptides, and the peptide chain is shorter. Preparation (Claimed): The preparation method comprises taking Wang resin into the reaction column, adding dichloromethane to fully swell the resin. washing with dimethylformamide, removing DCM, using prepared deprotecting agent to remove the Fmoc protecting group on the resin, taking off the cap for 20 minutes, washing 3 times with dimethylformamide, If the color test is blue, the Fmoc protecting group is successfully removed, If is colorless, means no response, Performing re-uncap (ii) adding second amino acid arginine according to the amino acid sequence of the polypeptide, adding condensing agent benzotriazole-N,N,N',N'-tetramethylurea hexafluorophosphate and catalyst N-methylmorpholine or N, N diiso propylethylamine conduct a condensation reaction; reaction is over, adding dimethylformamide for repeated washing, removing unreacted amino acid, (iii) checking ninhydrin whether the connection is complete, result of the test is that the solution is bright yellow, and transparenting resin and has no variegation, (iv) product obtained after washing is decapped and washed in the same way, adding amino acids in sequence according according to the amino acid sequence for condensation reaction and washing, connecting amino acids, removing last Fmoc-protecting group, and washing with dimethylformamide, (v) washing and shrinking with DCM and methanol respectively, adding trifluoroacetic acid TFA for cracking, filtering the lysed product to obtain the filtrate, (vi) adding 3 times volume of glacial ether (4 degrees C) to the filtrate obtained according to (vi) to promote the precipitation of the peptides, sealing and mixing upside down, and recover the precipitate; resuspending precipitate with cold ether, repeating the above steps three times, drying precipitate to obtain the crude polypeptide; (vii) purifying crude product by reversed-phase high performance liquid method and freeze-drying, determining purity of the purified and dried antimicrobial peptide by high performance liquid chromatography (HPLC), and determining molecular mass by mass spectrometry (MS) to be consistent with the theoretically calculated molecular weigh .

PN CN112608623-A; CN112608623-B
TI Preparation of ultra-high sound insulation performance nano composite carbon black, comprises dispersing P,P-oxo-phenylsulfonyl hydrazide in ethanol, then adding nano carbon black, uniformly stirring and vacuum extracting to remove ethanol.
AU WANG H
   SHAO L
   NIE X
   CHEN Z
AE QINGDAO BLACKCAT NEW MATERIAL RES INST (QING-Non-standard)
GA 202138290L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of ultra-high sound insulation performance nano composite carbon black, comprises (i) dispersing P,P-oxo-phenylsulfonyl hydrazide with a mass of 1/12-1/10 nano-carbon black in ethanol with a mass of 1.5-2 times the mass of the nano-carbon black, then adding nano carbon black and uniformly stirring; (ii) vacuum extracting to remove ethanol under reduced pressure to obtain nano carbon black with P, P-oxo-benzene sulfonyl hydrazide adsorbed, and heating at 140160 degrees C for 20-30 minutes; (iii) quickly adding liquid carbon dioxide 0.8-1.5 times the mass of nano carbon black through the pipeline, pressurize it to 7.5 MPa, and heating at 35 degrees C to become a supercritical state, and circulating for 10-20 minutes; and (iv) discharging carbon dioxide under reduced pressure to obtain ultra-high sound insulation performance nano-composite carbon black.
   USE - The method is useful for preparation of ultra-high sound insulation performance nano composite carbon black.
   ADVANTAGE - The method expands the internal cracks and surface pores of nano carbon black to increase the specific surface area and porosity; and improves sound-insulating performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) ultra-high sound insulation performance nano composite carbon black, is prepared as mentioned above;
   (2) sound insulation coating comprising 50-80 pts. wt. epoxy resin, 10-15 pts. wt. ultra-high sound insulation performance nano-composite carbon black, 0.1 pts. wt. drier, 0.2-0.5 pts. wt. wetting dispersant, 20-30 pts. wt. oil solvent and 3-5 pts. wt. P, P-oxo-benzene sulfonyl hydrazide; and
   (3) construction of the sound insulation coating comprises (i) coating the soundproof paint brush on the construction surface, firstly heating at 80 degrees C for 5-8 minutes, evaporating the solvent, (ii) then heating at 140 degrees C for 3-5 minutes to foam, then heating at 145 degrees C and curing for 1-3 minutes, and (iii) then heating to 150 degrees C for 1-2 minutes for foaming, then heating 160 degrees C and curing for 10-15 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The grain diameter of the nano-composite carbon black is 25-60 nm. The epoxy resin is E-44 epoxy resin. The drier is Yuanbang Y-3354. The wetting and dispersing agent is BYK110. The oily solvent is butyl acetate. The brushing thickness of the sound-insulating paint is 0.2-0.5mm. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the vacuum extracting at 60-70 degrees C under reduced pressure of -0.07-0.09 MPa. In the step (iii), the nano carbon black is made to absorb moisture until the moisture content reaches 3-5% before adding liquid carbon dioxide.

TI Expanding active carbon fiber by removing impurity by sending active carbon fiber into static impurity removing box to remove impurity, cleaning, and sending washed activated carbon fiber into drying box.
AU JIN S
AE SUZHOU YUANHAO NEW TEXTILE TECHNOLOGY CO (SUZH-Non-standard)
   SUZHOU YUANHAO NEW TEXTILE TECHNOLOGY CO (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2021371192
AB    NOVELTY - Expanding activated carbon fiber comprises removing impurities from activated carbon fiber; cleaning activated carbon fiber; drying washed activated carbon fiber; reacting dried activated carbon fiber with magnesium powder; washing activated carbon fiber; drying washed activated carbon fiber; placing dried activated carbon fiber in tube furnace, and heating modified activated carbon fiber; washing modified activated carbon fiber; drying washed activated carbon fiber; soaking dried activated carbon fiber in ethanol solution, and adding hydroxide as pore-expanding agent; continuously soaking soaked activated carbon fiber in surfactant, heating soaked activated carbon fiber at 300-500 degrees C under nitrogen atmosphere for 2-4 hours, switching nitrogen into mixed gas of water vapor and carbon dioxide, and heating soaked activated carbon fiber at 700-800 degrees C for 1-3 hours; washing modified activated carbon fiber; and drying washed activated carbon fiber.
   USE - The method is useful for expanding activated carbon fiber.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can effectively improve the expanding effect of the active carbon fiber and the whole strength of the active carbon fiber does not influence, so as to expand the market competitiveness, which meets the interest of the enterprise itself.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Expanding activated carbon fiber comprises removing impurities from activated carbon fiber; cleaning activated carbon fiber; drying washed activated carbon fiber; reacting dried activated carbon fiber with magnesium powder at 900-1300 degrees C for 30-70 minutes; washing activated carbon fiber; drying washed activated carbon fiber; placing dried activated carbon fiber in tube furnace, heating modified activated carbon fiber at 300 degrees C under nitrogen atmosphere, and heating modified activated carbon fiber at 900-1100 degrees C for 2-3 hours; washing modified activated carbon fiber; drying washed activated carbon fiber; soaking dried activated carbon fiber in ethanol solution, and adding hydroxide as pore-expanding agent; continuously soaking soaked activated carbon fiber in surfactant, heating soaked activated carbon fiber at 300-500 degrees C under nitrogen atmosphere for 2-4 hours, switching nitrogen into mixed gas of water vapor and carbon dioxide, and heating soaked activated carbon fiber at 700-800 degrees C for 1-3 hours; washing modified activated carbon fiber; and drying washed activated carbon fiber.

PN CN112441919-A
TI Improved synthesis method of trinexapac-ethyl, comprises e.g. adding solvent into 1,3-cyclohexanedione, then reacting with sodium hydride, condensing with cyclopropionyl chloride, and performing carboxylation and esterification reactions.
AU WAN L
AE JIANGSU AOLUNDA HIGH-TECH IND CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202124428K
AB    NOVELTY - Improved synthesis method of trinexapac-ethyl (IV), comprises (i) adding solvent into 1,3-cyclohexanedione (I), then reacting with sodium hydride at certain temperature and condensing with cyclopropionyl chloride to obtain condensation product 2-(cyclopropyl(hydroxy)methylene)cyclohexane-1,3-dione (II). The mass ratio between (I), cyclopropionyl chloride and sodium hydride is 1:0.94-0.95:0.22-0.23, and reaction temperature is -5 to 5 degrees C, (ii) passing (II) into carbon dioxide under the action of catalyst, and maintaining under certain pressure for carboxylation reaction to obtain 4-(cyclopropyl(hydroxy)methylene)-3,5-dioxocyclohexanecarboxylic acid (III). The reaction pressure is 1-4 atmospheric pressure, and reaction temperature is 65-125 degrees C, and (iii) subjecting (III) and ethanol to esterification reaction under sulfuric acid catalysis to obtain final product. The mass ratio between (III), ethanol and concentrated sulfuric acid is 1:0.42-0.70:0.065-0.15.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing trinexapac-ethyl.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improved synthesis method of trinexapac-ethyl of formula (IV), comprises (i) adding solvent into 1,3-cyclohexanedione of formula (I), then reacting with sodium hydride at certain temperature and condensing with cyclopropionyl chloride to obtain condensation product 2-(cyclopropyl(hydroxy)methylene)cyclohexane-1,3-dione of formula (II). The mass ratio between (I), cyclopropionyl chloride and sodium hydride is 1:0.94-0.95:0.22-0.23, and reaction temperature is -5 to 5 degrees C, (ii) passing (II) into carbon dioxide under the action of catalyst, and maintaining under certain pressure for carboxylation reaction to obtain 4-(cyclopropyl(hydroxy)methylene)-3,5-dioxocyclohexanecarboxylic acid of formula (III). The reaction pressure is 1-4 atmospheric pressure, and reaction temperature is 65-125 degrees C, and (iii) subjecting (III) and ethanol to esterification reaction under sulfuric acid catalysis to obtain final product. The mass ratio between (III), ethanol and concentrated sulfuric acid is 1:0.42-0.70:0.065-0.15.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i): solvent is dichloromethane, chloroform, toluene and/or non-polar solvents, preferably dichloromethane; and reaction temperature is 0 degrees C. In step (ii): catalyst is mixture of 1,8-diazabicycloundec-7-ene and graphene oxide; mass ratio between (II), 1,8-diazabicycloundec-7-ene and graphene oxide is 1:0.025-0.05:0.01-0.025; and reaction pressure is 2-3 atmospheric pressure, and reaction temperature is 85-120 degrees C. In step (iii): mass ratio between (III), ethanol and concentrated sulfuric acid is 1:0.25-0.3:0.075-0.1.

PN CN112359219-A
TI Recycling lead oxide from waste lead-acid storage battery involves disassembling recycled waste lead-acid storage battery, separating lead paste, washing, adding saturated ammonia aqueous solution, acid washing, pickling and desulfurizing.
AU YUAN H
   QIU H
AE MILUO JINSHENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (MILU-Non-standard)
GA 2021207035
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling lead oxide from waste lead-acid storage battery involves disassembling recycled waste lead-acid storage battery, respectively obtaining waste acid solution, plastic shell, plate grid and lead paste, separating lead paste, washing using purified water, adding saturated ammonia aqueous solution, continuously filling carbon dioxide, reacting, and filtering; adding sodium nitrite solution, standing, acid washing in dilute sulfuric acid, pickling and keeping temperature, and processing; mixing with sodium hydroxide solution for desulfurization reaction, continuously stirring, slowly dripping hydrogen peroxide, continuously stirring to reduce lead(IV) oxide to lead(II) oxide, heating, reacting, naturally cooling and filtering; and mixing with acetic acid aqueous solution, adding sodium chloride in supersaturated state, adding sodium carbonate to react, generating lead carbonate precipitate, separating lead carbonate precipitate, drying, and heating and vacuumizing.
   USE - The method is used for recycling lead oxide from waste lead-acid storage battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and easy, uses common raw material and device, simplifies process, and greatly reduces raw material cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling lead oxide from waste lead-acid storage battery involves:
   (A) disassembling recycled waste lead-acid storage battery, respectively obtaining waste acid solution, plastic shell, plate grid and lead paste, separating lead paste, washing using purified water to neutral or weak alkaline, adding saturated ammonia aqueous solution, continuously filling carbon dioxide, reacting for 45-60 minutes, and filtering to obtain first filter pressing paste;
   (B) adding sodium nitrite solution to prepare slurry, standing for 5-8 hours, acid washing in dilute sulfuric acid, pickling and keeping temperature at 50-60 degrees C, and processing for 90-120 minutes;
   (C) mixing with 25-30 wt.% sodium hydroxide solution for desulfurization reaction, continuously stirring for 10-15 minutes to obtain mixture solution, slowly dripping hydrogen peroxide, continuously stirring to reduce lead(IV) oxide to lead(II) oxide, heating, reacting to 70-90 degrees C for 30-45 minutes, naturally cooling and filtering to obtain third filter pressing paste; and
   (D) mixing with acetic acid aqueous solution, adding sodium chloride in supersaturated state, adding sodium carbonate to react, generating lead carbonate precipitate, separating lead carbonate precipitate, drying, and heating and vacuumizing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves washing recycled lead paste material by running water in water tank, and stopping when pH value is 7.0-8.2, adding 2-3 times of 3 mol/L sodium nitrite aqueous solution to first filter pressing lead paste, dripping 5-6 wt.% hydrogen peroxide as oxidant at speed of 3-5 drops/10 L mixture solution until there is no new precipitate generated and no gas generated, continuously dropping for 30-60 seconds, and heating and vacuumizing lead carbonate precipitate in protective gas environment of roasting furnace to 550-580 degrees C for 3-4 hours. Preferred Components: The molar ratio of dilute sulfuric acid to sodium nitrite solution is 2:3-2:5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acetic acid aqueous solution has acetic acid concentration of 5 mol/L and dosage of 150-180 wt.% of third filter pressing paste.

PN CN112029716-A
TI Isolating and preparing placental amniotic mesenchymal stem cells, by obtaining placenta, peeling amniotic membrane, adding amniotic membrane tissue, treating, adding e.g. collagenase I, centrifuging, and culturing medium.
AU WANG X
   MU H
   LI W
AE ZHONGKANG REGENERATIVE MEDICINE TECHNOLOGY HAINAN CO LTD (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 2020C6167B
AB    NOVELTY - Method for isolating and preparing placental amniotic mesenchymal stem cells, involves placing placenta in treatment tray, peeling the amniotic membrane from the placenta along the junction of the umbilical cord and the placenta by hand, washing with a cleaning solution, soaking with 75% alcohol, sterilizing 75% alcohol using surgical scissors to cut the rinsed amniotic membrane, washing with cleaning solution, adding washed amniotic membrane tissue into a test tube, adding the pancreas enzyme, treating at 35-37 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, washing the trypsin-treated amniotic membrane tissue with washing solution, cutting, placing in a centrifuge tube, placing 8 ml amniotic membrane tissue in each tube, adding collagenase I, DNase I and physiological saline, digesting, adding physiological saline after shaking and digesting, centrifuging, discarding the supernatant, inoculating centrifuged cells into a culture flask, and culturing the culture flask in carbon dioxide incubator.
   USE - The method is useful for isolating and preparing placental amniotic mesenchymal stem cells (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method isolates placental amniotic mesenchymal stem cells simply and cost-effectively.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for isolating and preparing placental amniotic mesenchymal stem cells, involves placing placenta in treatment tray, peeling the amniotic membrane from the placenta along the junction of the umbilical cord and the placenta by hand, washing with a cleaning solution, soaking with 75% alcohol, sterilizing 75% alcohol using surgical scissors to cut the rinsed amniotic membrane into 1 cm2 amniotic membrane tissue block, washing with cleaning solution, adding washed amniotic membrane tissue into a test tube, adding the pancreas enzyme, treating at 35-37 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, washing the trypsin-treated amniotic membrane tissue with washing solution, cutting to 1-2 mm2 amniotic membrane tissue pieces, placing in a centrifuge tube, placing 8 ml amniotic membrane tissue in each tube, adding collagenase I, DNase I and physiological saline, digesting with shaking at 150 revolutions per minute (rpm) and 35-37 degrees C for 50-60 minutes, adding physiological saline after shaking and digesting, centrifuging at 1300 rpm and 35-37 degrees C for 5 minutes, discarding the supernatant, inoculating centrifuged cells into a culture flask by adding 10 ml fresh medium, and culturing the culture flask in carbon dioxide incubator.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of collagenase I in the centrifuge tube is 0.1%, and the concentration of DNase I is 0.02%. The fresh medium is Dulbecco's modified eagle medium/nutrient mixture F-12 (DMEM/F12) medium. The culture temperature of the culture is 35-37 degrees C.

PN CN111939084-A
TI Gel facial mask useful for whitening and removing            freckle, comprises mask cloth, and gel immersed in mask            cloth comprising e.g. traditional Chinese medicinal            gel, polyacrylamide, xanthan gum, ginseng root extract            and phenoxyethanol.
AU QI X
AE QI X (QIXX-Individual)
GA 2020B7250K
AB    NOVELTY - Gel facial mask comprises a mask cloth and a                gel immersed in the mask cloth. The gel comprises                30-40 pts. wt. traditional Chinese medicinal gel,                3-5 pts. wt. polyacrylamide, 2-4 pts. wt. xanthan                gum, 2-4 pts. wt. ginseng root extract, 2-4 pts.                wt. phenoxyethanol, 2-4 pts. wt. carbomer, 0.1-3                pts. wt. glycerin, 3-5 pts. wt. essential oils,                10-15 pts. wt. viscous liquid and 0.1-0.3 pts. wt.                kojic acid. The Chinese medicinal gel comprises 1-2                pts. wt. radix astragali, 2-3 pts. wt.                Phellodendron, 3-4 pts. wt. frankincense, 3-4 pts.                wt. borneol, 15-20 pts. wt. water and 6-7 pts. wt.                egg white gel.
   USE - The facial mask is useful for whitening and                removing freckle (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The facial mask: can be hung on the ears                through the arc-shaped grooves, so that the user                cannot easily fall off the mask when walking; and                utilizes essential oils and viscous liquid, which                has better whitening effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the gel facial mask, comprising (i)                obtaining rose seed oil after pressing and                filtering the rose seed, mixing the fresh rose                petals into rose seed oil after pretreatment, fully                stirring them through the stirring mechanism, and                placing the stirred material in the carbon dioxide                supercritical extraction device for extraction to                obtain the mixed essential oil, (ii) mixing freshly                peeled lotus root, towel gourd and okra in a weight                ratio of 1:1:1, placing them in a mixer after                high-speed mixing, filtering off the filter residue                to obtain a viscous mixed liquid, and placing the                viscous mixed liquid in a carbon dioxide                supercritical extraction device for extraction to                obtain a mixed viscous liquid, and (iii) fully                stirring and mixing traditional Chinese medicine                gel, polyacrylamide, xanthan gum, ginseng root                extract, phenoxyethanol, carbomer, glycerin,                essential oil, viscous liquid and kojic acid at                room temperature to obtain a gel, and dipping the                gel into the mask cloth to obtain gel facial                mask.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The viscous liquid is                  lotus root juice, towel gourd juice and/or okra                  juice. The essential oil is rose seed oil and                  rose flower oil. The left and right sides of the                  facial mask cloth are provided with arc-shaped                  grooves for hanging on human ears. The egg white                  gel comprises mixture of dehydrated egg white and                  sodium hydroxide in a mass ratio of 10:1-8:1.                  Preferred Method: The rose petal preprocessing in                  step (i) specifically comprises (a) soaking the                  fresh rose petals in clean water for 15-30                  minutes and removing, and then soaking the rose                  petals in 3% salt water for 5-10 minutes and                  removing, (b) cutting the rose petals into small                  pieces through a cutting device, and (c) mixing                  the rose petals cut into blocks in step (b) with                  honey of 10 wt.% of the rose, fully stirring and                  leaving for 10-20 minutes, and washing using                  clean water to obtain pretreated rose petals. The                  specific treatment of the viscous liquid in the                  step (ii) is specifically: (1) washing fresh                  lotus root, towel gourd and okra with                  high-pressure water first to remove residual                  external impurities, (2) placing the lotus root,                  towel gourd and okra into hot water at 100                  degrees C for 30 seconds, quickly placing the                  lotus root, towel gourd and okra into ice water                  for 30 seconds, and rubbing the outer skin of                  lotus root, towel gourd and okra, (3) cutting and                  weighing the lotus root, towel gourd and okra in                  a weight ratio of 1:1:1, cutting the lotus root,                  towel gourd and okra into blocks, and placing                  them in a mixer for mixing, (4) filtering the                  stirred materials once, obtaining filter residue,                  soaking the filter residue in clean water for                  10-15 minutes, placing the filter residue and                  clean water mixture into a mixer for stirring,                  and performing secondary filtration to obtain a                  viscous mixture, and (5) placing the viscous                  mixed liquid in a carbon dioxide supercritical                  extraction device for extraction to obtain a                  mixed viscous liquid. The specific method in step                  (iii) is: placing the mask cloth into the gel for                  30-60 minutes, taking out the mask cloth, packing                  the mask cloth into a plastic bag, mixing the gel                  into the plastic bag, and after packaging the                  plastic bag, obtaining the finished mask. The                  method for preparing the egg white gel is: taking                  fresh egg white, extracting the free water in the                  egg white through a carbon dioxide supercritical                  extraction device, mixing dehydrated egg white                  and sodium hydroxide, and obtaining the egg white                  gel after stirring by high-speed mixer.

PN WO2020117145-A2; WO2020117145-A3
TI Reducing atmosphere spray dryer system for maintaining chemical, sensory and nutritional characteristics of products e.g. milk and milk products, has gas mixer in which nitrogen and inert and carbon dioxide and reducing gases are mixed.
AU DURIED A
   OERS B
AE UNIV IGDIR (UYIG-Non-standard)
   DURIED A (DURI-Individual)
GA 202051523A
AB    NOVELTY - The reducing atmosphere spray dryer system has a ceding tank (1) that is comprised of the product. A drying chamber (2) is provided to which product is sent for drying. A sprayer/atomizer (3) is configured to spray the product into the drying chamber. A cyclone (4) is provided in which the dry product obtained in powder form is deposited. A dry product chamber (5) is provided where dry product in powder form deposited in cyclone is transferred. A filter (6) is provided in which the dust of dry product which is not retained in cyclone, in drying atmosphere is separated. A product chamber (7) is provided in which powder dry product separated from filter is transferred. Two high-efficiency particulate air (HEPA) filters (8) with high efficiency particle capture capacity is provided. A condenser (10) is provided in which solvent is separated from product. A gas mixer (14) is provided in which nitrogen and inert and carbon dioxide and reducing gases are mixed in certain proportions.
   USE - Reducing atmosphere spray dryer system for maintaining chemical, sensory and nutritional characteristics of products such as milk and milk products such as milk powder, protein powder, casein powder, fruit and vegetable products such as powders of fruit and vegetable juices powders of fruit and vegetable extracts, encapsulated products such as encapsulated fat, encapsulated aroma, encapsulated microorganism, encapsulated functional compound, encapsulated mineral matter, coffee powder, tea powder, cocoa powder, instant yeast powder, egg powder, enzyme, baby food, herbal extracts and pharmaceuticals.
   ADVANTAGE - The negative effects of the oxygen are minimized. The losses caused by browning reactions in biological products without the use of preservatives that adversely affect health are prevented. The low operating cost and short drying time is achieved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a reducing atmosphere spray dryer system.
   Ceding tank (1)
   Drying chamber (2)
   Sprayer or atomizer (3)
   Cyclone (4)
   Dry product chamber (5)
   Filter (6)
   Product chamber (7)
   HEPA filters (8)
   Condenser (10)
   Gas mixer (14)

PT P
PN CN110643403-A
TI Processing chemical absorption combined with biological removal of hydrogen sulfide and sulfur resource in biogas comprises e.g. absorbing hydrogen sulfide in the biogas and treating the elemental sulfur-containing mixed liquid.
AU WANG X
   ZHANG T
   DANG K
AE UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
GA 202007566B
AB    NOVELTY - Processing chemical absorption combined with biological removal of hydrogen sulfide and sulfur resource in biogas comprises (i) absorbing hydrogen sulfide in the biogas by an absorption tower and producing solution of sodium sulfide and sodium sulfide by absorbing hydrogen sulfide in biogas in an absorption tower with an alkaline absorption liquid; (ii) producing solution containing sodium sulfide and sodium sulfide by absorbing hydrogen sulfide enters the microaerobic bioreactor, utilizing microorganisms to convert sulfide to elemental sulfur and simultaneously producing carbonate by the absorption of carbon dioxide contained in biogas provides a carbon source for sulfur-oxidizing bacteria; and (iii) treating the elemental sulfur-containing mixed liquid produced by the microaerobic bioreactor with a sulfur recovery system to obtain sulfur with a higher content, returning of alkaline solution produced by the sulfur recovery system to the desulfurization absorption tower for recycling.
   USE - The method is useful for processing chemical absorption combined with biological removal of hydrogen sulfide and sulfur resource in biogas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low energy consumption and small secondary pollution and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing chemical absorption combined with biological removal of hydrogen sulfide and sulfur resource in biogas comprises (i) absorbing hydrogen sulfide in the biogas by an absorption tower and producing solution of sodium sulfide and sodium sulfide by absorbing hydrogen sulfide in biogas in an absorption tower with an alkaline absorption liquid; (ii) producing solution containing sodium sulfide and sodium sulfide by absorbing hydrogen sulfide enters the microaerobic bioreactor, utilizing microorganisms to convert sulfide to elemental sulfur and simultaneously producing carbonate by the absorption of carbon dioxide contained in the biogas provides a carbon source for sulfur-oxidizing bacteria; and (iii) treating the elemental sulfur-containing mixed liquid produced by the microaerobic bioreactor with a sulfur recovery system to obtain sulfur with a higher content, returning of alkaline solution produced by the sulfur recovery system to the desulfurization absorption tower for recycling, where the absorption tower is a packed tower or a spray tower, the microaerobic bioreactor utilizes a microaerobic activated sludge reactor and the sulfur recovery system includes a sedimentation tank and a solid-liquid separation device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the desulfurization and absorption process, the alkaline absorption liquid is sodium hydroxide, sodium bicarbonate, and/or sodium carbonate and the alkaline supernatant of the precipitation tank of the sulfur recovery system. The pH value of the micro-oxygen bioreactor is 8.5-10, the temperature is 25-38 degrees C, the residence time is 3-24 hours, the ratio of carbon, nitrogen and phosphors is 500:5:1, and the dissolved oxygen content is 0.01-0.3 mg/l. Preferred Method: The nitrogen source used by microorganisms in the micro-oxygen reactor is ammonia sludge recovery liquid produced by sludge fermentation, and the phosphorus source is dihydrogen phosphate or dihydrogen phosphate.

PT P
PN CN110404541-A
TI Iron-based catalyst useful for efficient catalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to olefins, comprises ferrite as precursor, and active component comprising iron carbide, iron(II,III) oxide and inorganic compound.
AU GUO X
   YUAN F
   ZHANG G
   SONG C
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 201994370S
AB    NOVELTY - Iron-based catalyst comprises ferrite as a precursor, and active component comprising iron carbide, iron(II,III) oxide and an inorganic compound.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for efficient catalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to olefins (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst ensures that cobalt and iron in the catalyst are not easy to lose with the generated water, has stable reaction performance and high content of the active components, and facilitates the chain growth of the intermediate species obtained by the first-step reaction to cobalt due to the relatively close distance between the two metals.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Iron-based catalyst comprises ferrite of formula: MFexOy (I) as a precursor, and active component comprising iron carbide, iron(II,III) oxide and an inorganic compound of formula: R (II).
   M = non-ferrous metals;
   x, y = related to M composition and valence state;and
   R = an oxide of M, a pure metal of M and/or an alloy of Fe-M.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the catalyst, involving (i) dissolving iron source and source (M) in a solvent, and stirring at 15-40 degrees C to obtain a solution (A), where the iron source is an inorganic iron salt or an organic iron salt, the source (M) is an inorganic salt or an organic salt of element (M), the molar ratio of iron and source (M) is 1:(1-4), and the ratio of the iron source, the source (M) and the solvent is the total amount of the iron source and the source (M) to the volume of the solvent of 1:(10-40) mol/l, (ii) dissolving sodium hydroxide in water or an organic solvent to obtain a solution (B), where the ratio of sodium hydroxide to water or an organic solvent is (0.3-0.4):1 mol/l, and (iii) dripping the solution (B) in the solution (A), where the volume ratio of the solution (A) to the solution (B) is 1:(2-4), stirring mixture at 15-40 degrees C for more than 20 minutes, reacting at 180-200 degrees C for 18-48 hours, centrifuging, washing and drying to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The iron source is ferric nitrate or ferric chloride. The source (M) is a cobalt source. The iron source is an inorganic iron salt, and the cobalt source is an inorganic cobalt salt comprising cobalt nitrate or cobalt chloride. The solvent in step (i) is water. Preferred Conditions: The stirring step (iii) is carried out for 20-30 minutes. The molar ratio of iron and cobalt in the iron source and the cobalt source is 1:(1-2). The ratio of the iron source, the cobalt source and water is the total amount of the iron source and the cobalt source to the volume of water of 1:(10-20) mol/l.
   Preferred Definitions:
   M = transition metal comprising Co;and
   R = ferrum cobalt alloy.

PT P
PN RU2700531-C1
TI METHOD OF HUMAN RECOVERY IN THE PRESENCE OF RISK FACTORS OF CARDIOVASCULAR DISEASES.
AU BADALOV N G O
   MARFINA T V
   PERSIYANOVA D A L
   MUKHINA A A
   BORODULINA I V
   TARASOVA L YU
AE NAT MEDICAL RES REHABILITATION & BALNEOL (NAME-Non-standard)
GA 2019862526
AB    NOVELTY - Invention refers to medicine, namely to medical rehabilitation, therapeutic exercises, balneology, cardiology, prevention of cardiovascular diseases (CVD) and can be used in rehabilitation centers, fitness centers, sanatoriums and outpatient clinics. Method of human recovery in the presence of CVD risk factors involves the effect of a complex of physiotherapeutic procedures, one of which is the taking of carbonic baths. Another procedure of the complex is aquatherapy, which is carried out in the form of water aerobic training of medium intensity, depth of immersion at the level of a human breast. Water temperature 2829C. Classes are 3 times a week, total 10 sessions for 45 minutes. Occupation in water includes warm-up 5 minutes, main part 35 minutes, cool down exercises 5 minutes. Warm-up consists of exercises for warm-up of body muscles in the form of free swimming with arbitrary style, walking, jumps and running in place with gradually increasing rate and amplitude of motions. Main part includes aerobic exercises in the form of walking in water, running and jumping combined with movements of upper and lower extremities in all possible for the patient planes and directions performed based on basic movements of aerobics. At continuous performance of exercises patient constantly maintains such rate and amplitude of performed movements, which allow to keep intensity of load at level of 5070% of maximum heart rate. Final period (cool down exercises) includes exercises for coordination of movements, stretching of muscles, free swimming with arbitrary style and exercises for recovery of calm breathing. After each session with aquatherapy, the procedure for taking carbon dioxide baths, artificially prepared with carbon dioxide concentration of 1.21.4 g/l, temperature of 3032C, duration of each procedure 12 minutes, total 10 procedures.
   USE - Medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - Method provides organism recovery in the form of effective reduction of CVD risk factors or manifestations of initial CVS diseases in patients of various age groups (reduced dyslipidemia, decreased body weight index, improvement of exercise tolerance with allowance for heart rate and blood pressure, increase of maximum oxygen consumption and physical activity), individualization of load directly in process of universal cardiovascular training in water, increased compliance adherence of patients to healthy lifestyle and methods of CVD prevention.

PT P
PN CN110192578-A
TI Processing soymilk powder by soaking soybeans in Bacillus solution, grinding rinsed soybeans, centrifuging, filtering, homogenizing raw soymilk, enzymatically hydrolyzing, sterilizing, deodorizing, vacuum concentrating, and spray-drying.
AU WANG D
   WANG Z
   FAN Z
   CHEN L
   LUO Y
   LI B
   SUN B
   HE J
AE HEILONGJIANG PROVINCE WILDERNESS GREEN (HEIL-Non-standard)
GA 201977873V
AB    NOVELTY - Processing soymilk powder, involves (a) soaking cleaned soybeans in Bacillus solution in dark, and rinsing the soybeans with water, (b) adding treated soybeans to ethanol solution, pouring the mixture into a grinder, heating, grinding, centrifuging slurry, adding ethanol solution to the obtained residue, secondary grinding, centrifuging, combining the secondary filtrate, and filtering to obtain raw soymilk, (c) heating the soymilk using an ultrasonic oscillator, homogenizing, subjecting the soymilk to high-speed centrifugation, (d) heating centrifuge liquid, adjusting a pH, and enzymatically hydrolyzing using an alkaline protease, (e) heating hydrolyzate, heat-preserving, pouring the cooked soymilk to a high-pressure reaction kettle, introducing a supercritical carbon dioxide, heat-preserving, controlling pressure to perform deodorization treatment, releasing pressure, and leaving still to obtain the soymilk, and (f) adjusting the pH, vacuum concentrating, and spray-drying.
   USE - The method is useful for processing soymilk powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the retention amount of nutrients of soybean, enhances the emulsifying, dispersing and solubility properties of the soymilk powder, and removes the bean flavor in the soymilk, and the obtained soymilk powder is rich in nutrition, and has excellent stability and taste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing soymilk powder, involves (a) soaking cleaned soybeans in 3-4 volume Bacillus solution in dark, and rinsing the soybeans with water for 3-5 times to obtain treated soybeans, (b) adding the treated soybeans to 2 times ethanol solution, pouring the mixture into a grinder, heating, grinding, centrifuging a slurry, adding 2 times ethanol solution to the obtained residue, secondary grinding, centrifuging, combining the secondary filtrate, and filtering to obtain raw soymilk, (c) heating the raw soymilk using an ultrasonic oscillator, homogenizing for 2-3 times, subjecting the soymilk to high-speed centrifugation, (d) heating centrifuge liquid, adjusting a pH to 9, adding an alkaline protease, and enzymatically hydrolyzing, (e) heating hydrolyzate to boiling, heat-preserving, pouring the cooked soymilk to a high-pressure reaction kettle, introducing a supercritical carbon dioxide, heating, boosting, heat-preserving, controlling pressure to perform deodorization treatment, releasing pressure, and leaving still to obtain the soymilk, and (f) adjusting the pH to 7, concentrating to 1/3 of the original volume under vacuum, and spray-drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (d) involves adding 0.5 %mass alkaline protease, enzymatically hydrolyzing at 55 degrees C for 10 minutes. The step (f) involves the concentrating at 80-85 degrees C under vacuum. Preferred Conditions: In step (a), the soaking temperature is 25-30 degrees C, and the soaking time is 10-12 hours. In step (b), the grinding temperature is 40-50 degrees C, and filtered using a 120 mesh sieve. In step (c), the ultrasonic vibration temperature is 70-80 degrees C, and oscillation time is 10-15 minutes at an oscillation interval of 5-8 minutes, and the centrifuge speed is 1000-1200 rpm, and the centrifugation time is 10-15 minutes. In step (e), the heat-preserving time is 3-5 minutes, heating is carried out at 80-100 degrees C, the pressure is increased to 15-18 MPa, the deodorization treatment is carried out for 2-3 hours, and leaving still at 70-80 degrees C for 10-20 minutes. Preferred Composition: The concentration of the ethanol solution is 20%.

TI Breathing simulator used for calibrating gas analyzer, has electronic control unit configured to pilot first and second flow regulators via first and second electronic signals for simulating different breathing regimes.
AU MORO C
   LANGIN D
   CRAMPES F
   CALMELS Y
AE INSERM INST NAT SANTE & RECH MEDICALE (INRM-C)
   UNIV TOULOUSE III SABATIER PAUL (UTOS-C)
   UNIV TOULOUSE CENT HOSPITALIER (UYTO-Non-standard)
   INSERM INST NAT SANTE & RECH MEDICALE (INRM-C)
   UNIV TOULOUSE III SABATIER PAUL (UTOS-C)
   UNIV TOULOUSE CENT HOSPITALIER (UYTO-Non-standard)
GA 2019518465
AB    NOVELTY - The breathing simulator (2) has a gas mixer (28) configured to mix the gas mixture coming from a first inlet (22) with the air coming from a second inlet (24). A first fluid line (32) connects the first inlet to the gas mixer. A second fluid line (34) connects the second inlet to the gas mixer. A third fluid line (36) connects the gas mixer to an outlet (26). A first flow regulator (42) is mounted on the first fluid line. A second flow regulator (46) and a metering pump (48) are mounted on the third fluid line. An electronic control unit (50) pilots the first and second flow regulators via first and second electronic signals (S42,S46) for simulating different breathing regimes.
   USE - Breathing simulator for calibrating a gas analyzer. Can be used for a gas analyzer designed for determining a variable rate of oxygen consumption and a variable rate of carbon dioxide production of a living subject.
   ADVANTAGE - Provides a new breathing simulator that allows efficient calibration of a gas analyzer, with the use of a little amount of a known gas mixture with a predetermined composition. Simulates different breathing regimes with relatively low flow rates, which results in low consumption of gas mixture coming out of a reservoir.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a facility for calibrating a gas analyzer;
   (2) a calibration method for use on a gas analyzer;
   (3) a determination method used to determine a variable rate of oxygen consumption and a variable rate of carbon dioxide production of a living subject; and
   (4) a diagnostic method for evaluating the energetic dependency of a patient.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a facility including the breathing simulator.

   First inlet (22)
   Second inlet (24)
   Outlet (26)
   Gas mixer (28)
   First fluid line (32)
   Second fluid line (34)
   Third fluid line (36)
   First flow regulator (42)
   Second flow regulator (46)
   Metering pump (48)
   Electronic control unit (50)
   First and second electronic signals (S42,S46)

PN CN109734689-A
TI Preparation of furandicarboxylic acid using halogenated cuprous by taking 5-bromofuran-2-methyl carboxylate, copper powder, halogenated cuprous and organic alkali, stirring, filtering and pouring in diluted hydrochloric acid solution.
AU ZHONG Z
   ZHANG J
   SUN D
AE UNIV HENAN CHINESE MEDICINE (HECO-C)
GA 2019451756
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of furandicarboxylic acid (I) using halogenated cuprous involves adding 5-bromofuran-2-methyl carboxylate, copper powder, halogenated cuprous and organic alkali into sealed container containing organic solvent, filling carbon dioxide, keeping pressure, stirring, completely reacting, filtering, pouring filtrate in diluted hydrochloric acid solution, extracting by ethyl acetate, vacuum concentrating organic phase, adding sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, stirring, cooling, adjusting pH by hydrochloric acid to 1, separating out solid, and filtering to obtain 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid. The halogenated cuprous is cuprous chloride, cuprous bromide or cuprous iodide. The organic solvent is N,N'-dimethylformamide, N-methylpyrrolidone or dimethylsulfoxide. The organic base is 2,2'-bipyridyl, 1,10-phenanthroline, N,N,N',N'-tetramethylethylenediamine or N,N'-dimethylethylenediamine.
   USE - The method is used for furandicarboxylic acid using halogenated cuprous preparation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, energy-saving and suitable for large scale industrial production, and has easy operation, environment protection and low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of furandicarboxylic acid of formula (I) using halogenated cuprous involves adding 5-bromofuran-2-methyl carboxylate, copper powder, halogenated cuprous and organic alkali into sealed container containing organic solvent at room temperature, filling carbon dioxide to sealed container, keeping pressure at 1-3 atm, stirring at 100 degrees C for 4-6 hours, completely reacting, filtering reaction solution, pouring filtrate in 1 N diluted hydrochloric acid solution, extracting by ethyl acetate to obtain organic phase, vacuum concentrating organic phase to obtain concentrated liquid, adding 5 wt.% sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, stirring to 60 degrees C for 1 hour, cooling, adjusting pH by hydrochloric acid to 1, separating out solid, and filtering to obtain 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid. The halogenated cuprous is cuprous bromide, cuprous iodide or cuprous chloride. The organic solvent is dimethylsulfoxide, N-methylpyrrolidone or N,N'-dimethylformamide. The organic alkali is N,N,N',N'-tetramethylethylenediamine, N,N'-dimethylethylenediamine, 2,2'-bipyridyl or 1,10-phenanthroline. The amount ratio of 5-bromofuran-2-methyl carboxylate, halogenated cuprous, copper powder and organic base is 1:0.1-0.3:1-3:0.1-0.3. The weight ratio of 5-bromofuran-2-methyl carboxylate to solvent is 1:10-30.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves adding 20.3 g 5-bromofuran-2-methyl carboxylate, 9 g copper powder, 2.8 g cuprous bromide and 2.4 g N,N,N',N'-tetramethylethylenediamine into sealed container containing 200 ml dimethylsulfoxide, dissolving mixture in dimethylsulfoxide, filling carbon dioxide to sealed container, keeping pressure at 1-3 atm, stirring at 100 degrees C for 6 hours, completely reacting, filtering reaction solution, pouring filtrate in 1000 ml of 1 N diluted hydrochloric acid solution, extracting by 300 ml ethyl acetate to obtain organic phase, combining organic phases, vacuum concentrating organic phase to obtain concentrated liquid, adding 50 ml of 5 wt.% sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, stirring to 60 degrees C for 1 hour, cooling, adjusting pH by hydrochloric acid to not less than 1, separating out solid, and filtering to obtain 15 g 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid.

PN CN109602803-A
TI Improving limonin content in preparing granules useful for reducing uric acid level comprises crushing pine leaves, radix puerariae and dried orange peel, sieving, extracting, absorbing with methanol, eluting, concentrating, and drying.
AU RUAN X
   WANG Q
   YANG L
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG NINGBO TECHNOLOGY INST (NBLG-C)
GA 2019365394
AB    NOVELTY - Improving limonin content in preparing granules comprises crushing pine leaves, radix puerariae and dried orange peel, sieving to obtain mixed traditional Chinese medicine raw material powder, and packing into a supercritical extraction kettle, extracting in the supercritical extraction kettle under carbon dioxide fluid pressure, absorbing the extract as absorption liquid with methanol, where cosolvent comprises ethanol, cyclohexane and water, analyzing absorption liquid by D101 type macroporous resin, eluting distilled water of two bed column volumes per hour until the effluent is colourless, washing the column with ethanol, concentrating ethanol eluent under reduced pressure to near dryness, and drying to obtain light yellow extract.
   USE - The granules is useful for reducing uric acid level.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, ensures sufficient extraction of limonin, and extract has strong ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improving limonin content in preparing granules comprises (a) crushing pine leaves, radix puerariae and dried orange peel in a mass ratio of 4.5-4.75:4.5-4.75:0.5-1, sieving by 45-60 mesh sieve to obtain mixed traditional Chinese medicine raw material powder, and packing into a supercritical extraction kettle, (b) extracting in the supercritical extraction kettle at 35-55 degrees C under carbon dioxide fluid pressure of 30-60 MPa at a flow speed of cosolvent of 0.1-0.6 ml/minute for 2-4 hours, absorbing the extract as absorption liquid with methanol, where the volume-mass ratio of methanol to traditional Chinese medicine raw material powder is 1 ml:2 g, and cosolvent comprises ethanol, cyclohexane and water in a mass ratio of 7:1:2, and (c) analyzing absorption liquid by D101 type macroporous resin, eluting distilled water of two bed column volumes per hour until the effluent is colourless, washing the column with 10 column volume of 60% ethanol, concentrating ethanol eluent under reduced pressure of 450-500 Pa to near dryness, and drying at 105 degrees C for 24 hours to obtain light yellow extract composed of pine leaves and radix puerariae for lowering uric acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The extraction process in step (b) is carried out at 38 degrees C under carbon dioxide fluid pressure of 52 MPa, and cosolvent ethanol flow rate of 0.51 ml/minute for 2 hours.

PN CN109307726-A; CN109307726-B
TI Improving special impurity detection sensitivity of high purity gas comprises improving special impurity detection sensitivity of high purity gas by using carbon monoxide in a high-purity gas.
AU LI J
   CHEN G
   ZHANG X
   XIA T
   ZHANG G
   YANG J
   XU J
   HUA Y
   CHEN L
AE ZHEJIANG BORUI ELECTRONIC TECHNOLOGY CO (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 201916041A
AB    NOVELTY - Improving special impurity detection sensitivity of high purity gas comprises improving special impurity detection sensitivity of high purity gas by using carbon monoxide in a high-purity gas, where the catalytic conversion of special impurity is carbon dioxide is achieved by methane gas, pretreating the gas before the gas injection analysis, passing 0.1-1 pts. wt. high purity gas from the valve through the mass flow meter outlet, adsorbing the catalytic well, discharging through a three-way valve, adsorbing the impurities in the adsorption catalytic well, keeping the adsorption catalytic well is in a low temperature cold well, removing the cold well, inserting into the heater, performing catalytic hydrogenation, introducing 0.05-0.5 pts. wt. high purity hydrogen, carrying out catalysis at 100-200 degrees C for 0.1-1 hour, hitting the three-way valve to the detection position, and sending the gas in the adsorption catalytic well into the chromatograph detector for analysis.
   USE - The method is useful for improving special impurity detection sensitivity of high purity gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves analysis sensitivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improving special impurity detection sensitivity of high purity gas comprises improving special impurity detection sensitivity of high purity gas by using carbon monoxide in a high-purity gas, where the catalytic conversion of special impurity is carbon dioxide is achieved by methane gas, pretreating the gas before the gas injection analysis, passing 0.1-1 pts. wt. high purity gas from the valve through the mass flow meter outlet, adsorbing the catalytic well, discharging through a three-way valve, adsorbing the impurities in the adsorption catalytic well, keeping the adsorption catalytic well is in a low temperature cold well, removing the cold well, inserting into the heater, performing catalytic hydrogenation, introducing 0.05-0.5 pts. wt. high purity hydrogen, carrying out catalysis at 100-200 degrees C for 0.1-1 hour, at 300-400 degrees C for 0.5-1 hours, at 400-500 degrees C for 0.1-0.5 hours, hitting the three-way valve to the detection position, and sending the gas in the adsorption catalytic well into the chromatograph detector for analysis, where the adsorption catalytic well contains 10-20 pts. wt. carbon oxide adsorption hydrogenation catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon oxide hydrogenation catalyst prepared by adding 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. zirconium oxychloride octahydrate, 0.002-0.08 pts. wt. tris(2,2,6,6-tetramethyl-3,5-pimelic acid) europium, 0.005-0.05 pts. wt. tris(bis(trimethylsilyl)amino) lanthanum, 0.1-0.5 pts. wt. cobalt chloride, 0.3-1.2 pts. wt. nickel nitrate and 0.01-0.08 pts. wt. diisobutylaluminum acetylacetonate complex added into 20-30 pts. wt. deionized water, stirring uniformly, 0.5-2 pts. wt. 0.05-0.1 mol/l sulfuric acid is added, heating at 70-90 degrees C, adding 0.6-3 pts. wt. ammonia into the reaction system under stirring, reacting for 120-200 minutes, adding 0.01-0.6 pts. wt. propylene oxide, heating to 90-100 degrees C for 10-40 minutes, cooling, grinding and sintering at 300-350 degrees C for 60-120 minutes, immersing the sintered material in a 0.5-1.5 mol/l aminophenol solution for 8-12 hours, taking out, drying and sintering at 500-800 degrees C for 120-180 minutes. The low temperature cold well is liquid nitrogen cold well, and the temperature is 60-120 degrees C. Preferred Components: The carrier gas is high purity hydrogen or high purity nitrogen or high purity helium. The chromatograph detector is a thermal conductivity detector or a hydrogen flame ionization detector or electron capture detector.
