PN CN109233157-A
TI Thermally conductive fluororubber comprises e.g. fluororubber, magnesium oxide, calcium hydroxide, microcrystalline wax, accelerator, vulcanizing agent and modified thermally conductive filler.
AU CHEN J
   LIU X
   ZHU D
AE CHEN J (CHEN-Individual)
GA 2019133015
AB    NOVELTY - Thermally conductive fluororubber comprises 100-120 pts. wt. fluororubber, 2-8 pts. wt. magnesium oxide, 5-10 pts. wt. calcium hydroxide, 1-3 pts. wt. microcrystalline wax, 3-5 pts. wt. accelerator, 1-2 pts. wt. vulcanizing agent and 30-40 pts. wt. modified thermally conductive filler. The modified thermally conductive filler is prepared by e.g. mixing the aqueous graphene oxide solution with the aqueous solution of the carboxylated carbon nanotubes at mass ratio of 3:1-4:1, adjusting the pH to 8-9 to obtain graphene oxide-carboxy carbon nanotube mixture, taking the graphene oxide-carboxy carbon nanotube mixture and the polyethene polyamine, and mixing at a mass ratio of 5:1-6:1, ultrasonically dispersing, continuously adding 0.05-0.12 times of aqueous solution of glucose beta -lactone into the graphene oxide-carboxy carbon nanotube mixture, washing the gel and freeze-drying, mixing the modified thermally conductive filler blank with the alloy in a carbon dioxide atmosphere.
   USE - Used as thermally conductive fluororubber.
   ADVANTAGE - The fluororubber has excellent thermal conductivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Thermally conductive fluororubber comprises 100-120 pts. wt. fluororubber, 2-8 pts. wt. magnesium oxide, 5-10 pts. wt. calcium hydroxide, 1-3 pts. wt. microcrystalline wax, 3-5 pts. wt. accelerator, 1-2 pts. wt. vulcanizing agent and 30-40 pts. wt. modified thermally conductive filler. The modified thermally conductive filler is prepared by mixing the aqueous graphene oxide solution with the aqueous solution of the carboxylated carbon nanotubes at mass ratio of 3:1-4:1, adjusting the pH to 8-9 to obtain graphene oxide-carboxy carbon nanotube mixture, taking the graphene oxide-carboxy carbon nanotube mixture and the polyethene polyamine, and mixing at a mass ratio of 5:1-6:1, ultrasonically dispersing, continuously adding 0.05-0.12 times of aqueous solution of glucose beta -lactone into the graphene oxide-carboxy carbon nanotube mixture, allowing to stand to obtain gel, washing the gel and freeze-drying to obtain modified thermal conductive filler blank, mixing the modified thermally conductive filler blank with the alloy in a carbon dioxide atmosphere at mass ratio of 1:1-1:3, stirring and mixing, freeze-pulverizing and sieving the mixture to obtain modified thermally conductive filler.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The accelerator is either or both of bisphenol A.F and benzyltriphenylphosphonium chloride. The vulcanizing agent is dibenzoyl peroxide, hydroquinone and/or bis-(4-aminohexylcyclohexyl)methane carbamate. The polyethene polyamine is diethylenetriamine, triethylenetetramine or ethylenediamine. The alloy is lead-tin alloy in which the amount of bismuth is 29 wt.%, the amount of lead is 43 wt.%, and the amount of tin is 28 wt.%.Preferred Method: The method comprises mixing aqueous graphene oxide solution, graphene oxide and water in a mass ratio of 1:150-1:200, and ultrasonically dispersing to obtain aqueous graphene oxide solution. The method comprises where the aqueous solution of the carbon nanotubes is mixture of multi-walled carbon nanotubes and nitric acid having amount of 60-65 wt.% at mass ratio of 1:5, adding 2-3 times of nitric acid, 78-90 wt.% sulfuric acid, stirring and mixing, filtering, washing until the washing liquid is neutral to obtain carboxy group carbon nanotube, mixing the carboxy carbon nanotubes with water at mass ratio of 1:120-1:200, and ultrasonically dispersing to obtain aqueous solution of carboxylated carbon nanotube.

PN CN109108049-A; CN109108049-B
TI Conversion of sodium and fluorine-containing compounds in aluminum electrolytic hazardous waste residue includes crushing aluminum electrolysis dangerous waste residues, sieving, analyzing, determining, mixing, washing, calcining and drying.
AU LIU E
   SHAO X
AE UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
GA 201904033B
AB    NOVELTY - Conversion of sodium and fluorine-containing compounds in aluminum electrolytic hazardous waste residue includes crushing aluminum electrolysis dangerous waste residues, sieving, homogenizing, analyzing, determining dangerous waste residues, adding grinding body, grinding, adding metered dangerous waste slag powder, grinding, mixing conversion control dangerous waste residue powder and grinding body, adding water, mixing dangerous waste residues powder and water, converting dangerous waste residues containing sodium powder in fluorine-containing compound, adding transforming agent and grinding additive, milling, breaking gel, performing aging treatment, performing solid-liquid separation, washing, performing heat treatment, crushing to obtain fluorine-containing minerals and carbon mixed powder material, calcining, crystallizing, converting, adding cyanide conversion agent, drying, collecting reducing gasses and performing direct oxidation combustion.
   USE - Method of converting sodium and fluorine-containing compounds in aluminum electrolytic hazardous waste residue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple process, easy mass production, low production cost and no three-waste pollution and is environment friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Conversion of sodium and fluorine-containing compounds in aluminum electrolytic hazardous waste residue comprises crushing aluminum electrolysis dangerous waste residues, sieving, homogenizing to obtain 200 mu m dangerous waste slag powder, analyzing, determining dangerous waste residues, adding grinding body to conversion mill, grinding, adding metered dangerous waste slag powder, grinding, mixing conversion control dangerous waste residue powder and grinding body at 1:0.2-10, adding water, mixing dangerous waste residues powder and water at 1:1-10, converting dangerous waste residues containing sodium powder in fluorine-containing compound, adding transforming agent and grinding additive, milling at 10-1000 revolutions per minute, breaking gel, performing aging treatment at 60-150 degrees c for 0.5-5 hours, performing solid-liquid separation, washing, performing heat treatment for 1-10 hours, crushing to obtain fluorine-containing minerals and carbon mixed powder material, calcining fluorine-containing minerals and carbon mixed powder material in air atmosphere at 700-1200 degrees C for 0.5-5 hours, crystallizing to obtain concentrated solution, converting dangerous waste residues powder carbon-nitrogen ion into nitrogen, ammonia and carbon dioxide, adding cyanide conversion agent, converting cyanide-containing compound oxide converted into harmless nitrogen, ammonia and carbon dioxide, drying, collecting reducing gasses and performing direct oxidation combustion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sodium-containing compound is sodium fluoride, sodium hexafluoroaluminate, sodium cyanide or sodium ferrocyanide. The fluorine-containing compound is sodium fluoirde, sodium hexafluoroaluminate, aluminum fluoride, lithium fluoride, calcium fluoride, magnesium fluoride or potassium fluoride. The cyanide conversion agent is sodium carbonate pentahydrate, potassium carbonate pentahydrate, sodium oxide, potassium oxide, calcium oxide, strontium oxide, barium oxide, hydrogen peroxide, ammonium persulfate, sodium persulfate, potassium persulfate, potassium permanganate, oxygen or ozone. The grinding additive is polymeric polyol, polymeric alcohol amine, triisopropanolamine, ethylene glycol, propylene glycol, glycerol, diethylene glycol, triethanolamine, amide, stearic acid, oleic acid, sodium hexametaphosphate or sodium stearate.

GA 2018943318
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing coal water slurry by using various coal conversion wastewater comprises e.g. mixing washing water, desulfurization and poor liquid generated during coal conversion, sulfur water and carbonized wastewater and stirring, the mixing ratio of various wastewaters satisfies the mass ratio: washing water: desulfurization poor liquid: sulfur water: carbonized wastewater is 76-85:5-8:5-8:5-8, the washing water is gas washing, and organic wastewater generated during condensation, the desulfurized lean liquid is a waste liquid remaining after the desulfurizing agent reacts with the flue gas, the sulfur water is industrial wastewater obtained by sulfur pressure filtration washing, the carbonized wastewater is wastewater generated by the reaction of carbon dioxide and ammonia to produce ammonium bicarbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing coal water slurry by using various coal conversion wastewater.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing coal water slurry by using various coal conversion wastewater comprises (1) mixing washing water, desulfurization and poor liquid generated during coal conversion, sulfur water and carbonized wastewater and stirring, the mixing ratio of various wastewaters satisfies the mass ratio: washing water: desulfurization poor liquid: sulfur water: carbonized wastewater is 76-85:5-8:5-8:5-8, the washing water is gas washing, and organic wastewater generated during condensation, the desulfurized lean liquid is a waste liquid remaining after the desulfurizing agent reacts with the flue gas, the sulfur water is industrial wastewater obtained by sulfur pressure filtration washing, the carbonized wastewater is wastewater generated by the reaction of carbon dioxide and ammonia to produce ammonium bicarbonate, (2) using step (1) adding a complex alkali as a conditioning agent to the mixed wastewater, the amount of the complex alkali added is 0.4-1.0 wt.% the total mass of the wastewater, (3) treating wastewater with coal, adding additives to the rod mill, preparing wastewater water coal slurry, adding the waste water to the rod mill, the mass ratio of the additive is compound type wastewater coal water slurry additive made of lignosulfonate, polynaphthalene sulfonate, xanthan gum with ratio of 40-50:49.7-59.5:0.30.5, preferably 6:6:1.

PN CN108502903-A; CN108502903-B
TI Slag fluidized bed gasification system comprises fluidized bed gasification furnace, where top of fluidized bed gasification furnace is connected with cyclone separator, and primary separator is connected with secondary cyclone separator.
AU ZUO W
   ZHU H
   ZHU C
   CUI L
   WU Q
AE JIANGSU PROVINCIAL ACAD ENVIRONMENTAL SC (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201872399P
AB    NOVELTY - Slag fluidized bed gasification system comprises fluidized bed gasification furnace (2), where top of fluidized bed gasification furnace is connected with cyclone separator. The outlet of primary cyclone separator (3) is connected with secondary cyclone separator (4). The secondary cyclone separator is connected with heat exchanger (5) in fluidized bed gasification furnace which is set at bottom of fluidized air distributor (6). The inlet of fluidized air distributor is connected with heat exchanger. The lower portion of fluidized air distributor is provided with slag are respectively provided with feeder. The outlet material is on two sides of fluidized bed gasification furnace. The discharging port is connected with spiral water cooling device. The spiral water cooler is connected with crusher.
   USE - Slag fluidized bed gasification system.
   ADVANTAGE - The slag fluidized bed gasification system is cost effective and eco-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing salt product, which involves:
   (A) placing pre-concentrating salt slag through feeder into fluidized bed gasification furnace to realize primary separation of pre-concentrated salt residue, placing organic substance and salt in water and carbon dioxide into primary cyclone separator from top of fluidized bed gasification furnace with fluidized air, the fluidizingprimary dust enters the two-stage cyclone separator.then purifying and dust after waste heat exchanger, recycling fluidized bed gasification furnace, separating fly ash into incinerator, cooling gasification salt residue in fluidized bed gasification furnace by overflow of water which enters spiral cooler;
   (B) placing gasification salt residue in spiral water cooling device, crushing by crusher into blender, adding water to salt gasification residue which enters filter, dissolving, separating carbon dioxide and salt solution through filtering, buring filter residue into burning furnace, evaporating salt solution into crystallizing evaporator; and
   (C) crystallizing salt solution in evaporator by decompressing, evaporating and dissolving salt residue to stirrer to remove water to obtain salt product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of slag fluidized bed gasification system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Fluidized bed gasification furnace (2)
   Primary cyclone separator (3)
   Secondary cyclone separator (4)
   Heat exchanger (5)
   Air distributor (6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRONICS - Preferred Conditions: The fluid temperature of heat exchanger outlet is controlled at 350-650 degrees C. The ratio of spiral cooler of gasification salt slag with water is 1:2.5-5. Preferred Compositions: The content of pre-concentrated salt residue is 15-35%.

TI Ruthenium monoatomic catalyst used for catalytic            preparation of formate-base complex, comprises            nitrogen-containing chelate-type covalent organic            polymer material as carrier, and active component noble            metal ruthenium.
AU SHAO X
   GE H
   LI Z
   ZHAO Z
   WANG J
AE UNIV SHAANXI TECHNOLOGY (SHTH-C)
GA 201868317M
AB    NOVELTY - Ruthenium monoatomic catalyst comprises                nitrogen-containing chelate-type covalent organic                polymer material as a carrier, active component                noble metal ruthenium supported on the support, a                load of ruthenium in the catalyst amount is 0.5-3.0                wt.%.
   USE - Ruthenium monoatomic catalyst used for the                catalytic preparation of a formate-base complex                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The ruthenium monoatomic catalyst is easy to                prepare, has high yield, selectivity of 100% under                mild conditions, and repeatedly recycled.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for                following:
   (1) a method for preparing ruthenium                monoatomic catalyst, which involves adding pyridine                diformaldehyde and the triamino compound to an                organic solvent under a nitrogen atmosphere,                raising temperature to 160 degrees C, cooling to                room temperature, filtering reaction product to a                solid powder, washing solid powder, and drying to                obtain nitrogen-containing chelate type covalent                organic polymer material, where mass ratio of the                pyridine diformaldehyde, triamino compound, and                organic solvent is 0.405 g:(0.579-0.704)g:85 mL,                placing nitrogen-containing chelate-type covalent                organic polymer material in an alcohol solution,                adding ruthenium precursor solution, ultrasonically                shaking for 20-30 minutes, heating, and refluxing                for 6-12 hours, reacting the concentration is 1 M.                /L NaBH4 solution for 2-3 hours, cooling reaction                product; and washing, and drying obtain ruthenium                monoatomic catalyst of 0.5-3.0 wt.% metal ruthenium                and the balance nitrogen-containing chelate type                covalent organic polymer material, where mass ratio                of the organic polymer material, the alcohol                solution and the NaBH4 is 0.5 g:100 ml:10 mL;                and
   (2) a method for using ruthenium monoatomic                catalyst, which involves adding a monoatomic                ruthenium catalyst, water, alkali, carbon dioxide,                hydrogen to the autoclave at 2-8 MPa, carrying                reaction at 80-160 degrees C for 12-48 hours to                obtain a formic acid-base complex, where molar                concentration of the base in the reaction system is                1-4 mol / L;, catalyst is used is 0.1-20 wt.%,                adding base, and ratio of hydrogen to carbon                dioxide is (1-3):1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pyridine                  dialdehyde is 2,6-pyridinedialdehyde. The                  triamino compound is                  1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenyl)benzene, One of                  1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenyl)triazine,                  tris(4-aminophenyl)amine, and                  tris(4-aminophenyl)methane. The organic solvent                  is N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethyl One of                  acetamide, dimethyl sulfoxide and                  N-methylpyrrolidone. The base is sodium                  hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, triethylamine,                  sodium hydrogencarbonate, potassium                  hydrogencarbonate and ammonium hydrogencarbonate.                  Preferred Conditions: The temperature rise for                  2-4 hours at 80-120 degrees C, and 160 degrees C                  for 24 hours. The reaction product is filtered to                  removed solid powder by washing with a solvent,                  and the solid powder is washed with ethanol and                  dried under vacuum at a temperature of 80 degrees                  C for 12 hours. The alcohol is one of methanol,                  ethanol, n-propanol and isopropanol, precursor                  solution is a H2IrCl6 solution, washing is washed                  with water and ethanol, respectively, and the                  drying is vacuum dried at a temperature of 80                  degrees C for 12 hours.

PN CN108102750-A; CN108102750-B
TI Method for producing natural gas from synthetic gas, involves separating crude SNG product gas into SNG product gas, and returning methane desorption gas to top of first methanation reactor or to inlet of pre-heater.
AU GONG W
   YAN B
   LV J
   HOU N
   COLLODI G
AE WISON ENG CHINA CO LTD (WISO-Non-standard)
   FOSTER WHEELER ENERGIA OY (FOSX-C)
   WISON ENG CHINA CO LTD (WISO-Non-standard)
GA 201844984V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves desulfurizing a raw material synthesis gas (1) by a desulfurization device (12) to obtain a desulfurization gas. The desulfurized gas (19) is divided into two shares after heat exchange by a pre-heater (15). The first desulfurized gas (20) is passed into a top of a first methanation reactor (2). A second desulfurized gas (21) is passed into a top of a second methanation reactor (4). The product gas at a bottom of an N-methanation reactor enters a gas-liquid separation tank (10) after heat exchange through a waste heat recovery device. A process condensate (16) obtained at bottom of gas-liquid separation tank is sent out of bounds. The crude substitute natural gas (SNG) product gas (17) obtained at a top of gas-liquid separation tank is introduced into a carbon dioxide removal device (13). The crude SNG product gas is separated into SNG product gas (25). The methane desorption gas (14) is returned to a top of first methanation reactor or to an inlet of pre-heater.
   USE - Method for producing natural gas from synthetic gas used in power generation, chemical industry, city gas, automobile fuel and other industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The temperature rise of the methanation reactor is controlled, and the circulating gas volume of the recycle gas compressor is greatly reduced, so as to significantly reduce system energy consumption and equipment investment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The first product gas is passed into a top of a specific methanation reactor. The second product gas is compressed by a recycle gas compressor (11) and passed to a top of the first methanation reactor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating the method for producing natural gas from synthetic gas.
   Raw material synthesis gas (1)
   First methanation reactor (2)
   Second methanation reactor (4)
   Gas-liquid separation tank (10)
   Carbon dioxide removal device (13)
   Methane desorption gas (14)
   Pre-heater (15)
   Process condensate (16)
   Crude SNG product gas (17)
   Desulfurized gas (19)
   First desulfurized gas (20)

   SNG product gas (25)

TI High-strength regenerated concrete porous brick comprises modified waste aggregates, fly ash, cement, water, additive, and filler.
AU XU Y
   CHEN L
AE CHANGZHOU JIEXUAN TEXTILE TECHNOLOGY CO (CHAN-Non-standard)
   QINGHAI PINGXING CONSTR GROUP CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
   QINGHAI BAOHENG GREEN BUILDING IND CO (QING-Non-standard)
GA 201823902J
AB    NOVELTY - High-strength regenerated concrete porous brick comprises 120-180 pts. wt. modified waste aggregates, 15-25 pts. wt. fly ash, 35-50 pts. wt. cement, 12-18 pts. wt. water, 5-8 pts. wt. additive, and 8-12 pts. wt. filler. The high-strength regenerated concrete porous brick is prepared by e.g. (i) mixing the crushed brick material with crushed concrete, crushing, and sieving, mixing the refined material with water, and carbonizing in a carbon dioxide atmosphere, freezing and drying, mixing the pretreatment mixture with ethyl orthosilicate, stirring and mixing, and (ii) naturally curing.
   USE - Used as high-strength regenerated concrete porous brick.
   ADVANTAGE - The porous brick has higher compressive strength and water resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High-strength regenerated concrete porous brick comprises 120-180 pts. wt. modified waste aggregates, 15-25 pts. wt. fly ash, 35-50 pts. wt. cement, 12-18 pts. wt. water, 5-8 pts. wt. additive, and 8-12 pts. wt. filler. The high-strength regenerated concrete porous brick is prepared by (i) mixing the crushed brick material with crushed concrete, crushing in a crusher, and sieving to obtain refined mixture, mixing the refined material with water, and carbonizing in a carbon dioxide atmosphere, freezing and drying to obtain pre-mixed material, mixing the pretreatment mixture with ethyl orthosilicate, under the atmosphere of trimethyl aluminum, sealing, reacting at constant temperature and constant pressure, cooling, and filtering to obtain filter cake to obtain modified waste aggregate, where the additive is prepared by mixing chitosan with water, swelling, water and adding 40-50 times chitosan, adding 0.5-0.7 times of chitosan glutaraldehyde, stirring and mixing to obtain additive, where the high-strength regenerated concrete porous brick is prepared by taking raw materials, and (ii) taking the modified waste aggregate, fly ash, cement and filler into stirrer for stirring, adding water and additive into a stirrer, continuously stirring to obtain blank, placing the blank into the mold, vibration molding in the brick molding machine, allowing to stand, de-molding to obtain pre-brick, taking the pre-brick, artificially maintaining and then naturally curing, natural maintaining to obtain high-strength regenerated concrete porous brick.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cement is Portland cement, aluminate cement or sulfoaluminate cement. The filler is glass fiber, ceramic fiber or metal fiber.

TI Cementitious composition used for forming alkali-activated concrete material for concrete structure in construction application, comprises binder comprising ground granulated blast furnace slag and clay filter cake, and alkaline activator.
AU SHUBBAR A A
   SADIQUE M
   ABDULHUSSEIN SHUBBAR A
AE UNIV LIVERPOOL MOORES JOHN (UYLI-Non-standard)
GA 2021A6385F
AB    NOVELTY - A cementitious composition comprises binder and an alkaline activator. The binder comprises ground granulated blast furnace slag and clay filter cake.
   USE - Cementitious composition used for forming alkali-activated concrete material for concrete structure in construction application (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition has reduced carbon dioxide emissions. The alkali-activated concrete material has acceptable mechanical properties, high compressive strength, desirable flexural strength, low chloride ion penetrability and/or low water absorption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) formation of the alkali-activated concrete material, which involves mixing aggregate, binder and an alkaline activator to produce concrete mix, and curing the concrete mix; and
   (2) alkali-activated concrete material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composition comprises an aggregate. The aggregate comprises sand, gravel, limestone, sandstone, chalk, and/or soil. The alkaline activator comprises sodium silicate and/or sodium hydroxide in a weight ratio of (1:2)-(3:1).Preferred Process: The method involves curing the concrete mix in the presence of air and/or water at a temperature of 50degrees Celsius, preferably 18-25degrees Celsius. The method further involves forming the concrete mix into a concrete structure.Preferred Composition: The binder comprises 30-70 wt.% ground granulated blast furnace slag, and 30-70 wt.% clay filter cake. The clay filter cake comprises 1-60 wt.% calcium oxide and/or 10-80 wt.% silicon dioxide. The ground granulated blast furnace slag comprises 10-70 wt.% calcium oxide and/or 10-70 wt.% silicon dioxide.Preferred Properties: The weight ratio of alkaline activator to binder is (1:10)-(2:1). The alkali-activated concrete material has a compressive strength of at least 10 MPa as measured by compression testing.

   USE - Ruthenium-copper chromate photothermal catalytic system.
   ADVANTAGE - The ruthenium-copper chromate photothermal catalytic system effectively converts carbon dioxide to methane, and has methanogenesis efficiency of 10000 times more than pure ruthenium under condensed sunlight irradiation without separate heat supply.

   (B) mixing and bonding the copper chromium oxide and the ruthenium precursor in an organic solvent; and
   (C) bonding under heat treatment to prepare the ruthenium-copper chromium oxide photothermal catalyst.

The copper chromium trioxide is deposited on a glass substrate. The light irradiation is condensed sunlight irradiation.

TI Preparing copper-based catalyst, comprises e.g. mixing porous carrier with sulfur-containing anionic organic ligand solution and copper salt solution, calcining obtained precipitate, oxidizing, and performing electrochemical treatment.
AU SHI Y
   WANG M
   ZHANG B
   HE Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 202092241B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing copper-based catalyst, comprises (i) adding porous carrier into sulfur-containing anionic organic ligand solution, stirring and mixing, then adding copper salt solution, and mixing to obtain suspension, (ii) centrifuging, washing, and drying the suspension of step (i) to obtain precipitate, (iii) calcining precipitate in step (ii) under nitrogen atmosphere, and then oxidizing under air atmosphere, and (iv) performing electrochemical treatment using oxidized precipitate as working electrode to obtain copper-based catalyst.
   USE - The copper-based catalyst is useful in electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The copper-based catalyst: has high catalytic activity; exhibits excellent electrocatalytic stability; and has no decrease in electrocatalytic performance even after 20 hours of electrolysis.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sulfur-containing anionic organic ligand includes red acid, thiourea and/or methionine. The porous carrier includes carbon nanotubes, graphene aerogel, activated carbon fibers and/or mesoporous carbon. The concentration of sulfur-containing anionic organic ligand in sulfur-containing anion organic ligand solution is 1-6 mol/l, and concentration of copper salt in copper salt solution is 1-6 mol/l. The molar ratio between sulfur-containing anion organic ligand and copper salt is 1:0.8-1.2. The molar ratio between porous carrier and copper salt is 2-10 g:1 mol. The specific surface area of copper-based catalyst is 300-400 m2/g. Preferred Method: In step (ii), the calcining temperature is 600-800 degrees C, and the calcining time is 2-5 hours. In step (iii), the oxidation temperature is 180-220 degrees C, and the oxidation time is 3-5 hours. In step (iv), the electric potential of electrochemical treatment is -0.57 to -0.87 V, and time of electrochemical treatment is not less than 1 hour.

PN CN111593371-A; CN111593371-B
TI Preparing municipal sludge multi-grade carbon materials comprises taking municipal sludge, hydrothermally reacting , cooling, washing, drying under protection of inert gas, washing with water, drying and grinding.
AU YUAN H
   DENG L
   QIAN X
   WANG L
AE GUANGZHOU ENERGY INST CONVERSION CHINESE (CAEC-C)
   SOUTHERN MARINE SCI & ENG GUANGDONG LAB (SMAR-Non-standard)
GA 202085752M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing municipal sludge multi-grade carbon materials comprises (i) taking municipal sludge as raw material, hydrothermally reacting at 150 degrees C for 24 hours, cooling and washing until the solution is neutral and drying at 60 degrees C, (ii) drying obtained solid under protection of inert gas, pyrolyzing at 900 degrees C for 2 hours to obtain a solid product, and (iii) cooling solid product, acid washing, and washing with water until it is neutral, drying and grinding.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing municipal sludge mu.lti-grade carbon materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of municipal sludge multi-stage carbon material for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The surface of the 1 municipal sludge multi-stage carbon material is provided with carbon quantum dots. Preferred Method: The specific steps of step (iii), comprises and acid washed with 6 mol/l hydrochloric acid for 24 hours, solid product after pyrolysis is cooled, washing with water until it is neutral, drying at 80 degrees C, and ground to a size of 100-150 mesh to obtain municipal multi-grade carbon materials for sludge. The use method comprises (i) dispersing municipal sludge multi-grade carbon material to a volume ratio of 100:1 in mixture of ethanol and Nafion (RTM: Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer) solution, ultrasonic treating, preparing into a mixed liquid containing multi-grade carbon materials from municipal sludge, subsequently, mixed liquid containing municipal sludge multi-grade carbon material is evenly coated on graphite plate to make working electrode; (ii) assembling working electrode into a three-electrode system. In the three-electrode system, use 0.1-1.0M potassium bicarbonate as the electrolyte, silver/silver chloride electrode as the reference electrode, and titanium wire as the auxiliary electrode,(3) in three-electrode system, an external voltage of 1.1V to -1.5V is applied to perform electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide. In the step (iii), the applied voltage is -1.2 V to -1.4 V. In the step (iii), the reduction products after the electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide comprises methane, methanol and ethanol.

AU NA Y H
AE URBANMINER INC (URBA-Non-standard)
GA 202062388T
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a coffee activated carbon comprises (a) preparing a coffee by-product in powder form, (b) placing 1 kg powder into reaction chamber of hydrothermal synthesizer, performing hydrothermal reaction at 220-380 degrees C for 8-36 hours to obtain coffee by-product, (c) removing gas remaining in the reaction chamber of the hydrothermal synthesizer using a vacuum pump, and (d) injecting a mixed gas of inert gas, carbon dioxide, and water vapor into the reaction chamber of the hydrothermal synthesizer, performing hydrothermal reaction at 200-300 degrees C for 6-12 hours to produce coffee activated carbon.
   USE - The method is useful for producing a coffee activated carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple; reduces contamination; and improves yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixed gas includes 40-60 vol.% inert gas, 20-40 vol.% carbon dioxide and 10-30 vol.% water vapor. The mixed gas is injected at a volume ratio of 5-20 times the volume of coffee carbide. The inert gas is nitrogen gas. The water content in coffee by-product is 50-60 wt.%. The particle size of powder is 500-2500 mu m.

PN CN111134058-A; CN111134058-B
TI Processing water-saving seedling useful for bay            scallops comprises e.g. feeding high-concentration            fresh bait during cultivation, applying photosynthetic            bacteria and antibiotics at interval and            synergistically controlling the water quality.
AU ZHANG S
   LI L
   WANG W
   CONG W
   LIU L
   JIANG Y
   TIAN J
   LI B
   XU K
AE INST OCEANOLOGY CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAHY-C)
GA 2020425597
AB    NOVELTY - Processing water-saving seedling comprises                feeding high-concentration fresh bait during                cultivation and in the process of feeding fresh                bait, applying photosynthetic bacteria and                antibiotics at intervals, synergistically                controlling the water quality, where during the                water exchange period the scallop seedling raising                period can be extended and the success rate of                seedling raising can be improved.
   USE - The method is useful for processing                water-saving seedling.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is economical and environmentally                friendly; reduces the consumption of warming                seawater, emission of carbon dioxide, dust and                damage to seedlings caused by frequent water                changes; improves the success rate of nursery; and                promotes the healthy and sustainable                development.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The during 4th to 6th hour                  from the fertilized egg, add antibiotics to the                  nursery pond, and add photosynthetic bacteria to                  maintain the water quality by cooperative water                  quality control method i.e. cyclically processed                  until the seedlings have finished indoor                  cultivation, where the interval of two                  cooperative control of water quality is 24 hours,                  the interval between antibiotics and                  photosynthetic bacteria added in the one-time                  cooperative control of water quality is 24 hours.                  The added amount of the antibiotic is a final                  concentration in the nursery pond of 4-6 ppm. The                  use amount of the photosynthetic bacteria is                  100ml/25m3 water body, where the number of                  strains in the water body is greater than or                  equal to 10 billion/ml. The high-concentration                  fresh bait in the nursery pond is golden algae                  with a culture concentration of greater than or                  equal to 4 million cells/ml, the flat algae above                  1 million cells/ml and small crescent                  diamond-shaped diatoms with 3 million cells/ml.                  The during cultivation period, after the                  fertilized egg hatches for 24 hours, the initial                  culture density of larvae does not exceed 8/ml,                  where from the first day to the fourth day, the                  larvae are fed mainly with gold algae. The daily                  feeding volume is less than or equal to 20,000                  cells/ml, the after fourth day and before the                  metamorphosis the density of larvae does not                  exceed 5 per ml, the feeding is mainly based on                  mixed bait of Platymonas and Chlorella, the daily                  feeding amount gradually transitions to 100,000                  cells/ml, the metamorphosis of the larvae,                  feeding is mainly based on small crescent                  diamond-shaped diatoms, the daily feeding volume                  is less than or equal to 100,000 cells/ml. The                  larvae do not change water in the early stage,                  and the new pond is replaced every 4 days, and                  the pond will not be changed after the attachment                  of the base until the hanging sea is temporarily                  maintained.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); P14 (Animal management and care (A01K, L, M).)
MC D03-G; D04-A01; D04-B07F; D04-D; D05-A04; P14-A02

PN CN111139135-A
TI Removing capsaicin and dihydrocapsaicin from plant oil comprises adding modified activated carbon into plant oil, performing adsorption, centrifuging and separating and removing capsaicin and dihydrocapsaicin.
AU ZHANG W
   WU J
   QI Y
   HE J
AE UNIV WUHAN POLYTECHNIC (UWHP-C)
GA 202042448X
AB    NOVELTY - Removing capsaicin and dihydrocapsaicin from plant oil comprises adding modified activated carbon into plant oil, performing adsorption at 90-100 degrees C, centrifuging and separating to obtain upper clear liquid to obtain plant oil, removing capsaicin and dihydrocapsaicin. The preparation of the modified activated carbon includes soaking the dehydrated food-grade powder activated carbon in potassium hydroxide solution, carbonizing at 450-550 degrees C, washing and gasifying with carbon dioxide at 550-650 degrees C, where the particle size of the food-grade powdered activated carbon is 100-200 mesh.
   USE - The method is useful for removing capsaicin and dihydrocapsaicin from plant oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, easy, and highly feasible and realizes high removal rate and solves the problem of capsaicin residues.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The amount of modified activated carbon added is 2-3 wt.% relative to the plant oil. The plant oil includes peanut oil, colza oil, soybean oil, maize germ oil, Camellia seed oil, sunflower seed oil, olive oil, linseed oil and palm oil. The concentration of the potassium hydroxide solution is 0.05-0.07 mol/l. Preferred Method: The speed of centrifugal separation is 8000-12000 revolutions/minute and the time of centrifugation is 5-15 minutes. The adsorption time is 20-30 minutes. The steps to remove moisture include is drying food-grade powdered activated carbon in an oven at 100-105 degrees C for 7-8 hours. The immersion time in potassium hydroxide solution is 2-4 hours, and the temperature during immersion is 75-85 degrees C. The carbonization time is 2-4 hours.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)

PN CN111100317-A; CN111100317-B
TI Preparing flexible polyimide aerogel comprises e.g. reacting dianhydride monomer, isocyanate monomer, and polyether polyol monomer in polar aprotic organic solvent to obtain flexible polyamic acid, and placing in acetone for replacement.
AU LI B
   ZHAO N
   LIU B
   YU Y
   LI J
   WANG F
   ZHANG F
   PENG D
   ZHANG S
AE NEW MATERIALS INST SHANDONG ACAD SCI (UYQQ-C)
GA 202040369B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing flexible polyimide aerogel comprises (1) reacting dianhydride monomer, isocyanate monomer, and polyether polyol monomer in polar aprotic organic solvent to obtain flexible polyamic acid, (2) placing flexible polyamic acid in acetone for replacement to obtain semi-finished product, (3) drying semi-finished product using supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain polyamic acid aerogel, and (4) performing post-curing of polyamic acid aerogel to obtain polyimide aerogel.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing flexible polyimide aerogel.
   ADVANTAGE - The aerogel has good shock absorption, noise reduction, thermal insulation, dimensional stability, and impact resistance properties, and wider range of applications.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the flexible polyimide aerogel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the step (1), the reaction is carried out at 85-95 degrees C for 2-3 hours. In the step (2), the number of replacements is 2-5 times, each replacement for 12-24 hours. In the step (4), the post-curing is carried out at 70-150 degrees C for 5-24 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dianhydride monomer is 3,3',4,4'-biphenyltetracarboxylic dianhydride, 4,4'-biphenyl ether dianhydride, 3,3',4,4'-benzophenonetetracarboxylic dianhydride, and/or pyromellitic dianhydride. The isocyanate monomer is 4,4'-diphenylmethane diisocyanate, 2,4'-diphenylmethane diisocyanate, 2,4-methylphenyl diisocyanate, isophorone diisocyanate, and/or hydrogenated 4,4'-diphenylmethane diisocyanate. The polar aprotic organic solvent is N, N-dimethylformamide, N, N-dimethylacetamide, and/or N-methylpyrrolidone. The mass ratio of isocyanate monomer and polyether polyol monomer is 10-80:10-30:10-70; the mass ratio of polar aprotic solvent and polyimide is 8-15:1. The mass ratio of 3,3',4,4'-biphenyltetracarboxylic dianhydride, 4,4'-diphenylmethane diisocyanate, and trifunctional polypropylene glycol with molecular weight of 400 is 6.4:1.69:0.855; the mass ratio of 4,4'-biphenyl ether dianhydride, 4,4'-diphenylmethane diisocyanate, bifunctional polypropylene glycol with molecular weight of 400, and trifunctional polypropylene glycol with molecular weight of 400 is 1:3:1.2:1.01; the mass ratio of 3,3',4,4'-biphenyltetracarboxylic dianhydride, 4,4'-diphenylmethane diisocyanate, bifunctional polypropylene glycol with molecular weight of 2000, and trifunctional polypropylene glycol with molecular weight of 400 is 1.18:2.5:4:1.01; the mass ratio of 3,3',4,4'-biphenyltetracarboxylic dianhydride, 4,4'-diphenylmethane diisocyanate, bifunctional polypropylene glycol with molecular weight of 2000, and trifunctional polypropylene glycol with molecular weight of 400 is 3.21:2.1:1:3. The porosity of flexible polyimide aerogel is 75-85% with average pore size of 30-100 nm, density of 0.1-0.3 g/cm3, specific surface area of 300-700 m2/g, thermal conductivity of 0.02-0.03 W/(mx K), and thermal decomposition temperature of 310-492 degrees C. Preferred Process: In the step (3), the pressure of carbon dioxide gas is 8-10 MPa, and drying is carried out at 32-37 for 12-24 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyether polyol monomer is bifunctional polypropylene glycol and/or trifunctional polypropylene glycol with molecular weights of 400, 1000, and 2000.
DC A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

TI Producing fermented liquid food product, preferably yogurt and cheese by pasteurizing liquid, cooling pasteurized liquid completely or partially, introducing cooled liquid into tank, and adding ferments i.e. lactic bacteria to liquid.
AU CAMPO P
   IBARRA D
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE FRANCE IND SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)

   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE FRANCE IND SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 202013001F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing a fermented liquid food product involves pasteurizing liquid, cooling pasteurized liquid completely or partially, introducing cooled liquid into a tank, and adding ferments i.e. lactic bacteria to the liquid contained in the tank, where the liquid is previously inerted by sweeping the tank headspace using a neutral gas such as nitrogen, containing or not carbon dioxide, before introducing the liquid into the tank.
   USE - The method is useful for producing fermented liquid food product, preferably yogurt and cheese.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces high quality-fermented liquid food product having excellent texture and flavor in a simple and rapid manner with improved productivity, prevents foam formation during production, and requires reduced gas consumption as compared to existing one.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The method involves performing downstream or upstream of pasteurization, and in any event before introducing the liquid in the tank, to an injection in line of a neutral gas i.e. nitrogen or carbon dioxide or a mixture of such gases in the liquid, using a flow rate of 5-50 g/m3, preferably 10-30 g/m3 of liquid. The method involves injecting carbon dioxide or a gas comprising carbon dioxide in an amount of 20-1500 mg/l, preferably 150-900 mg/l in the liquid, before its introduction in tank. Preferred Component: The liquid is milk. The liquid is a mixture of several ingredients, liquid and optionally solid, where the mixture of these ingredients is subjected to inerting process.

PN CN110564471-A
TI Absorption liquid useful for desulfurization and decarbonization in synthetic natural gas synthesis gas, comprises alcohol amine, hindered amine, antifoaming agent, and water.
AU MAO S
   YU Y
   ZHU D
   CHEN Y
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC NANJING CHEM RES INST CO LTD (SNPC-C)
GA 2019A6645B
AB    NOVELTY - Absorption liquid comprises 25-65 wt.% alcohol amine, 5-30 wt.% hindered amine, 0.01-0.05 wt.% antifoaming agent and water (remaining amount), where the hindered amines are a mixture of sterically hindered amines, preferably a non-linear alcohol amine compound having at least one sterically hindered structures on a nitrogen atom.
   USE - The liquid is useful for desulfurization and decarbonization in SNG synthesis gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The liquid: removes hydrogen sulfide and carbon monoxide in the SNG synthesis gas; after purification, the hydrogen sulfide content in the purified gas is less than or equal to 20 mg/Nm3, carbon dioxide content is 2-2.5 wt.%; achieves the SNG synthesis gas purification index; and has high desulfurization and decarbonization rate, low regeneration energy consumption, and excellent stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alcohol amine is two kinds of mixed alcohol amines, having a concentration of 30-60 wt.%. The alcohol amine is a mixture of methyl diethanolamine and diethanolamine. The concentration of the hindered amine is 5-30 wt.%. The hindered amines comprise 5-20 wt.% hydroxyethylpiperazine, 5-15 wt.% morpholine, and 5-15 wt.% diazabicyclo. The concentration of the antifoaming agent is 0.02-0.05 wt.%.    ADVANTAGE - The acid-sensitive full biodegradable amphiphilic compound is self-assembled into nanomicelles in water, has good biocompatibility, and can be practical as a drug carrier, at the same time, acid degradation can occur, which can form small molecules and eventually become water and carbon dioxide, achieving complete degradation.

It has molecular structure of formulae (I-II), where n1 of 5-20, m1 of 5-10 as degree of polymerization, and has a number average molecular weight of 1000-3500, and n2 of 3-7, and m2 of 5-13 as the degree of polymerization, an has 1000-2600 number average molecular weight.. Preferred Processes: The Preparation involves placing the triethylene glycol monomethyl ether and catalyst in a reaction vessel containing a solvent, and stirring at a temperature of 0-10 degrees C and a rotation speed of 200-600 rotations/minute to form a first mixed liquid, placing the vinyl diethylene glycol ether in a reaction vessel containing a solvent and stirring to form a second mixed liquid, pouring the second mixture into first mixture, stirring and mixing, and then carrying out the reaction, after the reaction completed, purifying by filtration to obtain a product A, placing the product A and catalyst sequentially in a reaction vessel containing a solvent, and stirring and mixing at a rotation speed of 200-600 rotations/minute to form a third mixed liquid, putting vinyl hydroxybutyl ether into a reaction vessel containing a solvent and stirring to form a fourth mixed solution, and pouring the fourth mixture into third mixture, stirring and mixing, and then carrying out the reaction, after the reaction finished, carrying out filtration and purification to obtain Compound B. The preparation method involves placing the triethylene glycol monomethyl ether and catalyst in a reaction vessel containing a solvent, and stirring and mixing the mixture at a temperature of 0-10 degrees C and a rotation speed of 200-600 rotations/minute to form a first mixed liquid, placing vinyl hydroxybutyl ether in a reaction vessel containing a solvent and stirring to form a second mixed solution, pouring the second mixture into first mixture, stirring and mixing, and then performing the reaction, after the reaction completed, purifying by filtration to obtain a product C. The product C and the catalyst are sequentially placed in a reaction vessel containing a solvent, and stirred and mixed at a rotation speed of 200-600 rotations/minute to form a third mixed liquid. The vinyl diethylene glycol ether is placed in a reaction vessel containing a solvent and stirred to form a fourth mixed liquid, followed by pouring the fourth mixed liquid into third mixed liquid, stirring and mixing, and then performing the reaction, and after completion of the reaction, carrying out filtration and purification to obtain Compound D. Preferred Conditions: The reaction refers to the reaction at a temperature of 0-5 degrees C and a rotation speed of 1500-2000 rotations/minute, and the reaction time is 1-3 hours.

AE CHANGSHA ZICHEN TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019695142
AB    NOVELTY - A light calcium carbonate using calcium carbide slag is prepared by crushing calcium carbide slag, adding water to prepare suspended slurry, adding oxidant, oxidizing, or precipitant, precipitating, obtaining nontoxic suspension slurry, filtering, obtaining filter residue and suspension filtrate containing calcium hydroxide, pressing gaseous carbon dioxide into atomizing nozzle, spraying suspension filtrate, obtaining calcium hydroxide, filtering and separating, obtaining filter residue and water, obtaining filter residue as calcium carbonate, drying and grinding to obtain light calcium carbonate powder; or filtering, washing, purifying, drying and grinding to prepare light calcium carbonate powder; or filtering residue is adding water, and spray drying to prepare light calcium carbonate powder.
   USE - Method for preparing light calcium carbonate using calcium carbide slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environment-friendly and suitable for engineering constructi.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The light calcium carbonate using calcium carbide slag is prepared by crushing calcium carbide slag, adding water to prepare suspended slurry at 5-60 degrees C, preferably 8-55 degrees C with water/solid ratio of 15-3:1, preferably 13-5:1, adding 0.50-0.005 wt.% oxidant comprising chlorine dioxide, hydrogen peroxide, sodium percarbonate and ferrate, oxidizing, or 0.20-0.002 wt.% precipitant comprising copper sulfate and copper chloride, precipitating, obtaining nontoxic suspension slurry, filtering at 20-60 degrees C using 45-63 mu m filter pore size, obtaining filter residue and suspension filtrate containing 1.0-15.0 wt.% calcium hydroxide, pressing gaseous carbon dioxide into atomizing nozzle, spraying suspension filtrate at greater than or equal to 25 degrees C, preferably 28-80 degrees C, obtaining calcium hydroxide, filtering and separating, obtaining filter residue and water, obtaining filter residue as calcium carbonate, drying and grinding to obtain light calcium carbonate powder; or filtering, washing, purifying, drying and grinding to prepare light calcium carbonate powder; or filtering residue is adding water, and spray drying at 150-500 degrees C to prepare light calcium carbonate powder.

PN CN110040738-A
TI Method for producing sodium silicate for            processing green tire reinforcing agent, involves            concentrating obtained filtrate to prepare paste,            sending paste to oven, and drying obtained solid to            obtain powder by pulverizer.
AU ZHAO S
   WANG X
   TIAN Y
AE ANHUI LONGQUAN SILICON MATERIAL CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201966042G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves weighing quartz sand,                sodium carbonate and dispersant respectively. The                raw materials are added to a high-speed mixer and                mixed at a high speed. The mixture is heated and                melted in an inert atmosphere. The generated carbon                dioxide gas is continuously extracted during the                reaction. The calcined product is added to water,                ball milled, filtered. The filtrate is taken. The                obtained filtrate is concentrated to prepare a                paste. The paste is sent to an oven. The obtained                solid is dried to obtain a powder by a                pulverizer.
   USE - Method for producing sodium silicate for                processing green tire reinforcing agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The excellent reinforcing effect is                exhibited.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  the quartz sand, sodium carbonate and dispersant                  is 1: (1.1-1.2): (0.01-0.05). The molar amount of                  quartz sand is based on the silicon dioxide                  contained. The dispersing agent is selected from                  a titanate coupling agent.

PN CN110002932-A; CN110002932-B
TI Preparation of aromatic hydrocarbon using            synthesis gas by filling reaction area with catalyst,            converting synthesis gas into olefin-based hydrocarbon,            filling catalyst in reaction area, and mixing mixed gas            at outlet of reaction areas.
AU QIAN W
   SONG W
   WANG H
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 2019632271
AB    NOVELTY - An aromatic hydrocarbon is prepared using                synthesis gas by filling first-stage reaction area                with catalyst, converting synthesis gas into                olefin-based hydrocarbon, filling another catalyst                in second-stage reaction area, mixing mixed gas at                outlet of first-stage reaction area into                second-stage reaction area, adding benzene or                toluene, passing olefin through alkylation reaction                in the presence of hydrogen and carbon dioxide, and                highly selective converting to aromatics.
   USE - The method is used for aromatic hydrocarbon                using synthesis gas preparation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is easy to separate, and has high                raw material conversion rate, high aromatic                hydrocarbon yield, low cost and exit gas with low                olefin content.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst in                  first-stage reaction area comprises first metal                  component, second metal component and alkali                  auxiliary agent. The first metal component is                  iron, nickel and/or cobalt. The second metal                  component is zinc, chromium, manganese, copper,                  gallium, silver, gallium, lanthanum and/or                  cerium. The alkali auxiliary agent is sodium,                  potassium and/or calcium oxide. The catalyst in                  second-stage reaction area comprises single                  molecular sieve or molecular sieve and structure                  reinforcing agent. The molecular sieve comprises                  ZSM-5, ZSM-12, ZSM-11, ZSM-22, MCM-22, MCM-41,                  beta -molecular sieve and Y molecular sieve. The                  structure reinforcing agent is aluminum oxide or                  silicon oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves                  filling catalyst in first-stage reaction area and                  second-stage reaction area, introducing mixed gas                  having hydrogen/carbon monoxide molar ratio of                  1:1-8:1 into first-stage reaction area 3, olefin                  synthesizing at 250-450 degrees C at 0.1-5 MPa                  and volume space velocity of 500-10000 /hour,                  introducing olefin-containing mixed gas at outlet                  of first-stage reaction area into second-stage                  reaction area, adding benzene or toluene into                  second-stage reaction area, reacting with mixed                  gas at 170-350 degrees C at 0.1-5 MPa, and                  controlling volume space velocity of olefins to                  500-20000 /hour.
DC E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)

AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of hydrotalcite particles involves                dissolving aluminum hydroxide with alkaline                solution to obtain aluminate solution, reacting                aluminate solution with carbon dioxide to                precipitate low-crystalline aluminum compound,                preparing reactant containing hydrotalcite core                particles by mixing obtained low-crystalline                aluminum compound and magnesium compound and                carrying out primary reaction, and hydrothermally                reacting the obtained reaction product.

Preferred Properties: The                  low-crystalline aluminum compound has crystallite                  size of 35 Angstrom or less, determined from                  diffraction peak at 38 degrees in X-ray                  diffraction pattern, and BET specific surface                  area of 100-350 m2/g. The hydrotalcite                  particles have Brunauer-Emmett-Teller specific                  surface area of 8-18 m2/g.

PN RU2692619-C1; WO2019226078-A2; WO2019226078-A3
TI METHOD OF PRODUCING TEA FROM ROSEBAY comprises            rosebay leaves are collected, sorted.
AU LISINENKO I V
   LISINENKO I N
AE MAY CO LTD (MAYM-Non-standard)
GA 2019655436
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to the tea industry. Rosebay                leaves are collected, sorted, pre-wilted to                moisture content of 5570% and twisted. Twisted leaf                is treated with vitamin B6 solution in amount of                75140 mg of vitamin per 1 kg of initial raw                material. Treated sheet is subjected to two-step                fermentation. First fermentation step is carried                out in the darkness under anaerobic conditions,                under vacuum at pressure of 30100 mm Mercury or in                a medium saturated with nitrogen or carbon dioxide                for 16 hours at temperature of 3150C. Second                fermentation stage is carried out in aerobic                conditions at temperature of 2435C until fruity                notes formation in smell and product color change                to characteristic dark green. Product is dried till                residual moisture content is 712%.
   USE - Food industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention allows to produce tea from rosebay                with increased biological value.

PN CN109896945-A
TI Extracting emodin from giant knotweed comprises e.g. washing giant knotweed, drying to constant weight and crushing, adding ethanol and subjecting to sonication, adding propanol into ultrasonic mixture, concentrating and recrystallizing.
AU CHENG X
AE DONGMING GROWTH BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DONG-Non-standard)
GA 201956302V
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting emodin from giant knotweed comprises (i) washing giant knotweed, drying at 100-120 degrees C to constant weight, and crushing to 80-100 meshes, (ii) adding 3-5 volumes of ethanol and subjecting to sonication for 20-30 minutes, (iii) taking the ultrasonic mixture, adding propanol, and carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, (iv) concentrating the extract, and adding 0.01-0.03 times of enzyme for carrying out enzymatic hydrolysis, and(v) taking enzymatic hydrolyzed product solution, subjection to suction filtration, recrystallizing from ethanol and freeze-drying to obtain product.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting emodin from giant knotweed.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the utilization rate of raw materials, yield and purity of extracted emodin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the concentration of ethanol is 75-85%. In the step (iii), the amount of propanol added is 0.4-0.6 times the amount of ethanol. In the step (iv), carrying out enzymatic hydrolysis at 35-45 degrees C for 3-5 hours by using enzyme including cellulose, amylase or protease.

PN CN109609446-A; CN109609446-B
TI Composition useful for separating and culturing            the rabbit embryonic stem cell, comprises basic            fibroblast growth factor, leukemia inhibitory factor,            GSK3 inhibitor CHIR99021, and beta-catenin specific            inhibitor XAV-939.
AU DENG Y
   NONG T
   CHEN F
   YANG S
   SHI D
AE UNIV GUANGXI (UYGU-C)
   UNIV GUANGXI (UYGU-C)
GA 2019357902
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises basic fibroblast growth                factor (bFGF), leukemia inhibitory factor (LIF),                GSK3 inhibitor CHIR99021, and beta-catenin specific                inhibitor XAV-939.
   USE - The composition is useful for separating and                culturing the rabbit embryonic stem cell                (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                isolating and culturing rabbit embryonic stem                cells, comprising adopting mechanical separation                method, inoculating entire ICM isolated from each                blastocyst into a feeder layer containing the                culture solution, culturing at 37 degrees C with 5%                carbon dioxide under relative humidity of 95%,                where the primary culture is 4.31-5.63 days,                cloning and passaging using ReLeSR digestion method                to obtain rabbit embryonic stem cells, and                culturing the obtained rabbit embryonic stem cells                in vitro for 14 generations.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The final                  concentration ratio of basic fibroblast growth                  factor, leukemia inhibitory factor, CHIR99021,                  XAV-939 in the composition is (4-6 ng/ml): (9-11                  ng/ml): (2.5-3.5 mu M): (1.5-2.5 mu M). The each                  50 ml culture medium comprises 37 ml KnockOut                  DMEM medium, 7.5 ml serum replacement, 2.5 ml                  fetal bovine serum, 0.5 ml streptomycin, 0.5 ml                  non-essential amino acids, 0.5 ml glutamic acid,                  0.5 ml L-glutamine, 0.5 ml beta -mercaptoethanol,                  0.5 ml nucleosides, 4-6 ng/ml basic fibroblast                  growth factor, 9-11 ng/ml leukemia inhibitory                  factor, 2.5-3.5 mu M CHIR99021 and 1.5-2.5 mu M.c                  XAV-939. The final concentrations of basic                  fibroblast growth factor, leukemia inhibitory                  factor, CHIR99021, and XAV-939 in the culture                  solution are 4 ng/ml, 10 ng/ml, 3 mu M, and 2 mu                  M, respectively. The treatment time of the ReLeSR                  digestion method is 4-6 minutes. Preferred                  Method: The step of the mechanical separation                  method includes first digest the blastocyst with                  a chain protease to remove the mucin and the zona                  pellucida of the embryo, the ICM is separated                  from the trophoblast cells and the ICM is                  aspirated and transferred to the feeder layer.                  The ReLeSR digestion comprises discarding rabbit                  embryonic stem cell culture medium, washing twice                  with phosphate buffered saline, adding specific                  amount of ReLeSR for digestion for 1 minute,                  aspirating excess digestive juice, leaving a                  small amount of liquid, which not pass through                  the cells, incubating in an incubator for 3-5                  minutes, adding the new 0.5 ml rabbit embryonic                  stem cell culture medium to terminate the                  digestion, transferring the liquid to the                  eppendorf tube, washing the dig well with 0.5 ml                  phosphate buffered saline, transferring the                  phosphate buffered saline to the eppendorf tube,                  supplementing with 900 mu l rabbit embryonic stem                  cell culture medium, centrifuging at 1000                  revolutions/minute for 3 minutes, discarding the                  supernatant, leaving the bottom cell pellet,                  adding specific amount of rabbit embryonic stem                  cell culture solution, blowing the cell pellet                  and resuspending, transferring the liquid to a                  24-well plate with a new feeder layer or a                  12-well plate at a cell number of 1-2x 105/ml,                  shaking, mixing, culturing in an incubator for                  the next digestion and passaging.

PN CN109467536-A; CN109467536-B
TI Synthesizing 2-chloro/hydroxy-5-carboxypyrimidine comprises e.g. dissolving 2-chloro-5-bromopyrimidine and bis(tert-butoxycarbonyl)oxide in presence of active metal reagent, reacting to obtain 2-chloropyrimidine-5-tert-butyl carboxylate.
AU ZHANG J
   LIU Z
   LENG Y
AE CANGZHOU PURUI DONGFANG TECHNOLOGY CO (CANG-Non-standard)
GA 2019265316
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing 2-chloro/hydroxy-5-carboxypyrimidine comprises (i) dissolving 2-chloro-5-bromopyrimidine and bis(tert-butoxycarbonyl)oxide in the presence of active metal reagent, reacting to obtain 2-chloropyrimidine-5-tert-butyl carboxylate, and (ii) adding 2-chloropyrimidine-5-tert-butyl carboxylate in acid aqueous solution or alkali aqueous solution for reflux reaction, and processing to obtain 2-chloropyrimidine-5-carboxylic acid or 2-hydroxypyrimidine-5-carboxylic acid.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing 2-chloro/hydroxy-5-carboxypyrimidine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids the problem of low yield when the scale is enlarged by the conventional carbon dioxide method, and operation reproducibility between batches.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first step reaction is carried out using organic solvent, where the organic solvent is tetrahydrofuran, 2-methyltetrahydrofuran, methyl tert-butyl ether, cyclopentyl methyl ether, ethylene glycol dimethyl ether or diethoxymethane. In the first step, using active metal reagents including Grignard reagent, lithium reagent and alkyl magnesium-lithium reagent. The Grignard reagent is isopropyl Grignard reagent, sec-butyl Grignard reagent, isopropyl Grignard reagent lithium-lithium complex solution, and sec-butyl Grignard reagent-lithium chloride complex solution; lithium reagent is n-butyl lithium or hexyl lithium; alkyl magnesium lithium reagent is n-butyl magnesium lithium. In the first step, the Grignard reagent and alkyl magnesium-lithium reagent temperature is -20 to 20 degrees C, reaction temperature of lithium reagent is -75 to -40 degrees C. In the first step, 2-chloro-5-bromopyrimidine, bis(tert-butoxycarbonyl)oxide, active metal agent equivalent ratio is 1:1.05-1.2:0.35-1.4. In the second step, the alkali is sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide. During reaction, adding water miscible solvent to facilitate the reaction, where the water-miscible solvent is dioxane, tetrahydrofuran, acetone, acetonitrile, ethanol, methanol or isopropanol. In the process, adding alkali aqueous solution for reflux reaction, then adding acid solution to continue the reaction, then adjusting the pH value to 3-5 to obtain 2-hydroxypyrimidine-5-carboxylic acid. In the second step, the acid is hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid or hydrobromic acid.

PN CN109319840-A; CN109319840-B
TI Preparing strontium niobate/strontium composite nano-material useful e.g. in field of photo-catalysis, comprises e.g. taking tin(II) niobate material, and carrying hydrothermal reaction in mixed alkali solution.
AU DU Y
   JIN C
   HOU R
   WU J
   NIU Y
   FANG X
   LIANG J
   LI S
   GU H
AE UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY (UYBT-C)
   BEIJING SPECIAL CONSTR DESIGN & RES INST (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY (UYBT-C)
GA 2019172960
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing strontium niobate/strontium composite nano-materials comprises taking tin(II) niobate nano material as raw material, carrying out hydrothermal reaction in a mixed alkali solution of potassium hydroxide and strontium hydroxide in the presence of carbon dioxide.
   USE - The nano-material is useful in the field of photo-catalysis (claimed), preferably photocatalytic water treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The nano-material has good repeatability and simple operation; is suitable for mass production and economical; stable quality; and can promote the development of metastable citrate crystallization method and practical application of citrate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for strontium niobate/strontium composite nano-material prepared as mentioned above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) dispersing the tin(II) niobate nano material in deionized water, and adding potassium hydroxide and strontium hydroxide to form a paste; and (ii) using the paste-type mixture obtained in the step (i) as a reaction precursor, and performing heat treatment in a closed reaction vessel containing carbon dioxide. The temperature of the heat treatment in the step (ii) is 180-200 degrees C, preferably 180 degrees C, for 12-24 hours, preferably 24 hours . The amount of each material in the step (i) is 0.1-0.2 g tin(II) niobate nano-material, preferably 0.2 g, 0.4-0.9 g potassium hydroxide, preferably 0.9, and 0.2-0.8 g of strontium hydroxide, preferably 0.2 g are dissolving per 10 ml of deionized water. The ratio of strontium niobate and strontium carbonate formed in the step (ii) can be achieved by adjusting the input amount of carbon dioxide. When the amount of the tin(II) niobate nano-material is 0.1-0.2 g, the input amount of carbon dioxide in the step (ii) is 0.2 m3/hour, and maintained for 1-4 hours from the start of the heat treatment.

PN CN109022133-A
TI Preparing seabuckthorn seed oil useful for preparing auxiliary chemical liver injury products, comprises e.g. drying the seabuckthorn seeds at a low temperature, carrying out microwave irradiation treatment and pressing the oil extractor.
AU HUANG Y
   LI Y
AE TIANJIN TIENS BIOLOGICAL DEV CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A51538
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing seabuckthorn seed oil comprises (i) drying the seabuckthorn seeds at a low temperature, (ii) carrying out microwave irradiation treatment for 10 to 15 minutes, (iii) pressing the oil extractor to obtain sea buckthorn seed crude oil, (iv) carrying out carbon dioxide supercritical extraction of seabuckthorn seed crude oil using supercritical equipment and extracting to obtain an extract and (v) obtaining the seabuckthorn seed oil after the first-stage separation and the second-stage separation of the extract.
   USE - The seabuckthorn seed oil is useful for preparing auxiliary chemical liver injury products (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The oil is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the seabuckthorn seed oil is obtained by the above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the low-temperature drying comprises drying at 35-45 degrees C for 2 to 3 hours using a drying apparatus. In the step (ii), the microwave power is 550-600 W. In the step (iii), the pressing is carried out by a conventional pressing method. In the step (iv), the conditions for the supercritical CO2 extraction are: the seabuckthorn seed crude oil temperature is 36-37 degrees C, the pressure is 24-26 MPa and the supercritical equipment is used for carbon dioxide extraction for 1.5-2 hours. The first separation temperature is 36 degrees C, the pressure is 18 MPa, the secondary separation temperature is 36 degrees C and the pressure is 12 MPa. The seabuckthorn seed oil is obtained by drying the seabuckthorn seeds at 35-45 degrees C for 2-3 hours using a drying device, radiating for 10-15 minutes under microwave power 550-600 W, pressing the seabuckle seed crude oil, subjecting the sea buckthorn seed crude oil to carbon dioxide extraction for 1.5-2 hours 36-37 degrees C under a pressure of 24-26 MPa using supercritical equipment, subjecting the extract to primary separation and secondary separation to obtain sea buckthorn seed oil, where the primary separation temperature is 36 degrees C, the pressure is 18 MPa, the secondary separation temperature is 36 degrees C and the pressure is 12 MPa.
   ACTIVITY - Hepatotropic.

PN WO2018209362-A1; DE112018002467-T5; CN110769922-A; US2020188842-A1; JP2020522467-W
TI Process for gas phase membrane separation of alkene e.g. ethylene and/or propylene from compound in mixture comprising alkene and compound, involves using membrane comprising nonporous layer containing silver ionomer of fluorinated polymer.
AU LOPRETE K E
   NEMSER S M
AE COMPACT MEMBRANE SYSTEMS INC (COMP-Non-standard)
   COMPACT MEMBRANE SYSTEMS INC (COMP-Non-standard)
   COMPACT MEMBRANE SYSTEMS INC (COMP-Non-standard)
GA 201889867R
AB    NOVELTY - Process for gas phase membrane separation of an alkene from a compound (c2) in a mixture comprising the alkene and the compound (c2), involves using the membrane comprising a nonporous layer containing a silver ionomer of a fluorinated polymer. The compound (c2) is not 2C or more alkane.
   USE - Process for gas phase membrane separation of alkene e.g. ethylene and/or propylene (claimed) from compound in mixture comprising alkene and compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The membrane used in the method facilitated effective transport of alkenes from mixtures of alkenes with other gas which are not alkanes but comprises nitrogen, oxygen, carbon dioxide, argon and compounds which are produced in the chemical conversion of olefins.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The compound (c2) is nitrogen, methane or argon. The cyclic or cyclizable perfluorinated monomers are chosen from perfluoro(2,2-dimethyl-1,3-dioxole), perfluoro(2-methylene-4-methyl-1,3-dioxolane), perfluoroalkenyl perfluorovinyl ethers such as perfluoro-4-(1,2,2-trifluoroethenyloxy)-1-butene, and 2,2,4-trifluoro-5-trifluoromethoxy-l,3-dioxole. The fluoride group-containing monomer (I) is preferably a monomer of formula: CF2=CFOCF2CF(CF3)OCF2CF2SO2F or CF2=CFOCF2CF2SO2F.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The fluorinated polymer comprises sulfonyl repeating units derived from a fluoride group-containing monomer of formula: CF2=CF(OR'f)SO2F (I) and repeating units derived from one or more cyclic or cyclizable perfluorinated monomers. The fluorinated polymer further comprises repeating units chosen from tetrafluoroethylene, chlorotrifluoroethylene, trifluoroethylene, vinylidene fluoride, and vinyl fluoride.R'f = 2-20C perfluoroalkylene optionally substituted by ether oxygen.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

   Activated sludge (22)

TI Fire protection device for storing hydrobromic acid, has upper chamber that is filled with carbon dioxide gas, and barrel structure whose bottom surface is fixed with universal wheel by fastening bolts.
AU SUN X
AE ANHUI DONGJIAN CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
