PN CN108163871-A
TI Preparation of low-silicon-aluminum ratio chabazite used as gas adsorbent by mixing silicon source and aluminum source, adding strong base, mixing, calcining or adding chabazite crystal seed, adding water, stirring and crystallizing.
AU LIU L
   DU T
   WANG Z
   GONG H
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UYDB-C)
GA 2018492178
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of low-silicon-aluminum ratio chabazite comprises uniformly mixing silicon source and aluminum source, adding strong base, uniformly mixing, calcining or adding chabazite crystal seed, adding water, stirring to mix uniformly, adding the reactant into reaction kettle, crystallizing, filtering, washing and drying to obtain chabazite.
   USE - Preparation of low-silicon-aluminum ratio chabazite used as gas adsorbent for gas adsorption at 0-70 degrees C, where gas is oxygen (O2), carbon dioxide (CO2), methane (CH4), nitrogen (N2), or argon (Ar) (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not add organic template agent and has simple technique and strong operability. The silicon-aluminum ratio of chabazite is 1.0-2.2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The silicon source is silicon dioxide, sodium silicate, fly ash and/or kaolin. The aluminum source is aluminum oxide and/or sodium m-aluminate. The strong base is potassium hydroxide and/or sodium hydroxide. The mol ratio of silicon atom and aluminum atom in silicon and aluminum sources, respectively, is 1-5:1. The amount of strong base is 0.5-3 times the total mass of silicon source and aluminum source. Water is added at water and mixed material mass ratio of 2-20:1. The calcining step is conducted at 400-700 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours. The chabazite crystal seed is added at 5-30 mass% based on the mass of silicon source. The crystallization is conducted at 70-110 degrees C for 1-10 days. The washing step is conducted using deionized water for 3 times. The drying step is conducted at 60-100 degrees C for 4-10 hours.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Preparing nitrate radical intercalated cobalt-aluminum hydrotalcite by one-step co-precipitation involves taking aluminum nitrate nonahydrate, cobalt nitrate hexahydrate, sodium hydroxide and deionized water to prepare solution.
AU ZHU L
   ZHANG P
   QIN Y
   WU X
   DU X
   WANG L
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
   UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 201804824D
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of nitrate radical intercalated cobalt-aluminum hydrotalcite by one-step co-precipitation involves taking aluminum nitrate nonahydrate and cobalt nitrate hexahydrate as raw materials, sodium hydroxide as precipitant, and deionized water as solvent to prepare a solution, carrying out titration co-precipitation, performing hydrothermal treatment, washing, carrying out suction filtration, and drying to obtain product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nitrate radical intercalated cobalt-aluminum hydrotalcite by one-step co-precipitation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables rapid preparation of nitrate radical intercalated cobalt-aluminum hydrotalcite by one-step co-precipitation, and can effectively avoid the influence of carbon dioxide dissolved in the environment to form carbonate ions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation method of nitrate radical intercalated cobalt-aluminum hydrotalcite by one-step co-precipitation involves: (1) mixing sodium acetate solution with acetic acid solution, and controlling the concentration of sodium acetate at 0.036-0.140 mole.l-1 and concentration of acetic acid at 0.060-0.164 mole.l-1 to prepare acetic acid-sodium acetate buffer solution having pH-value of 4-5; (2) weighing cobalt nitrate hexahydrate and aluminum nitrate nonahydrate into a beaker, adding deionized water to the beaker, stirring the mixture for 5-15 minutes by magnetic stirrer to be completely dissolved, and transferring the prepared mixed salt solution to an bottle-type infusion apparatus; (3) weighing solid sodium hydroxide, then preparing weighed solid sodium hydroxide into sodium hydroxide aqueous solution with concentration of 1 mole.l-1, and transferring the sodium hydroxide aqueous solution to an bottle-type apparatus; (4) putting prepared acetic acid-sodium acetate buffer solution into reactor, placing the reactor in water bath kettle at a constant-temperature of 30-60 degrees C, continuously stirring, dripping prepared sodium hydroxide aqueous solution and mixed salt solution into reactor filled with buffer solution, monitoring the pH-value in the titration process in real time by an acidity meter, controlling the titration speed to enable the pH-value of the system to be fixed in the whole reaction process, continuously stirring for 20-30 minutes after dripping, and then sealing the obtained precipitate and mother liquor in a stainless steel reaction kettle lined with PTFE to obtain nitrate radical intercalated cobalt-aluminum hydrotalcite; (5) cooling the turbid solution to room temperature, then placing in Buchner funnel at an upper portion of suction filtration bottle to be subjected to suction filtration, retaining filter cake product on filter paper, and pumping the solution into filter flask; (6) putting the filter cake product into beaker, adding 100-200 ml deionized water, stirring and washing for 5-10 minutes, then placing the washing liquid in Buchner funnel for suction filtration, retaining filter cake product on the filter paper, and pumping the solution into filter flask, washing with deionized water, and carrying out suction filtration repeatedly until the filtrate is neutral; and (7) placing the filtered product in watch glass, putting the mixture into drying oven, drying at 80-100 degrees C for 12-24 hours to obtain product. In step (2), the concentration of aluminum nitrate in the mixed salt solution is controlled at 0.10-1 mole.l-1. In step (4), the pH-value of the system in the reaction process is controlled at 4-5.

TI Oxygen environment adjusting system combined with            plant factory and business hotel, has ammonium            bicarbonate carbon dioxide generator that is connected            with dispersing pipeline and control panel is connected            to variable frequency air conditioner.
AU GAO W
   GAO G
AE SHENZHEN YUNSHANG TIANYUAN INTERNET THIN (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2021E1481A
AB    (CN214746353-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model relates to the technical                field of adjusting system, and claims an oxygen                environment adjusting system combined plant factory                and business hotel, comprising a plant factory and                a business hotel, business hotel is built in the                plant factory; the plant factory and business hotel                comprise a single chip, a humidity sensor, a                temperature sensor, a bicarbonate ammonia carbon                dioxide generator, an oxygen content sensor, a                humidifying bottle, a ventilating fan, a dispersing                pipeline, a double-layer door, a control panel and                a variable frequency air conditioner; the oxygen                single chip respectively with the oxygen content                sensor, humidity sensor, temperature sensor,                double-layer door control panel, ventilation fan                and ammonium bicarbonate carbon dioxide generator                communication connection. The utility model                increases oxygen, improves the oxygen concentration                of the local space environment, so as to create an                ecological environment, low altitude area natural                environment in simulation state, building hotel                local oxygen-enriched environment system, the                indoor oxygen environment of the room reaches or                near the altitude oxygen-enriched level; The hotel                is combined with the plant environment to realize                the mutual benefit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of oxygen                environment regulation system combining plant                factory and business hotel. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)
EA    NOVELTY - The system has a plant factories and business                hotels includes single-chip microcomputers,                humidity sensors, temperature sensors, ammonium                bicarbonate and carbon dioxide generators, oxygen                content sensors, humidification bottles,                ventilation fans, dispersion pipes, double doors,                control panels and inverter air conditioners. The                oxygen single-chip microcomputer is respectively                connected with oxygen content sensor, humidity                sensor, temperature sensor, double door control                panel, ventilation fan and hydrogen carbonate                ammonia carbon dioxide generator. The ventilation                fan is installed at window of the business hotel.                The control panel is installed in the business                hotel interior. The ventilation fan is connected to                dispersion pipeline through the humidification                bottle. The ammonium bicarbonate and carbon dioxide                generator is communicated with the dispersion                pipeline. The control panel is connected with                inverter air conditioner, and the inverter air                conditioner is installed in business hotel.
   USE - Oxygen environment regulation system combining                plant factory and business hotel for realizing                continuous production of crop anniversary through                high precision environment control in                facility.
   ADVANTAGE - The system increases oxygen, improves the                oxygen concentration of the local space                environment, thereby creating an ecological                environment, simulates the natural environment in                low-altitude areas, builds a local oxygen-enriched                environment system in the hotel, and makes the                indoor oxygen environment of the guest room at or                close to the altitude oxygen-enriched level. The                hotel and the plant environment are combined to                achieve mutual benefit. The hotel has a unique                ecological environment keeping constant                temperature, constant humidity, constant oxygen                state, and effectively relieves the plateau                reaction and the stream of tourists in bad weather.                The system reduces the loss of oxygen in each time,                maintains the oxygen concentration in the room, and                adopts dispersion type oxygen supply, and oxygen                diffusion is more uniform.

TI Zero-carbon discharge cold and heat power supply system based on solar energy methanol decomposition synthesis cycle comprises solar heat supply and methanol storage tank, where storage tank with first channel inlet of heat exchanger.
AU GAN J
   WANG B
   LI Z
   HUANG R
   DOU X
   YU X
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2021C2837U
AB    NOVELTY - Zero-carbon discharge cold and heat power supply system based on solar energy methanol decomposition synthesis cycle comprises solar heat supply unit, methanol storage tank (4), first methanol pump (5), first heat exchanger (6), first reactor, first separator, second methanol pump, photovoltaic device, electrolytic tank, a first water pump, water storage tank, second separator, synthesis gas storage tank, second heat exchanger, absorption type refrigeration device, hydrogen storage tank, third methanol pump, second water pump, third separator, fluidized bed reactor, carbon dioxide storage tank, synthesis gas internal combustion engine, a generator, where the methanol storage tank is connected with the first channel inlet of the first heat exchanger through the first methanol pump, the first channel outlet of the first heat exchanger is connected with the first reactor inlet.
   USE - Zero-carbon discharge cold and heat power supply system based on solar energy methanol decomposition synthesis cycle.
   ADVANTAGE - The system does not need expensive energy storage device and has high energy efficienc.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a zero-carbon discharge cold and hot power supply method of the device, which involves pumping methanol into the first reactor through the first methanol pump under the catalytic action of the catalyst, decomposing methanol into carbon monoxide and hydrogen after the first reactor is provided with a first separator for separating the unreacted methanol, pumping back the methanol storage tank through the second methanol pump, storing carbon monoxide and hydrogen decomposed by methanol in the synthesis gas storage tank and directly supplying to the inlet channel of synthesis gas engine after the synthesis gas is burned in the synthesis gas internal combustion engine, pushing fast expanding gas the piston to move, so as to drive the crankshaft to rotate, driving crankshaft the generator to generate electricity, using the waste heat after combustion for supplying heat through the back portion of the second heat exchanger, using portion for absorbing refrigeration device for refrigeration, using one portion for organic Rankine cycle device to generate electricity, realizing cold and hot power supply, the product after complete reaction in the synthesis gas internal combustion engine is carbon dioxide and hydrogen, separating them to the corresponding water storage tank and carbon dioxide storage tank through the first separator, transferrong the water in the water storage tank to the electrolytic tank through the first water pump, storing the hydrogen generated by the cathode of the electrolytic tank in the hydrogen storage tank, hydrogen and carbon dioxide as reactant into the fluidized bed reactor under the action of the catalyst, reacting hydrogen and carbon dioxide to generate methanol and water, storing the low-temperature medium stored in the second cold tank in the second heat tank by exchanging heat generated in the fluidized bed reactor, supplying heat to the load, pumping product after reaction to the water storage tank through the second water pump, pumping methanol to the methanol storage tank through the third methanol pump, separating the unreacted hydrogen and carbon dioxide to the corresponding hydrogen storage tank and carbon dioxide storage tank.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a system.
   Solar panel (1)
   First heat tank (2)
   First cold tank (3)
   Methanol storage tank (4)
   First methanol pump (5)
   First heat exchanger (6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The synthesis gas is pre-produced and stored in the synthesis gas storage tank, when there is no solar energy or solar energy is not enough, the synthesis gas is stored in the synthesis gas storage tank. The synthesis gas pre-stored in the synthesis gas storage tank is used for generating energy in the synthesis gas internal combustion engine.

TI Conversion of polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons            comprises exposing feedstock comprising polycyclic            aromatic hydrocarbons to ozonolysis conditions in a            reactor comprising liquid carbon monoxide and            converting.
AU ZHOU H
   PATIL A O
   SUBRAMANIAM B
   JACKSON T A
   DANBY A M
   SHI H
   LUNDIN M D
AE EXXONMOBIL TECHNOLOGY & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 2022939698
AB    NOVELTY - Conversion of polycyclic aromatic                hydrocarbons, comprises exposing a feedstock                comprising polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons to                ozonolysis conditions in a reactor volume                comprising liquid carbon monoxide to convert at                least a portion of the polycyclic aromatic                hydrocarbons into oxygenated conversion products,                where the ozonolysis conditions comprising a                temperature of -25 degrees C to 50 degrees C under                a pressure of 1-15 MPa-a, a molar ratio of ozone to                the polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons in the                reaction medium is greater than or equal to                0.1.
   USE - The method is useful for conversion of                polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces or minimizes side reaction                that result in formation of carbon dioxide, and                also reduces or minimizes potential for hazardous                conditions related to the reaction                environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The ozonolysis conditions                  comprise a pressure of greater than or equal to                  7.4 MPa-a and a temperature is less than or equal                  to 31 degrees C. In the method: ozone is                  introduced into the reactor volume as a mixture                  of ozone and oxygen gas comprising 1-20 vol.%                  ozone relative to a volume of the mixture. The                  exposing comprises exposing the polycyclic                  aromatic hydrocarbons to ozonolysis conditions in                  a continuous process in a reactor to form a                  conversion effluent, the method further                  comprising recycling a portion of the conversion                  effluent back to the reactor. The ozonolysis                  conditions comprise a temperature of 0-31 degrees                  C. Preferred Components: The oxygenated                  conversion products comprise less than or equal                  to 1 wt.% water. The ozonolysis conditions                  comprise a molar ratio of ozone to polycyclic                  aromatic hydrocarbons is 1-50. The feedstock                  comprises at least one polycyclic aromatic                  hydrocarbon comprising non-aromatic rings, at                  least three aromatic rings or hydrocarbons                  comprising polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons. The                  ozonolysis conditions comprise a molar ratio of                  ozone to total hydrocarbons in the feedstock of                  0.1-50. The feedstock comprises at least a                  portion of a deasphalter rock and at least a                  portion of an aromatic extract fraction. The                  ozonolysis conditions comprise a concentration in                  the reaction medium of less than or equal to 1                  moles of the polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons per                  liter of carbon dioxide. The feedstock comprises:                  5-25 wt.% the polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons;                  and greater than or equal to 60 wt.% polycyclic                  aromatic hydrocarbons. The reactor volume                  comprises greater than or equal to 90 wt.% carbon                  dioxide. The reactor volume further comprises a                  co-solvent. The co-solvent comprises acetic acid.                  The molar ratio of co-solvent to carbon dioxide                  in the reactor volume is 0.01-0.15. The                  ozonolysis conditions comprise a single pass                  conversion of the polycyclic aromatic                  hydrocarbons of greater than or equal to 10 wt.%                  polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of chemical                solubility of various polycyclic aromatic                hydrocarbons was determined (qualitatively) in                liquid carbon dioxide and supercritical carbon                dioxide that do not support the claims.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

   USE - The method is useful for removing arsenic from                black copper mud used in pyrometallurgical                metallurgy.

The method further                  comprises cooling the material obtained by                  roasting, and the final temperature of the                  cooling is &#8804; 150&#8451;. The black                  copper mud is dried before being mixed, the                  temperature of the drying treatment is                  105-200&#8451;, and the time is 5-24                  hours.

   AFFAN M A
   TURKI A J
AE UNIV KING ABDULAZIZ (UYKI-Non-standard)

   USE - Haematococcus species KAU-01 for producing biomass
   ADVANTAGE - The Haematococcus species KAU-01 efficiently metabolize carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases, such as flue gases produced by coal-fired power plants and increase the efficiency of microalgae biomass production is the use of a filter system to remove inhibitors of microalgae growth without removing viable cells, and Haematococcus is harvested by sedimentation, filtration, or centrifugation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing biomass, which involves culturing the Haematococcus species in culture medium in the presence of light, where the light may be present intermittently, periodically or continuously.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The Haematococcus species is Haematococcus species KAU-01 or a subculture. The engineered variant of Haematococcus species KAU-01 has a mutation to its DNA, which is epigenetically modified, or that is transformed with an exogenous polynucleotide, or both. The light is natural, reflected, filtered, focused, or concentrated sunlight. The material is incorporated into a gas, liquid or solid prior to incorporating it into the culture medium. The culturing involves fed-batch culturing by replacing 5-50 wt.% spent culture medium with fresh culture medium and, optionally, adjusting the pH to range from 6-8. Preferred Components: The culture medium contain one material comprising a product obtained by combustion of coal and obtained from flue gas scrubber. The material is selected from carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, nitrogen oxide, sulfur oxide or a volatile organic compound. Preferred Process: The method further comprising reducing an amount of heavy metal present in the material prior to incorporating it into the culture medium; and recovering the biomass by filtration, centrifugation and/or sedimentation. PN CN114275790-A; CN114275790-B
TI Preparation of porous hydrated calcium silicate used e.g. for adsorbent, by leaching fly ash with alkali, dripping into calcium hydroxide suspension, mixing with organic alcohol solvent, azeotropic distilling, separating and roasting.
AU CHEN H
   SHEN X
   LI C
   ZHANG Z
   QU F
   YAN F
AE UNIV SOUTHERN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USOU-C)
GA 202252400D
AB    NOVELTY - A porous hydrated calcium silicate is prepared by leaching fly ash with alkali to obtain silicate leaching liquid, dripping at uniform speed into calcium hydroxide suspension, obtaining hydrated calcium silicate gel after stirring and heating reaction, mixing hydrated calcium silicate gel and organic alcohol solvent, azeotropic distilling, separating, drying and roasting.
   USE - The method is used for preparing porous hydrated calcium silicate for adsorbent for capturing carbon dioxide and/or absorbing heavy metal in waste water (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is suitable for large scale industrial application, has low cost and simple and easy operation, and obtains larger hole volume without using template agent. The product has better hole structure and excellent adsorption characteristic.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali is 10-30 wt.% sodium hydroxide aqueous solution. The silicate leaching liquid is sodium silicate leaching liquid. The desiliconated fly ash is used for recycling alumina. The mass ratio of sodium hydroxide and fly ash in sodium hydroxide aqueous solution is 0.4:1-0.8:1. The molar ratio of silicate in silicate leaching liquid and calcium hydroxide in calcium hydroxide suspension is 0.9:1-1.1:1. The concentration of silicate leaching liquid is 0.067-1.067 mol/L. The porous hydrated calcium silicate has specific surface area of greater than or equal to 400 m2/g, pore volume of greater than or equal to 2.7 cm3/g and pore diameter of 20-40 nm.Preferred Method: Leaching involves mixing sodium hydroxide aqueous solution with fly ash, performing hydrothermal reaction after heating at 105-115degrees Celsius for 0.4-0.6 hour, filtering and separating, obtaining silicate leaching liquid and desiliconated fly ash, stirring and heating reacting, filtering, obtaining hydrated calcium silicate and sodium hydroxide by-product, heating hydrated calcium gel and organic alcohol solvent to first temperature, distilling at constant temperature for 15-25 minutes, heating to second temperature, and distilling at constant temperature for 35-45 minutes. The first temperature is boiling point temperature of system formed by organic alcohol solvent and water. The second temperature is boiling point temperature of organic alcohol solvent. Drying is done at 85degrees Celsius for 10-14 hours. Roasting is done at 400-600degrees Celsius for 4-4.5 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic alcohol solvent is ethanol, propanol or butanol. The ratio of mass of hydrated calcium silicate gel and volume of organic alcohol solvent is 0.9 g:15-1.1 g:15 mL.

The mass ratio of the active component                  to the metal-organic frameworks is 1:12-15, where                  the mass of the activated component based on the                  mass of copper element contained. The mass of the                  auxiliary agent is calculated as the sum of the                  masses of the zinc element and the chromium                  element contained in it. The mass of the                  metal-organic frameworks is based on own mass.                  The metal-organic frameworks are metal organic                  ligand structures. The copper source is copper                  nitrate, copper acetate, and/or copper chloride.                  The auxiliary element source comprises zinc                  source and a chromium source. The zinc source is                  zinc nitrate, zinc acetate, zinc chloride, and/or                  zinc oxide. The chromium source is chromium                  nitrate and/or chromium chloride. The organic                  ligand is trimellitic acid, terephthalic acid,                  2,5-dihydroxyterephthalic acid, and/or                  2-methylimidazole; preferably, the molar ratio of                  the copper source, zinc source or chromium                  source, and organic ligand is 1:0.5-1.5:0.5-2;                  further preferably, the molar ratio of the copper                  source, the zinc source or the chromium source,                  and the organic ligand is 1:1:1.5; the number of                  moles of copper source is calculated based on the                  number of moles of copper element contained in                  it. The number of moles of zinc source or                  chromium source is calculated by the number of                  moles of zinc or chromium element contained, and                  the number of moles of organic ligand is                  calculated by the number of moles of itself. The                  reaction comprises solvothermal method,                  hydrothermal method, and mixing method;                  preferably, in the solvothermal method, the                  mixture further comprises organic solvent, and                  the organic solvent is ethanol, methanol, and                  N,N-dimethylformamide; preferably, the mixture                  further comprises water. The reducing gas                  atmosphere is a mixed gas atmosphere containing                  hydrogen, and the mass fraction of the hydrogen                  is 4.5-7.8%. Preferred Method: The reaction                  conditions are carried out at 100-180&#176; C                  for 4-72 hours; preferably, the mixture                  containing the zinc source or the chromium source                  and the organic ligand is first subjected to a                  solvothermal reaction treatment, and the copper                  source is impregnated by the impregnation method                  to obtain the catalyst precursor. the mixture is                  uniformly mixed; mixing the mixture uniformly,                  aging at 10-30&#176; C for 6-36 hours. In the                  hydrothermal method, grinding the mixture and                  mixing uniformly, and the catalyst precursor is                  obtained by hydrothermal synthesis; preferably,                  the reaction conditions are carried out at                  170-220&#176; C for 4-48 hours. In an inert                  gas atmosphere, the temperature is                  350-500&#176; C, and the time is 2-5 hours;                  preferably, the inert gas is nitrogen and argon;                  preferably, the reduction conditions are carried                  out under a reducing gas atmosphere, a                  temperature of 180-220&#176; C, and a time of                  5-12 hours. Preferred Components: The nano-cellulose is extracted from raw green onion, green onion skin and/or bagasse, and the particle size distribution is 0.5-5 nm.
   ACTIVITY - Antibacterial.

TI Aluminum nitride ceramic powder preparation involves reacting aluminum source and carbon source in atmosphere containing nitrogen to obtain semi-finished product containing aluminum nitride and excess carbon source.
AU SONG X
   WANG H
   LI X
   PAN G
AE SHANDONG SINOCERA FUNCTIONAL MATERIAL CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202077224G
AB    NOVELTY - Aluminum nitride ceramic powder preparation involves reacting aluminum source and carbon source in an atmosphere containing nitrogen to obtain a semi-finished product containing aluminum nitride and an excess carbon source. The carbon discharge treatment is performed on the semi-finished product in an oxygen-containing gas to obtain preliminary carbon powder. The obtained primary carbon powder is placed in a rotary kiln having carbon dioxide as a reaction gas for secondary carbon discharge treatment to obtain the aluminum nitride ceramic powder product.
   USE - Method for preparing aluminum nitride ceramic powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares the aluminum nitride ceramic powder in a simple, cost-effective and eco-friendly manner with improved carbon discharge efficiency and more conducive effect to industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The primary carbon powder is placed in a rotary kiln with carbon dioxide as reaction gas at 600-900 degrees C for 2-4 hours to obtain the aluminum nitride ceramic powder product. The aluminum source, the carbon source and the solvent are mixed by wet ball milling to form a wet mixture. The wet mixture is dried in a high-pressure fluid atmosphere to obtain a precursor. The precursor is milled in the nitrogen-containing atmosphere to obtain a semi-finished product containing aluminum nitride and excess carbon source. The wet mixture is placed in a drying chamber under inert gas or nitrogen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The solvent is water or an organic solvent, preferably ethanol, ethylene glycol or propanol.

PN CN111074672-A
TI Preparing low-temperature glass fiber aerogel composite paper comprises e.g. using one-step method to prepare silica sol, impregnation method to saturate glass fiber paper under vacuum to absorb silica sol, aging, and surface modifying.
AU CAO Y
   PAN J
   SONG D
AE GUIZHOU AEROSPACE WUJIANG ELECTRO-MECHAN (CAES-C)
GA 202039283X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing low-temperature glass fiber aerogel composite paper comprises using one-step method to prepare silica sol, impregnation method to saturate the glass fiber paper under vacuum to absorb the silica sol, aging under the specific condition, surface modifying of the alkylating agent, carrying out carbon dioxide supercritical fluid drying, and obtaining glass fiber aerogel composite thermal insulation paper.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing low-temperature glass fiber aerogel composite paper.
   ADVANTAGE - The paper has good thermal insulation performance, mechanical performance, heat prevention and waterproof performance, and is convenient for construction. The method avoids solvent replacement step, is easy to operate, has controllable process, and continuous production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises (1) preparing silica sol, (2) homogeneously preparing wet gel by sol and glass fiber paper, (3) carrying out aging and hydrophobic modification of wet gel, (4) performing wet gel drying to obtain glass fiber aerogel composite insulation paper, and (5) post-processing of glass fiber aerogel composite insulation paper. In the method, the homogeneous preparation of the wet gel by sol and glass fiber paper is as follows: evenly spraying the silica sol on the glass fiber paper under vacuum environment to saturate the glass fiber paper to adsorb the glue solution, and allowing to stand for 50-70 minutes to form gel. In the method, the aging is as follows: soaking the wet gel in ethanol solution at 35-45 degrees C for 24 hours, in the surface modification, adding the aged wet gel in ethanol solution with alkylating agent, and soaking at 50-60 degrees C for 12 hours to obtain low-temperature glass fiber aerogel composite paper. In the method, the drying is as follows: adding surface-modified wet gel into supercritical carbon dioxide drying system for drying, injecting carbon dioxide gas into extraction kettle, maintain temperature and pressure of the extraction kettle in contact with the material in the supercritical state of carbon dioxide gas, dissolving solvent in the material in the supercritical fluid, and collecting fluid from the extraction outlet of the extraction kettle. In the method, the supercritical state temperature of carbon dioxide gas is 45-55 degrees C, the pressure is 10-18 MPa, the flow rate of carbon dioxide gas through the drying kettle is 2000-2500 kg/hours, and the extraction time is 8-12 hours. Preferred Components: The raw materials of the silica sol comprises silicon source precursors, purified water, catalysts and solvents, the silicon source precursor comprises tetraethoxysilane, methyl orthosilicate, and trimethylchlorosilane, the purified water is deionized water, the catalyst comprises ammonia water, ammonium carbonate, ammonium bicarbonate, and ammonium fluoride, the solvent comprises methanol, ethanol, butanol, and linear alcohol with mass fraction of not greater than 96%. The silica sol comprises ethyl orthosilicate, 99% industrial alcohol, purified water and catalyst in ratio of 1:(14.81-98.88):(2.22-5.55):(0.04-0.24). The glass fiber paper comprises glass fiber material with nanometer diameter and density of not greater than 200 kg/m3, and having uniform texture.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The density of the cell suspension is 5x 105-1x 106 cells/ml. The ratio of the volume of the cell suspension to the number of the three-dimensional microcarriers is 600-1500 mu l per piece of three-dimensional microcarriers. The concentration of carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide incubator is 5%. Preferred Method: The cell suspension is obtained by resuspending mesenchymal stem cells in serum-free medium. The step (a) involves sterilizing at 121 degrees C for 20 minutes under a steam pressure of 103.4 kPa. The step (b) involves coating for 1-3 hours. The step (c) involves carrying out cell adsorption for 2-4 hours. The carbon dioxide incubator is carried out at 37 degrees C. The step (c) involves adding both the cell suspension and the carrier into a centrifuge tube, placing the centrifuge tube into the carbon dioxide incubator for cell adsorption, loosely covering the centrifuge tube cover, taking out the centrifuge tube, closing and shaking the centrifuge tube, and placing it back into the carbon dioxide incubator. Preferred Components: The carrier component is 100% pure polyethylene terephthalate.

The organic solvent includes ether, ethanol, acetone, chloroform or dichloromethane, preferably dichloromethane. The mass ratio of esterified compound to organic solvent is 90-100: 100, preferably 94-98: 100, and the esterified compound includes alpha -acetyl- gamma -butyrolactone. Preferred Method: The temperature of the chlorination reaction is 0-20 degrees C, preferably 5-15 degrees C. In the method, completing the chlorination reaction to obtain mixture of chloroester and water, and separating the chloroester and water to obtain chloroester. The time for standing and layering is 1.5-2.5 hours, preferably 1.8-2.2 hours. The extracted organic solvent is recovered by vacuum distillation.

PN CN110527777-A
TI Reduction of oxygen consumption of semi-steel steelmaking converter involves mixing semi-steel into converter, adding active lime, high magnesium lime and acidic composite slagging agent and using oxygen lance to supply oxygen.
AU CHEN L
AE PANGANG GROUP PANZHIHUA IRON & STEEL RES (ANSH-C)
GA 2019A41789
AB    NOVELTY - Reduction of oxygen consumption of semi-steel steelmaking converter comprises mixing semi-steel into converter, adding active lime, high magnesium lime and acidic composite slagging agent and using oxygen lance to supply oxygen and bottom-blown gas to carry out blowing until the end of blowing to obtain qualified molten steel where bottom-blow gas includes nitrogen, carbon dioxide and argon.
   USE - The method is used for reducing oxygen consumption of semi-steel steelmaking converter.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can reduce the oxygen consumption of converter of semi-steel steelmaking by changing and controlling the bottom blowing gas and bottom blowing mode of converter.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The bottom blowing gas supply method is done from the beginning of blowing to 1/3 stage of blowing, and the bottom blowing gas is nitrogen where the bottom blowing gas is carbon dioxide from 1/3 stage to 2/3 stage of blowing and the bottom blowing gasses are carbon dioxide and argon from 2/3 stage of blowing to the end of blowing. The bottom blowing gas supply method from the beginning of blowing to 1/3 stage of blowing has air supply intensity of 0.06-0.25 Nm3/t.minute. The blowing from 1/3-2/3 stage is done at air supply intensity of 0.10-0.35 Nm3/t.minute. The gas supply intensity from 2/3 stage of blowing to the end of blowing is 0.20-0.45 Nm3/t.minute. Preferred Composition: The volume ratio of carbon dioxide and argon in bottom-blown gas is 3:1 from the 2/3 stage of blowing to the end of blowing. The blowing step is carried out at blowing gun position of 1.4-1.8 m, blow gun position of 2 m and pulling carbon gun position of 1.4 m where nozzle of oxygen lance is 1.4-2 m away from the basic gun position of molten metal of converter. The slag alkalinity at the end point is controlled to 3-4. Preferred Composition: The raw materials added are 10-14 kg/t active lime, 10-14 kg/t high magnesium lime and 8-12 kg/t acidic composite slagging agent. The active lime contains 85-90 wt.% calcium oxide. The high magnesium lime contains 48-55 wt.% calcium oxide and 30-40 wt.% magnesium oxide. The content of silica in acidic composite slagging agent is greater than or equal to 40 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Composition: The semi-steel is made of 4.2-4.40 wt.% carbon, 0.10-0.15 wt.% silicon, 0.01-0.03 wt.% manganese, less than or equal to 0.07 wt.% phosphorus, less than or equal to 0.03 wt.% sulfur and balance iron and impurities.

PN GB2571136-A; WO2019162236-A1; EP3755655-A1
TI Producing hydrogen involves receiving feed gas            comprising hydrocarbons and performing reforming            processes so as to generate hydrogen in dependence on            feed gas, followed by performing reforming processes as            gas-heated reforming process.
AU RYTTER E
   REINERTSEN T R
AE REINERTSEN NEW ENERGY AS (REIN-Non-standard)
   REINERTSEN NEW ENERGY AS (REIN-Non-standard)
GA 201972001Q
AB    NOVELTY - Producing hydrogen involves receiving a feed                gas comprising hydrocarbons and performing                reforming processes so as to generate hydrogen in                dependence on the feed gas, followed by performing                reforming processes such as both a gas-heated                reforming process and an autothermal reforming                process and heat generating by the autothermal                reforming process which is supplied to the                gas-heated reforming process.
   USE - Method for producing hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce hydrogen is                improves overall efficiency of both methanol and                hydrogen production over the individual                efficiencies of separate methanol and hydrogen                plants, gives significantly lower consumption of                natural gas, less carbon-dioxide, significantly                improves energy efficiency and reduces greenhouse                gas emissions from a methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Components: The feed gas is                  natural gas. The feed gas is a hydrocarbon rich                  gaseous stream from or within an oil refinery, or                  a petrochemical plant. The hydrogen production                  plant and one or more further includes a methanol                  synthesis plant, a nitrogen production plant, a                  plant for Fischer-Tropsch synthesis, an oil                  refinery, an air-separation plant and a                  petrochemical plant. Preferred Conditions: The                  temperature of gas output from the autothermal                  reforming process is in the range 900-1100                  degrees C, and preferably between 1000 and 1050                  degrees C. The temperature of gas output from the                  gas-heated reformer is in the range 400-800                  degrees C, and preferably 450-700 degrees C, and                  further preferably 500-600 degrees C. The                  autothermal reforming process is supplied with                  oxygen from an auxiliary air separation unit that                  is of a methanol synthesis plant or a nitrogen                  production plant. The water-gasshift is performed                  in both a high-temperature-shift reactor and a                  low-temperature shift reactor. The                  water-gas-shift is performed in one reactor. The                  hydrogen is separated by one or more of a PSA                  unit, a membrane that is optionally a                  palladium-membrane and/or a membrane in which the                  permeate side of the membrane is flushed by                  steam. The carbon dioxide is separated by                  cryogenic processes and/or a solvent that is                  optionally methanol. The hydrogen and/or carbon                  dioxide is liquified using excess cooling                  capacity from an auxiliary processing plant,                  optionally the auxiliary processing plant is an                  air-separation plant and the excess cooling                  capacity is in the form of cold oxygen and/or                  nitrogen. The separated hydrogen is used to                  adjust the stoichiometric number of a methanol                  synthesis plant so that the stoichiometric number                  is 2.0 or slightly above 2. The separated                  hydrogen is used to reduce carbon dioxide                  emission from an auxiliary processing plant,                  optionally hydrogen is added to burners of a                  fired heater. Preferred Process: The method                  further involves saturating the received feed gas                  with water, adding a hydrogen rich stream to the                  gas and pre-reforming the gas, reforming on the                  pre-reformed gas, performing a water-gas-shift on                  the gas output from the reforming processes, and                  separating hydrogen from the shifted gas,                  compressing and, optionally, liquifying the                  separated hydrogen, separating carbon dioxide                  from the shifted gas, compressing and,                  optionally, liquifying the separated carbon                  dioxide, removing sulfur from the received feed                  gas prior to saturating the received feed gas                  with water. The method is performed in a hydrogen                  production plant. The one or more further                  processing plants include a methanol synthesis                  plant, a nitrogen production plant, a plant for                  Fischer-Tropsch synthesis, an oil refinery, an                  air-separation plant and a petrochemical plant.                  The added hydrogen rich stream is obtained from a                  synthesis loop in a methanol synthesis                  plant.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AU FU Z
   GUO J
   JIANG L
   WU Z
AE PANGANG GROUP RES INST CO LTD (ANSH-C)
GA 2019727209
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing low sodium vanadium ratio leachate by pressure leaching of calcined clinker involves mixing vanadium slag and calcium salt to roast, to obtain roasted clinker, adding water and sodium carbonate leaching to calcined clinker, and introducing carbon dioxide during leaching process, sodium carbonate is used in amount of sodium, ratio of sodium/vanadium is controlled to 1.0-1.5, solid-liquid separation to obtain final product.
   USE - Method for preparing low sodium vanadium ratio leachate by pressure leaching of calcined clinker.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare low sodium vanadium ratio leachate by pressure leaching of calcined clinker that improves vanadium precipitation rate of ammonium metavanadate.

The vanadium slag is common vanadium slag or high calcium high phosphorus vanadium slag obtained by oxidizing and blowing vanadium containing molten iron. Preferred Conditions: The granularity of slag is less than 0.096 millimeter. The calcium salt is used in amount of from 0-8 wt.% based on calcium oxide. The leachate solid ratio is controlled at 1.8-2.5:1. The pressure of carbon dioxide controlled leaching reaction system is 0.3-1.5 megapascal.

PN CN109181814-A
TI Preparing low free phenol content of calcium sulfide alkyl phenol comprises e.g. neutralizing alkylphenol, base oil, calcium oxide and sulfur, vulcanizing, carrying out carbonation reaction and adding amine and aldehyde.
AU FAN J
   WAN X
   QIAO Q
AE XINXIANG RUIFENG NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (XINX-Non-standard)
GA 201909306E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing low free phenol content of calcium sulfide alkyl phenol comprises (i) neutralizing alkylphenol, the base oil, the calcium oxide and the sulfur, vulcanizing under action of promoter, and charging carbon dioxide gas for carrying out carbonation reaction; (ii) adding amine and aldehyde after completing carbonation reaction and subjecting unvulcanized alkylphenol to Mannich reaction; and (iii) removing filter residue.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing low free phenol content of calcium sulfide alkyl phenol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces product with less than 1% free phenol, detergency, oxidation resistance and water separation effect; is simple and convenient; has mild reaction conditions; and does not cause secondary pollution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (ii) comprises removing water generated during Mannich reaction. The step (ii) comprises cooling system to 30-60 degrees C after completing carbonation reaction, adding amine and aldehyde, heating up again, controlling heating slowly to 150-250 degrees C for 2-8 hours and carrying out Mannich reaction. The step (iii) comprises cooling to 80-150 degrees C after completing Mannich reaction and adding filter aid into slag for filtering. The neutralization reaction in step (i) is carried out at 80-130 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours. The vulcanization reaction in step (i) is carried out at 130-185 degrees C for 3-8 hours using promoter. The carbonation reaction in step (i) is carried out at 130-180 degrees C for 2-7 hours by adding calcium oxide in multiple portions during the reaction. Preferred Components: The amine in step (ii) comprises polyisobutylene succinimide, aniline, aqueous ammonia (25m%), triethylenetetramine, ethylenediamine or diethylenetriamine and aldehyde comprises paraformaldehyde, 37m% formaldehyde, citral, benzaldehyde or undecanal.

PT P
PN CN109019611-A; CN109019611-B
TI Preparing block transparent silicon dioxide aerogel for thermal insulation material, involves organic silicon source, water, alcohol solvent, protic polar solvent and acid catalyst to process hydrolysis reaction to obtain solution.
AU LU B
   LU M
   ZHANG D
   ZUO X
   LU S
   LU J
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 2018A52158
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing block transparent silicon dioxide aerogel involves organic silicon source, water, alcohol solvent, protic polar solvent and acid catalyst to process hydrolysis reaction to obtain solution. The alkali catalyst is added in solution to react to obtain wet gel. The wet gel is passed through liquid carbon dioxide replacement and dried to obtain block transparent silicon dioxide aerogel.
   USE - Method for preparing block transparent silicon dioxide aerogel for thermal insulation material, sound absorbing material, and adsorbent material or explosion-proof material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare block transparent silicon dioxide aerogel that has high efficiency, large specific surface area, low heat conducting coefficient, and excellent combination property.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The ratio of organic silicon source, water, alcoholic solvent and non-proton polar solvent is 1 :0.05-5:0.5-8:0.5-8. The hydrolysis reaction time is 0.1-10 hours. The wet gel is pre-aged for 0.1-10 hours before liquid carbon dioxide replacement and drying. The temperature condition for replacement and drying process is (-)20-40 degrees C and pressure is 1.5-13.0 megapascal. Preferred Components: The non-proton polar solvent comprises methyl formate, ethyl formate, methyl acetate, ethyl acetate, propyl acetate, trifluoro-propane, 1-chloro,3,3,3-trifluoro-propane. The organic silicon source comprises tetraethoxysilane, tetramethoxysilane, methyltrimethoxy silane, methyl triethoxy silane. The alcohol solvent comprises methanol, ethanol, isopropanol and n-propanol. The acid catalyst comprises nitric acid, sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid, oxalic acid, acetic acid. The alkali catalyst comprises ammonia. Preferred Process: The method involves passing wet gel through liquid carbon dioxide replacement. The wet gel is placed in pressure container which comprises air inlet and air outlet of liquid carbon dioxide. The inlet solvent is filled with liquid carbon dioxide. The liquid carbon dioxide is replaced with solvent in wet gel. The replacement solvent is liquid carbon dioxide which is discharged from outlet, until solvent substitution in wet gel completely. The liquid carbon dioxide is stopped and adjusted pressure and temperature in pressure container to normal temperature and normal pressure to obtain block transparent silicon dioxide aerogel.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)
MC E12-C02; E31-P01; N04; N05-E03; N07-J

PN CN108251204-A
TI Oil with high antioxidant activity comprises walnut seed oil, snakegourd fruit seed oil, waxberry seed oil, rosa davurice pall seed oil and tomato seed oil.
AU LIN S
   LONG T
   XIE J
   LIN X
   YU J
AE GUANGDONG JINNIBAO TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201855331F
AB    NOVELTY - Oil with high antioxidant activity comprises 70-80% walnut seed oil, 2-8% snakegourd fruit seed oil, 5-10% waxberry seed oil, 2-5% rosa davurice pall seed oil and 5-10% tomato seed oil.
   USE - Used as oil with high antioxidant activity.
   ADVANTAGE - The oil has high stability; does not easily oxidized; and meets needs of market.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the oil, comprising extracting walnut seed, snakegourd fruit seed, waxberry seed, rosa davurice pall seed and tomato seed using multi-stage separation supercritical carbon dioxide extraction equipment to obtain 70-80% walnut oil, 2-8% snakegourd fruit seed oil, 5-10% waxberry seed oil, 2-5% rosa davurice pall seed oil and 5-10% tomato seed oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The preparation of the walnut seed oil comprises shelling and crushing the walnut seed, adding to a extraction kettle of the multi-stage separation supercritical carbon dioxide extraction equipment, adjusting the extraction process conditions, extracting at 35-55 degrees C under 28-40 MPa for 2-6 hours, completing extraction, vacuum separating of carbon dioxide and oil using a separator of separation vessel, adjusting the process conditions of the separation kettle, heating separation tank I at 40-60 degrees C and pressure of 10-25 MPa to obtain walnut seed oil, adding the obtained walnut oil to a molecular distillation sample tank, regulating molecular distillation process parameters, where the degree of vacuum is 2-9 Pa, the distillation temperature is 100-150 degrees C, the speed of the doctor blade is 100-300 r.min-1, the feed rate is 20-60 ml.min-1, and the condensation temperature is 30-50 degrees C, refining, finishing the refining, and collecting the refined walnut seed oil. The preparation of the snakegourd fruit seed oil comprises shelling and crushing the snakegourd fruit seed, adding the snakegourd fruit seed to the extraction kettle of the multi-stage separation supercritical carbon dioxide extraction equipment, extracting at 30-50 degrees C under 25-40 MPa for 2-6 hours, completing extraction, vacuum separating of carbon dioxide and oil using a separator of separation vessel, adjusting the process conditions of the separation kettle, heating separation tank I at 40-60 degrees C and pressure of 12-25 MPa to obtain snakegourd fruit seed, adding the obtained snakegourd fruit seed to a molecular distillation sample tank, regulating molecular distillation process parameters, where the degree of vacuum is 2-9 Pa, the distillation temperature is 100-150 degrees C, the speed of the doctor blade is 100-300 r.min-1, the feed rate is 20-60 ml.min-1, and the condensation temperature is 30-50 degrees C, refining, finishing the refining, and collecting the refined snakegourd fruit seed. The preparation of the waxberry seed oil comprises shelling and crushing the waxberry seed, adding the waxberry seed to the extraction kettle of the multi-stage separation supercritical carbon dioxide extraction equipment, extracting at 35-45 degrees C under 23-35 MPa for 2-5 hours, completing extraction, vacuum separating of carbon dioxide and oil using a separator of separation vessel, adjusting the process conditions of the separation kettle, heating separation tank I at 45-65 degrees C and pressure of 10-22 MPa to obtain waxberry seed, adding the obtained waxberry seed to a molecular distillation sample tank, regulating molecular distillation process parameters, where the degree of vacuum is 2-9 Pa, the distillation temperature is 100-150 degrees C, the speed of the doctor blade is 100-300 r.min-1, the feed rate is 20-60 ml.min-1, and the condensation temperature is 30-50 degrees C, refining, finishing the refining, and collecting the refined waxberry seed oil. The preparation of the tomato seed oil comprises shelling and crushing the tomato seed, adding the tomato seed to the extraction kettle of the multi-stage separation supercritical carbon dioxide extraction equipment, extracting at 35-50 degrees C under 28-38 MPa for 2-4.5 hours, completing extraction, vacuum separating of carbon dioxide and oil using a separator of separation vessel, adjusting the process conditions of the separation kettle, heating separation tank I at 40-65 degrees C and pressure of 15-28 MPa to obtain tomato seed, adding the obtained tomato seed to a molecular distillation sample tank, regulating molecular distillation process parameters, where the degree of vacuum is 2-9 Pa, the distillation temperature is 100-150 degrees C, the speed of the doctor blade is 100-300 r.min-1, the feed rate is 20-60 ml.min-1, and the condensation temperature is 30-50 degrees C, refining, finishing the refining, and collecting the tomato seed oil.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)
MC D10-A01; D10-A02
IP C11B-001/04; C11B-001/10; C11B-003/12

   HAGO W (HAGO-Individual)
   HAGO ENERGETICS INC (HAGO-Non-standard)
GA 201850933Y
AB    NOVELTY - Conversion of flue gas to combustible fuels involves pyrolyzing the biomass to produce a carbon (c1) and volatile gases, gasifying the carbon (c1) in the presence of carbon dioxide obtained from the flue gas to produce carbon monoxide in a gasification system (s1), gasifying the carbon (c1) in the presence of natural gas to produce hydrogen and a carbon (c2) in a gasification system (s2), and combining the generated carbon monoxide and hydrogen from the systems and inputting the mixture in a Fischer Tropsch process.
   USE - Conversion of flue gas to combustible fuels e.g. methanol (claimed) used for forming dimethyl ether.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient conversion of flue gas process with reduced carbon footprint.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The systems use microwave energy using frequency of 915-2450 MHz to lower their gasification temperatures. The synthesis gas ratio of carbon monoxide to hydrogen is adjustable. Preferred Process: The carbon (c1) generates excess biochar in addition to carbon that reacts with carbon dioxide. The carbon (c2) is used as biochar. The volatile gases are burned to produce energy useful for the systems.

GA 2018402065

TI Preparing lavender essential oil facial mask involves facial mask bottom, weighing hydrogel, where mask and bottom comprises fiber and auxiliary agent, hydro-gel comprises emulsifier, humectant and tonics, removes wrinkle.
AU YANG L
AE YANTAI YANSHENG INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY (YANT-Non-standard)
GA 2018264968
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing lavender essential oil facial mask involves facial mask bottom, weighing hydrogel, where mask and bottom comprises fiber and auxiliary agent, hydro-gel comprises emulsifier, humectant and tonics, where oil comprises lavender essential oil, taking lavender lysozyme enzyme and liquid carbon dioxide, extracting, and separating to obtain lavender essential oil, picking and storing one or mixture with any proportion and treating at (-)20-(-)5 degrees C.
   USE - Method for preparing lavender essential oil facial mask.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare lavender essential oil facial mask which is used as freckle-remover and whitening, sun-screen, removes wrinkle and skin and promotes impaired tissue regeneration function recovery.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mask base comprises 90-100 pts. wt., preferably 93-99 pts. wt. fibers and 0-10 pts. wt., preferably 1-7 pts. wt. auxiliary agents and hydrogel comprises 10-30 pts. wt., preferably 15 pts. wt. emulsifier, 3-20 pts. wt., preferably 8 pts. wt. moisturizer, 1-10 pts. wt., preferably 5 pts. wt. tonic and 1-5 pts. wt., preferably 3 pts. wt. lavender essential oil. Preferred Component: The lavender essential oil facial mask is further comprises thickening agent and film-forming agent, where fiber is spandex, terylene, tencel fiber, aramid fiber, plant fiber, soybean protein fiber, lignin, cellulose, hemicellulose and chitin i. The auxiliary agent is flame retardant, antioxidant, thermo-sensitive agent, antifoaming agent and coupling agent and emulsifier is polyglycerol fatty acid ester, lauric acid monoglyceride, propylene glycol fatty acid ester, sorbitan monopalmitate, sodium stearoyl lactylate, diacetyl tartaric acid esters monoglycerides, polyoxyethylene nonyl phenyl ether, polyoxyethylene oleate, polyoxyethylene stearate, sorbitan fatty acid ester, polyglycerol, diisooctyl sodium sulfosuccinate and fatty acid polyoxyethylene ester. The humectant is glycerol, fructose, propanediol, sorbierite, polyglutamic acid, niacinamide, polyethylene glycol and xylitol. The restorative is honey, sodium salicylate, superoxide dismutase, vitamin-A, -b glucan, vitamins-E, and vitamin-A of sodium oxamic acid. The thickening agent is sodium chloride, sodium carboxymethyl cellulose, glutin, and sodium alginate. The film forming agent is carbomer, sodium polyacrylate, polyacrylamide and sodium alga acid. The mass ratio of 50-99.5:0.5-50 composition. The retaining agent is glycol, propanetriol and isopropanol. The mixing is homogeneous stirring.Preferred Process: The preparation of lavender essential oil facial mask involves taking fresh lavender, lysozyme and water in mass ratio of 10-50:0,02-1:50-90. The enzymolysis temperature is 27-55 degrees C, enzymolysis time is 30-60 minutes, enzyme temperature is 80-120 degrees C, and inactivating time is 5-20 minutes, sterilizing pressure is 0-5 mega Pascal. The liquid carbon dioxide temperature is 15-31.26 degrees C, pressure is 5-10 mega Pascal, flow speed is 25-35 liter per hour, extracting time is 0.5-2 hours. The separation comprises one-stage separating pressure of separating kettle to separate and secondary separating kettle to separate, where primary separating kettle is 6-8 mega Pascal. The separation temperature is 30-50 degrees C, and separation time is 0.5 wt.% separation pressure for 3 hours. The pressure of separating kettle is 4-6 mega Pascal and temperature is 25-40 degrees C, and separation time is 0.5-3 hours, separated kettle to separated back bottom residue left, transferred mixture to oil-water separator, added flocculating agent into mixture, stand for 20-150 minutes, where mass ratio of flocculating agent is 99-99.99:0.01-1 at 30-50 degrees C to performed oil-water separation to obtain lavender wax and water treatment, filtered to obtain water and residue, where flocculating agent is active carclazyte, or polyaluminium chloride, sodium polyacrylate and polyacrylamide. The preparation of hydrogel involves mixing emulsifier, moisturizer and tonicity, adding thickener and lavender essential oil at 5-25 degrees C and (-)0.5-0.08 mega Pascal, de-foaming for 10-30 minutes to obtain hydrogel. The hydrogel is uniformly smeared on bottom of mask to get lavender essential oil mask.

TI Preparation of milk champagne comprises mixing, e.g. fresh milk, pure water, white granulated sugar, malt syrup, fruit juice, sodium carboxymethyl cellulose, pectin and edible sodium bicarbonate, and biologically fermenting twice.
AU GUOZHONG M
AE XINJIANG XIAOJINNIU FOOD CO LTD (XINJ-Non-standard)
GA 201811639A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of milk champagne comprises mixing fresh milk, pure water, white granulated sugar, malt syrup, fruit juice, sodium carboxymethyl cellulose, propylene glycol alginate (PGA), xanthan gum, pectin and edible sodium bicarbonate, biologically fermenting twice. The milk champagne has low alcoholic degree at 0.80-1.20% and carbon dioxide gas content of 1.4-2%.
   USE - Method for preparation of milk champagne.
   ADVANTAGE - The milk champagne is rich in nutrient components, such as isoleucine, lysine, leucine, amino acids, vitamin B1, vitamin B2, vitamin B6, biotin, folic acid and protein, and has milk flavor and taste, full-bodied fragrance, rich foam, and more exquisite and lubricating texture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The first biological fermentation is done using 1\106 CFU/mL Lactobacillus bulgaricus, 1x 106 CFU/mL Streptococcus thermophilus and 1x 106 CFU/mL Bifidobacteria. The second fermentation is done using 1x 107 CFU/mL yeast. The preparation of milk champagne comprises:(A) adding white granulated sugar to milk, ultra-high temperature sterilizing at 38-42 degrees C, adding to fermentation tank fermentation, adding activated bacteria, and fermenting at 15-20 MPa for 96 hours to obtain first fermented milk;(B) mixing sodium carboxymethyl cellulose, pectin and xanthan gum, adding water, adding to emulsification tank, emulsifying for 15-20 minutes, adding fruit juice, propylene glycol alginate and maltose syrup, emulsifying, adding first fermented milk, mixing, adjusting pH to 4.4 using edible sodium bicarbonate, and homogenizing at 50 MPa to obtain homogeneous emulsion;(C) adding activated yeast, stirring, fermenting to 39-42 degrees C for 30 hours, observing degree of fermentation, and stop fermenting when carbon dioxide gas content reaches 1.4-2%; and(D) pumping into filling machine, filling, pasteurizing at 65-82 degrees C for 30-50 minutes, and cooling to 30-40 degrees C.Preferred Components: The milk champagne raw material comprises 80-160 pts. wt. fresh milk, 40-120 pts. wt. pure water, 30-80 pts. wt. white granulated sugar, 30-80 pts. wt. malt syrup, 20-60 pts. wt. fruit juice, 5-10 pts. wt. sodium carboxymethyl cellulose, 5-10 pts. wt. propylene glycol alginate, 4-10 pts. wt. xanthan gum, 4-10 pts. wt. pectin, and 1-6 pts. wt. edible sodium bicarbonate. The fruit juice is bottled apple juice.

PN CN107557147-A; CN107557147-B
TI Extraction of rapeseed oil from fruit flavor, involves screening and cleaning rapeseed, removing impurities, peeling rapeseed, and isolating to obtain rapeseed kernel, crushing rapeseed kernel, and sieving to obtain rapeseed powder.
AU HUANG F
   ZHOU Q
   WAN C
   LI W
   LIU C
   GUO P
   ZHENG C
   JIA X
AE OIL CROPS RES INST CHINESE ACAD AGRIC SC (CAGS-C)
GA 201804787B
AB    NOVELTY - The extraction method of rapeseed oil from fruit flavor involves (i) screening and cleaning rapeseed, removing impurities, (ii) peeling rapeseed with an automatic peeling machine, and isolating to obtain rapeseed kernel, (iii) crushing rapeseed kernel, and sieving to obtain rapeseed powder, (iv) freeze-drying the fruit slices, grinding, and sieving to obtain dried fruit powder, (v) mixing desiccated rapeseed powder and dried fruit powder, extracting oil with supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain hair oil, (vi) centrifuging, and filtering the hair oil.
   USE - Extraction of rapeseed oil from fruit flavor (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The environmentally-friendly method enables the extraction of rapeseed oil from fruit flavor by simple process with high extraction efficiency, safety, purity, and oxidation stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Component: The fruit slice is orange slice, lemon slice, or grapefruit slice. The ratio of rapeseed powder and freeze-dried fruit powder is 15:1-1:1. Preferred Condition: The impurity in the rapeseed in step (i) is less than 10%. The rapeseed peeling power in step (ii) is 800-4000 W, and time is 2-20 minutes. The rapeseed kernel is crushed, and sieved in step (iii) to particle size of 0.5-5 mm. The freeze-dried fruit slices having water content of less than 1% is crushed and sieved to particle size of 0.1-2 mm. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction parameters in step (v) are extraction pressure of 7-30 MPa, extraction temperature of 20-50 degrees C, extraction time of 20-150 minutes, and extraction sequence of 1 to 5 times. The centrifuging speed in step (vi) is 3000-10000 rpm.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)
MC D10-A01; D10-A02

   MURAOKA H
AE MITSUBISHI MATERIALS CORP (MITV-C)
GA 2022A0917N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for recovering valuable metals,                involves heating a battery residue having a                positive electrode active material containing                nickel and/or cobalt and a negative electrode                active material containing graphite, oxidizing                graphite contained in the negative electrode active                material in the battery residue to generate carbon                dioxide, adding sulfuric acid to the residual                powder remaining after the carbon dioxide gas is                generated from the battery residue, and acid                leaching nickel and/or cobalt contained in the                positive electrode active material in the residual                powder into sulfuric acid to produce a leaching                solution.
   USE - The method is used for recovering valuable                metals.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures that nickel and/or cobalt                contained in the battery residue can be efficiently                leached into sulfuric acid and recovered.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device                for recovering valuable metals, comprising a                heating device for heating a battery residue having                a positive electrode active material containing                nickel and/or cobalt and a negative electrode                active material containing graphite, oxidizing                graphite contained in the negative electrode active                material in the battery residue to form carbonic                acid and generating gas, and an acid leaching unit                for adding sulfuric acid to the residual powder                remaining after the carbon dioxide gas is generated                from the battery residue, nickel and/or cobalt                contained in the positive electrode active material                in the residual powder is leached into the sulfuric                acid to form a leachate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  involves recovering nickel and/or cobalt                  contained in the leachate. The recovery step                  involves adding a sulfiding agent to the leachate                  to generate a treatment liquid, filtering the                  treatment liquid to convert the treatment liquid                  into a filtrate, which is a liquid component, and                  separating into a residue that is a solid                  component containing nickel and/or cobalt                  contained in the leachate. The method further                  involves crushing battery waste to produce                  crushed materials, removing aluminum pieces from                  the crushed materials and taking out the battery                  residue, and heating the removed battery residue.                  The acid leaching step involves adding greater                  than or equal to 2 mol/l sulfuric acid to the                  residual powder, heating the leaching solution at                  greater than or equal to 60 degrees C and                  stirring for greater than or equal to 4 hours.                  Preferred Conditions: In the acid leaching step,                  hydrogen peroxide is added to the residual powder                  together with the sulfuric acid. In the heating                  step, the battery residue is heated at greater                  than or equal to 700 degrees C. Preferred Device:                  The device further comprises a recovery device                  for recovering nickel and/or cobalt contained in                  the leachate.

GA 202228233W
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon capture system comprises smoke generated by the combustion of the boiler which enters the condenser 1 for cooling through the induced draft fan, and the cooled flue gas enters the absorption tower from the bottom. The carbon dioxide is captured by the organic amine absorbent in the tower, and the smoke is discharged into the atmosphere. The carbon dioxide-rich absorbent solution is pumped to the regeneration tower to separate the carbon dioxide. The discharged carbon dioxide gas is liquefied by the condenser 3, pressurized and sealed by the pressurizer. The separated solvent is reheated and pressurized by the reboiler, and heat transfer and conversion occurs with the rich liquid in the heat exchanger. The flow of flue gas and the flow of extraction steam entering the entire carbon capture system are controlled by regulating valve 1 and regulating valve 2, respectively.
   USE - Carbon capture system used for improving flexibility of coal-fired unit.

PN CN112107471-A
TI Cardiopulmonary resuscaton control system based on adaptive impedance adjustment, has force sensor and displacement sensor that are utilized to control processor to process signal to obtain elastic modulus value.
AU WANG R
   ZHU M
   ZHU L
   ZHOU Y
   LIU H
AE UNIV JILIN FIRST HOSPITAL (UYJI-C)
GA 2020D00628
AB    NOVELTY - The control system has a cardiopulmonary resuscitation machine based on adaptive impedance adjustment that includes a carbon dioxide sensor, a force sensor, a displacement sensor, a control processor, a fuzzy inference system, an impedance controller and an actuator. The signal collected by the carbon dioxide sensor is transmitted to the control processor for signal analysis and processing to detect the end-breathing carbon dioxide of the patient. The force sensor and displacement sensor are used to detect the pressing force and depth of the patient, and control the processor to process the signal to obtain the elastic modulus value. The input of the fuzzy system is the amount of carbon dioxide at the end of breath and the elastic modulus of the sternum. The parameters are used to adjust the depth deviation during compression to optimize the adjustment of the set compression depth. The compression depth is maintained or reduced to ensure the safety of compression.
   USE - Cardiopulmonary resuscaton control system based on adaptive impedance adjustment.
   ADVANTAGE - The cardiopulmonary resuscaton control system uses human physiological indicators to adjust impedance parameters to achieve the purpose of optimizing and adjusting the pressing depth. The adjustment ability of the cardiopulmonary resuscitation machine has good adaptability. The pressing depth is always kept within a safe range, which protects the patient and has good safety.

PN CN111793218-A; CN111793218-B
TI Preparation of Schiff base dicarboxylic acid ligand zin-copper metal organic framework material used as photocatalyst for photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction involves reacting Schiff base ligand and metal salt, washing, and drying.
AU HU M
   WANG X
   SONG X
AE UNIV QIQIHAR (UYQR-C)
GA 2020A62365
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of Schiff base dicarboxylic acid ligand zin-copper metal organic framework material comprises adding Schiff base dicarboxylic acid ligand and organic medium to PTFE-lined reaction kettle, dissolving zinc nitrate hexahydrate or copper(II) nitrate trihydrate in organic solvent, adding to reaction kettle, adding nitric acid solution, stirring, reacting, cooling to room temperature, filtering, washing collected solid phase with organic solvent for multiple times, soaking in organic solvent, washing, and drying.
   USE - The method is used for preparation of Schiff base dicarboxylic acid ligand zin-copper metal organic framework material as photocatalyst for photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The metal-organic framework photocatalyst has good performance, exhibits good reduction efficiency, and realizes good carbon dioxide photocatalytic reduction performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of Schiff base dicarboxylic acid ligand zin-copper metal organic framework material comprises adding Schiff base dicarboxylic acid ligand and organic medium to PTFE-lined reaction kettle, dissolving zinc nitrate hexahydrate or copper(II) nitrate trihydrate in an organic solvent to obtain a metal salt solution, adding to reaction kettle, adding nitric acid solution, stirring for 0.5-2 hours, reacting at 90-120 degrees C for 18-36 hours, cooling to room temperature, filtering, washing collected solid phase with organic solvent for multiple times, soaking in organic solvent, washing, and drying, where Schiff base dicarboxylic acid ligand is of formulae (I)-(II). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for application of metal organic framework material comprising adding metal organic framework material, sacrificial agent, solvent and deionized water to reaction bottle, bubbling with nitrogen and carbon dioxide, photocatalytic reacting, and illuminating with xenon lamp.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of Schiff base dicarboxylic acid ligand to zinc nitrate hexahydrate or copper(II) nitrate trihydrate is 1:1.5-1:3. The organic medium is N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-diethylformamide, and/or methanol. The organic solvent is dimethylformamide, diethylformamide, methanol, tetrahydrofuran, and/or acetone. The sacrificial agent is triethanolamine and/or triethylamine.

PT P
PN JP2020158979-A
TI Apparatus for use in control system for controlling objects e.g. door, has second control signal which changes target device from second state to first state is generated when condition is satisfied after generating first control signal.
AU TAKAGI Y
AE ASAHI KASEI ELECTRONICS CO LTD (ASAH-C)
GA 202095980N
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (10) has a detector (11) that detects the carbon dioxide concentration that changes with exhaled breath. A signal generation unit (12) generates a signal that controls the state of the target device based on the carbon dioxide concentration detected by the detection unit. A communication unit (13) transmits the signal generated by the signal generation unit to the target device. A first control signal which is a signal for changing the target device from the first state to the second state is generated when the carbon dioxide concentration satisfies the first condition. A second control signal which is a signal for changing the target device from the second state to the first state is generated when the second condition is satisfied after generating the first control signal. The first condition includes the carbon dioxide concentration which is less than the first threshold value becomes equal to or more than the first threshold value.

AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM RES (SNPC-C)
