   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 202068089H
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises an active component, an                auxiliary agent and a carrier. The active component                is one or more of Fe, Co, Ni, V, Mo, Cr, Mn, Cu or                Zn, and the auxiliary agent is selected from group                IA and IIA. One or more of group, rare earth                element, Al, Ti, Zr, Nb, Ga, preferably one or more                of Mg, K, Zr, Ce. The carrier is silicon-based                modified petroleum coke-based activated carbon. The                content of active components is 1-20 wt.%,                preferably 3-15 wt.%, and the content of auxiliary                elements is 1-10 wt.%, preferably 3-8 wt.%. The                content is 70-98 wt.%, preferably 77-94 wt.%.
   USE - Catalyst used in dehydrogenation of low-carbon                alkanes by carbon dioxide to produce low-carbon                olefins (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has good dispersion of active                components, high utilization rate, and high                selectivity of low-carbon olefins.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing a catalyst for preparing low-carbon                olefins by the dehydrogenation of low-carbon                alkanes by carbon dioxide oxidation, which                involves:
   (A) pretreating petroleum coke;
   (B) preparing silicon-based modified petroleum                coke;
   (C) preparing composite oxides containing                active components and additives;
   (D) mixing obtained silicon-based modified                petroleum coke, obtained composite oxide and                activator, after mixing uniformly; and
   (E) washing and drying obtained sample to                obtain a catalyst for the dehydrogenation of carbon                dioxide to oxidize light alkanes to produce light                olefins.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ammonium phosphate                  salt is one ormore of ammonium phosphate,                  ammoniumhydrogen phosphate, and ammonium                  dihydrogenphosphate, preferably ammonium                  phosphate. The water vapor gas is water vapor, or                  a mixed gas of water vapor and carrier gas. The                  carrier gas is nitrogen or inert gas, and the                  inert gas is one or more of helium, neon, argon,                  krypton, and xenon. The surfactant is one or more                  of cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, organic                  dicarboxylic acid and fatty amine, it is one or                  more of cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, glutaric                  acid and dodecylamine. The solvent is one or more                  of hydrochloric acid, absolute ethanol and citric                  acid. The active component soluble salt is one or                  more of nitrate, sulfate, hydrochloride,                  manganate, molybdate and vanadate, preferably                  nitrate, molybdate or vanadate, specifically iron                  nitrate, cobalt nitrate, nickel nitrate, ammonium                  metavanadate, ammonium molybdate, chromium                  nitrate, potassium permanganate, sodium                  permanganate, potassium molybdate, sodium                  molybdate, ammonium molybdate, molybdic acid,                  copper nitrate or zinc nitrate, more preferably                  iron nitrate, cobalt nitrate, nickel nitrate,                  ammonium metavanadate, ammonium molybdate,                  chromium nitrate, potassium molybdate, copper                  nitrate and zinc nitrate. The additive-containing                  metal soluble salt is one or more of nitrate,                  sulfate, hydrochloride, and oxalate, preferably                  nitrate, specifically magnesium nitrate, cerium                  nitrate, potassium nitrate, one or more of                  zirconium nitrate. The organic acid is an organic                  acid containing a carboxyl group, and the organic                  acid containing a carboxyl group is a                  hydroxycarboxylic acid composed of the elements                  C, H, and O, specifically maleic acid, citric                  acid, and one or more of horse acid, more                  preferably citric acid. Preferred Conditions: The                  volume ratio of water vapor to carrier gas in the                  mixed gas is 1:20-1:1, preferably 1:10-1:2. The                  mass ratio of the surfactant, solvent, and                  deionized water is 1:1-10:5-60. The active metal                  crystal grain size is 1.5-5.8nm, preferably                  2.5-5nm. Preferred Process: The pretreatment                  includes introducing ammonium phosphate into                  petroleum coke and then drying, pretreating                  obtained sample with water vapor gas. The                  preparation of the silicon-based modified                  petroleum coke mixing the surfactant, solvent and                  deionized water uniformly to obtain first                  solution. The pretreated petroleum coke,                  silicon-containing compound, and first solution                  are mixed uniformly, and then filtered, washed,                  and dried to obtain silicon-based modified                  petroleum coke. The composite oxide containing                  active components and additives is prepared by                  weiging the active component soluble salt, the                  auxiliary metal soluble salt and deionized water                  to prepare first metal precursor salt solution,                  adding uniformly first metal precursor salt                  solution under stirring conditions to the organic                  acid solution, adjusting the pH to 1-4, and then                  heating at 50-90 degrees C, until the solution                  becomes a viscous colloid, and then drying and                  calcining to obtain a composite oxide. The active                  components and additives are embedded in the                  amorphous defects of petroleum coke-based                  activated carbon and activated carbon graphite                  microchip layer.

   (3) preparation of the metal-supported                catalyst comprising dipping catalyst carrier in                soluble metal salt solution, stirring to dry at                room temperature for 5-15 hours, and calcining at                400-600 degrees C for 1-4 hours in air atmosphere                to obtain metal-supported catalyst, where the metal                soluble salt is chloride, nitrate (preferred),                and/or acetate of corresponding metal; and
   (4) use method of the metal-supported catalyst                comprising reducing supported catalyst at 400-500                degrees C under hydrogen atmosphere for 2-4 hours,                and carrying out conversion reaction on fixed bed                with carbon dioxide and hydrogen as reaction gas at                300-600 degrees C with a pressure of 0.1-3 MPa and                reaction space velocity of 2000-30000/hour, where                the ratio of hydrogen and carbon is 0.5-6.

The metal is nickel and/or cobalt; the                  metal load amount is 2-10 wt.%. Preferred Method:                  The method specifically includes (i) mixing                  anatase in gas treatment device, (ii) introducing                  treatment atmosphere gas at 500-600 degrees C                  with gas velocity of 10-100 ml/g anatase/minute,                  preferably 30-60 ml/g anatase/minute for 2-6                  hours, and performing high temperature treatment                  at 300-600 degrees C for 0.5-48 hours, and (iii)                  cooling helium gas to room temperature to obtain                  anatase catalyst carrier.

PN KR2020036416-A
TI Forward osmosis desalination method, comprises e.g. separating seawater with induced solution and recovering derived solution, and separating induced solution into fresh water and induced solute gas with plant's warm wastewater.
AU KANG S
   CHANGDEOKSU
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 2020285181
AB    NOVELTY - Forward osmosis desalination method, comprises (a) separating seawater with an induced solution and recovering the derived solution, (b) separating the induced solution into fresh water and an induced solute gas by exchanging the derived solution recovered in step (a) with the plant's warm wastewater, (c) separating the solute gas into ammonia and carbon dioxide, and (d) mixing power plant on-stage drainage of step (b) and the ammonia of step (c) and the off-gas of the power plant to form an induction solution.
   USE - The method and the system are useful for forward osmosis desalination (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method and the system: recycle collected carbon dioxide for preparation of a forward osmosis induction solution, carbonate mineralization, microalgal culture, organic acid or syngas production; capture carbon dioxide contained in the exhaust gas of a power plant e.g. coal-fired power plants, thus carbon dioxide emission can be reduced; and recover fresh water which can be used for drinking water, domestic water, and industrial water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for forward osmosis desalination system comprising forward osmosis module comprising an osmosis membrane, heat exchanger, gas separator, fluid circulation transport, power plant flue gas injection line, and induction solution supply unit, where the fluid circulation transfer path is to connect the induction solution supply unit, forward osmosis module, heat exchanger and gas separator, and the power plant flue gas injection line is connected to the induction solution supply.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The induction solution of step (a) is aqueous solution including ammonium carbonate, ammonium bicarbonate and/or ammonium carbamate. In step (b), the power plant warm water is discharged from a power plant selected from a thermal power plant and/or a nuclear power plant. In the step (d), the power plant flue gas includes at least one carbon dioxide, nitrogen, nitrogen oxides and sulfur oxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The step (c) uses a gas separation membrane comprising a material made of polyethylene, polyamide, polyimide, cellulose acetate, polysulfone, polydimethylsiloxane, polystyrene, polyvinyl acetate, silicone or ceramic to separate the solute gas.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Method for development of residual oil zones resource, involves quantitatively and qualitatively evaluating microbial self limitation conditions in residual oil zone (ROZ) through multiple processes, and selecting media in ROZ to be sampled.
AU VANCE D B
AE ARCADIS CORP SERVICES INC (ARCA-Non-standard)
GA 202018740Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves quantitatively and qualitatively evaluating the microbial self limitation conditions in a ROZ through multiple processes. The media is selected in the ROZ to be sampled, implementing sampling protocols and analytical techniques, and generating data relating to the conditions in the ROZ. The economics and operations associated with development of the ROZ are determined, and the ROZ is developed if the economics indicate that the ROZ is developed in predetermined economic standards of operation. The selected media comprises gas, water, petroleum, mineral matrix and their combinations. The physical chemical characterizations are based on available thermodynamic data available for multi-phase systems. The local and regional geothermal gradients that govern formation temperatures differentiate between thermogenic and biogenic sulfate reduction.
   USE - Method for development of residual oil zones resource.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures efficient identification and assessment of ROZ that significantly increases the geographic footprint and target locations into which carbon dioxide is injected and ultimately utilized and permanently sequestered in commercial operations generating value to partially offset the parasitic costs associated with the capture of anthropogenic carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for assessment, management and monitoring of the sequestration of carbon dioxide associated with carbon dioxide enhanced oil recovery operations; and
   (2) a method for creation and management of database of ROZ and associated microbial self limitation conditions.

AE CARRIER CORP (CARG-C)

GA 201995321L
AB    NOVELTY - The container (300) has a generator (304) for generating gas pressure to balance vacuum pressure that accumulates over time in the container. The generator is automatically activated after sensing oxygen levels in the container reaching a maximum allowable set point for storing the agricultural commodities, and generates inert gas, and nitrogen gas. A carbon dioxide scrubber (120) that automatically activates after sensing carbon dioxide levels in the container reaching a maximum allowable set point for storing the agricultural commodities. The scrubber and the generator are operated independently.
   USE - Atmospherically controlled container e.g. freight container and refrigerated freight container for shipping agricultural commodities e.g. carbon dioxide scrubber (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The container provides flow of generated gas to prevent air from outside the unsealed container from being drawn in and increasing available oxygen stored within the container to increase both market quality and shelf life such commodities can be stored in a low temperature high humidity environment, and eventually lead to loss of oxygen control in a controlled atmosphere system by storing such commodities at low temperature, so that respiration can be reduced and senescence is delayed, thus extending storage life.

PN RU2698200-C1
TI METHANOL SYNTHESIS METHOD consists in supply of synthesis gas with circulating gas for compression and contacting in reactor with copper-zinc catalyst.
AU AFANASEV S V
   TROFIMOV D I
   ROSHCHENKO A I
AE AFANASEV S V (AFAN-Individual)
GA 2019744260
AB    NOVELTY - Present invention relates to base organic synthesis, in particular to a method of producing methanol. Method consists in supply of synthesis gas with circulating gas for compression and contacting in reactor with copper-zinc catalyst at temperature of 220290C, followed by cooling of methanol-containing gas leaving the reactor and separation of raw methanol in the separator. At that, compression at volume rate of 3,00015,000 h-1 synthesis gas is fed with composition, vol%: hydrogen 62.075.0; argon 0.040.20; nitrogen 0.073.0; methane 0.84.0; carbon monoxide 7.820.0; carbon dioxide 3.711, obtained by steam-carbon dioxide conversion of natural gas in volume ratio methane: carbon dioxide = (350):1, contacting with catalyst is carried out at pressure of 212 MPa in reactor, divided into two isolated parts in ratio of 3:1 by number of sections, wherein the circulation gas is divided into two streams, and one of the two circulating gas streams is pre-mixed with the synthesis gas before entering the three-section catalyst reactor zone, and other flow of circulating gas is enriched with input of carbon dioxide to achieve volume ratio (550):(0.52) and is passed through built-in heat exchanger into second part of reactor with one catalyst section, followed by extraction of methanol from two processing lines.
   USE - Technological processes; biochemistry.
   ADVANTAGE - Disclosed method increases the methanol removal from the volume of the loaded catalyst and prolongs the service life of the catalyst system.

PN RU2694371-C1
TI METHOD OF ASSESSING THE GRADIENT OF TOXIC IMPURITIES IN AIRCRAFT PRESSURIZED CABIN AIR AND THE DEVICE FOR ITS IMPLEMENTATION.
AU MOGILNIKOV V P
   IONOV A V
   FROLKINA L V
AE GROMOV FLIGHT RES INST (GROM-Non-standard)
GA 201971429A
AB    NOVELTY - Group of inventions relates to ecology and analytical chemistry and can be used to assess the gradient of toxic impurities in aircraft hermobaths air. For this purpose one-step injection into carbon dioxide gas conditioning system is performed in concentration below permissible limit, 0.30.5 vol. %, for one minute with subsequent assessment of its concentration in several points of aircraft cabin. After the carbon dioxide gas is injected, a single-stage air sampling of the cabin is performed over the selected section of the grid, containing numbered sampling units, each consisting of two syringe samplers with a check valve and a syringe-pneumatic actuator placed along the selected section of the grid. In order to reduce sample losses, opening of the check valve of the analyzed syringe takes place at the moment of connection of the syringe sampler to the dosing valve of the chromatograph. Then, distribution of air flows inside the cabin is evaluated on the gradient of carbon dioxide concentration and stagnant zones of the cabin are identified, where these concentrations are maximum. Also disclosed is an air sampling device, comprising medical disposable syringes, which are combined into working sampling units of three and installed in one plane on a special plank-holder and attached together by rod ends.
   USE - Chemistry; ecology.
   ADVANTAGE - Group of inventions makes it possible to estimate gradient of toxic impurities in air of air-cocks of aircrafts due to single-stage automatic sampling of air contaminated with carbon dioxide in selected sections of cabin. 2 cl, 2 dwg

TI Electrode useful for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide, is prepared by pretreating substrate for degreasing and removing impurities and immersing pretreated substrate material in anode chamber of H-type electrolytic cell as anode.
AU QIU Y
   ZHANG H
   ZHANG T
   LI X
   YAO P
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201953300S
AB    NOVELTY - Electrode, is claimed. The surface catalyst particles of the electrode are distributed on the substrate in the form of nano-array perpendicular to the surface of the substrate, the nanowires have a length of 1-10 mu m, preferably a length of 2-6 mu m, nanowire has a diameter of 30-300 nm, preferably diameter of 50-150 nm, the distribution density of the nanowires is 0.5-10x 108/cm2 , and the distribution density is preferably 1-5x 108/cm2.
   USE - The electrode is useful for electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has shortened preparation cycle, diameter and length of catalyst in nano-array of prepared electrode can be flexibly regulated by adjusting anodization parameters and meets the needs of carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction technology to expand the reaction area of the electrode.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the electrode using electrochemical anodization comprising pretreating the substrate for degreasing and removing impurities, immersing the pretreated substrate material in anode chamber of H-type electrolytic cell as anode, adding an anodizing solution into the anode chamber, placing the cathode in cathode chamber and electrochemically anodizing in a constant current mode to obtain electrode having nano-array surface, washing the electrode with water to surface without anodizing solution, and drying under inert atmosphere; and
   (2) use of electrode in electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide and is mainly directed to a reaction system in which the reduction product is HCOOH or CO.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electrode substrate material is a metal piece, and the metal includes one of copper, indium, tin, silver, gold and palladium. The purity of the metal substrate is greater than or equal to 99.5% and the thickness of the substrate is 0.5-2 mm. The H-type electrolytic cell, intermediate separator between anode chamber and the cathode chamber of the electrolytic cell is an anion exchange membrane for performing anodization electrochemistry. The anodizing solution in the anode chamber is an alkali metal chloride solution and a cationic surfactant is added. The solution in the cathode chamber is an alkali metal hydroxide solution. The method during electrochemical anodization alkali metal chloride in the anode chamber, the alkali metal is lithium, sodium, potassium, and cesium. The alkali metal in the alkali metal chloride in the cathode chamber is lithium, sodium and potassium. The method of electrochemical anodization, the concentration of the alkali metal chloride solution in the anode chamber is 0.2-0.5 M, preferably 0.5-1.2 mm, the concentration of the alkali metal hydroxide solution in the cathode chamber is 0.1-2 M, preferably 0.3-1.5 mm. The hydroxide ion conductivity of the anion exchange membrane is greater than or equal to 0.01 Scm-1. The electrochemically anodized cationic surfactants are quaternary amino groups, including behenyl ammonium chloride, octadecyl trimethyl ammonium chloride, octadecyl trimethyl ammonium bromide, hexadecyl trimethyl ammonium (CTAB), cetyltrimethylammonium chloride (CTAC), cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, tetradecyl chloride, tetraalkyltrimethylammonium bromide compound, tetramethylammonium bromide and trimethylammonium chloride. The concentration of the cationic surfactant in the anode compartment is from 0.5-10 mM, preferably from 1-5 mM. Preferred Method: The electrochemical anodization current density is 0.5-10 mA cm2, preferably the control current density is 1-5 mA cm2. The electrochemical anodization time is 5-40 minutes, preferably 10-30 minutes, electrochemical anodization is carried out at a constant temperature of 40-85 degrees C, during the anodization, the oxidizing solution was stirred at a stirring speed of 300-800 rotations/minute. The base material degreasing process is immersed in a polar solvent at room temperature for at least 30 minutes. The polar solvent is anhydrous ethanol and acetone. The de-impurity treatment includes chemical treatment or electrochemical treatment. The chemical treatment is immersed in a concentrated hydrochloric acid solution having a mass concentration of 36-38%, the immersion time is 5-30 minutes, and after being cleaned it is blown dry in an inert atmosphere, the electrochemical treatment is performed by using phosphoric acid having a mass concentration of 85% as treatment liquid, and the treatment potential is 1-4 V with respect to the saturated calomel electrode, preferably the treatment potential is 2-3V and the treatment temperature is room temperature.

A dehumidifier sleeve encloses the filter body. The dehumidifier sleeve evaporates liquid absorbed by the filter body. The capillary path comprises a set of separate capillary channels formed in the filter body around the through-channel.
   USE - Bi-functional filter device for use in a gas sampling line (claimed) for transferring gas samples e.g. air with high concentration level of carbon dioxide exhaled by subject, to gas analyzers, a filter and a dehumidifier.

PN CN109628182-A
TI Method for purifying biogas by pressure water washing, involves closing solenoid valve to continue enriching biogas, collecting carbon dioxide, and turning off vacuum pump and other solenoid valve.
AU LI Y
   CHEN W
   GAO S
   XU H
   ZHANG C
AE ANHUI TIANMA ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2019379835
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves injecting (S1) water inside the water storage tank into the interior of the water washing tower through the water pump. The first solenoid valve and compressor are turned on (S2). The biogas is cooled by the cold dryer and delivered (S3) to the inside of the water washing tower. The electromagnetic valve is opened (S4) to collect biogas, when the methane concentration is lower than 85%. The solenoid valve is closed to continue enriching the biogas. The carbon dioxide is collected, when the carbon dioxide concentration is less than 10%. The vacuum pump and other solenoid valve are turned off (S5) in turn until the carbon dioxide concentration is again higher than 40%. The water washing is circulated (S6).
   USE - Method for purifying biogas by pressure water washing.
   ADVANTAGE - The concentration of methane in the biogas can not be greatly increased, and the high-pressure biogas is cooled by the cooperation of the cold dryer to avoid the high temperature of the biogas, thus reducing the water washing efficiency of the biogas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the flowchart illustrating the method for purifying biogas by pressure water washing. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for injecting water (S1)
   Step for turning on first solenoid valve and compressor (S2)
   Step for delivering biogas (S3)
   Step for opening electromagnetic valve (S4)
   Step for turning off vacuum pump and other solenoid valve (S5)
   Step for circulating water washing (S6)

TI Capnograph system for detecting spontaneous patient breaths, has ventilation system provided with gas source, monitoring circuit comprising processing component that generates pressure signal responsive to pressure detected in air path.
AU CHAPMAN F W
AE PHYSIO-CONTROL INC (PHYS-Non-standard)
GA 201846714W
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a ventilation system (101) provided with a gas source (120) to expel repeated bursts (124) of gas. An airway tube defines an air path (149) that communicates with the gas source. The airway tube is inserted in an airway of a patient to guide bursts of the gas as artificial inhalations to a lung (118) of the patient. A capnography module (160) comprises a carbon dioxide detector (168) to detect amount of carbon dioxide within the air path. A monitoring circuit (170) comprises a processing component (172) within the capnography module that is distinct from the carbon dioxide detector. The processing component generates a pressure signal responsive to the pressure detected in the air path by the monitoring circuit.
   USE - Capnograph system for detecting spontaneous patient breaths.
   ADVANTAGE - The system allows a pump to work momentarily less hard to maintain same rate of operation, thus momentarily drawing less current. The system utilizes an inflow valve to be closed to block an air path, thus preventing the bursts of gas from entering the lungs until a negative airway pressure threshold is met or exceeded.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for utilizing a capnograph system with a ventilation system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of components of a capnograph system and of a ventilation system.

   Gas source (120)

   Air path (149)
   Capnography module (160)
   Carbon dioxide detector (168)

   Processing component (172)

PN CN107970908-A
TI Zinc oxide-based carbon dioxide reduction electro-catalyst prepared by e.g. dissolving zinc ion soluble salt in deionized water, dissolving potassium hydroxide in deionized water and adding zinc salt solution to potassium hydroxide solution.
AU LU Y
   JIANG Q
   YANG W
   CHEN X
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 201835207S
AB    NOVELTY - Zinc oxide-based carbon dioxide reduction electro-catalyst is claimed. The chemical composition of the electro-catalyst is zinc oxide, and its appearance is dandelion-type particles with particle size of 2.0-3.4 mu m and the hexagonal prismatic zinc oxide constituting dandelion-type particles has a diameter of 60-90 nm and a length of 1.0-1.7 mu m.
   USE - Used as zinc oxide-based carbon dioxide reduction electro-catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The electro-catalyst: has small Tafel slope, low starting potential, excellent stability, high selectivity, simple preparation process and low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing zinc oxide-based carbon dioxide reduction electro-catalyst, comprising (i) dissolving zinc ion soluble salt in deionized water to obtain zinc salt solution having a concentration of 0.4-0.6 mol/l; dissolving potassium hydroxide in deionized water to obtain potassium hydroxide solution having a concentration of 3.5-4.5 mol/l, then stirring at a rate of 500-700 revolutions/minute and adding zinc salt solution to potassium hydroxide solution, where the mass ratio between zinc ions and hydroxide ion is 1:8, and (ii) placing suspension in a closed container and reacting at 40-60 degrees C for 10-15 hours, washing the precipitate with absolute ethanol and deionized water, then stopping washing when the pH of centrifugal liquid reaches 7-8, vacuum drying at 60-80 degrees C for 8-10 hours to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the soluble salt of zinc ion is zinc nitrate hexahydrate, zinc acetate and/or zinc chloride.

TI Preparing seasoning oil by frying onion and welsh onion in butter, adding e.g. chili, bean sauce, salt, chicken essence, ginger, garlic, glutinous rice and white wine, frying and extracting mixture using supercritical carbon dioxide.
AU DONG Z
   XIE Z
   QU M
   YANG G
AE CHONGQING MEIXIANGYUAN IND GROUP CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 2018206012
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing seasoning oil involves (a) heating butter, frying onion and welsh onion head in butter, adding chili, bean sauce, salt, monosodium glutamate, chicken essence, white sugar, ginger, garlic and pepper, mixing and frying mixture, adding glutinous rice, white wine and compound spices, frying mixture and filtering fried materials, and (b) adding butter to product obtained in step (a), extracting mixture, filtering extract, extracting filter residues using supercritical carbon dioxide, and mixing extract with filtrate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing seasoning oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves taste of the seasoning oil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing seasoning oil involves (a) frying dried chili at 100-120 degrees C for 40 minutes, mincing fried chili, heating butter at 110-120 degrees C, frying onion and welsh onion head in butter, adding chili, frying mixture for 5-6 times, adding bean sauce, salt, monosodium glutamate, chicken essence, white sugar, ginger, garlic and pepper, uniformly mixing and frying mixture for 7-8 times, adding glutinous rice, white wine and compound spices, frying mixture and filtering fried materials, and (b) adding 10 %mass butter to product obtained in step (a), mixing and extracting mixture at 130-140 degrees C and 0.3-0.6 MPa for 25-35 minutes, filtering extract, extracting filter residues at 20-25 degrees C for 50-70 minutes using supercritical carbon dioxide, where carbon dioxide flow rate is 24-30 kg/hour, and mixing extract with filtrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The hotpot oil is prepared using 38-45 pts. wt. butter, 0.5-1 pt. wt. onion, 0.5-1 pt. wt. welsh onion, 10-14 pts. wt. dried chili, 6-8 pts. wt. bean sauce, 5-7 pts. wt. ginger, 7-10 pts. wt. garlic, 3-4 pts. wt. pepper, 10-12 pts. wt. compound spices, 0.5-1 pt. wt. salt, 1-2 pts. wt. monosodium glutamate, 3-4 pts. wt. chicken essence, 0.5-0.8 pt. wt. white sugar, 1.2-1.8 pts. wt. glutinous rice and 2-2.5 pts. wt. white wine. The compound spices comprise 8-10 pts. wt. Cassia bark, 4-5 pts. wt. dried orange peels, 10-14 pts. wt. aniseed, 14-18 pts. wt. Amomum tsao-ko fruit and 20-25 pts. wt. thyme.

   ZHOU X
AE CHONGQING CHANGAN IND GROUP CO LTD (CHON-C)
GA 201821013G
AB    NOVELTY - Laser sealing a reserve lithium battery liquid bottle comprises turning on a carbon dioxide laser welding machine main power; starting a laser, and focusing lens in a workpiece; injecting 0.15-0.2 mL thionyl chloride solution in a reservoir; loading liquid bottles on a workbench, and sealing; disconnecting the carbon dioxide laser welding machine main power; and soaking the sealed fluid bottles in hot water at 80-90 degrees C, observing if a stored thionyl chloride solution reacts with water, taking out unreacted bottles after 5-10 minutes, wiping with a paper towel, and testing using pH test paper.
   USE - Method for laser sealing a reserve lithium battery liquid bottle.

TI Extracting nano-pearl comprises e.g. grinding pearl, grinding pearl, filtering solid-liquid mixture, mixing reaming pearl powder and acetic acid-sodium acetate buffer solution, centrifuging, concentrating and drying.
AU GU W
AE GUANGZHOU BIAO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201815046P
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting nano-pearl comprises e.g. (i) grinding pearl particles less than100 nm or 100 nm obtain pearl powder; (ii) mixing the pearl powder with aqueous ethanol solution in a tank reactor, introducing carbon dioxide gas, maintaining partial pressure of carbon dioxide and reaming; (iii) filtering solid-liquid mixture; (iv) washing filter cake with water and drying; (v) mixing reaming pearl powder and acetic acid-sodium acetate buffer solution in a tank reactor, extracting and centrifuging; and (vi) mixing supernatant and filtrate from step (iii), concentrating and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting nano-pearl.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting nano-pearl comprises (i) grinding pearl particles less than100 nm or 100 nm obtain pearl powder; (ii) mixing the pearl powder with aqueous ethanol solution containing 30-50 wt.% ethanol in a tank reactor, introducing carbon dioxide gas, maintaining partial pressure of carbon dioxide at 0.3-1.5 Mpa and reaming at -5 to 10 degrees C for 1-10 hours; (iii) filtering the solid-liquid mixture after reaming process to obtain a filtrate and a residue; (iv) washing filter cake with water and drying to obtain reaming pearl powder; (v) mixing reaming pearl powder and acetic acid-sodium acetate buffer solution with pH value 2-5 in a tank reactor, extracting and centrifuging to obtain the supernatant; and (vi) mixing supernatant and filtrate from step (iii), concentrating and drying INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) nano pearl, extracted as mentioned above; and
   (2) cosmetic comprises nano pearl extract.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The solid-liquid weight ratio of pearl powder to the ethanol aqueous solution in step (ii) is 1:0.3-5, the weight of the ethanol in the aqueous ethanol solution is 35-40 wt .%, partial pressure of carbon dioxide is 0.5-0.7 Mpa and reaming is carried out at preferably -1 to 4 degrees C. The pH value of the sodium acetate-sodium acetate buffer solution in step (v) is preferably 3-4. The sodium acetate-sodium acetate buffer solution comprises plum extract, where plum extract accounts for 0.1-1 wt.% of acid-sodium acetate buffer solution. The cosmetic is in the form of face wash, a bath, a shampoo, a lotion, a perfume, a cleansing water, a makeup remover, an essence, a stock solution, a honey, creams, hair creams, foundation creams, shampoos, concealers, cosmetic box, lipstick, hair wax, cleansing oil, emollient oil, polish and essence oil. Preferred Method: The drying in step (iv) and (vi) is carried out at 35-60 degrees C for 1-12 hours. The extraction in step (v) is carried out at 35-60 degrees C for 2-48 hours, where liquid to solid weight ratio of acetic acid-sodium acetate buffer solution and reaming pearl powder is preferably (1-5): 1 and pressure is the autogenous pressure in kettle reactor.

An air side of a main heat exchanger (13) is connected with a medium-pressure column (15). PN CN109749986-A; WO2020182190-A1; CN109749986-B; US2022186188-A1
TI Obtaining pancreatic precursor cells and islet            beta-cells by differentiation of human pluripotent stem            cells by culturing human pluripotent stem cells in            endoderm stage medium and continuing culturing in            pancreatic precursor medium.
AU JIANG W
   TAN M
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
   ASIA REGENERATIVE MEDICINE LTD (ASRE-Non-standard)
   ASIA REGENERATIVE MEDICINE LTD (ASRE-Non-standard)
GA 201944470U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for obtaining pancreatic precursor                cells and islet beta -cells by differentiation of                human pluripotent stem cells involves formulating                endoderm stage medium, culturing the human                pluripotent stem cells in the carbon dioxide                incubator using the endoderm stage medium,                preparing the pancreatic precursor cell culture                medium, replacing with the pancreatic precursor                cell culture medium, culturing, changing the                medium, preparing islet beta cell culture medium,                replacing with the islet beta cell culture medium                and culturing cells.
   USE - The method is useful for obtaining pancreatic                precursor cells and islet beta -cells by                differentiation of human pluripotent stem                cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces large number of islet beta                cells, which provides strong support for diabetes                cell therapy, drug screening and disease                model.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for obtaining pancreatic precursor                cells and islet beta -cells by differentiation of                human pluripotent stem cells involves (a) (i)                formulating endoderm stage medium (M1) and                culturing the human pluripotent stem cells in the                carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for 1 day                using the endoderm stage medium (M1) and (ii)                formulating endoderm stage medium (M2), replacing                the medium (M1) with medium (M2), culturing the                human pluripotent stem cells in the carbon dioxide                incubator at 37 degrees C for 3 days using the                endoderm stage medium (M2), and then changing the                medium every day, (b) preparing the pancreatic                precursor cell culture medium, replacing the cells                obtained in the step (a) with the pancreatic                precursor cell culture medium, culturing in a 37                degrees C carbon dioxide incubator for 5 days, and                changing the medium every day and (c) (i) preparing                islet beta cell culture medium (I1), replacing the                cells obtained in the step (b) with the islet beta                cell culture medium (I1), culturing in a 37 degrees                C carbon dioxide incubator for 5 days, and changing                the medium every day and (ii) preparing islet beta                cell culture medium (I2), replacing the cells                obtained in the step (b) with the islet beta cell                culture medium (I2), culturing in a 37 degrees C                carbon dioxide incubator for 5 days, and changing                the medium every day. The endoderm stage medium                (M1) is prepared by mixing the Iscove's Modified                Dulbecco's Media (IMDM) medium and nutrient mixture                F-12 (F12) medium in a ratio of 1:1 to obtain basic                medium and adding 0.2% bovine serum albumin (BSA),                1% penicillin, 1% streptomycin, 100 ng/ml                recombinant human activin-A (Activin A), 50 ng/ml                Wnt3a protein. The endoderm stage medium (M2) is                prepared by mixing the IMDM medium and nutrient                mixture F-12 (F12) medium in a ratio of 1:1 to                obtain basic medium and adding 0.2% BSA, 1%                penicillin, 1% streptomycin and 100 ng/ml                recombinant human activin-A. The pancreatic                precursor cell culture medium comprises MCDB 131                medium-based medium supplemented with 0.5% BSA, 1.5                g/l sodium bicarbonate, 1X valley GlutaMAX (RTM:                L-glutamine alternative), 10 mM glucose, 0.25 M                vitamin C, 1X                insulin-transferrin-selenium-ethanolamine additive                (ITS-X), 50 ng/ml fibroblast growth factor (KGF),                0.5 mu M SANT1 (inhibitor target is smo), 100 nM                retinoic acid (TTNPB), 500 nM phorbol                12,13-dibutyrate (PDBu), 2 mu M ALK inhibitor                (K02288) and WNT signaling pathway inhibitor. The                islet beta cell culture medium (M1) comprises                MCDB131 medium-based medium supplemented with 20 mM                glucose, 2% BSA, 1.5 g/l sodium bicarbonate, 1X                GlutaMAX (RTM: L-glutamine alternative), 0.05 mM                vitamin C, 1X ITS-X, 2 mu M ALK inhibitor (K02288),                1 mu M triiodothyronine (T3 ), 10 mu M YO-01027                (Notch signaling pathway inhibitor), 10 mu M zinc                sulfate. The islet beta cell culture medium (M2)                comprises MCDB131 medium-based medium supplemented                with 20 mM glucose, 2% BSA, 1.5 g/l sodium                bicarbonate, 1X GlutaMAX (RTM: L-glutamine                alternative), 0.05 mM vitamin C, 1X ITS-X, 2 mu M                ALK inhibitor (K02288), 1 mu M triiodothyronine (T3                ), 10 mu M YO-01027 (Notch signaling pathway                inhibitor), 10 mu M zinc sulfate. The composition                of the islet ? cell culture medium 2 is: MCDB131                medium-based medium, and further includes the                following working concentration components: 20 mM                glucose, 2% bovine serum albumin (BSA), 1.5 g/L                sodium bicarbonate, 1X GlutaMAX (RTM: L-glutamine                alternative), 0.05 mM vitamin C, 1X ITS-X, 1 mu M                triiodothyronine (T3), 10 mu M ALK5 inhibitor, 10                mu M vitamin E, 10 mu g/ml sodium heparin, 2 mu M                R428 (Axl inhibitor), 10 mu M zinc sulfate and 10                mM N-acetyl-L-cysteine (N-cys).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Cell: The human pluripotent stem                  cell is a human embryonic stem cell. The human                  pluripotent stem cell is a human induced                  pluripotent stem cell. Preferred Components: The                  WNT signaling pathway inhibitor is XAV-939 or                  IWR-1, preferably XAV-939 and has concentration                  of 2 mu M.

PT P
PN WO2019053750-A1; CA3075385-A1; KR2020055748-A; SG11202002031-A1; US2020216760-A1; CN111356540-A; EP3681651-A1; BR112020005026-A2; JP2020534153-W; RU2766001-C2; RU2020112988-A
TI Waste conversion system for conversion of organic            fractions of municipal and wider community waste to            fuels for use in transportation, with all solid, has            carbon dioxide inlet which is provided within lower            portion of scrubbing unit.
AU INGOLFSSON O
   MATTHIASSON A
   INGOLFSSON S
AE LIFDISILL EHF (LIFD-Non-standard)
   YMIR TECHNOLOGIES EHF (YMIR-Non-standard)
   YMIR TECHNOLOGIES EHF (YMIR-Non-standard)
   YMIR TECHNOLOGIES EHF (YMIR-Non-standard)
   LIFDISILL EHF (LIFD-Non-standard)
GA 201926162W
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a pre-treatment unit (1000) for                receiving a stream of organic waste. The                pretreatment unit has continuous-flow steam                explosion reactor. A separation unit (2000)                receives a stream of pre-treated waste from                pre-treatment unit. The separation unit has                conditioning section and separation section for the                separation of conditioned waste into fat/oil                component and aqueous component. The biodiesel                production unit (3000,4000) generates biodiesel                from the fat or oil component. The digestion unit                is provided for anaerobic digestion and                fermentation of the aqueous component. A composting                unit (5000) is provided for the generation of                compost from solid organic material. The carbon                dioxide scrubbing unit has carbon dioxide inlet                which is provided within a lower portion of the                scrubbing unit. The alkaline stream of material                from the high-pressure retention section meets                carbon dioxide rich stream to facilitate carbon                dioxide scrubbing.
   USE - Waste conversion system for conversion of                organic fractions of municipal and wider community                waste such as household waste, slaughterhouse                waste, food industry waste, fish industry waste,                waste from vegetable oil and fish oil industry,                sewage sludge, sewage grease and oils, agricultural                waste such as wheat-straw or other straw, rice                husk, soybean curd residue, and grass and animal                manure, garden waste, and waste wood (all claimed)                to fuels such as biodiesel and methane and ethanol                for use in transportation.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is energy self sufficient. The                process does not require external sources of                energy. The system provides a complete treatment                cycle for organic waste in short, minimizes or                eliminates downstream waste expenditure. The system                is run entirely energy self-sufficient and offers                flexibility, such that the system accommodate very                different waste streams, and does not require high                temperature gasification. The efficiency is                increased and energy demands as compared to                batch-operated steam explosion are reduced. The                system is designed to maximize synergy between                individual components beyond the current state of                the art, to minimize energy consumption and carbon                footprint and maximize environmental and economic                benefit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a process                for treating and converting waste involves                receiving a stream of organic waste. The stream of                waste or portion is introduced into a wetting and                mixing section. The stream is transferred from                wetting and mixing section into a continuous-flow                steam explosion reactor and subjecting it to steam                explosion treatment. The stream of waste treated in                steam explosion reactor is directed to a separation                unit, and the pre-treated waste stream is separated                into component comprising fat and/or oil, a                component comprising aqueous slurry of organic                matter, and a component comprising wet solid                organic material. The fat and oil component are                introduced into a biodiesel production unit. The                biodiesel is generated from fat/oil component. The                screw conveyor and disc centrifuge are each                independently rotatable around the axial bearing                shaft. The biodiesel reactor unit is a                continuous-flow reactor, adapted to receive a                continuous flow fat/oil component and generate a                continuous flow of biodiesel. The component                comprising aqueous slurry is introduced into a                digestion and fermentation unit and component is                digested and fermented through anaerobic digestion                and fermentation. The biodiesel unit accommodates                as input either or both of triglycerides and free                fatty acids. The catalyst catalyzes                transesterification of glycerides and                esterification of free fatty acids. The                purification and water removal is effectuated                through flash evaporation and ion exchange between                esterification and transesterification reactors.                The component comprising wet solid material is                introduced into a composting unit. The conditioning                material is fed from the pre-treatment unit in a                condition section and mixed with acidic solution to                lower the pH of the material. The purification and                water removal is effectuated through flash                evaporation and ion exchange between esterification                and transesterification reactors aeration in                proximity to the bottom of the chamber, which are                preferably air nozzles connected to pressurized                air. The cooling substrate in carbon dioxide                scrubbing section with heat-exchange cooling                elements recovers heat from the steam explosion                section and preferably uses the heat-exchange                cooling elements to pre-heat water for a steam                boiler of the system. The component is fed at a                temperature in the range of about 40-50 degrees to                an initiation section of unit to accelerate the                process by subjecting the substrate directly to                thermophile digestion and bypassing mesophilic                stage composting. The esterification and trans                esterification is promoted through the induction of                turbulent flow by static mixers placed between the                contact plates in the respective reaction zones.                The carbon-rich stock is selected from easily                digestible carbon rich waste such as fruit, dough                and glycerol preferably from the biodiesel                production of the process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the                waste conversion system.
   Pre-treatment unit (1000)
   Separation unit (2000)
   Biodiesel production units (3000,4000)
   Composting unit (5000)
   Rendering units (6000)

PN CN114381030-A
TI Foamable polymerthacrylimide precursor bead useful for e.g. preparing polymerthacrylimide foam product comprises oil phase and aqueous phase, where oil phase comprises methacrylic acid or acrylic acid, methacrylonitrile or acrylonitrile, crosslinking agent and initiator.
AU SHI X
   GAO Y
   LI S
   LIU W
   ZHANG G
AE UNIV NORTHWESTERN POLYTECHNICAL (UNWP-C)
GA 202260458K
AB    NOVELTY - Foamable polymerthacrylimide (PMI) precursor bead comprises oil phase and aqueous phase in a mass ratio of 10-35%. The oil phase comprises 40-65 wt.% main monomer M1, 30-60 wt.% main monomer M2, 0-10 wt.% crosslinking agent and 0.01-0.5 wt.% initiator. The ratio of each component weight ratio accounting for deionized water in the aqueous phase comprises 10-40 wt.% water-soluble inorganic salt, 0.05-3 wt.% dispersant, and 0.02-1 wt.% surfactant. The main monomer M1 comprises methacrylic acid or acrylic acid. The main monomer M2 comprises methacrylonitrile or acrylonitrile. The diameter of the foamable PMI precursor beads is 0.8-12 mm, and in-mold foaming is performed at a temperature of 160-240degreesC.
   USE - The bead is useful for preparing PMI foam product in automobile industry, aerospace, medical appliance, rail traffic, and wind power generation, aerospace, traffic transportation, wind power blade, medical appliances, and sports device.
   ADVANTAGE - The bead: has excellent foam mechanical property and heat resistance, and high foaming rate, particle size, bonding property between particles, and foam performance, and can be prepared in an environmental friendly manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the foamable PMI precursor beads based on an aqueous suspension method and supercritical (SC)-carbon dioxide, comprising (i) uniformly mixing 40-70 wt.% main monomer M1, 30-60 wt.% main monomer M2, 0-10 wt.% cross-linking agent and 0.01-0.5 wt.% initiator to obtain an oil phase, (ii) uniformly mixing 10-40 wt.% water-soluble inorganic salt, 0.05-3 wt.% dispersant, 0.02-1 wt.% surfactant and deionized water (remaining amount) to obtain aqueous phase, (iii) pouring the aqueous phase into a reaction kettle, stirring, controlling the temperature of the reaction kettle at 25-45degrees Celsius, adding the oil phase to the reaction kettle, continuously stirring for 5-60 minutes, increasing the reaction temperature to 45-75degreesC for 3-20 hours, subsequently raising the reaction temperature to 75-95degreesC for aging treatment for 1 hour, and naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain polymer beads, (iv) filtering the reacted polymer beads through a metal mesh, discarding the agglomerated material, cleaning, and drying to obtain PMI precursor beads, where the diameter of the PMI precursor beads is 0.5-10 mm, and (v) placing the PMI precursor beads in a supercritical carbon dioxide autoclave, adding SC-carbon dioxide co-foaming agent to the high pressure kettle, keeping the pressure in the high pressure kettle at 8-30 MPa, the temperature at 35-135degrees Celsius, and the saturation time for 3-30 hours, where content of the co-foaming agent of the SC-carbon dioxide can submerge the entire mold, vacuum extracting and foaming the high pressure kettle, releasing the pressure at less than 1 MPa/minutes, and taking out the beads in the kettle to obtain foamed PMI precursor beads.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The crosslinking agent comprises (meth)acrylamide, magnesium acrylate, magnesium methacrylate, zinc acrylate, zinc methacrylate or bismaleimide. The initiator comprises azobisisobutyronitrile, azobisisoheptanenitrile, dibenzoyl peroxide, lauroyl peroxide, methyl ethyl ketone peroxide, tert-butyl peroxide tert-butyl valerate, diisopropyl peroxydicarbonate or dicyclohexyl peroxydicarbonate. The water-soluble inorganic salt comprises sodium chloride, sodium sulfate, sodium nitrate, potassium chloride, potassium sulfate or potassium nitrate. The dispersant comprises polyvinyl alcohol, propyl cellulose, hydroxypropyl methyl cellulose, poly(meth)acrylic acid, gelatin, methyl cellulose, carboxymethyl cellulose, polyvinyl pyrrolidone, ethyl cellulose , hydroxyethyl cellulose, polyethylene glycol, methyl cellulose or carboxymethyl cellulose. The surfactant comprises sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate, sodium dodecyl sulfate, stearic acid, fatty acid glyceride or fatty acid sorbitan. The screen mesh is 500 mum. The SC-carbon dioxide co-foaming agent comprises pentane, ethanol, water, isooctane, methanol, n-propanol, isopropanol, n-butanol or isobutanol. Preferred Method: The stirring is carried out at 100-500 revolutions/minute.

PN CN112410070-A; CN112410070-B; WO2022077314-A1
TI Energy-saving method for recycling carbon from refinery dry gas, involves mixing gas phase obtained by desorption tower with gas phase of low pressure flash evaporation zone as carbon dioxide concentration gas to send.
AU ZHU W
   ZOU X
   HOU G
   HAN Z
   DONG H
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
   UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202127097Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves mixing a gas phase obtained by a desorption tower with a gas phase of a low pressure flash evaporation zone as a carbon dioxide concentration gas to send to a cracking furnace of an ethylene device. A liquid phase obtained by the desorption tower is returned to a multi-stage absorption tower as a poor solvent. A small portion is utilized for extracting carbon four to a boundary region. Fresh carbon four absorbent is supplemented. A high pressure flash evaporation zone is provided with a multi-stage high pressure zone flash tank.
   USE - Energy-saving method for recycling carbon from refinery dry gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple process, greatly reduced solvent dosage and energy consumption, small investment and high carbon two carbon three recovery rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an energy-saving device for recovering carbon dioxide from dry gas in a refinery.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an energy-saving device for recovering carbon dioxide from dry gas in a refinery.
EA    (WO2022077314-A1)   NOVELTY - Energy-saving method for recovering 2C component from refinery dry gas comprises (i) drying the refinery dry gas after deacidification, compressing in a dry gas pre-treatment system (1), cooling and passing into a multi-stage absorption tower (3) for treatment, subjecting the tower top gas phase to cold energy recovery and passing the bottom liquid phase into a high pressure zone flash tank (4) (ii) setting the multi-stage high-pressure zone flash tanks in the high-pressure flash zone and returning the gas phase to the compression section of dry gas pre-treatment system, recycling and sending a part to low pressure flash zone, (iii) passing the gas phase 2C enriched gas to the carbon (2C) enriched gas compressor system (6), and (iv) mixing the gas phase from desorption tower treatment with the gas phase in low pressure flash zone and passing into ethylene plant cracking furnace as 2C enriched gas, extracting small part and adding fresh 4C absorbent containing n-pentane and isopentane.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering 2C component from refinery dry gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and economical; has reduced usage of lean solvents and high 2C/3C recovery rate; and consumes less energy.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.)

PT P
PN CN111138806-A
TI Preparation of nano-epoxy insulating material involves mixing material containing modified nano silica, polyvinylpyrrolidone to form mixture, adding mixture to supercritical reaction device to form epoxy resin, sealing device, and drying.
AU FU X
AE SICHUAN ZHONGJIAN ENVIRONMENTAL TESTING (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 202042455X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of nano-epoxy insulating material involves adding material containing 10-15 pts. wt. modified nano silica in 30-50 pts. wt. ethanol aqueous solution, heating, stirring, drying to obtain mixture (A), adding material containing 30-50 pts. wt. epoxy resin to sealed container, placing the sealed container in electron accelerator, irradiating, stirring for 60 minutes to obtain mixture (B), adding mixture (A) and mixture (B) to supercritical reaction device, adding 3-5 pts. wt. silicon carbide whiskers, sealing the device, passing carbon dioxide at 30-60 MPa, stirring, releasing the pressure, cooling, curing to obtain epoxy resin, dissolving 50-100 pts. wt. epoxy resin and 5-10 pts. wt. furan dicarboxylic acid in 200-300 pts. wt. toluene, adding to the supercritical device, adding 0.5-1.5 pt. wt. dimethylacetamide, sealing the device, passing carbon dioxide at 30-60 MPa, stirring, releasing the pressure, cooling, precipitating ethanol, and drying.
   USE - Preparation of nano-epoxy insulating material.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared nano-epoxy insulation material has high strength, excellent insulation performance and long service life. The method uses modified nano-silica material which overcomes the effect of the agglomeration of micro-nano particles in the existing method on the electrical properties of the epoxy resin.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of nano-epoxy insulating material involves (1) adding 10-15 pts. wt. modified nano silica, 1-3 pts. wt. flame retardant, 1-3 pts. wt. dibutyl adipate, 1-5 pts. wt. sodium tripolyphosphate, 1-3 pts. wt. itaconic anhydride, 1-3 pts. wt. anti-aging agent, 3-5 pts. wt. N-methyl pyrrolidone, 1-3 pts. wt. dispersant in 30-50 pts. wt. ethanol aqueous solution, heating, stirring for 30-60 minutes, ultrasonically dispersing for 10-30 minutes, filtering, drying to obtain mixture (A), (2) adding 30-50 pts. wt. epoxy resin, 2-6 pts. wt. polyvinylpyrrolidone, 8-10 pts. wt. sodium methyl silicate, 1-2 pts. wt. hexahydrophthalic anhydride, 1-3 pts. wt. benzoic anhydride, 0.1-0.3 pt. wt. ethylene glycol diglycidyl ether and 0.5-1.5 pt. wt. N-phenyl-2-naphthylamine to sealed container with stirring, placing the sealed container in electron accelerator having 1.5 MeV, and 30 mA, irradiating, stirring for 60 minutes to obtain mixture (B), (3) adding mixture (A) and mixture (B) to supercritical reaction device, adding 3-5 pts. wt. silicon carbide whiskers, sealing the device, passing carbon dioxide at 30-60 MPa, stirring at 70-80 degrees C for 2-3 hours, releasing the pressure, cooling to room temperature, curing to obtain epoxy resin, (4) dissolving 50-100 pts. wt. epoxy resin and 5-10 pts. wt. furan dicarboxylic acid in 200-300 pts. wt. toluene, adding to the supercritical device, adding 0.5-1.5 pt. wt. dimethylacetamide, sealing the device, passing carbon dioxide at 30-60 MPa, stirring at 70-80 degrees C for 2-3 hours, releasing the pressure, cooling to room temperature, precipitating ethanol, and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation of the modified nano-silica involves adding 10-15 pts. wt. nano silica, 1-2 pts. wt. nano yttrium dioxide and 0.1-0.3 pt. wt. dispersant to 100 pts. wt. butanol aqueous solution, ultrasonically dispersing for 30 minutes, adding the ultrasonically dispersed solution to sealed container with stirring, adding 0.5-1.5 pt. wt. titanate coupling agent, placing the sealed container in electron accelerator of 1.5 MeV, and 30 mA for irradiation, stirring for 60 minutes, filtering, washing with ethanol and drying. The preparation of the modified nano-silica further involves adding 10-15 pts. wt. dried material to the supercritical reaction device, adding 20-30 pts. wt. butyl acrylate, 3-5 pts. wt. N-vinyl imidazole, 0.1-0.3 pt. wt. benzoyl peroxide, and 100- 120 pts. wt. water., sealing the device, passing carbon dioxide at 30-40 MPa, stirring at 70-80 degrees C for 2-3 hours, releasing the pressure of carbon dioxide, adding 20-25 pts. wt. cetyltrimethylammonium ammonium bromide, injecting carbon dioxide again at 40-60 MPa, stirring for 0.5-1 hour, releasing the pressure, filtering, washing with ethanol, and drying. Preferred Conditions: The ultrasonically dispersing in step (1) is carried out at frequency of 30-50 kHz. The irradiation in step (2) is carried out at dose rate of 200-300 kGy/hours having dose of 400-600 kGy. The stirring in step (2) is carried out at speed rate of 100-200 rpm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The antioxidant in step (1) is chosen from dimethylene tin octanoate and dimethyl tin in the weight ratio of 2:1. The dispersant in step (1) is chosen from any one of 1-ethyl-3 methyl imidazole chloride, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazole chloride, and 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazole lactic acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The epoxy resin is chosen from any one of bisphenol A-type epoxy resin, bisphenol F-type epoxy resin, bisphenol glycidyl ether, aliphatic glycidyl ether and phenolic epoxy resin.

PT P
PN DE102018121531-A1; WO2020025435-A1
TI Method for modification of solid carbon dioxide, involves making dry ice blocks, dry ice nuggets or dry ice pellets to be contacted or coated with additive or additive mixture before being placed on market.
AU HENKEL U
   SUESS A
AE CRYOTEC ANLAGENBAU GMBH (CRYO-Non-standard)
GA 202010861L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves pressing a carbonic acid snow formed on a manufacturing side in dry ice blocks (2), dry ice nuggets or dry ice pellets, depending on the application. The dry ice blocks, dry ice nuggets or dry ice pellets are brought into contact or coated with an additive or an additive mixture before being placed on the market. The coating is carried out by printing, spraying, dipping, sprinkling, powdering or pasting with the respective additive or the additive mixture. The food colors, odorants and/or flavorings are used as an additive or additive mixture. The substances to be transferred are used as an additive or additive mixture in the field of industrial applications or dry ice blasting, to the surface to be treated, where the additives or the additive mixture contains a corrosion protection or corrosion inhibitor and/or care agent. The additives or the additive mixture are in solid and/or liquid form and are brought into contact with dry ice pellets in a mixer.
   USE - Method for modification of solid carbon dioxide e.g. dry ice used in chemical industry for cooling reaction mixture, in medicine and biology fields, and in catering and food industries for cooling food, beverage and other sensitive goods.
   ADVANTAGE - The solid carbon dioxide modification carried out after the actual production of the dry ice blocks, dry ice nuggets or dry ice pellets enables special effects to be achieved. Thus, the color of the dry ice can be changed and adapted, and the taste or aroma and the appearance can be changed and adapted.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The total mixture obtained is fed to a new pressing process for the production of blocks of any shape. The core with an additive or an additive mixture is formed in the dry ice block, such that the additive which is in each case taken up or located in the core is exposed with increasing sublimation process. The additive or the additive mixture is comprised of color or olfactory perceptible substances that signal an interruption of a cold chain in the sense of a cooling indicator, where the additives or the additive mixture are present in the liquid and/or solid phase. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for modification of solid carbon dioxide, has a coating unit for coating the dry ice, by printing, spraying, dipping, sprinkling, powdering and/or pasting. The coating unit is suitable for processing additives present in the liquid and/or solid phase.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram illustrating the process for modification on dry ice already formed as blocks or nuggets by spraying liquid additives.
   Dry ice press (1)
   Dry ice block or dry ice nugget (2)
   Motor-driven conveyor belt (3)
   Modified dry ice block or dry ice nugget (4)

PT P
PN CN110437722-A
TI Polyurethane repairing liquid for solar photovoltaic back plate, comprises components comprising e.g. polycarbonate ether polyol, isocyanate monomer, wear-resistant pigment, hexamethylene diisocyanate trimer and toluene diisocyanate trimer.
AU WU X
AE SUZHOU DESHENGHUI NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201997197J
AB    NOVELTY - A polyurethane repairing liquid comprises a component (A) comprising 75-80 pts. wt. carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate ether polyol, 45-50 pts. wt. fluorine-containing polyether polyol, 160-180 pts. wt. solvent, 8-10 pts. wt. white pigment, 16-20 pts. wt. wear resistant pigment, 1.5-2 pts. wt. antioxidant, 80-100 pts. wt. isocyanate monomer, 0.06-0.08 pts. wt. catalyst, and a component (B) consisting 25-30 pts. wt. hexamethylene diisocyanate trimer and 15-20 pts. wt. toluene diisocyanate trimer, and hexamethylene diisocyanate trimer having molecular formula of toluene diisocyanate trimer.
   USE - Polyurethane repairing liquid used for photovoltaic back plate of solar battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The polyurethane repairing liquid has excellent wear resistance, self-cleaning, dustproof performance, and improved protection effects on solar battery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A polyurethane repairing liquid comprises a component (A) comprising 75-80 pts. wt. carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate ether polyol, 45-50 pts. wt. fluorine-containing polyether polyol, 160-180 pts. wt. solvent, 8-10 pts. wt. white pigment, 16-20 pts. wt. wear resistant pigment, 1.5-2 pts. wt. antioxidant, 80-100 pts. wt. isocyanate monomer, 0.06-0.08 pts. wt. catalyst, and a component (B) consisting 25-30 pts. wt. hexamethylene diisocyanate trimer is of formula (I) and 15-20 pts. wt. toluene diisocyanate trimer is of formula (II), and hexamethylene diisocyanate trimer having molecular formula of toluene diisocyanate trimer. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of component (A) for polyurethane repairing liquid, which involves dissolving (step I) carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate ether into liquid and standby, adding (step II) fluorine-containing polyether polyol into reaction vessel at one time and heating reaction vessel at one time, and heating reaction vessel at one time and controlling reaction temperature of material is 60-70 degrees C, for 2-3 hours, adding (step III) carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate ether solution from step I with material obtained in step II for 2-3 hours and temperature of material is controlled at 60-70 degrees C for 1-2 hours, adding (step IV) catalyst to material obtained from step III, which is been reacted for 1-2 hours, temperature of material is maintained at 60-70 degrees C for 2-3 hours, and adding wear resistant pigment, white filler and antioxidant are added to material obtained from step IV after reaction, stirred and dispersed for 1-2 hours, cooling water is passed and temperature of material is cooled to 40 degrees C and then packaged; and
   (2) preparation of component (B) for polyurethane repairing liquid, which involves adding hexamethylene diisocyanate trimer and toluene diisocyanate trimer in closed disperser at temperature of 23-26 degrees C and humidity of less than 40%, and stirred for 20-30 minutes under nitrogen for dispensing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate ether polyol is copolymerization of carbon dioxide and epoxy compound including ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, butylene oxide and epoxy ring, having one or more of hexane and average molecular weight is 4500-8000 and 10-30% carbon-dioxide. The fluorine-containing polyether polyol is bifunctional hydroxy fluorine-containing polyether polyol of formula: HOCH2CF2O(CF2CF2O)p(CF2O)qCF2CH2OH. The solvent is mixture of one or more toluene, ethyl acetate, xylene and acetone. The white pigment is one of titanium dioxide, calcium carbonate and aluminum oxide. The wear resistant pigment is one of strontium phosphate, silicon nitride, and nano-sized aluminum oxide. The isocyanate monomer is mixture of one or more of toluene diisocyanate, diphenylmethane diisocyanate, hexamethylene diisocyanate, and isophorone diisocyanate. The catalyst is mixture of one or more of organotin, organoanthracene, and organozinc.p = 3-8;andq = 10-30.

PT P
PN US2019082609-A1; US10999975-B2
TI System for delivering gas e.g. carbon dioxide to group of plants in various areas, has windbreak foil provided for shielding gas envelope from wind so that gas envelope remains near portion of plant for increased period of time.
AU ERICKSON S E
AE ERICKSON S E (ERIC-Individual)
   AGRIC GAS CO (AGRI-Non-standard)
GA 201926020N
AB    NOVELTY - The system has several gas delivery devices (10) provided for supplying gas to a group of plants. Each gas delivery device has an elongated vertical base (16) placed on the ground at a predetermined distance from an individual plant in the group and extended above the plant. An adjustable emitter assembly is provided on the base. A gas supply conduit is connected to the emitter assembly. A gas supply line (23A) is connected to the gas supply conduit. The emitter assembly is arranged on a downward face of a vertically arranged windbreak foil (20) in order to blow gas towards the individual plant. A gas envelope is formed near a portion of the plant by the gas from the emitter assembly. The gas envelope is shielded from wind (12) by the foil so that the gas envelope remains near a portion of the plant for an increased period of time.
   USE - System for delivering gas e.g. carbon dioxide to a group of plants in various areas. Uses include but are not limited to agricultural field, grove, vineyard, and orchard e.g. fruit and nut orchards and citrus orchards.
   ADVANTAGE - The system provides improved carbon dioxide enrichment technology for effective and efficient distribution, application and retention of CO2 gas in large open-field tracts of farmland. The windbreak foil is provided for magnifying CO2 residency, enabling higher CO2 concentrations, reducing CO2 costs, and enabling other agronomic functions. A low cost, high durability, high efficiency, and reliable sapling growth system is provided with windbreak foil and adjustable elements to increase the capability of system commensurate with sapling growth. The CO2 gas is supplied to the young saplings so that one or two years could be knocked off the production cycle in order to increase the financial benefits for the orchard and vineyard owners. The juvenile saplings are provided with CO2 to increase their vigor, reduce water stress and improve resiliency to a whole package of environmental stresses that lead to mortality. The CO2 application is performed without interfering with mechanical shaker so that the shaker is able to quickly approach the trunk of tree and hold the trunk of tree according to the requirement. The windbreak foil is provided with slits and flaps to maximize the containment of CO2 gas in low wind conditions, and allow air to freely pass through the windbreak foil in high wind conditions to prevent sail effect, tearing, and preserve the integrity of windbreak foil in high wind conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method of delivering carbon dioxide gas to an individual plant in an agricultural field.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a rear view of the gas delivery device.
   Gas delivery device (10)
   Wind (12)
   Base (16)
   Windbreak foil (20)
   Gas supply line (23A)
DC P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).); P42 (Spraying, atomising (B05).)

PN CN108823388-A
TI Laser heat treatment useful for the surface of a torsion bar spring suspension of a energy vehicle, comprises e.g. selecting the material of the torsion bar spring suspension and cutting the material.
AU GENG L
   LIU W
   LIN X
   XU Z
   GUO J
AE YANCHENG INST TECHNOLOGY (YANH-C)
GA 201891881W
AB    NOVELTY - Laser heat treatment comprises (i) selecting the material of the torsion bar spring suspension, cutting the material according to the length specified by the process, heating the material with the end heating device, using the upsetting machine, forming, heating the end portion after upsetting, cooling slowly by an annealing furnace and machining the end portion, (ii) feeding the material into the roller table through the material rod on the loading rack, (iii) preheating the material, heating, maintaining and quenching, (iv) using a cross-flow carbon dioxide laser to heat-treat the surface of the main-axis sample, where the output power of the cross-flow carbon dioxide laser is 1-5 KW and the scanning speed is 0.5 m/seconds, (v) discharging the material, transferring to the tempering furnace in time and (vi) using a pellet having a diameter and impacting the surface of the material at a speed of more than 100 m/seconds.
   USE - The method is useful for the surface of a torsion bar spring suspension of a energy vehicle (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Laser heat treatment comprises (i) selecting the material of the torsion bar spring suspension, cutting the material according to the length specified by the process, heating the material to 950-1000 degrees C with the end heating device, using the upsetting machine, forming, heating the end portion after upsetting, cooling slowly by an annealing furnace, where the initial temperature of the annealing furnace is 900-1000 degrees C and the cooling rate is 100-200 degrees C/hour and machining the end portion, (ii) feeding the material into the roller table through the material rod on the loading rack, driving roller feeds the material into the feeding device, clamping the material, pulling out through the clamping frame, replacing the material by two on the feeding device, sending the group driving roller by the cylinder clamping, collecting the material by the discharging rod in the blanking gantry, where the material is directly opposite to the pressing head, pressing the material on the feeding gantry through the pressing head and air cooling, (iii) preheating the material at 700 degrees C for 20-30 minutes, heating to 1000-1100 degrees C, maintaining for 30 minutes and quenching, where the treatment time is 20-30 seconds, (iv) using a cross-flow carbon dioxide laser to heat-treat the surface of the main-axis sample, where the output power of the cross-flow carbon dioxide laser is 1-5 KW and the scanning speed is 0.5 m/seconds, (v) discharging the material, transferring to the tempering furnace in time, where the tempering temperature is 400-500 degrees C and the maintaining time is 1-2 hours and (vi) using a pellet having a diameter of 50-200 mu m and impacting the surface of the material at a speed of more than 100 m/seconds.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: In the step (iii), the preheating heating device is a box furnace and the inlet protector is argon or nitrogen. The indenter is the output of a small press. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the machining sequence comprises machining center hole, turning outer diameter, rolling processing, turning outer diameter to turn the upset portion diameter to the finished product size, turning the part connected to the diameter of the main material smoothly and performing the rolling process by a rolling mill or a toothed machine to process the zigzag shape. The surface of the blanking gantry has an initial temperature is less than 700 degrees C. In the step (iii), the heating rate is more than 50 degrees C/minute.
