AB    NOVELTY - Photobioreactor comoprises e.g. a hybrid culture container having a culture space for culturing photosynthetic microorganisms, a container shell made of transparent material for accommodating culture solution and a lower frame coupled to lower portion of container shell and made of stainless steel; a gas supply unit for supplying air into container shell; gas discharging unit coupled to culture container for discharging internal air of container shell to outside; and a culture solution circulating unit connected to one end of culture container and having a main pipe.
   USE - The photobioreactor is useful for culturing photosynthetic microorganisms including microalgae (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Photobioreactor comoprises a hybrid culture container (100) having a culture space for culturing the photosynthetic microorganisms, a container shell made of transparent material for accommodating the culture solution contained in the culture space and a lower frame coupled to the lower portion of the container shell and made of stainless steel; a gas supply unit (300) for supplying air into the container shell; gas discharging unit coupled to the culture container for discharging internal air of the container shell to the outside; a culture solution circulating unit (500) connected to one end of the culture container and having a main pipe (510) for sending the culture solution discharged to the outside of the culture container into the culture container and a pump for circulating the culture solution flowing through the main pipe; a spraying member for increasing the supply efficiency of carbon dioxide and light by supplying culture solution and carbon dioxide into the photosynthetic microorganisms while absorbing light and moving the surface of the culture solution from the upper part of the culture container and connected to the culture solution circulating unit and arranged on the upper side of the culture container; a discharge pipe branched from the main pipe and connected to the culture container for supplying the culture solution branched from the main pipe into the container shell and moving the culture solution discharged from the discharge end portion to a water surface of the culture solution by moving at a distance shorter than the culture solution from the spray member; and a light source unit (200) having a light source for emitting light towards the culture container and located on the outer side of the culture container as a panel type structure separate from the culture container. The amount of carbon dioxide and light supplied into the culture solution sprayed through the spray member is greater than the amount of carbon dioxide and light supplied into the culture solution discharged through the discharge pipe in the form of water stream.

TI Carbon fixation unit gas circulation exchange            system comprises first culture unit provided with            bacteria to perform aerobic respiration, where algae            and plants capable of photosynthesis are arranged in            culture unit and carbon capture device connected with            culture unit for absorbing reaction gas.
AU LV X
   GE W
   ZHENG M
   WANG X
   SUN S
   LIU H
   LI G
   LIU Y
   ZHANG L
AE SPIC SCI & TECHNOLOGY RES INST CO LTD (CPIC-C)
GA 202260184K
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon fixation unit gas circulation exchange                system comprises a first culture unit 3, which is                provided with fungi that performs aerobic                respiration, a second culture unit 4, where algae                and/or plants capable of photosynthesis are                arranged in second culture unit. The system                comprises a carbon capture device 5 which is                connected with first culture unit for absorbing                reaction gas in first culture unit and carbon                dioxide in outside air. The carbon capture device                is connected with second culture unit for supplying                carbon dioxide to second culture unit. The system                comprises a gas purification device 6 connected                with first culture unit for supplying                oxygen-enriched gas into the first culture unit.                The gas purification device is connected with the                second culture unit for absorbing reaction gas in                the second culture unit. The system comprises an                energy collection device 1 for collecting renewable                energy, and an energy conversion device 2 connected                to the energy collection device.
   USE - Carbon fixation unit gas circulation exchange                system
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon fixation unit gas circulation                exchange system realizes function of carbon gas                internal circulation, effectively improves                stability, carbon fixation capacity and efficiency                of system, achieves purpose of low energy                consumption and carbon fixation, and improves                effect of increasing crop yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon fixation unit gas circulation exchange                system comprises a first culture unit, which is                provided with fungi that performs aerobic                respiration, a second culture unit, where algae                and/or plants capable of photosynthesis are                arranged in second culture unit. The system                comprises a carbon capture device which is                connected with first culture unit for absorbing the                reaction gas in first culture unit and carbon                dioxide in outside air. The carbon capture device                is connected with second culture unit for supplying                carbon dioxide to second culture unit. The system                comprises a gas purification device which is                connected with first culture unit for supplying                oxygen-enriched gas into the first culture unit.                The gas purification device is connected with the                second culture unit for absorbing reaction gas in                the second culture unit. The system comprises an                energy collection device for collecting renewable                energy, and an energy conversion device connected                to the energy collection device for converting the                renewable energy into electrical energy. The energy                conversion device supply power to carbon capture                device and gas purification device. The carbon                capture device, gas purification device, first                culture unit and second culture unit drives to form                a carbon gas internal circulation to realize low                energy consumption of the gas circulation exchange                system of carbon fixation unit carbon                sequestration.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic flow chart of a                carbon fixation unit gas circulation exchange                system. (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
   Energy collecting device (1)
   Energy conversion device (2)
   First culture unit (3)
   Second culture unit (4)
   Carbon capturing device (5)
   Gas purifying device (6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The second cultivation                  unit is provided with at least one of an algae                  unit, a plant unit and a mixed unit of algae and                  plant. The concentration range of carbon dioxide                  in algae unit is greater than or equal to 1000                  ppm and less than or equal to 100000 ppm, and/or                  concentration of carbon dioxide in plant unit is                  greater than or equal to 800 ppm and less than or                  equal to 2000 ppm. The algae are at least one of                  Spirulina, Chlorella, Candida, Trichomonas and                  Microcystis. The bacteria are at least one of                  Bacillus subtilis, Penicillium, saccharomycetes                  and mushroom that perform aerobic respiration.                  The carbon capture device adopts at least one of                  chemical absorption method, physical absorption                  method, exchange adsorption method, molecular                  sieve adsorption method, carbonate aqueous                  solution electrolysis and spray capture method to                  capture the carbon dioxide. The gas purification                  device adopts at least one of catalytic                  decomposition method, alkali liquor absorption                  method and carbon adsorption method to purify                  harmful gases in reaction gas.

PT P
PN CN114250085-A; CN114250085-B
TI Catalytically synthesizing biofuel by using            biomass pyrolysis gas comprises pre-treating the            biomass by drying, crushing and air separation,            gasification reaction, micro-explosion decomposition by            generating ash at the bottom of the reaction pipeline,            tar decomposing to form combustible gas.
AU LIU S
   ZHANG M
   FAN W
   ZHOU Y
   SHI Y
   GE S
   XIA C
   SHENG Y
AE UNIV NANJING FORESTRY (UNJF-C)
GA 202250435A
AB    NOVELTY - Catalytically synthesizing biofuel by using                biomass pyrolysis gas comprises (1) pre-treating                the biomass by drying, crushing and air separation,                processing the biomass to form powder-shaped                reaction raw material, (2) gasification reaction by                adding the reaction raw material into many reaction                pipelines in the gasification furnace, the reaction                raw material orderly passes through drying,                pyrolysis, combustion and reduction in the                corresponding reaction pipeline, removing water in                the reaction raw material in the reaction pipeline,                and converting into solid carbon after undergoing                destructive distillation pyrolysis process, liquid                tar and gasification gas, tar along with air in the                air pipeline sinks, C, H element reacts with the                oxygen in the air to generate carbon dioxide and                water, and releasing a lot of heat, vacuum                extracting tar at high temperature, after the                carbon dioxide reduction and steam reforming                generated by combustion.
   USE - The method is useful for catalytically                synthesizing biofuel by using biomass pyrolysis                gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalytically synthesizing biofuel by using                biomass pyrolysis gas comprises (1) pre-treating                the biomass by drying, crushing and air separation,                processing the biomass to form powder-shaped                reaction raw material with grain diameter of 3-6                mm, and the water content is 10-12%, (2)                gasification reaction by adding the reaction raw                material into many reaction pipeline in the                gasification furnace, the reaction raw material                orderly passes through drying, pyrolysis,                combustion and reduction in the corresponding                reaction pipeline, removing water in the reaction                raw material in the reaction pipeline, and                converting into solid carbon after undergoing                destructive distillation pyrolysis process, liquid                tar and gasification gas, tar along with air in the                air pipeline sinks, C, H element reacts with the                oxygen in the air to generate carbon dioxide and                water, and releasing a lot of heat, vacuum                extracting tar at high temperature, after the                carbon dioxide reduction and steam reforming                generated by combustion, generating a large amount                of combustible gas, mixing with the gasification                gas to form synthesis gas, (3) micro-explosion                decomposition by generating ash at the bottom of                the reaction pipeline, generating partial                discontinuous micro-explosion reaction at the                bottom of the gasification furnace, using energy                generated by explosion to dynamically move tar and                catalyst in the gasification furnace and further                decomposing, the heat energy generated by the                explosion can further provide the high temperature                condition for the decomposition of the tar, where                the micro-explosion reaction is mixed gas of                combustible gas and air with a certain                concentration, and generating after ignition of the                igniter, tar decomposing to form combustible gas,                and mixing with the synthesis gas in (2) to form                biological fuel and flowing out from the lower part                of the gasification furnace.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic structural view                of a gasification furnace.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (1), surface                  drying the biomass, adopting the way of edge                  crushing and winnowing, increasing the gap                  between the biomass, and ensuring the direction                  of the biomass is consistent, and the remove of                  the inner side of the biomass to obtain the                  reaction raw material. The micro-explosion                  reaction is generated by micro-explosion                  structure; the microexplosion structure                  comprises: a drainage pipeline, a micro-explosion                  body connected with the drainage pipeline, the                  micro-explosion body comprises an upper top                  cover, a lower fixing ring, and a supporting                  strip connected with the upper top cover and the                  lower fixing ring; the supporting strip is an                  elastic arc-shaped structure; the drainage                  pipeline is filled with microexplosion gas, the                  micro-explosion gas is a certain concentration of                  combustible gas and air and mixed gas; the                  combustible gas is derived from the biological                  fuel flowing out from the step (3), the air is                  from the air pipeline; the position close to the                  lower fixing ring is provided with a point fire                  electrode. The drainage pipeline is a straight                  pipe structure or a bent pipe structure. The one                  side close to the output end of the drainage                  pipeline is provided with a buffer bag. The                  micro-explosion structure is further provided                  with a reciprocating movement mechanism the                  reciprocating movement mechanism a moving plate,                  a linkage rod connected at the bottom of the                  moving plate, a swinging rod connected with the                  linkage rod, and a supporting rod connected with                  the swinging rod; the linkage rod is hinged with                  the swinging rod, one side of the swinging rod is                  connected with the drainage pipeline; two sides                  of the moving plate are provided with a sliding                  block and a linear guide rail, the sliding block                  is connected with the moving plate, and drives                  the moving plate to move along the length                  direction of the linear guide rail; the surface                  plate is provided with multiple vent holes and a                  matching hole, the vent hole is used for passing                  through the micro-explosion gas; the supporting                  drill through the matching hole, and extending                  and fixed to the upper top cover. The swinging                  rod jacks up or shrink the supporting drill                  according to the timing relationship. The                  position of the supporting drill close to the                  lower fixing ring is provided with an igniter.                  The reaction raw material is mixed with catalyst                  in the blanking process. The drainage pipeline is                  further provided with a valve, a flow meter and a                  combustible gas concentration detector. The valve                  and reciprocating movement mechanism moved at the                  same time.

AB    NOVELTY - Mechanochemical process for decontamination                and/or elimination of synthetic, biogenic and                biological materials (A), digestion of phosphates                (B), immobilization of metals and its compounds                (C), splitting of carbon dioxide (D) into the                elements and recovery of valuable products (E)                involves providing component (F) containing at                least one fluid, solution, suspension, finely                divided solid mixture and reactive gas and                component (G) chosen from pure, finely divided,                mineral coal, partially pyrolyzed coal, biochar and                activated carbon, contaminated or impregnated,                finely divided mineral coal, partially pyrolyzed                coal, biochar and activated carbon, finely divided                lignite and pure and contaminated or impregnated,                finely divided carbon suppliers, supplying                component (F) to a grinding chamber of at least one                mechanical mill or alternately supplying components                (A)-(D), component (G) and ground material (F) into                the grinding chamber of at least one mechanical                mill, grinding and recycling.
   USE - Mechanochemical process for decontamination                and/or elimination of synthetic, biogenic and                biological materials, digestion of phosphates,                immobilization of metals and its compounds,                splitting of carbon dioxide into elements and                recovery of valuable products, and for producing                fertilizer and catalyst (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The mechanochemical processing is carried out                efficiently and safely without affecting the                environment. The discharge of poisonous substances                can be eliminated.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The material (E) is                  soluble in medium chosen from lithium, sodium and                  potassium salts and magnesium and calcium salts,                  and biologically available phosphates (B). The                  immobilized material (C) is chosen from main                  group elements, transition metals, lanthanides                  and actinides. The at least one solid and finely                  divided product (I) is chosen from activated                  carbons (G). The piezoelectric particles and/or                  coatings on the grinding materials, drive shafts,                  walls of the grinding chamber and/or on the                  agitation unit are chosen from carbon, quartz,                  glass barium titanate (BTO), lead zirconate                  titanate (PZT), lead magnesium niobate (PMN),                  gallium orthophosphate, berlinite, tourmaline,                  zinc oxide, aluminum nitride, silicon nitride,                  silicon carbide, aluminum oxide, zirconium oxide                  and titanium nitride, polyvinylidene fluoride and                  ferroelectric and polycrystalline ceramics. The                  material (A) is chosen from dried slurry, liquid                  manure, fermentation residues, dry ferments,                  sewage sludge, ferments, composts, biowaste,                  vegetable waste, animal waste, waste from metal                  extraction, radioactive waste, waste from carbon                  fiber-reinforced plastics, celluloses,                  hemicelluloses, liginocelluloses, celluloses,                  hemicelluloses and/or liginocelluloses containing                  biomasses and waste, high molecular weight                  proteins and structural proteins, dry                  concentrates of the biological treatment stages                  of sewage treatment plants, chemical washers and                  filters, waste water, residues of the exhaust air                  treatment, nitrogen compounds, polycyclic                  aromatic hydrocarbons (PAH), volatile organic                  compounds (VOC), reactive gases, medical devices,                  hormones, micropollutants, phosphate-containing                  building materials, phosphate-containing body                  components, phosphate-containing food and feed,                  other phosphate sources, synthetic and natural,                  intensely smelling substances, synthetic and                  natural toxins, biocides, pyrotechnic materials,                  explosives, Viruses, bacteria, and mycoplasmas.                  At least one mill base contains at least one                  additive different from the mill base and chosen                  from finely divided solid, dissolved, suspended                  and emulsified, inorganic and organic salts,                  acids, bases, oxides, reducing agents, oxidizing                  agents and inert gases. Grinding is carried out                  at -273 degrees C to 1200 degrees C for 0.5                  minutes to 1000 hours. Preferred Apparatus: The                  mechanical mills are selected from ball mills,                  hammer mills, pinned-disk mills, jet mills,                  vibration mills, shaking mills, horizontal mills,                  attritors and planetary mills. The agitation unit                  is made up of titanium, steels, chromium steels,                  aluminum oxide, steatite, porcelain, zirconium                  oxide, glass, flint stone, steel, tungsten                  carbide, silicon carbide, silicon nitride, boron                  nitride or boron carbide. Plasma is present                  during the grinding, and is generated by gas                  discharge, hotspots, electrostatic charging,                  emission of exoelectrons, triboluminescence,                  crystal lattice defects, shredding, dislocations,                  crystal lattice vibrations, fracture formation,                  cutting processes, upsets, sanding, drilling and                  grinding.

PN CN110846094-A; CN110846094-B
TI Preparation of liquefied natural gas involves            preparing preheated purge gas from purified gas of            upgraded coal or coal water slurry, producing methane            product stream, gas-water separating, and performing            cryogenic liquefaction.
AU YU T
   WANG J
   XUE A
   WANG Z
   LV B
   ZHENG S
AE ZHEJIANG TIANLU ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202019940D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of liquefied natural gas from                purified gas involves passing a low-rank coal                through gasification reduction process, preparing                an upgraded coal and a rich gas to make a coal                water slurry, obtaining a purified gas (G1) from                the upgraded coal, preparing a purified gas (G2)                from the coal water slurry, and preparing a                purified gas (G3) from the rich gas, introducing at                least one of gases (G1)-(G3) into a reactor (R1)                and preheating to obtain a preheated purge gas,                passing the preheated purified gas as a methane                synthesis material into a reactor (R2), reacting                with carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen                in presence of a high-temperature-resistant methane                catalyst (C1) to obtain a methane stream (S1),                passing methane product stream through a gas-water                separation device to obtain a methane product                stream which is an original liquefied natural gas                stream, passing the natural gas stream into a                liquefaction process, and producing methane by                cryogenic liquefaction process.
   USE - Preparation of liquefied natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces liquefied natural gas                having less impurities and high quality, by fully                utilizing the volatiles and coal in the low-rank                coal.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of liquefied natural gas from                purified gas involves passing a low-rank coal                through gasification reduction process, preparing                an upgraded coal and a rich gas to make a coal                water slurry including the upgraded coal and/or low                rank coal, obtaining a purified gas (G1) from the                upgraded coal, preparing a purified gas (G2) from                the coal water slurry, and preparing a purified gas                (G3) from the rich gas, introducing at least one of                gases (G1)-(G3) into a reactor (R1) and preheating                to 150-500 degrees C to obtain a preheated purge                gas, passing the preheated purified gas as a                methane synthesis material into a reactor (R2),                reacting carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and                hydrogen with the methane synthesis material in                presence of a high-temperature-resistant methane                catalyst (C1) to synthesize methane to obtain a                methane stream (S1), passing stream (S1) as a                methane synthesis material into a reactor (R2),                reacting carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and                hydrogen with the methane synthesis material in                presence of a high-temperature-resistant methane                catalyst (C2) to synthesize methane to obtain a                methane stream (S2), passing stream (S2) into a                reactor (R3), reacting the methane synthesis                material with carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and                hydrogen in presence of a                high-temperature-resistant methane catalyst (C3) to                synthesize methane to obtain a methane stream (S3)                which is a methane product stream comprising less                than 5 vol.% carbon monoxide, passing the methane                product stream through a gas-water separation                device to obtain a methane product stream which is                an original liquefied natural gas stream, passing                the natural gas stream into a liquefaction process,                and producing more than 90 vol.% methane by                cryogenic liquefaction process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: Purified gas (G3) is                  passed into reactors (R1), (R2) and/or (R3), so                  that the reaction temperature is controlled at                  200-800 degrees C. Preferred Process: Preparation                  of liquefied natural gas further involves passing                  the methane product stream into a reactor (R4),                  reacting with carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and                  hydrogen in presence of a                  high-temperature-resistant methane catalyst (C4)                  to obtain a methane stream (S4) comprising less                  than 1 vol.% carbon monoxide, passing stream (S4)                  into a reactor (R5), producing methane stream                  (S5) comprising less than 6000 ppm carbon                  monoxide, passing stream (S4) or (S5) through a                  gas-water separation device to obtain the                  original stream, and introducing a carbon                  dioxide-rich gas into reactor (R2), (R3), (R4)                  and/or (R5) with a hydrogen-carbon ratio of                  (2.9-3.5):1, and passing a hydrogen-rich gas into                  reactor (R2), (R3), and/or (R4). The carbon                  dioxide-rich gas comprises less than 3 vol.%                  carbon dioxide, so that the reaction temperature                  is 200-500 degrees C. Liquefaction process is                  performed by decarburizing by entering liquefied                  raw gas into an absorption device, decarbonizing                  in the upper portion of the absorption device,                  separating the entrained solution by a demister,                  cooling to less than 40 degrees C, separating                  water through the gas-liquid separation device to                  obtain the raw material gas, which is sent to the                  liquefaction process. The carbon dioxide                  component in the liquefied feed gas is absorbed                  by with an active methyldiethanolamine composite                  solution.
DC H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

PN CN110527663-A
TI In vitro fertilization of mouse oocytes involves            droplet preparing with M2 medium, mouse treating,            collecting oocyte, performing sperm capacitation,            co-incubating sperm and eggs, preparing embryo culture            fluid, and performing culturing.
AU ZHANG J
   JING N
   HAO J
   ZHANG C
   ZHU K
   XU Q
   NIU H
   LI W
AE UNIV HENAN ANIMAL HUSBANDRY & ECONOMY (UAHE-C)
GA 2019A3841X
AB    NOVELTY - In vitro fertilization of mouse oocytes                involves making droplets with M2 medium,                equilibrating overnight in cell incubator with 5%                carbon dioxide, selecting healthy and unpregnant                female mice, injecting pregnant horse serum                gonadotropin , sacrificing, immersing in alcohol,                removing mouse fur, cutting abdominal cavity,                removing ovaries, washing, putting into in vitro                operating solution, piercing follicles to release                oocytes, selecting germinal vesicle-stage oocytes,                and incubating; perfoming sperm capacitation with                male Kunming mice; centrifuging harvested sperm,                adding vitro fertilization fluid, adjusting sperm                density, washing mature eggs with in vitro                fertilization fluid, and co-incubating; and                preparing embryo culture fluid, removing fertilized                oocyte, washing in balanced embryo culture fluid,                transferring washed fertilized eggs into a balanced                embryo culture medium, culturing, observing                cleavage of the fertilized eggs after 24 hours of                culture, and calculating cleavage rate.
   USE - The method is used for in vitro fertilizing                mouse oocytes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - In vitro fertilization of mouse oocytes                comprises:
   (A) making droplets with M2 medium,                transferring to 37 degrees C, equilibrating                overnight in cell incubator with 5% carbon dioxide,                selecting healthy and unpregnant female mice                weighing 22-25 g, injecting pregnant horse serum                gonadotropin at amount of 0.1 mL, sacrificing after                48 hours by cervical dislocation, immersing in 75                vol.% alcohol for 0.5-1 minute, removing mouse fur,                cutting abdominal cavity, removing ovaries, washing                blood and lipid droplets into phosphate-buffered                saline buffer solution, putting m into in vitro                operating solution, piercing follicles to release e                oocytes, selecting germinal vesicle-stage oocytes,                washing in M2 medium for 2-3 minutes, incubating at                37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide for 14 hours,                and observing oocyte maturation in vitro;
   (B) incubating sperm capacitation solution HTF                at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide for 12                hours, selecting healthy male Kunming mice of 8-10                weeks old, sacrificing, sterilizing in 75 vol.%                alcohol for 1-2 minutes, cutting hind abdomen of                the mouse, cutting testicles, epididymis, and vas                deferens, washing in phosphate-buffered saline                buffer, washing away lipid droplets and blood,                separating epididymis and vas deferens from testes,                putting epididymis and vas deferens in pre-balanced                sperm capacitation solution, shredding, incubating                for 10 minutes to swim sperm out, removing                fragmented tissue in the sperm capacitation                solution, centrifuging, discarding supernatant,                adding 200 mu L balanced sperm capacitation                solution and sperm, incubating at 37 degrees C,                capacitating for 40-80 minutes, semen collecting,                centrifuging, capacitating at 37 degrees C, and                calculating sperm density and sperm motility;
   (C) centrifuging harvested sperm, adding vitro                fertilization fluid, adjusting sperm density,                washing mature eggs with 2-3 times in vitro                fertilization fluid, and co-incubating at 37                degrees C for 2-8 hours; and
   (D) preparing embryo culture fluid, removing                fertilized oocyte, washing in balanced embryo                culture fluid for 2-3 times, transferring washed                fertilized eggs into a balanced embryo culture                medium, culturing at 37 degrees C, observing                cleavage of the fertilized eggs after 24 hours of                culture, and calculating cleavage rate.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN108653447-A
TI Preparing complex Chinese herbal formulation useful for e.g. treating pain by pulverizing pseudoginseng and safflower, adding snake oil, extracting, distilling extract, dissolving borneol in ethanol, adding extract, mixing and adjusting pH.
AU ZHONG J
AE JIANGXI RENJUTANG PHARM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018830762
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing complex Chinese herbal formulation involves (a) coarsely pulverizing pseudoginseng, safflower, Caesalpinia cucullata and Ledebouriella seseloides to obtain mixture (A), adding snake oil, extracting the mixture under reflux with ethanol, distilling the extract, passing the distillate through an adsorption resin column, washing with distilled water, then eluting with ethanol, collecting the eluate and distilling to obtain extract (B), (b) coarsely pulverizing cinnamon and camphor tree to obtain a mixture (C), performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain extract (D), (c) coarsely pulverizing mint, adding turpentine, wintergreen oil, clove oil and Eucalyptus oil to obtain a mixture (E), performing super critical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain an extract (F) and (d) dissolving borneol in ethanol, adding extracts (B), (D) and (F), uniformly mixing and adjusting the pH.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing complex Chinese herbal medicinal formulation in the form of solution, powder, lotion, oil agent, emulsion, ointment, paste, plaster, film coating agent or topical formulation for treating pain and providing anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects (all claimed). The complex Chinese herbal medicinal formulation is also useful for treating rheumatic bone pain, joint pain, shoulder pain and bruises and relieving excessive strain.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared complex Chinese herbal medicinal formulation has excellent biocompatibility and no allergic property, easily penetrates the skin and is easy to absorb and synergistic effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing complex Chinese herbal formulation involves (a) coarsely pulverizing 30-50 pts. wt. pseudoginseng, 10-20 pts. wt. safflower, 5-10 pts. wt. Caesalpinia cucullata and 10-20 pts. wt. Ledebouriella seseloides to obtain mixture (A), adding 5-8 pts. wt. snake oil, extracting the mixture under reflux with 50-80% ethanol, distilling the extract under reduced pressure to a drug content of 0.1-2 g/ml, passing the distillate through an adsorption resin column, washing with distilled water until the precipitate negatively to the Molisch test, then eluting with 50-80% ethanol, collecting the eluate and distilling under reduced pressure to recover the ethanol and obtain extract (B), (b) coarsely pulverizing 5-10 pts. wt. cinnamon and 5-10 pts. wt. camphor tree to obtain a mixture (C), performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain extract D having a drug content of 0.1-2 g/ml, (c) coarsely pulverizing 5-10 pts. wt. mint, adding 5-10 pts. wt. turpentine, 3-5 pts. wt. wintergreen oil, 3-5 pts. wt. clove oil and 3-5 pts. wt. Eucalyptus oil to obtain a mixture (E), performing super critical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain an extract (F) having a drug content of 0.1-2 g/ml and (d) dissolving 5-10 pts. wt. borneol in 50-80% ethanol, adding extracts (B), (D) and (F), uniformly mixing and adjusting the pH to 4.5-7.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves extracting the mixture twice at 60-80 degrees C for 3 hours (each time), where the liquid to material ratio is 3:1. In steps (a)-(c), pulverizing by jet milling, grinding, shear emulsification or a ball milling method. In step (a), the adsorption resin is XDA-1 macroporous adsorption resin, D101 macroporous adsorption resin or H20 macroporous adsorption resin. The steps (b) and (c) involves performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at 10-25 MPa and 20-50 degrees C for 1-3 hours. The step (d) involves adjusting the pH using phosphate buffer solution as a pH adjuster.
   ACTIVITY - Analgesic; Antiinflammatory; Antimicrobial; Vulnerary.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. TI Comprehensive utilization method of water lettuce comprises e.g. collecting fresh water lettuce stem leaf, cleaning, taking raw material, placing into glass container to mix uniformly, and placing the glass container.
AU TIAN L
AE UNIV NANYANG NORMAL (NYSF-C)
   UNIV NANYANG NORMAL (NYSF-C)
GA 201874812X
AB    NOVELTY - Comprehensive utilization method of water lettuce comprises preparing biological preparation, preparation process of bio-fuel, preparation process of collecting process and bio-food grade carbon dioxide, where specific step is (i) collecting fresh water lettuce stem leaf, cleaning, and water lettuce stem leaf and water is 1:8-12, taking raw material, placing into glass container to mix uniformly, placing the glass container into the microwave heating device to water lettuce stem leaf biologic active substance extracting and separating, the acid insoluble ash pre-processing the wood cellulose in the water lettuce stem leaf, removing in lignocellulose, which is convenient for subsequent enzymolysis and fermentation of lignocellulose, finishing the extraction in the extracting liquid and residue, separating the extracting solution vacuum extracting and concentrating into the vacuum evaporation tank.
   USE - The method is useful for comprehensive utilization method of water lettuce.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Comprehensive utilization method of water lettuce comprises preparing biological preparation, preparation process of bio-fuel, preparation process of collecting process and bio-food grade carbon dioxide, where specific step is (i) collecting fresh water lettuce stem leaf, cleaning, and water lettuce stem leaf and water is 1:8-12, taking raw material, placing into glass container to mix uniformly, (ii) placing the glass container into the microwave heating device to water lettuce stem leaf biologic active substance extracting and separating, the acid insoluble ash pre-processing the wood cellulose in the water lettuce stem leaf, removing in lignocellulose, which is convenient for subsequent enzymolysis and fermentation of lignocellulose, where the microwave power is 280-300 W, the heating temperature is 45-50 degrees C, the extracting time is 8-10 minutes, (iii) finishing the extraction in the extracting liquid and residue, separating the extracting solution vacuum extracting and concentrating into the vacuum evaporation tank, and the concentrated liquid preparation is a biological agent, vacuum extracting and condensing temperature is 20-30 degrees C, the pressure is -0.08 to 0.1 MPa, (iv) water lettuce stem leaf residues rich in cellulose biomass, by using yeast fermentation process for preparing bio-fuel ethanol, and collecting the carbon dioxide generated in the fermentation process, where the weight of the cellulase producing food grade carbon dioxide during the fermentation process in each gram of residue added is 38-42IU, the inoculation amount of the yeast is 3% the total amount of the residue 3.5%, the fermentation temperature is 35-38 degrees C, the fermentation time is 68-72 hours, ethanol yield is 10-10.5%, and (v) preparing bio cracking at high temperature and high pressure, the remaining main component of water lettuce yeast fermented residue is lignin, the temperature is 350-400 degrees C, the pressure is 8-12 MPa, the conversion rate of the lignin is 70-75%, the yield of the bio-oil is 68-70%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The weighed mixture of water lily lotus stems and water is placed in a crusher and pulverized into a thick suspension mixture, followed by microwave-assisted extraction and separation. In the step (ii), the microwave heating device is a vacuum microwave heating device, vacuum degree is 0.06-0.08 MPa. In the step (iii), preparation of biological preparation is oil antioxidant and microbial agent. In the step (iv), the used saccharomycetes is Saccharomyces cerevisiae and Pichia kudriavzevii, and Pichia pastoris is mass ratio of 1:1-1.5. In the step (iv), collecting the produced in the fermentation process of carbon dioxide to sterilize and after compression process to obtain the food grade carbon dioxide. In the step (v), the thermal cracking process, adding catalyst sodium formate, dosage is 0.5-8% of the total amount of the reaction system. In the step (v), the biological oil comprises phenol, guaiacol and syringyl aromatic compounds, where guaiacol and 2,6-dimethoxyphenol with mass fraction of 45-50%.

PN CN108147435-A; CN108147435-B
TI Carbonization process useful for soda production            comprises e.g. controlling ammonia brine temperature,            conveying into washing tower from top to bottom, flows            out through bottom of tower to obtain cleaning liquid,            and heating.
AU NIE Y
   YUAN R
   ZHAO L
AE CHONGQING YIHUA CHEM CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
   CHONGQING XIANGYU SALT CHEM CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 201847361D
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonization process comprises e.g.                controlling ammonia brine temperature at 15-22                degrees C, the composition ammonia/sodium chloride                in 1.1-1.3, conveying it into washing tower from                top to bottom, flows out through bottom of tower to                obtain cleaning liquid, heating the cleaning liquid                to 30-36 degrees C, and transferring to top part of                the alkali tower, controlling mid-stage gas                containing 40-45% carbon dioxide from the heavy                alkali calcining furnace at 0.25-0.30 MPa by the                mid-stage gas compressor, controlling at 42-47                degrees C by the mid-stage gas cooler, passing from                the middle of the alkali making tower into the                tower to participate in the carbonation reaction,                bottom gas containing 92-97% carbon dioxide from                the heavy alkali calciner, controlling pressure of                the lower stage compressor at 0.30-0.35 MPa,                controlling lower air cooler temperature at 28-32                degrees C, transferring to the bottom of the tower                and continuing to carbonize the cleaning                solution.
   USE - The method is useful for carbonization process                for soda production (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbonization process comprises (1)                controlling ammonia brine temperature at 15-22                degrees C, the composition ammonia/sodium chloride                in 1.1-1.3, conveying it into washing tower from                top to bottom, flows out through bottom of tower to                obtain cleaning liquid, heating the cleaning liquid                to 30-36 degrees C, and transferring to top part of                the alkali tower, (2) controlling mid-stage gas                containing 40-45% carbon dioxide from the heavy                alkali calcining furnace at 0.25-0.30 MPa by the                mid-stage gas compressor, controlling at 42-47                degrees C by the mid-stage gas cooler, passing from                the middle of the alkali making tower into the                tower to participate in the carbonation reaction,                (3) bottom gas containing 92-97% carbon dioxide                from the heavy alkali calciner, controlling                pressure of the lower stage compressor at 0.30-0.35                MPa, controlling lower air cooler temperature at                28-32 degrees C, transferring to the bottom of the                tower and continuing to carbonize the cleaning                solution, and (4) during the reaction, controlling                the upper temperature of the carbonization tower at                45-60 degrees C, and the middle temperature at                65-70 degrees C, the cooling rate between the                bottom of the middle part of the carbonation tower                and the lower part of 1/3 is 0.3-0.8 degrees                C/minute, the cooling rate in the lower 1/3 to 2/3                of the carbonation tower is 1.5-2.5 degrees                C/minute, the cooling rate of the remaining 1/3 of                the carbonization tower is 3-5 degrees C/minute,                taking out the produced sodium bicarbonate                suspension from the bottom of the tower and                conveying to the alkali concentration tank, which                completes the carbonization process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The temperature of the                  ammonia brine in step (1) is 18 degrees C, the                  composition ammonia/sodium chloride is 1.2 and                  the cleaning solution was heated to 33 degrees C.                  In the step (2), the carbon dioxide content in                  the mid-stream gas is 43%, the pressure in the                  middle section is 0.24 MPa and the mid-range gas                  temperature is 45 degrees C. In the step (3), the                  carbon dioxide content in the lower gas is 94%,                  the pressure in the lower gas is 0.3 MPa, and the                  temperature of the lower gas is 30 degrees C. In                  the step (4), the upper temperature of the                  carbonization tower is 56 degrees C and the                  middle temperature is 68 degrees C. In step (4),                  the cooling rate between the lowermost part of                  the carbonization tower and the lower part 1/3 is                  0.5 degrees C/minute, the cooling rate between                  the lower 1/3 to 2/3 of the carbonation tower is                  2 degrees C/minute, and the cooling rate in the                  remaining 1/3 section of the carbonation tower is                  4 degrees C/minute.

PN CN107868686-A
TI Energy-saving complete methanation of synthetic natural gas using non-recycled coal comprises e.g. performing gasification, conversion, and purification, rising temperature, heating, and mixing a strand-dispensing raw gas with water vapor.
AU QIN Z
   JIN Y
   MIAO M
   SHANGGUAN J
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 2018271226
AB    NOVELTY - Energy-saving complete methanation of synthetic natural gas using non-recycled coal comprises e.g. performing gasification, conversion, purification as raw material gas of the methanation of coal, reacting the raw material gas and the gas four-section insulating reactor at the first heat exchanger after heat exchange, rising the temperature to 240-270 degrees C, heating at the second heat exchanger and heating the second stage heat exchange type reactor outlet gas to 280-320 degrees C, total raw gas distribution into two parts, and mixing a strand-dispensing raw gas with water vapor.
   USE - The method is useful for energy-saving complete methanation of synthetic natural gas using non-recycled coal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is energy saving; and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Energy-saving complete methanation of synthetic natural gas using non-recycled coal comprises (i) performing gasification, conversion, purification as raw material gas of the methanation of coal, reacting the raw material gas and the gas four-section insulating reactor at the first heat exchanger after heat exchange, rising the temperature to 240-270 degrees C, heating at the second heat exchanger and heating the second stage heat exchange type reactor outlet gas to 280-320 degrees C, total raw gas distribution into two parts, mixing a strand-dispensing raw gas with water vapor, entering a section of heat exchange in the reactor, under function of nickel series catalyst, hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, performing the methanation reaction, (ii) heating the gas after a period of exchange reactor after steam heat recovery with the rest other raw material gas mixing, enters the two-stage heat exchange type reactor under the action of nickel series catalyst, hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide methanation reaction, (iii) cooling to 280 degrees C through the second heat exchanger of the gas after reaction by two-section heat-exchanging reactor, the three-section insulating reactor under function of nickel series catalyst, hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide performing methanation reaction, (iv) cooling the gas three-section insulating reactor after reaction through the third heat exchanger and the fourth heat exchanger to 200-240 degrees C, removing part of condensed water at the first steam-water separator, exchanging and warming up to 280 degrees C at the third heat exchanger, the four-section insulating reactor, under function of nickel series catalyst, hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide for methanation reaction, and (v) providing the gas after reaction by four-section insulating reactor, where the temperature is 350-400 degrees C, cooling the heat exchange at the first heat exchanger and raw material gas to below 80 degrees C, the cooling water by the second water separator, separating out the second steam-water separator lower part boundary region, and the second steam-water separator upper part of outlet gas is product gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The total sulfur content of the raw gas is less than or equal to 10ppb and the pressure is 2-4 MPa, and satisfy the stoichiometric ratio of (hydrogen-carbon dioxide)/carbon monoxide greater than or equal to 3. The gas after reaction through a section of heat exchange type reactor in methane content is 50-60%, heating temperature is controlled at 450-550 degrees C. The gas after reaction by two-section heat-exchanging reactor in methane content is 60-70%, heating temperature is controlled at 450-550 degrees C. The gas after the three-section heat exchange reactor in methane content 60-70%, carbon dioxide less than 3%, heating temperature is controlled at 400-450 degrees C. The reaction of the four-stage heat exchange reactor, the methane content in the gas is 80-90%, carbon dioxide content less than 1%, hot spot temperature is controlled at 350-400 degrees C. The second steam-water separator upper part of the gas is natural gas, the methane content of 90-93%, and the standard reaches international GB17820-1999 level, high calorific power reach more than 31.4 MJ/m3. The steam recovered by a section of heat exchange type reactor and two-section heat-exchanging reactor steam passes through the heater, overheating to 400-450 degrees C, sending the boundary region as the power of the rotating device. The heat exchange is carried out by circulation of the desalted water desalted water can be propelled tube side or walking shell column. The heat-exchanging reactor can refer to synthetic natural gas produced methanol in the process of heat exchange reactor, heat reactor can be vertical or is horizontal.

Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control.    ADVANTAGE - Invention increases degree of extraction of iodine, as well as exclude from process fire hazardous organic extractants and diluents, which provides environmental safety of method.

PT P
PN CN109439429-A
TI Supercritical extraction method of walnut oil,            involves drying walnut, crushing dried walnuts, and            extracting walnut oil from the crushed walnut kernel            using supercritical carbon dioxide fluid.
AU TIAN X
   TIAN G
   DENG L
AE GUIZHOU AEROSPACE WUJIANG ELECTRO-MECHAN (CAES-C)
GA 2019245035
AB    NOVELTY - The supercritical extraction method of walnut                oil involves (1) drying walnut, (2) crushing dried                walnuts, (3) extracting walnut oil from the crushed                walnut kernel using supercritical carbon dioxide                fluid.
   USE - Supercritical extraction method of walnut                oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables supercritical extraction of                walnut oil with low solvent consumption, high                yield, excellent quality, color and luster, high                purity, and short extracting period, and without                peculiar smell and solvent residue, by simple,                economical and environmentally-friendly                process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: During the extraction,                  the dried walnut powder is placed in a material                  bucket of an extraction kettle, injecting carbon                  dioxide gas into extraction vessel, keeping the                  temperature and pressure in the extraction vessel                  in contact with natural materials in the                  supercritical state of carbon dioxide gas,                  dissolving the walnut oil component in the walnut                  powder in the supercritical fluid to collect                  fluid containing walnut oil from an outlet of a                  separation kettle. Preferred Conditions: The                  temperature in the supercritical state is 35-45                  degrees C. The extraction pressure is 35-45 MPa.                  The flow rate of carbon dioxide gas through the                  extraction vessel is 150-250 kg/hour, the                  extraction time is 1.2-2.5 hours, the separation                  temperature is 30-45 degrees C, and the                  separation pressure is 14 MPa. The drying is                  performed by placing the washed walnut into an                  oven, drying the walnuts at 50-60 degrees C to a                  moisture content of 10-15%, placing in a crusher,                  and crushing into walnut powder having a particle                  size of more than 60 mesh.

PT P
PN IN201641030523-A; WO2018047191-A1; US2020231511-A1
TI Processing brine solution used to make fertilizer involves contacting brine solution with ion exchanger i.e. contacted with eluent solution comprising ammonium carbonates to remove metal cations, and recovering metal carbonate rich solution.
AU KOSURI T
   SHARMA K
AE KOSURI T (KOSU-Individual)
   SHARMA K (SHAR-Individual)
   SHARMA K (KRIS-Individual)
   KOSURI T (TANU-Individual)
   SHARMA K (SHAR-Individual)
   KOSURI T (KOSU-Individual)
GA 2018215668
AB    NOVELTY - Processing a brine solution, comprises: contacting the brine solution with an ion exchanger to bind metal cations including potassium and sodium with the ion exchanger; contacting the ion exchanger with an eluent solution(s) comprising ammonium carbonates to remove metal cations from the ion exchanger; recovering a metal carbonate rich solution from the solution eluted from the ion exchanger; and removing at least one of ammonia, carbon dioxide and ammonium carbonates from the metal carbonate rich solution to form a metal ion rich solution.
   USE - The method is useful for processing a brine solution to produce fertilizer formulation consisting of potassium, nitrogen-potassium, phosphorus-potassium, potassium-sulfur, NPK-sulfur or NPK fertilizers (claimed) that are useful for healthy growth of crops. No biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is a zero waste/effluent process as it allows to recycle many process streams and avoids the formation of harmful waste products and where the portions of the produced brine solutions can be recycled or discarded in the ocean or combined with other brine sources, hence environmentally beneficial; provides customized fertilizer compositions or enables the co-production of fertilizers and potable water, thus offering powerful socioeconomic benefits, because the fundamental needs of humans (food and water) are met through local production, distribution and consumption, while creating local jobs, eliminating dependency on imports, and offering clean and green environment; and allows the carbon dioxide to be converted into solid form, hence the process is carbon negative.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) selectively recovering potassium salts from a brine source, comprising selectively and preferentially removing potassium ions from a brine solution by an ion exchange media, treating the ion exchange media using first and second concentrations of ammoniacal salt solutions comprising carbonate or bicarbonate anions, treating the ion exchange media with a concentrated salt or brine solution, where a weight of total dissolved salt in the concentrated salt or brine solution is 3-60 wt.%, collecting eluates into multiple storage tanks, and either partially or fully recombining, regenerating, recycling, reusing, and reinjecting material from any of the above eluates back into the source to maximize the process efficiency; and
   (2) recovering carbon dioxide from the environment to obtain potassium carbonate, comprising selectively recovering potassium ions from brine solutions using an ion exchange media, reacting carbon dioxide recovered from the environment with ammonia to form ammonium carbonate or ammonium bicarbonate solution, treating the ion exchange media with eluents comprising ammonium carbonate solution to recover potassium carbonate, and removing at least one of ammonia, carbon dioxide, and ammonium carbonate from the metal carbonate rich solution to obtain potassium carbonate rich solution.
EA    (WO2018047191-A1)   NOVELTY - A brine solution is processed by contacting brine solution with ion exchanger to bind metal cations including potassium (K) and sodium (Na) with ion exchanger; contacting ion exchanger with eluent solution(s) comprising ammonium carbonates to remove metal cations from ion exchanger; recovering metal carbonate rich solution from solution eluted from ion exchanger; and removing ammonia, carbon dioxide, and/or ammonium carbonates from metal carbonate rich solution to form a metal ion rich solution.
   USE - Processing a brine solution for recovering a variety of high purity potassium based salts used for producing fertilizers (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides a process with zero waste/effluent and generation of customized fertilizer compositions containing primary, secondary, and micronutrients, and optionally, other chemicals necessary for healthy growth of crops.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises: contacting the ion exchanger with a first eluent solution comprising an ammoniacal solution having a first concentration of ammonium to preferably remove sodium cations from the ion exchanger to obtain a sodium rich stream; and contacting the ion exchanger with a second eluent solution comprising ammonium carbonates to remove potassium cations from the ion exchanger to obtain a potassium rich solution. The method further comprises: contacting the ion exchanger with an eluent solution comprising ammonium carbonates having concentration of 0.7-7 M to recover the metal cations from the ion exchanger; removing one or more of: ammonia, carbon dioxide, and ammonium carbonate from the metal carbonate rich solution to form a metal ion rich solution; and crystallizing the metal carbonate rich solution selectively to separate potassium carbonates/bicarbonates and sodium carbonates/bicarbonates from each other. The method further comprises a zero waste/effluent process, where the ammonium ions from the effluent stream are removed using the metal carbonates generated in the process to inject the discharge stream back into a salty body of water. The method further comprises the removal of at least one of ammonia, carbon dioxide, and ammonium carbonate from the metal carbonate rich solution, using a method comprising thermal, air stripping, steam stripping, sonication, agitation, membrane separation and/or solvent extraction to obtain the metal ion rich solution. The method further comprises concentrating the metal ion rich solution using a process comprising reverse osmosis, forward osmosis, water evaporation, water distillation, multi-stage flash evaporation, multi-effect distillation, multi-stage flash distillation, mechanical and thermal vapor compression, or solvent extraction, preferably using forward osmosis with seawater or seawater reject or concentrated seawater or concentrated brine or potassium depleted seawater or ammonium carbonate solutions, as draw solutions. The metal ion rich solution comprises a potassium rich solution, and the method further comprises drying the potassium rich solution to obtain potassium carbonate having a purity greater than 80%, or preferably greater than 90%. The first eluent solution comprising ammonium further comprises at least one additional ammonium salt of formula ((NH4)nX(n)) (I). The method further comprises removing ammonia and carbon dioxide from the potassium rich solution to recover compounds of formula (KX) (II).n = net negative charge;X = inorganic or organic anion (Cl, Br, I , F, borates, oxides, phosphonates, phosphates, carboxylates, sulfates, sulfonates, carbonates, SO4, HSO4, SO3, S2O3, S2O7, PO4, H2PO4, HPO4, OH, SiO4, NO3, CO3, HCO3, BO3, B2O7, BF4, CH3COO, R-PO3, R-COO and/or R-SO3; andR = organic moiety.The method further comprises contacting the metal carbonate rich solution or the metal-ion rich solution with at least one of organic or inorganic acids, metal salts, metal oxides, metal hydroxides, to form at least one nitrogen-phosphorus-potassium (N-P-K) fertilizing chemicals. The ammonia generated is contacted with a carbonate source under reaction conditions to obtain ammonium bicarbonate. The method further comprises contacting the gaseous ammonia from the metal carbonate rich solution with an aqueous or acid source under reaction conditions to obtain a recovered salt comprising ammonium salt. The method further comprises recycling the recovered ammonium salt solution to contact the ion exchanger. The method further comprises adjusting the concentration of the ammonium salt solution to form a first recovered eluent solution and contacting the first recovered eluent solution to preferentially remove sodium cations from the ion exchanger and form a sodium rich solution. The method further comprises adjusting the concentration of the ammonium bicarbonate and/or (I) to form a second recovered eluent solution, and contacting the second recovered eluent solution to preferentially remove potassium cations from the ion exchanger, to obtain a potassium carbonate or KX rich solution. The method further comprises processing the metal carbonate rich solution, the metal ion rich solution, or potassium rich solution to produce a fertilizer composition. The method further comprises adding at least one of primary and secondary macro- and micro- nutrients including at least one nutrient comprising phosphorus, nitrogen, sulfur, calcium, magnesium, boron, chlorine, manganese, iron, zinc, copper, molybdenum and nickel, and other additives comprising pesticides, insecticides, fungicides, herbicides, organic extracts or natural extracts, to the fertilizing composition. The method further comprises contacting the metal carbonate rich solution, metal ion rich solution, or the potassium rich solution with at least one of phosphoric acid, nitric acid, phosphorus rock, rock phosphate, calcium phosphates, ammonium phosphates, urea, calcium hydrogen phosphate/calcium apatite or phosphoric acid or nitric acid or sulfuric acid or sodium carbonate and bicarbonate or calcium hydroxide to obtain calcium carbonate or phosphoric acid or sodium hydroxide or ammonium hydroxide or calcium hydrogen phosphate or phosphates of sodium and potassium to produce NK, PK, and NPK fertilizers. Preferred Components: The potassium rich solution is potassium carbonate. The eluent solution has a second concentration of ammonium carbonates, where the first concentration is lower than the second concentration. The ammonium carbonate I ammonium carbonate and/or ammonium bicarbonate. The eluent solution has a first concentration of ammonium carbonates and any other ammonium salts having a weight of total dissolved salt of 0.2-50 wt.%, and where the eluent solution has a second concentration of ammonium carbonates having a weight of total dissolved salt of 0.4-50 wt.%. The first concentration of ammonium carbonates is 0.05-0.75M. The eluent solution has a second concentration of ammonium carbonates of 0.75-5M. The potassium ion rich solution has concentration from 3-100 g/l. The metal carbonate rich solution comprises at least one of phosphorus, nitrogen, sulfur, calcium, magnesium, boron, chlorine, manganese, iron, zinc, copper molybdenum and nickel. The potassium ion selective ion exchanger includes a material consisting of inorganic or organic or hybrid particulate materials, particulate materials that are coated or agglomerated with polymeric or inorganic binders/cross linkers, or heat treated particulate materials.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN115203879-A
TI Method for identifying carbon neutralizing            greenhouse gas emission based on large data correlation            analysis technology, involves clustering and analyzing            atmospheric backward trajectory model, establishing            electronic pollution map, and identifying carbon            emission source and discharge unit.
AU SUN Y
AE SUN Y (SUNY-Individual)
GA 2022D3591X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves extracting                high-concentration pixel points of statistical                satellite remote sensing products. Ground                observation data is acquired. Reverse simulation                analysis calculation process is performed. An                atmospheric backward trajectory model is clustered                and analyzed. An electronic pollution map is                established. A carbon emission source and a                discharge unit are identified. Statistics satellite                remote sensing products and high-concentration                pixel points are extracted based on satellite                remote sensing products. A major greenhouse gas                methane and carbon dioxide concentrations are                retrieved.
   USE - Method for identifying carbon neutralizing                greenhouse gas emission based on large data                correlation analysis technology. Can be used in                technical field of environment monitoring.
   ADVANTAGE - The reliability and accuracy of the                near-ground atmosphere methane and carbon dioxide                concentration product can be ensured. The                greenhouse gas space-time distribution                characteristic in large scale can be obtained. The                carbon discharge source can be identified                accurately.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                a method for identifying carbon neutralizing                greenhouse gas emission based on large data                correlation analysis technology (Drawing includes                non-English language text).

AU HUSAIN M

   ADVANTAGE - The two-dimensional heterojunction                graphene/organometalloporphyrin polymer: large                specific surface area, more exposed active sites,                fast charge transfer speed, monodispersed active                center and stable structure, and excellent                performance in electrocatalytic reduction of carbon                dioxide.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (3), the reaction                  atmosphere is composed of nitrogen, argon and/or                  oxygen during the reaction under a reaction                  atmosphere; and the reaction is performed at                  25-120&#8451; for 0.1-168 hours. In step (4),                  the post-processing process comprises suction                  filtering, washing with pyridine,                  N,N-dimethylformamide, ethanol, and deionized                  water, and performing centrifugation to obtain                  filter cake; soaking was in hydrochloric acid,                  washing with deionized water, and finally                  freeze-drying to obtain two-dimensional                  heterojunction graphene/organometalloporphyrin                  polymer.Preferred Components: In step (1), the                  coupling catalyst metal salt is copper(II)                  acetate, copper(II) chloride, copper powder,                  copper(I) iodide, copper(I) chloride or copper(I)                  bromide; the first solvent is composed of                  pyridine, triethylamine, ethylenediamine,                  tetramethylethylenediamine,                  diisopropylethylamine, N,N-dimethylformamide,                  N-methylpyrrolidone, dichloromethane and/or                  methanol; and the mass ratio of graphene and                  coupling catalyst metal salt is 1:0.01-1:10. In                  step (2), the                  5,10,15,20-tetrakis(4-ethynylphenyl)porphyrin                  metal monomer is of formula (I); and the second                  solvent is composed of pyridine, triethylamine,                  ethylenediamine, tetramethylethylenediamine,                  diisopropylethylamine, N,N-dimethylformamide,                  N-methylpyrrolidone, dichloromethane and/or                  methanol; the mass ratio of                  5,10,15,20-tetrakis(4-ethynylphenyl)porphyrin                  metal monomer to graphene is 1:0.1-1:10.R1-R16= H, fluoro, alkyl, alkynyl, alkenyl,                  aryl, amino, hydroxy, methoxy, carboxylate group,                  carboxy, ester group, cyano, aldehyde group,                  carbonyl or nitro; X = absent or chloro, carbony,                  pyridyl, methoxy or hydroxy; andM = manganese, iron, cobalt, nickel, copper,                  zinc, aluminum, palladium, molybdenum, ruthenium                  or platinum metal atoms.

AB    NOVELTY - Group of inventions relates to means of                purifying air and maintaining standard levels of                carbon dioxide in the air for breathing in confined                spaces. The purification method involves blowing                air through a regenerated carbon dioxide absorber.                The absorber is made in the form of activated                carbon granules impregnated with an aqueous                solution of diethylamine. Preferably, the absorber                is placed in several layers in a cartridge with                mesh walls to ensure its purging, essentially                evenly by volume. The air is additionally                disinfected with a UV emitter with radiation                wavelengths of more than 185 nm located upstream                from the absorber. After the absorber, the purified                air is enriched with a complex of microelements                that are part of sea or mountain salt. Regeneration                and disinfection of the absorber is carried out by                heating it to a temperature of at least 65C with a                stream of hot air. The concentration of CO2 in the                air of a limited space is maintained automatically                in a given range. The method is carried out using                an air purification device.
   USE - Air purification.
   ADVANTAGE - Group of inventions makes it possible to                expand the arsenal of means to improve the                efficiency and quality of air purification.

   HUO G

   CHEN Y
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 202270621L
AB    NOVELTY - Comprehensive utilization and treatment method                of wolframite resources, comprises (a) fine                grinding and activating wolframite, and carrying                out hydrochloric acid decomposition to obtain solid                tungstic acid and acid decomposition mother liquor,                (b) mixing solid tungstic acid with aqueous                ammonia, stirring and leaching to obtain primary                slag and ammonium tungstate solution, (c) mixing                primary slag with sodium hydroxide solution, and                subjecting to pressure leaching to obtain leaching                solution and tin, tantalum and niobium enriched                slag, (d) adding modifier into leaching solution                and mixing, heating in water bath, carrying out                reaction by passing carbon dioxide gas, and                performing solid-liquid separation to obtain                precipitate and sodium tungstate solution, and (e)                washing and drying the precipitate to obtain                finished white carbon black.
   USE - The method is useful for comprehensive                utilization and treatment method of wolframite                resources.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient decomposition                effect of wolframite, and has high extraction rate                of tungsten; and ensures recycling of hydrochloric                acid and comprehensive utilization of                wolframite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (a): the mass                  concentration of hydrochloric acid in the                  hydrochloric acid decomposition process is                  20-36%, the liquid-solid ratio in the                  hydrochloric acid decomposition process is 3-4                  ml/g, the temperature of hydrochloric acid                  decomposition is 85-95 degrees Celsius, and the                  decomposition time is 3-5 hours. In step (b): the                  mass concentration of ammonia water is 25%, and                  its addition amount is 1.2-1.5 times the                  theoretical amount; the intensity of stirring and                  leaching is 250-350 revolutions/minutes; the                  temperature of stirring and leaching is 25-35                  degrees Celsius, and the stirring and leaching                  time is 2-3 hours. In step (c): the temperature                  of pressure leaching is 160-200 degrees Celsius,                  the time is 2-4 hours, and the pressure is 0.5-1                  MPa. In step (d): the temperature of water bath                  heating is 40-50 degrees Celsius; the flow rate                  of carbon dioxide gas is 0.5-1 l/minutes; passing                  carbon dioxide gas until the pH value of mixed                  solution is 8-9, and continuing the reaction for                  10-30 minutes. The method comprises subjecting                  the acid decomposition mother liquor to spray                  pyrolysis to obtain iron oxide, manganese oxide                  powder and hydrochloric acid. The spray rate is                  100-200 ml/minutes; the pyrolysis temperature is                  400-500 degrees Celsius; introducing air of flow                  rate 300-500 ml/minutes to spray pyrolysis                  process.Preferred Components: In step (a): in fine                  grinding and activation the particle size of                  wolframite is 95 D not greater than 20 microm. In                  step (c): the liquid-solid ratio of sodium                  hydroxide solution to primary slag is 4-7 ml/g,                  and the concentration of sodium hydroxide                  solution is 250-300 g/l. In step (d): the                  modifier is polyethylene glycol-6000, sodium                  dodecylbenzenesulfonate, or carboxymethyl                  cellulose; the addition amount of modifier is                  10-15% of the mass of sodium silicate in the                  leachate.
