AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high purity fucoidan, comprises (i)                cleaning brown algae, drying and then crushing into                brown algae powder, (ii) carrying out                low-temperature atomization extraction with brown                algae powder in calcium hydroxide aqueous solution,                and filtering to obtain extract, (iii) introducing                carbon dioxide precipitate excess calcium hydroxide                into the extracting solution, and then filtering to                obtain filtrate, (iv) adding cation exchange resin                into the filter liquid, stirring for 1-2 hours,                filtering, and spray-drying to obtain final                product.
   USE - The high purity fucoidan is useful in                preparing cosmetic with moisturizing                effect(claimed).

In step                  (iii), when introducing carbon dioxide to                  precipitate excess calcium hydroxide, the pH of                  the extraction solution is controlled to be                  7.5-8.0.

PN CN113716731-A
TI Calcium treatment for lithium hexafluorophosphate industrial wastewater involves adding calcium hydroxide into pretreated lithium hexafluorophosphate wastewater, adjusting pH value, adding flocculating agent, subjecting to centrifugal filtration, and filtering to obtain centrifugal mother liquor.
AU HORIO H
AE MORITA NEW ENERGY MATERIALS ZHANGJIAGANG (MORI-Non-standard)
GA 2021E56755
AB    NOVELTY - The process involves pretreating lithium hexafluorophosphate wastewater, adding calcium hydroxide into the lithium hexafluorophosphate wastewater, stirring, and adjusting the pH value of the lithium hexafluorophosphate wastewater to 10-12. The flocculating agent is added flocculating agent, allowed to stand for a period of time, subjected to centrifugal filtration and filtered to obtain a centrifugal mother liquor. Carbon dioxide is pumped into the centrifugal mother liquor through an aeration system, fully mixed with into the centrifugal mother liquor through an aeration system and allowed to stand for reaction after the aeration is finished. The flocculating agent is added into the lithium hexafluorophosphate wastewater, fully reacted, and filtered to obtain a filtrate. The filtrate is measured. The pH value of the filtrate is 7-8, and the calcium ion concentration is less than 150 parts per million (ppm).
   USE - Method for calcium treatment for lithium hexafluorophosphate industrial wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The calcium ions in the wastewater are treated by adopting carbon dioxide aeration, so that secondary pollution is avoided, and the process is energy-saving and environment-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Conditions: The lithium hexafluorophosphate wastewater is acidic wastewater, and the pH value of the acidic wastewater is 4-5. The cationic type of the flocculating agent is polyacrylamide (PAM), concentration of the flocculating agent is 0.5%, and the time of standing after adding is 30 minutes. The volume of the introduced carbon dioxide is 0.1-0.5% of the total volume of the centrifugal mother liquor. Carbon dioxide is sucked in through the air inlet and then is sprayed out in a bubble shape through a nozzle of the aeration equipment to be mixed with the wastewater. Carbon dioxide is mixed with the wastewater through a mechanical aeration method, and the impeller speed of an aeration system is 600-800 rotations/minute. The flocculant in step (4) is polyaluminum ferric chloride (PFAC), concentration is 0.5%, and the reaction time is 30 minutes.

AU ZIELINSKI M

   RUDNICKA A A
AE UNIV WARMINSKO MAZURSKI W OLSZTYNIE (ZURS-C)
GA 2021E3086T
AB    NOVELTY - Biogas is upgraded to quality of biomethane based on waste materials from methane fermentation process by pumping sludge formed after methane fermentation into aeration chamber to be blown with compressed air in an amount of 10-40 times greater than the volume of the obtained biogas, discharging resulting exhaust gas outside the system, pumping decarbonized anaerobic sludge to sludge refreshment chamber to biochemically remove residual oxygen present after aeration to be free free of oxygen, returning significant amount of carbon dioxide to reactor, and pumping to filter to upgrade biogas with filtration filling. Filtration filling is a post-fermentation sludge subjected to liming process at a dose of 0.9 g calcium oxide (CaO) per 1 g dry matter of the sludge, and as a result of contact with the filter layer, carbon dioxide is removed from the biogas with a final methane content of 95-97%, which is discharged as biomethane outside the filter.
   USE - The method is useful for upgrading biogas to quality of biomethane based on waste materials from methane fermentation process.

   USE - Loaded palladium-cobalt alloy catalyst is used for low-temperature combustion-oxidation of methane into water and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has excellent low-temperature methane combustion activity and stability, comprises palladium-cobalt alloy nanoparticles of uniform size, high dispersion degree, and high stability, and can be prepared industrially by a simple method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A loaded palladium-cobalt alloy catalyst comprises a palladium-cobalt nano-alloy as active component loaded in an amount of 0.1-2 wt.%, and a ceria-zirconia solid solution of formula: CexZr1-xO2 as carrier.
   x = 0.1-0.9.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carrier is obtained by adding a cerium precursor salt, a zirconium precursor salt, and an ammonium salt to polyethylene glycol having a molecular weight of 200-2000, grinding, leaving still for 8-24 hours, crystallizing for 10-24 hours at 80-120 degrees C, washing, drying, and calcining at 400-850 degrees C for 3-6 hours. Preferred Compounds: The palladium precursor salt is palladium nitrate, chloropalladic acid, palladium acetylacetonate, or ammonium palladium nitrate. The cobalt precursor salt is cobalt nitrate, cobalt chloride, cobalt acetate, or cobalt acetylacetonate. The cerium precursor salt is cerium nitrate, cerium chloride, cerium acetate, or ceric ammonium nitrate. The zirconium precursor salt is zirconium oxynitrate, zirconium chloride, zirconium acetate, or zirconium sulfate. The ammonium salt is ammonium carbonate, ammonium hydrogen carbonate, or urea.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Component: The nano-alloy has a grain diameter of 3-6 nm and a palladium/cobalt mass ratio of 1-10. Preferred Method: The nano-alloy is obtained by adding an alcohol solution of palladium precursor salt and cobalt precursor salt with an alcohol solution of oleylamine under vigorous stirring condition, heating to 140-220 degrees C, reacting, centrifuging, and washing to obtain alloy nanoparticles.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The alcohol solution is methanol, ethanol, ethylene glycol, propanol, and/or propanetriol solution.

   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides catalyst producing 5-membered ring carbonate compound in high yield from carbon dioxide and epoxy compound under relatively mild reaction conditions; and can be recovered catalyst by relatively simple method after 5-membered ring carbonate compound is prepared.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) metal composite catalyst prepared by the above method; and
   (2) producing 5-membered ring carbonate compound using the metal composite catalyst, comprising (a1) performing an addition reaction of an epoxy compound and carbon dioxide in the presence of the metal complex catalyst, and (b1) separating the 5-membered ring carbonate compound formed by the addition reaction.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for producing metal composite catalyst (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (a), the imidazole is added after mixing the metal halogen and the amine-based ligand according to the molar ratio. The step (b) is carried out by washing the precipitate with ethanol and filtration to recover the precipitate. In the step (c), the drying is carried out in a vacuum at 80-120 degrees C. The metal composite catalyst is recovered and reused after the production of the 5-membered ring carbonate compound. The epoxy compound is reacted with the metal composite catalyst in molar ratio of 1000:1-25:1. The step (b1) further comprises recovering the catalyst. In the step (b1), the ether is added to the reactant to separate the 5-membered ring carbonate compound and the catalyst. Preferred Components: The halogen element of the metal halogen (MX2) includes chloride, bromide and/or iodide. The amine-based ligand is an organic ligand comprising 1-4 primary amines, secondary amines or tertiary amines as functional group. The organic ligand contains tetraamine-based compound of formula (I). The metal halogen and the amine-based ligand are mixed in molar ratio of 20:80-40:40. The greater the nucleophilicity of the halogen element of the metal halogen with respect to the amine-based ligand, the greater the reactivity of the catalyst. The epoxy compound includes propylene oxide, butylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, allyl glycidyl ether, glycidol and/or styrene oxide.R = methyl or H.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN112023974-A; CN112023974-B
TI Preparation of phosphorus-cerium dioxide/graphitic carbon nitride heterojunction material used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide by slowly adding graphitic carbon nitride into ultra-pure water, and ultrasonically dispersing.
AU DONG L
   LI W
   ZOU W
   PU Y
   WEI X
   ZHU C
AE UNIV NANJING (UNAJ-C)
GA 2020C4308K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of phosphorus (P)-cerium dioxide (CeO2)/graphitic carbon nitride (g-C3N4) heterojunction material comprises at room temperature, slowly adding g-C3N4 into ultra-pure water, ultrasonically dispersing uniformly, slowly adding cerium nitrate hexahydrate (Ce(NO3)3.6H2O), and fully stirring to obtain the dispersion liquid E; adding sodium phosphate dodecahydrate (Na3PO4.12H2O) into ultra-pure water, and fully stirring and mixing uniformly to obtain dispersion; slowly dropping the dispersion liquid F into the dispersion liquid E, fully stirring, after uniformly mixing the reactant, transferring the reaction liquid into a reaction kettle, and conducting constant temperature thermal reaction; and after finishing the reaction, naturally cooling to room temperature, respectively washing with ultra-pure water and absolute ethyl alcohol, and drying under vacuum.
   USE - The method is for preparation of P-CeO2/g-C3N4 heterojunction material used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (CO2) (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, has low cost raw materials, and provides product, which has strong practicability and good economic benefits and environment-friendly benefits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for P-CeO2/g-C3N4 heterojunction material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The ratio of g-C3N4, Ce(NO3)3.6H2O and ultra-pure water is 0.4-0.8 g:0.3-0.7 g:10-35 ml. The ultrasonic dispersing time is 0.5-1 hour. The ratio of Na3PO4.12H2O and ultra-pure water is 0.002-0.01 g:20-35 ml. The ratio of g-C3N4, Ce(NO3)3.6H2O and ultra-pure water is 0.6 g:0.5 g:25 ml. The ultrasonic dispersion time is 1 hour. The ratio of Na3PO4.12H2O and ultra-pure water is 0.008 g:25 ml. The constant temperature thermal reaction is conducted at 160-180 degrees C for 8-14 hours. The vacuum drying is conducted at 50-60 degrees C for 5-10 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The washing time of ultra-pure water and absolute ethyl alcohol is 5 times. The preparation of g-C3N4 photocatalyst comprises putting urea in ceramic crucible, putting in a muffle furnace for calcining, cooling to room temperature, taking out the product and grinding.

PN CN111943234-A
TI Preparing large-diameter medicinal grade sterile sodium bicarbonate particles useful in dialysis powder comprises e.g. preparing saturated sodium bicarbonate solution, adding iron ion chelating agent, stirring, filtering, and collecting.
AU SUN J
   SONG H
   WU Y
   ZHANG X
AE HEBEI HUACHEN PHARM CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 2020B7226E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing large-diameter medicinal grade sterile sodium bicarbonate particles comprises (i) preparing saturated sodium bicarbonate solution by using purified water, (ii) adding iron ion chelating agent according to specific proportion to above mentioned solution, stirring and raising temperature to dissolve, (iii) passing in carbon dioxide gas, filtering, and collecting filtrate, (iv) continuously stirring at 55-65 degrees C, (v) preparing saturated sodium carbonate solution by using purified water and stirring at 70-75 degrees C, (vi) feeding carbon dioxide gas into saturated sodium bicarbonate solution according to specific proportion, and adding saturated sodium carbonate solution at addition rate of 2-4 ml/minute, where reaction solution at 55-65 degrees C, and (vii) collecting discharged fine crystals at regular intervals.
   USE - The large-diameter medicinal grade sterile sodium bicarbonate particles is useful in dialysis powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has excellent stability and higher ingredients content; achieves the preparation of large particle size and high yield sodium bicarbonate with fewer impurities; meets the quality standards of the Chinese pharmacopoeia; and lays a foundation for the industrial production of large-size sodium bicarbonate particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises (viii) carrying out reaction, adding fine crystals to reaction solution according to quality of fine crystals (g)/total volume of reaction solution (l)=2-4%, reducing temperature at 40-45 degrees C under 0.08-0.15 degrees C/minute, and cooling down at room temperature under 0.5-0.8 degrees C/minute, and (ix) allowing to stand for 10-12 hours, and filtering and drying crystal slurry. In the step (iii), the carbon dioxide gas is passed to adjusting the pH value not to be higher than 8.6. In the step (iv), the stirring is carried out at 280-320 revolutions/minute (rpm). In the step (v), the stirring is carried out at 280-320 rpm. In the step (vii), the interval time for 60-80 minutes. In the step (viii), the reaction time is the same as the interval time in the step (vii). Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the iron ion chelating agent comprises edetic acid or edetate. The edetate comprises disodium edetate or calcium disodium edentate. The mass ratio of the iron ion chelating agent to the purified water is 0.01-0.04:3-6.

PN IN202011006298-A
TI Non-invasive method for determining fruit quality, involves configuring fruit selection unit, several fruits, ethylene and carbon dioxide (CO2) sensor, processing unit, display unit, LCD display, user, and power unit.
AU SINGH D K
   RAJA P
   JERATH H
   SINGH K
AE UNIV LOVELY PROFESSIONAL (UYLO-Non-standard)
GA 2020A1606C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves configuring fruit selection unit, several fruits, ethylene and carbon dioxide (CO2) sensor, processing unit, display unit, LCD display, user, and power unit. The fruit selection unit is provided to select the fruits to check the quality. The fruit selection in the fruit selection unit is performed through rotatory switch installed. The ethylene and CO2 sensor is provided to detect ethylene and CO2 gases emitted by the fruits. The processing unit is set to process the information form sensor based on threshold level of the gases from the fruits. The processing unit is set to provide indication about its ripening and decomposition of fruits. The display unit is provided to display the information of the fruit quality. The rechargeable batteries are set to provide power supply.

   ADVANTAGE - The display unit displays the information to the user whether the fruit quality is good or bad.

TI Sulfur vacancy-rich bismuth sulfide nanowires used in electrocatalytic material for reducing carbon dioxide to formic acid, have abundant sulfur vacancies on their surface, and specific width and length.
AU MAI L
   ZHU J
   YANG W
   HAN C
AE UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
   UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
GA 2020668030
AB    NOVELTY - Sulfur vacancy-rich bismuth sulfide (RSV-Bi2S3) nanowires have abundant sulfur vacancies on their surface, and a width of 50-300 nm and a length of 100-200 mm.
   USE - The bismuth sulfide nanowires are used in an electrocatalytic material for reducing carbon dioxide to formic acid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The bismuth sulfide nanowires exhibit excellent formic acid product selectivity, has simple preparation process and mild reaction conditions, and provides electrocatalytic materials with excellent electrochemical performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the sulfur vacancy-rich bismuth sulfide nanowires, comprising (a) adding sodium sulfide nonahydrate, bismuth nitrate pentahydrate, lithium nitrate or lithium hydroxide, and potassium nitrate into a reaction vessel, (b) shaking well to make the reactants uniformly mixed, adding deionized water, heating and maintaining the reaction warm, (c) washing and drying the obtained product to obtain bismuth sulfide nanowires, and (d) adding a reducing substance to deionized water, and stirring, and (e) adding the bismuth sulfide nanowires obtained in step (c) and stirring, and (f) washing the product and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: That step (a) comprises using 1.6-1.8 g sodium sulfide nonahydrate, 0.8 g bismuth nitrate pentahydrate, 5-6 g lithium nitrate or lithium hydroxide, and 9-10 g potassium nitrate. Preferred Conditions: The maintaining temperature in step (b) is 120-130 degrees C and the maintaining time is 72 hours. The stirring speed of step (e) is 300-400 rpm, and the stirring time is 3 hours. Preferred Composition: The deionized water used in step (b) is 5 ml. The concentration of the ascorbic acid solution in step (d) is 1 mol/l, and the dosage of ascorbic acid is 200-300 ml, and the dosage of bismuth sulfide is 200- 300 mg.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reducing substance is ascorbic acid.

TI Preparing 4,4'-dihydroxydiphenyl ether comprises            e.g. reacting 4,4'-dibromodiphenyl ether with alkaline            aqueous solution, dimethyl sulfoxide, catalytic amount            of inorganic catalyst containing copper ions, and            ligand compound A.
AU WANG Q
   ZHANG X
   LIU X
   XU S
   CHENG J
AE GANSU JINDUN CHEM CO LTD (GANS-Non-standard)
GA 202051891X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 4,4'-dihydroxydiphenyl ether                comprises reacting 4,4'-dibromodiphenyl ether with                alkaline aqueous solution, dimethyl sulfoxide,                catalytic amount of inorganic catalyst containing                copper ions, and ligand compound A to form sodium                phenolate salt, removing catalyst by precipitation,                then introducing carbon dioxide gas to replace                4,4'-dihydroxydiphenyl ether, precipitating the                crude 4,4'-dihydroxydiphenyl ether, recrystallizing                the crude 4,4'-dihydroxydiphenyl ether with an                organic solvent to obtain a finished product of                4,4'-dihydroxydiphenyl ether with a purity of more                than 99%, where the synthetic route is as given in                the specification.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                4,4'-dihydroxydiphenyl ether.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: obtains high yield and high                purity; is simple, environmentally friendly and                economical; uses inorganic catalyst containing                copper ions and ligands to avoid the problems of                high-temperature and high-pressure hydrolysis in                the existing process, large energy consumption, low                safety factor, low yield, etc.,; and uses carbon                dioxide acid hydrolysis to avoid the introduction                of ions restricted by electronic grade products, at                the same time cost and operation is suitable for                industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic catalyst                  containing copper ions is copper chloride, ketone                  chloride, copper oxide, cuprous oxide, cuprous                  iodide, acetophenone, and/or copper sulfate. The                  organic solvent used for the recrystallization is                  toluene or xylene. The molar ratio of                  4,4'-dibromodiphenyl ether, alkaline aqueous                  solution, dimethyl sulfoxide, catalytic amount of                  inorganic catalyst containing copper ions and                  ligand A is 1:1:4:0.1:0.05. The alkaline aqueous                  solution is 30-40 wt.% sodium hydroxide alkaline                  solution. Preferred Method: The reaction                  temperature is 130 degrees C for 6-7 hours. The                  carbon dioxide gas is introduced until the pH of                  the solution is 8-9.

PN CN111213812-A
TI Preparing Prunus mume plum extract, by removing core of plum, crushing, mixing with Lactobacillus acidophilus and Bacillus natto, sealing, fermenting, mixing with catalyst, and subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide drying treatment.
AU GU J
AE MEISTORY SUZHOU BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (MEIS-Non-standard)
GA 2020516202
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing Prunus mume plum extract, involves (i) removing core of P.mume plum, crushing the fruit, mixing the fruit paste with Saccharomyces rouxii, Lactobacillus acidophilus, Bacillus natto and Saccharomyces cerevisiae GGSF16 according to a weight ratio of (100-120):(1-3):(1-5):(2-3):(2-5), sealing and fermenting at room temperature for 2-3 days, and sterilizing at 100-120 degrees C to obtain a fermented product, (ii) mixing the fermented product with edible alcohol and a catalyst, and subjecting to catalytic reaction at 15-40 degrees C for 1-24 hours under stirring to obtain a plum extract, where the catalyst comprises an acid catalyst and a salt catalyst, and (iii) adjusting the extract to neutral by sodium bicarbonate or edible sodium carbonate, and subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide drying treatment to obtain a powder-like product.
   USE - Method for preparing P.mume plum extract.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively separates the effective components in the raw material of plum, and increases the content of furfural and ester in the plum extract.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (ii) involves centrifuging and filtering after the catalytic reaction to obtain the plum extract. The drying step (iii) is carried out by introducing supercritical carbon dioxide at 10-15 MPa, 35-45 degrees C and flow rate of 1500-2500 l/hour. Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of the catalyst to the fermented product is (1-5):100. The volume ratio of the fermented product to edible alcohol is 1:(2-3). The content of furfural and ester in the plum extract is more than 55 wt.%. Preferred Components: The catalyst comprises 80-99 wt.% acid catalyst, and 1-20 wt.% salt catalyst. The acid catalyst comprises edible grade hydrochloric acid and/or edible grade acetic acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The salt catalyst comprises zinc chloride, manganese chloride and/or iron(II) chloride.

PN CN111204709-A
TI Water vapor shift reaction comprises taking copper oxide-zinc oxide composite nanostructure catalyst, and catalyzing reaction of carbon monoxide with water to produce carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
AU ZHOU J
   HUANG Y
   ZHANG B
   HUANG C
AE UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
GA 202049802Q
AB    NOVELTY - Water vapor shift reaction comprises taking copper oxide-zinc oxide composite nanostructure catalyst, catalyzing the reaction of carbon monoxide with water to produce carbon dioxide and hydrogen, where the composite nanostructure catalyst is composed of 25-45 wt.% copper oxide and 55-75 wt.% zinc oxide, and the preparation of the composite nanostructure catalyst is step-wise precipitation method, the step-wise precipitation method includes adding alkaline carbonate solution into zinc nitrate solution, stabilizing the pH of the solution to 8.5-9.5, obtaining zinc carbonate and precipitating the zinc element first, adding copper nitrate solution into the solution, adding alkaline carbonate solution to make the pH of the solution stable between 8.5-9.5, obtaining copper carbonate and precipitating the copper element, stirring and reacting, subjecting the obtained precipitate to solid-liquid separation, washing, drying and calcining.
   USE - The method is useful for water vapor shift reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method stabilizes the conversion rate of carbon monoxide at 100% in wide temperature range.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a copper oxide-zinc oxide composite nanostructure catalyst for water vapor shift reaction prepared by above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pressure in the water vapor shift reaction is normal pressure, and the temperature is 100-300 degrees C, preferably 180-300 degrees C. In the catalyst preparation method, after precipitation to obtain copper carbonate, the reaction stirred at 40-80 degrees C for 10 minutes or more, preferably 30-24 hours. In the preparation of the catalyst, the temperature for drying the precipitate after washing is 60-120 degrees C, preferably 80-110 degrees C, the drying time is more than 2 hours, preferably 5-24 hours, the temperature for calcining the precipitate after drying is 350-500 degrees C, preferably 380-450 degrees C and the calcination time is 1-5 hours. In the preparation of the catalyst, the solid-liquid separation is filtration, and washing and filtration are performed simultaneously and the alkaline carbonate is ammonium carbonate or sodium carbonate, and the concentration of the alkaline carbonate solution is 0.5-2 mol/l. In the preparation of the catalyst, the ultrasonic dissolution and dispersion are used in the preparation of zinc nitrate solution and copper nitrate solution, and the method of adding the alkaline carbonate solution and the copper nitrate solution to the solution are both dropwise addition methods Preferred Components: The composite nanostructure catalyst composed of 25-35 wt.% copper oxide and 65-75 wt.% zinc oxide.

AU YANG B
   CHEN X
   DU Z

   SHAO Z
   QI G
AE UNIV HEFEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHE-C)
   QINGYANG HUIBAO MINING CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 202018693S
AB    NOVELTY - Inorganic/organic complex inducer comprises aluminum chloride, polyethylene glycol and sodium polyphosphate complex.
   USE - Used as inorganic/organic complex inducer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has low inducer cost, mild process conditions, low equipment requirements, and high production efficiency; is environmentally friendly; and recycles the by-product ammonium chloride.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for inducing synthesis of nanometer calcium carbonate with an inorganic/organic complex inducer, comprising (i) dissolving ammonium chloride in deionized water, adding metered calcium slag according to a certain ratio, reacting at room temperature for 1 hour, and filtering and removing solid impurities in the reaction solution to obtain calcium slag leaching solution with a certain calcium ion concentration, and (ii) placing the calcium carbide slag leaching solution obtained in the step (i) in a normal pressure reaction kettle, adding specific amount of inorganic/organic complex inducer, injecting carbon dioxide at a specific temperature, stirring and reacting, stopping injecting carbon dioxide when the pH value of the reaction solution reaches a certain value, after the carbonization, performing solid-liquid separation, where the obtained filtrate is ammonium chloride solution, which can be recycled for calcium carbide slag leaching, and washing and drying the obtained filter cake to obtain a nano-calcium carbonate sample.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The inorganic/organic complex inducer comprises aluminum chloride, polyethylene glycol and sodium polyphosphate complex in a mass ratio of 1:1-2.5:2-5. In the step (i), the molar ratio of sodium hydroxide and ammonium chloride in the calcium carbide slag is 1:2. The calcium ion concentration in the calcium carbide leaching solution obtained in the step (i) is 0.5-1.5 mol/l. In the step (ii), the inorganic/organic complex inducer is added in an amount of 1-6 wt.% of the theoretical yield of nano-calcium carbonate, the reaction temperature is 5-30 degrees C, and the reaction is terminated after the reaction reaches the system pH of 7-9.

TI Method for removing malodor of domestic garbage and odor of domestic sewage, involves oxidizing odor molecules into water and carbon dioxide and other harmless gases to remove odor of domestic garbage and odor of domestic sewage.
AU LI Q
   YANG Z
   SUN A
   LI S
   GONG J
   KANG Z
AE QINGHONG TECHNOLOGY SHANGHAI CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 202010072B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves making a highly active inorganic fiber from a new formula of multiple metal oxides and transition metal oxides. The physical and chemical properties of transition metal oxides and the ultra-high specific surface area characteristics of ultrafine fibers are utilized. The highly active inorganic fiber is weaved into a filter element screen. The filter mesh is made to restrict the passage of gas and increase the probability of collision between odorous molecules and active transition metal oxides. The odor molecules are catalytically oxidized into water and carbon dioxide and other harmless gases to completely remove the odor of domestic garbage and the odor of domestic sewage.
   USE - Method for removing malodor of domestic garbage and odor of domestic sewage.
   ADVANTAGE - The effect of odor purification is improved such that the harmful effects of odor on the human body can be eliminated, and the problem of odor purification quality is fundamentally solved.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The exhaust fan is turned ON for continuous ventilation, and the ammonium, methane, hydrogen sulfide, and carbon disulfide components and odor volatile organic compound values are detected in real time by the detector until no ammonium, methane, hydrogen sulfide, and carbon disulfide components and odor VOC values are detected. The metal oxide comprises a mixture of silicon dioxide, aluminum oxide, calcium oxide and magnesium oxide.

PN CN109999817-A
TI Glow discharge plasma-assisted preparation of copper/zinc oxide-zirconium dioxide solid solution catalyst by adding copper nitrate trihydrate, zinc nitrate hexahydrate and zirconium nitrate pentahydrate in deionized water.
AU XU Q
   CHENG W
   SONG F
   CHENG F
   GAO J
AE YANCHENG INST TECHNOLOGY (YANH-C)
GA 2019628695
AB    NOVELTY - Glow discharge plasma-assisted preparation of copper (Cu)/zinc oxide (ZnO)-zirconium dioxide (ZrO2) solid solution catalyst comprises adding copper nitrate trihydrate (Cu(NO3)2.3H2O), zinc nitrate hexahydrate (Zn(NO3)2.6H2O) and zirconium nitrate pentahydrate (Zr(NO3)4.5H2O) in deionized water, completely dissolving and mixing to prepare mixed metal nitrate solution, reacting at 70 degrees C, dropwise adding sodium carbonate (Na2CO3) solution, stirring, after finishing dropping, keeping a constant temperature, stirring, standing and aging, centrifuging, filtering, washing with deionized water to obtain solid precipitates, drying to obtain copper-doped zinc-zirconium solid solution catalyst precursor, grinding into powder, putting in glow discharge plasma apparatus for plasma treatment, placing the processed sample in a tubular furnace, and roasting.
   USE - The method is for glow discharge plasma-assisted preparation of Cu/ZnO-ZrO2 solid solution catalyst used for carbon dioxide hydrogenation to prepare methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The dispersion degree of copper in the catalyst is improved. The catalyst improves the conversion rate and methanol yield of catalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methanol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for Cu/ZnO-ZrO2 solid solution catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The concentration of Na2CO3 solution is 0.5-1 mol/L.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: The molar ratio of Cu/(Cu+Zn+Zr) is 5%. The molar ratio of Zn/(Cu+Zn+Zr) is 19%. The molar ratio of Zr/(Cu+Zn+Zr) is 76%.

PN CN109930165-A; CN109930165-B
TI Preparing bismuth/carbon catalyst useful for electro-catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises dispersing and dissolving reducing agent and alkali, adding conductive carbon powder, dropping bismuth nitrate solution and drying.
AU WANG G
   JIANG X
   BAO X
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2019590265
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing bismuth/carbon catalyst comprises (i) dispersing and dissolving reducing agent and alkali in de-ionized water ultrasonically; (ii) adding conductive carbon powder for 20-60 minutes; (iii) dropping bismuth nitrate solution with concentration not greater than 0.5mol l-1 and heating at not less than 30 degrees C under stirring for 6-10 hours; and (iv) filtering, washing with de-ionized water and drying under vacuum at room temperature.
   USE - The bismuth/carbon catalyst is useful for electro-catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The bismuth/carbon catalyst achieves maximum Faraday efficiency of formic acid of 90.7% and carbon monoxide of 9.1%, maximum geometric current density of formic acid is 50.6 mA cm-2 and maximum mass current density of 632.1 mA mg-1, which is much higher than highest reported value in literature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for bismuth/carbon catalyst, where bismuth/carbon catalyst is bismuth single atom and bismuth nano-particle co-supported on conductive carbon powder and bismuth nano particle diameter is less than 5 nm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reducing agent is sodium hypophosphite and tartaric acid. The mass ratio of sodium hypophosphite, tartaric acid, sodium hydroxide, carbon powder and de-ionized water is 80-300:2-4:1-5:1.5-20:400-1000. The conductive carbon powder is Vulcan XC-72R, BlackPearls 2000, Ketjenblack EC-300J or Ketjenblack EC-600JD. The concentration of bismuth nitrate is 0.01-0.5 mol l-1 Preferred Method: In the step (iii), dropping bismuth nitrate solution at rate of 0.5 ml/minute and heating at 30-90 degrees C. The bismuth nitrate solution is prepared by dissolving bismuth nitrate in ethylene glycol or concentrated nitric acid and diluting with water.

   TANG R
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201929491R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing calcium bicarbonate powder by a solvent method, involves (i) adding calcium salt to a solvent having a relative dielectric constant of less than 50 to obtain a solution (A) containing calcium ions, where the concentration of calcium ions in the solution (A) is 1-140 mmol/l, (ii) adding an alkaline substance to a solvent having a relative dielectric constant of less than 50 to obtain a solution (B), where the molar ratio of the alkaline substance in the solution (B) and the calcium salt in the step (i) is (0.5-4):1, and (iii) mixing the solutions (A) and (B), introducing a carbon dioxide gas, after a white turbid liquid of calcium bicarbonate is formed, centrifuging or filtering, and volatilizing the solvent to obtain calcium bicarbonate powder having a purity of at least 95%.
   USE - Method for preparing calcium bicarbonate powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method quickly prepares calcium bicarbonate having a purity of at least 95% in a stepwise manner, and solves the problems of difficulty in synthesizing calcium bicarbonate in the solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcium salt used in step (i) is calcium chloride, calcium nitrate, calcium bromide, and/or their hydrates. The concentration of the calcium salt in the solution (A) is 15-135 mmol/l.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent used in steps (i) and (ii) is methanol, ethanol, glycol, glycerol, diethylene glycol, triethylene glycol, diglyme, tetraethylene glycol, acetone and/or dimethyl sulfoxide, preferably methanol, ethanol or acetone. The alkaline substance is an organic small molecule amine compound comprising triethylamine, triethanolamine, ethanolamine, diethylamine and/or arginine, preferably triethylamine or triethanolamine. The molar ratio of the organic small molecule amine compound and the calcium salt is (1.5-2.5):1.

PN CN109232886-A
TI Preparation of carbazole group-based conjugated microporous polymer for trapping of carbon dioxide, involves stirring monomer, anhydrous organic solvent and anhydrous catalyst, filtering, washing, eluting, Soxhlet extracting, and drying.
AU QIAO S
   LI Z
   ZHANG B
   ZHAO J
   LI Q
   HAO Y
AE UNIV HEBEI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYKJ-C)
GA 201913309J
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation method of carbazole group-based conjugated microporous polymer involves orderly adding 0.32-0.48 mmol monomer, 30 mL anhydrous organic solvent and 2.5-3 mmol anhydrous catalyst to a high temperature dried pressure pipe, introducing inert gas to evacuate air in the pressure pipe, magnetically stirring at 160-180 degrees C for 20-30 hours, adding 100 mL alcohol to stop the reaction, stopping heating, magnetically stirring for 15-60 minutes at room temperature, filtering, washing the obtained precipitate with methanol, concentrated hydrochloric acid and pure water three times, placing the obtained crude product in a Soxhlet extractor, eluting with alcohol, organic solvent and tetrahydrofuran, Soxhlet extracting for 24 hours, and vacuum drying the obtained product at 65-85 degrees C for 24 hours to obtain black powder.
   USE - Preparation method of carbazole group-based conjugated microporous polymer used for trapping of acidic gas such as carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for application of carbazole group-based conjugated microporous polymer for trapping of acidic gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anhydrous catalyst is anhydrous ferric trichloride. The inert gas is nitrogen and/or argon gas.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The monomer is a compound obtained by linking a carbazole group and a middle core structure to a carboxylic acid group having a carbazole group at both ends and an intermediate core. The intermediate core is a compound containing an electron-rich group. The organic solvent is chloroform, carbon tetrachloride, acetone or dichloromethane. The alcohol is methanol, ethanol, ethylene glycol or isopropyl alcohol. Preferred Process: The monomer is prepared by (1) weighing 160-240 mg copper powder, adding to 200 mL conical flask, adding 5 mL concentrated hydrochloric acid and 100 mL acetone, magnetically stirring for 25-35 minutes, filtering, adding acetone to the precipitate, adding 3-5 g iodine particles, continuously activating by magnetically stirring for 25-35 minutes, filtering the obtained reddish brown copper powder as catalyst for the next reaction, and (2) adding 1.20-1.90 mmol intermediate core, 750-1000 mg carbazole, 160-240 mg activated copper powder, 4.5-8 mmol potassium carbonate and 20 mL nitrobenzene to 100 mL dry pear-shaped reaction bottle under the protection of inert gas, heating in an oil bath at 160-200 degrees C, magnetically stirring for 24 hours, cooling to room temperature after the reaction is completed, distilling off nitrobenzene solution using an oil pump, adding 30 mL aqueous ammonia at room temperature after distillation, magnetically stirring for 3 hours to remove catalyst, extracting the organic phase with water and dichloromethane, drying organic phase with anhydrous sodium sulfate, rotary-evaporating dichloromethane by a rotary evaporator, and separating and purifying the obtained crude product by silica gel column.
DC A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); E13 (Heterocyclics.); ; J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN108303767-A; CN108303767-B
TI Method for manufacturing mirror on optical waveguide, involves performing heat processing on inclined surface of V-shaped groove by using carbon dioxide laser, and plating concave surface of high reflecting film to produce concave mirror.
AU HUANG Y
   DENG Z
   WANG T
   DENG C
AE SUZHOU DERUI POWER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2018585340
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves cutting an optical waveguide to form a V-shaped groove by using V-shaped diamond cutter, where a side of a V-shaped groove is formed as an inclined surface. A cubic groove is etched by using an excimer laser adjacent to a vertical surface of the V-shaped groove, where width of the cubic groove is about 2/3-1 times of depth of the v-shaped groove. A plate piece is tilted, such that the inclined surface of the V-shaped groove is formed in horizontal shape. Heat processing is performed on the inclined surface of the V-shaped groove by using a carbon dioxide laser, such that the inclined surface is ablated into a concave surface. A concave surface of a high reflecting film i.e. metal film is plated to produce a concave mirror.
   USE - Method for manufacturing a mirror on an optical waveguide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improving thermal annealing effect, and obtaining low roughness of a reflecting mirror.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side view of an optical waveguide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The reflective film is made of titanium dioxide, silicon dioxide, zinc sulfide and magnesium fluoride.

PN CN108295778-A; CN108295778-B
TI Preparing noble metal aerogel comprises e.g.            obtaining noble metal/triacetate composite aerogel,            nano-porous noble metal gel, washing with deionized            water, subjecting to solvent exchange using acetone and            supercritical carbon dioxide drying.
AU WANG C
   ZHOU X
   FU Z
   YANG X
   ZHONG M
   MI R
   YUAN L
AE LASER FUSION RES CENT CHINA ACAD ENG PHY (CAEP-C)
GA 2018591488
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing noble metal aerogel comprises (i)                obtaining non-noble metal/triacetate composite                aerogel by electroless plating using cellulose                triacetate aerogel containing uniformly distributed                palladium catalyst as template, and placing                non-noble metal/triacetate composite aerogel in a                noble metal solution for chemical replacement                reaction to obtain noble metal/triacetate composite                aerogel, (ii) placing the obtained noble                metal/triacetate composite aerogel in lithium                hydroxide/urea solution, and carrying out cellulose                triacetate removal process to obtain the                nano-porous noble metal gel, (iii) washing the                nano-porous noble metal gel with deionized water                for 4-8 times 2 hours per time until there is no                noble metal ion in cleaning liquid, and (iv)                subjecting the obtained nano-porous noble metal gel                to solvent exchange using acetone for 1 day/time,                changing fresh acetone every day for 5-8 days, and                supercritical carbon dioxide drying to obtain noble                metal aerogel.
   USE - The noble metal aerogel is useful in hydrogen                storage, fuel cell and laser inertial confinement                fusion.
   ADVANTAGE - The noble metal aerogel: has high specific                surface area.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method specifically                  comprises adding 0.1-10 pts. wt. cellulose                  triacetate and 5-500 pts. wt. 1,4-dioxane to a                  reaction vessel, placing the reaction vessel on a                  device with a circulating cooling water system,                  stirring at 50-80 degrees C at a rotating speed                  of 300-500 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 3-5                  hours, maintaining same temperature and rotating                  speed, adding equal amount of anhydrous alcohol                  solvent to 1,4-dioxane to the reaction vessel in                  at a rate of 1-5 mL/min, stirring for 20-40                  minutes, adding 0.5-2 pts. wt. palladium(ii)                  chloride solution, continuing to stir for 5-10                  minutes, static sealing for 12-48 hours,                  naturally cooling to around room temperature, and                  aging to obtain cellulose triacetate aerogel with                  uniformly distributed palladium activation                  center. The electroless nickel plating is                  prepared by taking 0.1-0.3 pts. wt. nickel(ii)                  chloride hexahydrate, 4-6 pts. wt. ethanol and                  18-22 pts. wt. deionized water, fully stirring to                  dissolve to obtain light green clear solution,                  orderly adding 1-3 pts. wt. aqueous ammonia, 1-3                  pts. wt. hydrazine hydrate in a mass ratio of                  1:1, continuously stirring to obtain the chemical                  nickel plating solution, where the electroless                  copper plating comprises 15 g/l sodium citrate,                  30 g/l boric acid, 10 g/l anhydrous copper                  sulfate and 30 g/l sodium hypophosphite, and                  chemical plating is carried out for 1-6 times,                  each time for 12-24 hours. The lithium                  hydroxide/urea solution is prepared by taking 4-5                  pts. wt. lithium hydroxide, and 14-16 pts. wt.                  carbamide, adding 80-90 pts. wt. deionized water,                  and fully stirring to dissolve to obtain lithium                  hydroxide/urea solution, and the process of                  removing template of cellulose triacetate                  comprises placing the noble metal/triacetate                  composite gas aerogel in lithium hydroxide/urea                  solution, freezing at -20 to -12 degrees C,                  taking it out, stirring while thawing, and                  repeating the template removing process for 3-5                  times to obtain nano-porous noble metal gel. The                  replacing of cellulose triacetate aerogel                  containing uniformly distributed palladium                  catalyst with aerogel is replaced by treating                  cellulose triacetate in a bipolar square wave                  high voltage pulsed electric field at 75 degrees                  C to obtain pre-treated cellulose triacetate,                  adding 35-50 pts. wt. pre-treated cellulose                  tridiacetate, 5-10 pts. wt, itaconic anhydride,                  60-80 pts. wt. toluene, and 1-2 pyridine in a                  supercritical reaction device, sealing the                  system, passing carbon dioxide to 25-30 MPa,                  stirring and reacting at 75-85 degrees C for 5-10                  hours, vacuum extracting, adding 3-5 pts. wt. 4-                  aminophthalic anhydride, 10-30 pts. wt. toluene,                  and 0.5-1.5 pyridine, sealing the system, passing                  carbon dioxide to 35-45 MPa, stirring and                  reacting at 75-85 degrees C for 3-5 hours, vacuum                  extracting to obtain the reaction mixture,                  cooling to room temperature, filtering, drying,                  removing solvent by distilling to obtain the                  modified cellulose acetate, adding 2-10 pts. wt.                  modified cellulose acetate, 10-500 pts. wt.                  1,4-dioxane, 0.3-0.5 pts. wt. 1-hexyl-3-methyl                  imidazole tetrafluoroborate to reaction vessel,                  setting the reaction vessel with circulation                  cooling water system device at 50-80 degrees C,                  stirring at a speed of 300-500 rpm min for 3-5                  hours, maintain the same temperature and rotating                  speed, adding isopropanol as same amount as 1,4-                  dioxane at a rate of 5 mL/min, fully stirring for                  20-40 minutes, subsequently adding 0.5-2 pts. wt.                  palladium(II) chloride solution, continuing to                  stirring into solution, ultrasonically processing                  for 15-30 minutes, transferring the finally                  formed solution into mold, sealing still for                  12-48 hours to make it naturally cooled to around                  room temperature, and aging to obtain modified                  cellulose triacetate aerogel with a uniformly                  distributed palladium activation center.                  Preferred Components: The anhydrous alcohol                  solvent is methanol, ethanol or isopropanol. The                  noble metal solution is chloroauric acid                  solution, chloroplatinic acid solution,                  palladium(ii) chloride solution and/or silver                  nitrate solution. The specific area of cellulose                  triacetate aerogel is 300-1500 m2/g, density is                  0.01-0.05 g/cm3, and the pores are centralized                  distributed at 30-100 nm. The pulsed electric                  field intensity of the bipolar square wave high                  voltage pulse electric field is 40-50 kV/cm,                  frequency is 1000-1200 Hz, processing time is                  30-45 minutes, and the frequency of ultrasound is                  30-45 KHz, where ultrasonic uses intermittent                  irradiation, and the time during intermittent                  irradiation is 10-15/5-10 seconds. The specific                  surface area of noble metal aerogel is 80-320                  m2.m/g, particle size is 20-100 nm, the pore size                  is 3-50 nm, where the noble metal aerogel has a                  three-dimensional mesh structure connected to                  form the nano skeleton.
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PN EP4035653-A1; KR2022110112-A; US2022249357-A1; JP2022117487-A; CN114891563-A
TI Preparation of oily extract of flowers of Gardenia            jasminoides for use in cosmetic composition, involves            performing at least supercritical carbon dioxide            extraction of flower powder of Gardenia jasminoides at            preset temperature and pressure.
AU RILHAC V
   GILLET M
   VINCENT L H
   ZILEMEVA
AE CHANEL PARFUMS BEAUTE (CLPA-C)
   CHANEL PARFUMS BEAUTE (CLPA-C)
   CHANEL PARFUMS BEAUTE (CLPA-C)
GA 202297021U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of oily extract of flowers of                Gardenia jasminoides involves performing at least                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of flower                powder of Gardenia jasminoides at 40-80 degrees C,                preferably 50-70 degrees C and 25-50 MPa,                preferably 30-45 MPa.
   USE - Preparation of oily extract of flowers of                Gardenia jasminoides for use in cosmetic                composition for preventing and/or treating skin                alterations due to aging, photoaging (all claimed)                and disorders. Can also be used to impart smoothing                aging resistance.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides extract enriched in                non-polar molecules such as sterols and having                excellent skin aging resistance, and provides                composition having low toxicity, allergic response,                improved compatibility and stability and ensuring                suppressed discomfort such as redness, tightness                and tingling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following: (i) cosmetic composition, which                comprises at least one oily extract of Gardenia                jasminoides in a physiologically acceptable medium;                and (ii) non-therapeutic cosmetic use of oily                extract of flower of Gardenia jasminoides for                preventing and/or treating skin alterations due to                aging or photoaging.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The extraction is carried                  out in the presence of an oily solvent chosen                  from squalane (preferred), ethyl-2-hexyl                  palmitate, caprylic and capric acid triglycerides                  and vegetable oils. The method preferably                  involves (i) drying and crushing fresh flowers of                  Gardenia jasminoides to obtain the flower powder,                  (ii) performing supercritical carbon dioxide                  extraction of the flower powder for 30 minutes to                  6 hours in the presence squalane, (iii)                  evaporating carbon dioxide by lowering the                  pressure to 7.4 MPa or less, preferably 6 MPa or                  less, (iv) optionally introducing a stream of                  alcohol, preferably ethanol to collect the                  mixture of flower powder and solvent, evaporating                  the alcohol, (v) adding squalane until the weight                  ratio of dried flower and squalane is 1:5-1:20,                  and (vi) clarifying the oily extract by filtering                  using a membrane having a porosity of less than 1                  mu m. Preferred Composition: The volume ratio of                  carbon dioxide and squalane is 200:1-50:1.                  Preferred Conditions: In the extraction step, the                  pressure is lowered to pass carbon dioxide in the                  gaseous state, preferably at a pressure of 6 MPa                  or less.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Matrix metalloproteinase (MMP) activity                inhibitor; Tissue inhibitor of metalloproteinases                (TIMP1) and/or (TIMP2) gene expression                stimulator.

TI Method for measuring and calculating carbon            emission of a low-carbon sewage treatment process in a            sewage treatment plant, involves determined process to            be measured and external exchange of carbon tracer            factor according to boundary.
AU HUANG X
   LI Q
   LV L
   JIANG M
   PENG C
   SONG Y
   CHEN B
   BAI C
   SHEN Y
AE CHINA GEZHOUBA GROUP WATER SERVICE OPERA (CECG-C)
GA 2022A3985N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting a to-be-measured                process. Energy is determined. Boundary of                substance is exchanged. Process to be measured and                external exchange of carbon tracer factor (CFk) are                determined according to the boundary. Carbon                equivalent input matrix and carbon equivalent                output matrix are obtained. The carbon equivalent                CO2ek is sorted according to basis of the carbon                equivalent CFk. Carbon exchange carbon dioxide                increment is calculated according to total input,                total output, energy consumption of new energy                carbon factor net decrement and gaseous carbon                dioxide emission reduction equivalent.
   USE - Method for measuring and calculating carbon                emission of a low-carbon sewage treatment process                in a sewage treatment plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables carrying out carbon                emission measuring and calculating of the sewage                treatment process, which is good for optimizing and                managing the low-carbon sewage treatment treatment                process.

TI Preparing graphitic carbon nitride-lanthanum            orthophosphate core-shell structure nanorods with            surface defects comprises stirring lanthanum nitrate            hexahydrate and potassium dihydrogen phosphate,            hydrothermally reacting, vacuum heating, dispersing,            and vacuum drying.
AU YAN S
   CHEN L
   PENG F
   YU Y
AE HUAIYIN TECHNOLOGY INST (HUIA-C)
GA 202289229H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphitic carbon nitride-lanthanum                orthophosphatecore-shell structure nanorods with                surface defects comprises preparing LaPO4 nanorods                with oxygen vacancies by adding lanthanum nitrate                hexahydrate (La(NO3) 6H2O) and potassium dihydrogen                phosphate (KH2PO4) to hydrochloric acid (HCl)                solution and stirring to obtain a uniform mixture,                transferring the mixture to an autoclave for                hydrothermal reaction, naturally cooling to room                temperature, washing obtained white precipitate                with deionized water and drying, subjecting white                precipitate to vacuum heat treatment and then                air-cooling to room temperature to obtain LaPO4                nanorods with oxygen vacancies, preparing g-C3N4 by                calcining urea, preparing gC3N4/LaPO4-based                photocatalyst by dispersing LaPO4 nanorods and                gC3N4 in deionized water to sonicate into a                suspension, stirring the suspension, vacuum drying                to obtain gC3N4/LaPO4 core-shell nanorods with                surface defects.
   USE - The method is useful for Preparing graphitic                carbon nitride-lanthanum orthophosphate core-shell                structure nanorods.
   ADVANTAGE - The nanorods: has high photo-catalytic                reduction carbon dioxide activity and stable, which                can reduce the carbon dioxide gas molecule to                carbon monoxide under the illumination.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of                  La(NO3) 6H2O and KH2PO4 is 2.165:0.680-2.2:0.680.                  The molar concentration of the HCl solution is 1                  mol/l. The mass-to-volume ratio of LaPO4                  nanorods, g-C3N4 and deionized water was 100                  mg:1-7 mg:200 ml.Preferred Method: The method comprises                  hydrothermally reacting at 150-200&#8451; for                  15-25 hours. The vacuum heat treatment is carried                  out at a vacuum pressure of 5-20 mTorr from room                  temperature to 250&#8451;, heating rate of                  8-12oC/minute for 4-6 hours. The method comprises                  calcining the urea in g-C3N4 at                  500-600&#8451; for 2-4 hours. The method                  comprises ultrasonically treating at an                  ultrasonic frequency of 30-50 KHz for 2-4 hours.                  The method comprises stirring suspension for                  10-13 hours, and vacuum drying at                  100-250&#8451;.

PT P
PN WO2022050235-A1; TW202219024-A
TI Production of formate used for manufacturing            formic acid, involves reacting hydrogen with carbon            dioxide, bicarbonate or carbonate using catalyst to            produce formate in reaction solution which is two-phase            system in which solvent exists in state of being            separated into organic and aqueous phases.
AU MAKOTO H
   HIROKAZU M
   PIDKO E A
   FILONENKO G A
   REBREYEND C
   HIRANO M
   MATSUDA H
AE NITTO DENKO CORP (NITL-C)
GA 2022346519
AB    NOVELTY - Production of a formate involves reacting                hydrogen with carbon dioxide, bicarbonate or                carbonate using a catalyst in presence of a solvent                to produce formate in a reaction solution which is                a two-phase system in which the solvent exists in a                state of being separated into an organic phase and                an aqueous phase. The reaction solution has a base                concentration of 2.5 mol/L or more.
   USE - Production of formate used for manufacturing                formic acid for manufacturing antifreeze agent (all                claimed) for preventing salt damage caused by                calcium chloride. Can also be used for hydrogen                storage material, oil field mining, leather                application, and livestock feed.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces formate with high yield                and excellent productivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) manufacture of formic acid, which involves                producing the formate and electrodialyzing by                protonating at least one portion of the formate to                produce formic acid and water; and
   (2) manufacture of an antifreeze agent, which                involves producing the formate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The catalyst has a                  catalyst rotation speed (TON) of 10000 or more                  which is a ratio of a molar amount (X, mol) of                  potassium formate and a molar amount (Y, mol) of                  the catalyst used in the reaction measured by TON                  calculation reaction, where X is (W/M)x (Iax                  Ib/R)x (A/B), where W is an amount (g) of                  dimethyl sulfoxide used for quantifying potassium                  formate, M is a molecular weight of dimethyl                  sulfoxide, R is a ratio of number of protons of                  dimethyl sulfoxide and number of protons of                  potassium formate, Ia is a proton NMR integral                  value of potassium formate, Ib is an integral                  value of proton NMR of dimethyl sulfoxide, A is a                  mass (g) of a lower aqueous solution obtained by                  the reaction, and B is a mass (g) of the aqueous                  solution used for quantification of potassium                  formate. The TON calculation reaction is                  performed by adding 10 mmol potassium bicarbonate                  to 1 mL water in a glass vial with a stir bar in                  a glove box under inert gas, adding 1 mL toluene,                  dioxane, tetrahydrofuran, ethyl acetate,                  methylcyclohexane, and cyclopentyl methyl ether,                  adding 0.12 mu mol catalyst and 54 mu mol methyl                  trioctylammonium chloride to a solvent in which                  the TON having a highest catalyst is obtained,                  placing the vial in an autoclave, sealing the                  autoclave, taking out the glove box, heating the                  autoclave to 90 degrees C with stirring,                  pressurizing the autoclave to 4.5 MPa with                  hydrogen when a desired temperature is reached,                  stirring the reaction mixture for 18 hours,                  cooling the reaction mixture in an ice bath and                  releasing the pressure, removing the upper layer                  of the solution after the reaction, and obtaining                  an aqueous solution of the lower layer containing                  potassium formate and unreacted potassium                  bicarbonate. Preferred Process: The aqueous phase                  is separated and diluted to adjust the                  concentration of the formate in the aqueous                  phase, adding an acid to the aqueous phase and                  decarboxylating before being used in the                  electrodialysis. Water produced in the                  electrodialysis is used for the dilution.                  Manufacture of the antifreeze agent further                  involves adding at least one acid chosen from                  formic acid and acetic acid to the formate.                  Preferred Components: The catalyst is a metal                  complex catalyst and a ligand of the metal                  complex catalyst is further added. The catalyst                  is at least one of ruthenium complex of formula                  (I) and its tautomer, stereoisomer, or salt. The                  ligand is an organophosphorus ligand of formula                  (II). The organic phase comprises toluene or                  dioxane. In the production of formate, a                  quaternary ammonium salt is further used as a                  phase-transfer catalyst.R0 = H or alkyl;Q1 = CH2, NH, or O;R1 = alkyl or aryl, where at least one is                  aryl when Q1 is NH or O;A = CH, CR5, or N;R5 = alkyl, aryl, aralkyl, amino, hydroxy,                  or alkoxy;X = halo;n = 0-3;L = neutral or anionic ligand;Q2 = NH or O;andR3 = aryl.
   EXAMPLE - In a glove box under inert gas, 1 mL water was                added to a glass vial and 2.5 mmol potassium                hydrogen carbonate, 1 mL toluene, 0.12 mu mol                ruthenium catalyst of formula (1) and 54 mu mol                methyltrioctyl ammonium chloride were added, the                vial was placed vial in the autoclave and the                autoclave was sealed and taken out of the glove                box. The autoclave was heated to 90 degrees C with                stirring. When the desired temperature was reached,                the autoclave was pressurized to 4.5 MPa with                hydrogen. The reaction mixture was stirred for 2.5                hours, cooled in an ice bath and the pressure was                carefully released. The upper layer of the solution                after the reaction was removed, and the lower                aqueous solution containing potassium formate and                unreacted potassium hydrogen carbonate was                remained. 100 mu L Lower aqueous solution was                dissolved in 500 mu L heavy water and 300 mu L                dimethyl sulfoxide as an internal standard was                added and reacted to obtain formate having a                catalyst rotation speed of 13000.

PN CN113956681-A; CN113956681-B
TI Preparing tourmaline coated with calcium carbonate            useful in coating, comprises e.g. mixing calcium            hydroxide emulsion and tourmaline, introducing carbon            dioxide into reaction, and after completing reaction,            obtaining tourmaline coated with calcium carbonate by            post-treatment.
AU LI J
   ZHA C
   HAN Q
AE ASIA ECOLOGICAL IND CHUZHOU CO LTD (ASEC-Non-standard)
   YASHI CHUANGNENG TECHNOLOGY SHANGHAI CO (YASH-Non-standard)
GA 202226912W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing tourmaline coated with calcium                carbonate comprises mixing calcium hydroxide                emulsion and tourmaline, controlling the mass ratio                of calcium hydroxide and tourmaline to (2-4):1,                then introducing carbon dioxide into the reaction                at a rate of 0.3-0.5 l/minute, and after completing                the reaction, obtaining the tourmaline coated with                calcium carbonate by post-treatment.
   USE - The tourmaline coated with calcium carbonate                is useful in coating (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is economical; has high whitening                efficiency and simple process. The tourmaline                coated with calcium carbonate: has high whiteness,                no hard lumps in the container, is in a uniform                state after stirring, has excellent low temperature                stability, does not affect the construction of the                coating, has excellent low-temperature film-forming                properties; has normal appearance, high negative                ion release rate, can exert its excellent                performance of spontaneously releasing negative                ions, short drying time, high contrast ratio, and                outstanding alkali resistance and washing                resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                tourmaline coated with calcium carbonate prepared                by the above preparation method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of                  the calcium hydroxide emulsion is 4-6 wt.%. The                  concentration of the carbon dioxide is 50-70                  vol.%.Preferred Method: The preparation method of                  the calcium hydroxide emulsion comprises                  digesting, cooling and aging calcium oxide with                  boiling water, and then filtering and diluting to                  obtain the calcium hydroxide emulsion, preferably                  the aging time is 3-8 hours. The coating and                  whitening are carried out under stirring                  conditions, preferably during the mixing, the                  stirring speed is 700-900 revolutions/minute,                  preferably, and after the mixing, the stirring                  speed is adjusted to 400-800 revolutions/minute.                  The temperature of the reaction is 20-30degrees                  Celsius, preferably when the reaction ends, the                  pH of the system is 6.5-7. The post-treatment                  includes filtration, washing, drying, and                  dispersion. The preparation method comprises the                  following steps (1) digesting calcium oxide with                  boiling water, cooling and aging for 3-8 hours,                  and then filtering and diluting to obtain the                  calcium hydroxide emulsion, and (2) stirring and                  mixing calcium hydroxide emulsion and tourmaline                  at a speed of 700-900 revolutions/minute, after                  mixing, adjusting the rotating speed to 400-800                  revolutions/minute, controlling the concentration                  of calcium hydroxide emulsion to 4-6 wt.%, where                  the mass ratio of calcium hydroxide and                  tourmaline is (2-4):1, then introducing carbon                  dioxide with volume concentration of 50-70% at a                  rate of 0.3-0.5 l/minute, reacting at                  20-30degrees Celsius, the pH value of the system                  is 6.5-7 to complete the reaction, and obtaining                  the tourmaline coated with calcium carbonate                  through filtration, washing, drying, and                  dispersion.

AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing catalyst involves using template                method-vacuum drying method, adding manganese salt                and cerium salt to pure water, stirring and                dissolving to obtain manganese-cerium salt mixture,                soaking the mesoporous silicon template agent in                the manganese-cerium salt mixture, centrifuging to                removing the impregnating solution, subjecting the                obtained impregnated product to vacuum dried,                calcining to obtain a manganese-cerium solid                solution precursor, using an alkaline solution to                impregnating the obtained manganese-cerium solid                solution precursor to dissolving the template                agent, filtering, washing the filter cake with pure                water to neutral, and performig vacuum drying the                filter cake to obtain a mesoporous manganese-cerium                solid solution catalyst, where the catalyst is a                mesoporous manganese-cerium solid solution                structure.

TI Preparing carbon nano cage by green cycle method            used as e.g. electrode material, comprises mixing basic            magnesium carbonate and magnesium powder, heating mixed            powder, cooling, adding deionized water into black            powder, heating, cooling, introducing carbon dioxide,            filtering and collecting filtrate.

   ZHOU Y
   MIN C
   LIAN J
   BU Y
   SHI R
   LIANG H
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 2021C2544Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method comprises (a) mixing basic magnesium                carbonate and magnesium powder to obtain a mixed                powder, (b) heating the mixed powder under a                protection of inert gas to 600-800degrees Celsius,                maintaining the temperature for 1-2 hours, and                cooling to room temperature to obtain a black                powder, (c) adding deionized water into the black                powder, stirring, heating to 80-100degrees Celsius,                maintaining the temperature for 1-2 hours, and                cooling to the room temperature,(d) stirring,                continuously introducing carbon dioxide, filtering,                and drying the filter body to obtain the carbon                nanocage, and (e) collecting the filtrate, stirring                at 55-100degrees Celsius, and maintaining the                temperature to obtain the magnesium                carbonate.
   USE - The prepared carbon nano cage is useful as                electrode material of the super capacitor.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmental friendly, simple                and fast; and continuously increases amount of                basic magnesium carbonate raw material in the whole                circulation process. The prepared carbon nano cage:                has high capacitance performance.

The connection conduits connect the heat-source-side unit with the intermediate units. The intermediate units have an ejector (41a-41d) and a gas-liquid separator (42a-42d). TI New fusion protein comprises polypeptide having small subunit-like domains from carbon dioxide concentrating mechanism protein, carbonic anhydrase domain, and encapsulation peptide, for carbon fixation and for producing 3-phosphoglycerate.
AU GONZALEZ C R
   KERFELD C A
AE UNIV MICHIGAN STATE (UNMS-C)
GA 202179232Y
AB    NOVELTY - A fusion protein comprising a polypeptide comprising at least two small subunit-like domains (SSLDs) from a carbon dioxide concentrating mechanism (CcmM) protein, at least one carbonic anhydrase domain, and at least one encapsulation peptide, is new.
   USE - The fusion protein, the cell, or the plant is useful for carbon fixation; and for producing a product comprising 3-PGA, oils, fatty acids, alkanes, alkenes, ethanol hydrogen, isobutanol, ethylene, polyhydroxybutyrate, biomass, carbohydrates, phycocyanin, ethanol, hydrogen, isobutanol, ethylene, grains, vegetables, fruits, or combinations (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The chimeric protein assembles into a functional carboxysome core that can readily be transferred and utilized in other organisms.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) an expression cassette comprising a promoter operably linked to a nucleic acid segment encoding the fusion protein;
   (2) a cell comprising the expression cassette;
   (3) a plant comprising the expression cassette;
   (4) carbon fixation which involves culturing the cell or cultivating the plant; and
   (5) producing a product which involves culturing the cell to generate a population of cells that contain the product, and isolating the product from the cells; or cultivating the plant, and harvesting the plant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: At least two SSLDs from CcmM protein can bind or nucleate with ribulose-1,5-bisphosphate carboxylase/oxygenase (Rubisco), and the Rubisco can synthesize 3-phosphoglycerate (3-PGA). At least two SSLDs from CcmM protein comprise a CcmM short form protein with at least 95% sequence identity to any of at least fully defined 323 amino acids (SEQ ID NOS: 1-11, or 37) given in the specification. The carbonic anhydrase domain converts bicarbonate to carbon dioxide. It comprises at least 95% sequence identity to any of at least fully defined 272 amino acids (SEQ ID NOS: 17-21 or 71) given in the specification. The encapsulation peptide interacts with and/or binds one or more carboxysome shell protein. It comprises at least 95% sequence identity to any of fully defined 161 amino acids (SEQ ID NO: 12) or fully defined 18 amino acids (SEQ ID NO: 13) given in the specification. The cell is a bacterial cell, algal cell, a microalgal cell, a cyanobactrial cell, or a plant cell.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
