PN CN113075750-A
TI Method for determining minimum miscible pressure            of carbon dioxide with formation crude oil, involves            determining minimum miscible pressure according to new            oil system molar proportion models and miscible            pressure empirical models.
AU REN X
   CHENG H
   GU H
   DONG H
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 202185085T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting oil fields in an                oil storage area to be made into multiple                experimental groups. The multiple groups of values                are determined, according to a formation oil system                molar ratio model. A linear fitting is respectively                performed on the groups of values. A new oil system                molar proportion model is established, according to                the fitted values. The minimum miscible pressure is                determined, according to the new oil system molar                proportion model and the miscible pressure                empirical model, where the molar proportion of the                formation oil system, regression coefficient and                crude oil attachment packet interface (API) degree                are determined.
   USE - Method for determining minimum miscible                pressure of carbon dioxide with formation crude                oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is convenient to use in the actual                oil extraction process, effectively improves the                oil extraction, efficiency solves the problem of                large calculation error of the minimum miscible                pressure, and effectively improves the calculation                precision of the minimum miscible pressure. The                data obtained by adopting the method is easy, so                that the technical scheme has strong                operability.

AE TIANJIN DAGANG OILFIELD BINGANG GROUP (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021480980
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing corrosion inhibitor comprises adding                0.3-0.6 g solid polyaspartic acid powder into 500                ml beaker containing 300 ml tap water, stirring at                400 revolutions/minute for 1.5-2 hours to obtain                uniform liquid, adding 80-100 mg/l solid oxygen                scavenger N, N'-tetra-substituted phenylenediamine                to 500 ml beaker containing 300 mL tap water,                stirring at 400 revolutions/minute for 20-30                minutes to obtain uniform liquid, adding 100 g                liquid removing agent N-isopropyl hydroxylamine,                mixing to obtain oxygen removing agent mixture,                adding 0.1-0.2 wt.% polyaspartic acid solution, 100                g mixture, 50 g liquid isothiazolinone, 30 g liquid                pentaerythritol antiseptic monooleate to 500 ml                beaker, stirring for 20-30 minutes under magnetic                force to obtain uniform liquid, transferring liquid                to 1000 mL beaker, adding 300 g liquid imidazoline                amide salt, and stirring for 20-30 minutes under                magnetic force.
   USE - The inhibitor is useful for resisting carbon                dioxide corrosion.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the corrosion inhibition                rate of each kind of high temperature and high                pressure oil well pipeline, and improves quality                and improves efficiency.

AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 202120425L
AB    NOVELTY - Producing hydrogen from iron-making blast furnace gas comprises (i) de-sulfurizing iron-making blast furnace gas to obtain desulfurized blast furnace gas, (ii) subjecting the desulfurized blast furnace gas to pressure swing adsorption of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, and removing nitrogen to obtain concentrated gas, (iii) subjecting the carbon monoxide of the concentrated coal gas to steam conversion to produce hydrogen to obtain hydrogen-containing conversion gas, and (iv) separating and purifying the hydrogen-containing mixed gas by decarbonization and pressure swing adsorption to obtain pure hydrogen.
   USE - The method is useful for producing hydrogen from iron-making blast furnace gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: does not require fossil fuels; has low cost, improved additional value and excellent performances; and ensures 1 m3 hydrogen cost of 0.7-0.8 yuan, selling price of 1.1-1.2 yuan, which is equivalent to fuel cell vehicle and gasoline vehicle cost, and have high economic value (based on the budget).

PT P
PN CN112079678-A; CN112079678-B
TI Method for remote functionalization of olefins to construct carboxylic acids or alcohols involves mixing olefin compound, photocatalyst, alkali and radical precursor, filling with carbon dioxide after freezing and degassing, and stirring.
AU YU D
   SONG L
   FU D
   CHEN L
   JIANG Y
   YE J
   ZHU L
   LAN Y
   FU Q
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 2020C7997K
AB    NOVELTY - Method for remote functionalization of olefins to construct carboxylic acids or alcohols involves (1) mixing olefin compound, photocatalyst, alkali, and radical precursor, dissolving the resulting solid phase mixture in a nitrogen atmosphere, filling with carbon dioxide after freezing and degassing, stirring for 2-10 minutes, or adding aldehydes or ketones to the resulting solid phase mixture, dissolving in a nitrogen atmosphere, freezing, degassing and filling with nitrogen, stirring for 2-10 minutes, (2) reacting the product obtained in step (1) with light at room temperature for 2-30 hours, and purifying. The added amount of the photocatalyst is 0.01-20% molar equivalent of the olefin compound. The amount of free radical precursor added is 1-3 times the molar equivalent of the olefin compound. The amount of alkali added is 0.1-5 times the molar equivalent of the olefin compound.
   USE - Method for remote functionalization of olefins to construct carboxylic acids or alcohols.
   ADVANTAGE - The method shows excellent reactivity for both activated olefin and non-activated olefin substrates, and produces product with high yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The alkali is cesium carbonate, potassium carbonate, cesium pivalate, cesium fluoride or cesium acetate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The pressure of carbon dioxide after charging in the step (1) is 0.5-30.0 times the atmospheric pressure. Preferred Components: The olefin compound is of formula (I). The olefin compound is chosen from one or more of 6,6-diphenyl-1-hexene, 1-allyl-2-ethylbenzene, N-(1-(4-(trifluoromethyl)phenyl)-5-pentenyl)benzamide and N-((1-phenyl)-5-pentenyl)benzamide. The photocatalyst is chosen from one or more of (4,4'-Bis(1,1-dimethylethyl)-2,2'-bipyridine-N1,N1')bis(2-(2-pyridinyl-N)phenyl-C)iridium(III) hexafluorophosphate, iridium (2,2'-bipyridine-N,N')bis(2-(2-pyridinyl)phenyl-C,N)hexaflouorophosphate and 2,4,5,6-tetra(9H-carbazol-9-yl)isophthalonitrile. The radical precursor is chosen from one or more of sodium trifluoromethanesulfinate, diphenoxyphosphorus and N-benzyloxycarbonylproline. The carboxylic acid is of formula (II). The alcohol is of formula (III).R1 = phenyl, 4-methylphenyl, 4-methoxyphenyl, 4-methoxycarbonylphenyl, 4-cyanophenyl, 4-trifluoromethylphenyl, 4-chlorophenyl, tert-butyl, cyclopropyl, thiophenyl, cyclohexyl, n-propyl, tert-butoxy, benzyloxy or ethoxy;R2 = phenyl, methyl, methoxy, phenoxy, carboxy, methoxycarbonyl, diethylamino, fluoro, bromo, chloro, trifluoromethyl, tert-butoxycarbonyl or 2-propynyloxy;andR3-R5 = methyl or H.Preferred Condition: The light source in the step (2) is a blue LED lamp with a wavelength of 3-60 W and a wavelength of 400-500 nm.

AB    NOVELTY - Extracting quercetin from plantain seed comprises (i) crushing the fresh plantain seed and soaking in water, then placing into sieve blue and submerging in liquid nitrogen for 30-60 minutes, then lifting and placing at room temperature for 5-10 minutes, immersing the sieve blue whole in liquid nitrogen for 30-60 minutes, and repeating for 2-3 times, (ii) taking plantain seed, drying, crushing and passing through 5-30 mesh sieve at low temperature to obtain plantain seed powder, (iii) mixing the plantain seed powder with the solvent and placing into the extraction kettle, passing supercritical carbon dioxide into the extraction kettle, controlling the temperature of the extraction kettle to 40-50 degrees C and extraction pressure is 30-45 Mpa, and (iv) flowing carbon dioxide containing the extract out of the extraction kettle and conveying the separation kettle for decompression separation to obtain quercetin.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting quercetin from plantain seed.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: avoids considering the loss of quercetin; and improves the quality and yield of quercetin extracted from plantain seed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (iii), placing the plantain seed powder in the extraction kettle before mixing with 0.01-0.08 times the weight of the biological enzyme and mixing for 30-60 minutes, where the biological enzyme is beta -glucosidase, pectinase or cellulase. In the step (ii), vacuum drying the plantain seed at low temperature, stacking plantain seed staggered in a vacuum drying oven, controlling the vacuum degree to 0.009-0.36 Mpa, and vacuum drying temperature is 40-52 degrees C, drying the plantain seed to a moisture content of less than 5 wt.%. In the step (iv), the pressure of the separation kettle is 7-9 MPa, and the temperature is 25-30 degrees C. Preferred Components: The solvent is low boiling point solvent, preferably acetone, ethanol, ether, methanol and/or methylene chloride.

PN CN111137980-A
TI Ecological purification method for bottom mud of polluted water, involves inducing redox reactions and compound reactions, deconstructing molecular chain, producing carbon dioxide and water, to mineralize pollutants.
AU ZHANG X
   ZHOU T
   ZHANG Y
AE SHANGHAI JINDUO YUCHEN WATER ENVIRONMENT (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202042474E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves putting nano-structured diamond thin-film electrode material structure into water, and releasing low-energy electrons driven by a low-voltage electric field. The low-energy electrons act on various groups is complexed and adsorbed together in the bottom mud. The complex components are dispersed to form a large number of discrete structures with carbon as the main skeleton, diffused in water, and at the same time, low-energy electrons decompose water to produce active oxygen groups. The redox reactions and compound reactions are induced, molecular chain is deconstructed, and carbon dioxide and water are produced, to mineralize pollutants, after the oxidized group in the water body contacts the discrete structure with carbon as the main skeleton.
   USE - In-situ ecological purification method for bottom mud of polluted water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple process, low cost, good repair effect and quick effect of polluted bottom mud, and the diamond nanomaterial film is composed of carbon element, which cannot pollute the environment.

PT P
PN CN110589831-A; CN110589831-B
TI Low-temperature preparation of silicon/silicon carbide comprises grinding magnesium silicide with grinding ball, transferring into sealed reactor, passing carbon dioxide and heating, soaking in dilute hydrochloric acid, filtering and drying.
AU HUANG H
   BIAN F
   YU J
   LIANG C
   XIA Y
   GAN Y
   ZHANG J
   ZHANG W
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 2019A76133
AB    NOVELTY - Low-temperature preparation of silicon/silicon carbide comprises (i) grinding magnesium silicide with grinding ball in ball-to-material ratio of (20-30):1 at 300-1000 revolutions/minute for 6-108 hours under inert atmosphere and transferring the mixture into a sealed reactor; (ii) vacuumizing the reactor, passing carbon dioxide under pressure of 1-80 bar, and heating to 300-1000 degrees C for 0.5-10 hours at a rate of 0.5-10 degrees C/minute; and (iii) cooling to room temperature, taking out the product from the reactor, soaking the product in 1 mol/l dilute hydrochloric acid for 20-30 minutes, washing with deionized water for 2-3 times, then washing with alcohol for 2-3 times, filtering and drying at 70-80 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for low-temperature preparation of silicon/silicon carbide.
   ADVANTAGE - The silicon/silicon carbide material has wear resistant property, corrosion resistant property and high-temperature resistant property. The method reduces energy consumption, is economical and alleviates greenhouse effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Magnesium silicide is chemically pure magnesium silicide. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the ball-to-material ratio is 30:1 and the grinding is carried out at 500 revolutions/minutes for 24 hours. In the step (ii), the carbon dioxide passing pressure is 10 bar, and the heating is carried out at 650 degrees C for 5 hours at a rate of 2 degrees C/minute.

TI New pyrazinyl-based metal-organic framework material comprises metal hydrate complex.
AU ZHANG Z
   PENG Y
   WANG T
AE UNIV NANKAI (UNAN-C)
GA 201990262K
AB    NOVELTY - Pyrazinyl-based metal-organic framework material comprises metal hydrate complex, is new.
   USE - Used as the pyrazinyl-based metal-organic framework material.
   ADVANTAGE - The metal-organic framework material: has high stability, preferably water stability and acid-base stability; has specific selective adsorption property to light hydrocarbon compounds due to sulfhydryl group, hydroxy group, and seleno group which can form strong hydrogen bonds with light hydrocarbon molecules or carbon dioxide, and has low energy consumption of light hydrocarbon compounds; is groundbreaking discovery in the gas separation; and can meet the requirements of industrialization by large-scale production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Pyrazinyl-based metal-organic framework material comprises metal hydrate complex (M1(M2(L)2).2H2O), where the M1 is copper, nickel, cobalt, platinum, palladium, iron, and gold, the M2 is copper, nickel, cobalt, platinum, palladium, iron, and gold, and the L is 2,3-dimercaptopyrazine, 2,3-diselenopyrazine, and 2,3-dihydroxypyrazine, the metal ion M2 in the metal ligand and node metal ion M1, respectively is planar tetracoordinate, is new. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the metal-organic framework material with high-efficiency light hydrocarbon compound (4C hydrocarbon/3C hydrocarbon/2C hydrocarbon methane//carbon dioxide/nitrogen) separating effect, is claimed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The metal-organic framework material is prepared by using metal complex containing pyrazinyl group as a ligand, adding metal salt solution in a drop wise manner to the metal ligand solution at room temperature or heating while stirring, or slowly diffusing the two solutions in an H-shaped pipe to obtain a large single crystal material.

PN CN109433179-A
TI Preparation of flower-like hydrotalcite-activated carbon composite photo-catalyst by preparing salt solution containing titanium, lithium and aluminum ions, mixing with activated carbon-chitosan mixing solution, heating, and centrifuging.
AU ZHAO X
   ZHOU A
   DUAN F
   LEI D
   LI G
   LI P
   LI R
AE UNIV XIAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYXS-C)
GA 2019250150
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of flower-like hydrotalcite-activated carbon composite photo-catalyst by preparing mixed salt solution containing titanium ion, lithium ion and aluminum ion, adding into activated carbon-chitosan mixed solution system, placing in pressure capacity bomb, adding urea, heating and centrifuging slurry containing precipitates in third step, washing precipitates, suction filtering and vacuum drying at 65-85 degrees C for 10-152 hours to obtain flower-like hydrotalcite-activated carbon composite photocatalyst. The volume ratio of deionized water, activated carbon and chitosan is 40:0.08-0.3:0.8-3. The molar ratio of urea to titanium ion in mixed salt solution is 25-50:1.
   USE - Method for preparing flower-like hydrotalcite-activated carbon composite photo-catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts chitosan as guiding agent and urea as precipitating agent to carry out hydrothermal synthesis reaction, induces orderly growth of flower-like hydrotalcite on surface of activated carbon, and improves UV absorption performance, photocatalytic efficiency and carbon dioxide capture of composite photocatalyst. The carbon oxide and methane can be detected in reaction product of photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide by composite photocatalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The mixed salt solution is prepared by taking titanium tetrachloride in beaker, slowly dropping deionized water in beaker, stirring uniformly, weighing lithium chloride and aluminum chloride into beaker, slowly adding deionized water to obtain mixed solution, stirring until mixed solution is transparent and clear, and transferring mixed solution to bottles.

PN CN109420478-A
TI Magnetic solid adsorbent useful for removing carbon dioxide from flue gas, comprises nano-scale magnetic solid as a carrier and organic amine as a supported material.
AU MAO S
   ZHENG Y
   JIANG Y
   WANG D
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   NCIG RES INST (NCIG-Non-standard)
GA 2019233925
AB    NOVELTY - Magnetic solid adsorbent comprises nano-scale magnetic solid as a carrier and 10-60 wt.% organic amine as a supported material.
   USE - The magnetic solid adsorbent is useful for removing carbon dioxide from flue gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The magnetic solid adsorbent: can be uniformly dispersed in aqueous solution and can be used on solvent removing device for common use; has fast absorbing speed, large absorption capacity, low regeneration energy consumption; is convenient for recycling adsorbent; and does not easily lose.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the magnetic solid adsorbent, comprising: (i) adding ferric chloride hexahydrate, anhydrous sodium acetate, polyvinylpyrrolidone and ethylene glycol into a high pressure reactor and reacting to obtain ferrouso ferric oxide magnetic microspheres with uniform particle size; (ii) hydrolyzing sodium silicate or potassium silicate with aluminum isopropoxide on surface of ferrouso ferric oxide magnetic microspheres to obtain a core/shell structure of silica-ferrouso ferric oxide and alumina-ferrouso ferric oxide magnetic microspheres; and (iii) loading the organic amine on surface of silica-ferrouso ferric oxide and alumina-ferrouso ferric oxide magnetic microspheres by immersion method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic amine is tetraethylenepentamine or polyethyleneimine. The particle size of alumina-ferrouso ferric oxide magnetic solid is 200-300 nm. Preferred Method: The molar ratio of polyvinylpyrrolidone and ferric chloride hexahydrate is 3:40, the amount of sodium acetate is 10-30 g, the reaction time is 6-12 hours and the reaction temperature is 200 degrees C. The hydrolysis is carried out for 1-3 hours, and the thickness of silica or alumina shell layer is 10-50 nm. The adsorbing flue gas temperature is 30-75 degrees C and the adsorbent regeneration temperature is 90-120 degrees C.

TI Cultivating toad tadpole initial feed used for promoting digestion, by inoculating algae, adding sterilized lake water and nutrient substance, controlling carbon dioxide aeration of water, illuminating glass container and collecting algae.
AU FANG T
   LU W

   ZHOU X
   YANG K
   ZHAO X
   LIANG Y

AE ANHUI ACAD AGRIC SCI FISHERIES RES INST (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2019120707
AB    NOVELTY - Method for cultivating a toad tadpole initial feed involves (a) inoculating the algae into a sterilized glass container at a volume ratio of 5-10%, adding the sterilized lake water and nutrient substance, adjusting the salinity of the water to 20-30 and controlling the carbon dioxide aeration of the water at 0.03-0.05 VVM and the DO value at 25-38%, (b) illuminating the glass container for 5-12 hours every day at the illumination intensity of 3000-5000 lux, maintaining water temperature at 23-28 degrees C and replacing the water for every 3-5 days and (c) collecting algae when the concentration of the algae is 108-1010 cells/l.
   USE - The method is useful for cultivating a toad tadpole for promoting digestion.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces the toad tadpole in a lot of batches. The initial feed has excellent palatability, is rich in nutrients, promotes growth and development of toad tadpole and improves survival rate and metamorphosis rate of the toad tadpole.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Microorganisms: The algae is chosen from at least one of prototheca, Rhizosolenia styliformis, diatom, dinoflagellate, Chlamydomonas, crescent shaped algae and Candida algae. The concentration of the algae in step (a) is 104-105 cells/l. Preferred Composition: The nutrient substance comprises 20-50 pts. wt. soybean meal, 10-25 pts. wt. starch, 20-48 pts. wt. soybean curb residue, 0.003-0.008 pt. wt. calcium chloride, 0.1-0.2 pt. wt. sodium chloride, 0.005-0.01 pt. wt. magnesium chloride, 0.02-0.04 pt. wt. dipotassium hydrogen phosphate, 0.01-0.03 pt. wt. monopotassium phosphate, 0.02-0.05 pt. wt. sodium silicate and 0.01-0.03 pt. wt. ammonium nitrate. The nutrient substance further comprises 0.5-3 wt.% vitamin, 0.2-2 wt.% amino acid, 0.8-1.6 wt.% auxin and 5-10 wt.% water.

TI New grass carp skeletal muscle myoblast cell line            having deposit number for use in study of gene function            and study of nutrient-regulated muscle cell growth and            differentiation, which is utilizes for research of            specific gene functions and nutrients on regulation of            grass carp muscle growth.
AU LIU G
   MAO X
   WANG X
   CHEN W
   LONG X
   TAN Q
AE UNIV HUAZHONG AGRIC (UHZA-C)
GA 2022A60389
AB    NOVELTY - Grass carp skeletal muscle myoblast cell line                having deposit number of CCTCC NO: C2022130, is                new.
   USE - Grass carp skeletal muscle myoblast cell line                for use in the study of gene function and the study                of nutrient-regulated muscle cell growth and                differentiation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The cell line has good transfection                performance and different growth performance under                different arginine treatments, and utilizes for the                research of specific gene functions and nutrients                on the regulation of grass carp muscle growth and                differentiation and its molecular mechanism.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                construction method for grass carp skeletal muscle                myoblast cell line, which involves:
   (a) anesthetizing grass carp fry, dissecting                the white muscle tissue between the dorsal fin and                lateral line of grass carp, sterilizing with                sterilized medium, and finally cutting the                sterilized muscle tissue into pieces to obtain                tissue fragments;
   (b) transferring the tissue fragments to a                centrifuge tube for centrifugation, removing the                supernatant to obtain tissue precipitates, washing                the obtained tissue precipitates with a washing                medium, repeating the washing twice, adding                collagenase type IV digestion solution and                dissociation medium, incubating on ice shaker for                50-90 min, performing centrifugation to obtain                precipitate, and washing the obtained precipitate                twice with washing medium;
   (c) resuspending the precipitate in sterilized                medium, grinding 5-10 times with a pipette until                the homogenate of the precipitate can enter and                exit the pipette relatively easily, centrifuging                for separation, removing the supernatant to get a                precipitate, adding trypsin solution, dissociation                medium, and incubating on ice for 15-20 min, adding                complete medium to stop digestion, and centrifuging                to obtain supernatant and precipitate, transferring                the obtained supernatant to a new centrifuge tube,                and repeating centrifugation were for the obtained                precipitate until the tissue fragments are                completely digested, and a completing medium                suspension;
   (d) filtering the complete medium suspension                with a cell strainer to remove large tissue                fragments, centrifuging, removing the supernatant,                and collecting the precipitate, fully suspending                the obtained precipitate, removing the supernatant                after centrifugation, and collecting the pellet                again;
   (e) resuspending the final pellet in complete                medium, pipetting and mixing to determine the                number of viable cells, diluting the cells to                (1.0-2.5)&#215;105cells/mL, and inoculating the                cells into T-25 cell culture flasks untreated with                poly-L-lysine and laminin placed in 5% carbon                dioxide, aspirating and transferring the                supernatant to a T-25 cell culture flask previously                treated with poly-L-lysine and laminin to continue                culturing;
   (f) purifying when the cells are filled with                about 80-90% of the culture flask, first                inoculating the cells on non-poly-L-lysine-and                laminin-treated T-25 cell culture flasks containing                5%carbon dioxide, pre-incubating the cells in a 28                &#176;C cell incubator for 3-8 hours, and                transferring the supernatant to a T-25 cell culture                flask previously treated with poly-L-lysine and                laminin for further culture, continuous purifying                for 5-7 passages, when the cell purity reaches 90%,                and stopping the purification; and
   (g) carrying out the normal passage with the                passage medium in the T-25 cell culture flask after                the purification is completed, passaging the cells                every 3-5 days, when the cells are passaged to 70                generations, successfully establishing the cell                line, and obtaining the grass carp skeletal                myoblast cell line.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The size of the cell                  filter is 100 &#956;m.Preferred Method: The preparation method of                  subculture medium comprises adding 1%                  penicillin/streptomycin double antibody solution                  and 10% fetal bovine serum in Dulbecco`s Modified                  Eagle Medium. The penicillin concentration in the                  penicillin/streptomycin double antibody solution                  is 10000 IU/mL. The concentration of streptomycin                  is 10000 &#956;g/mL.

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform rapid artificial cultivation of Phellinus ignius, where management and cultivation of the strains is vigorous and the growth rate is fast, which can be planted on a large scale.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing rapid artificial cultivation of Phellinus ignius involves taking mulberry wood chips with a length of 2-4 cm, soaking in a sodium hydroxide solution with a mass fraction of 2-3 % for 10-15 hours (h), removing the water to control dryness, performing steam explosion at a pressure of 3-6 MPa and a temperature of 150-200 degrees Celsius for 100-150 s, taking 50-60 pts. wt. mulberry wood chips, 10-15 pts. wt. corn flour, 2-4 pts. wt. sucrose, 2-5 pts. wt. potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 0.001-0.003 pts. wt. vitamin B, 0.1-0.3 pts. wt. zinc sulfate, 0.05-0.1 pts. wt. calcium nitrate, 200-400 pts. wt. water to prepare liquid culture medium, sterilizing liquid medium under high pressure and high temperature, inoculating Phellinus ignius strains, and cultivating in the dark at 25-28 degrees Celsius for 20-25 days until the mycelium grows to obtain Phellinus ignius cultivars. 80-100 pts. wt. mulberry wood chips, 5-15 pts. wt. medical stone powder, 5-10 pts. wt. corn flour, 3-5 pts. wt. sodium alginate, 2-5 pts. wt. sodium carboxymethyl cellulose, 0.5-2 pts. wt. potassium dihydrogen phosphate, 0.2- 0.5 pts. wt. magnesium sulfate, 0.1-0.3 pts. wt. zinc sulfate, 1-3 pts. wt. calcium sulfate are taken, mixed well, put into polyethylene plastic bags, and sterilized at normal pressure at 100 degrees Celsius 18- 20 h to obtain Phellinus ignius cultivation bag. The Phellinus ignius cultivation bag is inoculated with the Phellinus ignius cultivar and cultivated in the dark at 25-28 degrees Celsius for 1-2 days later, transferred to a greenhouse to continue culturing, humidity of the greenhouse is kept at 70-80%, the light intensity is 50-100 lux, and the light time is 8-12 h, carbon dioxide is passed every day to keep the concentration not less than 4000-6000 ppm, and cultivated for 10-15 days. The humidity of the shed is kept at 80-90 %, the light intensity is 50- 100 lux, the light time is 8-12 h, stop the introduction of carbon dioxide, ventilated for 10-20 min every 8 h, when the thickness of the fruit body reaches 5-7 cm and the length reaches 10- 13 cm, ventilate until harvesting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Conditions: The corn flour is filtered through a 300-400 mesh screen. The medical stone powder is filtered through a 300-400 mesh screen. The step of high pressure and high temperature sterilization is to sterilize 1-2 under the pressure of 1-1.5 MPa and the temperature of 120-130 degrees Celsius. h. The inoculation method is punching inoculation, 3-6 holes are punched on the surface of the cultivation bag, inoculated with Phellinus ignius, and then sealed and cultivated. After cultivating for 10-15 days, fruit is cut on the surface of the cultivation bag, and use the ring cutting method to cut, shape is half-moon,ccc the length is one-third of the perimeter of the bag material, the area is 5-8 cm2, and the quantity is 3-5. The temperature is set to variable temperature treatment, the daytime temperature is 25-28 degrees Celsius, and the night temperature is 15-20 degrees Celsius, when the thickness of the fruiting body reaches 5- 7 cm, when the length reaches 10-13 cm, constant temperature treatment is adopted, and the temperature is controlled at 25-28 degrees Celsius.

PN CN113813289-A
TI Preparing hair follicle generation promoting solution based on umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell exosome, comprises e.g. disinfecting and pre-treating umbilical cord tissue sample, digesting with trypsin, subculturing, mixing cholesterol and lecithin, and performing ultrasonic dispersion.
AU ZHENG X
   WANG Z
   HUANG Y
AE SHENZHEN LAILISAI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202206968Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hair follicle generation promoting solution based on umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell exosome, comprises (i) disinfecting and pre-treating the umbilical cord tissue sample, cutting into small tissue segments, rinsing with sterile phosphate buffered saline solution, and cutting into tissue fragments, placing in ultra-clean petri dish, (ii) adding the modified DMEM medium, placing in incubator and introducing carbon dioxide gas, (iii) digesting with trypsin, and subculturing and expanding, (iv) replacing the serum-free basal medium to continue culturing, collecting culture medium supernatant to obtain a culture medium containing umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell exosomes, and resuspending in sterile phosphate buffered saline solution and freezing at -80degrees Celsius, (v) culturing the third-generation umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells, mixing cholesterol and lecithin, and performing ultrasonic dispersion to obtain a hair follicle generation promoting solution.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing hair follicle generation promoting solution based on umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell exosome.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the hair follicle generation promoting solution of hair follicle degeneration, atrophy, alopecia, alopecia areata and other symptoms, and has a significant and more lasting effect on promoting hair regeneration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing hair follicle generation promoting solution based on umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell exosome, comprises (i) disinfecting and pre-treating the umbilical cord tissue sample, removing the gelatinous material, umbilical cord adventitia and umbilical artery and vein, cutting into small tissue segments, rinsing three times with sterile phosphate buffered saline solution, and cutting into tissue fragments of 2plus minus0.5 mm3 volume, placing in an ultra-clean petri dish, (ii) adding the modified DMEM medium with 5-8 times the volume of the tissue fragments, shaking to make the tissue fragments evenly dispersed in the modified DMEM medium, and placing in a constant temperature incubator, introducing carbon dioxide gas until its volume fraction is 5-9%, and changing the half volume of the liquid every 2 days, after three times, changing the full amount of medium every 3 days, (iii) when the fusion degree of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells reaches 80-90%, digesting with trypsin, and subculturing and expanding, (iv) when the confluence of the third-generation umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells reaches 70-80%, replacing the serum-free basal medium to continue culturing, and when the confluence of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells reaches 85-95%, collecting the culture medium supernatant to obtain a culture medium containing umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell exosomes, centrifuging to remove the third-generation umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells and cell debris, and continue to ultracentrifuge the culture medium to obtain umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell exosomes, resuspending in sterile phosphate buffered saline solution and freezing at -80degrees Celsius, (v) culturing the third-generation umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells until the fifth-generation umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells reach 70-80% confluence, and collecting the fifth-generation umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells, (vi) mixing the umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell exosomes, obtaining the fifth-generation umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells and dermal papilla cells and placing in a serum-free basal medium for culture to obtain a mixed medium, mixing cholesterol and lecithin, dissolving in the chloroform solution with a mass concentration of 50%, evaporating to dryness to obtain liposomes, subsequently, adding the mixed medium, and performing ultrasonic dispersion to obtain a hair follicle generation promoting solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (i), the concrete steps of disinfection and pretreatment are (ia) sterilizing the umbilical cord tissue sample by immersing in 75% ethanol solution for 1-2 minutes and (ib) rinsing the sterilized umbilical cord tissue sample with sterile phosphate buffered saline solution 1-2 times to remove residual blood. In the step (i), the length of the tissue segment is 2plus minus0.5 cm. In step (ii), the modified DMEM culture medium contains 10% fetal bovine serum by volume, where the mass concentration of streptomycin is 100 g/l, the unit concentration of penicillin is 100 U/mL, the mass concentration of hydroxyproline is 23-28 mg/l, the mass concentration of chitosan is 55-60 mg/l, and the pH is 6.8-7.2. In step (ii), the temperature of the constant temperature incubator is 36.5-37.5degrees Celsius, and the humidity is saturated humidity. In the step (iii), the mass concentration of trypsin is 2.5 g/l, and the trypsin is mixed with 0.02% mass concentration of EDTA, and the passage ratio is 1:2-3. In step (vi), the method for obtaining the dermal papilla cells is as follows: taking the hair follicle tissue and washing in sterile phosphate buffered saline solution for 2-3 times, cutting into pieces of 2plus minus0.5 mm volume of tissue fragments, dissolving in enough collagenase type I, stirring for 2-4 hours, filtering and drying, dissolving in a sufficient amount of type IV collagenase, stirring and reacting for 2-4 hours, screening out the dermal papilla tissue under a microscope and placing in an ultra-clean petri dish, adding modified DMEM medium for culture, changing half-volume medium every 2 days, and changing the full volume every 3 days after three times, culturing for two weeks, passaging the cells for 72-75 hours, and collecting the primary dermal papilla cells.Preferred Components: In the step (iv), the serum-free basal culture medium is a phenol red-free alpha-MEM medium, where the mass concentration of D-glucose is 2250mg/l, the mass concentration of L-alanyl-L-glutamine is 380 mg/l, the mass concentration of serine is 30-33 mg/l, the concentration of described tryptophan is 8-12 mg/l, the mass concentration of biotin is 0.7-1 mg/l, the mass concentration of vitamin B12 is 0.02-0.03 mg/l, and the concentration of sodium pyruvate is 15-20 mmol/l. In step (vi), the mass ratio of cholesterol and lecithin is 7-9:1, the volume ratio of mixed medium, chloroform and liposome is 1.25-1.6:1:0.2, the mass concentration of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell exosomes in the mixed medium is 500-600 ng/l, the concentration of fifth-generation umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells is 109/l, and the concentration of dermal papilla cells was 1010/l.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN KR2328652-B1
TI Producing hot plate useful for thermal fusion of lead acid battery case, comprises e.g. adding graphite flakes into mixed solution containing nitric acid and sulfuric acid, stirring, and adding potassium chlorate to control rapid reaction.
AU MO C
   YOUN K
   EUNKIHONG
AE HANKOOK & CO CO LTD (KOKO-Non-standard)
GA 2021E0385K
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of hot plate involves adding 5 pts. wt. graphite flakes to a mixed solution consisting of 45 pts. wt. nitric acid and 90 pts. wt. sulfuric acid under stirring for 30 minutes-1 hour, adding 50 pts. wt. potassium chlorate several times at 0 degrees C to control the rapid reaction of the graphite flake mixture and the oxidizing agent to be added, stirring at room temperature for 120 hours, washing and filtering until pH 6.5 to stop the oxidation reaction by removing the residual oxidizing agent, and drying for 24 hours at 80 degrees C in a vacuum atmosphere to prepare graphite oxide, exposing the prepared graphite oxide to a high temperature environment of 1000 degrees C, generating carbon dioxide between the layers by the reaction between the functional groups and oxygen present in the graphite oxide, and the interlayer distance increases, wet coating the prepared graphene on the hot plate using a wet coating method, drying at 100 degrees C in a vacuum atmosphere for 24 hours.
   USE - Manufacture of hot plate for thermal fusion of lead acid battery case.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of hot plate having excellent corrosion resistance, thermal conductivity and wear resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manufacture of hot plate involves adding 5 pts. wt. graphite flakes to a mixed solution consisting of 45 pts. wt. nitric acid and 90 pts. wt. sulfuric acid under stirring for 30 minutes-1 hour, adding 50 pts. wt. potassium chlorate several times at 0 degrees C to control the rapid reaction of the graphite flake mixture and the oxidizing agent to be added, stirring at room temperature for 120 hours, washing and filtering until pH 6.5 to stop the oxidation reaction by removing the residual oxidizing agent, and drying for 24 hours at 80 degrees C in a vacuum atmosphere to prepare graphite oxide, exposing the prepared graphite oxide to a high temperature environment of 1000 degrees C, generating carbon dioxide between the layers by the reaction between the functional groups and oxygen present in the graphite oxide, and the interlayer distance increases, wet coating the prepared graphene on the hot plate using a wet coating method, drying at 100 degrees C in a vacuum atmosphere for 24 hours. The wet coating comprises a dispersion comprising a solvent in which 40 wt.% acetone and 60 wt.% distilled water are mixed, and the amount of graphene added to the solvent is 1:0.05 compared to the dispersion, and the prepared graphene dispersion is dispersed on a hot plate. The precipitation of the dispersion during the coating time is prevented by adding 5-10 pts. wt. carboxymethyl cellulose. The wet coating using the graphene dispersion is deposited using an electrospray coating (ESC) method. The oxide film and contaminants of the surface layer on the aluminum hot plate are removed before wet coating. The contaminated layer is polished using 800 grit silicon carbide abrasive paper, and after polishing, the contaminants are removed through ultrasonic cleaning in acetone for 10 minutes. The electrospray deposition involves flexibly providing a voltage of 10-15 kV to prevent agglomeration coating of the graphene dispersion, and a movement speed of 10 cm/minute is set at an interval of 10 mm in the X-axis direction, and then moved in the form of a square wave by uniform coating, spraying at a spraying rate of 500 mu L per minute, and coating at a spraying amount of 1 mL per unit area, and heating to 100 degrees C to quickly evaporate the solvent to minimize agglomeration.

AU HUN K
AE GARAM FOOD CORP (GARA-Non-standard)
GA 2021C2425A
AB    NOVELTY - Vacuum packaging meat, involves photographing an image of a meat, determining a thickness of a packaging container for packaging the meat based on the degree of maturity of the meat. The first vacuum is put into the inside of the packaging container with the juice storage attached to one side of the packaging container made of the selected material, and vacuum is created until the air in the receiving space of the packaging container is 210 hPa or less. An air replacement step of replaced the air in the first vacuum-packed container with a mixed gas comprising a volume of 68.37 wt.% carbon dioxide, 31.12 wt. % nitrogen, 0.21wt. % carbon monoxide, and 0.3wt. % oxygen; An electronic tag is attached to a refrigeration storage device after irradiating the meat with green light. The meat is irradiated with yellow light at an intensity of 28 mW/cm2 for 12 hours, and irradiates blue light to the meat when the meat is a chicken.
   USE - Method for vacuum packaging meat to degree of maturity of meat.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables freshness of meat is properly maintained by providing an appropriate packaging container to the maturity level of the meat. Improve the taste and quality of meat. Maintain the meat color for a long time. Increase the shelf life of the product. Low production of the volatile base nitrogen. Reduce the growth of microorganisms to prevent spoilage of meat.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Vacuum packaging meat, involves photographing an image of meat, determining a thickness of a packaging container for packaging the meat based on the degree of maturity of the meat. The first vacuum is put into the inside of the packaging container with the juice storage attached to one side of the packaging container made of the selected material, and vacuum is created until the air in the receiving space of the packaging container is 210 hPa or less. An air replacement step of replaced the air in the first vacuum-packed container with a mixed gas comprising a volume of 68.37 wt.% carbon dioxide, 31.12 wt. % nitrogen, 0.21wt. % carbon monoxide, and 0.3wt. % oxygen; second vacuum packaging step of creating a vacuum state until the air in the receiving space of the packaging container is 150 hPa or less; A meat juice extraction step of stored the meat after the second vacuum packaged has been completed by stored in water at 900 degrees C for 5 minutes to sterilized and flowing the juice generated from the meat into the juice storage unit for storage; a third vacuum packaging step of creating a vacuum state until the air in the receiving space of the packaging container that has undergone the juice extraction step becomes 100 hPa or less; the third vacuum packaging was completed at 55% RH/25 at 22.5 mL/m2 oxygen permeability of /24hours atmosphere and 5.3 g/m at 100% RH/252. A fourth vacuum packaging step of vacuum packaging using a low density polyethylene/nylon vacuum bag having a water vapor permeability of /24hours, an electronic tag for attached an electronic tag including information on determining whether to refrigerate the meat, whether to refrigerated in water, whether to pressure spray, or whether to freezed, is attached to the meat after the fourth vacuum packaging has been completed and discharged the meat to which the electronic tag is attached to a refrigeration storage device and irradiates blue light to the meat the meat is a chicken.

PN CN113319403-A
TI Welding construction method of ultra-thick plate            by filling and welding multiple welding layers of upper            groove, removing root of welding channel, welding            multiple welding layers of lower groove, and welding            upper groove and lower groove.
AU LU F
   ZHANG S
   LIU D
   WEI S
   XUE L
AE CHINA CONSTR FIRST BUILDING GROUP CO LTD (CSCE-C)
   CHINA CONSTR FIRST GROUP CORP LTD HUABEI (CSCE-C)
GA 2021A42144
AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises (i) using flame cutting                machine to make asymmetric X-shaped groove on part                to be welded on ultra-thick plate, (ii) uniformly                preheating the groove, (iii) using argon arc                welding to locate and weld blunt part; (iv) using                low-hydrogen carbon dioxide gas shielded welding to                carry out bottom welding to the blunt part; (v)                filling and welding multiple welding layers of                upper groove; (vi) removing the root of the welding                channel, (vii) filling and welding multiple welding                layers of the lower groove; (viii) welding upper                groove and lower groove; (ix) welding the top of                the upper groove and the lower groove to ensure the                top surface is smooth, and (x) using thermocouple                heating plate to carry out heat treatment to the                welded ultra-back plate, eliminating or reducing                the welding stress.
   USE - The method is useful for welding construction                of ultra-thick plate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively improves the                super-thick plate welding characteristic, prevents                welding channel root crack and air hole, ensures                the steel welding performance is stable, reduces                the cooling speed of the welding seam, prevents                joint generating quenching hard tissue, generating                crack, after welding, the heating process                effectively reduces or eliminates the welding                residual stress and ensures the welding                quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method comprises (i) using flame cutting                machine to make asymmetric X-shaped groove on part                to be welded on ultra-thick plate, where depth of                upper groove is greater than the depth of lower                groove; (ii) using flame heating nozzle to                continuously move on the groove surface to                uniformly preheat the groove to be welded, using                infrared laser thermometer to measure preheating                temperature and interlayer temperature, controlling                the preheating temperature and the interlayer                temperature at 150-180 degrees C; (iii) after                pre-heating the groove, using argon arc welding to                locate and weld blunt part; (iv) using low-hydrogen                carbon dioxide gas shielded welding to carry out                bottom welding to the blunt part; (v) filling and                welding multiple welding layers of the upper                groove; controlling the welding speed, reducing the                welding seam shrinkage; using thermal insulation                cotton to slowly cool the completed welds of                welding layers, detecting the deformation of the                filling welding; (vi) turning the welded                super-thick plate; processing the root of the                welding channel from the lower groove by using the                carbon arc air gouging gun; detecting whether there                is defect in the root of the welding channel; (vii)                filling and welding multiple welding layers of the                lower groove; controlling the welding speed,                reducing the welding seam shrinkage; using thermal                insulation cotton to slowly cool the completed                welding seam of the multiple weld layers, detecting                the deformation of the filling welding; (viii)                repeating step (v) and (vii), welding upper groove                and lower groove; in the welding process, measuring                the welding deformation condition at any time,                continuously adjusting the welding layer number and                turning times; (ix) welding the top of the upper                groove and the lower groove to ensure the top                surface is smooth, after the temperature of the                super-thick plate is naturally cooled to                environment temperature, starting to check the                appearance of the welding seam, observing and                deforming, measuring, checking, after correcting                without error, polishing the surface welding layer;                and (x) using thermocouple heating plate to carry                out heat treatment to the welded ultra-back plate,                eliminating or reducing the welding stress.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a flow chart illustrating the                welding construction method of a super-thick plate                (Drawing includes non-English language                text).

PN CN113104877-A
TI Preparing superfine dolomite involves crushing dolomite, sieving to obtain dolomite powder, adding dolomite powder, grinding aid into water, stirring uniformly to obtain dolomite slurry.
AU HE F
AE TONGLING BOFENG IND CO LTD (TONG-Non-standard)
GA 202181855T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing superfine dolomite involves crushing the dolomite, sieving to obtain the dolomite powder with grain diameter of 300-350 meshes, adding the dolomite powder, grinding aid into water, stirring uniformly to obtain dolomite slurry, adding the dolomite slurry into the peeling machine for ultra-fine peeling, obtaining superfine slurry, dehydrating the superfine slurry, drying, crushing to obtain dolomite fine powder. The dolomite powder and the calcium hydroxide solution are added into a four-mouth flask equipped with a stirrer, then placed the four-mouth flask in a constant temperature water bath, stirred and added the crystal form control agent, then introduced mixed gas of air and carbon dioxide into the three-mouth flask, continuously stirred and reacted, when the pH is equal to 7, stop the reaction, spray dried the reaction liquid, formed powder, to obtain the superfine dolomite.
   USE - Method for preparing superfine dolomite.
   ADVANTAGE - The superfine dolomite solves the problem that the grain diameter of the existing dolomite powder is large, the use range is small, and the superfine powder is easy to agglomerate and not easy to be uniformly dispersed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing superfine dolomite involves crushing the dolomite, sieving to obtain the dolomite powder with grain diameter of 300-350 meshes, adding the dolomite powder, grinding aid into water, stirring uniformly to obtain dolomite slurry, adding the dolomite slurry into the peeling machine for ultra-fine peeling, obtaining superfine slurry, dehydrating the superfine slurry, drying, crushing to obtain dolomite fine powder. The dolomite powder and the calcium hydroxide solution are added into a four-mouth flask equipped with a stirrer, then placed the four-mouth flask in a constant temperature water bath, stirred and added the crystal form control agent, then introduced mixed gas of air and carbon dioxide into the three-mouth flask, continuously stirred and reacted, when the pH is equal to 7, stop the reaction, spray dried the reaction liquid, formed powder, to obtain the superfine dolomite. The preparation process of the grinding aid involves adding the phenol to a three-mouth flask equipped with a condenser and a stirrer, then adding octanoic acid and triethanolamine, placing the three-mouth flask in the constant temperature oil bath, stirring and reacting for 7-9 hours under the condition of the temperature is 130-140 degrees C, the stirring speed is 100-200r/min, until the water completely discharged to obtain intermediate 1 crude product. The intermediate 1 crude product is dissolved in isooctane to obtain intermediate 1 crude solution. The intermediate 1 crude solution is washed with sodium hydroxide aqueous solution and distilled water 3-5 each time. The unreacted octanoic acid and triethanolamine and phenol are removed, then steamed the intermediate 1 crude solution to constant weight to obtain intermediate. The maleic anhydride is added to a thermometer, stirrer and four-mouth flask of the condensing pipe, then added intermediate 1, stirred at the temperature of 20-30 degrees C, stirring speed is 100-200r/min to the reaction liquid to clear, then raised the temperature to 50-60 degrees C continuously stirring for 30-50 minutes until forming light yellow clear solution, to obtain intermediate 2. The allylpolyoxyethylene ether is added into a four-mouth flask equipped with a thermometer, a stirrer and a condensing pipe. The intermediate 2 and de-ionized water are added, stirred and reacted under the condition of stirring speed of 100-200r/min, and heated, controlled the heating speed at 5 degrees C/minute, when the temperature is increased to 60-85 degrees C, added initiator, kept the temperature and stirred and reacted for 3-4 hours, after polymerization, cooled the reaction product to 40 degrees C, added sodium hydroxide water solution to adjust the pH to 6.5-7.5 to obtain the grinding aid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The weight ratio of the dolomite coarse powder to the grinding aid to the water is (50-75):(0.25-1.5): 25-50.1. The dosage ratio of the crystalline form control agent is 1g: 10mL: 0.01-0.02g, the quantity concentration of the substance of the calcium hydroxide solution is 0.25-0.35mol/L, the crystalline form control agent is zinc sulfate. The flow rate of the carbon dioxide is 0.4-0.6m3/h.

PN CN112266825-A
TI Essential oil comprises Suaeda salsa seed oil,            pepper seed oil, orange seed oil and fructus meliae            toosendan oil.
AU GONG Y
   ZHANG M
   LI H
   XING Z
   ZHANG H
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 2021123737
AB    NOVELTY - Essential oil comprises Suaeda salsa seed oil,                pepper seed oil, orange seed oil and fructus meliae                toosendan oil of ratio 3-4:1-2:2-3:5-6:5-6, the                preparation method of the Suaeda salsa seed oil is                comrpises taking Suaeda salsa, crushing, sieving,                adding in the microwave dynamic extraction device                barrel, adding 60 times of water, soaking for 45                minutes at room temperature, setting microwave                power 287 W microwave radiation for 30 minutes, the                microwave extraction device is connected with the                volatile oil separator, collecting the distilled                essential oil to obtain the Suaeda salsa seed oil.                The preparation method of pepper seed oil is                comprises taking pepper seed, crushing, sieving,                adding in supercritical CO2 extraction kettle,                adding 2.5 times of 70 % of ethanol, soaking for                150 minutes at room temperature, the extracting                condition is as follows: the extracting pressure is                40 MPa, the extracting temperature is 40 degrees C                for 3 hours.
   USE - The essential oil is useful in whitening the                skin and delay aging.
   ADVANTAGE - The essential oil is can inhibit the activity                of tyrosinase, with high efficiency and oxidation                resistance; reduce the volatilization of essential                oil, protects the active ingredient of essential                oil; it is mild and has no toxic and side effect;                long-term use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Essential oil comprises Suaeda salsa seed oil,                pepper seed oil, orange seed oil and fructus meliae                toosendan oil of ratio 3-4:1-2:2-3:5-6:5-6, the                preparation method of the Suaeda salsa seed oil is                comrpises taking Suaeda salsa, crushing, sieving,                adding in the microwave dynamic extraction device                barrel, adding 60 times of water, soaking for 45                minutes at room temperature, setting microwave                power 287 W microwave radiation for 30 minutes, the                microwave extraction device is connected with the                volatile oil separator, collecting the distilled                essential oil to obtain the Suaeda salsa seed oil.                The preparation method of pepper seed oil is                comprises taking pepper seed, crushing, sieving,                adding in supercritical CO2 extraction kettle,                adding 2.5 times of 70 % of ethanol, soaking for                150 minutes at room temperature, theextracting                condition is as follows: the extracting pressure is                40 MPa, the extracting temperature is 40 degrees C                for 3 hours, the carbon dioxide flow speed is 20                kg/h, filtering the extracting solution, using                rotary evaporator to recycle 70% ethanol and                volatilizing to obtain the pepper seed oil. The                preparation method of orange seed oil comprises                taking citrus seed, crushing, sieving, adding in                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle,                adding 3 times 70% ethanol, soaking for 150 minutes                at room temperature, the extracting condition                comprises extracting pressure is 35 MPa, the                extracting temperature is 45 degrees C, the                extracting time is 3 hours, the CO2 flow speed is                25 kg/h, filtering the extracting solution, using                rotary evaporator to recycle 70% ethanol and                volatilizing to obtain the orange seed oil. The                preparation method of the fructus meliae toosendan                oil comprises taking fructus meliae toosendan,                crushing, sieving, adding in round bottom flask,                adding 10 times of distilled water, using                hydrochloric acid to adjust pH to 4.5, adding 1%                cellulase into the container, slightly stirring,                adding in water bath at 50 degrees C constant                temperature and carrying out enzymolysis for 35                minutes, using water vapor distillation, installing                the volatile oil extractor, heating until the water                is boiling, start timing, distilling for 2.5 hours,                collecting the distilled essential oil to obtain                the fructus meliae toosendan oil, mixing alkali                seed oil, pepper seed oil, orange seed oil, fructus                meliae toosendan oil uniformly to obtain the                product. use method of compounded essential oil                nanocapsules comprising adding 10-15% nanocapsules                to whitening and anti-wrinkle cosmetics and can be                made into whitening anti-wrinkle moisturizing water                and cream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The ratio of Suaeda                  salsa seed oil, pepper seed oil, orange seed oil                  and fructus meliae toosendan oil is 3: 1: 2: 5.                  The compound essential oil nanocapsule is made                  into a nanocapsule containing 33% compound                  essential oil by using a reverse emulsification                  method. Preferred Method: The preparation method                  of the compound essential oil nanocapsule                  comprises taking 0.1 g acetyl chitosan, adding in                  a beaker, adding de-ionized water to 10 ml to                  obtain acetyl chitosan solution, taking 10 g                  sodium alginate, adding in a beaker, adding 10 ml                  compound essential oil ultrasonic emulsification                  completely, slowly dropping 0.5 mol/l sodium                  hydroxide to adjust the pH value to 7.5 to obtain                  sodium alginate nanocapsule, washing and                  centrifuging, adding to the acetyl chitosan                  solution, magnetically stirring for 3 hours to                  obtain the compound essential oil                  nanocapsule.
   ACTIVITY - Nootropic; Dermatological.

PT P
PN CN112225240-A
TI Preparing aluminum fluoride from fluorite, comprises e.g. finely crushing fluorite to obtain fluorite powder, mixing fluorite powder and ammonium sulfate powder, reacting ammonium fluoride with aluminum hydroxide, and carbonizing.
AU ZHANG X
   LI C
AE ZHANG X (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 202109245C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing aluminum fluoride from fluorite, comprises (i) finely crushing fluorite to obtain fluorite powder, mixing fluorite powder and ammonium sulfate powder in the reaction furnace and heating to obtain calcium sulfate solid and ammonium fluoride, and collecting the volatile ammonium fluoride, (ii) reacting ammonium fluoride with aluminum hydroxide or aluminum oxide to obtain aluminum fluoride and ammonia gas, (iii) carbonizing the ammonia to obtain ammonium carbonate solution, (iv) reacting ammonium carbonate solution with the calcium sulfate and filtering to obtain calcium carbonate filter cake and ammonium sulfate solution, evaporating the ammonium sulfate solution to obtain ammonium sulfate crystals, where ammonium sulfate is used in step (i) of the next production cycle, and (v) repeating steps (i) to (iv), the ammonium sulfate in step (i) adopts the ammonium sulfate produced in step (iv) in the previous production cycle, and enters the next production cycle.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing aluminum fluoride from fluorite.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not consume concentrated sulfuric acid and does not produce fluorogypsum, and has low cost and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device for preparing aluminum fluoride from fluorite, comprising high-temperature reaction furnace, ammonium fluoride heating kettle, fluidized bed reactor, ammonium fluoride condensation tank, and ammonium fluoride absorption tank which are sequentially connected by pipelines, gas compressor and gypsum carbonization kettle, top of ammonium fluoride heating kettle and the bottom of ammonium fluoride condensation tank are also connected by pipelines, first valve is provided on the pipe connecting the ammonium fluoride heating kettle and the fluidized bed reactor, second valve is provided on the pipe connecting the ammonium fluoride heating kettle and the ammonium fluoride condensation tank, (i) adding ammonium fluoride in the ammonium fluoride heating kettle in advance, adding gypsum suspension into gypsum carbonization kettle, and introducing carbon dioxide gas, adding deionized water into the ammonium fluoride absorption tank, heating in high temperature reactor, ammonium fluoride heating kettle and fluidized bed reactor, opening first valve, closing the second valve, turning on the gas compressor, adding fluorite powder and ammonium sulfate into the high temperature reaction furnace, adding aluminum hydroxide or aluminum oxide into the fluidized bed reactor to obtain calcium sulfate, ammonium fluoride, and fluorine in the high temperature reaction furnace, performing volatilization of ammonium and the separation of calcium sulfate, (ii) volatizing ammonium fluoride produced in the ammonium fluoride heating kettle into the fluidized bed reactor, reacting ammonium fluoride with aluminum hydroxide or aluminum oxide to obtain aluminum fluoride solid and ammonia gas, water vapor and residual ammonium fluoride gas, introducing mixed gas into ammonium fluoride condensation tank from the fluidized bed reactor to be condensed to obtain ammonium fluoride powder, introducing residual gas into ammonium fluoride absorption tank and is absorbed by deionized water, (iii) introducing gas from the ammonium fluoride absorption tank into gypsum carbonization kettle through the gas compressor, and reacting with the introduced carbon dioxide and water to generate ammonium carbonate, (iv) reacting ammonium carbonate and gypsum in the gypsum carbonization kettle to obtain ammonium sulfate solution and calcium carbonate solids, discharging calcium carbonate and ammonium sulfate suspension in the gypsum carbonization kettle, filtering and evaporating to obtain ammonium sulfate crystals, which are used in step (i) of the next production cycle, discharging solid calcium sulfate in the high temperature reaction furnace of next production cycle, and discharge the slag after silicon removal in the ammonium fluoride heating kettle, and (v) repeating steps (i)-(iv) to enter the next production cycle, adding ammonium sulfate into medium and high temperature reactor and the calcium sulfate added in gypsum carbonization kettle of the previous production cycle, there is no need to re-add it, so the production cycle is carried out.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (ii), the ammonium fluoride reacts with sodium fluoride or potassium fluoride before reacting with aluminum hydroxide or aluminum oxide to remove silicon tetrafluoride impurities in the ammonium fluoride. In the step (iii), carbonizing ammonia gas is to react ammonia gas with carbon dioxide and water to generate ammonium carbonate. The ammonium fluoride condensation tank is a cyclone separator. In step (i), while adding ammonium fluoride in advance in the ammonium fluoride heating kettle, adding sodium fluoride or potassium fluoride. In step (i), the ratio of calcium fluoride to ammonium sulfate in the fluorite powder is 1:1-1.4, the reaction time is 60-150 minutes, and the reaction temperature is 350-500 degrees C. In step (ii), the molar ratio of ammonium fluoride to aluminum in the raw material in the ammonium fluoride heating kettle is 12-25:1, and the reaction temperature is 500-600 degrees C.

PT P
PN WO2020229771-A1; FR3095947-A1; TW202108118-A; CA3140206-A1; AU2020273608-A1; CN114080229-A; KR2022027842-A; EP3969020-A1; BR112021022808-A2; IN202127054355-A; VN85517-A; FR3095947-B1; JP2022532373-W
TI Isolating molecules contained in aragonite and/or            calcitic organo-mineral layers of shell of bivalve            marine mollusk, involves e.g. grinding aragonite and/or            calcitic organo-mineral layers to obtain aragonite            powder and/or calcitic powder.
AU CAMPRASSE S
   CAMPRASSE G
   SERGE C
   GEORGES C
AE MBP MAURITIUS LTD (MBPM-Non-standard)
   MBP MAURITIUS LTD (MBPM-Non-standard)
   MBP MAURITIUS LTD (MBPM-Non-standard)
GA 2020B3704L
AB    NOVELTY - Isolating molecules contained in aragonite                organo-mineral layer and/or in calcitic                organo-mineral layer of shell of bivalve marine                mollusk, comprises: (a) grinding aragonite                organo-mineral layer and/or the calcitic                organo-mineral layer to obtain aragonite powder                and/or calcitic powder; (b) hot percolation of                aragonite powder and/or of calcite powder to obtain                (b1) saturated aragonite solution and/or saturated                calcite solution, and (b2) aragonite and/or                calcitic percolation powders; (c) separating                saturated aragonite solution and/or saturated                calcite solution to recover (c1) aragonitic and/or                calcitic liquid phases, and (c2) aragonitic and/or                calcitic solid phases; and (d) supercritical carbon                dioxide (CO2) treatment of percolating aragonitic                powder and/or percolating calcitic powder to obtain                (d1) aragonitic and/or calcitic powders treated                with supercritical CO2, and (d2) all or part of                soluble molecules contained in aragonitic and/or                calcitic organo-mineral layers.
   USE - The method is useful for isolating molecules                contained in an aragonite organo-mineral layer                and/or in a calcitic organo-mineral layer of a                shell of a bivalve marine mollusk. The composition                is used: as a medicament; as a culture medium,                preferably as a culture medium for the maturation                and/or proliferation of stem cells or progenitor                cells; and for the correction of ptosis, skin                depression, deep and superficial wrinkles, and/or                prevention of body aging (all claimed). No                biological data given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Isolating molecules contained in an aragonite                organo-mineral layer and/or in a calcitic                organo-mineral layer of a shell of a bivalve marine                mollusk, comprises: (a) grinding the aragonite                organo-mineral layer and/or the calcitic                organo-mineral layer to obtain an aragonite powder                and/or a calcitic powder; (b) hot percolation of                the aragonite powder and/or of the calcite powder                to obtain (b1) a saturated aragonite solution                and/or a saturated calcite solution, in which the                saturated aragonite solution comprises an aragonite                liquid phase and an aragonite solid phase and the                saturated calcite solution comprises a calcite                liquid phase and a calcite solid phase, and (b2) an                aragonite percolation powder and/or a calcitic                percolation powder; (c) separating the saturated                aragonite solution and/or the saturated calcite                solution to recover (c1) the aragonitic liquid                phase and/or the calcitic liquid phase, and (c2)                the aragonitic solid phase and/or the calcitic                solid phase; and (d) supercritical carbon dioxide                (CO2) treatment of the percolating aragonitic                powder and/or the percolating calcitic powder to                obtain (d1) an aragonitic powder treated with                supercritical CO2 and/or a calcitic powder treated                with supercritical CO2, and (d2) all or part of the                soluble molecules contained in the aragonitic                organo-mineral layer and/or in the calcitic                organo-mineral layer. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also                included for:
   (1) a composition comprising the aragonitic                solid phase and/or the calcitic solid phase                recovered during step (c) of separation, and at                least one component comprising the soluble                molecules, obtained during step (d) of treatment                with supercritical CO2, the aragonite colloidal                emulsion and/or the calcitic colloidal emulsion,                obtained during step (g) of sonication, or the                insoluble molecules recovered during steps (h) of                cold acid hydrolysis and (i) of washing and                super-centrifugation; and
   (2) a composition comprising the soluble                molecules obtained during step (d) of treatment                with supercritical CO2, and the aragonite colloidal                emulsion and/or the calcitic colloidal emulsion                obtained during step (g) sonication.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The bivalve marine                  mollusk comprises Pinctada Maxima, Pinctada                  Margaritifera, Pinctada Martensi, Pinctada                  Fucata, Tridacnae Gigas, Tridacnae Maxima,                  Tridacnae Hippopus Hippopus, Tridacnae Derasa,                  Tridacnae Tevaroa, Tridacnae Crocea, Tridacnae                  Squamosa and/or Tridacnae Porcelanus. The liquid                  used in step (b) of hot percolation is an aqueous                  solution, preferably an aqueous solution                  comprising methanol and/or an aqueous solution                  comprising a urea solution. Preferred Method: The                  step (b) of hot percolation is carried out by wet                  sieving with a liquid whose temperature is above                  30 degrees C. The separation step (c) is carried                  out by centrifugation and the recovered liquid                  phase is called the supernatant and the recovered                  solid phase is called the pellet. The method                  comprises, after step (c) of separation carried                  out by centrifugation: (e) filtration of the                  aragonite supernatant and/or the calcite                  supernatant to obtain a filtered aragonite                  supernatant and/or a filtered calcite                  supernatant; (f) concentration of the filtered                  aragonite supernatant and/or the filtered calcite                  supernatant to obtain an aragonite concentrate                  and/or a calcite concentrate; and (g) sonication                  of the aragonite concentrate and/or of the                  calcite concentrate to obtain an aragonite                  colloidal emulsion and/or a calcite colloidal                  emulsion. The method further comprises, after                  step (d) of treatment with supercritical CO2: (h)                  cold acid hydrolysis of the aragonite powder                  treated with supercritical CO2 and/or of the                  calcitic powder treated with supercritical CO2 to                  extract the insoluble molecules present in the                  powders; and (i) washing and super-centrifugation                  to isolate and recover the insoluble                  molecules.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological; Ophthalmological.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN111457538-A
TI Heating and ventilating device useful for heat supply building, comprises air inlet pipe, air exhaust pipe, heat exchanger, filter screen, atomizing nozzle, main shaft, air inlet fan, air exhaust fan, wind blade, support, and drive motor.
AU XU Y
AE XU Y (XUYY-Individual)
GA 202072645P
AB    NOVELTY - A heating and ventilating device comprises an air inlet pipe, an air exhaust pipe, a heat exchanger, a filter screen, an atomizing nozzle, a main shaft, an air inlet fan, an air exhaust fan, a wind blade, a support, and a drive motor. The air inlet pipe and the air exhaust pipe are penetrated through a wall. The heat exchanger is installed in the middle of the air inlet pipe and the air exhaust pipe. The filter screen is fixedly connected to one end of the air inlet pipe away from the room. The atomizing nozzle is rotatably connected, and located directly above the filter screen. The air inlet pipe and the air exhaust pipe pass through one end away from the room, and are coaxially connected with the main shaft. The two ends of the main shaft are extended to the air inlet pipe and the air exhaust pipe. The air inlet fan and the air exhaust fan are coaxially and fixedly connected. The wind blade is coaxially connected in the middle of the main shaft.
   USE - The heating and ventilating device is useful for heat supply building.
   ADVANTAGE - The heating and ventilating device controls the working state through the control system, controls the indoor temperature, humidity and air freshness, creates good heating environment for users to use, satisfies the user's need for indoor heating control, temperature adjustment, and ventilation rate adjustment, and improves the user's ability to control the heating and ventilation device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A heating and ventilating device comprises an air inlet pipe, an air exhaust pipe, a heat exchanger, a filter screen, an atomizing nozzle, a main shaft, an air inlet fan, an air exhaust fan, a wind blade, a support, and a drive motor. The air inlet pipe and the air exhaust pipe are penetrated through a wall. The heat exchanger is installed in the middle of the air inlet pipe and the air exhaust pipe. The filter screen is fixedly connected to one end of the air inlet pipe away from the room. The atomizing nozzle is rotatably connected, and located directly above the filter screen. The air inlet pipe and the air exhaust pipe pass through one end away from the room, and are coaxially connected with the main shaft. The two ends of the main shaft are extended to the air inlet pipe and the air exhaust pipe. The air inlet fan and the air exhaust fan are coaxially and fixedly connected. The wind blade is coaxially connected in the middle of the main shaft. The support is installed on the outer side of the air inlet pipe and the air exhaust pipe away from the indoor end. One end of the support away from the air inlet pipe and the air exhaust pipe is fixedly connected to the wall. The drive motor is fixedly connected to the support. The output end of the drive motor is connected with the main shaft. The support is provided with a rotation speed detection device and a deceleration device. The air exhaust pipe is extended to the outside of one end of the room with a temperature detection device, a carbon dioxide detection device and a humidity detection device. The atomizing nozzle, drive motor, the rotation speed detection device, the deceleration device, the temperature detection device, the carbon dioxide detection device and the humidity detection device are signally connected to a control system. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of using the heating and ventilating device for heat supply building.
DC Q74 (Heating, ranges, ventilating (F24)); X27 (Domestic Electric Appliances\n)
