PN US2020179868-A1
TI Production of biomethane used for pipelines, involves compressing biogas feed in compressor, passing compressed feed stream to separation stage, sending gas mixture stream into separation stage, recycling stream and withdrawing gas mixture.
AU DING Y
   MITARITEN M J
AE AIR LIQUIDE ADVANCED TECHNOLOGIES US LLC (AIRL-C)
GA 202051561W
AB    NOVELTY - Production of biomethane involves compressing a biogas feed in a compressor to produce a compressed feed stream, passing the compressed feed stream to a separation stage (10) comprising polymeric gas separation membrane to retain a gas mixture (M1) comprising at least 60% of methane, and to permeate a low-quality gas mixture comprising impurities present in the biogas feed stream and 20% or less methane, sending a stream of the gas mixture (M1) into a separation stage (30) comprising polymeric gas separation membrane, to retain a gas mixture (M2) containing at least 94% methane, and to permeate a permeate gas mixture (P1) comprising impurities present in the biogas feed stream and 70% or less methane, recycling a stream of the permeate gas mixture (P2) to the compressor, and withdrawing the gas mixture (M2) from the separation stage (30) as biomethane. The method excludes the use of regenerable adsorbent.
   USE - Production of biomethane used for pipelines.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables inexpensive and environmentally-friendly production of biomethane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of biomethane involves compressing a biogas feed in a compressor to produce a compressed feed stream, passing the compressed feed stream to a separation stage (10) comprising polymeric gas separation membrane to retain a gas mixture (M1) comprising at least 60% of methane, and to permeate a low-quality gas mixture comprising impurities present in the biogas feed stream and 20% or less methane, sending a stream of the gas mixture (M1) into a separation stage (30) comprising polymeric gas separation membrane, to retain a gas mixture (M2) containing at least 94% methane, and to permeate a permeate gas mixture (P2) comprising impurities present in the biogas feed stream and 70% or less methane, recycling a stream of the permeate gas mixture (P2) to the compressor, and withdrawing the gas mixture (M2) from the separation stage (30) as biomethane. The membrane of the separation stage (10) is different said membrane of the separation stage (30). The method excludes the use of regenerable adsorbent. The biomethane comprises at least 94% methane, 3% or less carbon dioxide, 4 ppm or less hydrogen sulfide, 100 ppm or less volatile organic compound, and 0.01 wt.% or less water. The biogas feed comprises 40-75% methane, 20-55% carbon dioxide, up to 5000 ppm hydrogen sulfide, preset amount of water, and up to 2000 ppm volatile organic compounds. The membrane of the separation stage (10) is selective for hydrogen sulfide and volatile organic compounds over methane. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a biomethane producing system, which comprises a source of a biogas feed, a compressor fluidly communicating with the source and being adapted and configured to receive and compress the biogas feed, a separation stage (10) in fluid communication with the compressed feed, a separation stage (30) in fluid communication with the separation stage (10), and an inlet for recycling a permeate from the separation stage (30) back to the compressor inlet, thereby commingling the biogas feed with the stage (30) permeate stream as a mixed gas feed to the compressor so that the compressor compresses and discharges the compressed feed to the separation stage (10), where no regenerable adsorbent beds are in fluid communication between the compressor and the membrane stage (30).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating production of biomethane.
   Main compressor (1)
   Membrane stage (10,30)
   Retenate output stream (15)
   Output stream (50)
   Compressed feed (75)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further involves passing the compressed feed stream to a water removal apparatus to remove water, after compressing a biogas feed and prior to introducing the feed into the separation stage (10). The passing of the compressed feed stream to the separation stage (10) further involves removing preset amount of hydrogen sulfide from the gas mixture (M1) using a hydrogen sulfide scavenger media to produce a low-hydrogen sulfide output stream having 4 ppm or less hydrogen sulfide from the scavenger media, and sending the low-hydrogen sulfide output stream into the separation stage (30) polymeric membrane while sending a stream of the gas mixture (M1) into a separation stage (30). The process of sending a stream of the gas mixture (M1) into the separation stage (30) further involves sending the gas mixture (M2) to a hydrogen sulfide scavenger media, to produce a low-hydrogen sulfide product stream having less than 4 ppm hydrogen sulfide from said media. The permeate of the stage (30) is compressed in a compressor (C2), and the compressed gas is fed to the stage (10). Preferred Apparatus: The system further comprises a water removal apparatus adapted and configured to remove water from the compressed biogas feed prior to feeding the feed into the separation stage (10) of membrane. The system further comprises a hydrogen sulfide scavenger media in fluid communication between the separation stages (10) and (30) that is adapted and configured to remove hydrogen sulfide from a retentate from the separation stage (10) before the retentate is sent to the separation stage (30). The system further comprises a hydrogen sulfide scavenger media located on a high-pressure retentate side of the separation stage (30), the media being in fluid communication with the separation stage (30) and being adapted and configured to remove at least some hydrogen sulfide from a retentate from the separation stage (30) to produce biomethane. Preferred Components: The separation stage (10) comprises porous polymeric substrate chosen from one or more of polyimides, polysulfones, polyether ether ketones, preferably polyether ether ketones. The separation layer is made of a copolymer or block polymer of formula (I). The separation layer is made of repeating units of the monomers of formula (II) or (III). The separation layer is made of a copolymer or block polymer of tetramethylene oxide, propylene oxide, and/or ethylene oxide. The membranes of the separation stage (30) are made of cellulose acetate, a polysulfone and/or a polyimide.PA = 6-12C aliphatic polyamide;PE = poly(ethylene oxide) or poly(tetramethylene oxide);andm,n,x,y,z = not defined.N.b: m, n, x, y, z are not defined. Preferred Composition: The biogas feed comprises 40-75% methane, and impurities of up to 10% nitrogen, up to 1% oxygen, 20-55% carbon dioxide, up to 5000 ppm hydrogen sulfide, siloxanes, up to 2000 ppm of volatile organic compounds and water. The compressed feed stream comprises a water content of 0.5 wt.% or less. The permeate gas mixture (P2) comprises 30-70% methane, at least 30% carbon dioxide, 1000 ppm or less hydrogen sulfide, 100 ppm or less volatile organic compound, and 0.05 wt.% or less water. Preferred Properties: The membrane of the separation stage (10) has a selectivity of at least 10 for hydrogen sulfide over methane. The separation stage (30) has a selectivity of at least 20 for carbon dioxide over methane. The selectivity of hydrogen sulfide over methane of the polymeric gas separation membranes of the separation stage (10) is higher than that of the polymeric gas separation membranes of the separation stage (30). Preferred Conditions: The stream of the gas mixture (M1) has a pressure drop of 50 psi or less from the feed gas.

PN CN110974915-A
TI Preparing oral liquid for e.g. preventing and treating gout, by preparing alkali raw rice alkaloids, adding water to Coix seed, root of kudzu vine, Sophora japonica, tuckahoe and dodder, decocting, filtering, mixing, canning and sterilizing.
AU SUN Y
   JU W
   TANG X
   WANG S
   BI N
   QIU M
   WANG P
   PENG L
AE HOMEY GROUP CO LTD (HOME-Non-standard)
GA 202031487E
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing oral liquid, involves (i) preparing alkali raw rice alkaloids (ii) adding 300-500 ml water to 10-12 pts. wt. Coix seed, 10-15 pts. wt. root of kudzu vine, 8-12 pts. wt. Sophora japonica, 12-15 pts. wt. tuckahoe, 8-10 pts. wt. dodder and 10-20 pts. wt. Dunaliella salina powder, decocting for 30-60 minutes, filtering, and taking the filtrate to a constant volume of 150-200 ml, leaving still, (iii) adding 0.1-0.3 pt. wt. alkali raw rice alkaloid obtained in step (i) to the constant volume filtrate obtained in step (ii), mixing well to obtain soup, and (iv) canning and sterilizing the soup medicine obtained in the step (iii) and sealing the can to obtain the product.
   USE - The oral liquid is useful for preventing and treating gout, achieving guiding purine synthesis and metabolism, lowering uric acid, and repairing liver and kidney functions.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problems of low purity of the existing extracted alkaloid and low curative effect, improves the extraction rate of the alkali raw rice alkaloid, and product purity, effectively removes small molecular plant proteins and pigments, and has excellent therapeutic effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing oral liquid, involves (i) preparing alkali raw rice alkaloids by (a) selecting dried alkali raw rice, pulverizing with ultrafine grinder, adding deionized water for wetting, placing the wet alkali raw rice material in steam blasting tank for steam blasting wall treatment for 5-8 minutes, and steam blasting the wall, adding ethanol solution to the processed raw rice material, uniformly mixing and leaving still, (b) adding mixed material obtained in the step (a) to 2-5% mixed strain of the mixed material, fermenting for 15-24 hours at 50-60 degrees C with pH of 3-6, performing ultrasonic treatment, after stopping the fermentation, centrifuging and taking the fermentation broth, (c) adjusting the pH value of the fermentation broth obtained in step (b) with sodium hydroxide solution to 7-13, centrifuging, taking the centrifuged liquid, and spray drying to obtain crude alkali raw rice alkaloid, (d) adding the crude alkali raw rice alkaloid obtained in step (c) to the hydrochloric acid solution, stirring, and fully reacting so that the final pH value of the mixed solution is less than 4, centrifuging, freeze-drying the centrifuged liquid, and placing in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device for extraction, collecting the residue after extraction and dissolving in deionized water, adjusting the pH to 7-8.9 with sodium hydroxide solution, filtering the membrane, and freeze-drying the filtrate to obtain alkaline raw rice alkaloid, and (e) placing the alkali raw rice alkaloid obtained in the step (d) in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, and using ethanol solution as a carrier for extraction treatment, collecting the extracted material, and freeze-drying, (ii) adding 300-500 ml water to 10-12 pts. wt. Coix seed, 10-15 pts. wt. root of kudzu vine, 8-12 pts. wt. Sophora japonica, 12-15 pts. wt. tuckahoe, 8-10 pts. wt. dodder and 10-20 pts. wt. Dunaliella salina powder, decocting for 30-60 minutes, filtering, and taking the filtrate to a constant volume of 150-200 ml, leaving still, (iii) adding 0.1-0.3 pt. wt. alkali raw rice alkaloid obtained in step (e) to the constant volume filtrate obtained in step (ii), mixing well to obtain soup, and (iv) canning and sterilizing the soup medicine obtained in the step (iii) and sealing the can to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: In step (a), the weight ratio of the ultrafinely crushed alkali raw rice to deionized water is 1:(0.2-0.5). In step (i), the mass percentage concentration of the ethanol solution is 20-35%. The step (i) involves mixing alkali raw rice material after steam blasting wall treatment and ethanol solution in a mass ratio of 1:(3-5). In step (d), the concentration of the hydrochloric acid solution is 0.1-0.15 mol/l, and sodium hydroxide solution is 0.01-0.03 mol/l, and the pore size of the filter membrane is 0.2-0.4 microns. In step (e), the concentration of the ethanol solution is 60-75%. Preferred Conditions: In step (a), the steam explosion pressure is carried out at 1-1.8 MPa for 5-10 seconds. In step (b), the frequency of ultrasonic treatment is 18-20 KHz, ultrasonic power 0.2-1 W/cm2, and ultrasonic application period 2-5 min/hours. In step (c), the concentration of the sodium hydroxide solution is 1-3 mol/l, the inlet temperature of spray drying process is 200-220 degrees C, the nozzle speed is 20-30 HZ, the flow rate is 3-5 ml/second, the outlet temperature is 85-90 degrees C, and the wind speed is 1-2 m/second. In step (d) and step (e), the pressure of extraction processing conditions of the supercritical carbon dioxide is 18-25 MPa, temperature is 25-35 degrees C, carbon dioxide flow rate is 20-25 kg/hour, and time is 10-15 minutes. Preferred Components: In step (b), the compound strain comprises Aspergillus, yeast, and Acetobacter in a mass ratio of 1:1:(3-5).
   ACTIVITY - Antigout; Metabolic; Hepatotropic; Nephrotropic.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN CN110761070-A
TI Graphene-coated wire coil preparation device comprises reaction kettle is provided with atomic fountain, field effect separator, atomic disperser, wire feeder, wire, reorganizer, fermion stubborn device and quality inspection device.
AU ZHANG W
AE ZHANG W (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 2020137412
AB    NOVELTY - Graphene-coated wire coil preparation device comprises reaction kettle is provided with atomic fountain, field effect separator, atomic disperser, wire feeder, wire, reorganizer, fermion stubborn device and quality inspection device. The wire tank and finished wire plating tank are also provided outside reaction kettle and atomic fountain is set in reaction kettle. The field effect separator is disposed below atomic fountain and atomic disperser is disposed below field effect separator. The wire conveyer is arranged in position corresponding to wire warehouse in kettle. The wire is disposed between wire feeder and recombiner. The recombiner is arranged below atomic disperser and beside wire feeder. The fermion reinforcer is arranged beside recombiner. The quality inspection device is arranged at position corresponding to finished product plating silo beside fermion tenacity. The wire warehouse is arranged outside reaction kettle.
   USE - Used as graphene-coated wire coil preparation device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: consumes less power of 48 degrees .
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Graphene-coated wire coil preparation device comprises reaction kettle is provided with atomic fountain, field effect separator, atomic disperser, wire feeder, wire, reorganizer, fermion stubborn device and quality inspection device. The wire tank and finished wire plating tank are also provided outside reaction kettle and atomic fountain is set in reaction kettle. The field effect separator is disposed below atomic fountain and atomic disperser is disposed below field effect separator. The wire conveyer is arranged in position corresponding to wire warehouse in kettle. The wire is disposed between wire feeder and recombiner. The recombiner is arranged below atomic disperser and beside wire feeder. The fermion reinforcer is arranged beside recombiner. The quality inspection device is arranged at position corresponding to finished product plating silo beside fermion tenacity. The wire warehouse is arranged outside reaction kettle. The finished plating wire silo is disposed outside reaction kettle. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing graphene-coated wire coil, comprising (i) adding a lid into the reactor and sealing it; (ii) sending nitrogen into the reaction kettle to replace the air in the kettle; (iii) sending carbon dioxide into the reaction kettle to replace the nitrogen in the kettle; (iv) sending inert protective gas into the reaction kettle to replace the carbon dioxide in the kettle; (v) removing impurities in the reaction kettle by inert gas circulation, so that the reaction kettle becomes an ultra-clean kettle; (vi) sending oxygen into the reaction kettle; (vii) sending acetylene into the atomic fountain; (viii) losing hydrogen atoms from acetylene molecule in atomic fountain, where atomic fountain releases a mixed gas of carbon elements and impurities; (ix) separating and purifying the carbon element from the mixed gas by field effect separator; (x) preventing the carbon atoms of the purified carbon elements from agglomerating within a specific time in atomic disperser; (xi) sending the wire into the reaction kettle by wire feeder; (xii) guiding the carbon element by reorganizer to wrap the wire material, wrapping wire material into a graphene film by recombined carbon element and growing graphene film by the carbon element package to obtain graphene plated wire;(xiii) adding fermion reinforcer works, and the tensile strength of the graphene plated wire; (xiv) inspecting the quality of graphene plated wire by quality inspection device; and (xv) winding up the graphene plating wire outside the reaction kettle by finished plating wire silo.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows the schematic representation of graphene-coated wire coil preparation device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The protective gases in the reaction kettle comprises at least one of helium gas, neon gas, argon gas, krypton gas, xenon gas, nitrogen gas or carbon dioxide, and is adjusted according to different carbon sources.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The atomic fountain, the field effect separator, the atomic disperser, and the wire material are composed of one group to one hundred groups according to different quantities. The finished wire plating silo is provided outside the reaction kettle at a position corresponding to the quality inspection device, and the finished wire plating silo is at the same pressure and gas as the reaction kettle. The wire warehouse is provided outside the reaction kettle at a position corresponding to the wire feeder, and the wire warehouse and the reactor are under the same pressure and air. The bottom of the reaction kettle is further provided with the electrical control box, an oxygen regulating assembly, an acetylene regulating assembly, an inert gas regulating assembly, a temperature and humidity regulator assembly, and a waste recycling assembly. Preferred Method: The Celsius temperature in the reaction kettle is between -100 degrees C and + 305 degrees C, humidity in the reaction kettle ranges from 1-99%, pressure in the reaction kettle is between 0.1-1 MPa, and the oxygen content in the reaction kettle is between 1-50%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Components: The plated wire materials used in the reaction kettle comprises natural biological silk, artificial organic silk or artificial inorganic silk, and is adjusted accordingly according to the needs of different products.

TI Activated T-lymphocyte culture solution useful for preparing activated T-lymphocyte, comprises ALyS505N-0 medium, interleukin-2, interleukin-6 and autologous serum.
AU YANG H
AE LANLIAN HANGZHOU BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (LANL-Non-standard)
GA 201976013A
AB    NOVELTY - Activated T-lymphocyte culture solution comprises 5-7 ml ALyS505N-0 medium, 70-90 mu l interleukin-2 (5x 105 IU/ml), 15-25 mu l interleukin-6 (5x 105 IU/ml) and 3-5 ml autologous serum.
   USE - The activated T-lymphocyte culture solution is useful for preparing activated T-lymphocyte (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high efficiency and high purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for used method of activated T-lymphocyte culture solution for preparing activated T-lymphocyte, comprising (i) treating plasma, collecting white blood cells, and transferring 1-10 ml white blood cells into 50 ml cell test tube; (ii) preparing T75 cell culture flask and culturing white blood cells for 2 hours in peripheral blood mononuclear cell; (iii) preparing T225 cell culture flask as activated T-lymphocyte culture bottle, adding 10-15 ml lymacin-T (5 mu g/ml) into activated T-lymphocyte culture bottle, closing lid, and gently shaking culture bottle until liquid covers entire bottom, and allowing to stand at room temperature for 2 hours and after 2 hour culture of peripheral blood mononuclear cell, ensuring cells adhere to bottom of bottle with microscope, gently shaking T75 flask back and forth to suspend non-adherent cells and transferring cell suspension into 50 ml tube, washing culture bottle by re-suspending cells in 50 ml tube by pipette, and transferring 2-10 ml cell suspension to each culture bottle in average manner; (iv) preparing culture solution in 50 ml test tube and adding culture solution into culture bottle until 40 ml, fastening lid of culture bottle, gently shaking and adding into culture bottle into incubator for 7 days at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide; (v) harvesting activated T-lymphocyte cells by (a) preparing 50 ml tube model and culture solution-A by adding 43-46 ml ALyS505N-0 medium, 48-52 mu l ionomycin (2 mu g/ml) and 48-52 mu l phorbol 12-myristate 13-acetate (200 ng/ml) into test tube at room temperature; (b) preparing 250 ml test tube model, observing all activated T-lymphocyte culture bottle by microscope, to ensure endotoxin test result is negative and pouring all culture solution into 250 ml test tube; (c) transferring 25 ml culture solution-A into empty culture bottle and lowering culture flask; (d) centrifuging 250 ml tube and discarding upper layer of liquid; (e) transferring 25 ml culture solution-A into 250 ml test tube, re-suspending cells, transferring 2-10 ml cell suspension into culture bottle to re-precipitate culture solution; and (f) adding culture bottle into culture machine for 2.8-3.2 hours at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide and after end of the culture, transferring liquid many times to suspend cells using pipette, transferring cell suspension into 250ml test tube and adding 8-12 ml ALyS505N-0 into flask and suspending cells with pipette, transferring cell suspension into 250 ml tube, observing adherent cells at bottom of tube with naked eye and microscope, centrifuging 250 ml tube, discarding supernatant, re-suspending cells with 2-10 ml ALyS505N-0, and if multiple tubes are used, transferring all cell suspensions to one tubes and adding 50 ml ALyS505N-0, measuring cell number using trypan Blue stain and calculating survival rate, centrifuging 250 ml tube, aspirating supernatant liquid, and adding 50 ml ALyS505N-0 to suspend cells, where centrifugal tube, sample for fluorescence activated cell sorting detection and cell liquid are chilled for temporary storage on ice, performing sterility test, temporarily storing cells on ice, adding frozen liquid into tube, mixing with pipette and freezing and storing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The activated T-lymphocyte culture solution comprises 6 ml ALyS505N-0 medium, 80 mu l interleukin-2 (5x 105 IU/ml), 20 mu l interleukin-6 (5x 105 IU/ml) and 4 ml autologous serum. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), preparing T75 cell culture flask by adding 10 ml ALyS505N-0 culture solution at room temperature to each culture flask, adding 10 ml ALyS505N-0 into cell tube with 10 ml pipette, re-suspending cells, adding 1 ml streptomycin double mixed solution, and 50 mu l glutamine (200mml), transferring 5 ml cell suspension to each T75 cell culture flask, attaching cap of culture flask, shakingg bottle slowly to make cell suspension uniform and adding into culture box for 2 hours, 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide. In the step (iii), 5 mu g/ml Lymacin-T is prepared by adding 50 mu l Lymacin-T (1mg/ml) into 10 ml hanks buffer. In the step (iv), culture bottle is specifically by: (1) absorbing and discarding solution in flask; (2) adding 25 ml physiological saline to each flask and shaking back and forth; (3) adding physiological saline into sterile container and discarding; (4) repeating steps (2) and (3) twice; and (5) absorbing remaining solution in culture bottle and at mouth of bottle. In the step (v), culture solution A is prepared by adding 45 ml ALyS505N-0 medium, 50 mu l 2 mu g/ml ionomycin and 50 mu l 200 phorbol 12-myristate 13-acetate (ng/ml) into test tube at room temperature.

PN CN109851475-A
TI Online wax removal by methanol synthesis comprises e.g. using equipment in methanol synthesis system, heating synthesis gas from compressor to certain temperature via the heat exchanger, and entering methanol synthesis tower.
AU SHAO S
   CHEN H
   ZHU G
   DAI X
   DAI S
   TANG L
   YE X
   CHEN J
AE JIANGSU SOPO GROUP CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGSU SUOPU ENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201953340N
AB    NOVELTY - Online wax removal by methanol synthesis comprises using equipment in the methanol synthesis system; where the equipment used in methanol synthesis system includes compressor, heat exchanger, methanol synthesis tower, steam drum, desalinated preheater, methanol water cooler, methanol separator and flash tank, heating the synthesis gas from the compressor to certain temperature via the heat exchanger, and entering the methanol synthesis tower; where in the methanol synthesis column the gas flows through the reaction tube from top to bottom, separately reacting the carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide with hydrogen under the action of catalyst to obtain methanol, transferring the heat of reaction to the boiling water of the shell side, producing the medium pressure steam by the steam drum, discharging the methanol synthesis tower, reducing the temperature of the synthesis gas in the heat exchanger after heat exchange with methanol synthesis tower gas, and cooling.
   USE - The method is useful for online wax removal by methanol synthesis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has short system parking time; and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Online wax removal by methanol synthesis comprises using equipment in the methanol synthesis system; where the equipment used in methanol synthesis system includes compressor, heat exchanger, methanol synthesis tower, steam drum, desalinated preheater, methanol water cooler, methanol separator and flash tank, heating the synthesis gas from the compressor to certain temperature via the heat exchanger, and entering the methanol synthesis tower; where in the methanol synthesis column the gas flows through the reaction tube from top to bottom, separately reacting the carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide with hydrogen under the action of catalyst to obtain methanol, transferring the heat of reaction to the boiling water of the shell side, producing the medium pressure steam by the steam drum, discharging the methanol synthesis tower, reducing the temperature of the synthesis gas in the heat exchanger after heat exchange with methanol synthesis tower gas, cooling to 40 degrees C by demineralized water preheater and methanol water cooler, carrying gas-liquid separation by methanol separator, degassing the liquid phase crude methanol from the flash tank for further gas-liquid separation; where the method for extracting wax on-line by methanol synthesis comprises (a) stopping the methanol synthesis system, opening an injector of the methanol synthesis tower, maintaining the temperature at 180-260 degrees C, keeping the liquid level of the methanol synthesis tower and the steam drum, passing the low-pressure nitrogen gas through the synthetic compression inlet to replace the aective gas in the system, stopping the syngas compressor, closing the inlet and outlet shut-off valves, maintainig the pressure of the methanol synthesis system at pressure of 30-50 kPa, emptying the methanol in the methanol separator, opening the methanol separator outlet short, connecting the temporary valve and the pipeline to discharge wax; (b) the methanol synthesis system turning on the medium pressure nitrogen blind plate, slowly opening the medium pressure nitrogen valve while maintaining the pressure of the methanol synthesis system; (c) venting the water of the desalinated preheater and the methanol water cooler; (d) turning on the blind plate of 2.5 MPa steam before the desalinated preheater, slowly opening the steam into the system, and warming the desalinated preheater to the methanol separator pipe; and (e) opening the temporary pipeline valve to discharge wax, judging whether the wax removal of the methanol synthesis system is finished by the discharge amount, stopping the steam after closing, and closing the nitrogen valve to reset the blind plate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (a), replacing the active gas in the system by low-pressure nitrogen gas, and where qualified standard is that the active gas in the system is less than 0.5 vol.%. In the step (b), the methanol synthesis system maintains a pressure of 30-50 KPa. In step four, heating the desalinated preheater to the methanol separator line to 80-120 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The methanol synthesis column (c) includes first methanol synthesis column and second methanol synthesis column, and the first methanol synthesis column and the second methanol synthesis column are connected with each other.

PN CN109650431-A
TI Preparing nano-calcium carbonate for modified            silane adhesive comprises e.g. mixing raw lime with            water, reacting, digesting, removing impurity to obtain            calcium hydroxide concentrate, heat preserving, aging,            and adjusting concentration.
AU DU N
   SUN Y
AE LUSHI GUOSONGLAI NANO TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (LUSH-Non-standard)
GA 201939107Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano-calcium carbonate for modified                silane adhesive comprises (i) mixing raw lime with                activity of more than 350 ml with water, reacting,                digesting, and removing impurity to obtain the                calcium hydroxide concentrate, (ii) heat                preserving, and aging calcium hydroxide slurry,                (iii) adjusting the concentration of aged calcium                hydroxide slurry, adding crystal form controlling                agent of 0.5-0.8 wt.% to nano-calcium carbonate dry                weight, and cooling, (iv) passing calcium hydroxide                concentrate to the carbonization kettle, carrying                out first carbonized reaction by purifying,                cooling, compressing lime kiln gas, stopping                carbonization is when the conductivity of the                reaction system is reduced to 2 ms/cm, (v) adding                the slurry obtained in the carbonization reaction                in the step (iv) to the post-treatment agent of                nano-calcium carbonate dry basis weight of 1.5-2%,                continuously stirring, introducing steam at same                time, heating, purifying kiln gas, and compressing                for secondary carbonization.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                nano-calcium carbonate for modified silane adhesive                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The adhesive: has excellent thixotropy,                extrudability, storage stability and surface                finishing after gluing, high tensile strength, high                elongation, excellent bonding performance, simple                and feasible process, low cost, low price, and                excellent economic benefit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nano-calcium carbonate for modified                silane adhesive comprises (i) mixing raw lime with                activity of more than 350 ml with water in a ratio                of 1:6, reacting, digesting, and removing the                impurity to obtain the calcium hydroxide                concentrate, (ii) heat preserving, and aging the                calcium hydroxide slurry, (iii) adjusting the                concentration of aged calcium hydroxide slurry to                1.2-1.5 mol/l, adding crystal form controlling                agent of 0.5-0.8 wt.% to nano-calcium carbonate dry                weight, and cooling to 28-33 degrees C, (iv)                passing calcium hydroxide concentrate of the step                (iii) to the carbonization kettle, carrying out                first carbonized reaction by purifying, cooling,                compressing lime kiln gas, where the kiln gas flow                rate is 1.2-1.5 m3/minute.m3, the volume content of                carbon dioxide in the kiln gas is 30-35%, and                stopping the carbonization is when the conductivity                of the reaction system is reduced to 2 ms/cm, (v)                adding the slurry obtained in the carbonization                reaction in the step (iv) to the post-treatment                agent of nano-calcium carbonate dry basis weight of                1.5-2%, continuously stirring, introducing steam at                the same time, heating to 90-95 degrees C, and                aging for 2-3 hours, purifying the kiln gas, and                compressing for secondary carbonization, the kiln                gas flow rate is 0.6-0.75 m3/minute.m3, the volume                of carbon dioxide in kiln gas is 30-35%, when the                pH of the reaction system is 6.5-7, stopping the                ventilation to obtain the calcium carbonate slurry,                (vi) adding 3-4 wt.% modifier to nanometer calcium                carbonate dry weight in calcium carbonate slurry,                stirring for at least 30 minutes, and then                transferring to a homogenization tank for                homogenization, and (vii) sequentially subjecting                the homogenized slurry to pressure filtration                dehydration, drying, crushing, and classifying to                obtain the product, where the nano-calcium                carbonate crystal form of modified silane adhesive                is a standard cube, and crystal is monodisperse,                and has an average particle diameter of 100-150 nm                and a specific surface area of 16-18 m2/g.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), removing                  impurity comprises passing through 200 meshes,                  320 meshes vibrating screen in turn, and then                  through a three-stage suspension separator in                  series, and digesting water at 50-60 degrees C.                  In the step (ii), aging for 50-60 hours.                  Preferred Components: In the step (iii), the                  crystal form controlling agent is allyl                  polyethylene glycol, sorbitol, or glucose. In the                  step (v), the post-treating agent is ammonium                  bicarbonate, and HOMOGENOL L-18, where the weight                  ratio of ammonium bicarbonate, and HOMOGENOL L-18                  is 5-6:1, preferably the post-treating agent is                  ammonium bicarbonate, and organic aluminum                  modified silane oligomer in the weight ratio of                  4:1. In the step (vi), the modifier is soap                  particles and/or coconut oil, where the weight                  ratio of soap particles, and/or coconut oil is                  5:1, preferably soap particles and aluminum                  stearate in the weight ratio of 4:1, more                  preferably soap particles and/or zinc stearate in                  the weight ratio of 5:1.

PN CN109504657-A
TI Expanding and culturing cytotoxic T lymphocytes in vitro by adding lymphocyte separation solution to blood, centrifuging, soaking in saline, centrifuging, culturing mononuclear cells, detecting and cryopreserving cytotoxic T lymphocytes.
AU YUAN W
   ZHOU L
   WANG X
   LV W
   WAN Q
AE SOYOUNGLIFE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SOYO-Non-standard)
GA 201929614Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for expanding and culturing cytotoxic T lymphocytes in vitro involves preparing culture medium i.e. X-VIVO 15 (RTM: Serum free hematopoietic cell medium) containing interleukin-2, pretreating the cell culture flask with cluster of differentiation 3 (CD3), adding lymphocyte separation solution to the venous blood to obtain separation solution, centrifuging, absorbing the intermediate white membrane layer i.e. a single nuclear cell layer, transferring into a new centrifuge tube, soaking the single nuclear cells in physiological saline, centrifuging, inoculating isolated mononuclear cells in the culture medium, culturing, subculturing the cells, collecting cell susupension, centrifuging, counting the, detecting the proportion of CD3+, CD3+-CD4+, CD3+-CD8+ and CD4+-CD25+ cells using flow cytometry and cryopreserving the cytotoxic T lymphocytes in liquid nitrogen.
   USE - The method is useful for expanding and culturing cytotoxic T lymphocytes in vitro.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses easily available materials, has no damage to the body and enables large amount of highly active cytotoxic T cells yield using small amount of peripheral blood, hence is suitable for large-scale culture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for expanding and culturing cytotoxic T lymphocytes in vitro involves (a) preparing culture medium, such that 1 ml X-VIVO 15 (RTM: Serum free hematopoietic cell medium) contains 1000 IU interleukin-2 (IL-2), pretreating the T cell culture flask having air filter with 50 g/ml cluster of differentiation 3 (CD3) and leaving at 4 degrees C overnight, (b) collecting 100 ml venous blood having viability of greater than or equal to 90%, adding equal amount of lymphocyte separation solution to the venous blood to obtain separation solution, gently operating to avoid breaking the boundary liquid surface, centrifuging using a centrifuge at 1800 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 15 minutes, absorbing the intermediate white membrane layer i.e. a single nuclear cell layer, and transferring into a new centrifuge tube, (c) soaking the single nuclear cells in physiological saline, centrifuging at 1800 rpm for 10 minutes and thoroughly rinsing 3 times, (d) inoculating 1-2x 106/ml isolated mononuclear cells in the culture medium in a coated T225cm2 cell culture flask, culturing at 37 degrees C under saturation humidity of 5 plus minus 0.5% carbon dioxide in an incubator, (e) adding second volume of culture medium into the T225cm2 cell culture flask on the 4th day and continuing culturing in the incubator, (f) observing the color of the medium on the 6th day, counting the number of cells, adding 150 ml culture medium and continuing culturing, (g) subculturing when the cells in the culture flask at 1x 106/ml on the 7th day, if the number is too low, the culture flask should be returned to the incubator, disinfecting the surface of the culture flask, placing in a biosafety cabinet, gently pipetting the cells were with a pipette, mixing, dividing into 5 T225 cm2 cell culture flasks, each of which was supplemented with culture medium preheated at 37 degrees C, uniformly mixing and culturing in a carbon dioxide incubator, (h) adding 200 ml 37 degrees C culture medium into the culture flasks on the 13th day and continuing culturing, (i) collecting cell susupension on the 20th day, centrifuging at room temperature and 1500 rpm for 10 minutes, (j) counting the cells and diluting to 200 times, where the number of harvestable cells is 3-4x 109 and (k) detecting the proportion of CD3+, CD3+-CD4+, CD3+-CD8+ and CD4+-CD25+ cells using flow cytometry and cryopreserving the cytotoxic T lymphocytes in liquid nitrogen using serum-free cryopreservation solution.

PN CN109321797-A
TI Cemented carbide material for a special tool for a beveling machine, comprises a hard alloy base and a coating.
AU XU Y
AE CHENGXI SHIPYARD CO LTD (CHSB-C)
GA 201917251K
AB    NOVELTY - Cemented carbide material comprises a hard alloy base and a coating. The cemented carbide substrate comprises 80-85% hard phase, 5-10% binder phase, 3-5% inhibition phase and 2-5% hardening phase, the hard phase comprises 65 to 71% tungsten carbide, 22 to 28% titanium carbide, and 3 to 7% tungsten boride, the bonding phase comprises 62-71% cobalt, 15-21% hydroxy nickel powder, 10-13% manganese, 3-5% hydroxy iron powder and 1-2% tungsten powder, the inhibition phase comprises 80-87% vinylene carbonate, 5-8% titanium dioxide, 3-6% niobium carbide, 3-6% niobium carbide, 1-3% chromium carbide and 0.5-2% vanadium carbide, the hardening phase comprises 51-60% zirconium carbide, 25-42% green silicon carbide, 4-9% yttrium oxide and 3-7% zirconia and the coating is a titanium nitride-titanium carbonitride-alumina complex coating.
   USE - The material is useful for a special tool for a beveling machine (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for preparing the cemented carbide material, comprising (i) taking the hard phase, the bonding phase, the suppression phase and the hardening phase material, wet-milling the raw materials by a roller ball mill, where the wet grinding medium is deionized water, the grinding ball is an alumina ball and the particle size of the alumina ball is 15-20 mm; drying the wet-milled slurry in a vacuum drying oven, where the drying temperature is 70-80 degrees C, the degree of vacuum is -0.15 to -0.08 MPa, and the drying is 5-10 hours; sintering the dried powder in a vacuum furnace at a sintering temperature of 1180-1250 degrees C under a vacuum of -0.08 to -0.01 Mpa and sintering for 4-8 hours; and placing the sintered formed substrate in a hydrogen atmosphere and carburizing at 1020-1140 degrees C for 30-50 minutes to obtain a hard alloy substrate and (ii) pre-vacuating the CVD coating reactor to less than 80 mbar, heating to a coating deposition temperature of 1000 degrees C; preheating a mixed gas of nitrogen, titanium tetrachloride and hydrogen to 700 degrees C into the reactor to obtain a titanium nitride coating, the volume ratio of the nitrogen, titanium tetrachloride and hydrogen is 1:2:4; pre-heating the acetonitrile and titanium tetrachloride reaction mixed gas to 800 degrees C into the reactor to obtain a titanium carbonitride coating, the aluminum chloride, where the volume ratio of titanium tetrachloride to acetonitrile is 1:10; preheating to 300 degrees C through a mixture of aluminum chloride, carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide into the reactor, preparing an alumina coating having a volume ratio of aluminum chloride, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide of 2:1:3:1; cooling to 100 degrees C under a hydrogen atmosphere after the coating is finished; where the coating structure of the cemented carbide substrate is a titanium nitride-titanium carbonitride-alumina complex coating; and #the special tool for a beveling machine, comprising a cutter body, a blade and a blade locking screw. The cutter body is provided with two blade connecting ends, and one blade connecting end is disposed at one end of the tool body and at an angle of 30 to 45 degree with the tool body. The other blade connection end is disposed in the middle of the tool body and is disposed perpendicular to the tool body. The blade connecting end is welded to the tool body or integrally formed with the tool body. The blade body and the blade are connected by a locking screw. The blade is made of the cemented carbide material.

The blade is a rectangular blade and the rectangular apex angle is rounded. The weight of the tool is 250 g.

PN CN108997538-A
TI Flame retardant halloysite aerogel material has nano-scale and micro-scale pores in the halloysite aerogel material, where halloysite nanotubes are added to a mixture of deionized water and ethanol, and halloysite dispersion is obtained.
AU LI H
   LI R
   LIU H
   YANG A
   LIAO X
AE UNIV TIANJIN CHENGJIAN (UTCJ-C)
GA 2018A3573B
AB    NOVELTY - Flame retardant halloysite aerogel material has average specific surface area of 601-606 m2g-61. The nano-scale and micro-scale pores exist in the halloysite aerogel material. The nano-scale pores average is 10-11 nm, and the micro-scale pores average is 20-21 mu m. 0.8-18 pts. wt. halloysite nanotubes are added to a mixture of 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol, and the ultrasonic dispersion is uniform to obtaining a halloysite dispersion. 0.8-18 pts. wt. sodium styrene sulfonate, 0.07-1.8 pts. wt. polydivinylsiloxane, 0.01-0.45 pts. wt. initiator, 0.05-9 pts. wt. hexabromocyclododecane is added to a mixture of 50 pts. wt. deionized water and 50 pts. wt. ethanol. After stirring, the solution is added to the halloysite dispersion, the ultrasonic dispersion is uniform, and the vacuum is maintained after vacuuming, returned to normal pressure, repeating vacuuming three times, and washed to obtain the product.
   USE - Flame retardant halloysite aerogel material.
   ADVANTAGE - The flame retardant halloysite aerogel material has flame retardant effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The polydivinylsiloxane has a number average molecular weight of 500-5000, preferably 1000-3000, and a vinyl content mole percentage (i.e., the ratio of the number of vinyl moles to the number of moles of the monoamino-terminated polydimethyldivinylsiloxane) a 0.2-4.6% amino mono-terminated polydimethyldivinylsiloxane or an amino-terminated polydimethyldivinylsiloxane. The product is dispersed in 100 pts. wt. water, and the temperature is raised to 52-78 degrees C in a water bath to initiate polymerization, and the polymerization time is 48 h to obtain the product. After washing the product, dispersing in 100 pts. wt. water, adding 0.07-18 pts. wt. bisphenol bis(diphenyl phosphate) and 0.01-1.6 pts. wt. initiator, and stirring uniformly. The temperature of the water bath is raised to 64-98 degrees C to initiate polymerization, and the polymerization time is 36 h to prepare a flame retardant halloysite hydrogel. The flame retardant halloysite hydrogel is placed in a carbon dioxide supercritical high pressure extraction device, and the supercritical drying is carried out at a temperature of 5-350 degrees C and a pressure of 0.5-30 MPa for 1 h with carbon dioxide as a medium to obtain a flame retardant halloysite aerogel material. The inner side of the wall of the halloysite nanotube has a positive charge, and the outside of the tube wall has a negative charge. The sodium styrene sulfonate has a negative charge, and the sodium styrene sulfonate is adsorbed on the halloysite nanometer by electrostatic action on the inner wall of the tube. The polydivinylsiloxane, initiator and hexabromocyclododecane are also dispersed into the hollow structure of the halloysite nanotubes. The hollow structure of the halloysite nanotube provides a microporous structure for the flame retardant halloysite aerogel material. After vacuuming and washing, the polydivinylsiloxane is copolymerized with sodium styrene sulfonate forming a crosslinked structure inside the halloysite nanotubes, and loading the hexabromocyclododecane in the halloysite nanotubes. The polymerization of the vinyl functional groups is performed on the polydivinylsiloxane located outside the hollow structure of the halloysite nanotubes under the action of an initiator to form a three-dimensional network structure between the halloysite nanotubes and the polydivinylsiloxane. The three-dimensional network structure provides a mesoporous structure for the flame retardant halloysite aerogel material while loading bisphenol bis(diphenyl phosphate) in the mesoporous structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The halloysite nanotubes are 1.5-9 pts. wt. is added to a mixture of deionized water and ethanol for ultrasonic dispersion for 1 h, and sodium styrene sulfonate is 1.5, polydivinylsiloxane is 0.15-14 pts. wt., the initiator is 0.01-0.1 pts. wt., and the hexabromocyclododecane is 0.2-4.6 pts. wt. are added. After adding to a mixture of deionized water and ethanol, stirring for 12-56 minutes, adding to the halloysite dispersion, ultrasonic dispersion for 30 min, and vacuuming the mixture for 1 h. The initiator is dibenzoyl peroxide (BPO) or azobisisobutyronitrile (ABIN). 0.15-18 pts. wt. bisphenol bis(diphenyl phosphate) and 0.01-1 pts. wt. initiator are added to the above dispersion for 1 hour. During the polymerization, the polymerization is carried out at 64-98 degrees C for 20-50 min, then the temperature is lowered to 32-68 degrees C for 20-30 h, and then at 80 degrees C, 90 degrees C and 100 degrees C respectively. Preferred Condition: The polymerization is carried out at 52-78 degrees C for 30-60 min, then the temperature is lowered to 12-56 degrees C for 12-24 h, followed by 80 degrees C, 90 degrees C and 100 degrees C is separately polymerized for 2.5-7.5 h. The time for supercritical drying is 2-4 h, preferably 3 h.

Testing.)

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The end plate anti-deformation tire is having an inverted U shape matched with the inner wall of the end plate. The inner surface is provided with a plurality of criss-crossed tire reinforcement ribs. The bottom plate anti-deformation tire includes a first flat plate and a second flat plate. The bottom surfaces of the first flat plate and the second flat plate are uniformly provided with a multiple of supporting legs, respectively. The support leg is composed of an I-beam. The top surface of the I-beam is welded to the flat plate. The opposite side of the first plate and the second plate is provided with an articulated shaft and an L-shaped arm, respectively. The two ends of the hinge shaft are respectively fixed to the first plate through a connecting block. The L-shaped arm is disposed on the second plate uniformly. The one side of the L-shaped arm is fixed to the second plate. The other side is provided a strip hole that cooperates with the hinge shaft. The hinge shaft is disposed in the strip hole. The opposite sides of the first plate and the second plate are uniformly provided with a plurality of sets of locking components. The locking assembly includes a first ear plate, a second ear plate, a screw and a nut. The first ear plate is fixed to the first plate. The second ear plate is fixed to the second plate. The first lug plate and the second lug plate are hook-shaped. The lifting fixture comprises a top beam, the two end of the bottom of the top beam is fixed with two lifting hydraulic cylinders. The output end of the lifting hydraulic cylinder is downward and is fixedly connected with the lifting beam. The thread through hole is as center to be distributed symmetrically with connecting ring. The screw thread through hole in matched against the bolt. In the process of making the shell, using the mechanical correction when the material is less deformed, using the flame heating correction when the deformation is large and forbiddening to use hammering or quenching directly.

TI Method of treating produced water containing            suspended solids, oil, barium, iron, magnesium, ammonia            and benzene, involves subjecting third effluent to            benzene polishing process and reducing benzene            concentration of third effluent.
AU BIAGINI B
   BLUMENSCHEIN C
   DALE C
   KRONEBUSCH H
   BLUMENSCHEIN C D
AE VEOLIA WATER SOLUTIONS & TECHNOLOGIES SU (VEOL-C)
   BIAGINI B (BIAG-Individual)
   BLUMENSCHEIN C (BLUM-Individual)
   DALE C (DALE-Individual)
   KRONEBUSCH H (KRON-Individual)
   VEOLIA WATER SOLUTIONS & TECHNOLOGIES SU (VEOL-C)
GA 201856509Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves directing the produced                water into a grit clarifier (30) and removing some                of the suspended solids and oil from the produced                water. The portion of the settled solids is                recycled from a barite clarifier (36) to a barite                reaction tank (34). The produced water is directed                downstream from the barite clarifier to a solids                contact reactor. The precipitated iron and                magnesium in the solids contact reactor are                settled. The settled iron and magnesium                precipitates are removed from the produced water.                The ammonia and benzene concentration in the first                effluent is reduced by biologically nitrifying and                denitrifying the first effluent which produces a                second effluent. The second effluent is subjected                to a solids-liquid separation process and suspended                solids are removed from the second effluent. The                third effluent is subjected to benzene polishing                process and the benzene concentration of the third                effluent is reduced.
   USE - Method of treating produced water containing                suspended solids, oil, barium, iron, magnesium,                ammonia and benzene.
   ADVANTAGE - By strategically precipitating the barium                sulfate and the NORM upstream of the hardness                removal process, the amount of sludge produced by                the process that contains NORM is minimized. The                concentration of residual benzene is reduced in an                advanced oxidation process that employs hydrogen                peroxide and ultraviolet light to oxidize benzene                to carbon dioxide and water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The sulfate source is mixed with the produced                water in the barite reaction tank and solids are                precipitated. The produced water is directed from                the barite reaction tank to a barite clarifier. The                de-aerator produces a vent stream that contains                benzene. The concentration of ammonia and benzene                in the first effluent is reduced by biologically                nitrifying and denitrifying the first effluent in a                moving bed bioreactor (MBBR). The third effluent is                subjected to a benzene polishing process which                comprises emitting ultraviolet (UV) light into the                third effluent. The hydrogen peroxide is injected                into the third effluent and the hydrogen peroxide                is reacted with the UV light to yield hydroxy                radicals, which in turn react with benzene to                oxidize the benzene to carbon dioxide and water.                The produced water also includes strontium. The pH                of the produced water is increased in the solids                contact clarifier by mixing a pH adjusting reagent                with the produced water in the solids contact                clarifier and increasing the pH of the produced                water in the solids contact clarifier to                approximately 10.8. The first effluent also                includes benzene and biologically nitrifying and                denitrifying the first effluent reduces the                concentration of the benzene in the first effluent                and produces a second effluent. The second effluent                is subjected to a solids-liquid separation process                and removing suspended solids from the second                effluent and in the process producing a third                effluent. The third effluent is subjected to a                benzene polishing process and further reducing the                benzene concentration of the third effluent. The                produced water further includes naturally occurring                radioactive material and the method minimizes the                production of sludge containing the naturally                occurring radioactive material by adsorbing the                naturally occurring radioactive material onto the                precipitated barium sulfate and removing the                precipitated barium sulfate with the adsorbed                naturally occurring radioactive material upstream                of the process where magnesium hydroxide is removed                from the produced water.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining                the process for treating produced water from a                hydrofracking operation.
   Grit clarifier (30)
   Larger equalization tank (32)
   Barite reaction tank (34)
   Barite clarifier (36)
   Barite clarifier pump tank (38)

PN WO2018109620-A1; AU2017374873-A1; IN201947027420-A; KR2019092519-A; EP3555300-A1; CN110462051-A; US2020010861-A1; BR112019011818-A2; JP2020511965-W; RU2019120991-A; RU2760291-C2; JP7127860-B2
TI Method for producing one polyhydroxyalkanoate            through gas fermentation, involves cultivating            gas-fermenting microorganisms with obtained gas-liquid            mixture to form cell mass containing one            polyhydroxyalkanoate.
AU YU J
   MUNASINGHE P
   YU Y
AE UNIV HAWAII (UYHW-C)
   UNIV HAWAII (UYHW-C)
   UNIV HAWAII (UYHW-C)
   UNIV HAWAII (UYHW-C)
GA 201849509J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing one gas                fermentation vessel having an internal volume                partially filled with a liquid fermentation broth                (11) comprising water, suspended gas-fermenting                microorganisms, and nutrients for the                gas-fermenting microorganisms. The remaining                portion of the volume of gas fermentation vessel                (2) is filled with a gas phase (15). An aliquot of                the liquid fermentation broth is continuously                withdrawn from the gas fermentation vessel. A                gaseous substrate (14) comprises carbon dioxide,                hydrogen and oxygen. The gaseous substrate is mixed                with liquid fermentation broth withdrawn from the                gas fermentation vessel by contacting the liquid                fermentation broth in the form of sprayed droplets                with the gaseous substrate in the gas phase. The                gas-fermenting microorganisms are cultivated with                the obtained gas-liquid mixture to form a cell mass                containing one polyhydroxyalkanoate. The one                polyhydroxyalkanoate is recovered from the cell                mass.
   USE - Method for producing one polyhydroxyalkanoate                through gas fermentation.
   ADVANTAGE - The fine droplets of the liquid-gas mixture                increase the gas mass transfer rate significantly.                The optical density of the fermentation broth is                increased exponentially at a specific growth rate.                The bioreactor saves gas and reduces energy                consumption. The spray bioreactor has a simple                structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method involves supplying a gaseous                substrate comprising carbon dioxide and oxygen and                mixing the gaseous substrate with the liquid                fermentation broth withdrawn from gas fermentation                vessel. The obtained gas-liquid mixture is sprayed                into gas fermentation vessel and gas-fermenting                microorganisms are cultivated to form a cell mass                containing one polyhydroxyalkanoate. The liquid                fermentation broth is withdrawn and reintroduced                into the gas fermentation vessel at a circulation                rate equal to or higher than 2 l/min, preferably                equal to or higher than 3 l/min. The gaseous                substrates is supplied into the gas fermentation                vessel with a gassing rate equal to or lowers than                0.001 m/s, preferably equal to or lower than 0.0005                m/s. The gaseous substrate is a flue gas. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a gas                fermentation bioreactor comprising a vessel having                an internal volume partly filled with a liquid                fermentation broth comprising gas-fermenting                microorganisms and a fermentable medium. The                remaining portion of the volume of the vessel is                filled with a gas phase. One effluent circulation                conduit (19) communicates with the interior of the                vessel through which an aliquot of the liquid                fermentation broth is withdrawn. The conduit is                connected to a circulation line (6) for circulating                aliquot of the liquid fermentation broth externally                with respect to the vessel and then re-introducing                it into the gas phase present in the vessel. One                spraying nozzle (12) is connected to the effluent                circulation line for spraying the liquid                fermentation broth into the gas phase. A feeding                system (30) is configured for feeding into the gas                phase a gaseous substrate comprising one or more                gases to cultivate the gas-fermenting                microorganisms. The feeding system is connected to                the gas phase through an inlet conduit                communicating with the interior of the vessel. The                circulation line comprises one static mixer (7) for                mixing the liquid fermentation broth withdrawn from                the vessel and the gaseous substrate to form the                gas-liquid mixture.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                spray nozzle bioreactor with two static mixers and                side-line gas introduction.
   Gas fermentation vessel (2)
   Circulation line (6)
   Static mixer (7)
   Liquid fermentation broth (11)
   Spraying nozzle (12)
   Gaseous substrate (14)
   Gas phase (15)
   Effluent circulation conduit (19)
   Feeding system (30)

PN CN107844682-A; WO2019100683-A1; CN107844682-B
TI Measuring soft components of converter gas based            on gas calorific value and smoke composition comprises            taking low calorific value of the dry gas of converter            gas and composition of the gas in combustion            furnace.
AU WANG H
   YE Y
   AN X
   MA L
   WANG Y
AE JIANGSU MARITIME INST (JIAG-C)
   JIANGSU MARITIME VOCATIONAL INST (JIAG-C)
GA 201825175E
AB    NOVELTY - Measuring soft components of converter gas                based on gas calorific value and smoke composition                comprises taking low calorific value of the dry gas                of converter gas and composition of the gas in                combustion furnace, calculating theoretical dry air                volume required for the combustion of a unit volume                of dry gas and theoretical dry flue gas volume                produced by the unit volume of dry gas combustion                according to the low level of the dry gas of the                converter gas and calorific value and calculating                the theoretical dry air volume per unit volume of                dry gas combustion and theoretical dry gas                production.
   USE - The method is useful for measuring soft                components of converter gas based on gas calorific                value and smoke composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the inconvenience and                difficulty that most steel plants are not provided                with on-line analyzer of converter gas components                to the combustion furnace, also be used for the                thermal efficiency test of the burner as long as                the gas calorific value is measured by the gas                calorimeter and the flue gas composition, which is                measured by the flue gas analyzer, identifies the                gas composition and used for calculation and                analysis of the performance of the combustion                furnace, where the gas sampling and analysis work                required by the traditional method is used for the                rapid measurement of the thermal efficiency of the                combustion furnace.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Measuring soft components of converter gas                based on gas calorific value and smoke composition                comprises taking the low calorific value of the dry                gas of the converter gas and the composition of the                gas in the combustion furnace through measurement,                calculating the theoretical dry air volume required                for the combustion of a unit volume of dry gas and                the theoretical dry flue gas volume produced by the                unit volume of dry gas combustion according to the                low level of the dry gas of the converter gas and                calorific value, where the theoretical dry flue gas                volume calculates the fuel characteristic factor                according to the theoretical dry air volume and dry                gas combustion per unit volume of dry gas                combustion required, calculating the excess air                coefficient according to the fuel characteristic                factor and the flue gas composition of the burner,                calculating the theoretical dry air volume per unit                volume of dry gas combustion and theoretical dry                gas production per unit volume of dry gas, amount                of flue gas and the excess air coefficient,                calculating the actual dry flue gas volume produced                by the unit volume of dry gas combustion,                calculating the volume content of carbon monoxide                in dry gas based on the actual dry flue gas volume                and combustion furnace flue gas composition                produced by the unit volume of dry gas combustion                percentage based on the lower calorific value of                dry gas in the converter and the percentage of                carbon monoxide in the dry gas in the volume                fraction of hydrogen in the gas, based on the                volume fraction of carbon monoxide in dry gas,                volume content of hydrogen in dry gas, and the                theoretical dry air volume per unit volume of dry                gas combustion, calculating the volume fraction of                oxygen in dry gas, calculating the volume fraction                of carbon dioxide in dry gas based on the                percentage of the actual dry flue gas produced by                the unit volume of dry gas combustion, the flue gas                content of the furnace, and the volume content of                carbon monoxide in the dry gas according to the                carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen, oxygen                in the dry gas percentage of volumetric content and                calculating volume fraction of nitrogen in dry                gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - COMPUTING AND CONTROL - Preferred Components: The burner flue gas                  composition includes the volume content                  percentage of oxygen, carbon monoxide and carbon                  dioxide in the dry flue gas. Preferred Method:                  The first calculation formula calculates the                  theoretical dry air volume required for the                  combustion of a unit volume of dry gas, where the                  first calculation formula is as specified, which                  is the theoretical dry air volume required for                  the combustion of dry gas per unit volume in                  m3/m3 (dry gas), Qd net is the low calorific                  value of the dry gas of the converter in kJ/m3,                  calculating the theoretical dry flue gas volume                  per unit volume of dry gas combustion through the                  second calculation formula, where the second                  calculation formula is as specified, which is the                  theoretical dry flue gas produced by gas                  combustion in m3/m3 (dry gas), Qd net is the low                  calorific value of the converter gas in kJ/m3.                  The fuel characteristic factor X is calculated by                  the third calculation formula, where the third                  calculation formula is as specified, in which X                  is the fuel characteristic factor, the                  theoretical dry flue gas volume per unit volume                  of dry gas combustion is m3/m3 (dry gas) and the                  theoretical dry air volume required for unit                  volume of dry gas combustion is m3/m3 (dry gas).                  The fourth calculation formula is used to                  calculate the excess air coefficient, where the                  fourth calculation formula is as specified, where                  alpha is the excess air coefficient, psi                  '(oxygen) and psi '(carbon monoxide) are the                  volume content percentages of oxygen and carbon                  monoxide in the dry flue gas in %. The actual dry                  smoke produced by the combustion of a unit volume                  of dry gas is calculated by the fifth calculation                  formula, where the fifth calculation formula is                  as specified, where Vgy is the actual dry smoke                  produced by the combustion of a unit volume of                  dry gas in m3/m3 (dry gas). The theoretical dry                  flue gas produced by the combustion of a unit                  volume of dry gas in m3/m3 (dry gas). The                  theoretical dry air required for the combustion                  of a unit volume of dry gas in m3/m3 (dry gas)                  and alpha is the excess air factor. The                  volumetric content percentage of carbon monoxide                  in dry gas is calculated by the sixth formula,                  where the sixth formula is as specified, where                  psi (carbon monoxide)=2.532 Vgy (21- psi                  '(oxygen))-52.367, where psi (carbon monoxide) is                  dry gas medium carbon monoxide content in                  percentage %, Vgy is the actual dry flue gas                  volume produced by unit volume of dry gas                  combustion in m3/m3 (dry gas) and the psi                  '(oxygen) is the volume content of oxygen in dry                  flue gas in %. The seventh formula is used to                  calculate the volume content percentage of                  hydrogen in the dry gas. Where the formula is as                  specified, where psi (hydrogen) is the percentage                  volume content of hydrogen in dry gas in %; Qd                  net is the low calorific value of converter gas                  base in kJ/m3 and psi (carbon monoxide) is the                  volume content of carbon monoxide in dry gas in                  %. The eighth formula is used to calculate the                  volume content percentage of oxygen in dry gas,                  where psi (oxygen) is the percentage volume                  content of oxygen in dry gas in %; psi (carbon                  monoxide) is the percentage content of carbon                  monoxide in dry gas in %; psi (hydrogen) is the                  percentage of volume content of hydrogen in dry                  gas in %. The theoretical dry air volume required                  for the combustion of dry gas per unit volume is                  m3/m3 (dry gas). The ninth formula is used to                  calculate the volume content percentage of carbon                  dioxide in dry gas, where the formula is as                  specified, where psi (carbon dioxide)=Vgy( psi '(                  carbon dioxide)+ psi '( carbon monoxide))- psi                  (carbon monoxide) where psi (carbon dioxide) and                  psi (carbon monoxide) are the percentages of the                  volume content of carbon dioxide and carbon                  monoxide in the dry gas, respectively in %. The                  Vgy is the actual dry flue gas volume produced by                  the combustion of a unit volume of dry gas. The                  psi ' (carbon dioxide) and psi '(carbon monoxide)                  are the percentages of the volume content of                  carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide in the dry                  flue gas respectively. The tenth formula is used                  to calculate the volume content percentage of                  nitrogen in dry gas, where the formula is as                  specified, where psi (nitrogen)=100- psi (carbon                  monoxide)- psi (carbon dioxide)- psi (H2)- psi                  (oxygen), where psi (nitrogen), psi (carbon                  monoxide), psi (carbon dioxide), psi (hydrogen)                  and psi (oxygen) are the percentage of volume                  content of nitrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon                  dioxide, hydrogen, and oxygen in dry gas                  respectively.
