PT P
PN WO2018051340-A1
TI Making population of matrix dependent mesenchymal stromal cells essentially free of cells of e.g. endothelial origin, used e.g. to treat alopecia, comprises contacting tissue fragments and solution of fibrinogen and removing excess solution.
AU ADANI B
   BASHEER M
   GORODETSKY R
   LAZMI-HAILU A
   PERETZ-YABLONSKI T
AE HADASIT MEDICAL RES SERVICES & DEV LTD (HSIT-C)
GA 201823377V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing (M1) a population of matrix dependent mesenchymal stromal cells essentially free of cells of hematopoietic and endothelial origin, comprises: e.g. (a) obtaining a tissue comprising matrix dependent mesenchymal stromal cells; (b) optionally briefly contacting fragments of the tissue with a solution comprising a protease; (c) optionally keeping the fragments for greater than or equal to 48 hours in motion while suspended in growth medium at 37 degrees C and an atmosphere with 5% carbon dioxide; and (d) contacting fragments of tissue with a solution comprising fibrinogen, and evacuating excess fibrinogen solution.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a population of matrix dependent mesenchymal stromal cells that are useful: in promoting a regenerative process in damaged tissue or organ for treating or mitigating acute radiation syndrome following exposure to high dose ionizing radiation, or failing bone marrow, where the damaged tissue is bone marrow, and the damaged organ comprises hair follicles or damaged pancreas; for reducing an inflammatory response associated with disease or injury, where the disease or injury is an autoimmune disease (including multiple sclerosis, Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis and type 1 diabetes mellitus), inflammatory disease, allergy, ischemic brain injury, critical limb ischemia, myocardial infarction, immune-mediated neurodegenerative disorder, metabolic disorder (i.e. diabetes mellitus type 2), graft-versus-host disease or transplanted organ rejection; for treating or mitigating alopecia caused by exposure to high dose ionizing radiation; and in regeneration of damaged pancreas or damaged hair follicles, where the damaged hair follicles are damaged following exposure to high dose radiation (all claimed). Test details are described but no results given.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides the population of matrix dependent mesenchymal stromal cells, which are essentially free of cells of hematopoietic and endothelial origin (claimed), and exhibit improved uniformity and yield; and enables a quicker expansion of their population.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing (M1) a population of matrix dependent mesenchymal stromal cells essentially free of cells of hematopoietic and endothelial origin, comprises: (a) obtaining a tissue comprising matrix dependent mesenchymal stromal cells; (b) optionally briefly contacting fragments of the tissue with a solution comprising a protease; (c) optionally keeping the fragments for greater than or equal to 48 hours in motion while suspended in growth medium at 37 degrees C and an atmosphere with 5% carbon dioxide; (d) contacting the fragments of the tissue with a solution comprising fibrinogen, and evacuating excess fibrinogen solution; (e) transferring the fragments to an unmodified plastic surface; (f) dispersing a solution comprising thrombin on the plastic surface and fragments, thus forming a thin fibrin film around the fragments that immobilizes the fragments to the plastic surface; (g) covering the fragments with growth medium; and (h) harvesting matrix dependent mesenchymal stromal cells that have migrated out from the fragments, thus preparing a population of matrix dependent mesenchymal stromal cells essentially free of cells of hematopoietic and endothelial origin. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a population of matrix dependent mesenchymal stromal cells essentially free of cells of hematopoietic and endothelial origin obtained by the method (M1);
   (2) a composition for cryo-preservation of biological tissue, comprising 50-90% fetal calf serum (FCS) or 5-50% human plasma albumin (HAS) in addition to dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO) and optionally polyethylene glycol (PEG) or cell culture medium; and
   (3) preserving (M2) biological tissue including normal tissue or tumor tissue, comprising immersing the biological tissue in the composition, and slowly reducing the temperature to -75 degrees C to -80 degrees c, thus providing frozen biological tissue and storing the frozen biological tissue in liquid nitrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the method (M1), the fragments of tissue are made from fragments or bulk tissue previously immersed in a cryo-preservation solution, frozen by slowly reducing the temperature to -75 degrees C to -80 degrees C, stored in liquid nitrogen, thawed and treated with a protease of step (b) comprising trypsin (preferred) and/or collagenase. The collagenase is used at 0.2-3 mg/ml. The method for isolating placental stromal cells from placenta, comprises: (a1) obtaining a tissue from the fetal side of the surface decidua of the chorion of placenta; (b1) briefly contacting fragments of the tissue with a solution comprising 1% trypsin, and optionally rinsing the fragments, cutting the fragments into finer fragments and contacting the finer fragments with a solution comprising trypsin following rinsing and washing; (c1) optionally keeping the fragments for greater than or equal to 48 hours in suspension in rotation while suspended in growth medium at 37 degrees C and an atmosphere with 5% carbon dioxide; (d1) briefly contacting fragments of the tissue of step (b1) or (c1) with a solution comprising 0.5-3%, preferably 1% fibrinogen, and removing excess fibrinogen leaving only a film on the fragments; (e1) transferring the fragments to an uncoated plastic surface, and removing excess liquid with fibrinogen; (f1) spraying a solution comprising at least 10 U/ml, preferably 100 U/ml thrombin on the plastic surface and fragments, thus forming a thin fibrin film around the fragments that immobilizes the fragments to the plastic surface; (g1) covering the fragments with growth medium; and (h1) harvesting matrix dependent mesenchymal stromal cells that have migrated out from the fragments, thus preparing a population of placental matrix dependent mesenchymal stromal cells essentially free of cells of hematopoietic and endothelial origin. Preferred Composition: The cryo-preservation solution comprises 50-90% FCS or 5-50% HAS in addition to DMSO and optionally PEG or cell culture medium. The cryo-preservation solution or composition comprises: 90% FCS, 5% DMSO and 5% PEG; 90% FCS and 10% DMSO; 10-20% HAS, 10% DMSO and 70-80% cell culture medium; or 10-20% HAS, 5% DMSO, 5% PEG and 70-80% cell culture medium. Preferred Components: The tissue is: placenta (preferred), breast, liver, kidney, tumors, uterus, bone, cartilage, spleen or skin; and obtained from the fetal side of the surface decidua of the chorion. The matrix dependent mesenchymal stromal cells are stromal cells, preferably placental stromal cells, where more than 95% of the cells are of fetal origin and the population is essentially free of cells of maternal origin. The stromal cells are human cells expressing cluster of differentiation (CD)-105, CD44, CD29 and CD90. The hematopoietic and endothelial origin are human cells expressing significant levels of CD45, CD19, CD11b, human leukocyte antigen-antigen D related, and CD34. The cells of hematopoietic and endothelial origin make up less than 1% of the population of matrix dependent mesenchymal stromal cells. The solution comprising fibrinogen comprises 0.5-3%, preferably 1% fibrinogen. The solution comprising thrombin comprises at least 10 U/ml, preferably 100 U/ml thrombin. The solution comprises 1% trypsin.
   ACTIVITY - Vulnerary; Antiemetic; Cytostatic; Immunostimulant; Osteopathic; Antiinflammatory; Endocrine-Gen.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - Stem cell therapy.
   ADMINISTRATION - Administration of the population of matrix dependent mesenchymal stromal cells is by subdermal, intraperitoneal, intracerebroventricular, intrathecal, intravenous or intramuscular injection (claimed). No dosage details given.
   EXAMPLE - Placenta was obtained in sterile conditions immediately following caesarian section of healthy male child by a healthy mother. The placenta was then rinsed extensively and small fractions from the surface decidua of the chorion in the fetal side were cut into small pieces which were then further minced to smaller tissue fragments. The fragments were briefly digested twice with trypsin immersed in medium with highly diluted 1% fibrinogen, then distributed on a plastic dish and sprayed with 100 U/ml thrombin to adhere them to the plastic surface of culture dish. The pieces were then cultured with low glucose Dulbeco modified eagle medium with 10% fetal calf serum, 1% antibiotics (penicillin streptomycin, optionally with neomycin) and 1% glutamine. The placental matrix dependent stromal cells from the selected tissue fragments was spontaneously migrated to the plastic surface to reach a uniform culture of pure stromal cells (cluster of differentiatin (CD)-91 positive, CD29 positive, CD105 positive partially positive and CD11b, CD34 and CD45 negative within 2 passages within 1-2 weeks. The cells were then expanded for 5-8 passages before their injection. The cells were harvested for implantation and counted, rinsed and re-suspended in PlasmaLyte A (sterile, nonpyrogenic isotonic solution).

TI Production of alkali-metal            hydroxide/alkaline-earth metal used for e.g. producing            vegetable oil and fat, involves subjecting liquid            comprising alkali-metal compound and/or alkaline-earth            metal compound and water to electrochemical reaction,            and neutralizing.
AU HIROMORI K
   KITAKAWA N
   KATAGAMI K
AE UNIV TOHOKU (TOHO-C)
GA 2022B58727
AB    NOVELTY - Production of alkali-metal                hydroxide/alkaline-earth metal involves subjecting                a liquid comprising alkali-metal compound (I)                and/or alkaline-earth metal compound (II) and water                to an electrochemical reaction to produce Kolbe                electrolysis at the anode to generate at least                hydrocarbon compound and carbon dioxide and                alkali-metal-ion (MB+) and/or alkaline-earth                metal-ion (MC2+), and neutralizing the MB+ion                and/or MC2+ion by hydroxide-ion produced by                electrolysis of water at the cathode.
   USE - Production of alkali-metal                hydroxide/alkaline-earth metal used for producing                vegetable oil and fat, paper and fatty acid ester                (all claimed), and recycling carboxylate                waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables production of alkali-metal                hydroxide/alkaline-earth metal with high                concentration, high purity and high                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of alkali-metal                hydroxide/alkaline-earth metal involves subjecting                a liquid comprising alkali-metal compound of                formula: RA-COOMB (I) and/or alkaline-earth metal                compound of formula: (RA-COO)2MC (II) and water to                an electrochemical reaction to produce Kolbe                electrolysis at the anode to generate at least                hydrocarbon compound of formula: RA-RA and carbon                dioxide and MB+ion and/or MC2+ion, and neutralizing                the MB+ion and/or MC2+ion by hydroxide-ion produced                by electrolysis of water at the cathode.
   RA=hydrocarbyl;
   MB=alkali-metal;and
   MC=alkaline-earth metal.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   production of vegetable oil and fat, which                involves deacidifying liquid containing the                compound (I) and/or (II) and water, obtaining the                alkali-metal hydroxide and/or alkaline-earth metal                hydroxide using a liquid containing the soapstock                produced in the process, and using the alkali-metal                hydroxide and/or alkaline-earth metal hydroxide for                the deoxidation step in the production of vegetable                oil;
   manufacture of paper, which involves obtaining                the alkali-metal hydroxide and/or the                alkaline-earth metal hydroxide using the liquid                comprising the compound (I) and/or (II) and water,                and a liquor containing black liquor generated in a                washing process after cooking in papermaking, and                using the alkali-metal hydroxide and/or                alkaline-earth metal hydroxide in the cooking                process in papermaking;
   production of fatty acid ester, which involves                obtaining the alkali-metal hydroxide and/or the                alkaline-earth metal hydroxide by using the liquid                comprising the compound (I) and/or (II) and water,                and a liquid containing soapy wastewater generated                in the step of washing a crude fatty acid ester                fraction in the production of a fatty acid ester,                and using the alkali-metal hydroxide and/or                alkaline-earth metal hydroxide in the production                reaction of the crude fatty acid ester in the                production of the fatty acid ester;
   a device, which consists of an anode, a                cathode, an electrolytic cell, and tank separated                by an ion-permeable diaphragm. In the                electrochemical device, alkali-metal hydroxide                (MB-OH) and/or alkaline-earth metal hydroxide                (MC-(OH)2) is generated in a reaction liquid by                subjecting a liquid containing an oil cake produced                in a deoxidation process in the production of                vegetable oil and fat to an electrochemical                reaction, and the obtained reaction liquid is put                on one side separated by the diaphragm of the tank,                water is put in the other side separated by the                diaphragm of the tank, and MB-OH and/or MC-(OH)2 in                the reaction liquid are shifted to the water;                and
   an alkali-metal/alkaline-earth metal hydroxide                recycling system, which consists of the device and                unit for supplying the alkali-metal hydroxide                and/or alkaline-earth metal hydroxide produced                using the production apparatus to a deoxidizing                step in the production of vegetable oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The production of                  alkali-metal hydroxide/alkaline-earth metal                  further involves generating slag generated in the                  deacidification in the production of vegetable                  oil, obtaining black liquor from post-cooking                  washing steps in papermaking, and generating soap                  wastewater generated in the step of washing crude                  fatty acid ester fractions in the production of                  fatty acid esters, separating anode side and the                  cathode side by a diaphragm, putting the liquid                  and water in the anode side, putting water in the                  cathode side, and performing a electrochemical                  reaction. In the electrochemical reaction, the                  electrolytic cell is separated by an                  ion-permeable diaphragm. In the electrochemical                  reaction, the anode and the cathode are placed on                  one side of the electrolytic cell separated by                  the diaphragm, and MB-OH and/or MC-(OH)2and                  introducing water into the other side of the                  electrolytic cell separated by the diaphragm to                  transfer the MB-OH and/or MC-(OH)2into the water.                  Preferred Components: The diaphragm is a cation                  exchange membrane. The sodium and/or potassium                  are included as alkali-metal.

PT P
PN CN114805980-A
TI Lignin-reinforced polyethylene water supply pipe            mixing material useful in nano antibacterial            polyethylene water supply pipeline, comprises resin,            modified lignin, powder silane coupling agent,            solubilizer, carbon black, aromatic diol chain            extender, antioxidant and lubricant.
AU HUANG S
   LIU Z
   DENG G
   LIU W
   CHEN Z
   LI Y
AE GUANGXI XIONGSU TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGXI ACAD SCI (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022A1644F
AB    NOVELTY - Lignin-reinforced polyethylene water supply                pipe mixing material comprises 100 pts. wt. resin,                2-3 pts. wt. modified lignin, 0.5 pts. wt. powder                silane coupling agent, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt.                solubilizer, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. carbon black, 0.2-1                pts. wt. aromatic diol chain extender, 0.5-1.5 pts.                wt. antioxidant and 0.05-0.2 pts. wt. lubricant.                The preparation of modified lignin comprises e.g.                (a) concentrating black liquor to obtain a                concentrated solution; (b) adding hydrogen peroxide                and lanthanum niobate into concentrated solution,                stirring and reacting to obtain reaction solution                A; (c) cooling the reaction solution A, introducing                carbon dioxide gas, adding phosphorous acid                solution and stirring to react to obtain the                reaction solution B; (d) cooling the reaction B,                adding sulfuric acid and a cationic polyacrylamide                solution to obtain flocculent precipitate; and (e)                subjecting the flocculent precipitate to pressure                filtration and drying to obtain modified                lignin.
   USE - The lignin-reinforced polyethylene water                supply pipe mixing material is useful in a nano                antibacterial polyethylene water supply pipeline                and high and low temperature resistant                double-performance hardware bathroom pipe.
   ADVANTAGE - The Lignin-reinforced polyethylene water                supply pipe mixing material has improved tensile                strength, impact strength and other comprehensive                mechanical strength.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Lignin-reinforced polyethylene water supply                pipe mixing material comprises 100 pts. wt. resin,                2-3 pts. wt. modified lignin, 0.5 pts. wt. powder                silane coupling agent, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt.                solubilizer, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. carbon black, 0.2-1                pts. wt. aromatic diol chain extender, 0.5-1.5 pts.                wt. antioxidant and 0.05-0.2 pts. wt. lubricant.                The preparation of modified lignin comprises (a)                concentrating the black liquor obtained by alkaline                pulping to obtain a concentrated solution with a                solid content of 20-35 wt.%; (b) adding hydrogen                peroxide and lanthanum niobate into the                concentrated solution obtained in step (a) in turn,                and stirring and reacting for 90 minutes at                80-85&#8451; and 300 revolutions/minute to                obtain reaction solution A; (c) cooling the                reaction solution A to 45-50&#8451;,                maintaining at a constant temperature, introducing                carbon dioxide gas into the reaction solution A                thus makes the pH value of the reaction solution A                to reach 6-6.5, adding phosphorous acid solution,                and stirring to react for 20 minutes at 300                revolutions/minute to obtain the reaction solution                B; (d) cooling the reaction B to room temperature,                adding 2 wt.% sulfuric acid into the reaction                solution B, so that the pH value of the reaction                solution B reaches 2-3, and adding a cationic                polyacrylamide solution to obtain flocculent                precipitate; and (e) subjecting the flocculent                precipitate to pressure filtration to obtain a                filter residue, and drying the filter residue to a                moisture content of less than 0.5 wt.% to obtain                modified lignin.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing lignin-reinforced polyethylene water                supply pipe mixing material, comprising (i) adding                the modified lignin into the high-speed mixer,                stirring at 500 revolutions/minute, when the                temperature reached 80-110&#8451;, adding                powdered silane coupling agent, mixing for 3                minutes, adding the compatibilizer, mixing                continuously for 5-10 minutes, heating to                140&#8451;, adding the aromatic diol chain                extender, mixing for 5 minutes, adding carbon                black, antioxidant, lubricant and resin in turn and                stirring for 10-12 minutes to obtain a mixture; and                (ii) adding the mixed material into the screw                extruder through the feed port pulling, cooling,                cutting, and sieving.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lanthanum zinc                  niobate is lamellar, and the chemical formula of                  the lanthanum zinc niobate comprises                  Zn1/2LaNb2O7, Zn1.5La1/3Nb2O7or ZnLa2/3Nb2O7, the                  hydrogen peroxide is 30 wt.% hydrogen peroxide,                  the cationic polyacrylamide solution is a 5 wt.%                  cationic polyacrylamide solution and the                  phosphorous acid solution is 10-25 wt.%                  phosphorous acid aqueous solution. The volume                  ratio of the concentrated solution to hydrogen                  peroxide is (80-800):1; the mass ratio of the                  concentrated solution to the lanthanum zinc                  niobate is (5-25) liter :1 g, the volume ratio of                  the concentrated solution to the phosphorous acid                  solution is (500-3000): 0.1 and the volume ratio                  of the concentrated solution to the cationic                  polyacrylamide solution is (1000-2500):0.1. The                  siliceous inert adsorbent is calcium silicate                  with the average pore diameter 0.8-1.2                  &#956;m, the porosity is 65-80%, and the                  specific surface area is 250-400 m2/g.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The phenolic hydroxy                  content of the modified lignin is greater than                  2.5 mmol/g, and the number average molecular                  weight is less than 11000 g/mol. The                  compatibilizer is styrene-glycidyl methacrylate                  copolymer, the aromatic diol chain extender                  comprises                  resorcinol-bis(&#946;-hydroxyethyl)ether or                  hydroquinone-bis(&#946;-hydroxyethyl)ether,                  the antioxidant is a mixture of hindered phenolic                  antioxidants and phosphite antioxidants in a mass                  ratio of 1.5:1 and the lubricant is polyethylene                  wax, preferably polyethylene wax with a number                  average molecular weight of 2000-3000.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The powder silane                  coupling agent is powdered 3-aminopropyl                  triethoxysilane and the powdered coupling agent                  is prepared by using a siliceous inert adsorbent                  as a carrier to adsorb powdered                  3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane. The median particle                  size D50 of the carbon black is less than 25 nm,                  and the specific surface area is above 500                  m2/g.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The screw diameter of                  the screw extruder is 71 mm, the screw groove                  ratio is 1.5, the screw groove depth is 14 mm,                  and the screw length-diameter ratio is 40/1, the                  screw is divided into 10 heating zones. The                  temperature of the 10 heating zones from the feed                  end is 135&#8451;, 145&#8451;,                  150&#8451;, 155&#8451;, 160&#8451;,                  165&#8451;, 170&#8451;, 160&#8451;,                  150&#8451; and 140&#8451; and the screw                  speed is 500 revolutions/minute.

PN CN114393688-A
TI Preparation of carbonized baking-free brick by dry ice includes raw material processing, mixing, dry ice mixing, compacting and carbonizing, and brick demolding.
AU XIA W
   ZHANG H
   SHAO G
   PAN Z
   LI J
   LIU S
   ZHONG Y
   CAI G
AE UNIV NANJING FORESTRY (UNJF-C)
GA 2022628323
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of carbonized baking-free brick includes pouring solid waste material into pre-processing chamber box feeding port, drying, crushing, sieving, and storing in discharging tank; coating thin layer lubricating oil in the inner part of the mold, flowing prepared granules into the mold, and moving the mold to the predetermined position in the stirring chamber; adding alkaline material into the storage tank, flowing to the mold, mixing alkaline material with granules to obtain mixed dry material, spraying the water on mixed dry material, and stirring to obtain mixture A; mixing mixture A with dry ice, moving the mold to preset position in the low temperature cabin, opening the refrigerating device, reducing temperature in the low temperature chamber, opening the dry ice granulator, performing dry ice granulation, flowing out dry ice particles into the mold, rotary stirring, and coating the surface of mixture B.
   USE - The method is for manufacturing carbonized baking-free brick.
   ADVANTAGE - The method economically and efficiently, accurately uses carbon dioxide, low carbon and uses dry ice to prepare carbonized baking-free brick, so as to solve the problem of long maintaining period, low intensity and bad durability in the existing brick making technology, easy to have secondary environment pollution and so on. The brick body has high strength and low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of carbonized baking-free brick comprises pouring solid waste material which is building solid waste, industrial solid waste or desert wind accumulated sand into pre-processing chamber box feeding port, drying at 60-105degrees Celsius for 1-3 hours, crushing to target grain diameter, sieving, and storing in discharging tank; adjusting the flow rate and outflow time of the metering valve A through controller A according to the volume of the mold, coating thin layer lubricating oil in the inner part of the mold, flowing prepared granules into the mold at volume of 50% of the volume of mold, starting the transmission and vibration mode of the transmission belt, moving the mold to the predetermined position in the stirring chamber, closing the transmission mode of transmission belt and continuously maintaining the vibration mode; adding alkaline material into the storage tank through the storage port, adjusting flow rate and outflow time of the metering valve C through the controller B, flowing out the alkaline material from the storage tank, flowing to the mold, starting the rotary stirrer A, mixing alkaline material with granules to obtain mixed dry material, adding water into the water box, flowing into the mold, and spraying the water on mixed dry material, stirring for 2-3 minutes to obtain mixture A; mixing mixture A with dry ice, moving the mold to preset position in the low temperature cabin, continuously maintaining the vibration mode, opening the refrigerating device, reducing temperature in the low temperature chamber to -30degrees Celsius to -50degrees Celsius, opening the dry ice granulator, performing dry ice granulation, flowing out dry ice particles into the mold, rotary stirring for 1-2 minutes to obtain mixture B, and coating the surface of mixture B; and moving the mold to predetermined position in the pressure chamber, pressing mixture, adjusting the temperature to 40-60degrees Celsius in the pressure chamber, adjusting pressure to 20-100 kPa and humidity to 40-60%, sublimating the dry ice particles in the mixture B into carbon dioxide gas, performing fast carbonization reaction with the alkaline material, cementing and reinforcing the target granules in the mold for 1-3 hours, moving the mold to the outside of the pressure chamber, and pushing the carbonized baking-free brick.

PN WO2022073090-A1; BR102020020534-A2
TI Production of composition of extracellular matrix            proteins by bioprinting tissue or 3D construct            constructed with some layers, homogenization of tissues            or 3D constructs in neutral phosphate-saline buffer            solution, and cell separation.
AU DE ANDREA DERNOWSEK J
AE DE ANDREA DERNOWSEK LTDA JANAINA (DAND-Non-standard)
GA 202251667W
AB    NOVELTY - Production of a composition of extracellular                matrix proteins involves: (1) preparing 2D cell                culture of cells, human or animals, in a culture                medium comprising a humidified environment at 37                degrees C containing 5% carbon dioxide (CO2) and                95% atmospheric air; (2) preparing bioink                formulation comprising 18-25 mass% ethylene oxide                (EO) and propylene oxide (PO) block copolymer,                1-10% peptides, in addition to collagen expression                inducing molecules (I, II, III and IV); (3)                bioprinting the tissue or 3D construct constructed                with some layers, in a cylindrical shape and                porosity of 20-60%; (4) homogenization of tissues                or 3D constructs in neutral phosphate-saline buffer                solution for 10-30 minutes, at 4-14 degrees C; (5)                cell separation step: (a) on a laboratory scale,                through centrifugation (1000-1500 revolution per                minute (rpm)) at 2-10 degrees C for 2-10 minutes,                and the pellet composed of cells is returned to                step (2); and (6) washing and concentration                step.
   USE - The method is useful for production of a                composition of extracellular matrix proteins                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - A scalable and sustainable bioprocess is                provided for extracellular matrix proteins of high                purity and structural characteristics using 3D                bioprinted tissues.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of a composition of extracellular                matrix proteins involves: (1) preparing 2D cell                culture of cells, human or animals, in a culture                medium comprising a humidified environment at 37                degrees C containing 5% carbon dioxide (CO2) and                95% atmospheric air; (2) preparing bioink                formulation comprising 18-25 mass% ethylene oxide                (EO) and propylene oxide (PO) block copolymer,                1-10% peptides, in addition to collagen expression                inducing molecules (I, II, III and IV); (3)                bioprinting the tissue or 3D construct constructed                with some layers, in a cylindrical shape and                porosity of 20-60%; (4) homogenization of tissues                or 3D constructs in neutral phosphate-saline buffer                solution for 10-30 minutes, at 4-14 degrees C; (5)                cell separation step: (a) on a laboratory scale,                through centrifugation (1000-1500 revolution per                minute (rpm)) at 2-10 degrees C for 2-10 minutes,                and the pellet composed of cells is returned to                step (2) and the supernatant containing proteins is                taken to an ultracentrifuge (10000-25000 G) for                15-35 minutes; or (b) on an industrial scale, step                5 involves a process of cell separation through                filtration where the filtrates containing cells are                washed 2-5 times returning to step (2); and the                protein solutions are taken to tangential                centrifugation, this process being repeated from 2                to 20 times, keeping the shear force of the feed                stream below 2000/s; and (6) washing and                concentration step: (a) on a laboratory scale, the                pellet obtained in the previous step is washed (2-4                times) and concentrated in a refrigerated                centrifuge at 4 to 10 -C with tubes containing a                specific membrane to retain high molecular weight                proteins from the extracellular matrix, until the                final structural protein concentrate having from 5                to 150 mg/ml is obtained; or (b) on an industrial                scale, step (6) involves a protein solution                obtained in the previous step undergoing washings                for its concentration, repeated 2-4 times in a                refrigerated centrifugation of 4-10 degrees C,                until the final structural protein concentrate                having 5-150 mg/ml is obtained. An INDEPENDENT                CLAIM is included for product obtained by the                method, comprising a composition of extracellular                matrix proteins obtained as a final product of the                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method further                  involves employing in step (2),                  arginine-glycine-aspartate (RGD) and fibronectin                  (0.05-5 mu g/ml). In step (5), centrifugation and                  ultracentrifugation phases are employed.                  Tangential flow filtering devices (FFT) are used                  in step (5). Collagen and other matrix proteins                  such as elastin, fibronectin and                  glycosaminoglycans are obtained at the end.                  Preferred Components: The cells of step (1) are                  chosen from sources comprising skin, cartilage,                  bone, bone marrow, and fat. The extracellular                  matrix proteins comprise collagen, elastin,                  fibronectin and glycosaminoglycans. The                  extracellular matrix protein composition                  comprises fibrillar collagen 1. Fibroblasts,                  chondroblasts, osteoblasts, myoblasts,                  cardiomyocytes, and induced adult and pluripotent                  stem cells are used in step (1). When                  chondroblasts are used, a high-purity protein                  solution of type 2 collagen, glycosaminoglycans                  and chondroitin is obtained. In step (1), culture                  medium is used, which can be essential medium 8,                  DMEM, RPMI or MEM, which can make use of cell                  growth bioreactors. Preferred Composition: The                  culture medium is added with 25 mM Hepes, 7.5 mM                  L-glutamine, 0.5 mM pyruvate, without animal                  protein such as 15-100 ng/ml recombinant human                  insulin, 5-15 ng/ml selenium, 5-15 mu g/ml                  transferrin, 20-50 ng/ml hydrocortisone, 5-100                  ng/ml fibroblast growth factor (FGF), 2-50 ng/ml                  transforming growth factor beta (TGF-B), 5-70 mu                  M/ml (ascorbic acid), 0.05-0.5% human albumin,                  10-40 ng/ml interleukin 13 and 5-20 ng/ml                  epidermal growth factor (EGF). Preferably, 1-10                  mass% Cell adhesion peptides, such as                  arginine-glycine-aspartate (RGD) and 0.05-5 mu                  g/ml fibronectin can be added to the hydrogel.                  The molecules that induce the expression of                  collagens (I, II, III and IV) comprise 10-40                  ng/ml interleukin 13, 15-100 mu g/ml (insulin),                  5-100 ng/ml fibroblast growth factor, and 5-70 mu                  g/ml ascorbic acid. The 3D constructs of step (3)                  are formed with 20-40 layers of bioink at a cell                  concentration of 1x 106-5x 106/ ml. Protein                  concentrators are used at 30-100 kDa. Preferred                  Condition: Temperature in step (4) varies at                  10-14 degrees C.

PN JP2022019473-A
TI Production of hydrofused fuel used for automobile, involves mixing fuel base oil, raw material water, and fusion additive, using soft water having preset hardness, applying ultrasonic waves-raw material water, mixing fuel base oil and raw material water, and heating and pressurizing mixed fluid.
AU SHIDA H
AE ATLAS CORE GK (ATLA-Non-standard)
GA 202250065T
AB    NOVELTY - Production of hydrofused fuel involves mixing 50 vol.% fuel base oil, 25-40 vol.% raw material water, and 10-25 vol.% fusion additive, using soft water having a hardness of 120 mg/m or less as the raw material water, agitating the raw material water by a stirring blade rotating, applying ultrasonic waves-the raw material water, subdividing the raw material water and subjecting-a treatment of promoting ionization by contacting the raw material water to which the raw material water is subdivided and ultrasonic waves are applied with a catalyst material that promotes ionization of water, reforming-a state in which ionization is progressed, applying ultrasonic waves-the fuel base oil, sending the product obtained by the heating/fusion step-a settling tank and allowed-stand-precipitate a substance unnecessary as a hydrofused fuel, taking out the portion of the product in the settling tank from which the precipitate is removed as a hydrofused fuel.
   USE - Production of hydrofused fuel used for automobile.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is efficient, enables production of hydrofused fuel having high combustion performance in a short period of time without separating into the fuel oil and water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of hydrofused fuel involves mixing 50 vol.% fuel base oil, 25-40 vol.% raw material water, and 10-25 vol.% fusion additive, using soft water having a hardness of 120 mg/m or less as the raw material water, agitating the raw material water by a stirring blade rotating at high speed in a water reforming tank-be sheared and subdivided, applying ultrasonic waves-the raw material water, subdividing the raw material water and subjecting-a treatment of promoting ionization by contacting the raw material water to which the raw material water is subdivided and ultrasonic waves are applied with a catalyst material that promotes ionization of water, reforming-a state in which ionization is progressed, applying ultrasonic waves-the fuel base oil, and stirring the fuel base oil with the stirring blade rotating at high speed in an oil reforming tank, reforming the molecule into an ionized state, reforming the raw material water-a state in which subdivision and ionization is progressed by the water reforming step, separating the fuel base oil subdivided and ionized by the fuel base oil reforming step, mixing the fuel base oil and the raw material water by putting them into a refining stirring tank with a surface-active fusion additive and stirring and mixing the raw material water, stirring the fuel base oil and the fusion additive with the stirring blade that rotates at high speed, while producing a mixed fluid with the fusion additive, by injecting an ultrafine valve of air or carbon dioxide gas into the mixed fluid in the refining stirring tank through an ultrafine bubble generator, combining the mixed fluid of raw water, fuel base oil and fusion additive with hydrogen-ion of water, purifying a state containing a large amount of hydrocarbons produced by the reaction of ionized carbon dioxide with carbon-ion, and heating and pressurizing the mixed fluid purified through the mixed fluid refining step in a heating/pressurizing tank, sending the product obtained by the heating/fusion step-a settling tank and allowed-stand-precipitate a substance unnecessary as a hydrofused fuel, taking out the portion of the product in the settling tank from which the precipitate is removed as a hydrofused fuel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The soft water has an electric conductivity of 50 ms/m or less. Preferred Composition: The soft water comprises 1 meq/l or less sodium-ion and potassium-ion (in total), 4 meq/l or less calcium(II)-ion, 4 meq/l or less magnesium(II)-ion, 1 meq/l or less chlorine-ion, 2 meq/l or less bicarbonate-ion, and 1 meq/l or less sulfate-ion and nitrate-ion. The fusion additive comprises 0-4 vol.% catalase, 55-65 vol.% oleic acid, 10-15 vol.% propylene glycol, 15-25 vol.% methyl alcohol, and 5.5-8.8 vol.% aqueous ammonia. Preferred Process: The water is ionized by using a catalyst material. The catalyst material is irradiated with ultrasonic waves to excite and promote ionization. In the fuel base oil reforming step, the fuel base oil in the oil reforming tank is ultrasonically excited to excite the catalyst material to promote ionization of the fuel base oil. The method further involves agitating the mixed fluid of the fuel base oil, the raw material water, and the fusion additive, by the stirring blade rotating in the refining stirring tank, subdividing and ionizing at the nano-level by ultrasonic waves, performing nano-level subdivision processing and ionization promotion treatment that further subdivide and ionize by passing through a mixer, heating and pressurizing the mixed fluid under temperature of 25-90 degrees C, and atmospheric pressure of 2-15. Preferred Components: The catalyst material is at least one mineral chosen from mineral group containing tourmaline, black stone, pearlite, pine algae, and granite, and at least one metal chosen from metal group containing aluminum, magnesium, manganese, sodium, zinc, copper, iron, nickel, titanium, calcium and silicon and/or a ceramic containing an ore containing silicon dioxide and alumina as main component.

AB    NOVELTY - Processing waste water and recycling salt by                disperse dye production involves: introducing                disperse dye production waste water into waste                water pre-processing device, adding sodium                carbonate or sodium hydroxide, adjusting pH value                of disperse dye production waste water to weak acid                5.5-6.5, and reacting disperse dye production waste                water in sulfuric acid to generate sodium sulfate;                and entering processed disperse dye production                waste water into ECO sub-critical water catalytic                oxidation device, pressurizing by the high pressure                pump, entering heat recycling system, heating air                and disperse dye production waste water to                120-280degrees Celsius by heater, entering reactor,                setting pressure in reactor to 5.0-9.8 MPa and                temperature to 160-300degrees Celsius, and                degrading macromolecule organic matter in disperse                dye production waste water into small molecule                organic matter, acid substance, carbon dioxide and                water under the action of catalyst.
   USE - The method is used for processing waste water                and recycling salt by disperse dye                production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has outstanding substantive                features and obvious progress, realizes maximum                resource and purpose of zero waste water discharge,                shortens process route, and reduces operation cost                and actual operation difficulty.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing waste water and recycling salt by                disperse dye production involves: introducing                disperse dye production waste water into waste                water pre-processing device, adding sodium                carbonate or sodium hydroxide, adjusting pH value                of disperse dye production waste water to weak acid                5.5-6.5, and reacting disperse dye production waste                water in sulfuric acid to generate sodium sulfate;                entering processed disperse dye production waste                water into ECO sub-critical water catalytic                oxidation device, pressurizing by the high pressure                pump, entering heat recycling system, heating air                and disperse dye production waste water to                120-280degrees Celsius by heater, entering reactor,                setting pressure in reactor to 5.0-9.8 MPa and                temperature to 160-300degrees Celsius, and                degrading macromolecule organic matter in disperse                dye production waste water into small molecule                organic matter, acid substance, carbon dioxide and                water under the action of catalyst; continuously                flowing disperse dye production waste water into                cooler, control valve and gas-liquid separator,                passing through gas-liquid separator of coarse                filtering function, discharging disperse dye                production waste water in gas from exhaust gas                absorber to obtain remaining oxidation liquid,                entering oxidizing liquid into freezing                crystallizing device through oxidizing liquid                discharging pump, freezing decahydrate sodium                sulfate, and freezing crystallizing at                -15-10degrees Celsius; and freezing oxidized liquid                into nanofiltration membrane concentrating device,                continuously flowing concentrated liquid back to                freezing crystallizing device, circulating and                concentrating oxidized liquid until total recycling                rate of sodium sulfate decahydrate is 90-99.9%,                recovering solvent in oxidized liquid by                concentrating and separating and recycling device                to recycle sodium formate salt or formic acid, and                continuously entering remaining oxidized liquid                into biochemical device to process until reaching                discharge standard. The ECO sub-critical water                catalytic oxidation device comprises air buffer                tank, air compressor, high pressure pump, heat                recycling system, reactor, cooler, control valve,                gas-liquid separator, oxidizing liquid discharging                pump, exhaust gas absorber, high pressure air                pressure reducing valve and heater. The air                compressor pressurizes air to 4-20 MPa and inputs                air buffer tank and enters heat recovery system                after passing through high pressure air pressure                reducing valve. The reactor is provided with                catalyst. The freezing crystallizing device and                nanofiltration membrane concentrating device form                membrane concentrated liquid circulating                channel.

AU YOSHIMURA M
   YOSHIMOTO N
AE PANASONIC INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY MANAGEME (MATU-C)
GA 2021E8098G
AB    NOVELTY - A hydrogen purification system (200) comprises gas supply unit for supplying hydrogen-containing gas containing carbon dioxide and ammonia; electrochemical device (10) having membrane-electrode junction, supplying hydrogen-containing gas to anode (12), and passing a current in a predetermined direction between anode and cathode (13) to purify hydrogen at the cathode; a set of bubbling devices (130, 131) in which aqueous solvent capable of dissolving hydrogen is stored, and substance caused by hydrogen dissolved in aqueous solvent; concentration sensor (170); hydrogen-containing gas supply flow path (104) for bubbling hydrogen-containing gas; hydrogen-containing gas discharge channel (109) that supplies hydrogen-containing gas separated from aqueous solvent to anode, and hydrogen-containing gas supplied to anode from anode to cathode via electrolyte film (11); and anodic off gas supply flow path (146) that discharges anodic off gas without permeation to the outside.
   USE - A hydrogen purification system.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrogen purification system can immobilize ammonia contained in a hydrogen-containing gas and remove it, and can suppress the lowering of the efficiency of hydrogen purification due to the ammonia poisoning of an electrochemical device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A hydrogen purification system (200) comprises gas supply unit for supplying hydrogen-containing gas containing carbon dioxide and ammonia; electrochemical device (10) having membrane-electrode junction, supplying hydrogen-containing gas to anode (12), and passing a current in a predetermined direction between anode and cathode (13) to purify hydrogen at the cathode, where anode and cathode are arranged on each surface of electrolyte film (11); power source for passing current; a set of bubbling devices (130, 131) in which aqueous solvent capable of dissolving hydrogen is stored, and substance caused by hydrogen dissolved in aqueous solvent, which is individually provided for each of the set of the bubbling devices; concentration sensor (170) for measuring concentration; hydrogen-containing gas supply flow path (104) for bubbling hydrogen-containing gas discharged from gas supply unit into aqueous solvent of bubbling device; hydrogen-containing gas discharge channel (109) that supplies hydrogen-containing gas separated from aqueous solvent to anode, and hydrogen-containing gas supplied to anode from anode to cathode via electrolyte film; anodic off gas supply flow path (146) that discharges anodic off gas without permeation to the outside; hydrogen-containing gas supply flow path selection unit selected as the bubbling device for bubbling in aqueous solvent; and controller (160). From the bubbling device having a concentration less than a predetermined value when the contained ammonia cannot be dissolved in the aqueous solvent, the hydrogen-containing gas is converted into the aqueous solvent by the hydrogen-containing gas supply flow path selection unit. When the bubbling device to be bubbling in is selected, the operation of purifying hydrogen in the electrochemical device is performed, and the concentration measured by the concentration sensor of the selected bubbling device reaches the predetermined value, and the other bubbling device for bubbling the hydrogen-containing gas into the aqueous solvent is selected from the bubbling devices having a concentration of less than a predetermined value by the hydrogen-containing gas supply flow path selection unit.

TI Tunable mid-infrared laser source for use in sensing system, comprises non-linear crystal for receiving first and second laser beams and generating third laser beam, where variable poling period is distributed across width of crystal.
AU FAROOQ A
   MARANGONI M
   LAMPERTI M
   SHAKFA M K
AE POLITECNICO DI MILANO (MIPT-C)
   UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
GA 2021C7386W
AB    NOVELTY - Tunable mid-infrared laser source comprises a non-linear crystal configured to receive simultaneously first laser beam 110 and second laser beam 120 and to generate a third laser beam 140 that has a third wavelength, which is larger than each of the two wavelengths, where the non-linear crystal 130 has a length and a width, and a variable poling period is distributed across the width so that the third wavelength varies within a given wavelength range based on an incident position of the laser beams along the width of the crystal. The crystal is an orientation-patterned gallium arsenide (GaAs) crystal. The first laser device is an external cavity or a distributed-feedback quantum cascade laser, and the second laser device is a carbon dioxide gas laser 122.
   USE - Laser source is used in sensing system for high-resolution spectroscopy and in air quality monitoring and medical and combustion diagnostics.
   ADVANTAGE - The non-linear crystal is configured to receive simultaneously the first and second laser beams and to generate a third laser beam that has a third wavelength, which is larger than each of the primary and secondary wavelengths, and thus enables to provide broad tunability and can be used for high-sensitivity detection of gaseous chemical species in the mid-infrared (MIR) range with high resolution and accuracy, and also improves the spectral resolution of Fourier transform infrared (FTIR) spectrometry.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) A method for generating a mid-infrared laser beam with a laser source, which involves (a) injecting a first laser beam having a first wavelength into a non-linear crysta.
   (b) injecting a second laser beam having a second wavelength, which is different from the first wavelength, into the non-linear crystal.
   (c) moving the non-linear crystal, perpendicular to the first and second laser beams, to generate a third laser beam that has a third wavelength, which is larger than each of the first and second wavelengths, where the non-linear crystal has a length and a width, and a variable poling period is distributed across the width so that the third wavelength varies within a given wavelength range.
   2. A sensing system, which comprises a laser source configured to generate a mid-infrared laser beam based on a first laser device, which generates a first laser beam having a first wavelength, a second laser device, which generates a second laser beam having a second wavelength, which is different from the first wavelength, and a non-linear crystal that receives simultaneously the first and second laser beams and generates a mid-infrared laser beam having a third wavelength, which is larger than each of the first and second wavelengths, a moving mechanism configured to move the non-linear crystal along a direction perpendicular to the first and second laser beams to change the third wavelength, a chamber configured to receive a chemical element to be measured, wherein the mid-infrared laser beam is configured to enter the chamber, and a sensor configured to receive the mid-infrared laser beam after exiting the chamber and also configured to determine a wavelength or intensity of the mid-infrared laser beam.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematicview of a laser source that combines two different laser beams in a non-linear crystal to generate a third laser beam having a higher wavelength than each of the first and second laser beams.
   First laser beam (110)
   Second laser beam (120)
   Carbon dioxide gas laser (122)
   Non-linear crystal (130)
   Third laser beam (140)

(iv) adding equal volume diluted siRNA pre-mixed solution in the diluted Lipofectamine 3000 reagent in each tube, (v) room temperature incubation for 15 minutes after the siRNA-Lipofectamine 3000 complex added to the cell culture medium, the final concentration is 100 nM, culturing for 6 hours in the culture box under the condition of 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide and changing the liquid, (vi) culturing for 48 hours, extracting the total RNA in the laryngeal squamous cancer cell line; and

The step (iii) of real-time quantitative PCR detection hsa-circ-0000033 specific back to the back primer of the nucleotide sequence is shown as acaggaattgcagcgagaa (SEQ ID NO.2), aaagcagcaggatggtggc (SEQID NO.3): F1 at 5'-TACAGGAATTGCAGCGAGAA-3' APOS, SEQ IDNO.3, R1: 5'-AAAGCAGCAGGATGGTGGC-3 'APOS, nucleotide sequence of specific amplification upstream and downstream primer of the internal reference gene 18 s is shown as SEQ ID NO.4, SEQID NO.5: SEQ ID NO.4, F2 is 5'-CCTGGATACCGCAGCTAGGA-3 '; SEQ ID NO. 5, R2: 5'-GCGGCGCAATACGAATGCCCC-3 '. Preferred Method: The method comprises adding diluted siRNA in the diluted Lipofectamine 3000 reagent in each tube inthe step (iv); the volume ratio of the specifically added siRNA pre-mixed solution to the diluted Lipofectamine3000 reagent is 1:1; in the step (vi), extracting total RNA in the laryngeal squamous cancer cell line; The specific steps comprises (a) transfecting siRNA for 48 hours, taking out the culture plate for extracting RNA; (ii) adding TRIzol on the ice, allowing to stand for 10 minutes, releasing the RNA in the cell to the solution; (iii) adding 0.2 ml chloroform, vortex oscillation mixing uniformly, allowing to stand at room temperature for 10 minutes at 4 degrees C, 13000 revolutions/minute centrifuging for 10 minutes, the liquid layering, where the upper aqueous phase is enriched with RNA, absorbing the upper aqueous phase to 1.5 ml RNase-free centrifugal tube; (iv) adding equal volume of isopropanol, vortex oscillation mixing uniformly, allowing to stand for 30 minutes at 4 degrees C, 12000 revolutions/minutes at 4 degrees C, centrifuging for 10 minutes and removing the supernatant; (e) adding 1 ml 75% of ethanol, mixing uniformly, 12000 revolutions/minutes at 4 degrees C, centrifuging for 5 minutes, removing the supernatant, adding 50 mu l of RNase-free water to dissolve precipitate total RNA extracting solution in the cell, -80 degrees C.

PN CN112010492-A; CN112010492-B
TI 1-Aminoanthraquinone production wastewater physicochemical-biochemical treatment involves subjecting 1-aminoanthraquinone production wastewater to biochemical treatment, performing anoxic/aeration process, decoloring and oxidizing.
AU LI J
   YU Z
   JIANG C
AE NANJING LVDAO ENVIRONMENT ENG CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020C2063M
AB    NOVELTY - Nitrified acid water, nitrified neutralized water and reduced mother liquor produced during production of 1-aminoanthraquinone are pretreated, and stored in wastewater collection tank. Ground flushing water and domestic sewage in factory area are discharged to low-concentration wastewater tank. The high-concentration production wastewater in collection tank and low-concentration wastewater in low-concentration wastewater tank are subjected to biochemical treatment. The obtained wastewater is processed in multi-stage anoxic/aeration (A/O) tank. The denitrifying bacteria in anoxic tank (A) perform denitrification under anoxic environment and remove portion of chemical oxygen demand (COD). The aerobic microorganisms in aerobic (O) tank oxidize ammoniacal nitrogen in wastewater to nitrate nitrogen, and COD to carbon dioxide and water. The obtained wastewater is decolored and oxidized, and added to flocculation sedimentation tank for flocculation and sedimentation process.
   USE - 1-Aminoanthraquinone production wastewater physicochemical-biochemical treatment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Nitrified acid water, nitrified neutralized water and reduced mother liquor produced during the production of 1-aminoanthraquinone are separately collected and pretreated. The pretreatment method of nitrified acid water includes macroporous resin adsorption, lime neutralization, and filter press dehydration. The pretreatment method of nitrified neutralized water includes flocculation precipitation and activated carbon filtration process. The pretreatment method of reduced mother liquor is evaporation treatment. The reduced washing water discharged from the workshop, and the pretreated nitrified acid water, nitrified neutralized water and reduced mother liquor evaporation condensate are discharged to a wastewater collection tank for storage. Ground flushing water and domestic sewage in the factory area are discharged to a low-concentration wastewater tank for storage. The high-concentration production wastewater in the collection tank and the low-concentration wastewater in the low-concentration wastewater tank are pumped to an adjustment tank, and nutrients are added for biochemical treatment. The obtained wastewater is lifted to a coagulation sedimentation tank with a water pump, alkali is added to adjust pH in the mixing zone of the coagulation tank, polyaluminum chloride (PAC) and polyacrylamide (PAM) are added, and reacted, to remove colloidal substances and suspended solids in wastewater. The resultant wastewater is pumped into a hydrolysis acidification tank for treatment, and the macromolecular substances in the wastewater are converted into small molecules which are easily biodegradable. The obtained wastewater is processed in a multi-stage anoxic/aeration (A/O) tank including an anoxic (A) tank and an aerobic (O) tank, and the mixed liquid in the aerobic (O) tank is returned to the anoxic (A) tank. The denitrifying bacteria in the anoxic (A) perform denitrification under anoxic environment and remove portion of the chemical oxygen demand (COD). The aerobic microorganisms in the aerobic (O) tank oxidize ammoniacal nitrogen in wastewater to nitrate nitrogen, and COD to carbon dioxide and water. The resultant wastewater is discharged into an intermediate sedimentation tank for mud-water separation, and the sedimentation sludge is returned to the first-level anoxic (A) tank by the return pump, and supernatant is discharged to the subsequent unit for processing. The obtained wastewater is introduced into a decolorization reaction tank, a decolorizing agent is added to remove the chromaticity of wastewater, and an oxidant dosing device is provided to remove residual organic substance in the wastewater. The decolorized and oxidized wastewater is added to a flocculation sedimentation tank with PAC and PAM for flocculation and sedimentation, and obtained effluent meets the first-level discharge standard GB8978-1996 (Integrated Wastewater Discharge Standard). The remaining sludge produced by the system is collected in a sludge tank, dehydrated by filter press, dehydrated sludge is transported for disposal, and the filtrate is discharged to the adjustment tank and process is repeated.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Process: Nitrified acid water is adsorbed by resin, and neutralized by adding calcium hydroxide powder, such that calcium hydroxide and sulfate in wastewater form calcium sulfate, and the pH of wastewater is 7-10 as the end point of the neutralization reaction. The total salt content and COD concentration of the adjustment tank is controlled to 14000 plus minus 1000 mg/L and less than 3000 mg/L, respectively as standard. Carbon sources, phosphorus and other nutrient elements are added to the adjustment tank, and then pH of wastewater is adjusted to 6-8 using alkali. The hydrolysis acidification tank is provided with a suspended vinylon soft filler having specific surface area of 3000-5000 m2/m3, and diameter of 3-5 mu m, to increase the total amount of microorganisms and diversity of microorganisms. In the multi-stage A/O tank, the total COD volume load is controlled at 0.3-0.8 kg COD/m3.day, and the activated sludge biological amount is controlled at 3000-8000 mg/L. The content of dissolved oxygen in anoxic (A) tank is controlled at 0.2-0.5 mg/L, and content of dissolved oxygen in aerobic (O) tank is controlled at 2-5 mg/L. Preferred Property: The height of the filler layer is 2.5-3.5 m.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The oxidant is sodium hypochlorite.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The decolorizing agent is liquid quaternary amine-type decolorizing agent. The addition amount of decolorizing agent is 5-50 mg/L.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The addition amount of PAC is 50-300 mg/L, and PAM is 5-10 mg/L.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC D04-A01B; D04-A01F1; D04-A01F2; D04-A01J; D04-A01K2; D04-A01P1; D04-B07C; E11-Q02C; E31-C; E32-A04; E34-C03; E34-D01; A04-D04A2; A10-E09B; A12-M01; A12-W11E; A12-W11J

PN CN111921337-A
TI Pressure swing adsorption nitrogen equipment includes substrate, air compressor, air buffer tank, support frame, processing box, activated carbon filter plate, bacteriostatic plate, water processor, freeze dryer, and nitrogen generators.
AU LI Q
   XU H
AE ZHEJIANG QUINCE AIR SEPARATION PLANT CO (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020B5346M
AB    NOVELTY - Pressure swing adsorption nitrogen equipment comprises upper substrate (1), air compressor (4) fixedly installed at right end of top front surface of upper base plate, air buffer tank (5) fixedly installed on back of air compressor, support frame fixedly installed on periphery of middle of air buffer tank, processing box (7) fixedly installed on left side of air buffer tank, activated carbon filter plate fixedly installed at lower end of inside of processing box, bacteriostatic plate fixedly installed at upper end of processing box, water processor fixedly installed on left side of processing box, freeze dryer (11) fixedly installed on left side of water processor, conveying pipe installed on freeze dryer, control valves installed at delivery pipe, two nitrogen generators fixedly installed on top of right end of conveying pipe, controller fixedly installed on upper ends of inner sides of two nitrogen generators, and nitrogen exhaust pipes installed on two nitrogen generators.
   USE - The equipment is used for pressure swing adsorption of nitrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The operator uses control valve to realize asynchronous work of two nitrogen generators to ensure working efficiency of equipment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Pressure swing adsorption nitrogen equipment comprises upper substrate (1), air compressor (4) fixedly installed at right end of top front surface of upper base plate, air buffer tank (5) fixedly installed on back of air compressor, support frame fixedly installed on periphery of middle of air buffer tank, processing box (7) fixedly installed on left side of air buffer tank, activated carbon filter plate fixedly installed at lower end of inside of processing box, bacteriostatic plate fixedly installed at upper end of processing box, water processor fixedly installed on left side of processing box, freeze dryer (11) fixedly installed on left side of water processor, conveying pipe fixedly installed on left side of freeze dryer, two control valves fixedly installed at right end of delivery pipe, two nitrogen generators fixedly installed on top of right end of conveying pipe, controller fixedly installed on upper ends of inner sides of two nitrogen generators, and nitrogen exhaust pipes fixedly installed on tops of two nitrogen generators. The air buffer tank includes shell, protective liner fixedly installed inside shell, buffer strip fixedly installed on right side of protective liner, and stressed shell fixedly installed on surface of buffer strip. When using pressure swing adsorption nitrogen equipment, the air compressor draws outside air into it, and enters treated air to air buffer tank. The high-pressure air is injected into air buffer tank. The gas pressure will directly hit force shell at this time, and the buffered gas will enter processing box homeopathically. The gas will pass through activated carbon filter plate and antibacterial plate. The oil and saturated water in air will be processed and discharged. After nitrogen, oxygen and free water are processed by freeze dryer, the free water is also processed while oxygen, nitrogen and carbon dioxide begin enter nitrogen generator, and are processed by carbon molecular sieve adsorbents. The oxygen and carbon dioxide in air will be retained in nitrogen generator. The higher purity nitrogen will be discharged from nitrogen exhaust pipe into storage tank. In order to ensure smooth operation of equipment, the operator uses control valve to realize asynchronous work of two nitrogen generators to ensure working efficiency of equipment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of pressure swing adsorption nitrogen equipment.
   Upper substrate (1)
   Air compressor (4)
   Air buffer tank (5)
   Processing box (7)
   Freeze dryer (11)

PN CN111905920-A; CN111905920-B
TI Recovery of valuable elements e.g. gold from            cyanidation gold extraction waste residue involves            acidifying residue, drying, adding e.g. activated            carbon, roasting, adding e.g. butylammonium black            powder collector, and performing flotation.
AU WANG J
   ZHANG Y
   WANG M
   WANG T
   LIU J
   SUN X
   XU L
   ZHU D
   GUO J
   YU Z
   JIAO L
   WANG L
AE SHANDONG GUODA GOLD CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020B3827C
AB    NOVELTY - Recovery of valuable elements from cyanidation                gold extraction waste residue involves performing                acidification of cyanidation gold extraction waste                residue, dehydrating, filtering, drying, adding                sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide as activators,                drying, adding water, performing closed-circuit                flotation to obtain a mixed flotation concentrate,                adding water to the concentrate, adding activated                carbon, stirring, adding butylammonium black powder                collector, performing closed-circuit flotation to                obtain a metal concentrate and a high-grade sulfur                concentrate with a sulfur content of 47-52%,                roasting the high-grade sulfur concentrate into a                roasting furnace in a reducing atmosphere to obtain                a slag, and performing slag flotation by adding                water to the slag, adding sodium hydrosulfide,                grinding, adding water, adding butylammonium black                powder collector and oil foaming agent, and                performing closed-circuit flotation to obtain a                gold concentrate and an iron concentrate.
   USE - Recovery of valuable elements e.g. iron,                sulfur, copper, lead, zinc, gold and silver from                cyanidation gold extraction waste residue.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and economical                recovery of valuable elements from cyanidation gold                extraction waste residue with low environmental                pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovery of valuable elements from cyanidation                gold extraction waste residue involves performing                (i) acidification by adding water to cyanidation                gold extraction waste residue to adjust the slurry                concentration to 60-65%, adjusting the slurry pH to                6.5-7 using sulfuric acid, adding 10-20 kg/ton                sodium sulfite, performing acidification at 40-50                degrees C for 4-6 hours, dehydrating, filtering,                naturally stacking the filter cake, and drying for                20-30 days until the moisture content is 10-12%,                performing (ii) dry activation treatment by drying                and dehydrating the obtained dried product in a                rotary kiln, adding sulfur dioxide and carbon                dioxide as activators, and drying at 200-300                degrees C for 4-6 hours until the moisture content                is 5-8% and pH is 5.5-6, performing (iii) mixed                flotation by adding water to the obtained dried                product to adjust the slurry concentration to                30-35%, adding the obtained mixture and a mixed                collector to a flotation machine, and performing                closed-circuit flotation to obtain a mixed                flotation concentrate and mixed flotation tailings,                performing (iv) separation and flotation by adding                water to the mixed flotation concentrate to adjust                the slurry concentration to 30-35%, using lime to                adjust the slurry pH to 9.5-10.5, adding 500-800                g/ton activated carbon, stirring for 4-6 hours,                adding the obtained slurry to 150-200 g/ton                butylammonium black powder collector and performing                closed-circuit flotation to obtain a metal                concentrate comprising 2-5 g/ton gold, 50-60 g/ton                silver, 8-10% copper, 6-8% lead, 5-6% zinc and                18-20% sulfur, and a high-grade sulfur concentrate                with a sulfur content of 47-52%, performing (v)                roasting by passing the high-grade sulfur                concentrate into a roasting furnace, controlling                temperature of the roasting furnace to 800-900                degrees C, and roasting in a reducing atmosphere to                obtain a slag, and performing (vi) slag flotation                by adding water to the slag to adjust the slurry                concentration to 60-65%, adding 300-500 g/ton                sodium hydrosulfide, grinding for 15-20 minutes to                obtain 90-95% ground product with a grinding                fineness of -400 mesh, adding water to the ground                product to adjust the slurry concentration to                30-35%, adding 150-200 g/ton butylammonium black                powder collector and 30-50 g/ton oil foaming agent,                and performing closed-circuit flotation to obtain a                gold concentrate and an iron concentrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The usage amount of                  sulfur dioxide in the process (ii) is 10-20                  kg/ton, and carbon dioxide is 10-20 kg/ton. The                  gold concentrate comprises 20-40 g/ton gold,                  50-60 g/ton silver, 2-4% copper, 1-2% lead, 1-2%                  zinc and 8-10% sulfur. The iron concentrate                  comprises 0.2-0.3% sulfur and 62-68% iron.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mixed collector                  is a mixture of butyl xanthate and butylammonium                  black powder collector in a mass ratio of                  (1.8-2.2):1, and the usage amount of the mixed                  collector is 80-120 g/ton.
