PT P
PN CN111841317-A; CN111841317-B
TI Structured packing catalyst used for decomposition            of phosgene in tail gas, comprises set of strip-shaped            corrugated packing sheet wrapped with wire mesh and            strip-shaped structured packing sheet fixed between            upper and lower chassis.
AU DENG R
   WU X
   ZHAO L
   HU M
   WANG P
AE WANHUA CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WHCH-C)
   WANHUA CHEM NINGBO CO LTD (WHCH-C)
   WANHUA CHEM FUJIAN CO LTD (WANH-Non-standard)
GA 2020B2705D
AB    NOVELTY - A structured packing catalyst comprises an                upper chassis, a lower chassis, and a set of                components (A) and (B) in a ratio of 1:1-10,                preferably 1:2-5 fixed between the upper and lower                chassis. Components (A) and (B) are arranged in                parallel and staggered. Component (A) is a                strip-shaped corrugated packing sheet wrapped with                a wire mesh on an outer surface. The wire mesh is                filled with 1-20 %mass, preferably 2-10 %mass                aluminum-silicate-supported silicon nitride                catalyst particles. Component B is a strip-shaped                structured packing sheet.
   USE - Structured packing catalyst is used for                decomposition of phosgene in tail gas                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The structured packing catalyst has improved                phosgene decomposition efficiency even when used in                small amount, and decomposes phosgene with reduced                operating cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) preparation of the structured packing                catalyst, which involves uniformly mixing silicon                nitride nanoparticles, silicate, an aluminum salt,                water, a dispersant and a binder to obtain a                mixture (M1), drying to a moisture content of less                than 2 wt.%, extruding, carbonizing in a muffle                furnace at 500-700 degrees C for 1-3 hours under                nitrogen atmosphere, continually heating to                1200-1500 degrees C for calcination for 3-6 hours,                cooling to room temperature, pickling and washing                in sequence, drying to a constant moisture content                to obtain aluminum-silicate-supported silicon                nitride catalyst particles, filling the silicon                nitride catalyst particles in the wire mesh,                wrapping on a surface of the strip-shaped                corrugated packing sheet, stitching the wire mesh                to make component (A), arranging component (B) in                parallel and staggered on the lower chassis, and                covering the upper chassis;
   (2) phosgene-containing tail gas treatment                device, which has a high-gravity rotating packed                bed reactor, a dilute hydrochloric acid                intermediate storage tank, a circulating pump, a                circulating cooler, and a negative pressure fan.                The high-gravity rotating packed bed reactor is                provided inside with a rotating catalyst packing                layer which is the structured packing catalyst, and                a gas phase inlet and a liquid outlet provided in a                lower portion, and a gas phase outlet and a liquid                inlet provided on an upper portion. The gas phase                inlet is connected to an exhaust port of the                negative pressure fan. The gas phase outlet is                connected to a subsequent organic solvent                adsorption system. The liquid inlet is connected to                the dilute acid intermediate storage tank through                the circulating pump and circulating cooler. The                intermediate storage tank and liquid outlet are                connected to an inlet of the intermediate storage                tank through a U-shaped liquid seal; and
   (3) treatment of the phosgene-containing tail                gas, which involves feeding the phosgene-containing                tail gas into the supergravity rotating packed bed                reactor, sending the dilute hydrochloric acid in                the dilute acid intermediate storage tank to the                supergravity rotating packed bed reactor,                counter-currently contacting the two phases of gas                and liquid in the high-speed rotating catalyst                packing layer, performing catalytic hydrolysis                reaction of phosgene and water under the action of                high gravity field and catalyst to generate carbon                dioxide and hydrochloric acid, dissolving the                hydrochloric acid in water to form dilute                hydrochloric acid, entering dilute hydrochloric                acid into the intermediate storage tank through the                U-shaped liquid seal through the liquid outlet                while controlling the concentration of dilute                hydrochloric acid to 0.5-20 wt.%, preferably 1-10                wt.% through the efflux portion of dilute                hydrochloric acid, supplementing with the same                amount of deionized water, feeding the generated                carbon dioxide and treated tail gas into the                subsequent organic solvent adsorption system from                the gas phase outlet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The                  aluminum-silicate-supported silicon nitride                  catalyst particles have a particle size of 0.5-20                  mm, preferably 2-6 mm, a specific surface area of                  150-800 m3/g, preferably 350-600 m3/g, a pore                  size of 5-120 nm, preferably 10-50 nm, and a                  catalyst strength of 2-16 N/cm2, preferably 5-10                  N/cm2. The silicon nitride nanoparticles have a                  particle size of 20-500 nm, preferably 50-150 nm.                  Preferred Composition: The silicon nitride                  catalyst comprises 10-55 vol.%, preferably 25-40                  vol.% aluminum silicate. Acid washing is                  performed using dilute sulfuric acid, dilute                  nitric acid, or dilute acetic acid, preferably                  2-15 wt.%, preferably 5-10 wt.% dilute sulfuric                  acid. The phosgene concentration in the                  phosgene-containing tail gas is 1000-100000                  ppm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The silicate is                  methyl silicate, ethyl silicate, or butyl                  silicate, preferably methyl silicate. The                  aluminum salt is organic aluminum salts such as                  aluminum isopropoxide and aluminum n-propoxide,                  or inorganic aluminum salts such as aluminum                  nitrate and aluminum sulfate, preferably aluminum                  isopropoxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The strip-shaped                  corrugated packing sheet has a corrugation angle                  of 30-60 degrees . The upper and lower chassis                  have a circular shape and a diameter of 600-1200                  mm. The structured packing catalyst has a height                  of 500-800mm. Components (A) and (B) are evenly                  distributed on the chassis, and the distance                  between components (A) and (B) is 25-60 mm.                  Preferred Composition: The strip-shaped                  corrugated packing sheet is made of acid and                  alkali-corrosion-resistant ceramics,                  polyvinylidene fluoride and polypropylene,                  preferably polyvinylidene fluoride. The wire mesh                  of component (A) is made of polypropylene,                  polyvinyl chloride, and nylon, preferably a nylon                  mesh, and has a size of 100-120 mesh. The                  strip-shaped structured packing sheet is a                  strip-shaped window-opening diversion-type                  packing sheet or a strip-shaped orifice plate                  corrugated packing sheet, and is made of acid and                  alkali-corrosion-resistant ceramics,                  polyvinylidene fluoride and polypropylene,                  preferably polyvinylidene fluoride. The upper and                  lower chassis are made of polyvinylidene                  fluoride. Mixture (M1) comprises 10-40 wt.%,                  preferably 20-35 wt.% silicon nitride                  nanoparticles, 30-60 wt.%, preferably 35-50 wt.%                  silicate, 10-30 wt.%, preferably 20-25 wt.%                  aluminum salt, 1-20 wt.%, preferably 5-10 wt.%                  water, 2-15 wt.%, preferably 5-10 wt.% dispersant                  and 2-10 wt.%, preferably 5-8 wt.% binder. The                  dispersant is sodium dialkyl sulfate,                  methylpentanol, cellulose derivative, or                  polyacrylamide. The binder is an                  organopolysiloxane binder, a phenolic resin, a                  hydroxypropyl methyl cellulose, or a polyacryl                  alcohol. Preferred Conditions: Carbonization is                  preferably performed at 550-650 degrees C at a                  rate of 2-8 degrees C/minute, preferably 3-5                  degrees C/minute for 1.5-2.5 hours. Calcination                  is preferably performed at 1300-1450 degrees C at                  a rate of 2-8 degrees C/minute, preferably 3-5                  degrees C/minute for 4-5 hours. The gas-liquid                  ratio in the high-gravity rotating packed bed                  reactor is controlled at 50-1000:1, preferably                  200-500:1. The residence time of the                  phosgene-containing tail gas in the supergravity                  rotating packed bed reactor is 1-20 seconds,                  preferably 5-15 seconds. The rotation speed of                  rotating catalyst packing layer is 200-800 rpm,                  preferably 300-500 rpm.

PN CN111802171-A
TI Reducing pollution rate of seafood mushroom bag inoculation comprises collecting culture material for cultivating seafood mushrooms to complete bag making of bacteria bag, and controlling moisture content of culture material after mixing.
AU RAO H
   XIAO S
   HUANG Z
   LIAO Y
   LIAN C
   YOU A
   ZHONG J
   LU J
AE FUJIAN YUANSHAN HUIMIN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
   LONGYAN SOIL FERTILIZER TECHNOLOGY STATION (LONG-Non-standard)
GA 2020A6721H
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing pollution rate of seafood mushroom bag inoculation comprises (i) collecting the culture material for cultivating seafood mushrooms to complete the bag making of the bacteria bag, controlling the moisture content of the culture material to be between 61 and 62%, and the pH value to stabilize between 8.9 and 9.1 after mixing, (ii) sterilizing the bacteria bag prepared in step (i) by an autoclave, applying vacuum twice before sterilization, and controlling the vacuum degree to 0.05 MPa vacuum for each vacuum, controlling the temperature of the sterilization pot to 105 degrees C and keep it warm for 60 minutes during the sterilization process, when the temperature is increased to 115 degrees C, keep it warm for 40 minutes and to 126 degrees C, keep it for 180 minutes, and after the sterilization, continuing to low flame for 30 minutes.
   USE - The method is useful for reducing pollution rate of seafood mushroom bag inoculation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces the contamination rate of bacteria bag; increases the rate of finished bacteria bag; achieves the consistency of the production of the bacteria bag to the greatest extent, and high and stable production of seafood mushrooms; has simple operation process, which can ensure the quality, safety and sanitation of the products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Reducing pollution rate of seafood mushroom bag inoculation comprises (i) collecting the culture material for cultivating seafood mushrooms to complete the bag making of the bacteria bag, controlling the moisture content of the culture material to be between 61 and 62%, and the pH value to stabilize between 8.9 and 9.1 after mixing, (ii) sterilizing the bacteria bag prepared in step (i) by an autoclave, applying vacuum twice before sterilization, and controlling the vacuum degree to 0.05 MPa vacuum for each vacuum, controlling the temperature of the sterilization pot to 105 degrees C and keep it warm for 60 minutes during the sterilization process, when the temperature is increased to 115 degrees C, keep it warm for 40 minutes and to 126 degrees C, keep it for 180 minutes, and after the sterilization, continuing to low flame for 30 minutes, (iii) when the pressure of the autoclave is reset to zero at the end of the simmering process in step (ii), when the temperature drops below 80 degrees C, transferring to the 100,000 fresh air cooling room, when the temperature is lowered to 60 degrees C, transferring to 10,000-grade aseptic strong cold room, cooling until the temperature of the culture material in the bacteria bag drops to 22-24 degrees C, and taking out for inoculation operation, (iv) quantitatively inoculating the cultivated seafood mushroom liquid strains into the bacterial bag prepared by steps (i) to (iii) to realize the inoculation of the strains, carrying out the inoculation operation in the inoculation area in the hundred-level purification workshop, maintaining under the condition of positive pressure and fresh air, and carrying out the flow-water inoculation operation according to the requirements of aseptic operation, and (v) placing the inoculated bacteria bag into a constant temperature culture room for hyphae culture, in the pre-culture stage, controlling the temperature of the culture room to 22-24 degrees C, the carbon dioxide concentration is 5000-6000 ppm, after 50-55 days of cultivation, conveying the post-maturing cultivation stage, adjusting the space temperature of the cultivation room to 24-26 degrees C, the carbon dioxide concentration is 7000-8000 ppm, and the bacteria bag matures after 45-50 days of cultivation and enters the fruiting management.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the culture material composition for cultivating seafood mushrooms includes 40-46 pts. wt. cottonseed hulls, 15-20 pts. wt. miscellaneous sawdust, 7-10 pts. wt. bagasse, 17-21 pts. wt. wheat husk, 3-5 pts. wt. soybean meal, 3-5 pts. wt. corn flour, before mixing, determining the water content of each component in advance, during the mixing process, using bucket to supply water to ensure that the water content of the culture material is 61-62%. In the bag making process in step (i), the amount of culture material used for each bag making is calculated in advance, and the pH regulator is added quantitatively to realize the control of pH. The pH adjusting agent is lime. In the step (iii), the pH value of the culture material in the bacteria bag after sterilization and cooling is 6-6.5. The preparation process of the seafood mushroom liquid strain in step (iv) (a) talking 2-10 pts. wt. commercially available seafood special medium for mushrooms, 5-10 pts. wt. white sugar, 0.1-0.2 pts. wt. antifoaming agent and 200-400 pts. wt. sterile water, and stirring and thoroughly mixing, (b) passing the medium prepared in step (a) into high-pressure steam and sterilizing it at 123 degrees C for 70 minutes, and (c) passing the sterilized medium into cold water and cooling to 22-24 degrees C, and inserting the pre-cultured seafood mushroom mother species under aseptic conditions, aeration culturing at 22-24 degrees C for 8 days, and obtaining the seafood mushroom liquid medium. The aeration culture in step (c), taking out the strains under aseptic conditions when the culture reaches the 6th day and connecting to the test tube for culture to test the activity of the seafood mushroom strains and selecting the dominant strains, when the culture reaches the 8th day, checking the pH of the dominant bacteria, when the pH is 6.2-6.4, and the pH value of the culture material in the bag does not exceed 0.5, and can be used for the inoculation of the bag. The antifoaming agent in the step (a) is silicone antifoaming agent.

PN CN111484046-A
TI Extracting lithium from brine by using salt lake brine, adding aluminum source, precipitating magnesium and aluminum into layered double metal hydroxides, separating, adsorbing, and adding carbonate ions to obtain lithium carbonate.
AU LV L
   LI J
   WANG Y
   ZENG H
   WU Y
   LU T
   LI L
   XIE Z
AE UNIV QUZHOU (UNQZ-C)
   LV L (LVLL-Individual)
GA 202075371P
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting lithium from brine, involves using salt lake brine with a high magnesium-to-lithium ratio, adding an aluminum source, and precipitating with alkali to precipitate magnesium and aluminum into layered double metal hydroxides (MgAl-LDHs), separating by filtration, remaining lithium ions in the filtrate, concentrating or using ion selective adsorption to enrich the lithium, and adding carbonate ions to precipitate to obtain lithium carbonate. The method includes (a) weighing aluminum source, adding to brine and dissolving alkali in water to prepare a precipitant, mixing the brine salt solution and the precipitant, stirring of the system for nucleation and crystallization, filtering, and spray-drying, (b) concentrating the filtrate, introducing carbon dioxide to precipitate lithium carbonate, and filtering, and (c) adding ion selective adsorbent to absorb the lithium ions in the filtrate, desorbing the adsorbent and adding sodium carbonate to obtain lithium carbonate.
   USE - The method is used for extracting lithium from brine with high magnesium-lithium ratio. The LDH is used in laminate, and the obtained LDHs is used as magnesium-based functional materials, which are used in flame retardancy, wastewater treatment, and soil remediation.
   ADVANTAGE - The obtained product changes chemical composition and the chemical properties of the laminate and adjusts the charge density of the laminate to adapt to new uses. The method achieves lithium extraction from salt lake brine with a high magnesium-to-lithium ratio, and realizes comprehensive utilization of brine resources. The method has short process flow, simple operation, excellent magnesium-lithium separation effect, completely utilizes magnesium resources while extracting lithium, and solves the problem of magnesium damage in salt lakes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for extracting lithium from brine with high magnesium-lithium ratio, involves using salt lake brine with a high magnesium-to-lithium ratio as raw material, adding an aluminum source, and precipitating with alkali to quickly precipitate magnesium and aluminum into layered double metal hydroxides (MgAl-LDHs), separating by filtration, remaining lithium ions in the filtrate, concentrating or using ion selective adsorption to enrich the lithium, and adding carbonate ions for precipitation to obtain lithium carbonate. The method includes (a) precipitating magnesium, weighing a certain amount of aluminum source, adding it to brine, stirring and uniformly mixing, then weighing a certain amount of alkali and dissolving in water to prepare a precipitant, quickly mixing the brine salt solution and the precipitant under high-speed shearing and stirring, maintaining a certain temperature, stirring speed and pH value of the system for nucleation and crystallization for 1-12 hours, then filtering the reaction solution, leaving lithium ions in the filtrate, washing the filter cake with pure water to weakly alkaline or neutral, and spray-drying to obtain MgAl-LDHs, (b) concentrating and extracting lithium by concentrating the filtrate in step (a) until the lithium ion concentration reaches a certain value, introducing carbon dioxide to precipitate lithium ions into lithium carbonate, filtering and washing to obtain crude lithium carbonate which is used to prepare refined lithium carbonate or lithium hydroxide, and (c) extracting lithium by ion selective adsorption in the filtrate in step (a), adding an appropriate amount of ion selective adsorbent to fully absorb the lithium ions in the filtrate, filtering and separating, desorbing the adsorbent after the adsorption of lithium by bipolar membrane electrodialysis to obtain a concentrated lithium chloride solution to achieve the enrichment of lithium ions, and then adding sodium carbonate to precipitate to obtain crude lithium carbonate, which is used to prepare refined lithium carbonate or hydroxide lithium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (a), the nucleation crystallization temperature is 25-100 degrees C. In step (b), the filtrate is concentrated until the lithium ion concentration reaches 0.5-5 mol/l. Preferred Composition: In step (a), the amount of aluminum in the aluminum source added is 1:5-1:2 of the amount of magnesium in the brine. In step (a), the amount of alkali is 1.5-4 times the sum of the amounts of magnesium and aluminum substances. Preferred Components: The added aluminum source is one or a mixture of aluminum chloride, aluminum nitrate, aluminum hydroxide, and pseudo-boehmite. The precipitating agent is one or a mixture of sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide and sodium carbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In step (c), the ion selective adsorbent is a lithium ion imprinted polymer.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)
MC A10-E01; A12-W11D; D04-A01
IP C01D-015/02; C01D-015/08; C01F-007/00

PT P
PN KR2126638-B1
TI Preparing sundae by aging pig intestine in anthocyanin carbonated water, steaming Korean thistle, drying, preparing sundae mixture, adding to aged pig small intestine, steam spraying evergreen bark extract, cooling and packing dried sundae.
AU JUNG Y G
AE JUNG Y G (JUNG-Individual)
GA 202062407G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing sundae involves (a) preparing anthocyanin carbonated water, (b) aging pig intestine using the anthocyanin carbonated water, (c) performing primary steaming Korean thistle, (d) performing primary drying the primary steamed Korean thistle, (e) performing secondary steaming the thistle, (f) performing secondary drying the secondary steamed thistle, (g) preparing a sundae mixture comprising secondary dried thistle, (h) preparing sundae by adding sundae mixture to the aged pig small intestine, (i) preparing evergreen bark extract, (j) steam spraying the evergreen bark extract on the sundae for 10-20 seconds, (k) rapidly cooling the steam sprayed sundae, and (l) packing the dried sundae.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing sundae (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases antioxidant capacity of thistle by steaming thus provides a healthy food, and softens pig intestine. The steaming step eliminates propagation of harmful bacteria and improves storage stability of product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing sundae involves (a) preparing anthocyanin carbonated water, (b) aging pig intestine using the anthocyanin carbonated water, (c) performing primary steaming Korean thistle, (d) performing primary drying the primary steamed Korean thistle, (e) performing secondary steaming the thistle, (f) performing secondary drying the secondary steamed thistle, (g) preparing a sundae mixture comprising secondary dried thistle, (h) preparing sundae by adding sundae mixture to the aged pig small intestine, (i) preparing evergreen bark extract, (j) steam spraying the evergreen bark extract on the sundae for 10-20 seconds, (k) rapidly cooling the steam sprayed sundae, and (l) packing the dried sundae. The step (c) and (e) are carried out in a thermo-hygrostat for 8 hours at 30-35 degrees C and humidity of 40%. The steps (d) and (f) are carried out in a freeze dryer and freeze-dried at -50 degrees C for 10 hours. The anthocyanin carbonated water is prepared by washing organic sweet potato, cutting the washed sweet potato into a 3-4 mm thick sweet potato peel, freeze-drying the sweet potato peel at -50 degrees C for 6 hours, adding 5-15 wt.% freeze-dried sweet potato peel to 85-95 wt.% purified water, performing primary aging at 30-40 degrees C for 4-5 hours, filtering using a cotton cloth of 200 mesh, performing secondary aging the filtered anthocyanin extract water at a low temperature and high temperature maintained at 6 degrees C for 2 days, and injecting carbon dioxide gas into the aged extracted water. The evergreen bark extract is prepared using the evergreen bark collected from at least one chosen from spruce, cypress, and yew trees. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for sundae, which comprises 27.5 pts. wt. cabbage, 15.8 pts. wt. anthocyanin-aged pork intestine, 10.3 pts. wt. vermicelli, 6.9 pts. wt. pork, 6.9 pts. wt. chicken, 6.9 pts. wt. pork, 3 pts. wt. rapeseed oil, 3.1 pts. wt. rice, 2.7 pts. wt. corn starch, 2.3 pts. wt. steamed Korean thistle, 2.1 pts. wt. egg, 1.7 pts. wt. processed products, 1.4 pts. wt. leek, 1.4 pts. wt. onion, 1.4 pts. wt. seasoning powder, 1.4 pts. wt. miso, 1.4 pts. wt. carrot, 1.4 pts. wt. leek, 1 pt. wt. garlic, 0.7 pt. wt. Cheongyang pepper, and 0.7 pt. wt. ginger, where the anthocyanin aged pig intestine comprises 2-10 wt.% anthocyanin carbonated water, the anthocyanin carbonated water is prepared by mixing 85-95 wt.% purified water and 5-15 wt.% freeze-dried organic sweet potato peel, and injecting carbon dioxide gas for performing two-stage aging process, and the steamed thistle has an electron donating capacity of 75% or more.

PN CN111303988-A; CN111303988-B
TI Preparing sterol-rich algae oil, by culturing            Schizochytrium strains, inoculating colonies, crushing            Plukenetia volubilis fruit, adding fruit powder, adding            cellulase, hydrolyzing, adding suspension, adding            anhydrous ethanol and extracting.
AU LV X
   XUE H
   YANG P
   BI Y
   XI C
   LI H
   HAN D
   JIANG L
AE PROYA COSMETICS CO LTD (PROY-Non-standard)
GA 202059299L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing sterol-rich algae oil,                involves (a) transferring Schizochytrium strains to                a solid culture medium, culturing and inoculating                colonies into a liquid culture medium to obtain                Schizochytrium suspension, (b) crushing Plukenetia                volubilis fruit, sieving to obtain P.volubilis                fruit powder, adding the fruit powder and deionized                water to obtain a solution, adding e.g. cellulase                and hydrolyzing to obtain a enzymatic hydrolysate                of fruit powder, (c) adding suspension, adding                liquid culture medium, adding enzymatic                hydrolysate, adding sea chlorophyll to obtain                Schizochytrium fermented broth, centrifuging and                drying to obtain Schizochytrium powder, (d) adding                the powder and deionized water to obtain an algal                pulp, treating to obtain Schizochytrium solution,                centrifuging and drying to obtain Schizochytrium                powder, and (e) transferring the powder to                extraction tank, adding anhydrous ethanol and                extracting.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing sterol-rich                algae oil as an active substance in cosmetics                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The algae oil has high sterol content of                19.25-20.33 g/kg, improves stability, and is                convenient to be used as an active substance in                cosmetics.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing sterol-rich algae oil,                involves (a) transferring Schizochytrium strains                stored in liquid nitrogen to a solid culture                medium, culturing in a shaking flask at 20-25                degrees C for 48 hours, picking out the colonies                and inoculating into a liquid culture medium to                obtain Schizochytrium suspension, where the solid                culture medium comprises 15-20 g/l glucose, 2-5 g/l                yeast powder, 10-20 g/l sea salt and 15-20 g/l agar                powder, and the liquid culture medium comprises                15-25 g/l glucose, 3-6 g/l yeast powder and 10-20                g/l sea salt, (b) mechanically crushing Plukenetia                volubilis fruit, sieving through 80 mesh sieve to                obtain P.volubilis fruit powder, adding the fruit                powder and deionized water in a mass ratio of                (2-5):10 to obtain a solution, adding 250-460 U/g                cellulase and 10-20 U/g beta -glucosidase,                adjusting the pH of the solution to 4.8-5.5, and                enzymatically hydrolyzing for 24-60 hours in a 100                rpm constant temperature shaker to obtain a                enzymatic hydrolysate of fruit powder, (c) adding                5-10 %mass Schizochytrium suspension into a                fermenter, adding 10 times liquid culture medium,                adding enzymatic hydrolysate of fruit powder,                adding 200-300 mu l/kg sea chlorophyll for                amplification, keeping the temperature at 20-25                degrees C for 24-48 hours with oxygen supply                culture, controlling oxygen concentration at                0.05-0.08 mol/l to obtain Schizochytrium fermented                broth, centrifuging at 12000 rpm for 15 minutes,                washing with deionized water for 3 times,                collecting the lower layer of bacteria and drying                to constant weight in a vacuum drying oven to                obtain Schizochytrium powder, (d) adding the                Schizochytrium powder and deionized water in mass                ratio of (1-3):10 to obtain an algal pulp, placing                in a constant temperature water bath at 55-60                degrees C, ultrasonically treating at a frequency                of 250-300 W for 3-5 minutes to obtain                Schizochytrium aqueous solution, centrifuging at                12000 rpm for 15 minutes, collecting the lower                solid, and drying to constant weight in a vacuum                drying oven to obtain Schizochytrium powder, and                (e) transferring the powder in step (d) to a                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction tank,                adding 5 wt.% anhydrous ethanol as an entrainer,                and extracting at a pressure of 10-50 MPa at 20-35                degrees C for 20-100min with carbon dioxide flow                rate of 10-30 kg/hour to obtain the product.

PN CN110564561-A; US2021115364-A1
TI Flavoring and brewing fruit wine using cutinase comprises crushing e.g. grapes, adding e.g. complex enzyme I e.g. cellulase, enzymolysis, adding complex enzyme II e.g. pectinase, inoculating non-brewing yeast, fermenting and filtering.
AU YAN H
   LI J
   LIU C
   ZHU F
   HE M
AE UNIV HEBEI NORMAL SCI TECHNOLOGY (UYHE-Non-standard)
   UNIV HEBEI NORMAL SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHE-Non-standard)
GA 2019A69994
AB    NOVELTY - Flavoring and brewing fruit wine using cutinase comprises (a) crushing grapes or apple, adding sodium metabisulfite, vitamin C and carbon dioxide, (b) heating the pomace, (c) mixing clear fruit juice with pomace after heat, adding complex enzyme I including cellulase, pectinase, xylanase and cutinase to the mixed pomace soultion, performing enzymolysis, (d) mixing the pomace with the clear fruit juice, adding complex enzyme II including pectinase and beta -glucosidase, (e) measuring the sugar content of the mixed juice and adding sugar, (f) inoculating non-brewing yeast for performing fermentation, (g) inoculating dry yeast seed liquid into the fermentation system, (h) centrifuging the fermentation liquid and using the supernatant as raw wine, (i) mixing raw wine with bentonite, egg white powder and sodium metabisulfite based on the volume of raw wine and performing fining process for 8-10 days, (j) removing the sediment and (k) filtering the wine.
   USE - The method is useful for flavoring and brewing fruit wine using cutinase.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures large-scale production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Flavoring and brewing fruit wine using cutinase comprises (a) crushing grapes or apple to obtain clear fruit juice and pomace, adding 50-70 mg/l sodium metabisulfite during crushing process based on the mixed juice volume of the clear fruit juice and pomace, adding 20-40 mg/l vitamin C and carbon dioxide during crushing based on the volume of mixed juice, when the raw material is apple, (b) heating the pomace at 72-75 degrees C for 1 hour, (c) mixing 20% clear fruit juice obtained in the step (a) with pomace after heat treatment in the step (b) to obtain a mixed pomace soultion, adding 20-50 mg/l complex enzyme I including cellulase, pectinase, xylanase and cutinase in a mass ratio of 2-4:2-4:1-2:1-2 to the mixed pomace soultion based on the volume of mixed pomace soultion, performing enzymolysis at 45-55 degrees C for 2 hours, (d) mixing the pomace after enzyme treatment in the step (c) with the clear fruit juice obtained in the step (a) to obtain a mixed juice, adding 20-50 mg/l complex enzyme II including pectinase and beta -glucosidase in a mass ratio of 2-3:1-2 based on the volume of mixed juice, (e) measuring the sugar content of the mixed juice and adding sugar to adjust the sugar content of the mixed juice based on standard of brewing 12 degrees dry apple wine, (f) inoculating 106 colony forming units/ml non-brewing yeast for performing fermentation at 12-15 degrees C for 6-8 days, (g) inoculating dry yeast seed liquid into the fermentation system after finishing pre-fermenting, seed inoculating 10% non-brewing yeast in the fermentation system at 25-30 degrees C, ending fermentation when the specific gravity is reduced to 0.994-0.992 to obtain a fermentation liquid, (h) centrifuging the fermentation liquid and using the supernatant as raw wine, (i) mixing raw wine with 0.3-0.5 g/l bentonite, 0.04-0.06 g/l egg white powder and 18-20 mg/l sodium metabisulfite based on the volume of raw wine and performing fining process for 8-10 days, (j) removing the sediment to obtain wine at -4 to -4.5 degrees C for 10-14 days and (k) filtering the wine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (h) comprises separating the pomace, subjecting the dry-red raw wine to malolactic fermentation, inoculating 106 colony forming units/ml lactic acid bacteria in the raw wine, fermenting at 28-30 degrees C, until no presence of malic acid is detected. Preferred Components: The adding amount of vitamin C in the step (a) is 20 mg/l and the filling quantity of the carbon dioxide is 0.5 kg/kl. The adding amount of complex enzyme I in the step (c) is 50 mg/l based on the volume of mixed residue. The complex enzyme I comprises cellulase, pectinase, xylanase and cutinase in a mass ratio of 2:2:1:1. The adding amount of complex enzyme II in the step (d) is 40 mg/l based on the volume of the mixed juice. The complex enzyme II comprises pectinase and beta -glucosidase in a mass ratio of 2:1. The sugar content of the mixed juice in the step (e) is 23%, where the mass ratio of sucrose and fructose is 2:1. The non-brewing yeast in the step (f) is Torulaspora delbrueckii, Lachancea thermotolerans or Candida zemplinina. The dry yeast in the step (g) is Angel wine active dry yeast RV002.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

TI Peanut shell composite medium for use in            industrial cultivation of edible fungi, comprises            peanut shells, maize cob, rice bran, beet pulp, dried            bean dregs, beer grains, soybean hulls and light            calcium carbonate in specific weight parts.
AU FENG Z
   YU Y
   LI H
   GE N
AE JIANGSU CHINA GREEN BIOTECH CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019805875
AB    NOVELTY - A peanut shell composite medium comprises 3-10                pts. wt. peanut shells, 30-40 pts. wt. maize cob,                30-40 pts. wt. rice bran, 10-15 pts. wt. bran, 3-10                pts. wt. beet pulp, 3-10 pts. wt. dried bean dregs,                3-10 pts. wt. beer grains, 3-10 pts. wt. soybean                hulls, 1-3 pts. wt. shell powder and 0.1-2 pts. wt.                light calcium carbonate. The medium is prepared by                mixing the raw materials and stirring in a                predetermined ratio to prepare a peanut shell                composite medium. The culture bottle is filled with                the peanut shell composite medium. The liquid                strain is connected to the cultivation bottle and                moved to the breeding room to bud. The flower buds                are subjected to light suppression and wind                suppression treatment. The harvest height is                reached when the Flammulina velutipes grows to                15-16 cm and harvested.
   USE - Peanut shell composite medium for use in                industrial cultivation of edible fungi.
   ADVANTAGE - The medium has solves the problem of the                increase in the price of the main raw material for                cultivation as an edible fungus, the use of wood                chips widely used in the cultivation of edible                fungi easily causes environmental problem and the                risk of enrichment of pesticides and heavy metals                in cottonseed hulls in low cost, safe and abundant                nutrient-rich materials instead of raw                materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A peanut shell composite medium comprises 3-10                pts. wt. peanut shells, 30-40 pts. wt. maize cob,                30-40 pts. wt. rice bran, 10-15 pts. wt. bran, 3-10                pts. wt. beet pulp, 3-10 pts. wt. dried bean dregs,                3-10 pts. wt. beer grains, 3-10 pts. wt. soybean                hulls, 1-3 pts. wt. shell powder and 0.1-2 pts. wt.                light calcium carbonate. The medium is prepared by                mixing peanut shell, maize cob, rice bran, bran,                beet pulp, dried bean dregs, beer lees, soybean                hulls, shell powder and light calcium carbonate and                stirring in a predetermined ratio to prepare a                peanut shell composite medium. The culture bottle                is filled with the peanut shell composite medium,                which is autoclaved and cooled to room temperature.                The liquid strain is connected to the cultivation                bottle through the automatic inoculation machine                and cultured in the culture chamber, where the                culture temperature is 13-15 degrees C, the air                humidity is 60-90% and the culture in 20-23 days.                The cultured hyphae is overgrown with the                cultivation bottle and the bacteria are treated,                the old hyphae of the dough is removed and                mechanical stimulation is formed, the cultivation                bottle is moved to the breeding room to bud and the                dough is covered with mushroom buds after 4-6 days.                The flower buds are subjected to light suppression                and wind suppression treatment after the end of the                bud that the carbon dioxide concentration is                controlled at 3000-20000 ppm and the gradient is                cooled to 4-5 degrees C, the mushroom buds grown                into mushroom buds when the mushroom bud grows out                of the bottle mouth by 1-2 cm that the carbon                dioxide concentration is increased to 10,000 ppm or                more and the elongation of the mushroom stipe is                promoted. The harvest height is reached when the                Flammulina velutipes grows to 15-16 cm and                harvested.

PN CN109248175-A
TI Cell surface marker specific enriched human periodontal ligament stem cell microcapsule used to prepare regenerative medicine to treat bone diseases comprises human periodontal ligament stem cells and cell surface marker e.g. SB-10/CD166.
AU XU S
AE NANJING TAISHENG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2019108376
AB    NOVELTY - Cell surface marker specific enriched human periodontal ligament stem cell microcapsule comprises a core and a shell, human periodontal ligament stem cells (from 19-29 years old), cell surface marker including cluster of differentiation (CD)18, STRO-1, CD106/vascular cell adhesion protein 1 (VCAM)-1, CD146/melanoma-associated antigen (MUC)-18, HOP-26, CD49A/integrin beta 1 and SB-10/CD166. The microspheres are less than 500 mu m in diameter. The microcapsule material is collagen, alginate, chitosan and/or hyaluronic acid.
   USE - The microcapsule is useful for preparing regenerative medicine and tissue engineering materials for treating bone diseases (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the cell surface marker specific enriched human periodontal ligament stem cell microcapsule, comprising (a) dissolving alginate in phosphate buffered saline (PBS), preparing into 1-3% alginate solution, filtering via 0.22 mu m filter, and storing in portions, (b) culturing CD18-positive periodontal ligament stem cells to the fourth generation, subjecting to trypsinization, centrifuging at 900 revolutions/minute for 5 minutes and washing the cells once with PBS, (c) re-suspending the CD18-positive periodontal ligament stem cells with an alginate solution, adjusting the cell concentration to (1-2)x 106/ml, (d) adding alginate solution containing the cells into the dish containing 0.1-0.2M calcium chloride solution through a channel of a horizontal and vertical two-channel microfluidic device in a drop wise manner to form cell microspheres, where the channel flows laterally, to intercept the alginate solution, and controlling the size of the cell microspheres, (e) incubating the microspheres at 37 degrees C for 15-45 minutes until completely crosslinked, (f) coating the alginate cell microspheres in (0.1-1)% chitosan solution for 15-45 minutes, (g) incubating in 1-5 mg/ml aqueous solution of genipin for 1-5 hours at 37 degrees C to complete the crosslinking to prepare cell microcapsules, (h) washing and collecting the cell microcapsules with alpha -minimum essential medium, inoculating 10-20% fetal bovine serum, 50-200 mu M ascorbic acid 2-phosphate, 2mM glutamine, 100 U/ml penicillin, 100 mu g/ml streptomycin in alpha -MEM medium, placing in an incubator at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide and (i) observing the shape and diameter of the formed cell microcapsules using an optical microscope;
   (2) dry detection of human periodontal ligament stem cell microcapsule, comprising (l) collecting cell microcapsules, inoculating with 10-20% fetal bovine serum, 50-200 mu M ascorbic acid 2-phosphate, 2 mM glutamine, 100 U/ml penicillin, 100 mu g/ml streptomycin in alpha -MEM medium and incubating in an incubator at 37 degrees C under 5% carbon dioxide, (m) after 24-48 hours of cultivation, replacing with osteogenic induction medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum, 100 U/ml penicillin, 100 mu g/ml streptomycin, 0.05-0.5 mM dexamethasone, 10-100 mg/ml vitamin C and 5-15 mM beta -phosphoglycerol in alpha -MEM medium, changing the fluid every 3-4 days, (n) fixing the cell microcapsules with 4% paraformaldehyde after induction of culture for 4 weeks at room temperature for 30 minutes, (o) placing in PBS for 15 minutes, (p) dehydrating, paraffin embedding, slicing, dewaxing and rehydration, (q) placing the cell microcapsule tissue section in 2% alizarin red solution, staining at room temperature for 5 minutes, (r) washing with tap water and counterstaining with hematoxylin and (s) placing a coverslip for microscopic examination; and
   (3) use of human periodontal ligament stem cell microcapsule comprising preparing regenerative medicine and tissue engineering materials for bone diseases.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The preparation steps of the CD18-positive periodontal ligament stem cells cultured to the fourth generation comprises (i) collecting normal adult male wisdom teeth and immediately placing in 4 degrees C pre-cooled 50-300 U/ml penicillin-containing, 50-300 mu g/ml streptomycin-containing and 1-5 mu g/ml amphotericin B-containing alpha -MEM medium, transferring to a clean laboratory at 2-8 degrees C, where the age of the adult is 19-29 years, and the time interval from the collection to the start of extraction of periodontal ligament stem cells does not exceed 72 hours, (ii) transferring to a biological safety cabinet, taking out the wisdom tooth to a culture dish, washing with 4 degrees C pre-cooled sterile phosphate buffer and washing the outer surface of the PBS, (iii) gently peeling off residual periodontal ligament tissue from the surface of the root, (iv) placing the periodontal ligament tissue in PBS containing 1-10 mg/ml type I collagenase and 1-10 mg/ml dispase, and digesting at 37 degrees C for 1 hour, (v) filtering the digestive juice through 70 mu m cell sieve to obtain a single cell suspension containing human periodontal ligament stem cells, centrifuging at 900 revolutions/minute for 5 minutes, and washing the cells once with PBS, (vi) re-suspending the cells in PBS, adding CD18 monoclonal antibody, mixing, incubatnig at room temperature for 30 minutes in the dark, (vii) washing, centrifuging at 900 revolutions/minute for 5 minutes, and re-suspending the cells with PBS, (viii) obtaining CD18-positive stem cell subset by flow cytometry sorting or immunomagnetic bead sorting, and (ix) changing the solution every 3-4 days, sub-culturing the cells to 1:3 after the cells are 90% confluent, and using the cells for preparing the microspheres. The use method comprises transferring the CD18 gene into stromal stem cells by transgenic technology and preparing regenerative medicine and tissue engineering materials for treating bone diseases. The transgenic technology is mediated by retroviruses.Preferred Components: The microcapsule uses CD18 as a cell surface marker. The CD18 is labeled with a fluorescent label or a magnetic bead. The microcapsule core material is alginate. The microcapsule shell material is chitosan film, which is covalently crosslinked. The stromal stem cell source is teeth, fat, bone marrow, umbilical cord, placenta or cord blood.
   ACTIVITY - Osteopathic.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. TI Heat insulation layer comprises methyl vinyl silicon rubber, methyl vinyl phenyl silicon rubber, white carbon black and hydroxy silicone oil.
AU PANG L
   LIU D
   XU X
   YAO L
   JIANG W
   HU J
   BAI Y
   YI J
   LIU W
   HAO Y
   LU Y
   LONG S
   WU C
   SU Q
   XU W
AE SICHUAN RUITIE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CREN-C)
   UNIV SOUTHWEST JIAOTONG (UYSJ-C)
GA 201900888X
AB    NOVELTY - Heat insulation layer comprises 200-240 pts. wt. methyl vinyl silicon rubber or methyl vinyl phenyl silicon rubber, 85-90 pts. wt. white carbon black and 3-5 pts. wt. hydroxy silicone oil.
   USE - Used as heat insulation layer.
   ADVANTAGE - The insulation layer has rigid structure, long service life and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMA are also included for:
   (1) preparing heat insulation layer comprising (i) placing the white carbon black in a vacuum oven, and vacuum-drying at 120 degrees C for 3 hours and removing the moisture in the white gray black to obtain a dry white carbon black, (ii) sequentially adding methyl vinyl silicone rubber or methyl vinyl phenyl silicone rubber, white carbon black of step (i) and hydroxy silicone oil in to the internal mixer, rotating clockwise at a speed of 80-100 revolutions/min and a temperature of 100-110 degrees C, and kneading for 15 minutes to obtain a first mixture, taking out the first mixture from the internal mixer and allowing to stand, (iii) placing the first mixture obtained in step (ii) in an internal mixer, sealing and kneading for 15 minutes in a counterclockwise rotation speed of 80-100 revolutions/minute and a temperature of 100-110 degrees C to obtain a second mixture, (iv) placing the second mixture obtained in step (iii) in a vacuum oven, vacuum heat treating at 100-110 degrees C for 3 hours, cooling, placing in an internal mixer and adding with dicumyl peroxide vulcanizing agent, rotated clockwise, smelting the mixture at a speed of 25-35 revolutions/minutes for 8 minutes to obtain a third mixture, (v) taking out the third mixture obtained in step (iv), cooling, placing in an internal mixer, and kneading in a counterclockwise rotation speed of 25-35 revolutions/minute for 8 minutes to obtain a silicone rubber compound, and (v) placing the silicone rubber compound obtained in step (v) in a flat vulcanizing machine and pressing at 125?OC, 10 MPa for 5-8 minutes;
   (2) blowing type layered complex elastic cushion comprising a micro-porous foam material, installed between the track and the concrete pillow, and a middle cushion layer of the rectangular structure and jointed on the middle cushion coat. The lower insulating layer is for insulating the middle cushion layer and an upper heat insulating layer. The lower insulating layer and an upper heat insulating layer are prepared by the method mentioned above; and
   (3) foaming process for a foamed layered complex elastic block comprising foamed layered complex elastic block comprises a rigid rectangular body between a rigid rail and a concrete pillow, an intermediate cushion layer of the structure, and a lower thermal insulation layer and an upper thermal insulation layer attached to the intermediate cushion layer for the insulation of the intermediate cushion layer, the foaming process comprises (a) placing the upper thermal insulation layer, the intermediate cushion layer and the lower thermal insulation layer and bonding from top to bottom in a high pressure reaction vessel, and introducing low pressure carbon dioxide and filling with a high pressure reaction and draining the air in the autoclave, (b) introducing high-pressure carbon dioxide of 20-28 MPa into the high-pressure reaction kettle, permeating and swelling for 3 hours, stationary, and releasing pressure to obtain a foaming intermediate product, and (c) placing the foamed intermediate product in a blast oven, drying at 170 degrees C for 30-40 minutes and heat treating at 200-250 degrees C for 3 hours.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the blowing type layered complex elastic cushion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The heat insulation layer comprises 220 pts. wt. methyl vinyl silicon rubber or methyl vinyl phenyl silicon rubber, 88 pts. wt. white carbon black and 4 pts. wt. hydroxy silicone oil. The elastic cushion further comprises from up to down orderly penetrating static rigidity adjusting hole for heat preservation and heat insulation layer, middle layer and the lower heat insulating layer is set. The elastic cushion further comprises from up to down in turn upper heat insulating layer, the middle cushion layer and the lower heat insulating layer and with the two nail mounting hole of the screw spike hole corresponding on the pillow. The elastic cushion further comprises from up to down orderly runs through the heat insulating layer, the middle cushion layer and the lower heat insulating layer, and arc plane of connecting with any of the spike mounting hole. Preferred Components: The arc radius of the arc-shaped surface is 4.5 10mm. The static rigidity adjusting hole is 30~120 mm in diameter. The thickness of the upper insulating layer is 1-3mm. The thickness of the middle cushion layer is 10-14mm. The thickness of lower insulating layer is 1-3 mm.

PN CN109010204-A
TI Papaya perfume contains Caozhou papaya essential oil, neroli essential oil, Verbena officinalis essential oil, Ocimum gratissimum essential oil, plant essence extract, traditional Chinese herbal extract, citric acid and sodium alginate.
AU BI X
   GUO P
AE UNIV DALIAN MINZU (UYND-C)
GA 2018A5509C
AB    NOVELTY - A papaya perfume comprises 13-20 pts. wt. Caozhou papaya essential oil, 8 pts. wt. neroli essential oil, 4 pts. wt. Verbena officinalis essential oil, 3 pts. wt. Ocimum gratissimum essential oil, 70 pts. wt. orange leaf tincture, 12 pts. wt. plant essence extract, 8 pts. wt. traditional Chinese herbal extract, 4 pts. wt. citric acid and 10 pts. wt. sodium alginate. The papaya perfume is prepared by adding Caozhou papaya essential oil, neroli essential oil, V.officinalis essential oil and O.gratissimum basil oil to an aqueous solution of sodium alginate, mixing uniformly, then adding the plant essence extract and traditional Chinese herbal extract under mixing, finally adding the orange leaf tincture and citric acid, leaving still for 48 hours, placing the mixture in a dark bottle, and sealing for 7 weeks.
   USE - Papaya perfume.
   ADVANTAGE - The papaya perfume has delicate fragrance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A papaya perfume comprises 13-20 pts. wt. Caozhou papaya essential oil, 8 pts. wt. neroli essential oil, 4 pts. wt. Verbena officinalis essential oil, 3 pts. wt. Ocimum gratissimum essential oil, 70 pts. wt. orange leaf tincture, 12 pts. wt. plant essence extract, 8 pts. wt. traditional Chinese herbal extract, 4 pts. wt. citric acid and 10 pts. wt. sodium alginate. The papaya perfume is prepared by: (1) adding sodium alginate to distilled water, and stirring while heating to obtain an aqueous solution of sodium alginate; (2) drying Caozhou papaya with skin, neroli flower petal, V.officinalis and O.gratissimum leaves, followed by pulverizing into 20 mesh particles, carrying out supercritical extraction using carbon dioxide at a temperature of 38 degrees C under a pressure of 30 MPa for 150 minutes, and respectively collecting Caozhou papaya essential oil, neroli essential oil, V.officinalis essential oil and O.gratissimum essential oil; (3) mixing jujube kernels, lily, Lotus seeds, Schisandra chinensis, tangerine peel, Acanthopanax senticosus, Ganoderma lucidum and Codonopsis pilosula in a mass ratio of 1:3:3:2:1:1:4:2, pulverizing into 30 mesh granules, and carrying out supercritical extraction with carbon dioxide at a temperature of 58 degrees C under a extraction pressure of 40 MPa for 150 minutes, and collecting the traditional Chinese herbal medicine extract; (4) mixing chamomile, Rosa rugosa, orange peel, Camellia japonica, grape seed, apple seed, pear seed and pomegranate seed in the same mass ratio to obtain a mixed powder, adding 100 times of purified water to the mixed powder, carrying out ultrasonic-assisted extraction at a temperature of 10 degrees C and ultrasonic power of 500 W for 10 minutes, transferring the ultrasonically treated liquid to a distillation pot, continuously distilling at a temperature of 75 degrees C under a pressure of 3 kPa for 2 hours, increasing the temperature to 180 degrees C, distilling the aromatic vegetable oil-water mixed solution, condensing the aromatic vegetable oil-water mixed solution through a condenser, where the inlet temperature of the condenser is 150 degrees C, and the outlet temperature of the condenser is 60 degrees C, and then separating by a water separator to obtain a plant essence extract containing essential oils; (5) cutting the dried orange leaves into 2 mm pieces, putting the pieces into a 250 ml conical flask, adding 100 times of medical alcohol, sealing the mixture and protecting from light for 20 days to obtain orange leaf tincture; and (6) adding Caozhou papaya essential oil, neroli essential oil, V.officinalis essential oil and O.gratissimum basil oil to the sodium alginate aqueous solution, mixing uniformly, then adding the plant essence extract, traditional Chinese herbal extract under mixing, finally adding the orange leaf tincture and citric acid, leaving still for 48 hours, placing the mixture in a dark bottle, and sealing for 7 weeks.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The papaya perfume comprises 15 pts. wt. Caozhou papaya essential oil.

PN CN108821303-A; CN108821303-B
TI Comprehensive utilization of boric sludge            comprises e.g. grinding the boric sludge, fully mixing            pretreated boric sludge with anhydrous magnesium            chloride, calcining in furnace, and magnetically            selecting iron in the calcined boron sludge.
AU LU C
   MA Y
   GAO F
AE DALIAN DEETOP ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (DALI-Non-standard)
GA 2018919266
AB    NOVELTY - Comprehensive utilization of boric sludge                comprises e.g. (1) grinding the boric sludge so the                proportion of the 120-200 meshes boric sludge is                above 95%, and obtaining the pretreated boric                sludge; (2) fully mixing the resulting pretreated                boric sludge with anhydrous magnesium chloride                according to a mass ratio of 1:1.6-1.8, and placing                into a suspension calcination furnace for low                temperature calcination to obtain the low                temperature calcined boric sludge; (3) magnetically                selecting iron in the calcined boron sludge, the                magnetic field strength is 350-500 mT, and the iron                recovery rate is between 70-85%, and obtaining                de-ironing boric sludge; (4) after the iron                removal, subjecting the boric sludge to high                temperature calcination through a suspension                calciner, and (5) after the high temperature                calcination, sintering at high temperature after                the boric sludge with an ammonium salt solution                into the ammonia evaporating pot, by steam indirect                heating to carry out chemical reaction.
   USE - The method is useful for comprehensive                utilization of boric sludge.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can utilize the boric sludge to                prepare high-purity magnesium oxide, has high                magnesium recovery rate, realize the complete                comprehensive utilization of the boric sludge, and                realizes recycling of the carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Comprehensive utilization of boric sludge                comprises (1) grinding the boric sludge so the                proportion of the 120-200 meshes boric sludge is                above 95%, and obtaining the pretreated boric                sludge; (2) fully mixing the resulting pretreated                boric sludge with anhydrous magnesium chloride                according to a mass ratio of 1:1.6-1.8, and placing                into a suspension calcination furnace for low                temperature calcination to obtain the low                temperature calcined boric sludge; (3) magnetically                selecting iron in the calcined boron sludge, the                magnetic field strength is 350-500 mT, and the iron                recovery rate is between 70-85%, and obtaining the                de-ironed boric sludge; (4) after the iron removal                in step (3), subjecting the boric sludge to high                temperature calcination through a suspension                calciner to obtain the high temperature calcined                boric sludge, (5) after the high temperature                calcination, sintering at high temperature after                the boric sludge with an ammonium salt solution                into the ammonia evaporating pot, by steam indirect                heating to carry out chemical reaction and to                obtain the steamed ammonia slurry; (6) filtering                the steamed slurry, and obtaining the magnesium                salt leaching solution and the filter residue; (7)                adding the step (6) filter residue to sodium                carbonate and carbon dioxide for leaching and                filtration to obtain a filtrate and a filter                residue; (8) evaporating the resulting filtrate,                crystallizing, and separating to obtain crude                borax; (9) performing acidolysis of resulting                residue, and filtering to obtain filtrate and                silicon slag, (10) extracting the resulting                filtrate, and adjusting the pH to 8-10 to obtain a                magnesium-containing mixed liquid; (11) filtering                the magnesium-containing mixed solution to obtain a                filtrate and a filter residue; (12) using the                resulting filter residue as a building filler; (13)                mixing the magnesium salt leaching solution                obtained In the step (6), and filtrate obtained in                the step (12), adding concentrated aqueous ammonia                and a precipitating agent for decomposition                reaction, precipitating, and filtering to obtain a                filtrate and a magnesium hydroxide filter cake; and                (14) returning the step (7) filtrate to the ammonia                tank of the step (5), and drying and burning the                magnesium hydroxide cake of the step (7) to obtain                the light magnesium oxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (2), low                  temperature calcining temperature is 550-700                  degrees C and the calcining time is 10-30                  minutes. In the step (4), high temperature                  calcining temperature is 1500-1650 degrees C and                  the calcining time is 1-2 hours. the step (2)                  low-temperature calcination and the step (4)                  high-temperature calcination of the                  high-temperature exhaust gas for heat exchange,                  the steam cycle is used for the steaming process,                  and the carbon dioxide is subjecting to a                  compression cycle for the leaching process. In                  the step (5), the control index of the steaming                  process is: (a) steam pressure greater than or                  equal to 0.2MPa; (b) reaction temperature 105-115                  degrees C; (c) reaction pressure is 0-0.01MPa;                  (d) steaming ammonia time 1-3 hours, and (e) the                  solid-liquid ratio of boric sludge powder and                  ammonium salt solution is 50-100 g/l. In the step                  (7), the leaching temperature is 120-150 degrees                  C, the leaching time is 2-4 hours, the leaching                  pressure is 0.5-0.8 MPa, the solid-liquid ratio                  is 1:1.5-2, and the sodium carbonate                  concentration is 15-30%. In the step (8), the                  evaporation temperature is 105-115 degrees C, the                  crystallization temperature is 24-26 degrees C,                  and the crystallization time is 1-3 hours. In the                  step (13), the ratio of the concentrated aqueous                  ammonia to the magnesium-containing solution is                  2-4:1, the precipitating agent is sodium                  hydroxide, and the reaction precipitation time is                  8-12 hours. Preferred Components: In the step (),                  the acid solution is any one of hydrochloric                  acid, sulfuric acid and aqua regia (vol ratio of                  hydrochloric acid to nitric acid is 3:1). In the                  step (14), the drying temperature is 105 degrees                  C, the burning temperature is 650-750 degrees C,                  and the generated high-temperature exhaust gas is                  used for drying.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).); P41 (Crushing: centrifuging, separating solids (B02, B03, B04).)

PN CN108624555-A
TI Preparing stem cell natural secreted polypeptide active additive, by cutting umbilical cord, adding phosphate-buffered saline, adding medium containing fetal bovine, aspirating, dispensing, and adding fetal bovine medium.
AU ZHOU B
   ZHAO Q
AE ZHEJIANG YINZHAOAI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2018823136
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing stem cell natural secreted polypeptide active additive, involves collecting umbilical cord tissue of more than 20 cm at 2-3 cm near the end of the placenta, cutting umbilical cord into small pieces of 2 cm, adding phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) containing 2% double-antibody to the disposable culture dish, adding washed umbilical cord into culture dish, washing residual blood, adding umbilical cord with the blood vessel into the glass culture dish, cutting the umbilical cord using scissors, adding the medium containing the fetal bovine, aspirating paste-like umbilical cord tissue, dispensing into several culture flasks, and adding fetal bovine medium to the culture flask.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing stem cell natural secreted polypeptide active additive (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method forms good microenvironment perfect absorption of skin cell.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing stem cell natural secreted polypeptide active additive, involves collecting umbilical cord tissue of more than 20 cm at 2-3 cm near the end of the placenta, cutting umbilical cord into small pieces of 2 cm, adding phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) containing 2% double-antibody to the disposable culture dish, adding washed umbilical cord into culture dish, washing residual blood, adding umbilical cord with the blood vessel into the glass culture dish, cutting the umbilical cord using scissors, adding the medium containing the fetal bovine, aspirating paste-like umbilical cord tissue, dispensing into several culture flasks, and adding fetal bovine medium to the culture flask. The umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells are cultured by adding medium containing fetal bovine in each culture bottle for 7 days, noting that the culture bottle cannot stand sideways when adding liquid, inserting pipette into the culture bottle, slowly adding to avoid tissue movement, obtaining mesenchymal stem cells (MSC) cells, adding the medium to continue culture, changing the whole amount of liquid for 3 days, adding tissue pieces that has not been removed with a pipette, digesting with trypsin, and combining the cells of the two culture flasks into one bottle. The stem cell passage step involves aspirating medium when the degree of cell fusion reaches 85-90%, washing with PBS, adding trypsin to the culture flask, pipetting the cell suspension in 15 ml centrifuge tube, centrifuging at 1000 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 5 minutes, adding supernatant, resuspending in 5 ml medium, centrifuging, resuspending the cells by adding 7 ml of the medium, mixing, adding the mixture to the T-75 flask, adding the mixture into incubator to continue culture, obtaining secreted cell polypeptide component of P3, P4, and P5 generations in the vigorous growth phase. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) masking liquid comprising 10-13 pts. wt. lavender extract powder, 3-5 pts. wt. hyaluronic acid, 5-8 pts. wt. glycerol, 6-9 pts. wt. vitamin E, 7-10 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol (PEG)-40, 89-93 pts. wt. hydrogenated castor oil and de-ionized water; and
   (2) method for preparing a masking liquid of polypeptide, involves obtaining lavender extract by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process, collecting fresh lavender, air-drying, crushing into powder, adding lavender powder into extraction tank, introducing carbon dioxide at 6.5 MPa and 4 degrees C for 1.5 hours, discharging the extract from the analytical tank, adding other raw materials into the mixture, cooling the mixture in stirring vessel under static conditions, reducing the temperature to 40 degrees C, adding stem cell natural secreted polypeptide active additive and collagen to the mixture and stirring the mixture at 500 revolutions per minute (rpm).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The stirring speed is t, which satisfies the quantitative relationship t is 500 rpm.

AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN DESIGN & RES INST CO (UYDB-C)
GA 201834468A
AB    NOVELTY - Utilization of low-grade aluminum material comprises mixing raw materials of sodium ferrite, aluminum-containing materials and low grade lime to prepare leaching material and adding sodium ferrite material; mixing caustic alkali solution and material to be leached, leach-reacting, and filtering; adding lime or lime milk and reacting; filtering and washing solid with hot water; mixing polymer solution, and heating tricalcium aluminate washing liquid to evaporate to sodium hydroxide; mixing evaporation mother liquor and tricalcium aluminate filter cake and reacting; filtering calcium carbonate slurry, washing solid with hot water, and filtering; mixing calcium carbonate filtrate, calcium carbonate washing liquid and caustic liquor, adding carbon dioxide, and carbonation decomposing; filtering, washing filter cake, and drying; and passing carbon fraction mother liquor and aluminum hydroxide slurry through high pressure steam, filtering, and returning mother liquor to sixth step.
   USE - Method for utilizing low-grade aluminum material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces aluminum ultra-white aluminum hydroxide and calcium iron garnet and recycles by-products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Utilization of low-grade aluminum material comprises:
   (A) mixing sodium ferrite, aluminum-containing materials and low grade lime to prepare leaching material and adding sodium ferrite material, where mass ratio of iron oxide to alumina is 0.4-0.6, ratio of lime to calcium oxide and alumina is 0.6-0.8, and low-grade aluminum-containing raw material is e low-grade bauxite, red mud, fly ash or nepheline having alumina content of 70%;
   (B) mixing caustic alkali solution and material to be leached at liquid to solid ratio of 4.0-5.0, reacting and leaching at 230-275 degrees C for 0.5-1.5 hours, and filtering to obtained residue and leachate, where caustic alkali solution is 220-280 g/L sodium hydroxide;
   (C) adding lime or lime milk to leachate and reacting at 60-80 degrees C for 1.0-3.0 hours to get aluminate slurry, where mass of lime milk is 12-18 wt.%;
   (D) filtering to obtain filtrate and solid phase and washing solid phase with 0.5-1.5 times hot water of 80-95 degrees C to obtained tricalcium aluminate, tricalcium cake washing liquid, and polymer solution as filtrate;
   (E) mixing polymer solution, heating tricalcium aluminate washing liquid to evaporate to sodium hydroxide, and returning 90-95% caustic liquor to first step;
   (F) mixing tricalcium aluminate filter cake with evaporation mother liquor, reacting at 160-200 degrees C for 0.5-2.0 hours to obtain calcium carbonate slurry, where evaporation mother liquor contains 100-200 g/L sodium carbonate and 10-20 g/L sodium hydroxide and mass ratio of evaporated mother liquor and tricalcium aluminate filter cake is 4-5;
   (G) filtering calcium carbonate slurry to obtain second filtrate and solid phase, washing solid with 0.5-1.5 times 80-95 degrees C hot water, and filtering to get calcium carbonate filter cake, calcium carbonate slurry, and calcium carbonate filtrate;
   (H) mixing calcium carbonate filtrate, calcium carbonate washing liquid and caustic liquor, adding carbon dioxide, and performing carbonation decomposition;
   (I) filtering slurry to obtain tertiary solid and carbon fraction mother liquor, washing with 0.5-1.5 times 80-95 degrees C hot water to obtain aluminum hydroxide filter cake and aluminum hydroxide slurry, washing drying filter cake, and drying; and
   (J) passing carbon fraction mother liquor and aluminum hydroxide slurry to high pressure steam, filtering, and returning mother liquor to sixth step.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The low-grade aluminum-containing material feedstock contains less than or equal to 2% aqueous aluminum hydroxide and less than or equal to 0.6% alkali.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The second step is done by washing leach residue with 0.5-1.5 times 80-95 degrees C hot water. The seventh step further comprises calcining calcium carbonate cake at 800-1050 degrees C for 1-3 hours to give lime; adding 90-100 degrees C hot water and digesting to get lime milk. The tenth step is done by washing filter cake with sodium carbonate, mixing with rust or iron ore, and calcining for 30-120 minutes at 800-1000 degrees C to obtain starting material in first step.
