PN CN107843725-A
TI Detecting chewing gum type tobacco by culturing human oral keratinocytes, adding trypsin solution, digesting cells, adding oral keratinocyte medium, centrifuging cell suspension, staining cells and counting micronucleus rate.
AU GUAN Y
   YAO J
   LI X
   XU Y
   LU S
   GAO Q
   MI Q
   ZHU Z
AE CHINA TOBACCO YUNNAN IND CO LTD (CNTA-C)
GA 2018251959
AB    NOVELTY - Method for detecting chewing gum type tobacco involves (a) adding chewing gum type tobacco into oral keratinocyte medium (OKM), grinding and filtering the medium, and adding serum, (b) preparing single cell suspension by culturing human oral keratinocytes under flow of carbon dioxide, adding trypsin solution and digesting cells, (c) adding OKM medium and test sample into test sample group, adding cyclophosphamide solution and centrifuging cell suspension, or (d) adding methanol and glacial acetic acid, staining cells and counting micronucleus rate.
   USE - The method is useful for detecting chewing gum type tobacco.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for detecting chewing gum type tobacco involves (a) adding 1 g chewing gum type tobacco into 10 ml oral keratinocyte medium (OKM), grinding medium at 37 degrees C for 10 minutes, filtering the medium, and adding 10 vol.% serum to filtrate, (b) preparing single cell suspension by culturing human oral keratinocytes at 37 degrees C in a incubator under flow of 5% carbon dioxide, when cell confluence rate reaches 80-90%, washing keratinocytes twice using phosphate buffer solution having pH of 7.2, discarding washing solution, adding 1-2 ml of 0.25 weight/volume% trypsin solution into 25 cm2 culture area, digesting cells for 1-2 minutes, suspending cells in OKM medium, calculating concentration of single cell suspension by hemocytometer counting method, calculating number of live cells in each ml of single cell suspension, diluting the single cell suspension to 1-1.5x 105 cells/ml using the OKM medium, inoculating 200 mu l/well diluted suspension into cell culture plate, incubating the culture plate at 37 degrees C for 24 hours, (c) dividing the culture solution into cell control group, positive control group and test sample group, adding 2000 mu l OKM medium into each well of cell control group and the positive control group, adding 2000 mu l OKM medium and 0-60 vol.% test sample into wells of test sample group in different doses, adding 6 mu l of 1 mg/ml cyclophosphamide solution into cell control group, positive control group and sample group, again adding 0.5 mu l of 1 mg/ml cyclophosphamide solution into positive control group, incubating the groups at 37 degrees C for 24 hours, digesting cells, centrifuging cell suspension, removing supernatant, adding 3 ml of 0.075 mol/l potassium chloride solution and blowing the cells, (d) adding 2 ml mixed solution comprising methanol and glacial acetic acid (4:1), centrifuging suspension, dropwise adding 0.5 ml mixed solution and blowing the cells, adding cell suspension on a glass slide, drying suspension, staining cells for 15 minutes using Giemsa stain, washing and drying the slide, and observing at least 1000 binucleated cells in the slide and counting micronucleus rate, and (e) comparing micronucleus rate of positive control group and cell control group, if there is no significant difference between micronucleus rate of positive control group and cell control group, then the test is invalid, or if there is significant difference, then the test is valid, when the test is valid, comparing micronucleus rate of test sample group and cell control group, if there is significant difference, then there is dose-response relationship between dose and micronucleus rate of the test sample group, result is positive and the chewing gum type tobacco is genetically toxic to cells and affects the micronucleus rate of cells, or if there is significant difference, then the result is negative and the chewing gum type tobacco is not genetically toxic to cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves adding 1 g chewing gum type tobacco into 10 ml OKM, grinding medium at 37 degrees C and 30 revolutions/minute for 10 minutes, filtering the medium using qualitative filter paper and 0.45 mu m organic filter membrane, and adding 10 vol.% serum to filtrate. The step (c) involves adding 2000 mu l OKM medium and 0-60 vol.% test sample into wells of test sample group in different doses of 7.5%, 15%, 30% and 60%, adding 6 mu l of 1 mg/ml cyclophosphamide solution into cell control group, positive control group and sample group, again adding 0.5 mu l of 1 mg/ml cyclophosphamide solution into positive control group, incubating the groups at 37 degrees C for 24 hours, digesting cells, centrifuging cell suspension at 1000 revolutions/minute for 5 minutes, removing supernatant, adding 3 ml of 0.075 mol/l potassium chloride solution and blowing the cells. Preferred Component: The cell culture plate is 6-well plate.

TI Producing bio-based ferulic acid used for producing ferulic acid derivative, by preparing raw or starting material comprising oryzanol and subjecting g-oryzanol-containing raw material to glyceride hydrolysis process in presence of enzyme.
AU BLANCHARD C O
   SUTTERLIN W R
   LONG R A
AE INVENTURE RENEWABLES INC (INVE-Non-standard)
GA 2021771213
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing bio-based ferulic acid, involves (a) preparing a raw or starting material comprising an oryzanol, optionally g-oryzanol, and optionally, preparing the starting material by drying and adding water and solvent(s), where optionally the raw or starting material comprises or is derived from a plant or microbial (optionally bacterial, algal or fungal) material or byproduct, and optionally the raw or starting material comprises or derived from a rice bran oil, a rice bran soap stock, a waste material, a plant by-product, or a mixture of the rice bran oil, rice bran soap stock, waste material and by-product generated from the manufacture of rice bran oil, and (b) subjecting g-oryzanol-containing raw material to a glyceride hydrolysis process in the presence of an enzyme or alkali or base.
   USE - The method is useful for producing bio-based ferulic acid for producing ferulic acid derivative.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures high purity ferulic acid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing bio-based ferulic acid, involves (a) preparing a raw or starting material comprising an oryzanol, optionally g-oryzanol, and optionally, preparing the starting material by drying and adding water and solvent(s), where optionally the raw or starting material comprises or is derived from a plant or microbial (optionally bacterial, algal or fungal) material or byproduct, and optionally the raw or starting material comprises or derived from a rice bran oil, a rice bran soap stock, a waste material, a plant by-product, or a mixture of the rice bran oil, rice bran soap stock, waste material and by-product generated from the manufacture of rice bran oil, and (b) subjecting g-oryzanol-containing raw material to a glyceride hydrolysis process in the presence of an enzyme or alkali or base, where the step of glyceride hydrolysis process involves (i) mixing the raw or starting material with an enzyme and/or first alkali or base, agitating the resultant mixture, optionally while heating and optionally the raw or starting material and enzyme and/or alkali or base mixture at 20-200 degrees C, preferably 80-100 degrees C, optionally heating for 1-240 minutes or 30-60 minutes to obtain a first hydrolyzed mixture or first saponified mixture and/or one or more derivatives of the first hydrolysis mixture or first saponified mixture, and optionally adjusting the pH to 8-11 and drying the first hydrolyzed mixture or first saponified mixture, (ii) extracting or substantially extracting the first saponified mixture and/or derivatives of the first saponified mixture with a first solvent, so as to remove or substantially remove the materials soluble in the first solvent, creating or generating a first solvent phase, where the derivatives comprise fatty acid soaps with a water content of 0.01-99%, and optionally repeating step (iii) to create multiple solvent phases, where the first solvent comprises water, supercritical carbon dioxide, dichloromethane, chloroform, hexane, heptane, methanol, ethanol, isopropanol, butanol and/or ethyl acetate, (iii) removing or substantially removing the solvent from the first solvent phase to generate a first extract, (iv) mixing the first extract with an enzyme, and, optionally a solvent to effect hydrolysis of (all or substantially all of) the contained oryzanol or mixture of ferulic acid esters of phytosterols and triterpenoids into sterols and ferulate/ferulic acid, so as to create a first ferulic mixture, where optionally, heating the first extract and enzyme mixture (with or without a solvent) to accelerate the oryzanol hydrolysis rate and optionally the extract and enzyme mixture is heated to 20-200 degrees C, preferably 80-100 degrees C for 1-240 minutes, preferably 30-60 minutes, where the solvent comprises water, supercritical carbon dioxide, dichloromethane, chloroform, hexane, heptane, methanol, ethanol, isopropanol, butanol and/or ethyl acetate, (v) adding a basic solution to the hydrolyzed mixture to extract the ferulate/ferulic acid, and generating a first ferulate salt mixture, where the base is added neat or as a solution in water or an aqueous solution or polar solvent comprising methanol, ethanol, propanol and/or butanol with a pH of 7-14, where the first ferulate salt mixture comprises impurities which are not soluble in the basic mixture and can be removed or substantially removed, so as to afford or to generate a first ferulate salt solution and a first ferulate salt impurity, where optionally the first ferulate salt impurity comprises unsaponifiables that can be upgraded or purified, and optionally the unsaponifiables in the first ferulate salt impurity are purified by chromatography, crystallization, liquid-liquid extraction, and/or precipitation, (vi) acidifying the basic ferulate salt solution, optionally acidifying to pH of 0-4, preferably 2-2.5 to precipitate ferulic acid with high purity, optionally to a purity of 70%, 75% 80%, 85% or 90% purity or greater, or a purity of 65-99%, from a now acidified solution, and optionally the acidifying comprises use of an organic acid (and optionally the organic acid comprises formic, acetic, oxalic, citric, lactic, uric, malic or tartaric acid) or an inorganic acid (and optionally the inorganic acid comprises sulfuric, hydrochloric, phosphoric or nitric acid), and (vii) recovering or substantially recovering high purity ferulic acid by filtration, washing to remove residual acidified solution from the ferulic acid, and drying to less than 5%, preferably 1-4% volatile content and affording to obtain the product, where the ferulic acid derivative comprises a bio-based vanillin or 4-hydroxy-3-methoxybenzaldehyde, ethylvanillin, acetovanillone and/or conjugates or derivatives of vanillin, optionally that qualify as naturally produced vanillin, ethylvanillin, acetovanillone and/or conjugates or derivatives of vanillin, and the ferulic acid derivative comprises vanillin. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method or process for manufacturing ferulic acid, involving (1a) preparing or having provided a raw or starting material comprising oryzanol, or gamma -oryzanol or g-oryzanol, or a mixture of ferulic acid esters of phytosterols and triterpenoids optionally comprising cycloartenyl ferulate, 24-methylenecycloartanyl ferulate and/or campesteryl ferulate, where optionally the raw or starting material or the mixture of ferulic acid esters of phytosterols and triterpenoids comprises or derived from a plant material or byproduct, and optionally methods of preparing the raw starting material comprise drying and adding water and solvent(s), and (1b) subjecting the raw or starting material to a hydrolysis process in the presence of an alkali or base.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw or starting material comprises a rice bran soap stock generated from a chemical refining of a crude rice bran oil from a rice bran; an alkaline oil cake generated from a chemical refining of a crude rice bran oil from a rice bran; or a rice bran and/or a derivative of the rice bran. The raw or starting material is a physically refined rice bran oil generated from a physical refining of a crude rice bran oil from a rice bran. The alkali or base comprises an oxide, hydroxide, carbonate and/or bicarbonate. The ion swapping agents comprises reagents comprising calcium, magnesium, aluminum and/or iron. The alcohol solvent comprises a butanol, optionally an n-butanol or an isobutanol. The ferulic acid comprises trans-ferulic acid. The produced ferulic acid is used to manufacture vanillin (or 4-hydroxy-3-methoxybenzaldehyde), ethylvanillin, acetovanillone and/or conjugates or derivatives of vanillin. Preferred Method: The first precipitate mixture is further separated into a first precipitate and a first brine solution. The hydrolysis is conducted in the presence of a solvent, where optionally the solvent comprises at least one alcohol or alcohols. The first solvent phase is subjected to one or more operations comprising evaporation, ion exchange, chromatography and/or crystallization. Preferred Property: The ferulic acid has a purity of 50% or greater, 45-100% or 40-95%, which is produced.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Processing cultivation useful for increasing yield            of Agaricus bisporus comprises e.g. taking wheat straw,            chicken manure and gypsum, mixing, pre-wetting,            building, adding, adjusting, mixing and tempering,            measuring, and primary fermenting.
AU LI J
   XIA X
   PAN A
   WANG W
   ZHANG X
   SUN A
AE JIANGSU FUDE TAIDA AGRIC DEV CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202081108T
AB    NOVELTY - Processing the cultivation comprises e.g. (i)                performing primary fermenting including (a)                preparing the raw material involving taking wheat                straw, chicken manure and gypsum to obtain culture                material, (b) mixing of extract, straw and poultry                manure to building pile including (b1) pre-wetting                wheat straw in soaking tank, using forklift to                repeatedly pushing loose wheat straw into the                dipping pool to mixing and soaking, using forklift                to dumping, taking out materials and building a                pile at material field, and (b2) adding gypsum                powder to chicken manure after conditioning,                building small pile mixing, adjusting water                content, mixing chicken manure and gypsum on mixing                production line with wheat straw piling up with                material, flexibly adjusting according to the water                content of culture material, mixing and tempering,                taking multiple samples to measuring moisture,                nitrogen content, ash, carbon to nitrogen ratio and                pH value, and (c) primary fermenting in the storage                hopper.
   USE - The method is useful for increasing the yield                of Agaricus bisporus (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: gives high mushroom yield and                reduces the problem of mushroom production                revenue.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing the cultivation comprises (i)                performing primary fermenting including (a)                preparing the raw material involving taking 1000 kg                wheat straw, 1400 kg chicken manure and 110 kg                gypsum to obtain culture material, where the water                content of wheat straw is 18%, the nitrogen content                is 0.48%, the water content of chicken manure is                45%, the nitrogen content is 3%, the initial                nitrogen content in the culture feed is 1.6%, and                the carbon to nitrogen ratio is 23:1 and ratio of                manure to straw is 4.8:5.2, (b) mixing of extract,                straw and poultry manure to building a pile                including (b1) pre-wetting the wheat straw in the                soaking tank, using a forklift to repeatedly                pushing the loose wheat straw into the dipping pool                to mixing and soaking for 48 hours, using a                forklift to dumping for 3-4 times for 48 hours,                taking out materials and building a pile at the                material field, and (b2) adding 80% gypsum powder                to 80% chicken manure after conditioning, building                small pile mixing, adjusting the water content to                60-65%, mixing chicken manure and gypsum on the                mixing production line with the wheat straw piling                up with the material, flexibly adjusting according                to the water content of the culture material to                reaching 73-75%, mixing and tempering, taking                multiple samples to measuring the moisture,                nitrogen content, ash, carbon to nitrogen ratio and                pH value, the key quality control data after                tempering are 1.5-1.7% initial nitrogen content,                73-75% moisture, pH value of 8.3-8.5%, and                carbon-nitrogen ratio 23-27:1, and (c) primary                fermenting in the storage hopper including passing                the culture material through the step (b),                immediately using the primary feeding machine into                the storage hopper, carrying out a fermenting,                controlling the environment according to the set                program, quickly rising the material temperature at                70-80 degrees C under suitable conditions, and                starting to dropping the material temperature on                the 4-5th day, (ii) performing secondary fermenting                including (a) exiting the primary fermenting in the                storage hopper and entering the secondary                fermenting room involving opening the storage                hopper, transferring the culture material to                another storage hopper by using feeding machine,                fermenting, adding the remaining 20% chicken manure                and 20% gypsum to the culture material during the                transferring process, restarting fermenting, rising                the material temperature at 70-80 degrees C for 4-5                days, and ending the fermenting in the storage                hopper, (b) performing the secondary fermenting in                tunnel including transporting the culture material                fermented in an excellent storage hopper into the                secondary fermenting tunnel for pasteurizing for                7-9 days, using the throwing head feeding machine                when installing the tunnel to loosely and uniformly                throwing the culture material at the end of the                first phase into a piling of 4 meters wide and                1.8-2.1 meters thick, and the piling density is one                ton per square meter, and (c) seeding and culturing                mycelium including reducing the temperature of the                culture medium at 26-28 degrees C transferring to                the automatic feeding machine in the mushroom room                by using a material transporting vehicle, carrying                out seeding and feeding operations, covering the                surface of the culture material with a layer of                mulching film, and culturing the mycelium, (iii)                performing tertiary fermenting including (a)                culturing of grass-covering carbon soil hypha                including uniformly covering the surface of the                culture material with a layer of peat soil,                controlling the thickness within 3-4 cm, covering                the peat soil, watering the peat soil of the                culture bed surface, the watering amount is 1-1.5                l/m2 per day for 5-7 days, (b) scratching bacteria,                adjusting and cooling stimulation including (b1)                scratching bacteria including harrowing the peat                soil to a thickness of about 2 cm by using harrow                and other tools, when hypha penetrating 70-80% of                the thickness of peat soil, (b2) adjusting                including removing the excess peat soil on the bed                surface with harrow and other tools, reaching the                the hypha growing out of the bed more uniform,                neatly kinking the entire cultivation chamber, and                concentrating picking to laying the foundation for                future unified management, and (b3) watering the                culture material on the culture bed once, the                watering volume is 0.5-0.8 l/m2, controlling the                room temperature at 15-17 degrees C, controlling                the temperature of the culture material below at 22                degrees C, controlling the air humidity at 70-78%,                carbon dioxide concentration 800-1000 ppm, the                cooling process is carried out for 30-38 hours for                3 days, when the diameter of the mushroom bud is                0.5-1.5 cm, watering the cultivation bed at 2.5-3                l/m2 per day, adjusting the moisture content of                peat soil to 77-79%, and pouring the water for                three days, and (c) picking mushrooms in four                batches, taking each batch for 3-4 days, finishing                the each picking of the first three batches,                watering the culture bed surface at 1.5-2 l/m2 per                day for 3-4 days, controlling the room temperature                at 18-20 degrees C, controlling the temperature of                the culture medium at 19-22 degrees C, controlling                air humidity at 65-80%, and carbon dioxide                concentration 800-1500 ppm, and (iv) discharging                including taking out the used culture material,                transferring into novel culture material, and                performing to the next cycle of mushroom                production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: In the step (i) (a), the                  storage of wheat straw in the mushroom plant is                  stacking in square bales, feeding the amount of                  wheat straw used for harvesting and storing in                  each season in batches according to the                  production plan, when feeding, unbaling and                  fluffing the wheat straw, drying the choosing                  chicken manure, crushing the agglomeration when                  the water content of chicken manure is greater                  than 50%, adding loose wine lees to 1/2 of the                  volume for pre-fermenting, tempering chicken                  manure for 4-5 days before use, and making                  chicken manure suitable for water content and                  loose structure to facilitating the mixing. In In                  the step (i), the autoclave is sterilizing at                  120-135 degrees C for 30-40 minutes. In the step                  (ii) (a), the culture material data obtained by                  excellent storage hopper fermenting comprises                  73.5-74.5% moisture, pH value of 8.1-8.2, 750-950                  ppm ammonia, 1.7-1.9% nitrogen content, 25-29 ash                  and carbon-nitrogen ratio 21-26:1. In the step                  (ii) (c), the seeding amount is 0.6-0.8 kg/m2,                  the temperature of the culture medium is                  controlled at 24-26 degrees C, the indoor air                  humidity is controlled at 90%, the carbon dioxide                  concentration is controlled at 5000-11000 ppm,                  culturing for 280-300 hours, and the physical and                  chemical index containing 65-67% moisture,                  2.2-2.4% nitrogen with pH value of 6.4-6.9. In                  the step (iii) (a), before using the peat soil,                  adding calcium carbonate and the ratio is 85%                  peat soil and 15% calcium carbonate, adding                  calcium carbonate and gypsum to increasing the pH                  value, increasing the content of calcium,                  increasing the specific gravity of the soil,                  adjusting the pH value of the peat soil to                  7.5-7.8, adjusting the moisture to 75-78%, and                  the room temperature is controlled at 20-23                  degrees C, the temperature of the culture medium                  is controlled at 24-26 degrees C, the air                  humidity is controlled at 93-95%, and the carbon                  dioxide concentration controlled at 8000-10000                  ppm for the first 4 days, and controlled at                  10000-13000 ppm for the next 4 days. In the step                  (iii), (b2), the room temperature is controlled                  at 25 degrees C, the material temperature is                  controlled below at 27 degrees C, the humidity is                  controlled at 95%, and the concentration of                  carbon dioxide is 15000-16000 ppm for 12-24                  hours. In the step (iii), (b3), the room                  temperature is controlled at 16-18 degrees C, the                  temperature of the culture medium is controlled                  at 20-22 degrees C, the air humidity is                  controlled at 65-70%, and the carbon dioxide                  concentration is 800-1200 ppm.

TI Distillation energy-saving system for carbon monoxide to ethanol, comprises a clear liquid first-stage preheater, a clear liquid secondary preheater, concentrated liquid primary preheater and concentrated liquid secondary preheater.
AU LIU J
   CHU Y
   LI X
   WANG X
AE CHINA LIGHT IND XIAN DESIGN ENG CO LTD (CHLI-Non-standard)
GA 201957358G
AB    NOVELTY - Distillation energy-saving system comprises a clear liquid first-stage preheater (1), a clear liquid secondary preheater (2), a clearing liquid three-stage preheater (3), concentrated liquid primary preheater (4), concentrated liquid secondary preheater (5), first carbon dioxide (CO2) separator (6), crude crucible I (7), rough crucible I condenser (8), scrub column (9), crude alcohol tank (10), crude tower I reboiler (11), composite tower (13), crude tower II reboiler (14), first coarse alcohol preheater (15), second coarse alcohol preheater (16), rectification column I (17), refinery I reboiler (18), fusel oil separator (19), condensation water distributor (20), refined tower II return pump (21), crude alcohol pump (22), refined tower I return pump (23) and refined tower II overfeed pump (24).
   USE - Distillation energy-saving system is used for carbon monoxide to ethanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The distillation energy-saving system adopts reasonable distillation thermal coupling technology and three-stage differential pressure distillation technology, which can fully utilize the energy coupling in the production of alcohol, so that the distillation energy consumption is reduced by about 20% compared with the conventional distillation, and the distillation method is simple, ensuring that the energy between the towers is fully utilized, the energy consumption is low, and the production cost is significantly reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for a distillation energy saving system for carbon monoxide to ethanol, which involves:
   (A) pumping the fermented clear liquid into the first-stage preheater of the clearing liquid and the wine steam from the dewatering section for primary heat exchange, and the wine steam is condensed back to the dewatering section through the cooler;
   (B) sending the first-stage heated clearing liquid to the second preheater of the clearing liquid to carry out secondary heat exchange with the distiller's grains from the bottom of the composite tower, and the wine grains are exchanging and returning to the fermentation section for reuse;
   (C) sending the second-stage heated clear liquid to the clearing liquid three-stage preheater for three-stage heat exchange with the residual water from the bottom of the rectifying tower I;
   (D) entering the CO2 gas separated in the first CO2 separator to the inlet of the bottom of the side wall of the scrubber tower from the top gas outlet, and the gas is washing by adding water to the water supply device in the scrubber tower, and the washed gas is discharging through a vacuum pump, and the washed light wine entering the coarse alcohol can;
   (E) entering sputum after separating the gas in the first CO2 separator to the composite tower, and the wine vapor at the top of the composite tower is heating by the crude tower I reboiler to heat the crude sluice tower I, and the heated alcohol condensate is sending to the composite tower through the rectification pump of the condensate tower II, and top first inlet is reflowing;
   (F) sending the fuse alcohol in the middle outlet of the composite tower to the second crude alcohol preheater via the fine tower II feed pump, and exchanges heat with the steam condensate from the condensate distributor, and the heated fusel wine is sending to the rectification, and the first entrance to the middle of the tower I;
   (G) pumping the fermented concentrated liquid into the concentrated first-stage heat exchanger and the top wine from the top of the crude tower I for heat exchange, and the wine vapor is exchanging and condensing by the crude tower I condenser, and then entering the scrubber is washing and discharging;
   (H) sending the concentrated sputum after the first stage heating to the second preheater of the concentrated liquid and the bottom distiller of the crude sputum I for secondary heat exchange, and the distiller's grains are sending to the feed workshop after heat exchange, and the secondary heated concentrated liquid enters the second CO2 separator;
   (I) entering the CO2 gas separated by the second CO2 separator to the scrubber to be washing and discharging, and the washed liquor is combined with the condensed liquor of the crude tower I condenser, and the condensed crude wine of the concentrated liquid first-stage preheater flows into the first inlet of the coarse alcohol tank, and the second CO2 separator separates the gas and the concentrated liquid enters the crude tower I;
   (J) entering the light wine in the dehydrated light liquor pipeline to the second inlet of the coarse alcohol tank, and the crude alcohol in the coarse alcohol tank passes the crude alcohol, pumping into the first crude alcohol preheater, in the first coarse alcohol preheater, exchanges heat with the steam condensate from the condensate distributor and enters the second inlet of the middle of the distillation column I, and the steam condensed water is returned to the boiler room after heat exchange;
   (K) enetering the top wine vapor of the distillation column I enters the crude tower II reboiler heating composite tower, and the heated alcohol condensate is sending to the second inlet of the top of the composite tower through the refinery I reflux pump, and the other is sent through the bypass pipe. Returning to the upper feed port of the rectification column I;
   (L) enetring the fusel oil of the middle outlet of the distillation column I to the fusel oil separator, and the fusel oil separated in the fusel oil separator is sending to the storage tank, and the light wine of the fusel oil separator enters the coarse alcohol tank; and
   (M) entering the boiler comes to the steam through the steam pipe and entering the refining tower I reboiler to heat the rectification tower I, and the heated steam condensate enters the condensate distributor, and the steam condensate in the condensate distributor heats the coarse alcohol in the first coarse alcohol preheater, the other heats the fuse of the second coarse alcohol preheater, and the heated two-way steam condenses back to the boiler room.

TI Greenhouse cultivating of high quality Ginseng comprises e.g. building water and fertilizer integrated intelligent agricultural greenhouse, installing sprinkler irrigation facilities, sprinkling lime powder on ground, and paving a layer.
AU WANG J
AE WANG J (WANG-Individual)
GA 201934196S
AB    NOVELTY - Greenhouse cultivating of high quality Ginseng comprises (i) building water and fertilizer integrated intelligent agricultural greenhouse, and installing sprinkler irrigation facilities, (ii) sprinkling lime powder on ground, surrounding of agricultural greenhouse in (i), and paving a layer of anti-grass cloth on ground inside agricultural greenhouse after five days of sprinkling lime powder, (iii) selecting peat soil exposed to sun for three days as planting substrate, selecting Ginseng seedlings soaked in 50% carbendazim WP (diluting 1000 times) to liquid for planting, and adjusting temperature, humidity, luminosity, and ventilation conditions in agricultural greenhouses during Ginseng planting, (iv) connecting outlet with agricultural greenhouse by pipeline, arranging purifier on pipeline for passing carbon dioxide generated by straw combustion into agricultural greenhouse, and promoting the growth of Ginseng, and (v) irrigating Ginseng.
   USE - The method is useful for greenhouse cultivating of high quality Ginseng.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has excellent safety, and high quality; is harmless to the human body; promotes the growth of Ginseng; and improves the growth efficiency of Ginseng.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Greenhouse cultivating of high quality Ginseng comprises (i) building water and fertilizer integrated intelligent agricultural greenhouse, and installing sprinkler irrigation facilities, (ii) sprinkling lime powder on ground, surrounding of agricultural greenhouse in (i), and paving a layer of anti-grass cloth on ground inside agricultural greenhouse after five days of sprinkling the lime powder, (iii) selecting peat soil exposed to sun for three days as planting substrate, selecting Ginseng seedlings soaked in 50% carbendazim WP (diluting 1000 times) to liquid for planting, and adjusting temperature, humidity, luminosity, and ventilation conditions in agricultural greenhouses during Ginseng planting, (iv) connecting outlet with agricultural greenhouse through pipeline, arranging purifier on pipeline for passing carbon dioxide generated by straw combustion into agricultural greenhouse, and promoting the growth of Ginseng, and (v) on the basis of (i), (ii), (iii), and (iv), regularly irrigating the Ginseng in agricultural greenhouse for promoting the growth of Ginseng, where the intelligent agricultural greenhouse with integrated water and fertilizer comprises greenhouse framework, transparent thermal insulating top film, and heat preservation wall. The transparent thermal insulating top film is provided on greenhouse framework. The heat preservation wall is provided at left and right ends of greenhouse framework, and is connected with greenhouse frame, the heat preservation wall is provided with vent, and is made of double-layer polyester glass plate, and the double layer is gathered. The ester glass plate is filled with polystyrene insulation board. The water and fertilizer integrated intelligent agricultural greenhouse further comprises rainwater collecting box, box body, motor, stirring unit, adjusting unit, cleaning unit, first air bag, conical plate, and controller. The controller is used to control work of intelligent agricultural greenhouse, and one end of box body is connected to rainwater collecting tank through a pipe. The other end of box body is connected to agricultural greenhouse through the No.1 pipe, and pump is provided at the pipe and No.1 pipe. A set of nozzles is arranged on No.1 pipe in agricultural greenhouse, and the crops in the agricultural greenhouse are sprayed through nozzles. The first air bag is used to reduce friction caused by adjustment unit when adjusting. The number of first airbags is two, two airbags are located in recesses of box body, and one end of two airbags is fixed on side wall of groove, and other end is adjustment unit is connected. The cleaning unit is located outside transparent thermal insulating top film. The cleaning unit is used for cleaning dust on outer surface of transparent thermal insulating top film. The top of box body is provided with feeding port and smoke outlet, and smoke purifier is connected to purifier. The tapered plate is located in box body, and two ends of tapered plate are fixedly attached to side wall of box body. A through hole is formed in tapered plate, and through hole is controlled by electromagnetic switch. The upper surface of tapered plate forms straw burning zone with top of box body. The lower surface of tapered plate forms mixed area with bottom of box body. The ash produced by burning of straw falls into mixing zone through tapered plate. The adjusting unit is provided on the box body. The adjusting unit is for controlling the amount of water delivered by rainwater collecting tank into box body. The stirring unit is rotatably provided on side wall of box body, and connected to motor fixed to outside of box body. The stirring unit is used to accelerate mixing of ash and water, where, adjusting unit comprises fixing column, fixed shaft, buoyancy plate, first rope, first pulley, No.2 pulley, and No.1 board. One side of fixing column is fixedly connected to side wall of box body, and fixing column is provided with groove. The fixed shaft is located in first groove, and fixed shaft is fixed to first groove by a spring. The other end of fixed shaft is fixedly connected to buoyancy plate, in initial state. The water in the box body pushes buoyancy plate to highest position. The highest position of buoyancy plate is maximum amount of water in box body. The first pulley, and second pulley are respectively fixed to inner side wall and outer side wall of box body by rods. One end of first rope is fixedly connected to buoyancy plate. The other end of first rope is fixedly connected to first plate, and transitioned through first pulley and second pulley. The end of first plate is fixed to groove of side plate of rainwater collecting box by a spring. A right angle trapezoidal opening is provided on first plate. The stirring unit comprises first wheel, second wheel, third wheel, first belt, dial, T-shaped stirring plate, No.1 shaft, No.1 mixing plate, rotating column, No.1 screw, No.1 slider, and second rope. The number of the first wheel is two, and two wheels are respectively provided on side wall of box body by rotating the shaft, and rotating shaft of first wheel on left side is fixedly connected to output shaft of motor through a bearing. The first belt is used for transmission of second wheel. The number of second wheel, and third wheel are both two. The second wheel is provided on rotating shaft of first wheel through rotating ring. The third wheel is provided on first wheel shaft.

The second wheel is located between first wheel and third wheel, and the second wheel is provided with groove. TI Separation process for low energy consumption methanol to olefins product gas, comprises e.g. cooling methanol to olefins product gas by quenching with water, treating the product gas, separately oxide and acid gas and post processing.
AU PEI D
   ZHANG Z
AE BEIJING HENGTAI CLEAN ENERGY TECHNOLOGY (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201879955M
AB    NOVELTY - Separation process for low energy consumption methanol to olefins (MTO) product gas, comprises e.g. (i) cooling (MTO) product gas in reactor by quenching with water, then sending into a first stage compression unit and a second stage compression unit of the olefin separation unit, (ii) treating the product gas with secondary compression by compressor, then separately sending the product gas into a oxide removal tower and a alkali washing tower, and separately removing the oxide and acid gas, (iii) compressing the obtained purified compressed gas by a three stage compression unit, and then cooling by a cooler, sending the gas phase material at the top of the tank to the gas phase dryer, pumping the liquid phase material of the bottom of the tank into a liquid phase product gas coalescer to remove part of the water and then sending into a liquid phase dryer, and (iv) drying of the gas phase and liquid phase product gas, cooling and post processing.
   USE - The method is useful for separation process for low energy consumption methanol to olefins product gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method removes moisture of the product gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separation process for low energy consumption methanol to olefins (MTO) product gas, comprises (i) cooling (MTO) product gas in reactor by quenching with water, then sending into a first stage compression unit and a second stage compression unit of the olefin separation unit, (ii) treating the product gas with secondary compression by compressor, then separately sending the product gas into a oxide removal tower and a alkali washing tower, and separately removing the oxide and acid gas, (iii) compressing the obtained purified compressed gas by a three stage compression unit, and then cooling by a cooler, sending the gas phase material at the top of the tank to the gas phase dryer, pumping the liquid phase material of the bottom of the tank into a liquid phase product gas coalescer to remove part of the water and then sending into a liquid phase dryer, (iv) drying of the gas phase and liquid phase product gas, then directly adding into the high pressure decarbonization three tower, cooling the gas phase material at the top of the high pressure decarbonization three column and then adding into decarbonization tower, then sending the tower material into three sub-high pressure decarbonization towers, condensing the top material through a condenser and adding into the reflux tank, sending out the liquid phase mixed carbon in the reflux tank by a conveyor pump, and cooling a part of the liquid phase mixed carbon by a cooler and then sending into the top of the decarbonization tower, washing and adding absorbing liquid for separation of carbon-gas phase-1 and carbon-gas phase-2, (v) sending the carbon-gas phase material at the top of the decarbonization tower the unpowered cryogenic equipment, returning the cooled carbon-carbon- or carbon-containing liquid phase material to the top of the decarbonization tower or the carbon dioxide recovery tower, mixing another part of the secondary high-pressure decarburization three-column reflux tank with carbon three and returned to the secondary high-pressure decarburization three-stage as reflux, and directly sending excess into the propylene rectification tower, (vi) self-pressurizing the degassing tower's tower material into the decarbonized two towers, partially condensing the gas phase of the top of the decarbonized 2 column by the condenser and refluxing the liquid phase by the decarbonized two column, according to the acetylene content, directly sending the gas phase carbon-2 to the ethylene distillation tower or after removing acetylene from the acetylene hydrogenation reactor and then sending the ethylene distillation column, and then self-pressing the tower material of the decarbonized two column into the propylene rectification column, (vii) separating the carbon dioxide in the ethylene distillation tower into the polymeric grade ethylene product and ethane product, then sending the polymeric grade ethylene product to the ethylene storage tank, combining the ethane product with the carbon-tail gas in the unpowered cryogenic equipment, reheating the mixture of the ethane and carbon-gas mixture gas and then directly adding the fuel gas system into the dryer regeneration system, (viii) separating carbon trichloride in the propylene distillation tower into polymeric grade propylene product and propane product, sending the polymeric grade propylene product and propane product into propylene tank and propane tank respectively, and (ix) adding the column material of the secondary high-pressure decarburization three towers to the decarburization four towers by pressure into mixed carbon four products and mixed carbon five products in the decarburized four towers, then respectively adding the two products into the mixed carbon four storage tank and the mixed carbon five storage tank respectively. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for separation system for low energy consumption MTO product gas, comprising a compression unit and a two-stage compression unit which are sequentially connected by a pipeline, an oxide removal tower, an alkali washing tower, a three-stage compression unit, a cooler, and a dryer feed separator tank the top of the dryer feeding separating tank, a vapor phase dryer is connecting to the top of the dryer feed liquid separation tank, a bottom of the dryer feed liquid separation tank is connecting with a liquid phase dryer via a transfer pump, a liquid product gas coalescer is connecting between the transfer pump and the liquid phase dryer, the vapor phase dryer and the liquid phase dryer are respectively connecting with a high pressure decarbonization three tower, the high-pressure decarburization three-column is connecting with a decarburization one tower, and the decarburization one tower is connecting with a non-powered cryogenic equipment, the decarburization tower is further connecting with a decarburized two column, and the decarburized two column is connecting with an acetylene hydrogenation reactor, the acetylene hydrogenation reactor is connecting to an ethylene distillation column, the ethylene rectification column is connecting with a non-powered cryogenic device, and the decarburized two column is also connected with a propylene rectification column, the bottom of the high-pressure decarburization three tower is connecting with a secondary high-pressure decarburization three tower, a condenser is connecting to the top of the secondary high pressure decarburization three tower, and to a reflux tank, and the reflux tank is connecting with a reflux pump, and also is connecting with a propylene rectification column, and the bottom of the secondary high pressure decarbonization three column is connecting with a decarburized four column.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the separation system for low energy consumption methanol to olefins product gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The vapor phase dryer is disposed downstream of the three-stage compression unit. The anti-surge line of the three-stage compression unit is disposed in front of the vapor phase dryer, and the third anti-surge line of the product gas compressor starts from the top of the dryer feed separation tank. The cooler in the top condenser of the high pressure decarbonization three-column and the sub-high pressure decarbonization three-column is circulates water or air to cool.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) cooling (MTO) product gas in reactor by quenching with water to 38-42 degrees C, then compressing by a compression unit entering the olefin separation unit under pressure of 0.2-0.4 MPa, and then compressing in the second-stage compression unit under pressure of 0.8-1.3 MPa, (ii) treating the product gas with secondary compression by compressor, then separately sending the product gas into a oxide removal tower and a alkali washing tower, and separately removing the oxide and acid gas, (iii) compressing the obtained purified compressed gas by a three stage compression unit under pressure of 2-2.8 MPa, and then cooling by a cooler to 10-15 degrees C, sending the gas phase material at the top of the tank to the gas phase dryer, pumping the liquid phase material of the bottom of the tank into a liquid phase product gas coalescer to remove part of the water and then sending into a liquid phase dryer, (iv) drying of the gas phase and liquid phase product gas at 38-45 degrees C, then directly adding into the high pressure decarbonization three tower, cooling the gas phase material at the top of the high pressure decarbonization three column and then adding into decarbonization tower, then sending the tower material into three sub-high pressure decarbonization towers, condensing the top material through a condenser at -40 to -35 degrees C and adding into the reflux tank, sending out the liquid phase mixed carbon in the reflux tank by a conveyor pump, and cooling a part of the liquid phase mixed carbon by a cooler to -25 to -15 degrees C and then sending into the top of the decarbonization tower, washing and adding absorbing liquid for separation of carbon-gas phase-1 and carbon-gas phase-2, (v) sending the carbon-gas phase material at the top of the decarbonization tower the unpowered cryogenic equipment, cooling the liquid material rich in carbon two or carbon three to -130 to -110 degrees C and re-heating to -40 to -38 degrees C in a cold box, and mixing with the washing absorbent material for carbon-to-carbon separation, returning the cooled carbon-carbon- or carbon-containing liquid phase material to the top of the decarbonization tower or the carbon dioxide recovery tower, mixing another part of the secondary high-pressure decarburization three-column reflux tank with carbon three and returned to the secondary high-pressure decarburization three-stage as reflux, and directly sending excess into the propylene rectification tower, (vi) self-pressurizing the degassing tower's tower material into the decarbonized two towers, partially condensing the gas phase of the top of the decarbonized 2 column by the condenser at -23 to -15 degrees C and refluxing the liquid phase by the decarbonized two column, according to the acetylene content, directly sending the gas phase carbon-2 to the ethylene distillation tower or after removing acetylene from the acetylene hydrogenation reactor and then sending the ethylene distillation column, and then self-pressing the tower material of the decarbonized two column into the propylene rectification column, (vii) separating the carbon dioxide in the ethylene distillation tower into the polymeric grade ethylene product and ethane product, then sending the polymeric grade ethylene product to the ethylene storage tank, combining the ethane product with the carbon-tail gas in the unpowered cryogenic equipment at -100 to -70 degrees C, reheating the mixture of the ethane and carbon-gas mixture gas and then directly adding the fuel gas system into the dryer regeneration system, (viii) separating carbon trichloride in the propylene distillation tower into polymeric grade propylene product and propane product, sending the polymeric grade propylene product and propane product into propylene tank and propane tank respectively, and (ix) adding the column material of the secondary high-pressure decarburization three towers to the decarburization four towers by pressure into mixed carbon four products and mixed carbon five products in the decarburized four towers, then respectively adding the two products into the mixed carbon four storage tank and the mixed carbon five storage tank respectively. The working pressure of the high pressure decarbonization three tower is 2- 2.8 MPa, the working pressure of the secondary high pressure decarbonization three tower is 1.2-1.7 MPa, and the working pressure range of the decarbonization tower is 2-2.8 MPa, the working pressure range of the decarbonized two column is 2-2.5 MPa, the working pressure of the acetylene hydrogenation reactor is 1.6-2 MPa, the working pressure of the ethylene distillation column is 1.6-1.8 MPa, and the working pressure of the decarburized four towers is 0.3-0.4 MPa. The method comprises cooling the washing absorbent material for separating carbon-carbon and carbon two from the secondary high-pressure decarburization three-column reflux pump upstream of the decarbonization tower to -25 degrees C to -20 degrees C and rich in carbon, reheating and mixing with the carbon three cryogenic equipment recovery liquid and sending into the tower, and is not circulated between the decarbonization tower, the decarbonization two tower, the high pressure decarbonization three tower, the secondary high pressure decarbonization three tower or the propylene rectification tower, since the decarbonization tower and the high pressure decarburization three tower are jointly controlled and operated under pressure of 2-2.8 MPa, and then sending the gas phase material at the top of the high pressure decarbonization three tower to the decarbonization tower through the top of the reflux tank. The ethane product at the bottom of the ethylene rectification column is depressurized under pressure of 0.35-1 MPa and then transferrd to anunpowered cryogenic equipment. When the compressor starts to start, the starting point of the anti-surge line for the three-stage compression unit is on the gas phase line at the top of the dryer feed liquid separation tank, then circulating the material to the suction port of the three-stage compression unit does not pass through the gas phase dryer, sending the mixed carbon three materials in the decarbonized two columns by the cooler and cooling to -25 to -15 degrees C and refluxing in the decarbonized two columns.

TI Capturing target species from gas, comprises            contacting gas containing target species with first            absorbent solution comprising capture species,            dissolving target species, electrochemically separating            target anion, and releasing target species.
AU GOBAILLE-SHAW G
   GHOSH S
   CHADWICK N
AE MISSION ZERO TECHNOLOGIES LTD (MISS-Non-standard)
GA 2022C1776F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves contacting a gas that                contains the target species with a first absorbent                solution comprising a capture species. The species                are dissolved in the first solution to form a                target anion, electrochemically separates the anion                from the solution by contacting the solution with                the ion-exchange membrane and transfers the target                ion through the membrane into a second solution and                the species are released from the second solution,                in which the membranes are not permeable to the                capture species, so that the species does not pass                through the membranes.
   USE - Method for capturing a target species from a                gas for storage or conversion to less harmful                compounds, where the target species is carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the ion-exchange membranes                that are not permeable to the capture species, so                that capture species does not pass through the one                or more ion exchange membranes; and provides the                target species that is in a relatively more                volatile state in the second absorbent solution,                and there is no need to apply heat to decompose                target species-capture species compounds in order                to liberate target species from the second                absorption solution, thus avoiding the need to                separate target species bound to capture species in                the first absorption solution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                apparatus for capturing a target species from a                gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                target species method.

TI Preparing water-washable light-curable            three-dimensional printing resin comprises e.g. mixing            ethylene carbonate and ethylenediamine, reacting            isocyanoethyl acrylate, hydroquinone and dibutyltin            dilaurate, and adding glycerated triacrylate and            photoinitiator into single-end acrylate-based            prepolymer.
AU CUI Y
   LIU B
   SONG L
   YIN X
AE UNIV GUANGDONG TECHNOLOGY (UGTE-C)
GA 2022A5651G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing water-washable light-curable                three-dimensional printing resin i.e.                2-{[(2-{[(2-{[(2-hydroxypropoxy)carbonyl]amino}ethyl)carbamoyl]oxy}propoxy)carbonyl]amino}ethyl                2-methylprop-2-enoate comprises (i) mixing cyclic                carbonate and diamine, stirring evenly, and                reacting at 70-90 &#8451; to obtain a diol                intermediate, (ii) cooling the diol intermediate,                adding isocyanoethyl acrylate, polymerization                inhibitor and catalyst, and reacting at 50-90                &#8451; to obtain a single-end acrylate-based                prepolymer, and (iii) adding a reactive diluent and                a photoinitiator into the single-end acrylate-based                prepolymer, and mixing to obtain water-washable                light-curable three-dimensional printing resin                containing carbon dioxide groups.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                water-washable light-curable three-dimensional                printing resin i.e.                2-{[(2-{[(2-{[(2-hydroxypropoxy)carbonyl]amino}ethyl)carbamoyl]oxy}propoxy)carbonyl]amino}ethyl                2-methylprop-2-enoate.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                water-washable light-curable three-dimensional                printing resin i.e.                2-{[(2-{[(2-{[(2-hydroxypropoxy)carbonyl]amino}ethyl)carbamoyl]oxy}propoxy)carbonyl]amino}ethyl                2-methylprop-2-enoate obtained by above                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In step (i), the                  consumption of the cyclic carbonate is 50-75 pts.                  wt., and the consumption of the diamine is 15-20                  pts. wt.; in the step (ii), the amount of the                  diol intermediate is 60-90 pts. wt., the amount                  of the isocyanoethyl acrylate is 15-25 pts. wt.,                  and the amount of the polymerization inhibitor is                  0.01-0.05 pts. wt., catalyst amount is 0.01-0.025                  pts. wt.; in the step (iii), the amount of the                  single-end acrylate-based prepolymer is 20-50                  pts. wt., the amount of the active agent is 30-75                  pts. wt., and the amount of the photoinitiator is                  1-4 pts. wt. In step (i), cyclic carbonate is                  ethylene carbonate and propylene carbonate;                  diamine is ethylenediamine, hexamethylenediamine,                  2-methylpentanediamine, decanediamine,                  isophoronediamine, p-phenylenediamine,                  phenylenediamine and propylenediamine. In the                  step (ii), the polymerization inhibitor is                  p-hydroxyanisole, hydroquinone, p-benzoquinone,                  methyl hydroquinone and tert-butyl hydroquinone;                  and catalyst is dibutyltin dilaurate, stannous                  octoate and bismuth isooctanoate. In step (iii):                  reactive diluent is acryloyl morpholine,                  hydroxyethyl methacrylate, hydroxypropyl                  methacrylate, hydroxyethyl acrylate,                  hydroxypropyl acrylate, ethoxylated                  trimethylolpropane triacrylate, glycerated                  triacrylate, polyethylene glycol 400 diacrylate,                  methoxy polyethylene glycol 1000 acrylate,                  dimethylaminoethyl methacrylate and                  ethoxyethoxyethyl acrylate; and photoinitiator is                  2-methyl-1-[4-(methylthio)phenyl]-2-(4-morpholinyl)-1-propanone,                  2,4,6-trimethylbenzoyl-diphenylphosphine oxide,                  2-benzyl-2-dimethylamino-1-(4-morpholinophenyl)butanone                  and/or 2-hydroxy-2-methyl-1-phenylacetone.Preferred Method: The step (i) comprises                  adding cyclic carbonate and the diamine into a                  three-necked flask with mechanical stirring and                  thermometer, and placing in a water bath to react                  for 1-4 hours. In the step (ii), the reaction                  time is 1-5 hours. In the step (ii), the reaction                  temperature is 70 &#8451;, and the reaction                  time is 3 hours.

TI Purifying liraglutide/semaglutide for large scale,            fine preparation of liraglutide/semaglutide, involves            getting crude peptide, dissolving in solvent, adding            acetonitrile, collecting sample liquid, purifying            sample liquid by reversed-phase high performance liquid            chromatography twice.
AU YU P
   TANG Y
   HUANG J
   JIANG X
   CHEN M
AE HYBIO PHARM CO LTD (HYPH-C)
GA 202286584F
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying liraglutide/semaglutide involves                getting crude peptide, dissolving in solvent,                adding acetonitrile, collecting sample liquid after                filtration, purifying the sample liquid by                reversed-phase high performance liquid                chromatography (HPLC) twice, and collecting the                qualified fractions. The equal volume of water                dilution is added in the qualified fraction to                prepare column desalting, and collected desalting                fraction. The desalted fraction is treated at a low                temperature and the temperature is maintained, and                added carbon monoxide (CO) to the saturation, and                then added pure water to adjust the pH or added an                acidic reagent to adjust the pH. The target peptide                is precipitated and kept, filtered, and dried in                vacuum to obtain refined peptide powder.
   USE - Method for purifying liraglutide/semaglutide                for the large scale, fine preparation of                liraglutide/semaglutide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method allows precipitation of carbon                dioxide (CO2) to separate out the target peptide,                that is, the purified qualified fraction does not                need to be concentrated under reduced pressure,                ultrafiltration, freeze-drying and other                operations, saves the time-consuming of rotary                evaporation, and the purification process is simple                and easy, with short time consumption and low                energy consumption, and can be used for large-scale                and industrialized production, which greatly                reduces the production cost and improves the                production efficiency, and suitable for purified                fractions of liraglutide or semaglutide, and the                effect is remarkable.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent includes                  ammonium bicarbonate solution. PN CN114471620-A
TI Tin tungstate/indium sulfide composite            photocatalyst, where tin tungstate nanosheets are            uniformly loaded on surface of indium sulfide to            increase contact area between two, promoting separation            and migration of photogenerated carriers, and obtaining            photocatalytic activity.
AU SONG J
   ZHANG X
   ZHANG Y
   WANG L
   LU X
   LI Q
AE UNIV HUAIBEI NORMAL (UYHU-Non-standard)
GA 202270468U
AB    NOVELTY - Tin tungstate(aSnWO4)/indium sulfide composite                photocatalyst, is claimed. The a-tin tungstate                nanosheets are uniformly loaded on the surface of                indium sulfide to increase the contact area between                the two, promoting the separation and migration of                photogenerated carriers, and obtaining                photocatalytic activity higher than that of indium                sulfide or a-tin tungstate.
   USE - Used as tin tungstate(aSnWO4)/indium sulfide                composite photocatalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst: is beneficial to the                separation and migration of photogenerated                carriers; can continuously degrade the organic                pollutant rhodamine B reagent under the condition                of visible light; is easy to synthesize; has common                raw materials for preparation and high yield; is                economical and suitable for actual production; and                has broad application prospects and                industrialization prospects in the fields of                photolysis of water for hydrogen production,                photodegradation of organic pollutants and carbon                dioxide reduction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the photocatalyst, comprising (1) using                hydrothermal method to prepare a-tin tungstate                nanosheets, (2) preparing a-tin tungstate                suspension, and preparing indium chloride                homogeneous solution at the same time, and (3)                pouring the indium chloride solution into the a-tin                tungstate suspension for reaction, slowly pouring                the sodium sulfide solution into the mixed solution                to continue the reaction, collecting and drying the                reaction product to obtain the finished                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio of                  a-tin tungstate to indium sulfide is 1:1, 1:3 and                  1:5. In the step (3), the mass ratio of a-tin                  tungstate to indium chloride is                  0.126-0.632:1.Preferred Method: In the step (1), the                  preparation of a-tin tungstate nanosheets by                  hydrothermal method comprises (1) completely                  dissolving stannous chloride dihydrate in                  hydrogen chloride solution, completely dissolving                  sodium tungstate dihydrate in deionized water,                  adding the sodium tungstate dihydrate solution                  dropwise to the stannous chloride dihydrate                  solution, and vigorously stirring to form mixed                  solution, (2) adjusting the pH value of the mixed                  solution in step (1-7) with sodium hydroxide                  solution and vigorously stirring for one hour,                  and (3) transferring the solution in the step (2)                  to reaction kettle for reaction, collecting the                  product after the reaction and drying in vacuum                  drying oven to obtain a- tin tungstate                  nanosheets. In the step (3), the reaction                  temperature in the reactor is 200degrees Celsius,                  and the reaction times is 24 hours, collecting                  the product after the reaction in vacuum drying                  oven and drying at 60degrees Celsius for 12 hours                  to obtain a-tin tungstate nanosheets. The indium                  chloride solution is poured into the a-tin                  tungstate suspension, the reaction is carried out                  in water bath at 55degrees Celsius for 2 hours,                  the sodium sulfide solution is slowly poured into                  the above mixed solution, the reaction is                  continued in a 55 degreesC water bath for 1                  hours, and the reaction product is collected and                  dried in vacuum oven. The reaction product is                  centrifugally washed with deionized water and                  absolute ethanol, placing in vacuum drying oven                  for drying at 70degrees Celsius for 12 hours, and                  ground to obtain a-tin tungstate/indium sulfide                  composite photocatalyst. In the step (2), the                  preparation of a-tin tungstate suspension refers                  to placing anhydrous citric acid in container A,                  adding deionized water and stirring to dissolve                  completely, adding a-tin tungstate to container                  A, uniformly stirring and sonicating, and                  reacting in water bath at 55degrees Celsius for 2                  hours to obtain a-tin tungstate suspension. The                  preparation of the indium chloride homogeneous                  solution refers to placing indium (III) chloride                  tetrahydrate in container B, adding deionized                  water and stirring to dissolve it completely to                  obtain homogeneous solution.

PN IN201831015657-A
TI Producing urea from high ash coal by converting high ash Indian coal into synthesis gas or coal gas, cleaning gas, removing acid, adsorbing coal gas components, forming compressed raw gas, introducing into ammonia reactor, and condensing.
AU GOLLA S
   MANIKANTA K
   DHANDAYUDHAM R
   POCHA S G
AE BHARAT HEAVY ELECTRICALS LTD (BHAR-C)
GA 202236588U
AB    NOVELTY - Urea is produced from high ash coal by converting high ash Indian coal into synthesis gas or coal gas under oxy blown gasification conditions in gasifier, driving coal gas with particulates to cyclone where particulates are separated as cyclone bottom and gas as cyclone outlet gas, reducing temperature of cyclone outlet gas to generate Economizer outlet gas at 280 degrees C, allowing gas filter outlet coal gas to pass through a gas cleaning unit (GCU) to produce GCU outlet gas, preheating to 150 degrees C, moving GCU outlet gas to shift reaction system to generate LTS separator gas, moving into acid gas removal (AGR) unit, further flowing AGR clean gas to pressure swing adsorption (PSA) beds to adsorb coal gas components except hydrogen (H2), flowing H2 product along with nitrogen (N2) product to raw gas compressor to form compressed raw gas, heating, introducing into ammonia reactor to form ammonia gas, condensing to form liquid ammonia, and reacting with carbon dioxide (CO2) in urea reactor.
   USE - The method is useful for producing urea from high ash coal, where urea is useful as a chemical fertilizer from synthesis gas that is generated through bubbling fluidized bed gasification of the coal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient production of urea from high ash coal with high yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Processes: Oxy blown gasification conditions comprise providing a steam and oxidant mixture as fluidizing medium along with coal, in the gasifier. Cyclone outlet gas heat content is utilized to produce saturated steam from boiler feed water using combination of two heat exchangers, i.e. heat recovery boiler and economizer. Economizer outlet gas enters a gas filter, where all the fly ash gets removed up to desired levels. Gas filter outlet coal gas gets further cleaned up with respect to NH3, HCN, and trace elements in GCU by producing GCU outlet gas. GCU outlet gas moves to shift reaction system where CO content of the coal gas gets converted into H2, where shift reaction system comprises of HTS reactor, LTS reactor, and its associated heat recovery equipment and gas-liquid separators. LTS separator gas further moves to AGR unit, where its H2S and CO2 components are separated as AGR H2S and AGR CO2 using solvents, such as ethanolamine, and LTS separator gas leaves as AGR clean gas. AGR clean gas flows to PSA for adsorbing coal gas components which get detached during pressure swing or reduction and flow in reverse direction as PSA waste gas. H2 product along with N2 product at a mole ratio of 1:3 (H2:N2), flows to raw gas compressor for compression up to around 200 bar pressure to form compressed raw gas. Compressed raw gas gets heated up in raw gas heater up to 500 degrees C using steam as heating medium to form hot raw gas. Ammonia gas exchanges heat for producing steam in steam generator-3 and steam generator-3 outlet gas moves to an ammonia separator, where condensed ammonia separates out as liquid ammonia and unconverted gas as recycle gas, where recycle gas gets compressed in an recycle gas compressor and discharges quench gas which directly gets admitted back into ammonia reactor beds as reactant and coolant. Liquid ammonia at 200 bar pressure enters into the urea reactor along with CO2 with a mole ratio of 2:1-4:1 (NH3:CO2). AGR CO2 is further pressurized by CO2 compressor up to order of 200 bar pressure to obtain compressed CO2 for further usage in pressurization of coal lock and coal receiver using coal lock pressurization line and coal receiver pressurization line and for pressurization of lock and receiver vessels in the bottom ash and fly ash removal mechanisms. Compressed CO2 splits into excess CO2, urea CO2, and oxidant and pressurization CO2 gas and OPC gas further splits into oxidant CO2 and pressurization CO2 gas. N2 product is produced as a product along with O2 product in an air separation unit which consumes compressed ambient air as feed. O2 product further mixes with oxidant CO2 in an O2-CO2 mixing vessel to form oxidant which further splits into transport oxidant and raw oxidant, raw oxidant and gasification steam combining in a steam-oxidant mixing vessel to form steam and oxidant mixture. Preferred Components: Synthesis gas generated in gasifier contains carbon monoxide (CO), H2, CO2, water vapor (H2O), methane (CH4), ammonia (NH3), hydrogen sulfide (H2S), hydrogen cyanide (HCN), N2, and other minor trace elements, such as calcium (Ca), magnesium (Mg), sodium (Na), potassium (K), lead (Pb), and vanadium (V). PSA waste gas basically contains CH4, H2O, N2, and CO which can be used further for heating purpose.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.)
MC C10-A13C; C11-C05; C14-T
IP B01D-053/047; C10K-001/00; C10K-001/02; C10K-001/04; C10K-001/10
PD IN201831015657-A   18 Feb 2022   B01D-053/047   202226   Pages: 20   English
AD IN201831015657-A    IN31015657    25 Apr 2018
PI IN31015657    25 Apr 2018

   (3) producing the polyamide-imide film comprising preparing polyamide-imide polymer solution by polymerizing diamine compound, dianhydride compound, and dicarbonyl compound in an organic solvent in which the metal salt is dissolved, pouring the polymer solution into a tank, manufacturing a gel sheet by extruding and casting the polymer solution in the tank and then drying, and heat-treating the gel sheet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal of the metal salt is a metal belonging to Group 1. The content of the metal salt is 0.2-1.5 pts. wt. The polyamide-imide polymer comprises ketone-based repeating units of formulae (I) and (II).E1 and J1 = divalent 6-30C aliphatic ring, divalent 4-30C heteroaliphatic ring, divalent 6-30C aromatic ring, divalent 4-30C heteroaromatic ring, 1-30C alkylene, 2-30C alkenylene, 2-30C alkynylene (all are optionally substituted), -O-, -S-, -C(=O)-, -CH(OH)-, -S(=O)2-, -Si(CH3)2-, -C(CH3)2-, or -C(CF3)2-;e and j = 1 to 5, where at least two of E1 are same or different from each other, when e is greater than or equal to 2 or more, and at least two of J1 are same or different from each other, when j is greater than or equal to 2; andG1 = tetravalent 6-30C aliphatic ring, tetravalent 4-30C heteroaliphatic ring, tetravalent 6-30C aromatic ring, or tetravalent 4-30C heteroaromatic ring (all are optionally substituted), where aliphatic ring, heteroaliphatic ring, aromatic ring or heteroaromatic ring exists alone, or are joined to each other to form a condensed ring, and connected by a linking group including 1-30C alkylene, 2-30C alkenylene, 2-30C alkynylene (all are optionally substituted), -O-, -S-, -C(=O)-, -CH(OH)-, -S(=O)2-, -Si(CH3)2-, -C(CH3)2-, or -C(CF3)2-.The diamine compound includes 2,2'-bis(trifluoromethyl)-4,4'-diaminobiphenyl, 2,2'-Bis-(3,4-Dicarboxyphenyl)hexafluoropropane dianhydride, biphenyl-tetracarboxylic acid dianhydride, terephthaloyl chloride, 1,1'-biphenyl-4,4'-dicarbonyl dichloride, isophthaloylchloride, and/or sebacoyl chloride. Preferred Method: The step of preparing the polymer solution further comprises the step of adjusting the viscosity of the polymer solution to 100000-500000 cps at room temperature. The step of preparing the polymer solution comprises (a) preparing first polymer solution by simultaneously or sequentially mixing and reacting diamine compound, dianhydride compound, and dicarbonyl compound in organic solvent in which the metal salt is dissolved, (b) measuring the viscosity of the first polymer solution to evaluate whether a target viscosity is reached, and (c) preparing a second polymer solution having a target viscosity by additionally adding the dicarbonyl compound when the viscosity of the first polymer solution does not reach the target viscosity. The stirring speed when preparing the first polymer solution is faster than the stirring speed when preparing the second polymer solution.
   EXAMPLE - Dimethylacetamide (DMAc) 85.68 g, and organic solvent was filled in a double-jacketed 1 l glass reactor with temperature control under nitrogen atmosphere at 20 degrees C, and then 0.86 g lithium metal carbonate was slowly added and dissolved. Then 64.0 g (0.2 mol) 2,2'-bis(trifluoromethyl)-4,4'-diaminophenyl was slowly added and dissolved. 4.44 g (0.01 mol) 2,2-bis (3,4-dicarboxyphenyl) hexafluoropropane dianhydride (6FDA) was slowly added thereto and stirred for 1 hour. Then 8.12 g (0.04 mol) isophthaloyl chloride was added and stirred for 1 hour, and 30.45 g (0.15 mol) of terephthaloyl chloride (TPC) was added and stirred for 1 hour to prepare a polymer solution. Then obtained polymer solution was applied to a glass plate, dried with hot air at 80 degrees C for 30 minutes, peeled from the glass plate, fixed to a pin frame, and heated at a rate of 2 degrees C/minute in a temperature range of 80-300 degrees C to obtain polyamide-imide film having thickness of 50 mu m.
