TI Arc heating test mixed test medium enthalpy value determining method for aircraft ground pneumatic thermal test research field, involves performing iterative calculation, and obtaining entalpy value of mixed test media.
AU ZENG H
AE CHINA ACAD AEROSPACE AERODYNAMICS (CAER-C)
GA 2022170470
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves inputting an actual measurement parameter of arc heating test, where the actual measurement parameters are hot gas pressure of mixing chamber, cold gas pressure of mixing chamber and cold mass flow of argon. The argon gas mass flow in a mixing chamber except the argon is marked as gas B mass flow. The cold air pressure partial pressure of the mixed chamber is obtained according to the mass flow rate. The gas B cold pressure is combined to obtain the air flow temperature heat. Determination is made to check whether the heat is converged or not. An enthalpy value of the gas B is obtained. A mixed test medium is determined.The gas B is air and carbon dioxide or nitrogen.
   USE - Method for determining enthalpy of mixed test medium of electric arc heating test used in aircraft ground pneumatic thermal test research field for thermal protection ground test of new-type hypersonic aircraft.
   ADVANTAGE - The method determines the enthalpy of the mixed test medium with argon shielding gas, thus satisfying the non-pure air single test medium, and hence realizing more accurate simulation of the aircraft into the pneumatic heat environment.

PN GB2592585-A
TI Power generating boiler apparatus has heat exchanger unit, turbine, turbine inlet pipe and separation chamber which are used such that water or refrigerant is dropped onto turbine using force of gravity to drive generator for production of electricity.
AU GORA P
AE GORA P (GORA-Individual)
GA 2021A3271G
AB    NOVELTY - The power generating boiler apparatus has a turbine (1), a heat exchanger unit (3) and a turbine inlet pipe (12) which are in fluid communication to each other and contain water or refrigerant such that they allow the water or refrigerant to travel cyclically around the apparatus. The water or refrigerant within the heat exchanger unit is arranged to absorb heat from an external heat source using the surface area of the heat exchanger unit. A separation chamber is located above the heat exchanger unit. The heat exchanger unit, the turbine, the turbine inlet pipe and the separation chamber are used such that water or refrigerant is dropped onto the turbine using the force of gravity to drive a generator (2) for production of electricity.
   USE - Power generating boiler apparatus.
   ADVANTAGE - Allows industrial processes that discharge heat to become more efficient, but also contributes to reducing amount of heat and carbon dioxide pollution produced from entering atmosphere, therefore helping to reduce negative effects of climate change and global warming. Performs combination of boiling process of liquid with hydro-electric power generation.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of a power generating boiler system.
   Turbine (1)
   Generator (2)
   Heat exchnager unit (3)
   Tube (4)
   Turbine inlet pipe (12)

PN RU2752856-C1
TI METHOD FOR REHABILITATION OF POST-VIRAL INJURIES OF LUNG PARENCHYMA AND DEVICE FOR ITS IMPLEMENTATION.
AU UDUT V V
   NAUMOV S A
   UDUT E V
   NAUMOV S S
AE UDUT V V (UDUT-Individual)
   NAUMOV S A (NAUM-Individual)
GA 2021976699
AB    NOVELTY - Inventions group relates to medicine, namely to pulmonology, and can be used in the treatment and rehabilitation of post-viral injuries of the lung parenchyma. For this, a method is proposed, which consists in inhaling a gas mixture of xenon with oxygen to a patient before the onset of signs of a therapeutic effect. Inhalation is carried out using a device operating in a semi-closed circuit. The composition of the xenon-oxygen gas mixture: xenon 70-79 vol.%, oxygen - 30-21 vol.%. The maximum time for one inhalation is 60 seconds. Inhalations are carried out daily for 5 days. Also proposed is a device for implementing the method comprising a source of a gas mixture of xenon and oxygen connected to a breathing bag. The breathing bag is connected to the breathing circuit through a valve. The breathing circuit is connected through a breathing tube and an antibacterial filter to a mouth-nose mask. The inspiratory and expiratory lines are made in the form of separate channels equipped with directional valves. At the same time, a port is installed on the inhalation line for additional supply of a gas mixture or oxygen, and an overpressure relief valve and a column with a carbon dioxide sorbent are installed on the exhalation line.
   USE - Medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - Inventions provide effective rehabilitation of post-viral injuries of the lung parenchyma, shorten the time of the inhalation procedure, reduce xenon consumption, simplifying the inhalation device.

PN CN113180102-A
TI Method for storing and preserving blueberries,            involves air-drying surface of blueberry fruits, and            putting blueberry tray containing blueberry fruits into            storage, and storing fruits fresh under preset            atmosphere conditions.
AU LIANG M
AE YUNNAN YUNLAN BLUEBERRY TECHNOLOGY DEV (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 202193423B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves putting freshly picked                blueberries into a blueberry tray (1), and paving a                layer of blueberry fruits at bottom of blueberry                tray. The blueberry tray containing blueberry                fruits is put into first processing liquid tank, so                that the blueberry fruits are completely immersed                and soaked in first treatment liquid for 10-15                seconds. The blueberry tray containing blueberry                fruits is put into second processing liquid tank,                so that blueberry fruits are completely immersed                and soaked in the second treatment liquid for 5-8                seconds. The blueberry tray containing blueberry                fruits is put into an air-drying device (2), and                surface of blueberry fruits is air-dried. The                blueberry tray containing blueberry fruits is put                into storage, and fruits are stored fresh under                preset atmosphere conditions. The liquid tank is 75                %vol of ethanol water solution. The preset                atmosphere condition includes 85-88 % of nitrogen,                10-11 % of carbon dioxide and the rest of                oxygen.
   USE - Method for storing and preserving                blueberries.
   ADVANTAGE - The problem that the liquid-cooled blueberry                preservation technology is likely to cause                blueberry spoilage can be solved. The content of                pesticide residue on the surface of blueberry can                be reduced. The storage period of blueberry can be                effectively improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the air                drying device.
   1Blueberry tray
   2Air-drying device

   103Bottom plate
   203Blower

PN WO2021113978-A1; CA3158899-A1
TI Producing humic acids from coal by mixing comminuted coal solids fraction derived from coal with alkaline solution, treating pre-solubilized slurry under subcritical extraction conditions, and separating humic acid solution product stream.
AU NASSAR N N
   MANASRAH A D
AE CARBON OXYTECH INC (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 2021681960
AB    NOVELTY - Humic acids are produced from coal comprising humic substance content by mixing comminuted coal solids fraction derived from coal with alkaline solution to produce pre-solubilized slurry; treating pre-solubilized slurry under subcritical extraction conditions in a basic aqueous extraction medium for effective extraction residence time period of less than or equal to 2 hours to extract humic acids from coal solids, at extraction conditions of 25-50 degrees C, 0.1-0.5 MPa, flow of oxygen-containing gas, and potassium hydroxide (KOH); and separating humic acid solution product stream from residual solids and recovering humic acid solution product stream comprising humic acids and fulvic acids. Amount of humic acids is greater than or equal to 85%; conversion of humic substance content of coal into humic acids and fulvic acids of humic acid solution product stream is greater than or equal to 90%; and proportion of humic substance content of coal converted to carbon dioxide under extraction conditions during the extraction time period is less than or equal to 5%.
   USE - Production of humic acids from coal comprising humic substance content.
   ADVANTAGE - The method allows for high yield production of humic acids using water-oxygen systems. These processes facilitate the creation of economic values from low-rank coal by conversion into value-added products, such as humic acids, via water-oxygen media.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: Extraction temperature is on average less than or equal to 51 degrees C. Extraction pressure is on average less than or equal to 0.5 MPa. Oxygen-containing gas is air. Effective extraction residence time period is less than or equal to 3 hours, preferably less than or equal to 1 hours. Preferred Compositions: Conversion of the humic substance content of the coal into humic acids and fulvic acids of the humic acid solution product stream is greater than or equal to 94%. Coal is a lignite. Comminuted coal solids comprises particles of size 53-950 mu m. Mass ratio of coal to liquid is ratio of lignite to water of 0.05-0.075. Humic substance content of coal converted to CO2 under the extraction conditions during the extraction time period is less than or equal to 4%, preferably less than or equal to 0.5%. Preferred Processes: Flow of oxygen-containing gas comprises sparging the basic aqueous extraction medium with the oxygen-containing gas. The method further comprises comminuting the coal to provide the comminuted coal solids fraction. The method further comprises adding ammonium hydroxide (NH4OH) to the basic aqueous extraction medium in an amount effective in an ammonolysis process to convert lignite to a humate fertilizer.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing powder coating composition, comprising                (i) melt extruding bisphenol A epoxy resin,                titanate chelating agent, urea-formaldehyde resin,                fillers, ultra-fine powder coating waste through                the screw extruder at 130-150 degrees C, tableting                and crushing to obtain intermediates; and (ii)                mixing oxazolidinone epoxy resin, phenolic aldehyde                and imidazole accelerant, melting at 120-130                degrees C for melt reaction extrusion, crushing the                tablets, adding gas phase silicon dioxide during                the grinding process and passing through a                100-meshes sieve to obtain a qualified powder                coating with a particle size of 20-150 mu m.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The filler comprises                  barium sulfate, wollastonite, and combinations                  thereof with good chemical resistance commonly                  used in heavy-duty anti-corrosion powder                  coatings.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The titanate chelating                  agent comprises acetylacetone type titanate                  coupling agent or ethyl acetoacetate type                  titanate coupling agent. The imidazole                  accelerator comprises imidazole, dimethyl                  imidazole and phenyl imidazole.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The bisphenol A epoxy                  resin is bisphenol A epoxy resin with an epoxy                  equivalent of 400-1000 and a softening point of                  60-105 degrees C. The urea-formaldehyde resin                  comprises n-butyl etherification, isobutyl                  etherification, and methyl etherification                  urea-formaldehyde resins. The superfine powder                  coating waste is superfine drill pipe and tubing                  internal coating epoxy powder waste. The epoxy                  equivalent of the oxazolidinone epoxy resin is                  380-460. The softening point of the phenolic                  resin is 85-110 degrees C.

PN WO2021096445-A1; KR2022104191-A; EP4073013-A1
TI Aqueous grinding aid composition for use in            production of high-performance cement comprises at            least one polycarboxylate polymer and at least one            surfactant.
AU AKALIN O
   OZELLE A
AE PLUSTECHNO DANISMANLIK VE YAPI KIMYASALL (PLUS-Non-standard)
   PLUSTECHNO DANISMANLIK VE YAPI KIMYASALL (PLUS-Non-standard)
GA 2021555273
AB    NOVELTY - An aqueous grinding aid composition comprises                greater than or equal to 1 polycarboxylate polymer                and greater than or equal to 1 surfactant.
   USE - An aqueous grinding aid composition for use in                production of high-performance cement                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The addition of surfactant enhances the                grinding efficiency and the strength of the cement.                The dispersibility of cement clinker during                grinding is improved by the addition of                polycarboxylate polymer composition. In addition,                this composition used as a grinding aid results in                a reduced energy consumption. The method enables                the mill to grind the clinker to a smaller size                with less energy and within a short time. The                reduction in amount of energy necessary allows the                carbon dioxide emissions from the production of                cement clinker to be greatly reduced by                substitution of mineral(s).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) manufacture of high performance cement                which involves (a) providing 1st additive by mixing                greater than or equal to 1 organic amine and                greater than or equal to 1 amino alcohol, (b)                providing 2nd additive by mixing greater than or                equal to 1 alkanolamine and greater than or equal                to 1 glycol, (c) mixing 1st additive with 2nd                additive to obtain additive composition, (d)                providing aqueous composition of polycarboxylate                polymer, and (e) introducing together or separately                the additive composition and the aqueous                composition of polycarboxylate polymer into the                grinding of cement; and
   (2) high-performance cement comprising 5-45                wt.% mineral additives by weight of cement.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Compositions: High-performance                  cement comprises 10-35 wt.% mineral additives by                  weight of cement. Mineral additives are selected                  from siliceous fly ash, calcareous fly ash, blast                  furnace slag, limestone (calcium carbonate),                  silicon dioxide (silica sand), natural pozzolan,                  industrial pozzolan, and burnt shale.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: The 1st additive is                  provided by mixing greater than or equal to 1                  organic amine, greater than or equal to 1 amino                  alcohol, and sodium gluconate. Preferred                  Compositions: Organic amine is selected from                  2-ethyldiisopropylamine, diethylamine,                  triethylamine, dimethylamine pyridine,                  ethyldiisopropylamine, procaine, dibenzylamine,                  N-methylmorpholine, dihydroabietylamine,                  1-ephenamine, and/or methyl-piperidine,                  preferably is 2-ethyldiisopropylamine. Amount of                  organic amine is 35-50 wt.% by weight of the 1st                  additive. Amino alcohol is selected from                  1-(N,N-bis(2-hydroxyethyl)amino)propan-2-ol                  (diethanolisopropanolamine, DEIPA), ethanolamine,                  diethanolamine, triethanolamine, diaminomethanal                  (urea), N-butyldiethanolamine,                  M-methyldiethanolamine, N-ethyldiethanolamine,                  diisopropanolamine, methyl diisopropanolamine,                  tetrahydroxypropylethylenediamine, and/or                  trimethylaminoethylethanolamine, preferably                  DEIPA. Glycol is selected from                  2-(hydroxyethoxy)ethan-2-ol (diethylene glycol),                  ethylene glycol, propylene glycol, glycerol                  (propan-1,2,3-triole), polyethylene glycol,                  and/or monopropylene glycol. Glycol is glycerol                  and/or diethylene glycol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Compositions: Polycarboxylate                  polymer is polycarboxylate ether or                  polycarboxylate ester polymer. Surfactant is                  non-ionic. Amount of surfactant is 0.1-0.3 wt.%.                  The 1st additive and the 2nd additive further                  comprise greater than or equal to 1 surfactant.                  Ratio of 1st additive to 2nd additive is                  1:0.5-4:1. Preferred Properties: Polycarboxylate                  polymer has molecular weight of 2000-50000.                  Preferred Processes: Composition is obtained by                  diluting polycarboxylate polymer with non-ionized                  water and with a surfactant by mixing during                  30-40 minutes. Preferred Condition: Step (c) is                  performed at 30-65 degrees C, preferably 40-60                  degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN112781184-A
TI Smart consumption reduction method for air-conditioning system, involves querying operation strategy data table to determine current operation strategy according to current environmental parameters of building automation control system.
AU MEI Q
   DONG J
   HU Y
AE CHINA RAILWAY CONSTR ELECTRIFICATION BUR (CRCC-C)
   XIAMEN ETOM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIAM-Non-standard)
GA 202155027T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves adjusting targets for comfort and energy efficiency according to air-conditioning management. The environmental parameters of the data test point are analyzed according to the environmental parameters and the parameters of the building automation control system to determine the energy-saving and consumption-reducing hybrid strategy of adjusting the energy consumption of air conditioning according to the carbon dioxide (CO2) concentration. The operation strategy data tables are generated through dynamic online learning of historical data. The operating strategy data table is provided with historical environmental parameters and the corresponding relationship between historical parameters of the building automation control system and the adjustment strategy. The operation strategy data table is queried to determine the current operation strategy according to the current environmental parameters and the current parameters of the building automation control system.

PN CN112358883-A
TI Self-disintegrating soil conditioner is prepared by using biomass ash, marine biological powder, calcium powder, organic matter, lemon salt, composite filler, and water.
AU LI H
   ZHANG X
   ZHANG C
   GUO B
   DING H
   XIE S
AE ANHUI GUOZHEN ECO-TECH CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202118297F
AB    NOVELTY - A self-disintegrating soil conditioner is prepared by adding 54-65 pts. wt. biomass ash, 10-20 pts. wt. marine biological powder, 15 pts. wt. calcium powder, 0-20 pts. wt. organic matter, 1 pt. wt. lemon salt, and 20-35 pts. wt. composite filler into ball mill, ball milling at 450-500 rpm for 5 minutes, obtaining a mixture, (2) adding the mixture and 20 pts. wt. water to reaction mixer, stirring at a speed of 150-200 rpm for 30 minutes, obtaining slurry, adding the slurry to disc granulator to make granules, drying the granules at 100-110 degrees C for 5-10 minutes, obtaining dried granules, (3) sieving the dried granules with a double-layer sieve, packing the granules in the middle layer of the double-layer sieve, returning the materials on and under the double-layer sieve to the disc granulator for re-granulation until the sieve to the middle layer.
   USE - Self-disintegrating soil conditioner.
   ADVANTAGE - The soil conditioner forms water-insoluble carbonate by separating carbon dioxide from the outside world with metal oxides and water in the soil conditioner through organic matter. The water-insoluble carbonate and hydroxide are reacted by citrate to form salts and nutrients that can be dissolved in water and absorbed by crops.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the self-disintegrating soil conditioner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The organic matter is cow dung, and the citrate is sodium citrate. Preferred Property: The pH of the self-disintegrating soil conditioner is 8-12. Preferred Process: The composite filler is prepared by (1) crushing wheat straw, washing three times with deionized water, transferring to the reactor, passing nitrogen to exhaust the air, increasing the temperature to 400 degrees C at a rate of 10 degrees C/minute, decomposing at this temperature for 2 hours, increasing the temperature to 600 degrees C for 10 hours, cooling, adding 10 %mass sodium hydroxide solution, heating and soaking in a water bath at 130-150 degrees C for 12 hours, washing 3 times with deionized water, obtaining biochar after drying, adding biochar into mixed solution, heating in a water bath at 50-70 degrees C and stirring at a constant speed of 100-150 rpm for 30 minutes, adding acrylic acid and ultrasonically processing at 50-60 W for 10 minutes, performing suction filtration, drying, obtaining treated biochar, (2) adding chitosan to a 10 %mass dilute hydrochloric acid solution, heating to 40-50 degrees C in water bath, reacting for 1 hour, adding ammonium thiocyanate and chloroacetic acid, magnetic stirring for 10 minutes, adding absolute ethanol and magnetically stirring at 100 rpm for 2 hours, (3) centrifuging at 10000 rpm for 3 minutes, using Soxhlet extractor to reflux for 2 hours, adding extracted product to deionized water, obtaining a modified chitosan solution, controlling the weight ratio of product to deionized water at 1:10, adding the treated biochar into the modified chitosan solution, dispersing at 500-550 rpm for 3 hours, centrifuging, filtering, and drying. Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of the treated biochar to the modified chitosan solution is 1:10. The weight ratio of biochar, mixed liquid and acrylic acid is 2:5:10. The weight ratio of chitosan, dilute hydrochloric acid solution, ammonium thiocyanate, chloroacetic acid and absolute ethanol is 1:20:2:3:10.

TI Thermal energy storage for producing electrical energy in power plant, has thermal energy storage elements that are separated into layers, where each layer forms channel between fluid inlet and fluid outlet.
AU EGGERS J R
   PAGELSEN N
   WAGNER J V
   ZACZEK A
AE SIEMENS GAMESA RENEWABLE ENERGY INC (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS GAMESA RENEWABLE ENERGY INC (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS GAMESA RENEWABLE ENERGY INC (SIEI-C)
GA 2021050796
AB    NOVELTY - The thermal energy storage has a housing (10) having a fluid inlet (12) and a fluid outlet (18). A thermal energy storage structure (20) that is arranged within the housing between the fluid inlet and the fluid outlet. The thermal energy storage structure comprising thermal energy storage elements and flexible separator elements (22). The flexible separator elements are arranged such that the thermal energy storage elements are separated into layers (24) and each layer forms a channel between the fluid inlet and fluid outlet. The fluid inlet comprises an inlet fluid distribution structure is adapted to selectively guide a working fluid towards the channels.
   USE - Thermal energy storage for producing an electrical energy in a power plant (claimed) such as wind power plant, steam power plant, organic rankine cycle (ORC) power plant or supercritical carbon dioxide cycle (sCO2) power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The thermal energy is selectively supplied to channels having a lower temperature than other channels in order to reduce temperature differences within the structure. The thermal energy storage provides a high degree of flexibility as the channels are shaped in a variety of ways.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of manufacturing a thermal energy storage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side-view of a thermal energy storage.

AU BAO G
AE BAO G (BAOG-Individual)
GA 202108270W
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model relates to the technical field of logistics transportation device, specifically to a food fresh-keeping transportation device, comprising a box body; the inner side of the box body is fixedly connected with a clapboard; the upper side of the clapboard is provided with several fixing frames fixedly connected with the box body; the inner side frame wall of the fixing frame is fixedly connected with a supporting plate; the top part of the supporting plate is provided with a placing mechanism for placing the food; the placing mechanism is connected with the fresh-keeping mechanism set at the lower side of the clapboard; the utility model is provided with a fresh-keeping mechanism; the nitrogen generated by the nitrogen manufacturing device can discharge the carbon dioxide in the box body; at the same time, the oxygen in the box body is lightly washed; avoiding the oxidation of food; at the same time, with cooling function, obviously improving the fresh-keeping effect; by setting the placing mechanism, using the through hole to discharge the water generated by the fruit and vegetable breathing effect; by setting the water guide pipe, the water can be directly discharged into the waste water tank; The invention avoids the secondary pollution to the fruit and vegetable caused by the shaking of the vehicle in the fixed frame.

PN CN112142897-A; CN112142897-B
TI Use of organosilane for preparing linear low density polyethylene from ethylene and alpha-olefin monomer and used as raw material for polyethylene extrusion foaming, where alpha-olefin monomer is 1-butene, 1-pentene, 1-hexene and 1-octene.
AU DONG J
   LIU X
   QIN Y
AE CHINESE ACAD SCI CHEM INST (CSCI-C)
GA 202103108G
AB    NOVELTY - Use of organosilane is claimed for preparing linear low density polyethylene from ethylene and alpha-olefin monomer, where alpha-olefin monomer is 1-butene, 1-pentene, 1-hexene, 1-heptene, and 1-octene, and organosilane is R1SiX2R2, where R1 is a 2-20C alpha-olefin group, X is a halogen, and R2 is a 1-20C linear, branched or isomerized alkyl group.
   USE - Organosilane for preparing linear low density polyethylene from ethylene and alpha-olefin monomer, and used as raw material for polyethylene extrusion foaming (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The organosilane has characteristic of long-chain branched structure and high melt strength, performs extrusion foaming process with carbon dioxide or nitrogen as foaming agent to prepare high-foaming polyethylene material, and replaces low-density polyethylene with butane or pentane as foaming agent to extrude and foam to prepare foamed polyethylene material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing linear low density polyethylene, which involves:
   (A) copolymerizing ethylene monomer and alpha-olefin monomer in presence of hydrogen and a catalyst to obtain a polymer;
   (B) subjecting polymer to water vapor treatment; and
   (C) removing ethylene before copolymerization of monomer and alpha-olefin monomer and/or during the copolymerization of ethylene monomer and alpha-olefin monomer, and adding organosilane to polymerization system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The X is F, Cl or Br. The organosilane is 9-decenylmethyldichlorosilane, 9-decenylethyldichlorosilane, 8-nonenylmethyldichlorosilane, 8-nonenylmethyldichlorosilane, 7-octenylmethyldichlorosilane, 7-octenylethyldichlorosilane, 6-heptenylmethyldichlorosilane, 6-heptenylethyldichlorosilane, 5-hexenylmethyldichlorosilane, 5-hexenylethyldichlorosilane, 4-pentenylmethyldichlorosilane, 4-pentenylethyldichlorosilane, 3-butenylmethyldichlorosilane and 3-butenylethyldichlorosilane, preferably 3-butenylmethyldichlorosilane, 4-pentenylmethyldichlorosilane, 5-hexenylmethyldichlorosilane, 6-heptenylmethyldichlorosilane and 7-octenylmethyldichlorosilane. The catalyst is Ziegler-Natta catalyst and chromium-based catalyst. Preferred Conditions: The amount of alpha-olefin monomer is 0.05-20 wt.% based on total weight of ethylene and alpha-olefin monomer. The amount of organosilane is 1x 10-6-20 pts. wt and hydrogen is 1x 10-4-10 pts. wt. relative to 100 pts. wt of ethylene monomer. The copolymerization is performed at pressure of 0.1-3MPa and temperature of 30-150 degrees C for 0.1-6 hours. The water vapor treatment is performed at a temperature of 80-120 degrees C for 5-60 minutes. The long-chain branch density of linear low-density polyethylene is 0.1-50 long-chain branches per 10000 carbon atoms, and linear low-density polyethylene has a density of 0.915-0.940g/cm3, melting temperature of more than 110 degrees C, crystallinity of more than 30% and melt strength of 18-45cN.

AU HU S
   LI L
   ZHENG Z
   LV M
   SUN Y
   CHANG L
AE HENAN SOAR VETERINARY PHARM CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
   HENAN MUXIANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 202103204G
AB    NOVELTY - Diluent of vonimulin hydrochloride comprises 85-90 wt.% water for injection, 1-3 wt.% 2,2-dihydroxymethyl butyric acid, 1-3 wt.% aqueous-beta-cyclodextrin polymer, 1-2 wt.% copovidone S630, 1-2 wt.% chelating agent, 1-3 wt.% emulsifier, 2-3 wt.% stabilizer.
   USE - Used as diluent of vonimulin hydrochloride.
   ADVANTAGE - The vornimulin hydrochloride has good needle penetration, no bulging in the injection position, good stability and stable clinical effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing vornemulin hydrochloride diluent comprising (A) adding 2,2-dimethylolbutyric acid, aqueous beta -cyclodextrin polymer, copovidone S630 and part water for injection into the reactor, and sealing the reactor, (B) stirring the mixed liquid through the stirring mechanism in the reactor, stirring, pressing and heating the inside of the reactor, raising the temperature in the reactor to the set temperature of 50-70 degrees C, and filling the reactor with carbon dioxide to the pressure in the reactor to the set pressure of 2-6 MPa, after the temperature and pressure reach the set value, maintaining it for 5~10 minutes and then continue to relieving the pressure to normal pressure to obtain the mixed liquid, (C) opening the reactor, adding chelating agents, emulsifiers, and stabilizers to the mixed solution, stirring, and continuously adding the remaining water for injection into the mixed solution, and (D) taking out the mixed liquid, filtering and sterilizing the mixed liquid to obtain the diluent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The diluent of vonimulin hydrochloride comprises 88 wt.% water for injection, 2 wt.% 2,2-dihydroxymethyl butyric acid, 3 wt.% aqueous-beta-cyclodextrin polymer, 1 wt.% copovidone S630, 2 wt.% chelating agent, 2 wt.% emulsifier, and 2 wt.% stabilizer. Preferred Components: The water for injection is deionized water and/or distilled water. The emulsifier is polyethylene glycol cetostearyl ether 20, vitamin E polyethylene glycol succinate, and/or castor oil polyoxyl ester 35. The chelating agent is potassium sodium tartrate, ammonium citrate, and/or ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid. The stabilizer is adenine and/or polypropylene glycol. In step (a), the amount of the part of the water for injection is 40-60 wt.% of the total water for injection. Preferred Method: In step (b), the rotation speed of the stirring is 5000-8000 revolutions/minute, and the time is 10-15 minutes. In step (c), the rotation speed of the stirring is 3000-4000 revolutions/minute, and the time is 8-10 minutes. In step (b), the temperature and pressure in the reactor are increased to the set value within 8 minutes. In step (d), a 0.22 mu m filter is used for filtration and sterilization.

TI Non-combustible gas generator for being coupled to vehicle, such as truck for fire fighting inside buildings, has combustion chamber where liquid or gaseous fuel is preferably burned, and compressed air supply comes from external compressor.
AU MANZANEDA GARRIDO J
   ABRIL DURO J
AE MANZANEDA GARRIDO J (GARR-Individual)
   ABRIL DURO J (DURO-Individual)
GA 2021101779
AB    NOVELTY - The non-combustible gas generator comprises a combustion chamber (C) where a liquid or gaseous fuel is preferably burned, resulting in the group of gasses carbon dioxide, nitrogen, hydrogen superoxide, all of them non-combustible. The compressed air supply (K) can come from an external compressor, or from a turbocharger driven by the gasses generated or by the steam from the evaporation chamber. A water chamber or circuit that, taking advantage of the heat of the combustion chamber, will produce a large volume of steam which is another non-combustible gas that will bind to the gasses generated by the combustion chamber.
   USE - Non-combustible gas generator for being coupled to any type of vehicle, such as truck, helicopter or a drone for fire fighting inside buildings.
   ADVANTAGE - In open-air fires, such as forest fires, they are attacked by directing the jet of gasses directly at the fire, being more effective downwind. In closed or semi-closed premises, the aim is to put in more volume of gasses than the amount of air that can enter through other ducts, thus the fire is put out quickly so that the damage is minimal.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a non-combustible gas generator.
   Tubes (A,B)
   Combustion chamber (C)
   Fuel supply (F)
   Compressed air supply (K)
   Spark plug or resistance (Z)

   XIA Y

GA 202100658L

PT P
PN CN112047525-A; CN112047525-B
TI Breaking cyanide resin by preheating pre-treated raw water, pouring into resin tank, standing, detecting cyanide ions, injecting raw water containing sodium cyanide into resin tank, adding lye, and reacting oxidant and cyanide.
AU LIU Z
   HUANG M
   LUAN H
   YIN S
   SUN Y
   WU S
AE YINGCHUANG SANZHENG YINGKOU FINE CHEM CO (YING-Non-standard)
GA 2020C4949Y
AB    NOVELTY - Breaking cyanide resin comprises preheating pre-treated raw water to 40-50 degrees C to obtain heated raw water, where raw water includes complex cyanide with detected concentration of 1600-2000 mg/L; pouring heated raw water into resin tank where pH of heated raw water is 3-5, standing for 40-50 hours so that resin in resin tank absorbs complexed cyanide, and detecting cyanide ions in raw water containing sodium cyanide concentration; injecting raw water containing sodium cyanide into resin tank, and repeating step until concentration of cyanide ion matches concentration of cyanide ion; injecting raw water containing sodium cyanide into reaction tank, putting oxidant with concentration of 600-800 mg/L into reaction tank, adding lye until pH of solution is greater than 10, and reacting oxidant and cyanide in sodium cyanide to convert cyanide ions into nitrogen and carbon dioxide gas. The filling gas is inert gas. The temperature in reactor is kept at room temperature.
   USE - The method is used for breaking cyanide resin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method not only uses resin to adsorb metal ions in raw water, but also uses oxidant to convert cyanide ions released from complex cyanide. The method removes complex cyanide in raw water, improves treatment effect of raw water and optimizes water circulation.

TI Powdered activated carbon carrier useful in sewage treatment applications, comprises water, ash content, and activated carbon particles with specified particle size, where carrier has specific iodine value, surface area and bulk density.
AU ZHU Y
AE PROBIOTIC SHANGHAI CO LTD (PROB-Non-standard)
GA 2020C1464U
AB    NOVELTY - Powdered activated carbon carrier comprises 2.8-3 %mass water, 11.8-12.2 %mass ash content, and greater than or equal to 80% activated carbon particles with a particle size of 200-325 mesh, where the carrier has an iodine value of 954-984 mg/l, a specific surface area of 850-855 m2/g, a bulk density of 0.54-0.59 g/cm3, and a pH of 6.8-8.5.
   USE - The carrier is useful in sewage treatment applications (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carrier is simple to operate and economical, and has prolonged life, wide range of applications, large specific surface area, so that the number of immobilized microorganisms is larger and excellent treatment effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for The method for preparing the carrier involving (a) crushing raw coal, pulverizing, drying and sieving to form coarse coal, heating and carbonizing to obtain carbonized coal, (b) activating the carbonized coal powder to form an activation material using carbon dioxide as a gas activator, and (c) grinding and sieving the activated material to obtain the product; #use of the powdered activated carbon carrier in sewage treatment involving compounding the powdered activated carbon carrier with chemical oxygen demand (COD) degrading bacteria, and adding it to the sewage treatment process system for reducing COD in the sewage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The carrier comprises 2.9 %mass water, 12 %mass ash content, and greater than or equal to 90% activated carbon particles with a particle size of more than 200 mesh. The mass ratio of the powdered activated carbon carrier to the COD degrading bacteria is (1:4)-(1:6). Preferred Properties: The carrier has an iodine value of 984 mg/l, a specific surface area of 852 m2/g, a bulk density of 0.57 g/cm3, and a pH of 7-8.4. Preferred Method: The step (a) involves heating and carbonizing at 200-500 degrees C with a heating rate of 3-5 degrees C/minute and a pressure of 80-100 kPa. The step (b) involves activating at 950 degrees C and a gas activator flow rate of 2 l/minute. The step (c) involves screening by filtering with a 200 mesh screen. The step of sewage treatment process system adopts any one of activated sludge method system, anoxic aerobic method system, anaerobic-anoxic-aerobic method system, periodic cycle activated sludge method system, and sequential batch activity sludge method system.

   HU H
   GUO Y
   ZHANG K
   WANG Y
AE NO 38 RES INST CHINA ELECTRONIC SCI & TE (CETC-C)
GA 2020B2117G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves bonding a welding sheet                (2) on a carrier plate (1). A solder column array                (21) is cut on the welding sheet. The solder column                array is processed. The redundant welding sheet is                teared or peeled off. The carrier plate and a                bonding pad (3) which is provided to manufacture                the convex point area array is aligned. A flip-chip                welding is performed. The solder column array is                transferred to the bonding pad to manufacture the                convex point area array. The bonding pad is                reflowed with the solder column array. The solder                column array is melted into a ball. A regular                solder bump array (22) is formed. A UV or                carbon-dioxide picosecond laser processing device                is used. An inert or reducing atmosphere protection                is used, when the welding sheet is an gold-tin                welding sheet.

TI Mesoporous titania nanoparticle having oxygen            vacancies and titanium(III) ion defects at specific            signal intensity used to degrade e.g. 2-chlorophenol,            dyes, pesticide, aromatic compounds and herbicides, and            produce hydrogen and hydrocarbon.
AU JALIL A A
   TRIWAHYONO S
   JAAFAR N F B
AE UNIV MALAYSIA TEKNOLOGI UTM (UYMA-Non-standard)
GA 2022940405
AB    NOVELTY - Mesoporous titania nanoparticle comprising                oxygen vacancies at a signal intensity of 900-1800                a.u. and titanium(III) ion defects at a signal                intensity of 900-1300 a.u. so that to degrade                84-96% 2-chlorophenol over a time period of 0.5-6                hours.
   USE - The nanoparticle is useful for degrading                2-chlorophenol (claimed), chlorinated phenol, dyes,                pesticide, aromatic compounds, and herbicides,                producing hydrogen from water and producing                hydrocarbon from water and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The nanoparticle: degrades 84-96%                2-chlorophenol over a time period of 0.5-6 hours;                is produced by microwave irradiation method which                is economic, time saving, energy saving, has high                productivity with better photolytic activity's                quality end product and provides good aging and                fast reaction environment; has enhanced photolytic                activity, and minor decrement of the reusability                performance of 91-82%; and has a better                photocatalytic activity under the visible compared                with UV light irradiation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                producing mesoporous titania nanoparticle                comprising dissolving a surfactant in a mixture of                water, propanol and ammonia to form a solution,                adding titanium(IV) isopropoxide to the solution,                subjecting the titanium containing solution to                microwave irradiation power operating at 0.12-0.56                Wg-1 for 110-150 minutes so as to produce the                mesoporous titania nanoparticle having an average                particle size of 11.36-13.25 nm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The surfactant is                  cetyltrimethyl ammonium bromide,                  octyltrimethylammonium bromide,                  tetradecyltrimethyl ammonium bromide, sodium                  dodecyl sulfate and sodium dioctylsulfosuccinate.                  The molar ratio of the surfactant to titanium(IV)                  isopropoxide is 0.55-1.25 and 1-1.7.

PN US2020196650-A1; WO2020132217-A2; WO2020132217-A3
TI Seasoning compositions for use in the culinary arts, comprises additives, food-grade sodium bicarbonate as base and citric acid as dried acid, where additives include water-insoluble/soluble components, buffers, thickeners, and flavorants.
AU LAURENT A D
   LAURENT R N
AE BROTH BOMB LLC (BROT-Non-standard)
   BROTH BOMB LLC (BROT-Non-standard)
GA 202057085J
AB    NOVELTY - Seasoning composition comprises one or more additives, a base and a dried acid. The base and the dried acid creates an effervescent effect when added to a quantity of water.
   USE - Seasoning compositions for promoting food tenderness (claimed) used in culinary arts.
   ADVANTAGE - The base and the dried acid have an effervescent effect when added to a quantity of water to improve the desirability of a recipe, increasing the breakdown of foodstuff in the recipe, and enhancing the flavor uptake of the foodstuff within the recipe.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the folllowing:
   (1) a method of using the composition, which involves immersing the composition in a volume of food containing water, dissolving the composition so that the food-grade sodium bicarbonate and the food-grade citric acid can interact to produce carbon dioxide for promoting food tenderness; and
   (2) a method of manufacturing a seasoning for use with food, which involves combining food additives with a food-grade sodium bicarbonate, wetting the combination of the food additives and the food-grade sodium bicarbonate with a mist of liquid with a generally neutral pH level, adding a dry powdered food-grade citric acid, forming the mixture to form a predetermined shape and drying the mixture to solidify the combination in the predetermined shape.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The one or more additives include one or more water-insoluble components, one or more water-soluble components, one or more buffers, one or more thickeners, one or more flavorants, one or more sweeteners, one or more processing aids, and one or more surfactants. The base is sodium bicarbonate. The dried acid is citric acid. The sodium bicarbonate is food-grade sodium bicarbonate, and the citric acid is food-grade citric acid. Preferred Conditions: The food-grade sodium bicarbonate and the food-grade citric acid are provided at a ratio between 2:3 and 2:1 respectively. The one or more additives, the base and the dried acid are provided in a first layer, and the one or more additives are also provided in a second layer. The composition is provided in multiple forms. The food-grade sodium bicarbonate and the food-grade citric acid have an effervescent effect when added to a quantity of water, and chemical reaction facilitates the breakdown of a foodstuff and the uptake of the one or more additives. All ingredients of the seasoning composition are compressed together and solidified to form a predetermined shape.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN111244489-A; CN111244489-B
TI Electrode material used in zinc bromine single flow battery, has positive electrode material that is activated graphite felt obtained by carbonizing, graphitizing at high temperature and activating pre-oxidized felt.
AU YUAN C
   LI X
   ZHENG Q
   ZHANG H
   LIU X
   ZHENG J
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
   HUAQIN ENERGY STORAGE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUAQ-Non-standard)
GA 2020516373
AB    NOVELTY - The electrode material has a positive electrode material which is an activated graphite felt obtained by carbonization, graphitization at high temperature and activation treatment of a pre-oxidized felt. The negative electrode material is a carbon felt obtained by carbonization of the pre-oxidized felt. A positive and negative electrolytes of zinc bromine single flow battery are zinc bromide solutions. The positive and negative electrolytes of the zinc-bromine single-flow battery are both zinc bromide solutions, and the zinc bromide concentration is 2-4 mol/L. The carbonization process of the positive electrode pre-oxygen felt places the pre-oxygen felt in a carbonization furnace at a temperature of 1000-1800 degrees C for 5-10 minutes to obtain a carbon felt. The activation process is sulfuric acid activation or steam and carbon dioxide activation.
   USE - Electrode material used in zinc bromine single flow battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The single flow battery undergoes carbonization treatment without high-temperature graphitization and sulfuric acid oxidation activation processes during preparation of negative electrode. The generation of redundant oxygen-containing functional groups is eliminated on the surface of the electrode, so that the surface of the electrode is more uniform. The electrode is not activated by sulfuric acid and the activity of electrode is low, which slows the deposition rate of zinc and is more conducive to improving the uniformity of zinc deposition.

PT P
PN CN110985102-A; CN110985102-B
TI Sulfuric acid slag composite explosion suppression material useful for suppressing coal dust explosion in the field of mining engineering comprises ettringite, iron glue, hydrated silicate gel, calcium hydroxide and calcium carbonate.
AU GONG C
   LEI J
   GAO X
   LU L
AE UNIV JINAN SHANDONG (UNJN-C)
   UNIV JINAN (UNJN-C)
GA 202031649Q
AB    NOVELTY - Sulfuric acid slag composite explosion suppression material comprises ettringite, iron glue, hydrated silicate gel, calcium hydroxide and calcium carbonate.
   USE - The sulfuric acid slag composite explosion suppression material is useful for suppressing coal dust explosion in the field of mining engineering (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The suppression material: can synergistically suppress explosion in stages, and has excellent explosion suppression effect. The method: realizes the resource utilization of sulfuric acid slag.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing sulfuric acid slag composite explosion suppression material comprising mixing the sulfuric acid slag powder, coal powder, gypsum, calcium oxide and water uniformly, drying the obtained wet powder and calcinating, and after completion, crushing the calcined product to obtain the active powder, (2) placing the active powder obtained in step (1) in a mixed solution of calcium hydroxide solution, aluminate coupling agent, water-soluble hexametaphosphate and persulfate, heating and soaking, separating upper layer flocculent turbid liquid and the lower layer residue, (3) washing the flocculent turbid liquid obtained in step (2), drying and pulverizing to obtain ultra-fine powder, (4) steam-curing the residue obtained in step (2) under heat and pressure conditions, placing the residue under heat and pressure conditions, carbonizing in a carbon dioxide atmosphere, after completion, mixing the carbonized product and the super fine powder to obtain explosive suppression material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In the step (1), raw material comprises 40-70 pts. wt. sulfuric acid slag powder, 5-10 pts. wt. coal powder, 3-10 pts. wt. gypsum, 10-20 pts. wt. calcium oxide and 2- 5 pts. wt. water. Preferred Method: In the step (1), the calcination temperature is 1000-1100 degrees C and the time is 2-3 hours. In the step (1), before drying, the slurry is first pressed into blocks, and drying, the particle size of the active powder is 80-300 mesh. In the step (2), the hexametaphosphate includes sodium hexametaphosphate or potassium hexametaphosphate. In the step (2), the persulfate salt includes ammonium persulfate, sodium persulfate, and/or potassium persulfate. In the step (2), the addition ratio of the active powder, calcium hydroxide solution, aluminate coupling agent, persulfate and sodium hexametaphosphate is 40-60:180-230:1-3:1-3:15-28, and the mass concentration of calcium hydroxide is 5-9 wt.%, the heating temperature is 50-60 degrees C, and the soaking time is 4-6 hours. In the step (3), performing the washing by water washing, preferably tap water washing or distilled water washing. In the step (3), the drying temperature is 50-60 degrees C and the time is 12-14 hours, in the step (3), the particle size of the ultrafine powder is not less than 300 mesh, in the step (4), steam-curing the residue at 150-200 degrees C and 1.2-1.6 MPa for 8-12 hours, alternatively, in the step (4), carbonizing the residue for 2-4.5 hours under the conditions of 2-3 MPa, 80-85% carbon dioxide concentration and 30-55 degrees C. The step (2) further comprises secondary extraction of the residue obtained in step (2), steaming the residue not directly, using residue of step (1) and (2) to obtain the secondary flocculent turbid liquid and secondary residue, preparing the secondary flocculent turbid liquid into secondary ultrafine powder by the method of step (3), carbonizing the secondary residue by the method of step (4), and after completion, the ultrafine powder, and mixing the secondary ultrafine powder and the carbonized product to obtain an explosion suppression material.

PT P
PN CN110882380-A
TI Liver-protecting liquor additive used e.g. for            reducing fever includes giant salamander albumen            extract, giant salamander small molecule peptide, radix            scutellariae, large-headed atractylodes rhizome, and            root of kudzu vine.
AU FU J
   XIA Y
   JI J
   YANG B
AE FU J (FUJJ-Individual)
   HUBEI SHIHOU YAOBANG JITIAN AGRIC DEV CO (HUBE-Non-standard)
   XIA Y (XIAY-Individual)
   JI J (JIJJ-Individual)
   YANG B (YANG-Individual)
GA 202022495V
AB    NOVELTY - Liver-protecting liquor additive comprises                18-26 pts. wt. giant salamander albumen extract,                10-12 pts. wt. giant salamander small molecule                peptide, 6-12 pts. wt. radix scutellariae,7-13pts.                wt. large-headed atractylodes rhizome, 4-6 pts. wt.                root of kudzu vine, 9-18 pts. wt. Salvia                miltiorrhiza, 12-18 pts. wt. schizandra berry, 3-8                pts. wt. hawthorn, and 6-12 pts. wt.                honeysuckle.
   USE - The liver-protecting liquor additive is used                for reducing fever, detoxifying, nourishing the                stomach, protecting the liver, and calming the                nerves (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The liver-protecting liquor additive has                simple formulation, easy to obtain raw materials,                and simple preparation, can be directly mixed with                the liquor, does not affect the taste of wine, and                ensures the health of drinkers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the liver-protecting liquor additive                comprising weighing each raw material, crushing                large-headed atractylodes rhizome, obtaining                large-headed atractylodes rhizome extract by carbon                dioxide (CO2) supercritical extraction, weighing                radix scutellariae, ultrasonically extracting with                ethanol, concentrating, eluting with macroporous                resin column, drying to obtain radix scutellariae                extract, weighing honeysuckle, hawthorn, kudzu vine                root, S. miltiorrhiza and schizandra berry, mixing                and crushing, stewing, drying, obtaining mixed                medicine extract, weighing the large-headed                atractylodes rhizome extract, radix scutellariae                extract, giant salamander albumen extract, giant                salamander small-molecular peptide, and mixed                medicine extract, and uniformly mixing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises mixing the liver-protecting liquor                  additive with liquor at 1-5:100 mass ratio. The                  giant salamander albumen extract is prepared by                  collecting giant salamander adhesive liquid,                  mixing with alkali protease at 10:1.5-2 mass                  ratio, adjusting pH at 9.5-10, carrying out                  enzymolysis at 50-55 degrees C for 2-3 hours,                  inactivating enzyme, centrifuging, taking the                  supernatant, decoloring with activated carbon at                  3-5:10 mass ratio to remove the fishy smell,                  centrifuging again, taking supernatant, filtering                  with 0.45 mu m filter membrane, sterilizing,                  freeze drying, and crushing to obtain giant                  salamander albumen extract. The giant salamander                  small molecule peptide is prepared by taking                  fresh giant salamander meat, giant salamander                  bone and giant salamander skin, mincing into                  paste, adding water at material liquid ratio of                  10 g:10-15 ml, adding complex enzyme, mixing,                  conducting enzymolysis for 3-4 hours at 45-50                  degrees C, enzyme inactivating, sterilizing,                  freeze drying, and crushing to obtain giant                  salamander small molecular peptide, where complex                  enzyme is mixture of trypsin and elastase at 1:1                  volume ratio, and the mass ratio of complex                  enzyme and paste is 0.5-1:10. The large-headed                  atractylodes rhizome extract is prepared by                  crushing large-headed atractylodes rhizome into                  80-100 mesh, extracting the large-headed                  atractylodes rhizome powder with carbon dioxide                  (CO2) medium at 25-28 MPa and 45-50 degrees C,                  separating in CO2 medium at 5-8 MPa and 35-45                  degrees C, and obtaining large-headed                  atractylodes rhizome extract, where the flow rate                  of CO2 is 60-65 L/hour. The radix scutellariae                  extract is prepared by crushing radix                  scutellariae into 100-120 mesh, mixing with                  ethanol at 1 g:100-150 ml material liquid ratio,                  soaking for 1-2 hours, ultrasonically extracting                  at 500-600 W for 20-40 minutes, filtering to                  obtain extract solution, concentrating at 50-70                  degrees C to 30-40% of the original volume,                  putting into macroporous resin column to elute,                  adding 70-80% ethanol water solution, eluting                  with 10-12 column volume, collecting the eluate,                  vacuum drying at 80-90 degrees C, and obtaining                  radix scutellariae extract.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Component: The liver-protecting                  liquor additive comprises 19-24 pts. wt,                  preferably 22 pts. wt. giant salamander albumen                  extract, 10-11 pts. wt, preferably 10.5 pts. wt.                  giant salamander small molecule peptide, 8-11                  pts. wt, preferably 9 pts. wt. radix                  scutellariae, 9-10 pts. wt, preferably 10 pts.                  wt. large-headed atractylodes rhizome, 5-6 pts.                  wt, preferably 5 pts. wt. root of kudzu vine,                  13-16 pts. wt, preferably 14 pts. wt. PN CN110042014-A
TI Mild clearing agent for kitchen pipes, comprises trimethylchlorosilane, bentonite, surfactant, foaming agent, and further comprises compound dredging base material and compounding materials.
AU CHEN T
AE CHEN T (CHEN-Individual)
GA 201966014Q
AB    NOVELTY - Mild clearing agent comprises 3-7 pts. wt. trimethylchlorosilane, 1-4 pts. wt. bentonite, 3-7 pts. wt. surfactant, 2-5 pts. wt. foaming agent, and further comprises 25-40 pts. wt. compound dredging base material and 12-20 pts. wt. compounding materials.
   USE - Mild clearing agent is used for kitchen pipes.
   ADVANTAGE - The mild clearing agent solves the problem that the common pipe dredging agent itself is alkaline and causes the local temperature of the pipe to be too high to damage the pipe, and the ferrous nitrate and ferric nitrate of the present innovation produce nanometer ferroferric oxide under an alkaline system, which acts as a cooling and osmotic effect on the blocked pipe, and causes the blockage portion to be contracted by cold to hinder the formation of the blockage, and the innovation adds a selenium-containing reagent, can react with iron plasma at a low temperature to form a metal selenide, and can cooperate with subsequent foaming and viscosity-reducing effects to quickly achieve the dredging effect, and the added phospholipid has an emulsification effect, hence destroying the fat, and after the gas overflows, the tiny bubbles can be adsorbed around the grease, and the polyallylamine is stored in combination with the carbon dioxide, and with the penetration of the blocking substance, the viscosity-reducing and clearing effect is further improved, and the corrosion of the pipeline and the high-temperature damage can be avoided.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method for preparing the composite dredging base, which involves taking 8-15 pts. wt. ferrous nitrate, 15-30 pts. wt. ferric nitrate, 80-100 pts. wt. water at 25-35 degrees C to mixing, then adjust the pH to obtain a mixture, then adding the mixture to a mixture of 3-7 wt.% of lecithin, mixing with 5-8 wt.% of Tween-80, mixing at constant temperature, and keeping warm for standing. The aging liquid is obtained, and the aging liquid is concentrated under reduced pressure to obtain a concentrated liquid, and the concentrated liquid is spray-granulated to obtain hollow nano-beads, and at 1-4 degrees C, the crushed ice, additive and water are mixed according to the mass ratio of 1:0.04:6-10, and the crushed ice liquid is obtained. According to the mass fraction, 40-60 pts. wt. crushed ice liquid are taken, 6-10 pts. wt. hollow nanobeads, 10-15 pts. wt. absolute ethanol, 12-20 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol, 7-12 pts. wt. citric acid solution, 2-5 pts. wt. auxiliary, 8-14 pts. wt. polymer, at 2-5 degrees C, then crushed ice liquid, hollow nano-beads, absolute ethanol, and polyethylene glycol are mixed and stirred, and citric acid solution, auxiliary agent, and polymer material are added, stirred at a high speed, vacuum concentrated, and refrigerated to obtain a composite dredging base. Preferred Conditions: The additive is obtained by mixing lactose and mannitol at a mass ratio of 1:3-7, hence obtaining an additive. The auxiliary agent is obtained by mixing microcrystalline paraffin, magnesium stearate and castor oil according to a mass ratio of 1:2-4:1, hence obtaining an auxiliary agent. The selenium-containing reagent is obtained by mixing a mixture of diphenyl selenide and diphenyl diselenide according to a mass ratio of 4-8:3, hence obtaining a selenium-containing reagent. The foaming agent is prepared by mixing a fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether sulfate and petroleum ether at a mass ratio of 3-6:1. The surfactant is obtained by mixing dodecyl dimethyl betaine and sodium secondary alkylbenzene sulfonate in a mass ratio of 2-4:1 to obtain a surfactant.

PN DE102018000926-A1
TI Method for driving e.g. car, involves driving gas compressor by fuel cell engine and waste heat from fuel cell or compression energy from gas compressor, and regenerating expanded liquefied gas after liquefied gas is heated to high pressure.
AU SEUFFERHELD J E
AE SEUFFERHELD J E (SEUF-Individual)
GA 201942028H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves utilizing a piston drive motor with a regenerative carbon dioxide circulation part for driving a vehicle. A right-handed cyclic process of the drive motor is carried out by a left-handed cyclic process of a cooling circuit and shown in a pressure-enthalpy diagram. Left-handed cooling circuit is used to liquefy right-handed high-pressure gas, which is used to drive an engine (17) i.e. fuel cell engine. A gas compressor is driven by the fuel cell engine and waste heat from a fuel cell or compression energy from the gas compressor. The expanded liquefied gas is regenerated after the liquefied gas is heated to high pressure. A regulating valve (24) is used to regulate flow rate of the liquefied gas.
   USE - Method for driving a vehicle e.g. car, lorry and ship.

Potassium chloride and concentrated sulfuric acid are separately metered and added to the reaction furnace. The outside the furnace wall is heated, the material is heated and reacted under the action of stirring and turning at the bottom of the furnace. The hydrogen chloride gas generated by the reaction is absorbed by the by-product hydrochloric acid, and the solid material after the reaction is potassium sulfate product. The a special alloy steel furnace with high heat transfer coefficient or other materials capable of achieving the same heat transfer and corrosion resistance (such as a silicon carbide furnace) is used as a reaction furnace body.

   LI X
AE SICHUAN JUCHUANG GRAPHENE TECHNOLOGY CO (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 2019286665
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphene oxide/carbon nanotube/carbon nano onion cement-based composite material, comprises (a) dispersing graphene oxide, carbon nanotubes and carbon nano onions in a solution to form mixing liquid; (b) adding cement to the mixed solution and stirring uniformly to form a cement slurry; (c) adding a filler to the cement slurry to form a graphene oxide/carbon nanotube/carbon nano onion cement-based composite material, where the oxidation is performed by weight the amount of graphene is 0.01-5 % of the cement, the amount of the carbon nanotubes is 0.01-3 % of the cement, and the amount of the carbon nano onion is 0.05-3 % of the cement; and (d) The filler is used in an amount of 200- 350 % of the cement.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing graphene oxide/carbon nanotube/carbon nano onion cement-based composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can improve the mechanical properties and corrosion resistance of the cement material; improves the tensile strength, flexural strength, crack resistance and compressive strength of the cement-based composite material; can effectively prevent various harmful media e.g. chloride ion, carbon dioxide and sulfuric acid and erosion and destruction of cement-based composite materials caused by root ion invasion; has simple preparation process, and is environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the product obtained by the above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises aging and curing the cement-based composite material to form a solid graphene oxide/carbon nanotube/carbon nano onion cement base composite material. The step of forming the mixed solution by the solution of carbon nanotube, graphene oxide solution and carbon nano-onion uniformly mixing is achieved. The step of forming the cement slurry further comprises adding dispersing agent into the mixed solution, water reducing agent and the antifoaming agent, where the filler is sand and/or slag. Preferred Components: The amount of graphene oxide is 1.5%, cement is 3% and the carbon nano-tube accounts for the cement of 1.2-2%, the amount of the carbon nano-onion of the cement is 1-2%.The amount of the filler is the cement of 250-300%.

AE UNIV SHAANXI NORMAL (UNSN-C)

   USE - Stable porous cobalt complex for absorbing gas of small molecule and for the separation of small molecules of gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Stable porous cobalt complex has good adsorption ability, and has good separating ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Stable porous cobalt complex is represented by a linear formula, that is (Co4 ( mu 2-fluorobezyloxy) 3 ( mu 3-SO4) (PTB) (TPP) 2), where PTB represents removing three -COOH on hydrogen atom of -2, 4, 6-acidmonovalent anion, TPP represents pyridine, 2, 4, 6-tri-(4-pyridine), single crystal structure of the coordination compound belongs to cubic system, P213 space group, cell parameter is alpha =90 degrees , beta =90 degrees, gamma =90 degrees . An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing stable porous cobalt complex, which involves using cobalt sulfate, -2, 4, 6-tri-benzoic acid, -2, 4, 6-tri-(4-pyridine) according to mol ratio of 2:0.5-1:0.5-1, adding fluoroboric acid and N, N-dimethylacetamide, ethanol, 1, 4-dioxane whose volume ratio is 1:10-15:5-8:5-8 in the mixed solution, stirring uniformly, standing the reaction at 120-135 degrees C for 4-6 days constant temperature in the closed condition to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The small gas molecule is methane, carbon dioxide, acetylene, ethylene, and ethane. Preferred Condition: The stable porous cobalt complex is immersed in acetonitrile for 3-5 days, and replaced every day, acetonitrile is used for 1-2 times, and then degassed under vacuum at 115-135 degrees C for 10-16 hours.

PN KR2019025095-A
TI Producing catalytic materials used for complete combustion of hydrocarbons and reducing greenhouse gases, comprises mixing e.g. tin oxide, aluminum oxide, zinc oxide, manganese oxide, cobalt oxide, tungsten oxide and chromium oxide.
AU JUNG C S
AE JUNG C S (JUNG-Individual)
GA 201925657A
AB    NOVELTY - Producing catalytic materials comprises mixing 30-75 pts. wt. tin oxide, aluminum oxide, zinc oxide, manganese oxide, cobalt oxide, tungsten oxide, chromium oxide, nickel oxide, titanium oxide, magnesium oxide, nickel, cerium oxide, calcium and/or antimony; 20-50 pts. wt. citric acid, succinic acid, hydrofluoric acid, nitric acid, hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, phosphoric acid and/or lactic acid; 3-25 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium silicate, lithium and/or magnesium; 0.05-7 pts. wt. sodium-ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid; and 30-50 pts. wt. silica, monazite, talc, illite, iron ore, calcium, gypsum, and/or zeolite.
   USE - The catalytic materials are useful in contamination prevention system to generate complete combustion energy by using water for complete combustion of hydrocarbons and reducing greenhouse gases (claimed), preferably in automobiles and internal combustion engines.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalytic materials reduce exhaust gas emitted from vehicle to near perfect level using water contained in air as energy source, reduce amount of emissions due to excessive emission of carbon dioxide, completely burn nitrogen oxides (NOx) and carbonaceous materials in automobiles and internal combustion engines to reduce soot or carbon monoxide, saves energy, minimizes environmental pollution, and prevent bronchial obstruction and other diseases caused by fine dust in residence space by preventing soot or carbon monoxide generated by incomplete combustion from being emitted in catalyst system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (a) crushing 30-75 pts. wt. tin oxide, aluminum oxide, zinc oxide, manganese oxide, cobalt oxide, tungsten oxide, chromium oxide, nickel oxide, titanium oxide, magnesium oxide, nickel, cerium oxide, calcium and/or antimony to 325 mesh (43 mu m) particle size, (b) charging 20-50 pts. wt. citric acid, succinic acid, hydrofluoric acid, nitric acid, hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, phosphoric acid and/or lactic acid into reaction tank, (c) slowly adding crushed material of step (a) into reaction tank, (d) heating at 130-200 degrees C, (e) slowly adding 3-25 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium silicate, lithium and/or magnesium to have neutral value, (f) preparing suspension containing 100 pts. wt. general water and 0.05-7 pts. wt. sodium-ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid as coupling agent, (g) adding the suspension into reaction product obtained in the step (e), (h) adding 30-50 pts. wt. silica, monazite, talc, illite, iron ore, calcium, gypsum, and/or zeolite, and mixing, (i) drying the mixture in hot air drier at 150-200 degrees CC for 10-20 hours, and (j) pulverizing the dried product in a pulverizer.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109438996-A; CN109438996-B
TI Preparing biomass plastic based on animal skin comprises e.g. degreasing animal skin, unhairing, expanding, splitting, softening, buffering water and animal skin with phosphoric acid phosphate, treating with glyceraldehyde and adjusting pH.
AU SHAN Z
   WANG Y
   DING X
   CHEN H
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 201924872U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing biomass plastic based on animal skin comprises degreasing the animal skin, unhairing, expanding, splitting, softening, buffering 100 pts. wt. water, and 100 pts. wt. animal skin with 5-10 pts. wt. phosphoric acid phosphate to a pH of 5-5.5 according to weight ratiol, treating with 2-5 pts. wt. glyceraldehyde, adjusting pH of sodium bicarbonate to 6-6.5 to carry out filling treatment and toughening treatment, draining, washing off the surface residue with 100 pts. wt. water for 10 minutes, pouring out, squeezing water, performing shaving process according to thickness requirements, vacuum dewatering at 40-50 degrees C to 20-35% water, hot-press molding at 5-10 MPa, at 90-120 degrees C for 20-30 minutes with a flat plate ironing machine, and finally water-proof coating according to the requirement.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing biomass plastic based on animal skin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces the excessive dependence of light plastic manufacturing on petroleum and carbon dioxide emissions; the prepared plastic is non-toxic and degradable; has no volatile organic compound emissions during use; is semi-transparent; and has beautiful decorative function.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for biomass plastic based on animal skin prepared by the above mentioned method to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The animal skin is sheep skin, goat skin, cow hide, or pig skin. Preferred Method: The filling treatment comprises using 10 pts. wt. sodium hexametaphosphate, and calcium chloride according to the mass ratio of 1:1, or 10 pts. wt. sodium hexametaphosphate, and calcium nitrate according to the mass ratio of 1:1, rotating and filling at 30 degrees C for 6-10 hours in the drum, and allowing to stand for 4-6 hours. The toughening treatment is carried out by using 1-5 pts. wt. glycerol, and 1-5 pts. wt. triethanolamine.

PN CN109251891-A; CN109251891-B
TI Amplifying peripheral blood mononuclear cell (PBMC) by cluster of differentiation (CD) 40 combined to programmed death-ligand (PD-L) and cytokine includes diluting human peripheral blood, centrifuging, adding cytokine solution and culturing.
AU GU Y
   ZHANG X
   HUANG Z
   LIU C
AE UNIV SOOCHOW FIRST AFFILIATED HOSPITAL (USWZ-C)
   UNIV SUZHOU FIRST AFFILIATED HOSPITAL (USWZ-C)
GA 201910844S
AB    NOVELTY - Amplifying peripheral blood mononuclear cell (PBMC) by cluster of differentiation (CD) 40 combined with programmed death-ligand (PD-L) and cytokine comprises (a) mixing and diluting the normal human peripheral blood with phosphate-buffered saline and then performing Ficoll density gradient centrifugation, (b) then achieving 4 layers of liquid after completing centrifugation, then pipetting the intermediate mist layer cells with a pipette, then washing and centrifuging with 10 times volumes of phosphate-buffered saline to obtain PBMC, (c) then mixing the PBMC cells with Roswell Park memorial institute (RMPI) 1640 complete medium to obtain a cell suspension, then transferring it to a 6-well plate and 24-well plate, then adding corresponding cytokine solution to each group of cell culture solution and then placing the plate in a carbon dioxide incubator for culturing, and (d) then adding cytokine solution once for every 2-3 days depending on the cell state.
   USE - The method is useful for amplifying peripheral blood mononuclear cell (PBMC) by cluster of differentiation 40 (CD) 40combined with programmed death-ligand (PD-L) and cytokine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can amplify PBMC to prepare novel adoptive immunotherapy cells as an entry point; has low requirements on experiments, simple operation, high collection rate of adoptive immune cells and has high purity; and is time saving.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The RMPI 1640 complete medium comprises 10% calf serum. The 6-well plate is 5x 106/well. The 24-well plate is 2x 105/well. Preferred Method: The method comprises adding the cell culture liquid to the corresponding cytokine solution comprising CD3 induced group, adding 1000 U/ml interferon- gamma in the beginning i.e. 0 day, then adding 1000 U/ml interleukin-2, 100 U/ml interleukin-1 alpha and 100 ng/ml anti-CD3 monoclonal antibody after 24 hours. The method comprises adding cell culture liquid to the corresponding cytokine solution comprising CD40 excitation group, adding 2 mu g/ml monoclonal anti-mmr/cd206/mannose receptor antibody (5C11), 1 mu g/ml programmed death-ligand 1, 30 ng/ml anti-CD3 monoclonal antibody, 300 U/ml interleukin-2, 5 ng/ml interleukin-7 and 5ng/ml interleukin-15 at the beginning, i.e. 0 day. The method comprises supplementing the CD3 group with 1000 U/ml interleukin-2 during the addition of cytokine solution. The method comprises supplementing the CD40 group with 200 U/ml interleukin-2, 2 ng/ml interleukin-7 and 2 ng/ml interleukin-15 during the addition of cytokine solution. The Ficoll density gradient centrifugation comprises firstly placing the Ficoll at room temperature condition, which is advancely added with Fahrenheit tube, then according to the Ficoll and diluted blood ratio i.e. 1:2 diluting the blood slowly along the tube wall and centrifuging in a temperature-controlled centrifuge. The culture conditions in the carbon dioxide incubator comprises having carbon dioxide concentration of 5%, temperature of 37 degrees C and saturated humidity of 95%. The centrifugation is carried out at 20-24 degrees C at a rate of 1800 revolutions/minute for 30 minutes.

TI External optical feedback element for tuning output beam of gas laser that has multiple wavelengths, comprises reflecting optical element that is positioned on beam path of the output beam outside of internal optical cavity of gas laser.
AU LIMANOVIC J
   BETHEL J
   WHEELER R
   KOLSCH P
   WELLE R
   LITMANOVIC J
AE NOVANTA CORP (NOVA-Non-standard)
   NOVANTA CORP (NOVA-Non-standard)
   NOVANTA CORP (NOVA-Non-standard)
   NOVANTA CORP (NOVA-Non-standard)
GA 2018898866
AB    NOVELTY - The external optical feedback element comprises a partially reflecting optical element that is positioned on a beam path (106) of the output beam outside of an internal optical cavity (104) of the gas laser (102). A stage (114) is provided to support the optical element and adjust a rotation, a horizontal tilt angle, and a vertical tilt angle of the optical element with respect to the beam path of the output beam. The output beam is partially reflected at the optical element and fed back into the internal optical cavity of the gas laser through the beam path. A transparent optic is used with a dielectric coating (112) that provides reflectivity varying with wavelengths. A gas laser is a carbon dioxide laser.
   USE - External optical feedback element for tuning an output beam of a gas laser that has multiple wavelengths.
   ADVANTAGE - The external optical feedback element provides feedback into the laser cavity to enhance a desired output beam wavelength, either at one output wavelength (single line) or at multiple wavelengths (multiple lines) simultaneously.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for tuning an output beam of a gas laser has multiple wavelengths.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an external optical feedback element.
   Laser (102)
   Internal optical cavity (104)
   Beam path (106)
   Dielectric coating (112)
   Stage (114)
DC V08 (Lasers and Masers)
MC V08-A01A2; V08-A01D; V08-A03C1; V08-A03C2; V08-A04B
