PT P
PN CN108708225-A; CN108708225-B
TI Preparing stenciled cigarette paper involves obtaining wet paper by pulping, pressing and dehydrating papermaking by a paper-feeding roller, putting it to a plaque-like stainless steel roll and pressing it with rubber roll.
AU DU W
   TAO X
   XING L
AE CHUZHOU CIGARETTE MATERIALS FACTORY (CNTA-C)
GA 201886103K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing stenciled cigarette paper involves obtaining wet paper by pulping, pressing and dehydrating papermaking by a paper-feeding roller, putting it to a plaque-like stainless steel roll and pressing it with rubber roll to form a lattice-shaped stamping, embossing and then drying paper to obtain dried paper piece. The paper piece is marked on embossed surface of check mark and corrugated area, and corrugated area facing the marked pleat area in the drying cylinder is draped by the blade. The iron oxide, 20-40% hydrochloric acid solution, gelatin and polyethylene glycol are mixed and stirred in a mass ratio of (1-1.5):(180-200):(4-5):(24-26) to obtain a dyeing and coating solution. The coating solution is poured into dyeing tank of printing machine with corresponding plaid, and dyeing solution is applied on cigarette paper through dyeing tank, and then feed it into oven for drying at 100-120 degrees C to obtain final cigarette paper.
   USE - Method for preparing stenciled cigarette paper.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare stenciled cigarette paper, which has excellent catalytic properties of iron oxide, converts harmful component such as carbon monoxide into carbon dioxide, reduces damage effect, improves harm reduction efficiency, effectively increases appearance quality and suction quality of paper, and increases added value of cigarette.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The wet paper is pressed and dehydrated to control moisture content to be less than 30%. The lattice shape includes two directions of indentation, and indentations in the same direction are parallel to each other, and angle of oblique intersection between indentations in different directions is 55-75 degrees . The indentation spacing in same direction is 0.5-0.8cm, indentation depth is 0.1-0.2mm, and indentation width is 0.1-0.15mm. The coating area and the corrugated area are arranged at intervals, and each zone has diamond-shaped region formed by the embossing indentation. The angle between tangential line of blade edge and cylinder surface contact point is 40-45 degrees . The mass ratio of iron oxide, 20-40% hydrochloric acid solution, gelatin and polyethylene glycol is 1:190:4:25. The cigarette paper is prebaked at 70-80 degrees C for 15-20 minutes, and then sent to an oven.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); F09 (Paper-making production of cellulose, chemical treatment of wood - including chipboard and fibre-board (D21).); P72 (Working paper (B31).); S06 (Electrophotography and Photography)
MC A03-C01; A05-H03; A12-H11; A12-W; F05-A05B; F05-A06; F05-A06A1; F05-A06B; F05-A06C; F05-A06D; P72-A07; P72-B07; S06-C03A; S06-C04; S06-K02; S06-K07

PN CN108686329-A; CN108686329-B
TI Building firefighting equipment electronic            detection and protection device, has mounting box whose            inner wall is provided with sealing mechanism, and            protective mechanism connected with sealing            mechanism.
AU NIU D
AE GUANGDONG PEARL RIVER GAS GROUP CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGDONG ZHUJIANG GAS GROUP CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018851371
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a mounting box (1) provided                with an electronic detector main body (2). A top                part of the electronic detector main body is                provided with an air bag (3) that is filled with                carbon dioxide. Left and right sides of the                mounting box are formed with a wire through hole                (4). A wire (5) passes through the wire through                hole (4) and connected with the electronic detector                main body. An inner wall of the mounting box is                provided with a sealing mechanism (6). A bottom                part of the mounting box is provided with a                protective mechanism (7) that is connected with the                sealing mechanism. The sealing mechanism comprises                a slide rail, a slide block, a baffle block, a                primary blade, a fixed block, a spring, a fixed                pulley, a pull wire and a hanging ring. The                mounting box is made of fireproof material.
   USE - Building firefighting equipment electronic                detection and protection device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device can realize quick protecting                function of an electronic detector body after                detecting fire in the electronic detector body so                as to avoid damage of the electronic detector body                in the fire.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a partial sectional view of                a building firefighting equipment electronic                detection and protection device.
   Mounting box (1)
   Electronic detector main body (2)
   Air bag (3)
   Wire through hole (4)
   Wire (5)
   Sealing mechanism (6)
   Protective mechanism (7)

PT P
PN CN108522688-A
TI Processing black tea involves selecting fresh fresh tea twig with one bud and two leaves as raw material, subjecting raw material to withering to obtain withered leaves an adding withered leaves into sealed container and steaming.
AU REN B
   REN Y
   LIU S
   MOU J
   LUO X
   PENG M
AE GUIZHOU FENGGANG QIANYUZHI ECOLOGICAL (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 201875393C
AB    NOVELTY - Processing black tea involves selecting fresh fresh tea twig with one bud and two leaves as raw material. The raw material is subjected to withering to obtain withered leaves. The withered leaves are added into a sealed container and heated with high-pressure steam for 2-3 minutes, at a temperature of 55-65 degrees C, so that the gas pressure in the sealed container reaches 1.5-2 megapascal (MPa), and then the gas pressure in the container is dropped to 4 MPa within 1-5 seconds to obtain steamed withered leaves. The steamed withered leaves are dried for 50-60 minutes then put into roller for rolling treatment for 25-30 minutes. The fresh tea leaves after rolling are put into the fermentation room for fermentation, where during fermentation the tea leaves turn dark brown and fermented tea leaves are obtained. The fermented tea leaves are extracted by supercritical carbon dioxide, at a temperature of 50-80 degrees C, pressure is 16-30 MPa for 2-3 hours to obtain tea polyphenols.
   USE - Method for processing black tea.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to process black tea, which has high quality, long lasting fragrance, provides sweet taste during drinking, is pure and fresh and does not cause gastrointestinal problems.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The extracted tea leaves are further dried and crushed to obtain desired black tea.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The fresh fresh tea twigs are subjected to withering using hot air, at a temperature of 25-27 degrees C for 8-10 hours, where the thickness of the tea leaves spreading is 15-18 cm. The room temperature of the fermentation chamber is controlled to 24-26 degrees C, and relative humidity to 70-75%. The drying is performed at a temperature of 120-140 degrees C for 40-45 minutes.
   ACTIVITY - Gastrointestinal-Gen.

TI Low-temperature denitration catalyst comprises            microporous activated carbon material as carrier            component and zirconium oxide, cesium oxide, lanthanum            oxide, and yutterium oxides active components, and            which has better effect.
AU LI X
   YIN H
AE CHENGDU DAQI ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
   CHENGDU DAQI ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY CO (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2018714892
AB    NOVELTY - Low-temperature denitration catalyst comprises                microporous activated carbon material as carrier                component and zirconium oxide, cesium oxide,                lanthanum oxide, and yutterium oxide as active                components.
   USE - Low-temperature denitration catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The low-temperature denitration catalyst which                has better effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing low-temperature denitration catalyst,                which involves:
   (A) preparing activated carbon by heating the                activated carbon in a furnace to 350-500 degrees C,                heating up N2 protection is adopted, and raising                temperature to 350-500 degrees C, keeping at a                constant temperature for a period of time in the                constant temperature process, and introducing mixed                gas of oxygen and carbon dioxide instead of                nitrogen;
   (B) using Ce(NO3)2.6H2O, ZrOCO3,                La(NO3)3.6H2O, Y(NO3)3.6H2O as raw materials to                prepare cerium-zirconium-cerium-strontium mixed                salt solution, adding hydrogen peroxide to the                zirconium-niobium-niobium mixed salt solution,                mixing the above solution, and then stirring, to                prepare a good catalytic component solution;
   (C) immersing the catalytic component solution                obtained into the activated carbon obtained,                stirring the mixed solution and the activated                carbon, and then standing;
   (D) drying the impregnated material obtained                to obtain a dry SCR low temperature denitration                catalyst precursor; and
   (E) calcining dried SCR low-temperature                denitration catalyst precursor obtained under the                condition of isolating oxygen to obtain final                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The low-temperature                  denitration catalyst comprises microporous                  activated carbon material as carrier component                  and 1-10% zirconium oxide, 1-10% cesium oxide,                  0.1-1% lanthanum oxide, and 0.1-1% yutterium                  oxideas active components. The proportion of                  micropores having a pore diameter of 2 nm in the                  micropores on the surface of the activated carbon                  is 80%. Preferred Conditions: The weight                  percentage of Ce(NO3)2.6H2O, ZrOCO3,                  La(NO3)3.6H2O, Y(NO3)3.6H2O is 65%:33%:0.9%:1.1%.                  The mixed solution accounts for 40-65 wt.%                  activated carbon. The PVA solution is further                  added to the cerium-zirconium-cerium-strontium                  mixed salt solution. The precursor of the dried                  SCR low-temperature denitration catalyst is                  calcined at 350-600 degrees C for 1-3 hours under                  the condition of isolating oxygen.

   CUI Y
   XIE W
   WEI Z
   ZHAO Y
   ZHONG Z
   WEI C
AE CHINESE ACAD INSPECTION & QUARANTINE (CAIQ-C)
GA 201851413T
AB    NOVELTY - Method for screening human constitutive androstane receptor indirect activator involves (a) diluting type I rat tail collagen to 100 mu g/ml using 130 mu g/ml N-Cyclohexyl-N'-(2-morpholinoethyl)carbodiimide metho-p-toluenesulfonate aqueous solution, (b) adding 20 mu g/ml nanographene as a sensitizer to the rat tail collagen solution prepared in step (a), and then adding solution to 96-well cell culture plate, and incubating plate at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide concentration for 3-4 hours and coating of cell culture plate and rinsing plate with sterile water and phosphate buffer and (c) inoculating human squamous cell carcinoma cell line A431 on the coated 96-well cell culture plate, replacing medium containing the test chemical after 12 hours, and the performing existing cell proliferation test and the positive contrast test.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method                for preparing modified milk powder, which involves                mixing milk powder, milk protein raw materials                and/or carbohydrate raw materials with prebiotics,                adding water, uniformly mixing and pumping into a                temporary storage tank, homogenizing materials at                55-65&#176; C and 15-22 MPa, sterilizing                homogenized solution at 85-92&#176; C for 20-25                seconds, concentrating sterilized product at -8 to                -21 KPa and 17-21&#176; C to obtain                55-65&#176; Baume, spray drying concentrate at                15-25 MPa, where air inlet temperature is                170-210&#176; C and exhaust temperature is                80-90&#176; C, cooling dried material to room                temperature through fluidized bed until water                content reaches 2-3.5 wt.%, adding probiotics and                lactase, and uniformly mixing mixture using spiral                mixer, packing product in a air-conditioned bag                under vacuum by filling nitrogen or carbon                dioxide.

The                  probiotics are chosen from Lactobacillus                  acidophilus, Bifidobacterium animalis, B.lactis,                  Lactobacillus rhamnosus, and L.reuteri. The                  prebiotics are chosen from fructooligosaccharide,                  galactooligosaccharide, xylooligosaccharide,                  isomaltooligosaccharide, polydextrose, inulin,                  human milk oligosaccharide, and stachyose. The                  milk powder is whole milk powder, skim milk                  powder, lactose-free milk powder or fermented                  milk powder. The milk protein raw material is                  chosen from concentrated whey protein powder,                  milk protein peptide, isolated whey protein                  powder, and &#945; -lactalbumin. The                  carbohydrate raw material is chosen from                  maltodextrin, starch, corn syrup solid, and                  glucose. The content of lactase is 2000-10000                  acid lactase unit, content of probiotics is 1x                  108-1010colony forming unit and content of                  prebiotics is 0.1-1.5 g.
   ACTIVITY - Gastrointestinal-Gen.

PN WO2018042482-A1; JP2018536370-X; JP6569815-B2
TI Heat pump system for heating liquid thermal media e.g. water, has coolant piping which is spirally wound along spiral groove, and compressor which is arranged in space covered with gas cooler.
AU TONEGAWA T
AE MITSUBISHI ELECTRIC CORP (MITQ-C)
   MITSUBISHI ELECTRIC CORP (MITQ-C)
GA 201819456L
AB    NOVELTY - The system has gas cooler (2) which exchanges heat between coolant compressed by compressor (1) and liquid thermal medium. A decompression device (3) decompresses the refrigerant. An evaporator (4) exchanges heat with outside air. The thermal-medium piping having spiral groove is formed in the outer peripheral surface. A coolant piping is spirally wound along the spiral groove. The compressor is arranged in space covered with the gas cooler. Carbon dioxide is used as coolant.
   USE - Heat pump system for heating liquid thermal media such as water, calcium chloride aqueous solution, ethylene glycol aqueous solution and alcohol.
   ADVANTAGE - Coolant piping is helically wound along the spiral groove by which the gas cooler is formed in the outer peripheral surface of thermal-medium piping. Comparatively high-temperature coolant piping faces the compressor in the position which adjoined to the compressor rather than relatively low temperature thermal-medium piping. The surrounding of compressor is maintained at high temperature, so that heat loss by thermal radiation from compressor is suppressed and the energy efficiency of system is improved. The convection heating area of coolant piping and the thermal-medium piping is changed by changing branch number of refrigerant piping, so that flow-path design is optimized easily.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the heat pump system.
   Compressor (1)
   Gas cooler (2)
   Decompression device (3)
   Evaporator (4)
   Thermal storage tank (20)

GA 201813624Q
AB    NOVELTY - A composition comprises a supported reactant                including an adsorbent with high affinity for                energetic compounds; a first bioremediation                material comprising organism(s) capable of                degrading an energetic compound; a polymeric                substance fueling the first bioremediation material                during the degrading of the energetic compound; and                a second bioremediation material breaking the                polymeric substance into smaller molecules over a                degradation time, where adsorbent includes a                magnesium-aluminum alloy or a ferrotitanium                alloy.

The elemental                iron impregnated within the porous structure of the                activated carbon is not consumed and performs in a                true catalytic fashion accelerating the rate of                contaminant degradation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A composition comprises a supported reactant                including an adsorbent with high affinity for                energetic compounds; a first bioremediation                material comprising organism(s) capable of                degrading an energetic compound; a polymeric                substance fueling the first bioremediation material                during the degrading of the energetic compound; and                a second bioremediation material breaking the                polymeric substance into smaller molecules over a                degradation time to provide the fueling of the                first bioremediation material in a time-release                manner, where adsorbent includes magnesium-aluminum                alloy, ferrotitanium alloy, copper-plated iron or                iron activated with platinum or palladium.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The first                  bioremediation material includes bacteria, fungi,                  aerobic microorganism, anaerobic microorganism,                  algae, protozoa, or actinomycetes.

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Composition: The adsorbent is                  impregnated with zero valent iron. It includes                  zinc, tin, platinum, palladium, copper,                  manganese, iridium, cobalt, titanium, and/or                  nickel. The adsorbent includes copper-plated iron                  or iron activated with platinum or                  palladium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The degradation time                  is &#8805; 20 days in duration. The polymeric                  substance comprises polyamide, polysaccharide,                  complex carbohydrate, polymeric fatty acid,                  polymer of an amino acid, or protein. It                  comprises starch and/or chitin. The starch is a                  food grade starch.

PN CN114956834-A
TI Reinforced graphene composite aerogel useful for            thermal protection requirements of aircraft in            high-temperature environment, comprises two-dimensional            nanographene sheet, and two-dimensional nano-graphene            sheet loaded with nano-scale silicon            carbon-nitride-aluminum oxide ceramic particles.
AU CHEN J
   CHU X
   SUI Y
   LIU W
   YUAN W
   LIU H
AE UNIV TIANJIN CHENGJIAN (UTCJ-C)
GA 2022B55015
AB    NOVELTY - Reinforced graphene composite aerogel                comprises two-dimensional nanographene sheets,                where the two-dimensional nano-graphene sheet is                loaded with nano-scale silicon                carbon-nitride-aluminum oxide ceramic particles,                the silicon carbon-nitride-aluminum oxide ceramic                particles is connected to each other, and form a                nano-interpenetrating structure with                two-dimensional nano-graphene sheet, the silicon                carbon-nitride-aluminum oxide ceramic particles and                two-dimensional nano-graphene sheet form a porous                skeleton network, and the porous framework network                has nano-scale uniform pores.
   USE - The reinforced graphene composite aerogel is                useful for thermal protection requirements of                aircraft in high-temperature environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The aerogel has porous skeleton network,                nano-scale uniform pores, excellent mechanical                properties and good high-temperature thermal                insulation properties, which can meet thermal                protection requirements of aircraft in                high-temperature environments.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparing                reinforced graphene composite aerogel, comprising                choosing graphene oxide and polysilazane ceramic                precursors as raw materials, using                aluminum-tri-sec-butoxide as an aluminum source,                and divinylbenzene as a cross-linking agent, adding                an organic solvent to obtain wet gel by                hydrothermal reaction, drying wet gel by                supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain ceramic                precursor/reduced graphene oxide aerogel, high                temperature cracking, and converting ceramic                precursor aerogel into ceramic aerogel to obtain                silicon carbon-nitride-aluminum oxide/rGO composite                aerogel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reinforced                  graphene composite aerogel has specific surface                  area is higher than graphene aerogels, thermal                  conductivity is lower than graphene aerogels, and                  toughness is higher than silicon                  carbon-nitride-aluminum oxide ceramic particles                  composite gas condensation.Preferred Method: In the method, preparation                  of reinforced graphene composite aerogel                  specifically comprises (i) ultrasonically mixing                  and dissolving 2-6 pts. wt. polysilazane ceramic                  precursor, 1-4 pts. wt. aluminum sec-butoxide,                  1-3 pts. wt. divinylbenzene, 10-20 pts. wt.                  organic solvent to fully dissolve, adding 1-4                  pts. wt. graphene oxide powder to mixed solution,                  and carrying out ultrasonication again AB    NOVELTY - The method involves generating the                electromagnetic excitation radiation with a carrier                frequency, where the carrier frequency is selected                such that a target gas and an absorption gas absorb                the radiation. An amplitude or carrier frequency of                the radiation is modulated with a modulation                frequency. An absorption section (5) is illuminated                superficially from a skin of a living being with                the radiation, and a sound wave is generated by                absorbing the radiation in the absorption gas. The                amplitude or phase of the wave is detected as a                measure of a concentration of the target gas on an                absorption path. The target gas is carbon dioxide                (CO2).

AE BEIJING JINYU TONGDA REFRACTORY TECHNOLO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202200163B
AB    NOVELTY - Titanium-based aluminate cement comprises titanium iron slag and carbide slag in a weight ratio of 60-70:30-40.
   USE - Titanium-based aluminate cement is used as raw material for the production of electronics, aerospace, military products.
   ADVANTAGE - The titanium-based aluminate cement has better mechanical property and higher temperature-resistant property. The method enables to provide large-scale high-value utilization of titanium iron slag and carbide slag, relieves the problem of shortage of bauxite resources, has important significance for saving bauxite and limestone resources and realizing high-value utilization of metallurgical solid waste titanium iron slag and carbide slag, and calcium oxide in the titanium iron slag and carbide slag is a calcium source of non-calcium carbonate and cannot discharge carbon dioxide in sintering process, which reduces carbon emission in the production process, and carbon is pushed to reach the peak by assistance. The aluminate cement prepared has better mechanical property and higher temperature resistance than common aluminate cement, has better temperature resistance than common aluminate cement, positive social benefit and obvious economic benefit, and better used as a bonding agent for refractory casting material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing titanium-based aluminate cement, which involves batching and grinding the raw materials of the titanium-based aluminate cement based on the proportions to obtain cement raw material, firing cement raw material to obtain clinker, and quenching and secondary grinding the clinker.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Composition: The index requirements of the titanium-iron slag comprises 60 wt.% aluminum oxide, 16 wt.% titanium dioxide, 20 wt.% calcium oxide, 2 wt.% magnesium oxide, 1 wt.% silicon dioxide, 1 wt.% ferric oxide, 0.5 wt.% sum of potassium oxide and sodium oxide, and loss on ignition is 1%. The index requirements of carbide slag comprises 5 wt.% aluminum oxide, 1 wt.% titanium dioxide, 60 wt.% calcium oxide, 2.5 wt.% magnesium oxide, 8 wt.% silicon dioxide, 1.5 wt.% ferric oxide, 1 wt.% sum of potassium oxide and sodium oxide, and loss on ignition is 20-30%. The titanium-based aluminate cement comprises 60-70 pts. wt. titanium iron slag and 30-40 pts. wt. carbide slag. The mineral phase composition of titanium-based aluminate cement comprises 35-60 wt.% calcium monoaluminate, 10-35 wt.% calcium dialuminate, 10-15 wt.% calcium titanate, and sum of aluminum square column stone, dicalcium ferrite, magnesium aluminum spinel and dodecacalcium heptaaluminate is 10-25 wt.%.Preferred Conditions: The clinker fineness of the secondary grinding is 325 meshes and sieve residue is less than 20%.Preferred Process: The titanium iron slag and calcium carbide slag are respectively ground and mixed or mixed together during grinding process, where titanium-iron slag and the calcium carbide slag are ground to a fineness of less than or equal to 200 meshes. The clinker is quenched by air cooling.

PT P
PN CN113694883-A; CN113694883-B
TI Preparing iron-loaded biochar with poplar wood            useful for uranium-containing wastewater, comprises            e.g. dissolving ferrous sulfate and ferric trichloride            hexahydrate in deionized water, immersing poplar wood            biomass in balanced modification solution, heating and            cooling iron-loaded biochar.
AU XING H
   LIU X
   LI C
   NIU J
   WANG Y
AE BEIJING CHEM ENG & METALLURGY RES INST (CNNU-C)
GA 2021E2243V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing iron-loaded biochar with poplar wood                as a carrier comprises (i) weighing the same molar                mass of ferrous sulfate and ferric trichloride                hexahydrate and dissolving in deionized water to                prepare a balanced modification solution with                equivalent iron (II)/iron (III) concentration, (ii)                taking poplar wood biomass and immersing in the                balanced modification solution for 48 hours, and                stirring regularly to modify the poplar wood                biomass, (iii) modifying poplar biomass, filtering,                and drying the separated and modified poplar                biomass in an oven at 40-60degrees Celsius, and                turning in due course, (iv) placing the dried                modified poplar wood biomass into a crucible,                placing the crucible in the middle part of the                quartz tube of the vacuum tube furnace, under the                protection of carbon dioxide, heating for 120                minutes at 900degrees Celsius to obtain iron-loaded                biochar, and (v) taking out the iron-loaded                biochar, and cooling to room temperature under the                protection of nitrogen.
   USE - The method for preparing iron-loaded biochar                with poplar wood as carrier useful for                uranium-containing wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the rate at which                carbonized biomass reacts with water vapor to                generate carbon monoxide in a high-temperature                environment, avoids the consumption of carbon,                avoids consumption of iron-loaded biochar, reduces                the loss of raw materials and improves the                efficiency of material preparation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  concentrations of the iron (II) and iron                  (III)solutions in the equilibrium modification                  solution are both set to 0.5 mol/l, and the                  material of the container for the equilibrium                  modification solution is set to                  glass/polyethylene. In the step (ii), the mass                  ratio of the iron (II) and iron (III) balance                  modification solution to the poplar biomass is                  set to 1:5, the stirring period is set to                  2hours/time, the single stirring time is set to 2                  minute, and the stirring speed is set to 60                  revolutions/minute. In the step (iii), using a                  200-mesh non-metallic screen for filtering,                  drying for 1.5-2 hours, and the moisture content                  of the modified poplar wood biomass after drying                  is less than 3.5%. In the step (iv), the vacuum                  tube furnace is set to a GSL-1400 vacuum tube                  furnace, the heating rate is set to 8degrees                  Celsius/minute, and the total firing time is set                  to 4 hours. In the step (iii), the oven is set as                  a 101-3AB electric heating blast drying oven. In                  step (ii), the poplar wood biomass is made from                  poplar wood as a raw material, dried, ground and                  crushed, and passing through 100 wood sieves. In                  the step (ii), the soaking process is set as a                  vacuum environment.

PN CN113617202-A
TI Composite organic gas pollutant purifying agent            with infrared thermal effect, for capturing pollutants            in air at low temperature and indoor household surface,            comprises infrared thermal effect/adsorption catalyst,            aqueous film-forming agent, surfactant, moisturizer,            dispersant, and deionized water.
AU MAO S
   FU H
   LIANG J
   CHEN B
   LUO Y
   FU X
AE ZHEJIANG JUHUA NEW MATERIAL RES INST CO (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021D5663J
AB    NOVELTY - A composite organic gas pollutant purifying                agent with infrared thermal effect, comprises 1-5                parts mass infrared thermal effect/adsorption                catalyst, 1-5 parts mass aqueous film-forming                agent, 0.1-2 parts mass surfactant, 0.1-1 part mass                moisturizer, 0.5-5 parts mass dispersant, and 100                parts mass deionized water.
   USE - Composite organic gas pollutant purifying                agent with infrared thermal effect, for capturing                formaldehyde, benzene, toluene, xylene, and other                organic gas pollutant in air and indoor household                surface.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite organic gas pollutant purifying                agent with infrared thermal effect, quickly                captures formaldehyde, benzene, toluene, xylene,                and other organic gas pollutant in air at low                temperature and indoor household surface, and                catalytically decomposes into carbon dioxide and                water, over a long period of time all day and in                all four seasons.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the composite organic gas pollutant                purifying agent, which involves grinding an                antimony-tin oxide-manganese dioxide/diatomaceous                earth catalyst using a spherical grinder to 2500                mesh, adding the catalyst and dispersant at                20-35degrees Celsius to deionized water, stirring                for 3-5 hours at 200-500rprm, adding the                moisturizer, surfactant, and film-forming agent                when the solution becomes a uniform and stable                suspension, and stirring for 4 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The surfactant is                  fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether, sodium                  alkylbenzenesulfonate, and/or sodium alkyl                  sulfosuccinate. The film-forming agent is                  degradable hydroxypropyl chitosan or chitosan                  quaternary ammonium salt. The dispersant is                  xanthan gum, dimethylsiloxane polymer, and/or                  hydroxypropyl methyl cellulose.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The moisturizer is                  glycerol, urea, and/or hydroxyethyl urea.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The catalyst is obtained                  by dissolving tin chloride pentahydrate and                  antimony trichloride in a tin/antimony mass ratio                  of 5:1-10:1, in 10-50 mL absolute ethanol, and                  adding 0.1 g tartaric acid to obtain coating                  liquid (A), weighing 1-5 g urea to adjust pH,                  adding 60-100 mL deionized water, magnetically                  stirring to dissolve, adding 1-5 g manganese                  dioxide and 0.1-1 g polyvinylpyrrolidone,                  stir-mixing, and ultrasonically dispersing for                  50-100 minutes to obtain mixed slurry (B), adding                  liquid (A) to slurry (B) when the reaction                  temperature is 50-100degrees Celsius through a                  peristaltic pump at 0.4 rpm, condensing and                  refluxing at 50-100degrees Celsius for 5 hours,                  reacting to obtain a precursor slurry, leaving                  still, cooling for 3 hours, filtering, washing,                  vacuum-drying at 60degreesC for 5 hours, grinding                  to obtain a precursor powder, calcining in an air                  atmosphere at 500-650degreesC for 2-3 hours to                  obtain antimony-tin oxide-manganese dioxide                  catalyst, dispersing the prepared catalyst and                  nano diatomaceous earth in distilled water,                  stirring, adding citric acid as complexing agent,                  adjusting the pH to 5-7 using aqueous ammonia,                  heating to 70degreesC until the mixture becomes a                  wet gel, baking the wet gel at 105degreesC until                  the wet gel becomes a dry gel, placing the dried                  dry gel in a muffle furnace at 500-650degreesC                  for 1-2 hours, cooling to room temperature, and                  grinding.

PN CN113548965-A; CN113548965-B
TI Preparing 1, 4 alkene alkyne compound comprises            adding allyl alcohol, alkyne acid, combined catalyst            into organic solvent, stirring and reacting, extracting            reaction liquid by water and ethyl acetate to remove            part of reaction solvent, drying and concentrating, and            purifying.
AU QIU J
   XIE P
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 2021C3465H
AB    NOVELTY - Method comprises (i) adding allyl alcohol,                alkyne acid, combined catalyst under the atmosphere                of inert gas into the organic solvent, stirring and                reacting for 12-24 hours at 80-100degrees Celsius                to obtain the reaction liquid, where the combined                catalyst comprising organic palladium, fluorine                salt and cesium fluoride, the fluorine salt is                bis(trifluoromethane)sulfonimide salt or double                triflate, the organic solvent comprises dimethyl                sulfoxide, N, N-dimethyl acetamide, N, N-dimethyl                formamide, glycol dimethyl ether, the molar volume                ratio of allyl alcohol, alkyne acid, organic                palladium, fluorine salt, cesium fluoride and                organic solvent is (0.3-10) mmol: (0.45-15) mmol:                (0.015-1) mmol: (0.03-1.2) mmol: (0.03-1.2) mmol:                (2-15) ml, (ii) extracting the reaction liquid by                water and ethyl acetate to remove part of reaction                solvent, drying and concentrating the organic                phase, then using petroleum ether/ethyl acetate                developing agent system to purify by thin layer                chromatography.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 1, 4 alkene                alkyne compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, convenient; produces                by-product includes water and carbon dioxide; has                high atom economy; is environmentally-friendly;                uses economically available raw material; and has                wide range of applicable substrates.

The                  inert gas is nitrogen or argon gas.

The total amount of carbon dioxide is                measured.

The carbon flow                numerical value acquisition is not accurate enough                and the carbon discharge calculation is not                convenient and fast in the existing method.

TI Method for calculating flow of gas emitted by            source into atmosphere, involves integrating second            quantity calculated from first integrated global            quantities obtained from interpolated curve and/or from            wind speed present on line.
AU DONNAT L

   LUDOVIC D
   OLIVIER D
AE TOTAL SE (PETF-C)
   UNIV REIMS CHAMPAGNE-ARDENNE (UYRE-Non-standard)

   TOTAL SA (PETF-C)
   CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
   UNIV REIMS CHAMPAGNE-ARDENNE (UYRE-Non-standard)
GA 2021B8163T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves recovering data on                contents of a gas measured in an atmosphere away                from a source along a set of lines parallel to a                direction. A curve of integrated global quantities                is interpolated as a function of a coordinate along                another direction perpendicular to the former                direction from the global quantities. Another                quantity calculated from the former global                quantities obtained from the interpolated curve and                wind speed present on the line following the latter                direction is integrated to obtain a gross gas flow.                The gas flow emitted by the source is determined                from a raw gas flow by a calculation system.
   USE - Method for calculating a flow of a gas e.g.                methane and carbon dioxide, emitted by a source                into the atmosphere, in a petroleum or chemical                industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively calculates the flow of                the gas emitted by the source into the atmosphere                without requiring data taken as close as possible                to the source, while being precise and easy to                use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a method for calculating a flow of a                gas;
   (2) system for calculating a flow of at least                one gas emitted by a source into the atmosphere;                and
   (3) kit for measuring emissions of at least                one gas by a source into the atmosphere comprising                a drone.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                measurement kit.

PT P
PN CN113351212-A; CN113351212-B
TI Preparing nickel-doped hydrotalcite with rich oxygen vacancies used as e.g. catalyst for removing organic wastewater comprises e.g. taking copper salt, magnesium salt and iron salt, stirring, aging, filtering, washing, and evaporating.
AU XU Y
   ZHU J
   TANG Z
   GE F
AE UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
GA 2021A7764P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nickel-doped hydrotalcite with rich oxygen vacancies comprises (i) taking copper salt, magnesium salt and iron salt, adding to pure water to obtain a copper-magnesium-iron salt complex solution, preparing hydroxide solution, slowly dropping the prepared copper-magnesium-iron salt complex solution and hydroxide solution to a container filled with pure water, stirring, isolating the carbon dioxide in the air, dropping the solution, aging under water bath conditions, filtering, washing the filter residue for multiple times with pure water, drying the filter residue washed filter residue, grinding the dried solid to obtain a sample of copper-magnesium-iron hydrotalcite, and (ii) immersing the copper-magnesium-iron hydrotalcite in nickel salt solution, and evaporating to dryness causes lattice distortion to obtain nickel-based copper-magnesium-iron hydrotalcite.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nickel-doped hydrotalcite with rich oxygen vacancies, which is used as a catalyst for removing of organic wastewater, preferably the wet catalytic air oxidation and degradation of novel pollutants including benzotriazole ultraviolet absorbers and stabilizers (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, economical and beneficial to industrialized production. The hydrotalcite: is economical; and has high organic matter removal rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a nickel-doped hydrotalcite with rich oxygen vacancies prepared by above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the hydroxide solution comprises 1-3 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution. The molar ratio of copper salt, magnesium salt, and iron salt is 0-2:0-2:1. The copper salt comprises copper nitrate, copper sulfate, and/or copper chloride. The magnesium salt comprises magnesium nitrate, magnesium sulfate, and/or magnesium chloride. The iron salt comprises iron nitrate, iron sulfate, and/or iron chloride. The nickel salt comprises nickel nitrate, nickel sulfate, and/or nickel chloride. In the step (ii), the concentration of the nickel salt solution is 1-3 mol/l. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the prepared copper-magnesium-iron salt complex solution and hydroxide solution are slowly dropped to the container containing pure water, and at the same time stirring the mixed solution in the container, where the temperature of the mixed solution in the control container is 60-70 degrees C, and controlling the pH value of the mixed solution in the container to 5-12. The carbon dioxide in the air is isolated by blowing nitrogen into a container filled with pure water. The aging is carried at 68-72 degrees C under water bath conditions for 12-24 hours. The drying in the oven is carried at 78-82 degrees C for 8-16 hours. In the step (ii), the immersion time of copper-magnesium-iron hydrotalcite in nickel salt solution is carried out for 18-24 hours. The evaporation is carried out in a rotary evaporator at 40-100 degrees C under 0-0.1 MPa. The process of degradation of benzotriazole ultraviolet absorbers is carried out at room temperature and normal pressure conditions.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing photocatalyst comprises (a) preparing flower-like indium(III) oxide by adding indium source, urea and sodium lauryl sulfate into deionized water to fully dissolve, uniformly mixing, subjecting mixed solution to hydrothermal reaction, cooling product to room temperature, washing by centrifuging, drying and calcining to prepare flower-like indium(III) oxide, (b) preparing flower-like indium oxide/zinc-cadmium-selenium complex (In2O3/ZnxCd1-xSe) by dispersing flower-like indium(III) oxide, adding to organic amine-hydrazine hydrate binary mixed solvent to fully dissolve and mixing, adding cadmium source, zinc source and selenium source, mixing, placing resulting mixture in a microwave reactor for reaction, cooling product to room temperature, centrifuging, washing and drying to obtain the flower-like indium oxide/zinc-cadmium-selenium complex (In2O3/ZnxCd1-xSe).
   USE - The photocatalyst is useful in photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to prepare carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has low energy consumption, mild reaction conditions, and good repeatability; is simple and efficient; and utilizes easily available raw materials. The photocatalyst has high stability, large specific surface area, high catalytic efficiency and broad application prospects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for photocatalyst is prepared by the above mentioned method, where mass ratio of flower-like indium(III) oxide to cadmium source, zinc source and selenium source is 1:1-20:2-30:1-30.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The indium source is at least one of indium trichloride, indium nitrate, indium acetate or indium sulfate. The molar ratio of the indium source, urea and sodium lauryl sulfate is 1:5-10:2-5. The organic amine is at least one of diethylenetriamine, ethylenediamine, triethylenetetramine and tetraethylenepentamine. The cadmium source is at least one of cadmium chloride, indium nitrate, cadmium acetate and cadmium sulfate. The zinc source is at least one of zinc chloride, zinc acetate, zinc oxide, zinc citrate, zinc methacrylate, zinc nitrate and zinc sulfate. The selenium source is at least one of selenocysteine, sodium selenite and selenium powder. The volume ratio of the organic amine and hydrazine hydrate is 1:0.2-0.6. Preferred Method: The hydrothermal reaction is performed at 100-140 degrees C for 10-14 hours. The calcination is performed at 300-500 degrees C for 1-4 hours. The microwave reaction is carried out at 120-160 degrees C for 15-30 minutes.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

Preferred Method: In the step (i), the crushing is performed using 100-150 mesh seive. In the step (i), the quality of the ethanol is 5-10 times the quality of the slurry. In the step (i), the ultrasonic power is 200-400 W, the ultrasonic temperature is 30-40 degrees C and the time is 0.5-1 hours. In the step (i), the extraction temperature is 55-60 degrees C, the extraction pressure is 15-20 MPa, and the extraction time is 15-20 minutes. In the step (ii), the stirring speed is 500-1000 revolutions/minute, and time is 2-3 hours. In the step (iii), the homogeneous pressure is 25-40 MPa, and the time is 1.5-2 hours.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological; Antibacterial.

TI Automatic window opening and closing device has devices designed for automatic opening and closing of window sashes for natural ventilation of rooms.
AU RAZUMIKHIN S I
AE RAZUMIKHIN S I (RAZU-Individual)
GA 202187368W
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to devices designed for automatic opening and closing of window sashes for natural ventilation of rooms with a controlled climate in the absence of air conditioning systems. It can be used in residential, office, industrial premises, greenhouses, vehicles for automatic ventilation of premises and maintenance of established climatic parameters (temperature, humidity, carbon dioxide content). The device makes it possible to optimize the mode of natural ventilation of premises in the absence of air conditioning systems. The device contains an electric drive based on a gear motor, an electronic control unit that includes digital sensors for monitoring climate parameters and a sensor that regulates the position of the sash, and the corresponding software, a flexible drive that transmits force from the gear motor to the sash. According to the invention, a profile flexible guide with variable stiffness, made of a polymer, metal or composite material, is used as a flexible drive, while one end of the guide is fixed to a fixed part of the window, and the gear motor fixed to the movable window sash, moves along the flexible guide on the principle of rack and pinion or flexible transmission, opening or closing the window in accordance with the program which is regulated by the specified initial and variable current climatic parameters received from the sensors to the control unit.
   USE - Automatic opening equipment.
   ADVANTAGE - Possibility to optimize the mode of natural ventilation of premises in the absence of air conditioning systems.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing reinforcing agent for interlayer                spraying of natural macromolecule modified paper,                which involves:

   (B) adding starch to material A, stirring and                dispersing uniformly, adding crosslinking agent                after heating to 60-80 degrees C, and crosslinking                for 30-60 minutes to obtain mixture of material B';                and
   (C) adding dispersant to material B' and                stirring for 30-60 minutes to prepare reinforcing                agent for spraying natural macromolecule modified                paper interlayer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polysaccharide                  mixture is natural polymer mixture structure                  comprising D-xylose, L-arabinose, D-glucose,                  D-galactose, D-mannose, D-glucuronic acid and                  4-O-methyl-D-glucuronic acid. The                  acid comprises organic acid and inorganic acid,                  where organic acid is selected from formic acid,                  terylene and acetic acid, and inorganic acids is                  selected from sulfuric acid and hydrochloric                  acid. The crosslinking agent is a metal salt of                  multi-hydroxyl and ammonium. The starch is                  selected from raw starch, oxidized starch, and                  pregelatinized starch. The raw starch is selected                  from corn starch, cassava starch, and potato                  starch. The dispersant is selected from sorbitol,                  potassium sorbate, urea, and citric acid.                  Preferred Conditions: The molecular total amount                  of polysaccharide mixture is 2-5 million. The                  reaction temperature is normal temperature of the                  reaction in the field, enzymatic hydrolysis is                  50-60 degrees C, and acidolysis is 80-95 degrees                  C, and reaction time is normal temperature of                  reaction in the field, where time is preferably                  30-60 minutes for enzymatic hydrolysis and                  120-180 minutes for acid hydrolysis. The                  concentration of the enzyme preparation is the                  original concentration, and the enzyme activity                  is 2-50,000 per unit, and the acid concentration                  is 10-30%.

PN CN112495414-A
TI Carbon nitride supported catalyst for preparing low-carbon mixed alcohols from synthesis gas comprises carbon nitride, copper, iron, manganese, zinc, zirconium, cobalt.
AU FANG K
   LI W
   ZHAO L
   MU X
   ZHANG M
   ZHANG L
AE INST COAL CHEM CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAXI-C)
GA 2021285029
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon nitride supported catalyst for preparing low-carbon mixed alcohols from synthesis gas comprises 30-50 wt.% carbon nitride, 20-35 wt.% copper, 10-25 wt.% iron, 5-15 wt.% manganese, 5-15 wt.% zinc, 5-15 wt.% zirconium, 1-5 wt.% cobalt, and 0.1-5 wt.% M. The M is alkali metals, alkaline earth metals and/or transition metal elements. The alkali metal is sodium, potassium, lithium or cesium. The alkaline earth metal is magnesium, calcium or barium. The transition metal element is aluminum, silicon, molybdenum, chromium, lanthanum or cerium.
   USE - Used as carbon nitride supported catalyst for preparing low-carbon mixed alcohols from synthesis gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has low cost, good stability, long life and high selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing carbon nitride supported catalyst for preparing low-carbon mixed alcohol from synthesis gas, comprising using loading iron, copper, cobalt, manganese, zinc, zirconium on carbon nitride, then introducing M metal with strong carbon chain growth ability, improving the activity of CO hydrogenation reaction, improving the selectivity of C2+ alcohol and C4+ hydrocarbon, and simultaneously suppress the formation of carbon dioxide and methane by-products; and
   (2) use of carbon nitride supported catalyst, comprising reacting synthesis gas to low-carbon mixed alcohol, where the reaction temperature is 220-280 degrees C, the pressure is 4.5-12 MPa, space velocity is 1000-1000 h-1, and hydrogen/carbon monoxide is 0.5-2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The particle size of the carbon nitride supported catalyst for preparing low-carbon mixed alcohol from synthesis gas is 40-60 meshes. The carbon nitride includes alpha -carbon nitride, beta -carbon nitride, cubic carbon nitride, pseudo cubic carbon nitride, graphite phase g-carbon nitride, or alpha -carbon nitride, beta -carbon nitride, cubic carbon nitride, mixture of pseudo cubic carbon nitride and graphite phase g-carbon nitride. The soluble alkali metal carbonate solution is sodium carbonate or potassium carbonate. Preferred Method: The method comprises adding carbon nitride to distilled water at 20-90 degrees C, preparing 0.1-10 wt.% carbon nitride suspension solution at stirring rate of 15-30 revolutions/minute, dissolving the metal nitrate in the carbon nitride suspension solution and uniformly mixing to obtain mixed solution with a nitrate concentration of 30-60 wt.%, adding 30 wt.% aqueous ammonia solution or soluble alkali metal carbonate solution to the mixed solution for co-current precipitating, and the stirring rate during precipitation is controlled at 30-45 rpm. The roasted synthesis gas produced low-carbon mixed alcohol carbon nitride supported catalyst is crushed to 40-60 meshes, precipitation temperature is 40-80 degrees C, maintaining the pH to 6-10 until the precipitation is complete, aging for 3-5 hours, and then washing the precipitate with distilled water until it is neutral, drying precipitate at 80-120 degrees C and calcining in 350-500 degrees C air atmosphere for 6 hours, crushing to 40-60 meshes to obtain carbon nitride supported catalyst for synthesis of low-carbon mixed alcohol from synthesis gas.

   ADVANTAGE - The method provides the biofuel which is                transportable and easily stored makes it possible                to develop and consolidate an industrial stationary                energy sector (used at a fixed point, the hearth                and unlike biofuels) and reduces environmental                impacts (fossil carbon dioxide emissions, with                biomass without fertilizers or                phytosanitary).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                installation for the production of a biofuel by                steam cracking of biomass comprising a continuous                or discontinuous steam cracking reactor comprising                at least one means of taking a sample of steam                cracked products in or at the outlet of the                chamber, steam cracking and a physico-chemical                analysis on the sample to characterize the steam                cracked biomass, and at least one means of                adjusting the parameters.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The measuring step                  consists of taking a sample of the biomass                  entering the steam cracking chamber and applying                  a physicochemical analysis to the sample for                  characterize the biomass collected. The measuring                  step consists in taking a sample of the gaseous                  or liquid effluents in or at the outlet of the                  steam cracking chamber and in applying a                  physico-chemical analysis on the sample for                  characterization. The measuring step consists in                  taking a sample of steam cracked products in or                  at the outlet of the steam cracking chamber and                  in applying a physicochemical analysis to the                  sample to characterize steam cracked biomass. The                  measuring step consists in taking a sample of                  granules and applying a physicochemical analysis                  on the sample to characterize the pellets made                  with steam cracked biomass. The method one                  proceeds to the periodic time-stamped recording                  of at least part of the results of the                  measurements, thus as results of the measurement                  on a sample of granules obtained during the same                  cycle. The results are injected into a block                  chain. The biofuel is injected into a supervised                  learning system to produce the digital model. The                  model is determined by a series of chemical                  simulations. Preferred Components: The adjusted                  parameter comprises at least one of parameters                  comprising severity factor, steam cracking                  pressure, steam cracking temperature, steam                  cracking duration, stopping steam cracking,                  steam/solid ratio (leaching, stripping), filling                  rate of the steam cracking tank, forward speed in                  the steam cracking chamber, compression ratio at                  the inlet, compression ratio at the outlet of the                  reactor discharge with the orifice diameter, feed                  rate, humidity, particle size.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN111995791-A
TI Preparing thermoplastic light foam plastic comprises e.g. preparing flame-retardant liquid, mixing with polystyrene foaming beads, stirring, coating the phenolic resin coating liquid, carrying out press molding and aging.
AU XIA S
AE ANHUI XIANGXIN NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2020C2340Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing thermoplastic light foam plastic comprises (i) mixing silicon dioxide aerogel powder and 90 wt.% phenolic resin, stirring for 3-5 minutes at stirring speed is more than 100 revolutions/minute to obtain flame-retardant liquid, (ii) mixing flame retardant liquid and polystyrene (PS) foaming beads, stirring on electric stirrer for 5 minutes at 500 revolutions/minute, uniformly coating the phenolic resin coating liquid on the surface of the PS foaming beads, adding 20 wt.% of PS foaming beads with curing agent, continuously stirring for 2 minutes and taking out to obtain coated foaming particles and (iii) pouring the coated foaming particles into the mold, placing the mold in the flat vulcanizing machine for mold pressing molding and aging to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing thermoplastic light foam plastic.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: uses supercritical carbon dioxide as a foaming agent to foam styrene plastics and adding nano-silica as nucleating agent, which reduces cell diameter, increases cell density and uniformity and significantly reduces the bulk density of PS foaming beads; involves addition of silicon dioxide aerogel with extremely low bulk density and good heat insulation into the flame retardant liquid, which reduces the density of the flame retardant liquid and the foam plastic remains low in bulk density after being coated with the flame retardant liquid, realizes the light-weight of flame-retardant foam material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method comprises controlling temperature of the upper and lower plates of the vulcanizer at 103-105 degrees C, the molding pressure is 8 MPa and molding time is 4 minutes. The PS foaming beads are prepared by (iv) uniformly mixing styrene with nano-silicon dioxide in high speed mixer, adding monoglyceride/liquid paraffin mixed solution of 3-7 wt.% in the mixing process, fully stirring to obtain the mixture one, (v) adding the mixture into the co-rotating twin-screw-single-screw two-stage extruder for foaming, injecting supercritical carbon dioxide into co-rotating twin-screw extruder during foaming process, where the temperature of each section of the double-screw extruder is: 174 degrees C, 182 degrees C, 187 degrees C, 184 degrees C, 178 degrees C, 178 degrees C, 179 degrees C, 175 degrees C, 175 degrees C, the rotation speed of the screw is 320 revolutions/minute, the gas injection quantity is set 0.8 kg/hour, (vi) extruding to obtain the foaming blank by die, (vii) cutting the foaming blank into particles, placing the blank particles in the constant temperature environment of 160 degrees C continuously for 20 minutes to obtain final product. Preferred Components: The weight ratio of the flame-retardant liquid to the PS foaming beads is 1:3. The mass ratio of silicon dioxide aerogel powder to the phenolic resin is 1:5-10. The addition amount of nano-silicon dioxide is 5 wt.% of styrene, the size of the die is 2x 20 mm, the temperature of the die is 113 degrees C, the critical temperature of the supercritical carbon dioxide is 32 degrees C and the critical pressure is 7.5 Mpa.
DC A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); A13 (Polymers of aromatic mono-olefins; including polystyrene.); A21 (Epoxides; aminoplasts; phenoplasts.); A82 (Coatings, impregnations, polishes - excluding textile finishing.)

PN CN111945104-A
TI Thin-layer nitrocarburizing method comprises quenching, heating parts, cooling to room temperature in oil, tempering, cooling to room temperature in air, processing raw materials prepared for heat treatment, and using cleaning agents.
AU JIAO Q
   WEI T
   WANG X
AE SHENYANG AIRCRAFT IND GROUP CO LTD (CHAV-C)
GA 2020B7193N
AB    NOVELTY - Thin-layer nitrocarburizing method comprises (a) quenching, heating the parts to 1000-1020 degrees C and cooling to room temperature in oil, tempering, heating the parts to 660-690 degrees C, and cooling to room temperature in the air, (b) processing the raw materials prepared for heat treatment, (c) using cleaning agents e.g. alcohol, (d) exhausting gas at 180-200 degrees C, and nitrogen with a volume of 2 times the volume of the furnace is first introduced, ammonia with a volume of 2 times the volume of the furnace is introduced to heat to 390-410 degrees C, passing in 5-10 ml tetrachloroethylene, performing surface activation treatment, where the ammonia decomposition rate is stable within the range of 20-30%, the ammonia gas intake ratio is 80-90%, and the carbon dioxide intake ratio is 10-20%, and heating to 510-530 degrees C for 30-50 minutes, quickly cooling to 150 degrees C by passing nitrogen gas and cool off the furnace with air, and (e) checking the hardness of the infiltration layer and core of the parts.
   USE - The method is useful for thin-layer nitrocarburizing.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: quenches, tempers and gas nitrocarburizes the 1Cr11Ni2W2MoV stainless steel parts; accurately controls the penetration depth of the parts with high precision; and has infiltration layer hardness, brittleness level and core hardness meet the requirements.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises , (f) checking the brittleness of the part infiltration layer, (g) checking the penetration depth of the parts and meet the requirements of 0.01-0.04 mm, and (h) checking the seepage layer structure of the parts, and meet the requirements of HB5022.

TI Device for reducing nitrogen oxide emission in cement manufacturing apparatus, has decomposing furnace located at downstream of smoke chamber, and air pipe connected to collecting part, where gas is discharged from combustion chamber through junction part.
AU FEISS M
   STREETER N
AE KHD HUMBOLDT WEDAG GMBH (KLOH-C)
GA 2020B4961T
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a device for reducing nitrogen oxide discharge and cement manufacturing device, the device comprises: converging part, the converging part is set between the decomposing furnace and the smoke chamber; the decomposing furnace is connected with a cyclone cylinder; a combustion chamber, the combustion chamber is formed into a pipeline shape; one end of the combustion chamber is connected to the smoke chamber; the other end of the combustion chamber is connected to the junction part; the flow direction of the exhaust gas discharged from the smoke chamber; the decomposition furnace is located downstream of the smoke chamber; an air pipe, the air pipe is connected to the junction part, the air can be introduced into the junction part through the air pipe; the gas from the air pipe and the gas discharged from the combustion chamber can be guided to the decomposition furnace through the junction part. By using the technical solution, the nitrogen oxide is introduced into the combustion chamber and carbon monoxide is formed in the combustion chamber; the nitrogen oxide and carbon monoxide are converted into nitrogen and carbon dioxide under the high temperature environment of the combustion chamber; the excess carbon monoxide can be processed by introducing air to ignite, so that the finally discharged gas is harmless.

   HUA M
AE JILIN YONGLI LASER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JILI-Non-standard)
GA 2020A4680L
AB    NOVELTY - The invention claims a laser cavity mirror sticking tool, relating to the manufacturing field of carbon dioxide laser, comprising a connecting cylinder, a column substrate, a first discharge tube and a second discharge tube; the first discharge tube and the second discharge tube are installed in the connecting cylinder; the front surface of the column substrate is provided with a connecting curved surface; the back surface of the laser cavity mirror is fixed at the centre of the connecting curved surface; the working surface of the laser cavity mirror is outward; the connecting cylinder is provided with a curved surface tightly matched with the connecting curved surface; the laser cavity mirror is embedded in the connecting cylinder. PN CN111735604-A
TI Device for measuring atmospheric turbulence anisotropy factor based on spatial coherence degree, has laser transmitting device, optical transmitting antenna and receiving antennas and data processor connected together.
AU ZHAO L
   XU Y
AE UNIV XIHUA (UYXH-C)
GA 2020993220
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a laser transmitting device (1), an optical transmitting antenna (2), an optical receiving antenna (3), a spatial coherence measuring instrument (4) and a data processor (5) connected together. The laser is formed as Helium -Neon laser, neodymium-doped yttrium aluminum garnet laser, Carbon dioxide laser or fiber laser. The optical transmitting antenna is formed as an inverted Galileo telescope or Kepler telescope. The optical receiving antenna is formed as a Cassegrain telescope, or Gregorian telescope, or Newton telescope, or Galileo telescope, or Kepler telescope. The spatial coherence measuring instrument is formed as a system provided with a polarization beam splitting prism and two identical CCD cameras. The CCD camera is formed as a Baslerpilot series camera. The data processor is formed as a computer installed with a compiled calculation program.
   USE - Device for measuring atmospheric turbulence anisotropy factor based on spatial coherence degree.
   ADVANTAGE - The device is simple in structure, and has convenient operation and strong universality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for measuring atmospheric turbulence anisotropy factor based on spatial coherence degree.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a device for measuring atmospheric turbulence anisotropy factor based on spatial coherence degree. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Laser transmitting device (1)
   Optical transmitting antenna (2)
   Optical receiving antenna (3)
   Spatial coherence measuring instrument (4)
   Data processor (5)

PN WO2020194228-A1; CN113613781-A; US2022143584-A1; IN202117041649-A
TI Using and regenerating catalyst, comprises e.g.            flowing feed gas having ethane and oxidant comprising            oxygen to reactor, and reacting in reactor ethane with            oxidant in presence of catalyst to acetic acid and            flowing regenerating gas stream.
AU GOPAL S
   AL-SMARI T
   AZAM S
   KARIM K
   AI S T
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 2020961511
AB    NOVELTY - Using and regenerating catalyst, comprises:                flowing feed gas comprising ethane and an oxidant                comprising oxygen to a reactor, in which a catalyst                comprising an (molybdenum-vanadium-niobium-                palladium) oxide is disposed; reacting, in the                reactor, the ethane with the oxidant in presence of                the catalyst to produce acetic acid, when the                catalyst's ability to catalyze the reaction between                the ethane and the oxidant is reduced by greater                than or equal to 30%, ceasing the flow of the feed                gas and the oxidant to the reactor; and flowing a                regenerating gas stream comprising 2-21 mol.%                oxygen at a temperature of 200-375 degrees C and                gas hourly space velocity of 1000-10000/hour                through the reactor to contact the regenerating gas                stream with the catalyst, thus increasing the                catalyst's ability to catalyze the reaction between                the ethane and the oxidant.
   USE - The method is useful using and regenerating                catalyst to produce acetic acid from ethane                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is capable of reactivating                partially deactivated catalyst to at least 70% of                activity and selectivity of fresh catalyst,                resulting in lower carbon dioxide and water                formation during production of acetic acid compared                to conventional methods; and provides catalyst that                to prevent gradual increase of catalyst operating                temperature, thus reducing production cost, results                improved catalyst life and safety level for                production system, and cost-effective.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises: before flowing the regenerating gas                  stream, flushing feed lines leading to the                  reactor and the reactor with an inert gas;                  recycling unreacted ethane and/or unreacted                  oxidant to the reactor; and purifying the acetic                  acid by removing byproducts including carbon                  dioxide and/or water. The regenerating gas stream                  is flowed through the reactor after at least 3                  months, preferably at least 3-24 hours of                  operation of the reactor. The reactor is                  maintained at a temperature to prevent formation                  of liquid water. The flowing the regenerating gas                  through the reactor is capable of restoring the                  activity of the catalyst 70-100% of a fresh                  catalyst. The step of reacting is carried out at                  150-450 degrees C, at pressure of 1-50 bar, and                  at a gas hourly space velocity of 50-50000/hour.                  Preferred Components: The inert gas comprises                  nitrogen, argon, and/or carbon dioxide. The                  regenerating gas stream further comprises                  nitrogen, carbon dioxide, and/or argon. The                  reactor comprises a fixed bed reactor, and/or                  fluidized bed reactor. The catalyst is supported                  on alumina, silica, titanium, and/or zinc oxide.                  The molar ratio of the oxygen and the ethane                  flowed into the reactor is 1:1000-1:1.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Low energy consumption methanol catalytic heating device, has heat preservation shell whose side is provided with air inlet pipe that is connected with heat exchange cavity, and air outlet pipe arranged on heat preservation shell.
AU QIU G
   LI J
   GUO J
AE FOSHAN GUANGTENG NEW ENERGY CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 2020926882
AB    NOVELTY - A low energy consumption methanol catalytic heating, comprising a heat preservation shell, the heat preservation shell is provided with a heat exchange cavity; the heat exchange cavity is provided with multiple transversely distributed heat exchange pipe; the heat exchange pipe and the heat exchange cavity are relatively sealed; two ends of the multiple heat exchange pipes are connected in parallel through the water inlet pipe and the water outlet pipe; the heat exchange cavity is filled with catalyst, the catalyst is covered on the outer column surface of the heat exchange pipe; and filling the gap between the heat exchange pipes; one side of the heat preservation shell is provided with an air inlet pipe connected with the heat exchange cavity; the other side of the heat preservation shell is provided with an air outlet pipe connected with the heat exchange cavity; the air inlet pipe is provided with a blower; the blower forcibly fills the external air into the heat exchange cavity through the air inlet pipe to contact with the catalyst. The beneficial effects of the utility model are as follows: The methanol catalytic heating process of the invention is a flame-free heating device; the waste gas generated after the catalytic reaction is only carbon dioxide; it is reliable and safe without pollution.

AU HAN Z

AE COMMONWEALTH SCI & IND RES ORG (CSIR-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a vertical branched graphene                comprises: either treating a pristine vertical                graphene with an inert plasma in the absence of an                introduced carbon source to develop a vertical                branched graphene; or (a) pre-treating a substrate                surface with an inert plasma, (b) depositing a                pristine vertical graphene onto the substrate                surface by contacting the substrate surface with a                deposition plasma comprising a carbon source gas                for a deposition period and (c) treating the                vertical graphene with an inert plasma in the                absence of an introduced carbon source to develop a                vertical branched graphene with vertical branched                graphene structure. The vertical branched graphene                is argon plasma. The carbon source gas is                methane.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a vertical                branched graphene and freestanding branched                graphene, which is useful in a catalyst support in                an electrocatalytic process, hydrogen or oxygen                production, in the production of a liquid carbon                product, in the production of at least one of                n-propanol, ethanol or formate formed from the                reduction of carbon dioxide and in an energy                storage device e.g. a battery or a supercapacitor                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides the vertical graphene that                exhibit desirable controllable electronic, optical                and mechanical properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a vertical branched graphene attached to a                substrate surface;
   (2) a freestanding branched graphene with                proximal end and a distal end; and
   (3) a catalyst support.
