TI Preparing human parathyroid single cell            suspension, comprises e.g. taking hyperplastic            parathyroid tissue of human patient, rinsing, cutting            into pieces, adding type-IV collagenase to shredded            parathyroid tissue, and mixing.
AU YE W
   NI K
   YAO M
   LIU Y
   LU H
AE UNIV NANTONG (UYNT-C)
GA 202079534X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing human parathyroid single cell                suspension, comprises (i) taking hyperplastic                parathyroid tissue of a human patient, rinsing, and                cutting into pieces, (ii) adding type-IV                collagenase to the shredded parathyroid tissue,                mixing, and digesting, (iii) terminating the                digestion, filtering the mixed solution obtained                after the digestion, centrifuging, removing the                supernatant, adding Dulbecco's phosphate-buffered                saline (DPBS), and suspending, (iv) filtering the                solution, centrifuging, removing the supernatant,                and adding DPBS to suspend, and (v) adding red                blood cell lysate to removing red blood cells,                allowing to stand, centrifuging, removing the                supernatant, adding DPBS to wash the cells twice,                removing the supernatant, and adding DPBS to                suspend.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing human                parathyroid single cell suspension.
   ADVANTAGE - The method stably and reliably digests cells,                and the resulting cell content and activity are                higher.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                detecting human parathyroid single cell suspension,                comprising (a) taking a human parathyroid single                cell suspension, adding RPMI-1640 medium containing                10% fetal bovine serum, culturing at 37&#176; C                and 5% carbon dioxide according to conventional                cell culture methods, and observing the growth                status of the primary and sub-generation cells                under the microscope every day, and (b) extracting                the RNA of the first generation of cells, and                detecting the RNA of specific markers of                parathyroid cells by using PCR method, including                parathyroid hormone and neuroglial deletion                transcription factor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  parathyroid tissue is washed with serum-free                  Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium (DMEM) medium to                  remove blood. In the step (ii), the type IV                  collagenase is pre-dissolved in RPMI-1640 medium,                  the working concentration is 2 mg/ml, and the                  dosage is 10 ml each time, and digesting in a                  constant temperature culture shaker at                  37&#176; C with a speed of 40                  revolutions/minute (rpm) for 40-45 minutes. In                  the step (iii), RPMI-1640 medium containing 10%                  fetal bovine serum and double antibodies is used                  to terminate the digestion, and the amount used                  is 2-3 times the volume of the mixed solution                  obtained after digestion in the step (ii), and                  the centrifugation is carried out at 400x g for 4                  minutes. In the step (iv), the pore size of the                  cell strainer used to filter the solution                  obtained in step (3) is 40 &#956; m, the                  centrifugal speed is 300x g, time is 5 minutes,                  and the volume of DPBS is 1 ml, and is pre-cooled                  at 4&#176; C. In the step (v), the used                  volume of the red blood cell lysate is 3 ml,                  pre-cooled at 4&#176; C allowed to stand on                  ice or 4&#176; C for 5 minutes, centrifugal                  speed of 300x g and time is 5 minutes, when DPBS                  is added to wash the cells the first two times,                  the volume of DPBS added is 10 ml for the third                  DPBS suspension, and the volume of DPBS added is                  1 ml. In the step (ii), the method specifically                  comprises (iia) extracting total RNA by using                  RNAprep pure to culture cells/bacteria total RNA                  extraction kit to extract RNA, (iib) performing                  reverse transcription by using PrimeScriptTM RT                  reagent Kit with gDNA-Eraser (Perfect Real Time)                  (Takara, RR047A), (iic) designing primers through                  NCBI primer-blast: PTH upstream primer                  5'-cagctactaacatacctgaacga-3', PTH downstream                  primer 5'-tcgagttcagatgttttcccagg-3'; the                  upstream primer of GCM2 is                  5'-cctgccctacaaaccgtgat-3', and the downstream                  primer of GCM2 is 5'-aaaggctcttcacctctcgc-3';                  (iid) amplifying with 2x Taq MasterMix (TIANGEN,                  KT201) at 94&#176; C for 3 minutes,                  4&#176; C for 30 seconds, 55&#176; C for                  30 seconds, 72&#176; C for 60 seconds, 30                  cycles in total, and (iie) electrophoresing the                  PCR products on 1% agarose gel. Preferred                  Components: In the step (i), the double                  antibodies are 100 U/ml penicillin and 100                  &#956; g/ml streptomycin, the pore size of                  the cell filter used in the mixed solution                  obtained after filtration and digestion is 100                  &#956; m, and the volume of DPBS is 10                  ml.

AB    NOVELTY - Process for producing a syngas composition                involves (i) charging an oxidant and a feedstock                composition comprising recycle plastics and a solid                fossil fuel to a gasification zone within a                gasifier, (ii) gasifying the feedstock composition                together with the oxidant in the gasification zone                to produce the syngas composition and (iii)                discharging at least a portion of the syngas                composition from the gasifier, where the recycled                plastics are added at a feed point comprising a                solid fossil fuel belt feeding a grinder after the                solid fossil fuel is loaded on the belt, a solid                fossil fuel belt feeding a grinder before the solid                fossil fuel is loaded onto the belt, or a solid                fossil fuel slurry storage tank containing a slurry                of the solid fossil fuel ground to a size as the                size fed to the gasification zone.
   USE - The process is useful for producing a syngas                composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The process can gasify plastics material in a                slurry that is stable, pumpable and added in a                manner so as not to disrupt the operations of the                solid fossil fuel slurry to the feed injector in                the gasifier, minimizes an increase in carbon                dioxide content over that generated by the solid                fossil fuels alone, provides pumpable slurries of                high solids content with a minimum of grinding and                adopts preheater to reduce the heat load on the                gasifier and improve the efficiency of utilization                of both fuel and oxygen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: The process involves                  adding the recycle plastics to a solid fossil                  fuel grinder or a belt containing a fossil fuel                  feeding the grinder, where the recycle plastics                  are added to the solid fossil fuel in a                  low-pressure section that has a lower pressure                  than the pressure within the gasifier, the                  recycle plastics are added to solid fossil fuel                  on a feed belt and the recycle plastics are                  deposited onto a belt before solid fossil fuel is                  added onto the belt. The solid fossil fuel is on                  top of the recycle plastics on the belt, where                  the recycle plastics are added to solid fossil                  fuel on a coal feed belt. The recycle plastics                  are added to a grinding mill containing coal and                  water. The recycle plastics are added to a slurry                  storage tank located downstream of a slurry                  grinding operation. The process involves adopting                  at least one of the following conditions such as                  (1) gasification within the gasification zone is                  conducted at a temperature of at least 1000                  degrees C, (2) the pressure within the                  gasification zone is greater than 2.7 MPa, (3)                  the feedstock composition is a slurry, (4) no                  steam is introduced to the gasifier that flows                  into the gasification zone, (5) the plastics are                  pre-ground such that at least 90% of the                  particles have a particle size of less than 2 mm,                  (6) the tar yield is less than 4 wt.%, (7) the                  gasifier contains no membrane wall in the                  gasification zone or (8) a combination of two or                  more of the above conditions. The process                  involves adopting all of the following conditions                  such as (a) steam is not supplied to the                  gasification zone, (b) the feedstock stream                  containing at least plastics and ground solid                  fossil fuel flowing to the gasifier, or the                  feedstock stream is introduced to a injector or                  charge pipe, or the feedstock stream is                  introduced into the gasification zone, or a                  combination of all the above conditions, and does                  not contain gases compressed in equipment for gas                  compression, (c) no gas stream containing more                  than 0.03 mole % carbon dioxide is charged to the                  gasifier or gasification zone, (d) methane gas is                  not charged to the gasifier or to the                  gasification zone, (e) the gasifier and the                  process do not contain a pyrolysis step or zone                  or a plasma treatment process, (f) a membrane                  wall, or a steam generating membrane, or a steam                  jacket in the gasification zone or between inner                  surfaces facing the gasification zone and the                  gasifier shell walls, are not present, (g) the                  gasifier is a down-flow reactor and the flow of                  syngas is vertically downward, (h) the gasifier                  is a single stage reactor, (i) the location for                  withdrawing the syngas stream from the gasifier                  is lower than at least one location for                  introducing the feedstock stream, (j) the                  gasifier contains refractory lining in the                  gasification zone, (k) the gasification zone is                  operated at a temperature of more than 1000                  degrees C, (l) the gasifier is operated at a                  pressure within the gasification zone (or                  combustion chamber) of at least 400 psig (2.76                  MPa) and (m) the average residence time of gases                  in the gasifier reactor is not greater than 25                  seconds. Preferred Components: The gasifier is an                  entrained flow slagging gasifier. The slurry                  storage tank contains milled solid fossil fuel                  and water prior to addition of the recycle                  plastics to the slurry storage tank, where the                  plastics are pre-ground prior to addition to the                  fossil fuel(s) or depositing onto a feed belt to                  a solid fossil fuel grinding mill. Preferred                  Composition: The feedstock stream contains not                  greater than 4 wt.% sewage sludge, waste paper,                  biomass or their combination, each based on the                  weight of the solids in the feedstock                  stream.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The recycle plastics                  in the feedstock composition or as fed to or                  combined with a solid fossil fuel is less than or                  equal to 2 mm, where 90 wt.% recycle plastics                  have a particle size in the largest dimension of                  not greater than 2 mm. Preferred Composition: The                  feedstock stream comprises plastics in an amount                  of 0.1 wt.% to less than 25 wt.% based on the                  weight of all solids, where the feedstock stream                  has a viscosity under 25000 cP. Preferred                  Components: The pre-ground plastics comprise at                  least the polymers obtained from at least the                  articles such as bottles, carpet, cellulosics,                  copolyesters, polyolefins, eyeglass frame or                  crosslinked polyethylene pipes.

TI Dephosphorization method for liquid steel slag, involves producing vaporized phosphorus during reaction is discharged through exhaust port on top of dephosphorization device and then enriched and recycled into phosphate fertilizer for reuse.
AU LI Z
   YU G
AE SHANGHAI CHICHUN ENERGY SAVING TECHNOLOG (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202075954H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using a slag tank to transport the liquid steel slag, and then use the lifting equipment to pour the liquid steel slag in the slag tank into the dephosphorization device. The liquid steel slag after the primary reaction treatment flows into the carbon reduction bed in the lower part of the dephosphorization device under the action of the high head difference, and reacts with the carbon in the carbon reduction bed twice. The vaporized phosphorus produced during the reaction is discharged through the exhaust port on the top of the dephosphorization device, and then enriched and recycled into phosphate fertilizer for reuse. The gas is carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, nitrogen, argon or blast furnace gas. The fuel is gas, oil, coal or coke. The mineral powder is clay, sand, kaolin, fly ash, coal powder, red mud, top ash.
   USE - Dephosphorization method for liquid steel slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the dephosphorization of steel slag in a high-temperature molten state and a liquid state, so that the dephosphorization of the steel slag is continuous and stable, the process is simple, automated, and sealed and pollution-free clean production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a dephosphorization device for liquid steel slag.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of a dephosphorization method for liquid steel slag. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PN CN111471022-A
TI New sulfamonomethoxine sodium crystal form I, prepared by adding sulfa-6-methoxypyrimidine into sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, stirring, adding activated carbon, stirring, decolorizing, filtering and adding organic solvent.
AU CAO C
   LIU A
   LI S
   LI Y
   NIE L
AE RINGPU (TIANJIN) BIOMEDICAL CO LTD (RING-Non-standard)
GA 202074526E
AB    NOVELTY - Sulfamonomethoxine sodium crystal form I exhibits an X-Ray powder diffraction pattern with peaks given in the specification, is new.
   USE - Used as sulfamonomethoxine sodium crystal form I.
   ADVANTAGE - The crystal form I and crystal form II: has stable properties and resistance to moisture; and preparation process is simple and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing sulfamonomethoxine sodium crystal form I; and
   (2) sulfamonomethoxine sodium crystal form II exhibits an X-Ray powder diffraction pattern with peaks given in the specification, is new.; and
   (3) preparing sulfamonomethoxine sodium crystal form II by supercritical fluid technology, comprising (1) adding sulfa-6-methoxine into a 20% aqueous sodium hydroxide solution at 70 degrees C, stirring at 60 degrees C for 1 hour, adding activated carbon and stirring for 0.5 hour to decolorize, filtering and adding organic solvent into the filtrate to obtain sulfamethoxine sodium mixed solution, (2) adding the sulfamonomethoxine sodium mixed solution into the extraction tank for extraction, passing the carbon dioxide fluid to a certain pressure, heating, stirring and maintaining the pressure and temperature for a certain period of time, (3) adjusting the pressure and temperature of the high-pressure pump to the crystallization tank for a certain period of time and (4) cooling the system down and drying under reduced pressure to obtain the sulfamonomethoxine sodium crystal form II.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation method of sulfamonomethoxine sodium crystal form I comprises (i) adding sulfa-6-methoxypyrimidine into a 10% sodium hydroxide aqueous solution at 70-80 degrees C and stirring at 50-60 degrees C for 1 hour; and (ii) adding activated carbon into the solution in step (i), stirring, decolorizing for 0.5 hours, filtering, adding organic solvent into the filtrate, cooling to 0-5 degrees C, maintaining for 2-4 hours, filtering and drying the solid obtained under reduced pressure at 60-65 degrees C to obtain crystal form I. Preferred Components: The X-ray powder diffraction pattern of the crystal form I is shown as specified. In the step (i), the mass-volume ratio of the sulfa-6-methoxypyrimidine to the aqueous sodium hydroxide solution is 1:1.2-1.6 and in the step (ii), the amount of activated carbon added is 0.4-0.6 wt.% of the mass of sulfa-6-methoxypyrimidine. in the step (ii), the organic solvent is tetrahydrofuran, and the volume mass ratio of tetrahydrofuran to sulfa-6-methoxypyrimidine is 2-5:1. The X-ray powder diffraction pattern of the crystal form II is shown as specified. In the step (1), the organic solvent comprising acohol solvents or ketone solvents comprising at least one of methanol, ethanol, isopropanol, n-butanol, acetone, methyl ethyl ketone or methyl isobutyl ketone and the volume-mass ratio of the added organic solvent to the solid sulfamonomethoxine sodium is 2:1-3:1. Preferred Method: The reaction conditions for crystallization and separation of the sulfamonomethoxine sodium mixed solution in the preparation of the supercritical fluid technology are, in the step (2), the extraction pressure is 10-30 Mpa, the temperature is 40-60 degrees C, and the time is 5-20 minutes and in step (iii), the crystallization pressure is 1-10 Mpa, the crystallization temperature is 20-30 degrees C, and the crystallization time is 15-40 minutes.

   CHEN Y
   SHI J
AE SHANGHAI INST CERAMICS CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGU-C)
GA 2020745930
AB    NOVELTY - Rare earth bromine oxide photocatalytic                material is rare earth bromine oxide. The rare                earth element is lanthanide light metal element,                preferably cerium, praseodymium and/or                neodymium.
   USE - The rare earth bromine oxide photocatalytic                material is used in photocatalytic hydrogen                production and high-efficiency carbon dioxide (CO2)                absorption (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the rare earth bromine oxide                photocatalytic material comprising taking rare                earth oxide and ammonium bromide as raw materials                and potassium bromide and/or sodium bromide as                cosolvent, mixing and solid phase reacting in                protective atmosphere at 850-1000 degrees C for 30                minutes-8 hours, obtaining rare earth bromine oxide                photocatalytic material, and ultrasonically                dispersing in solvent to obtain the rare earth                bromine oxide photocatalytic material with                two-dimensional nano-belt structure; taking rare                earth oxide and ammonium bromide as raw materials,                mixing, putting in protective atmosphere, reacting                for 4-8 hours at 300-500 degrees C to obtain a                sample, taking potassium bromide and/or sodium                bromide as cosolvent, mixing with the obtained                sample, putting in protective atmosphere at                850-1250 degrees C, solid phase reacting for 30                minutes-8 hours, obtaining rare earth bromine oxide                photocatalytic material, and ultrasonically                dispersing in solvent to obtain the rare earth                bromine oxide photocatalytic material with                two-dimensional nano-belt structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The rare earth bromine                  oxide has two-dimensional nano-belt structure. It                  has a length of 50-200 nm, width of 10-35 nm, and                  thickness of 2-30 nm. The purity of rare earth                  bromine oxide is greater than or equal to 80                  wt.%, preferably greater than or equal to 90                  wt.%. Preferred Method: The protective atmosphere                  is vacuum atmosphere, nitrogen atmosphere,                  hydrogen atmosphere, and/or inert atmosphere.                  When the protective atmosphere is vacuum                  atmosphere, the solid phase reaction is at                  850-1000 degrees C for 1-4 hours. The mass ratio                  of rare earth oxide and ammonium bromide is                  1:0.1-1:10. The mass ratio of rare earth oxide                  and cosolvent is 1:0.1-1:10.

PT P
PN CN111461263-A
TI Method for predicting concentration of dissolved gas in transformer oil, involves determining evaluation standard to obtain two indexes of average relative percentage error and maximum relative percentage error.
AU LIU K
   WU S
   LI H
   XU Y
   GOU J
   HE J
   WANG Q
   LIU T
   CHEN L
   CHEN X
   RUAN J
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 2020729485
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting gas dissolved in transformer oil to obtain original concentration of hydrogen, methane, ethane, ethylene, acetylene, carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide and characteristic vector. Empirical mode decomposition process is performed based on feature vectors. Normalization process is performed for decomposing a sub-sequence. A random forest model is constructed and trained. A predicted value of a sub-sequence component is obtained. Inverse normalization process is performed based on the predicted value. Data is overlapped after performing inverse normalization process to obtain a final prediction result. The final prediction result is compared with an actual value of dissolved gas concentration in the transformer oil. Model prediction performance is evaluated to perform error analysis of a prediction result and an actual value. An evaluation standard is determined to obtain two indexes of average relative percentage error and maximum relative percentage error.
   USE - Method for predicting concentration of dissolved gas in transformer oil based on empirical mode decomposition and random forest.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces difficulties of mutual influence, increases prediction precision between characteristic gas concentrations, accurately predicts change trend of dissolved gas concentration in oil and provides preventative overhauling of a subsequent transformer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for predicting concentration of dissolved gas in transformer oil based on empirical mode decomposition and random forest (Drawing includes non-English language text).

AB    NOVELTY - Grain cereal coating composition having a water absorption preventing effect comprises 2-10 wt.% bamboo extract wax, 2-5 wt.% sweet potato starch, 2-5 wt.% carrageenan, 0.5-5 wt.% polylactic acid, 2-5 wt.% xylitol, 2-5 wt.% carboxymethyl cellulose, 5-30 wt.% corn syrup solids, 3-7 wt.% lecithin, 1-5 wt.% erythritol powder, 1-5 wt.% Boswellia serrata powder, 2-5 wt.% green tea powder, and water (balance).    ADVANTAGE - The coating composition: prevents moisture absorption; and maintains crispy texture for long time.

PN WO2020124072-A1; CA3123236-A1; EP3893950-A1; US2022023496-A1
TI Method for producing nanofiber or microfiber structure, involves expanding nanofiber or microfiber mat after fixing process by exposing nanofiber or microfiber mat to gas bubbles, thus producing nanofiber or microfiber structure.
AU XIE J
   CHEN S
AE UNIV NEBRASKA (UNEB-C)
   UNIV NEBRASKA (UNEB-C)
   UNIV NEBRASKA (UNEB-C)
GA 2020543034
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves fixing a point of nanofiber or microfiber mat. The nanofiber or microfiber mat is expanded after fixing process by exposing the nanofiber or microfiber mat to gas bubbles, thus producing nanofiber or microfiber structure. The nanofiber or microfiber mat is exposed to a subcritical fluid and depressurizing. The subcritical fluid comprises carbon dioxide (CO2), nitrogen (N2), nitrous oxide (N2O), hydrocarbons or fluorocarbons. The nanofiber or microfiber mat comprises a poloxamer 407. The nanofiber or microfiber structure further comprise a material selected from the group consisting of gelatin, chitosan, starch, pectin, cellulose, methylcellulose, sodium polyacrylate and starch-acrylonitrile co-polymers.
   USE - Method for producing nanofiber or microfiber structure used during promotion process of bone regeneration in subject (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The methods allow for the fabrication of 3D objects of any size, thickness, and/or shape with controlled nanofiber alignment, pore size and/or porosity. By longer exposure to the bubbles, the thickness and porosity of the expanded nanofiber structure is increased. The method comprises placing the nanofibrous mat and dry ice in a sealed container, allowing the dry ice to turn into liquid CO2, and then unsealing the container to allow depressurization. The coating is used to enhance its mechanical property and maintain its integrity during the frozen section process.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating the transformation of two dimensional (2D) nanofiber mats to predesigned three dimensional (3D) complex shapes.

TI Method for preventing neglect of children and the elderly in a vehicle using environmental information, involves measuring environmental information inside a vehicle through sensors, which includes at least one of a human body sensor.
AU KIM S K
AE SMART CITY GRID CO LTD (SMAR-Non-standard)
GA 2020406341
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves measuring environmental information inside a vehicle through sensors, which includes at least one of a human body sensor, a carbon dioxide sensor, a temperature sensor, and a humidity sensor. The controlling modules are installed in the vehicle to control the air conditioner, heater, window and horn of the vehicle, when it is determined that the child and the elderly in the vehicle are neglected based on environmental information. The environmental information and neglect information alarm are transmitted to a mobile communication terminal of a manager or guardian. The movement of the human body is detected by the human body sensor, when the vehicle is turned off.
   USE - Method for preventing neglect of children and the elderly in a vehicle using environmental information.
   ADVANTAGE - Method for preventing neglect of children and the elderly in a vehicle using environmental information is capable of preventing safety accidents for children or the elderly, who are left in the vehicle. The control unit controls the overall operation of the system for preventing children and the elderly from leaving the vehicle using environmental information. The temperature inside the vehicle is controlled to prevent heat stroke or verbs of children or the elderly, who are left in the vehicle.

PN TW593908-U
TI Nozzle device for connecting outer tube to outer tube for conveying compressed gas and inner tube for conveying two gasified carbon gases, has guiding section connected to end face adjacent to large-diameter hole for sheathing inner tube.
AU XU W
   TSAO K J
   CHEN J
   LIU Q
AE HABOR PRECISION INC (HABO-Non-standard)
GA 202155478H
AB    NOVELTY - A nozzle device includes a body connected to an outer tube, a nozzle connected to the body, and a cover connected to the body and surrounding the nozzle and defining an annular chamber. The body includes a surrounding wall defining a channel. The channel is suitable for passing an inner tube. The surrounding wall has a hole communicating with the channel and the outside. The nozzle includes a large-diameter hole and a small-diameter hole connected to the channel, a tapered section connected to the end surface adjacent to the small-diameter hole, and a guide section connected to the end surface adjacent to the large-diameter hole. The large-diameter hole sleeves the inner tube. The small-diameter hole is used to cause the passing carbon dioxide gas to generate particles due to the phase change. When the compressed gas enters the ring chamber through the hole, a spiral gas flow is generated in the ring chamber through the hole, and flows in accordance with the conical section, and entrains the external air flow and particles to be injected forward. In this way, the air flow restricted by the annular chamber can only flow along the conical section, which not only can increase the flow velocity, but also improve the collimation during injection.
DC P42 (Spraying, atomising (B05).)
MC P42-A01; P42-T01A
IP B05B-007/02

PT P
PN CN110982859-A
TI Obtaining carbohydrates from pig wastewater involves using pig wastewater as a culture substrate, and culturing microalgae in two stages or semi-continuously to produce carbohydrates, where microalgae is Chlorella kessleri QWY28 strain.
AU HE S
   QU W
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 2020314197
AB    NOVELTY - Obtaining carbohydrates from pig wastewater involves using pig wastewater as a culture substrate, and culturing microalgae in two stages or semi-continuously to produce carbohydrates. The microalgae is Chlorella kessleri QWY28 strain, Scenedesmus or Desmodesmus species QWY36 or Chlamydomonas species QWY37. The steps of the two-stage cultivation involves placing microalgae in pig wastewater at 20-35 degrees C, under light intensity of 200-600 mu mol/m2/second, and continuous passage of 2.5% carbon-dioxide (CO2), cultivating until the concentration of microalgae cells in the algae liquid reaches 9.0g/L in first stage. The second stage involves increasing the light intensity to 700-800 mu mol/m2/second, and cultivating at 20-35 degrees C.
   USE - Method for obtaining carbohydrates from pig wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to obtain carbohydrates from pig wastewater, which allows to purify sewage untreated pig sterilized and yield carbohydrate and is good for protecting ecological environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The steps of the semi-continuous cultivation involves placing microalgae in pig sewage at 20-35 degrees C, under light intensity of 200-1000 mu mol/m2/second, and the microalgae cell concentration in the algae liquid reaches 9.9 10.0g/L, then replacing 20-80 vol.% algae fluid with pig wastewater, continue to culture, and replacing pig wastewater with the same replacement volume for 3-6 times repeatedly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The pig wastewater comprises 814 plus minus 34-970 plus minus 47mg/L COD, 22 plus minus 3-51 plus minus 5mg/L nitrate ion (NO3-), 425 plus minus 23 One or more of 493 plus minus 42mg/L and 31 plus minus 4-42 plus minus 6 mg/L nitrate ion. Preferred Conditions: The cultivation temperature of the microalgae in the first stage in the two-stage cultivation is 30 degrees C, and light intensity is 500 mu mol/m 2/second. The cultivation temperature of the second stage microalgae in the two-stage cultivation is 30 degrees C, and the light intensity is 750 mu mol/m 2/second. The inoculation amount of microalgae in the two-stage culture is 80 mg/L based on the volume of pig wastewater in the semi-continuous culture, and inoculation of microalgae amount is 80mg/L. The temperature of semi-continuous cultivation of microalgae cultivation is 30 degrees C, and light intensity is 500 mu mol/m2/second. The replacement algal fluid is to replace 20 vol.% or 60 vol.% algal fluid with pig sewage, and the replacement is repeated 5 times, and the interval of each replacement is 47.8 plus minus 2.7 hours or 96.2 plus minus 5.2 hours.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

GA 202020767N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves defining a threshold value of oxygen storage capacity and an internal temperature value of a catalyst of an ultra-charged spark-ignition engine (1) such that the catalyst is stabilized. Richness setpoint is intrusively modified by imposing a slot to empty the oxygen catalyst. Oxygen storage capacity is calculated during a lean phase. The calculated value of the oxygen storage capacity is compared with threshold value. The catalyst is not sufficiently degraded. A chamber scan is carried out when the calculated value of the oxygen storage capacity is greater than the threshold value.

PN CN110668664-A; CN110668664-B
TI Microbial inoculant filler useful for            environmentally-friendly ecological toilet, comprises            e.g. Penicillium, streptomycete, saccharomycete, lactic            acid bacteria, Cyanobacteria, Bacillus, Pseudomonas,            Trichoderma, and Rhodococcus.
AU LIU Z
   XU Y
   LIU S
   HUANG S
   REN Y
AE UNIV CHINA AGRICULTURAL (UCAG-C)
GA 2020067925
AB    NOVELTY - Microbial inoculant filler comprises                Penicillium, streptomycete, saccharomycete, lactic                acid bacteria, Cyanobacteria, Bacillus,                Pseudomonas, Trichoderma, Rhodococcus, and                sulfur-reducing bacteria. The microbial inoculant                filler obtained by mixing and culturing                Penicillium, streptomycete, saccharomycete, lactic                acid bacteria, Cyanobacteria, Bacillus,                Pseudomonas, Trichoderma, Rhodococcus, and                sulfur-reducing bacteria with a filler base                material containing sawdust, rice hull, peanut hull                and corn starch, and sterilizing.
   USE - The microbial inoculant filler is useful for                environmentally-friendly ecological toilet                (claimed), microbial degradation, and toilet manure                degradation.
   ADVANTAGE - The microbial inoculant realizes the harmless                treatment of toilet manure, converts the organic                matter in the manure into water and carbon dioxide,                reduces the content of harmful microorganisms e.g.                Escherichia coli in the feces by 5-90%, and the                generation of odor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the microbial inoculant filler,                comprising (i) mixing and culturing Penicillium,                streptomycete, saccharomycete, lactic acid                bacteria, Cyanobacteria, Bacillus, Pseudomonas,                Trichoderma, Rhodococcus, and sulfur-reducing                bacteria, and obtaining microbial agent, (ii)                mixing the sterilized raw material containing                sawdust, rice hull, peanut shell and corn starch,                adjusting moisture content to 20-70%, sterilizing                at 180-220 degrees C for 15-30 minutes using the                sterilizing kettle, and obtaining filler base                material, and (iii) uniformly stirring the                microbial agent with the filler base material in a                mixing ratio of 1-10:10000, stirring and uniformly                mixing for every 2-6 hours, and pre-fermenting for                24-72 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Components: The microbial inoculum                  comprises the strains, where the strain includes                  1-3 pts. wt. rope-shaped blue mold fungus powder,                  1-3 pts. wt. Penicillium bacteria powder, 1-3                  pts. wt. Down Streptomyces bacteria powder, 1-3                  pts. wt. yeast powder, 1- 3 pts. wt. Cryptococcus                  laurentii ball, 1-3 pts. wt. Candida krusei                  bacteria powder, 1-3 pts. wt. Pediococcus                  acidilactici bacterial powder, 1-3 pts. wt.                  Cyanobacteria powder, 1-3 pts. wt. Bacillus                  subtilis bacteria powder, 1-3 pts. wt. Bacillus                  amyloliquefaciens bacterial powder, 1-3 pts. wt.                  Lysinibacillus sphaericus bacterial powder, 1-3                  pts. wt. Bacillus brevis bacteria powder, 1-3                  pts. wt. Bacillus cereus bacteria powder, 1-3                  pts. wt. Lactobacillus acidophilus bacteria                  powder, 1-3 pts. wt. Bacillus halophilus powder,                  1-3 pts. wt. heat-resisting Bacillus                  licheniformis bacteria powder, 1-3 pts. wt.                  sulfur reduction bacteria powder, 1-3 pts. wt.                  Pseudomonas fluorescens bacteria powder, 1-3 pts.                  wt. Rhodopseudomonas palustris bacteria powder,                  1-3 pts. wt. Trichoderma powder, and 1-3 pts. wt.                  Rhodococcus erythropolis bacteria powder, where                  the bacterial activity of the bacterial powders                  is 106-1010 cfu/g. The base filler material                  comprises 150-200 pts. wt. sawdust, 100-130 pts.                  wt. rice husk, 10-30 pts. wt. peanut shell, 10-30                  pts. wt. corn starch, 5-10 pts. wt. bean pulp,                  10-25 pts. wt. fly ash and water, where adjusting                  the moisture content to 20-70% and sterilizing at                  110-220 degrees C for 10-60 minutes. Preferred                  Method: The mixing ratio of the microbial                  inoculum and filler is 1-10:10000. The filler has                  a replacement cycle of 1-12 months and a shelf                  life of 1-4 months.

PN WO2019230416-A1; JP2020519455-X; TW202003246-A; JP6819820-B2; IN202017049242-A; IN388031-B
TI Multilayer film used in food packaging bag, is obtained by layering surface layer, intermediate layer and seal layer, each layer comprises propylene resin, and intermediate layer comprises plant-derived low-density polyethylene.
AU YASUSHI W
   KEISUKE H
   WATANABE Y
   HAMASAKI K
AE DIC CORP (DNIN-C)
   DIC CORP (DNIN-C)
GA 2019A2492Y
AB    NOVELTY - Multilayer film is obtained by layering a surface layer, an intermediate layer, and a seal layer, where each layer contains a propylene resin, and the intermediate layer additionally contains a plant-derived low-density polyethylene (B1).
   USE - The film is used in food packaging bag for bread packaging (all claimed), for pillow packaging bags, for making bottom gusset bags. The film is used for making packaging bags, containers and container lids for foods, medicines, industrial parts, miscellaneous goods and magazines. The film is used as a matte film.
   ADVANTAGE - The film maintains high moldability, is environmentally-friendly, has high transparency, high rigidity, high scratch resistance and high bag breaking resistance. The film exhibits excellent impact resistance even at low temperatures, excellent heat sealability, excellent productivity, and has excellent easy-openability during pillow packaging. The film ensures high fusing seal strength when a gusset portion is formed, and high bag making suitability. The film provides high-class feeling when used for food packaging. The film suppresses bag breakage and leakage. The film has excellent hygiene and reduces cost. The film maintains processability and high stability during bag making, suppresses the decrease in rigidity, high mat feeling, reduces carbon dioxide emission, and improves environmental compatibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a food packaging bag comprising the multilayer film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The intermediate layer comprises 1-15 %mass component (B1) based on the resin contained in the intermediate layer. The intermediate layer further contains 5-20 %mass fossil fuel-derived polyethylene (B2) based on the resin contained in the intermediate layer. The propylene resin in the intermediate layer contains 10-50 %mass propylene- alpha -olefin random copolymer, 55-85 %mass propylene homopolymer, 20-50 %mass propylene- alpha -olefin block copolymer. The content of propylene resin in the surface layer and the seal layer is 50 %mass or more. Preferred Components: The component (B2) is a linear low density polyethylene. The propylene resin in the surface layer and the seal layer contains propylene- alpha -olefin copolymer. Preferred Properties: The melt flow rate of the component (B1) is 1-7 g/10 minutes. The component (B2) has a melt flow rate of 2-10 g/10 minutes. In the thickness of the multilayer film, the proportion of the surface layer is 10-35%, the proportion of the intermediate layer is 45-85%, and the proportion of the seal layer is 5-20%. The multilayer film has an average thickness of 25-50 mu m. Preferred Product: The food packaging bag further comprises a gusset portion.

TI Bio-waste treatment and recovery system for facilitating e.g. anaerobic digestion of carbonaceous feedstock, has reactor for producing effluent streams, and absorber for discharging specific amount of hydro-bromic acid solution.
AU PARKER M L
   PARKER R Z
AE CHEMERGY INC (CHEM-Non-standard)
GA 2019950572
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a reactor (40) for producing a first effluent stream and a second effluent stream. A heat exchanger (60) recovers specific amount of heat from the second effluent stream. A first absorber (70) separates the first effluent stream. The first absorber discharges first amount of hydro-bromic acid solution. An electrolyzer (90) electrolytically disassociates hydro-bromic acid into solution of hydrogen bromide and bromine, and solution of hydrogen bromide and hydrogen. A second absorber (80) separates hydrogen gas and water from the solution of hydrogen bromide and hydrogen. The second absorber discharges second amount of hydro-bromic acid solution.
   USE - Bio-waste treatment and recovery system for facilitating anaerobic digestion of carbonaceous feedstock, production of aqueous solution of hydrogen bromide and carbon dioxide, and recovery of hydrogen through electrolysis of aqueous hydro-bromic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The system absorbs excess power to allow a grid to operate effectively by avoiding need for spinning reserve and allowing sourcing from intermittent renewable power, and facilitates rapid and essentially irreversible reactions to convert hydrogen sulfide waste into sulfuric acid and easily dissociates hydrobromic acid.

   Mixer (30)
   Reactor (40)
   Heat exchanger (60)
   Absorbers (70, 80)
   Electrolyzer (90)
   Bio-waste treatment and recovery system (100)

PN CN209608129-U
TI Assembled solid insulating cabinet frame, has lower frame whose top part is provided with L-shaped air bag that is filled with carbon dioxide gas, where lower frame and upper frame are rotatably connected with door that is fixed with switch handle.
AU PENG Z
AE XINYANG PINGGAO ELECTRIC CO LTD (XINY-Non-standard)
GA 201997237C
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims an assembled solid insulating cabinet frame, comprising a lower frame and an upper frame, the lower frame of the top and upper frame is fixedly connected with the lower surface of the lower frame is fixedly connected with a 2 asterisk 2 four locking arrangement of the universal wheel. inner side of the bottom wall of the lower frame middle position fixedly connected with a fixing rod, the fixing rod is inserted with a supporting rod, the supporting rod is respectively rotatably connected with two connecting block through the two connecting rods, the two connecting blocks are fixedly connected with the telescopic rod through a connecting plate. the telescopic rod far away from one side of the connecting plate is fixedly connected on the inner side wall of the lower frame, the telescopic rod is sleeved with a telescopic spring. The utility model through a connecting rod and a rotary shaft pin, a fixing rod, a supporting rod, a connecting block, a connecting plate, a telescopic rod and matched with the telescopic spring, which can make placement of combined insulating cabinet plate is in shaking process can effectively buffer, so as to realize the protection of the device.
DC X13 (Switchgear, Protection, Electric Drives)
MC X13-A03; X13-A04C2; X13-E01; X13-E02; X13-E09

PT P
PN CN110393979-A
TI Preparing polyvinyl alcohol fiber degradable filter material by opening, carding and lapping polyvinyl alcohol fiber, adding base cloth, needling, performing singeing treatment, dipping in post-finishing agent and heat-setting material.
AU HE L
   LIU J
   YU M
   XIA Q
   CHEN X
AE NANJING JIHUA 3521 ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECT (XINX-C)
GA 201992768W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing polyvinyl alcohol fiber degradable filter material using degradable polyvinyl alcohol fiber, involves (i) opening, mixing, carding and lapping polyvinyl alcohol fiber, adding a base cloth after grid connection, where the base cloth raw material is polyvinyl alcohol fiber, the gram weight of the base cloth is 130 g/m2, and feeding into a pre-needling machine for pre-needling at a pre-needling density of 60 200 puncture/cm2, (ii) carrying out primary needling or spunlace reinforcement on a pre-needled felt to obtain a filter material felt with the gram weight of 300-600 g/m2, (iii) carrying out singeing treatment on spunlace felt at a singeing speed of 25-30 m/minute, (iv) dipping the singed spunlace felt into a post-finishing agent, and (v) feeding the dipped filter felt into a drying oven under the action of a drawing force of a drying oven for pre-baking and baking, and heat-setting the filter material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing polyvinyl alcohol fiber degradable filter material.
   ADVANTAGE - The filter material can withstand high temperature of 150 degrees C, has excellent acid resistance and alkali resistance, is a high-performance fiber which can be degraded in hot water to water and carbon dioxide, and does not cause environmental pollution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Conditions: In step (ii), if the main needling is adopted for reinforcement treatment, the needling density is 400-600 g/cm2. In step (ii), if the spunlace reinforcement is adopted, the filter material needs to be pre-baked at 60-80 degrees C for 15-20 minutes and baked at 100-120?OC for 5-10 minutes after spunlace reinforcement, where the spunlace pressure is 200-380 bar. In step (v), the dipped filter felt in the drying oven is pre-baked at 120-150 degrees C for 15-20 minutes, and baked at 150-170 degrees C for 5-10 minutes. Preferred Components: In step (iv), the post-finishing agent comprises water, sodium dodecyl sulfate, defoaming agent and stabilizer, where the concentration of the sodium dodecyl sulfate is 1-5%. Preferred Properties: The polyvinyl alcohol fiber has a fineness of 1-3 dtex and strength of greater than or equal to 14 cn/dtex. The polyvinyl alcohol fiber is a degradable filter material, and the basis weight is 300-600 g/m2.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); F04 (Braiding, knitting - including trimmings and non-woven fabrics (D04).); ; J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
